facts: GAMA is a non-profit trade association which passionately promotes growth, profitability, and workforce development in Georgia’s and the region's automotive and ground transportation industry.
GAMA is a community of businesses, both domestic and international, with common interests and goals, which provides a highly interactive forum to help members achieve their objectives.
In January 2023, Rick Walker, President & CEO of GAMA, was a speaker at the 29th Annual U.S. Federal Reserve Bank Automotive Insights Symposium.
The page features a map showing just some of the current major activity relating to the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) near and around Savannah, Georgia.
GAMA has had record attendance at its last venue, as well as four sold-out meetings in a row before that.
Registration is now open for SAC 2026, with exhibit booths currently selling fast.
GAMA fosters and promotes an inclusive environment that leverages the unique contributions of diverse individuals to improve the lives of its members.
"GAMA", "GEORGIA AUTOMOTIVE MANUFACTURERS ASSOCIATION", AND "DRIVING THE GROWTH OF GEORGIA'S AUTOMOTIVE INDUSTRY" ARE FEDERALLY REGISTERED TRADEMARKS OF GAMA.
source_title: GAMA - Homepage

facts: Ecoplastic Corporation will invest approximately $205 million in a new manufacturing facility in Bulloch County.
The project will create 456 new jobs.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the investment and job creation on January 5, 2023.
Ecoplastic Corporation is a Korea-based company established in June 1984 as Apollo Ind Co., Ltd. that manufactures plastic automobile parts for original equipment manufacturers and other suppliers.
The company is a Tier-1 supplier for Hyundai Motor Group (HMG) and Kia.
Shang Han serves as President and CEO of Ecoplastic Corporation.
The new facility will be located at 4822 Highway 301 South Register near Statesboro.
The plant will feature injection molding and painting capabilities.
The facility will produce exterior parts, bumpers and garnishes, interior parts, consoles, and trims specifically for electric vehicles.
Ecoplastic expects to begin operations by October 2024.
The company will be hiring for HR, finance, parts development, quality control, facility maintenance, injection, and paint production positions.
Billy Allen serves as Chair of the Development Authority of Bulloch County.
Alyce Thornhill is the Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy at the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce team.
The project was executed in partnership with the Development Authority of Bulloch County, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia EMC.
Pat Wilson serves as Commissioner for the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
Ecoplastic will serve its client HMGMA (Hyundai Motor Group Manufacturing America) from its new Statesboro location, which offers close proximity to Georgia's ports system.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that the company will be able to serve the Hyundai Metaplant well from Statesboro due to its community and port access.
Billy Allen expressed confidence that Bulloch County's location, workforce, and quality of life will help Ecoplastic succeed.
Pat Wilson noted that Georgia is building a supplier network for the Hyundai metaplant in Bryan County and spreading suppliers across the region to provide well-paying, high-tech manufacturing jobs.

Georgia is positioned as a hub for the electric mobility industry, with more than 30 EV-related projects announced since 2020 bringing more than $21 billion in investment and more than 26,300 jobs to the state.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground on October 25 for its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility in neighboring Bryan County.
Offsite suppliers connected to the Hyundai project were predicted to invest approximately $1 billion in other locations around Georgia.
Ecoplastic established its first Technical Research Center in 1987 and was publicly listed on the Korean Stock Exchange in March 2000.
The company currently employs more than 770 employees globally.
Ecoplastic recorded sales revenue of more than $700 million in 2021.

links.context: Deputy press contact for Governor's office

source_title: Kia and HMG Supplier to Build Manufacturing Facility in Bulloch County | Georgia

entity_name: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution Joint Venture
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution Joint Venture
facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution will invest an additional $2 billion in their battery cell manufacturing joint venture at the Metaplant in Bryan County.
The additional investment raises the JV’s total investment value to more than $4.3 billion.
The expansion will create another 400 new jobs.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that in a single year, they broke ground on the largest project in state history, landed multiple suppliers across the state for Hyundai's Metaplant, and welcomed LGES to Bryan County.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia continues to make itself the e-mobility capital of the nation.
Governor Brian Kemp noted that major investments ultimately go to hardworking Georgians in the form of paychecks, improved schools and infrastructure, and more.
Governor Brian Kemp thanked Hyundai Motor Group and LGES for recognizing that Georgia is the No. 1 state for business.

José Muñoz, president and global COO of Hyundai Motor Company and president and CEO of Hyundai and Genesis Motor North America, stated the incremental investment reflects a continued commitment to create a more sustainable future powered by American workers.

José Muñoz stated Hyundai is proud to partner with LGES and grateful for the support of Governor Kemp and Georgia communities in driving operations as they work to be a global leader in the electrified mobility industry.

In May 2023, Hyundai Motor Group and LGES signed a memorandum of understanding establishing LGES as the partner for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America’s (HMGMA) onsite battery cell manufacturing JV.
The EV manufacturing facility and the battery JV now represent an estimated $7.59 billion in investment and will create 8,500 new jobs for the region over the next eight years.

Dong-Myung Kim, president and head of the Advanced Automotive Battery Division of LG Energy Solution, stated the investment underscores a dedication to driving America’s EV transition while bolstering the local economy through quality jobs.
Dong-Myung Kim thanked the State of Georgia for support and expressed excitement to bring top-quality products and operational experience to grow with its communities.
The 30 GWh facility will be able to support the production of 300,000 units of EVs annually at full operations.
Hyundai Mobis will assemble battery packs using cells from the plant, then supply them to Hyundai Motor Group’s U.S. manufacturing facilities for production of Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EV models.

Carter Infinger, Chairman of the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority, stated the additional investment will bring 400 additional well-paying jobs for those in the Savannah region.
Carter Infinger noted that continued investment by Hyundai Motor Group and announced suppliers like LG Energy Solution is truly remarkable and transformative for the region.

Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project in partnership with the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority (JDA), Georgia Power, Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated Governor Kemp set a goal of Georgia becoming the top location for EV manufacturing in the United States, and the state is in a great position to claim that distinction.

Pat Wilson stated Georgia continues to add to its industry-leading success by building an ecosystem that supports the entire EV supply chain and bringing jobs home to Georgians in every corner of the state.

Pat Wilson noted that state and community partners like the Savannah-area JDA, utility providers, Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Quick Start create a collaborative, business-friendly environment for success.
Since 2018, Georgia has pursued job creation along the entire EV supply chain, resulting in more than $25 billion in investments and the creation of over 30,000 jobs.
Battery related projects have accounted for at least $11 billion in investment.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent when compared to FY21.
Hyundai Motor Group is a global enterprise with about 250,000 employees worldwide and mobility brands including Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis.
LG Energy Solution (KRX: 373220) is a split-off from LG Chem and a leading global manufacturer of lithium-ion batteries for electric vehicles, mobility, IT, and energy storage systems.
LG Energy Solution has 30 years of experience in battery technology and is the top battery-related patent holder in the world with over 25,000 patents.
LG Energy Solution's global network spans North America, Europe, Asia, and Australia, including JV facilities with General Motors, Stellantis N.V., Hyundai Motor Group, and Honda Motor Co., Ltd.
LG Energy Solution aims to achieve carbon neutral operations by 2050.

links.context: More information about Hyundai Motor Group
Learn more about LG Energy Solution’s ideas and innovations
Press contact for Governor Brian P. Kemp
Deputy press contact for Governor Brian P. Kemp
Communications manager contact for Georgia Department of Economic Development

source_title: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution to Invest Additional $2B in | Georgia

facts: Rivian Inc. will invest $5 billion in a carbon-conscious campus in Georgia for its electric adventure vehicles.
Across operations, Rivian will create approximately 7,500 jobs on just under 2,000 acres at the East Atlanta Megasite.
The site is represented by the Joint Development Authority of Jasper, Morgan, Newton, and Walton counties.
Once manufacturing operations are fully ramped up, the Georgia facility will be capable of producing up to 400,000 vehicles per year.
Rivian’s manufacturing plant represents the single-largest economic development project in state history.
Governor Brian P. Kemp cited Georgia's long-term focus on logistics, talent, workforce, and responsive government as contributing factors to the announcement.
Governor Kemp noted his administration's recent emphasis on innovation and development in the electric mobility ecosystem equipped the state for this pivotal announcement.
Georgia Quick Start workforce training resources are prepared to meet Rivian's production and research and development needs.
World-class higher education institutions and statewide logistics infrastructure assets are prepared to meet Rivian's production and R&D needs.

The Electric Mobility and Innovation Alliance is a statewide initiative between government, industries, electric utilities, nonprofits, and other relevant stakeholders focused on growing the electric mobility ecosystem in Georgia.
Construction on the facility at Stanton Springs North is expected to begin in summer 2022.
The start of production is slated for 2024.
Rivian’s technology centers at the new campus will support the company’s research and development programs.
The entire project will support the expansion of the U.S. electric vehicle industry and decarbonize the transportation and energy sectors.
In August, Governor Kemp announced the launch of the Electric Mobility and Innovation Alliance.
Georgia is committed to ensuring existing industry and accompanying 55,000 automotive manufacturing jobs receive support for retraining and expansions to transition to electrified transportation.
Rivian Chief People Officer Helen Russell stated alongside Illinois, Georgia is the place for Rivian to scale and thrive.
Rivian has developed and vertically integrated a connected electric platform flexibly applied to adventure products and B2B products like last-mile delivery vans launched in partnership with Amazon.
Rivian’s launch products are the R1T and R1S.
The Rivian R1T all-electric truck earned the 2022 truck of the year from MotorTrend.
Joint Development Authority of Stanton Springs Chairman Jerry Silvio welcomed Rivian to the country’s premier joint economic development partnership.
The Joint Development Authority of Stanton Springs began over 25 years ago with the goal of creating high-tech, regional-focused jobs for generations.
Economic development directors Shane Short and Serra P. Hall and attorney Andrea Gray provided dedication to ensure the vision is met.
Four counties and utility partners planned strategically to ensure minimal impact on the local environment.
Rivian’s strategic location on the I-20 corridor will enable access to resources and speed products to market.
Georgia offers 1,200 miles of highway and 5,000 miles of rail.
The newly opened Mason Mega Rail Terminal at the Port of Savannah is the largest on-terminal rail facility in North America.

Griff Lynch, Executive Director for the Georgia Ports Authority, stated the authority will expand facilities for processing containers and finished vehicles to provide supply chain solutions to Rivian.
Russell McMurry, Commissioner of the Georgia Department of Transportation, stated Georgia is recognized as a national leader in EV corridor readiness.
Mike Hopkins, Executive Director of the Newton County Water and Sewerage Authority, partnered with Rivian and the Joint Development Authority of Stanton Springs for a landmark opportunity.
The water system is uniquely designed to support citizens, businesses, and the natural environment in the Southeast.
Georgia’s talent pool is bolstered by one of the largest state university systems in the country.
Eighty-five accredited public and private universities in the state award more than 132,000 degrees and certificates annually.
The Technical College System of Georgia consists of 22 colleges offering education and training in numerous industries.
Metro Atlanta Chamber President and CEO Katie Kirkpatrick stated Metro Atlanta is ideally suited for Rivian’s new operations due to a diverse and highly skilled workforce.
Georgia Quick Start will build and operate a state-of-the-art manufacturing training center providing a fully customized training program for Rivian’s start-up needs.
This training initiative will build capacity and curricula within the Technical College System of Georgia to maintain a long-term pipeline of highly skilled employees.
The Georgia Department of Labor will assist in identifying and recruiting workers.
Hank Evans, Director of GDEcD’s Aerospace, Defense, and Advanced Manufacturing team, represented GDEcD’s Global Commerce division on this project.

Partners include the Joint Development Authority (JDA) of Jasper, Morgan, Newton, and Walton Counties; Georgia Ports Authority; Georgia Department of Transportation; Georgia Quick Start; Metro Atlanta Chamber of Commerce; and Georgia Power.
Pat Wilson, Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner, stated more change in the automotive industry in the next 10 years than in the past 100.
Within the last five years, 78 automotive-related companies have located or expanded operations in Georgia, creating thousands of new jobs.
Existing companies in Georgia have expanded portfolios to include parts for EVs.
Companies from Korea, Germany, Turkey, and the Netherlands have recently announced plans to build EV-related facilities in the state.
Rivian designs, develops, and manufactures electric vehicles and accessories, selling directly to consumer and commercial markets.

Deputy Press Secretary for Governor Kemp

source_title: Gov. Kemp Welcomes Rivian for Single-Largest Project in State History | Georgia

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on February 19, 2025 that Duracell will establish its new Global Headquarters for Research and Development in Georgia.
The project involves creating 110 jobs and investing approximately $56 million.
Duracell's new Global Headquarters for Research and Development will be located at 101 Nerem Street NW in Atlanta.

The headquarters site is adjacent to Georgia Tech’s Midtown Atlanta campus within Science Square, an 18-acre multi-phase development featuring more than 1.8 million square feet of lab and office space.
Duracell currently operates a manufacturing facility in LaGrange, Georgia, that has been in operation since 1980.
The LaGrange facility currently supports approximately 400 jobs.
Duracell operates a logistics and distribution plant in Fairburn, Georgia, that began operations in 2020.
The Fairburn plant supports an additional 275 jobs.
Duracell is an American manufacturer of alkaline, lithium coin, and hearing aid batteries.
The Duracell brand and company was started in the 1920s.
Duracell was acquired by Berkshire Hathaway Inc. (NYSE-BRK.A, BRK.B) in 2016.
Berkshire Hathaway Inc. is a $250 billion holding company owning subsidiaries that engage in diverse business activities.
Governor Brian Kemp stated Georgia has set itself apart as a leader in attracting innovative companies with its research institutions, world-class logistics network, and pro-business environment.

Mayor Andre Dickens stated Atlanta’s transportation infrastructure, diverse talent pool from top-tier universities, and a thriving tech ecosystem make the city an ideal environment for corporate innovation and growth.

Katie Kirkpatrick, President & CEO of the Metro Atlanta Chamber, stated Duracell's decision strengthens the region’s reputation as a hub for innovation and furthers Georgia's growing battery ecosystem.
Robb Pitts, Chairman of the Fulton County Board of Commissioners, stated Duracell’s choice solidifies Fulton’s leadership in innovation and talent attraction.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated research and development is critical to Duracell's long-term success and that locating the new Global Headquarters in Atlanta shows Georgia as a long-term partner.
Dr. Liben Hailu, Chief Technology Officer at Duracell, stated the move to Atlanta will strengthen their position as a global leader in the industry and drive innovation in battery technology.

Assistant Director of Statewide Projects John Soper represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this project in partnership with Invest Atlanta, Select Fulton, Metro Atlanta Chamber, Georgia Power, and the University System of Georgia.
The 110 new innovation jobs will provide opportunities for more Atlanta residents to build promising careers.
Duracell is locating literally next door to the world-class talent at Georgia Tech and in close proximity to other tens of thousands of new graduates in the region.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Duracell Selects Georgia for New R&D Headquarters | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Ecoplastic Corporation will create 456 new jobs and invest approximately $205 million in a new manufacturing facility in Bulloch County.
Ecoplastic Corporation was established in June 1984 as Apollo Ind Co., Ltd.
Ecoplastic is a Korea-based company that manufactures plastic automobile parts for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and other suppliers.
Ecoplastic is a Tier-1 supplier for Hyundai Motor Group (HMG) and Kia.
The company engages in the manufacturing of automotive interior and exterior plastic parts, including bumpers, consoles, trims, and plastic molds.
Shang Han serves as President and CEO of Ecoplastic.
Ecoplastic's new facility will be located at 4822 Highway 301 South Register near Statesboro.
The new plant will be capable of injection molding and painting.
The facility will produce exterior parts, bumpers and garnishes, interior parts, consoles, and trims for electric vehicles.
The company expects to begin operations by October 2024.
Ecoplastic will be hiring for HR, finance, parts development, quality control, facility maintenance, injection, and paint production positions.
Billy Allen serves as Chair of the Development Authority of Bulloch County.
Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy at the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team, represented the agency on this project.
The project was developed in partnership with the Development Authority of Bulloch County, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia EMC.
Pat Wilson serves as GDEcD Commissioner.
Since 2020, more than 30 EV-related projects have announced more than $21 billion in investment and more than 26,300 jobs in Georgia.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground on October 25 in neighboring Bryan County for its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility.
Offsite suppliers connected to the Hyundai project were predicted to invest approximately $1 billion in other locations around the state.
Ecoplastic currently employs more than 770 people.
Ecoplastic recorded sales revenue of more than $700 million in 2021.
Ecoplastic established its first Technical Research Center in 1987.
Ecoplastic was publicly listed on the Korean Stock Exchange in March 2000.

facts: Hanon Systems will create 160 new jobs and invest more than $40 million in a new manufacturing facility in Bulloch County.
The new facility will be located at Gateway Regional Industrial Park in Bulloch County.
The facility will assemble automotive thermal solutions.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in May 2024.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Georgia’s automotive and e-mobility industries continue to grow as they welcome Hanon Systems to Georgia.
Governor Brian P. Kemp noted that as the state furthers its position as the EV capital of America, new jobs and opportunities are coming to hardworking Georgians all over the state.

Min Sung, President and Chief Executive Officer of Hanon Systems, expressed pleasure in contributing to the local economy of Bulloch County through job creation and thanked the State of Georgia and its agencies for guiding the project.
Min Sung stated that the announcement aligns with Hanon Systems' strategy to support the industry shift toward electrification while growing its North America footprint.
The project was supported by the Development Authority of Bulloch County, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy Alyce Thornhill represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
Billy Allen, Chair of the Development Authority of Bulloch County, stated that Statesboro-Bulloch County is honored by Hanon Systems’ decision to locate its new facility there.
Billy Allen noted excitement over welcoming another automotive supplier to the community, particularly one that will work closely with the Hyundai Metaplant and the electric vehicle industry.
Billy Allen described the decision as an example of the resources offered by the community and region to help companies succeed.
Georgia Ports Authority (GPA) Executive Director Griff Lynch stated that the announcement further solidifies Georgia’s position as a growing hub for auto manufacturing.
Griff Lynch said GPA is proud to support the operations of Hyundai Motor Group and its suppliers as they deliver jobs and opportunity for Georgia residents.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that suppliers like Hanon Systems can successfully support larger industry ecosystems in Georgia thanks to partnership-driven solutions ranging from workforce training to logistics systems efficiencies.
Pat Wilson noted that Bulloch County, as part of the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority, has been part of a growing success story from the beginning.
Since 2020, over 40 EV-related projects have announced more than $22.7 billion in investment and 28,400 jobs in Georgia.
On October 25, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground in neighboring Bryan County on its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility.
Hanon Systems was formed in 1986 and has grown into a leading supplier of automotive thermal and energy management solutions for electrified and conventional vehicles.
Hanon Systems' offerings include solutions in heating ventilation and air conditioning, powertrain cooling, compressor, fluid transport, and electronics and fluid pressure.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Automotive Supplier Hanon Systems to Create 160 New Jobs in Bulloch County | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

entity_name: SK Innovation EV Battery Plant in Commerce, Georgia
canonical_name: SK Innovation EV Battery Plant in Commerce, Georgia
facts: On March 12, 2021, Governor Brian Kemp sent a letter to President Biden repeating his request to overturn an International Trade Commission (ITC) ruling against SK Innovation.
The ITC ruling prevents SK Innovation from importing battery parts and components for ten years.
The ITC ruling includes a limited exception allowing the company to produce batteries at the Commerce, Georgia plant for Ford for four years and for Volkswagen for two years.
SK Innovation told the state of Georgia that the ITC ruling will make the Commerce plant economically unviable.
Absent presidential action to disapprove the ITC ruling, SK Innovation will be forced to shutter the Commerce facility.
The Commerce, Georgia EV battery plant will employ nearly 2,600 people upon completion.
The project represents the largest foreign investment in Georgia's history at nearly $2.6 billion.
When completed, the factory will account for nearly half of the nation's vitally needed non-captive EV batteries, available on the free market for EV manufacturers.
The plant's initial yearly output will supply 22 GWh of EV battery capacity.
The initial capacity is enough for 330,000 electric cars.
SK Innovation has plans to expand the facility to employ more than 6,000 workers and produce 50 GWh annually by 2025.
The factory is owned by the Korean company SK Innovation ("SKI").
The Commerce plant will be the only major EV battery plant in the nation built without federal subsidies.
President Biden's administration is conducting an important review regarding the future of the EV battery plant in Commerce, Georgia.
Governor Kemp stated the Commerce plant aligns with President Biden's publicly announced goal of electrifying the U.S. auto fleet with good, high-paying jobs for local workers.
President Biden's recently announced Executive Order on supply chains recognized the critical role of EV batteries to the economy and national security.
China is currently the leading producer of EV batteries.
Closing the Commerce, Georgia plant will result in the United States falling further behind China in the global EV battery race.

Governor Kemp requested that President Biden exercise his legal authority to disapprove the ITC ruling on grounds that it is contrary to the public interest and will seriously jeopardize the administration's environmental and economic goals.
President Obama took similar action in 2013 when he concluded that an ITC decision threatened the public interest and would harm U.S. consumers.
Governor Kemp emphasized that the livelihoods of thousands of Georgians are at stake due to the ruling.
source_title: Gov. Kemp Advocates for Georgia Jobs, Asks Biden to Overturn Trade Commission Ruling | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

entity_name: Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia
canonical_name: Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia
facts: On November 5th, 2025, Governor Brian Kemp and Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America President and CEO Tony Heo joined state and local leaders to celebrate the grand opening of the Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia.
The center is an 89,000-square-foot state-of-the-art facility designed to train the next generation of electric vehicle (EV) manufacturing professionals.
The facility is strategically located adjacent to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) in Bryan County.
This marks the first training center in Georgia geared toward EVs and sets a new standard for workforce development.
HMGMA has announced an expansion that will include hybrid vehicles, and the flexible facility is designed to accommodate training for those vehicles in the future.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that with record-breaking economic development statewide, the focus remains on bringing quality jobs to hardworking Georgians and connecting them to the skills needed for success.
Governor Brian Kemp added that the Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia will give thousands of people over the years the knowledge they need to benefit from this generational project.
José Muñoz, President and CEO of Hyundai Motor Company, stated that the center represents a commitment to investing in people as the company continues to create high-paying American jobs.

José Muñoz noted that as production expands at HMGMA, one of the most advanced facilities in the world, a workforce equipped with skills to build next-generation vehicles with next-generation production processes is needed.
José Muñoz mentioned that the partnership with Georgia Quick Start ensures Meta Pros have access to world-class training that will help them succeed and help Georgia lead in advanced manufacturing.
José Muñoz thanked Governor Kemp, Commissioner Pat Wilson, and the economic development team in Georgia for their ongoing support.
Hyundai’s project in Georgia will create approximately 8,500 jobs by 2031.
At full capacity, the facility can train up to 824 individuals at a time.
The center provides customized, immersive education tailored to Hyundai’s advanced EV manufacturing processes.
The center is operated by Georgia Quick Start, the State’s renowned workforce development program and part of the Technical College System of Georgia (TCSG).
The center will prepare employees for roles at HMGMA and affiliate companies.
Greg Dozier, Commissioner of TCSG, stated that the center is about giving Georgians the tools to build their own futures right here at home.
Greg Dozier added that the center reflects Georgia’s commitment to innovation, accessibility, and long-term economic opportunity.
Quick Start’s training professionals collaborated with HMGMA to design and build world-class infrastructure that closely mirrors the processes trainees will encounter at the Metaplant.

The facility includes a STEP Line (Safe Transition for Efficient Production), an introductory line where trainees build timing, awareness, and coordination skills in a controlled, digital environment.

The facility includes an IMPACT Line (Immersive Manufacturing Process and Conveyor Training), a full-length, moving production line with 53 integrated assembly activities and a built-in quality check system replicating real-world EV production.
The facility includes a High Voltage Battery and Safety Lab for hands-on learning with high-voltage systems critical to EV and hybrid vehicle performance.
The facility includes a VR Experience Lab, a simulated environment for safety and hazard identification.
The facility includes an Automation & Mechatronics Lab focused on programmable logic controllers (PLCs), sensors, motion systems, and industrial automation.
Quick Start Deputy Commissioner Scott McMurray stated that subject matter experts partnered directly with HMGMA to learn the skills its Meta Pros will need when they hit the production floor.
Scott McMurray added that the team has simulated those processes in a safe, hands-on training environment.

Additional facilities include a 5,764-square-foot multipurpose “Momentum Room,” six flexible classrooms, a robotics lab, welding lab, paint lab, and a robotic dog that supports advanced automation training.
The training center stands as a core pillar of Georgia’s broader strategy to prepare and train the state’s workforce for success in advanced manufacturing.
The launch reaffirms Georgia’s leadership in the global mobility and EV manufacturing sectors while delivering unmatched value to communities across the state.
Tony Heo stated that the grand opening marks a proud milestone for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, the State of Georgia, the Technical College System, and Georgia Quick Start.
Tony Heo added that the training center gives confidence that trained talent will be available to shape the future of mobility and American manufacturing right here in Southeast Georgia.
source_title: HMTCG Grand Opening - Georgia Quick Start

facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) is mere months away from producing its first electric vehicles.
Construction crews are putting finishing touches on a two-kilometer long mass production plant in Ellabell, Ga.
HMGMA's Meta Pros will be ready to hit the shop floor on day one.
Training for HMGMA is provided by Georgia Quick Start.
An essential part of the training Quick Start provides to HMGMA is a general assembly overview.
Trainees learn shop floor roles, safety requirements and policies, and the sequential process of car assembly.
General assembly takes place after the stamping, welding, and paint departments have completed their work on an automobile.
The department installs many parts of the car, ranging from safety features such as air bags and seat belts to final touches such as sun visors and weather stripping.
Final automation covers the battery, center console, seats, and connection of hoses and cables, which is an especially critical step in the EV manufacturing process.
Automated door install is among the last steps before the car is checked for quality and sent out for distribution.

Quick Start’s training for HMGMA general assembly is currently taking place at an interim facility in Port Wentworth while the Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia is being constructed next to the Metaplant.
source_title: HMGMA — General Assembly - Georgia Quick Start

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Joon Georgia, Inc., an automotive parts manufacturer, will create 630 new jobs and invest $317 million in Bulloch County.
The company’s new manufacturing facility marks it as the first confirmed supplier for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America in Bryan County.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that less than two weeks after Hyundai Motor Group's groundbreaking, the region is already seeing its impact.
Governor Brian Kemp noted that Joon Georgia will be the first of many companies drawn to Georgia to support the Metaplant, creating jobs and opportunity for generations of Georgians.
Joon Georgia is part of Ajin USA, a current supplier for multiple Hyundai Motor Group facilities.
The company produces a variety of automotive body parts and electronics for original equipment manufacturers.

Jung Ho Sea, CEO of Ajin USA, expressed excitement about building Joon Georgia's first plant in Georgia as one of the first Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America automotive suppliers in Bulloch County.
Joon Georgia, Inc. will create approximately 630 new jobs with competitive benefits in Bulloch County and the State of Georgia.

The company committed to being the best parts supplier for automotive and EV companies like Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, as well as a top employer and corporate citizen in Bulloch County and the City of Statesboro.
Joon Georgia’s new facility will be located at Bruce Yawn Commerce Park (previously known as Southern Gateway Commerce Park) near Statesboro and in close proximity to the Port of Savannah.
Connections to the port will allow both Hyundai and Joon Georgia to access global markets quickly and efficiently.
Operations at the Bruce Yawn Commerce Park are expected to begin in mid-2024, with hiring starting in mid-2023.
Billy Allen, Chair of the Development Authority of Bulloch County, welcomed Joon Georgia to the Statesboro-Bulloch County community and region.

Billy Allen noted that the site's proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America and local education resources like Georgia Southern University and Ogeechee Technical College will provide critical workforce support.

Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy Alyce Thornhill represented the GDEcD Global Commerce team on the project in partnership with the Development Authority of Bulloch County, Georgia Quick Start, Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Power.
Workforce development for the new facility will be supported by Quick Start, the top-ranked workforce training program in the nation.

Partnering with the Technical College System of Georgia, Quick Start will help assess workers, train new employees on unique processes, and develop customized job-specific training using current techniques and media.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson called Joon Georgia's announcement a landmark moment for driving Georgia’s automotive industry into the future.
Pat Wilson stated that Hyundai Motor Group, its suppliers, and the Savannah area JDA are creating generational opportunities through regional collaboration that shares benefits across county lines.
Pat Wilson expressed excitement about welcoming the company to Georgia and looking forward to its future in Bulloch County.
Georgia is positioned as a hub for the electric mobility industry due to assets that make the automotive industry successful.
Since 2020, more than 30 EV-related projects have contributed over $13.3 billion in investment and announced more than 18,100 jobs in Georgia.
On October 25, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground in neighboring Bryan County on its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility.
Non-affiliated suppliers connected to the Hyundai project are predicted to invest approximately $1 billion in other locations around the state.
source_title: First Hyundai Motor Group supplier to build facility in Bulloch County – Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority

facts: Federal workplace safety inspectors found a global electric vehicle battery manufacturer exposing employees to serious safety and health hazards at its Commerce plant for the second time in less than a year.
Workers suffered potentially permanent respiratory damage in an October 2023 lithium battery fire at the facility.
OSHA determined SK Battery America Inc. failed to train its employees on how to protect themselves adequately in the toxic atmosphere that left multiple workers injured.
The agency cited SK Battery for five serious violations.
The company exposed workers to inhalation hazards, including hydrofluoric acid vapors produced in lithium battery fires, by failing to establish a complete emergency response plan.
The company did not ensure its staffing agency, Moveret Inc., who supplied workers at the site, made their employees aware of the hazards associated with lithium battery fires.
The company failed to train on-site emergency responders and members of the fire brigade on hazards associated with lithium battery fires and equip them to treat employees exposed to such hazards.
The company did not train workers on hazardous chemicals in their work areas and a means to protect themselves from lithium battery fires.
The company failed to annually fit-test workers required to utilize tight-fitting facepiece respirators.
OSHA has assessed the company $77,200 in proposed penalties, an amount set by federal statute.
In December 2023, OSHA cited SK Battery for exposing employees working with cobalt, nickel, and total dust to levels above the Permissible Exposure Limit.
The December 2023 inspection found the employer failed to institute feasible administrative or engineering controls for chemical hazards.
The December 2023 inspection also found the employer exposed workers to high levels of occupational noise and failed to implement a monitoring program and audiometric testing.
Another inspection resulted in a citation that was issued in January 2024 for a hazard associated with the company’s energy control program.
The company is contesting these findings before the independent Occupational Safety and Health Review Commission.

OSHA Area Office Director Joshua Turner stated that on multiple occasions in less than a year, SK Battery America has failed to meet required federal standards designed to help every worker end their shift safely.
Joshua Turner noted that while emerging industries bring innovation and employment opportunities to communities, employers must ensure the safety of their employees is their priority.
A subsidiary of SK On, SK Battery America Inc. employs about 3,100 people at two battery manufacturing plants in Commerce.
SK On is part of the Seoul-based SK Group, South Korea’s second-largest conglomerate with companies operating in the energy, advanced materials, biopharmaceuticals and digital industries.
source_title: US Department of Labor finds electric vehicle battery maker again exposes workers to serious health hazards after investigation of Commerce fire | U.S. Department of Labor

canonical_name: U.S. Battery Manufacturing of Augusta Inc.
facts: U.S. Battery Manufacturing of Augusta cited for 4 violations, faces $160K in penalties.

A federal workplace safety investigation found an Augusta battery manufacturer exposed employees to unsafe levels of lead based on personal air monitoring of workers and lead accumulation on respirators and counters in areas where workers took lunch breaks.
OSHA issued two repeat citations after May 2023 inspections found the company failed to have engineering and work practice controls in place to reduce exposures to lead.
Inspections found the company did not prevent lead from accumulating on surfaces.
OSHA issued two serious citations after finding U.S. Battery exposed employees to lead levels in concentrations greater than permissible.
The agency found the company did not ensure employees’ respirators were cleaned and disinfected.
OSHA proposed $160,727 in penalties.

OSHA Area Office Director Josh Turner stated that elevated lead levels can cause debilitating and permanent health issues and that employers are legally responsible for minimizing workers' exposure using engineering controls, safe work practices, and clean protective equipment.
Since 2018, federal safety inspections at U.S. Battery's Augusta facility have identified numerous violations related to lead exposure.
In 2019, OSHA issued citations for one serious, one repeat, and one other-than-serious violation at the facility.
In 2018, OSHA found nine serious violations and one repeat and one other-than-serious violation at the facility.
Founded in 1926, U.S. Battery Mfg. Augusta Inc. designs and manufactures deep cycle batteries for scissor lifts, golf carts, boats, and other uses by numerous industries in the U.S. and abroad.
The company has locations in Corona, California, and in Evans and Augusta, Georgia.

The company has 15 business days from receipt of its citations and penalties to comply, request an informal conference with OSHA's area director, or contest the findings before the independent Occupational Safety and Health Review Commission.
source_title: US Department of Labor finds Augusta battery manufacturer exposing employees to unsafe lead levels, other hazards | Occupational Safety and Health Administration

canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, LLC
facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) is currently accepting applications for Production Team Member positions.
HMGMA currently has open positions for Maintenance Team Members.
HMGMA currently has open positions for Salaried/Administrative Positions.
The facility offers competitive wages and bonus potential to employees.
93% of Medical, Dental, and Vision insurance is company-paid for Meta Pros and their families, effective the first day.
HMGMA provides company-paid Life Insurance, AD&D, Short-Term Disability, and Long-Term Disability coverage.
Employees receive 15 paid company holidays, comprising 10 federal holidays and a 5-day winter shutdown.
Staff members are eligible for up to 23 days of paid time off, including up to 20 days of vacation based on length of service plus up to 3 days of emergency PTO.
HMGMA offers a 401(k) savings plan that matches 100% of the first 6% contributed by the employee.
Employees receive discounts on new Hyundai and Genesis vehicles.
The facility includes an on-site cafeteria and clinic, plus a free limited amount of team wear.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, LLC (HMGMA) is Hyundai Motor Group’s first dedicated mass-production Electric Vehicle plant.
The all-new manufacturing facility is located just outside of Savannah, Georgia.
The plant will feature a highly-connected, automated, and flexible manufacturing system.
All production processes—order collection, procurement, logistics, and production—will be optimized utilizing AI and data.
The innovative manufacturing system is designed to create a human-centered work environment with robots assisting human workers.
The plant will build more than 300,000 vehicles per year.
Production will include Hyundai's luxury brand Genesis.
HMGMA refers to its facility as the Metaplant and its employees as Meta Pros.

HMGMA has a policy prohibiting discrimination, harassment, and retaliation against team members, job applicants, or other individuals based on race, color, religion, gender, sex, sexual orientation, pregnancy, marital status, age, national origin, disability, genetic/family medical history, service in the uniformed services, filing for bankruptcy, engaging in concerted protected activity, filing a workers’ compensation claim, exercising rights under the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 (“FMLA”) and/or the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970 (“OSHA Act”), or other legally protected activities.
source_title: HMG Metaplant America Careers

facts: The job is located in Ellabell, GA, US, with Savannah as the nearest major market.
The position is for a Parts Development Supplier Quality Development Specialist.
Responsibilities include developing, implementing, and controlling procedures and tools to improve Supplier Quality Management System efficiency.
The role requires fostering strong relationships with suppliers for effective communication and collaboration.
The specialist will collaborate with suppliers to establish and maintain quality standards.
Regular audits of supplier facilities will be conducted with findings documented.
Suppliers will be supported in implementing best practices and quality control processes.
Quality assurance processes throughout the supply chain will be monitored and enhanced.
The position requires working closely with internal teams to ensure quality alignment.
Supplier quality issues must be investigated and resolved promptly.
Corrective and preventive actions will be implemented and monitored.
Key performance indicators (KPIs) for supplier quality will be tracked and analyzed.
Reports on supplier performance and quality improvements will be prepared and presented.
The role drives continuous improvement efforts and promotes a culture of quality among suppliers.
Compliance with industry standards and regulations must be ensured.
Accurate documentation will be maintained to support internal and external audits.
Potential risks in the supply chain will be identified and mitigated.
The specialist will contribute to strategic initiatives for supplier quality improvement.
Monitoring and supporting the prevention of supply chain disruptions that could impact HMGMA is required.
Collaboration with Engineering (R&D), Quality, and Production Planners is necessary.
Information related to production/potential suppliers must be planned, organized, and disseminated.
The up-to-date supply base information database will be maintained.
HMGMA stands for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
HMGMA is Hyundai Motor Group's first dedicated EV mass-production plant.
The plant is described as innovative with cutting-edge production technology applied.
A Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Business Management, Supply Chain, Finance, Accounting, or a related field from an accredited university is required.
High proficiency in Microsoft Office Suites, especially Excel and PowerPoint, is required.
A minimum of 5 years of related job experience is preferred.
Experience in the Automotive industry in Purchasing, APQP, PPAP, Supplier Development/Engineering, IATF 16949, Launch, Program Management, or related fields is strongly preferred.

Experience in manufacturing processes including injection molding, press, stamping, die casting, welding, assembly, paint, chroming, surface treatments, foaming, SMC, extrusion, SMT, and various raw materials is preferred.
Experience with Six Sigma or other quality improvement methodologies is preferred.
Experience from an OEM or major commodity suppliers is strongly preferred.
Bilingual ability in any two combinations of English, Korean, and/or Spanish is preferred.
Multicultural openness to embrace and work with different cultural values is required.
Flexibility in working hours to include weekends, holidays, and off shifts is required.
Domestic and international travel is required for this role.
HMGMA offers opportunities for growth, work flexibility, competitive pay, and benefits.
Benefits include competitive Medical/Vision/Dental insurance, 15 paid holidays, paid time off, and a competitive 401k plan.
Strong command of the English language is essential for safety, communication, documentation, and collaboration across the organization.
The position is primarily sedentary in an office setting but requires occasional lifting of up to 15 pounds.
When in the production area, responsibilities include standing, walking, bending, and reaching for extended durations.
Production area duties may involve repeated lifting, carrying, pushing, or pulling of materials and products weighing up to 25–30 pounds.
The role requires manual dexterity for handling power tools, machinery, or production equipment.
Work environments may be noisy, hot, or require the use of personal protective equipment (PPE).
Employees must be able to climb ladders or stairs and operate equipment safely and effectively.
The position requires the capability to learn and work on multiple production processes as required.
Adequate visual and auditory abilities are needed to identify safety hazards and communicate clearly.
Consistent and punctual attendance is expected.
The role requires the ability to collaborate and perform effectively in a team-oriented environment.
The FLSA Classification for this position is Exempt/Salary.
Submitting a resume may result in creating a registration with the Georgia Department of Labor on Employ Georgia.

HMGMA is an equal opportunity employer that provides consideration without regard to age, ancestry, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws.
source_title: Parts Development Supplier Quality Development Specialist Job Details | Hyundai Motor Company

facts: SK Innovation announced that SK Battery America (SKBA), a U.S. production corporation for the electric battery business, has begun large-scale recruitment in preparation for the commercial operation of its first plant early next year.

SKBA is searching for outstanding candidates to apply for jobs in all areas of their operations, from production to quality control, maintenance, safety management, logistics, finance, and human resources.
This is the largest recruitment effort since it began hiring production supervisors and professional engineers last June.
Advertisements have appeared in local newspapers including the Jackson Herald, Braselton News, and Banks County News.
Application information is also posted on various state and local websites and social media channels.
SKBA plans to hold multiple job fairs starting at the end of April.
It will host its second "Drive-thru Job Fair" to make the application process more accessible during the COVID-19 pandemic.
The first such job fair was held in the second half of last year, proved popular with applicants, and yielded successful hires.

SKBA CEO Jun-ho Hwang remarked, "We appreciate the overwhelming support we have received from Georgians as we move forward with our plans. We are excited to be recruiting from the area again, and to be contributing to the continued economic growth and development of the region."

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson thanked SK Innovation, Lanier Technical College, and Georgia Quick Start for working together and already making these jobs of the future a reality for hundreds of Georgians.

Commissioner Pat Wilson stated, "This is only the beginning, and I can't wait to see the transformative impact SK Innovation and our coordinated workforce training programs will have as they create and attract thousands of clean energy jobs to Georgia."
The construction of SK's second plant is on track.
SKBA's construction partners are actively leasing materials and equipment warehouses, reviving the commercial real estate market in the region.
SKBA plans to complete construction of the second plant by the end of 2022.
SKBA plans to begin commercial operation of the second plant by the end of 2023.

Representative Director of SK Innovation Battery Business Dong-seob Jee thanked all elected officials in Georgia and the residents of Commerce for their unwavering support of their new battery plants, even when they encountered difficulties.
Dong-seob Jee added, "We are pleased that our commercial operations will proceed as planned."
SK Innovation will invest a total of 2.6 billion USD (approximately 3.016 trillion KRW) to complete construction of its first and second battery plants in Commerce, Georgia.
Approximately 2,600 American jobs will be created by the end of 2023, including nearly 1,000 jobs by the end of this year.
SK Battery America, Inc. is a wholly-owned subsidiary of SK Innovation Co. Ltd., and the leading developer of lithium-ion batteries for electric vehicles.
source_title: SK Battery America To Hire Hundreds Of Employees For First Battery Plant, Construction Of Second Plant On Track

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Ecoplastic Corporation will create 456 new jobs.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Ecoplastic Corporation will invest approximately $205 million in a new manufacturing facility in Bulloch County.
Ecoplastic Corporation is an automotive parts manufacturer.
Ecoplastic Corporation was established in 1984.
Ecoplastic Corporation is a Korea-based company.
Ecoplastic manufactures plastic automobile parts for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and other suppliers.
Ecoplastic is a Tier-1 supplier for Hyundai Motor Group (HMG) and Kia.
Ecoplastic engages in the manufacturing of automotive interior and exterior plastic parts, including bumpers, consoles, trims, and plastic molds.
Shang Han, President and CEO of Ecoplastic, stated the company is honored to establish a production facility for electric vehicle parts in Bulloch County, Georgia.
Shang Han noted the facility will increase new jobs within the area and provide a stable supply of high-quality products to client HMGMA.
Shang Han expressed appreciation for the support of State and County governments.
Ecoplastic’s new facility will be located at 4822 Highway 301 South Register near Statesboro.
The new plant is capable of injection molding and painting.
The plant will produce exterior parts, bumpers and garnishes, interior parts, consoles, and trims for electric vehicles.
The company expects to begin operations by October 2024.
The company will be hiring for HR, finance, parts development, quality control, facility maintenance, injection, and paint production positions.
Development Authority of Bulloch County Chair Billy Allen stated they are honored to welcome Ecoplastic to Bulloch County.
Billy Allen stated they are pleased that the important Hyundai supplier has chosen their community.
Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was in partnership with the Development Authority of Bulloch County, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia EMC.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated Ecoplastic has decades of experience as a supplier for Kia and Hyundai.
Pat Wilson noted they are building the supplier network for the Hyundai metaplant in Bryan County.
Pat Wilson highlighted the project provides well-paying, high-tech manufacturing jobs for Georgians.
Georgia is positioned as a hub for the electric mobility industry.
Since 2020, more than 30 EV-related projects have announced more than $21 billion in investment and more than 26,300 jobs in Georgia.
On October 25, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground in neighboring Bryan County on its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility.
Offsite suppliers connected to the Hyundai project were predicted to invest approximately $1 billion in other locations around the state.
Projects announced to date have already exceeded this predicted amount.
Ecoplastic Corporation was established in June 1984 as Apollo Ind Co., Ltd.
It established its first Technical Research Center in 1987.
It was publicly listed on the Korean Stock Exchange in March 2000.
It currently employs more than 770 employees.
It recorded sales revenue of more than $700 million in 2021.
source_title: Longtime Kia and HMG Supplier to Build Manufacturing Facility in Bulloch County – Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hanwha Advanced Materials Georgia, Inc. (HAGA), a manufacturer of lightweight advanced materials for sustainable technology, will create more than 160 new jobs in the City of Cartersville.
HAGA will invest an estimated $147 million in a new manufacturing facility to supply the Qcells facility in Cartersville.
Hanwha Advanced Materials Georgia, Inc. is a subsidiary of Hanwha Group.
HAGA will supply Qcells with encapsulant film used in solar cells to ensure long-term panel durability.
HAGA will be the only company in the United States manufacturing solar encapsulants.
The new facility will be located at Highland 75 Corporate/Industrial Park, a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) certified site in the City of Cartersville.
The facility is predicted to come online in Summer 2024.
HAGA will be hiring for engineers and line operators.
Qcells announced in January that it would expand its solar panel production capacity in Georgia to 8.4 gigawatts.
3.3 gigawatts of the expanded capacity will be manufactured at the Bartow County facility by 2024.
Since welcoming Qcells to Georgia in 2018, more than 4,000 related jobs have been announced for Georgians.
Solar photovoltaic is the fastest-growing source of energy in Georgia, and the state ranks as a Top Ten state for solar energy production.
The Solar Energy Industries Association ranked Georgia seventh by cumulative solar capacity in 2022.
Over the past five years, clean-tech jobs have been one of the fastest growing manufacturing sectors in Georgia.
Project Manager Jacob Lee represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.

The project was completed in partnership with the Bartow-Cartersville Joint Development Authority, Cartersville-Bartow County Department of Economic Development, Georgia Electric Membership Corporation (Georgia EMC), and Georgia Quick Start.

Hanwha Advanced Materials Company is a global leader in irreplaceable, high-performance, lightweight composite materials and provides products essential for customers in the field of lightweight composite materials and solar materials.
Qcells is headquartered in Seoul, South Korea (Global Executive HQ) and Thalheim, Germany (Technology & Innovation HQ), with manufacturing facilities in the U.S., Malaysia, China, and South Korea.

Deputy press contact for Governor Kemp's office

source_title: Qcells Supplier to Invest $147 Million in Cartersville Facility | Georgia

facts: Claims that car manufacturer Hyundai would be closing down a sprawling auto plant "megasite" located in rural Georgia spread across the internet in early September 2025.
The rumor spread following a raid by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) on Sept. 4, 2025, at a Hyundai manufacturing facility in Ellabell, Georgia, located outside Savannah.
ICE detained 475 people during the raid, most of them South Korean nationals.
Social media users alleged the company was pulling out of the area, claiming "8500 American jobs and 4.3 billion dollar investment from South Korea... gone."
The Ellabell plant currently employs about 1,200 people with plans to eventually employ at least 8,100 workers.
The raid focused on a construction site for HL-GA Battery Company, a joint partnership between Hyundai and LG Energy Solution announced in 2023 to produce electric vehicle battery cells.
HL-GA was touted as an additional $2 billion investment following Hyundai's larger $5.5 billion electric vehicle plant announced in 2022.

Steven Schrank, special agent in charge of Homeland Security Investigations in Georgia and Alabama, stated detained employees worked for various companies on the site, including subcontractors, not just the parent company.
There was no evidence that Hyundai was closing the Georgia facility in light of the raid.
The Associated Press reported HL-GA would be pausing construction of the battery plant, which is scheduled to open in 2026.
Michael Stewart, director of public relations and communications at Hyundai North America, stated there have been no changes to Hyundai's operations in the U.S.
Hyundai's official statement claimed none of those detained are directly employed by Hyundai Motor Company and that the company has zero tolerance for law violators.
Hyundai is aware of the immigration enforcement action at its supplier HL-GA Battery Company in Bryan County, Georgia, and is closely monitoring the situation.
Hyundai expects all partners, suppliers, contractors, and subcontractors to comply with employment verification requirements and immigration laws.
North America Chief Manufacturing Officer Chris Susock will assume governance of the entire megasite in Georgia and conduct an investigation to ensure supplier and subcontractor compliance.
CNN reported the raid's impact on the local community was less visible than in other immigration crackdowns because many detainees were temporary employees during construction.
Few permanent employees expected to fill 8,500 slots have been hired, and the bulk of the current workforce consists of transient single men on temporary visas or contracts rotating through monthly.
A local business owner reported an overnight drop in business at Viet Huong Supermarket in Ellabell, noting only three Korean customers instead of the usual 10 to 15.
One local resident stated Hyundai's facility has not yet brought many jobs to the community and felt more construction jobs at the battery plant should have gone to local residents.
source_title: Unpacking claims Hyundai is closing Georgia plant following ICE raid | Snopes.com

facts: Three Hyundai suppliers plan to add manufacturing plants in Georgia for a combined investment of nearly $180 million.
Gov. Brian Kemp announced the supplier investments.
Automotive parts manufacturer NVH Korea will invest over $72 million in a new manufacturing facility in Locust Grove, Georgia.
NVH Korea will produce EV battery system components that protect, connect, and monitor battery cells' performance.
NVH Korea's new facility is expected to create 160 jobs.
NVH Korea's operations are anticipated to begin in Q2 of 2024.
The Locust Grove plant will be NVH Korea's second Georgia facility.
NVH Korea's subsidiary, AFS America, operates a facility in Columbus that produces floor and carbo mats and cargo trays.
South Korea-based Daesol Ausys will establish a $72 million facility in West Point.
Daesol Ausys's new plant will create 140 jobs across production, quality control, maintenance, human resources, and management.
Daesol Ausys's production is expected to begin in December 2024.
Daesol Ausys will produce luggage boards and covers.
Daesol Ausys supplies General Motors and Kia.
Kia operates a manufacturing facility in West Point.
In July, Kia announced a $200 million investment to expand its West Point plant to produce a new electric SUV model beginning in Q2 of the following year.
Car seat maker Das Corp will establish a $35 million plant in Metter.
Das Corp's new facility will produce automotive seat structures when operations begin in the second half of 2024.
Das Corp's investment will create 300 jobs.
The three new factories follow Hyundai's announcement of a $5.5 billion manufacturing campus in Ellabell, which is the largest economic development project in Georgia’s history.

Gov. Brian Kemp stated that suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant resulted in over $2 billion in investment last fiscal year alone, helping Georgia achieve a third straight year of record-breaking economic growth.
In late May, Hyundai and LG Energy Solution struck a joint venture deal to build a $4.3 billion battery cell plant adjacent to Hyundai’s EV campus.
In early September, Hyundai and LG Energy Solution announced an additional $2 billion investment toward the upcoming plant.
The total joint venture investment will reach $6.3 billion.
Hyundai and LG Energy Solution will each hold a 50% stake in the venture and facility.
Hyundai will assemble battery packs using cells from the joint venture plant to send to its U.S. manufacturing sites.
A state official told Manufacturing Dive that the total $6.3 billion investment will create 8,500 jobs.
source_title: Hyundai’s Georgia EV supplier network adds 3 more manufacturers | Manufacturing Dive

The plant has produced its first IONIQ 5 electric.

facts: Georgia Tech has over 20 faculty and more than 150 researchers working to power the future with next generation energy storage technologies.
The center's focus is on batteries for electric mobility, grid, and renewable energy storage.
The Georgia Tech Advanced Battery Center operates state-of-art facilities for battery technology development, testing, and characterization.
The center is working to establish additional facilities, including a grid storage “living lab” to enable R&D on realistic energy storage use scenarios.
The Georgia Tech Advanced Battery Center builds and supports interactions between a variety of industry partners and Georgia Tech researchers.
The center welcomes the opportunity to bring its expertise to bear in solving industry-relevant energy storage challenges.

The Georgia Tech Advanced Battery Center unites the expertise of Georgia Tech’s faculty and students to create next battery technologies for electric vehicles, grid energy storage, electric aviation, and other applications.
The center is advancing the state-of-the-art in battery materials and technologies.
The center provides comprehensive facilities for battery fabrication, testing, and characterization.
The center focuses on education and workforce development to educate the engineers, scientists, and workforce needed for an electrified society.
GTABC Co-Directors Dr. Matthew McDowell and Dr. Gleb Yushin bring decades of experience in battery research and innovative development.
source_title: Georgia Tech Advanced Battery Center

facts: As gas prices rise, Georgia is emerging as a leader in electric vehicle manufacturing, which will help fight climate change and provide millions of jobs.
Between Bluebird and SK Innovations, to the newer Rivian and Hyundai plants, Georgia is certainly becoming a hub for the construction of electric vehicles.
Governor Brian Kemp helped strike a deal between the State of Georgia and Rivian earlier this year to build a massive electric vehicle factory.
The Rivian plant will produce electric trucks when it begins operating in 2024.
The Rivian plant will provide an estimated 7,500 jobs.
The Rivian plant will cost $5 billion to construct.
The state of Georgia has offered Rivian $1.5 billion in tax incentives for the project.
The Rivian deal has created controversy in the surrounding community regarding concerns about how the plant will impact quality of life, especially air and water quality.
GCV stands with residents in their environmental concerns and expects Rivian to work closely with the community to build the new plant in an ethical and environmentally sound manner.
President Biden announced that Hyundai will spend $5 billion to open a new electric vehicle factory in Savannah, Georgia.
The Hyundai plant in Savannah will add 8,000 new jobs to the area.
A nationwide network of EV chargers supported by the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law will enable families to travel gas-free.
Georgians are predicted to save $1.3 billion on gasoline per year by 2035 with the increase in usage of electric vehicles.
The National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure (NEVI) Formula Program was established by President Biden’s Bipartisan Infrastructure Law to build out a national electric vehicle charging network.
Earlier this year, the U.S. Departments of Transportation and Energy announced nearly $5 billion that will be made available to states for EV infrastructure.
Georgia will receive an estimated $134,975,283 from the NEVI program for the first five years.

The Georgia legislature and the state Department of Transportation are working on an EV Infrastructure Deployment Plan that must be submitted no later than August 1, 2022 before the state can access NEVI funds.
Senator Warnock and Senator Ossoff are fighting to make Georgia a top contender for electric vehicle manufacturing jobs and clean energy investments.
source_title: As gas prices rise, Georgia expands its Electric Vehicle market - Georgia Conservation Voters

facts: Rivian Automotive's $5 billion Georgia plant represents a pivotal gamble in the high-stakes race to dominate the electric vehicle market.
The Georgia facility is slated to begin production in 2028.
The facility spans 1,800 acres.
The plant will initially produce 200,000 vehicles annually.
A second phase of the plant could potentially double that capacity.
Rivian's current per-unit costs currently hinder its ability to compete with Tesla and traditional automakers like Ford and General Motors.
The Georgia plant will focus on producing the R2 SUV, which starts at $45,000.
The Georgia plant will also produce the smaller R3 model aimed at broadening appeal beyond its current premium niche.
The state of Georgia has sweetened the deal with a $1.5 billion incentive package tied to creating 7,500 jobs.
Rivian's flagship R1T model is priced at $69,900.
Rivian's flagship R1S model is priced at $75,900.
The R1T and R1S models cater to a luxury audience but limit mass-market adoption.
Analyst Alex Oyler of SBD Automotive argues that scale is the linchpin of EV profitability.
Rivian's projected 200,000-unit annual output from the Georgia plant could allow the company to spread fixed costs and achieve breakeven margins.
Rivian reported a $1.66 billion loss in the first half of 2025.
The financial loss was exacerbated by the removal of federal EV tax credits and rising tariffs that add $2,000 per vehicle.
Rivian's CEO remains confident in the company's ability to compete on product quality and innovation.
Rivian's strategy targets the adventure-oriented EV segment with rugged pickups and SUVs.
The R1T and R1S carved out a unique niche but face competition from models like the Ford F-150 Lightning and Chevrolet Silverado EV.
Rivian held a 3% market share in the first half of 2025.
Tesla holds 45% dominance in the EV market.
Ford and GM are accelerating their EV rollouts, with Ford's Blue Oval City in Tennessee and GM's $7 billion investment in Ohio posing direct threats.
Rivian relies on third-party suppliers for battery cells, unlike Tesla's in-house production.
Risks to the Georgia plant include delays in construction, environmental concerns from local residents, and the high cost of scaling production.
Rivian's stock has plummeted over 80% since its 2021 IPO.
Rivian has a partnership with Volkswagen involving a $5.8 billion investment for technology access.
Rivian received a $6.6 billion loan from the U.S. Department of Energy.
source_title: Rivian's Georgia Plant: A Strategic Inflection Point for EV Market Share and Scalability

facts: Following a $217 million expansion, EV6 joins the EV9 as the plant’s second electric vehicle and fifth overall vehicle currently being assembled in West Point, Georgia.
On Nov. 21, 2024, it was announced at the 2024 Los Angeles Auto Show that the all-electric 2025 EV6 SUV will be assembled by the Kia Georgia team in West Point, Ga., beginning in early 2025.
The EV6 joins the all-electric EV9 SUV and the Telluride, Sorento and Sportage SUVs on the plant’s assembly line.

Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Kia's announcement is a testament to the decades-long partnership between Kia and Georgia, helping make the state the No. 1 state for business and accelerating its development as the electric mobility capital of America.
Governor Brian P. Kemp thanked President Sean Yoon and Stuart Countess, as well as their teams, for the decision and the opportunities it will bring to hardworking Georgians.
During the past year-and-a-half, Kia Georgia has undergone a transformation that allows for the production of EVs and the plant’s traditional products all along the same line.
The $217 million expansion created approximately 200 jobs in Troup County.
The expansion brings the plant’s total employment to more than 3,200 team members.
SeungKyu (Sean) Yoon, President and CEO of Kia North America and Kia America, stated that the award-winning EV6 has been an integral part of Kia’s ‘Plan S’ strategy since day one.
The announcement that the EV6 will be built in Georgia is a testament to Kia’s ongoing commitment to the U.S. market and the state of Georgia.

As the home to previous World Car of the Year winners Telluride and EV9, there is no doubt that the team at Kia Georgia will continue its legacy of successful launches with EV6, according to SeungKyu (Sean) Yoon.
Kia Georgia, Inc. is the first manufacturing site in North America for Kia Corporation based in Seoul, Korea.
With an annual capacity of 350,000 units, Kia Georgia is located on 2,200 acres in West Point, Ga. and began mass production on Nov. 16, 2009.
Kia Georgia is home to the Telluride SUV, Sorento SUV (excludes HEV and PHEV models), Sportage SUV (excludes HEV and PHEV models) and the all-electric EV9 SUV.
The announcement excludes production of the EV6 GT model.
source_title: Kia Georgia Adding EV6 Production to its Lineup - Kia Georgia

facts: In 2019, the U.S. had only two operational EV battery factories, with another two under construction.
As of the article's publication, there are 32 battery factories either planned, under construction, or already operational across the country.
The Inflation Reduction Act (IRA), passed in 2022, provided significant incentives including tax credits for EV buyers and manufacturers to invest in U.S.-based facilities.
Donald Trump's victory in the 2024 elections has introduced uncertainty regarding the future pace of investments and regulatory support for the EV battery sector.
By 2021, many automakers began announcing plans to build EVs and batteries closer to home to avoid supply chain disruptions like those seen during the COVID-19 pandemic.
Over $112 billion in investments have been pledged for new plants and facilities by 2030.
These facilities are expected to produce up to 1,200 gigawatt-hours of battery capacity annually, enough to power about 18 million EVs.
SK Battery America’s Plant in Commerce, Georgia, received a $2.6 billion investment, started production in 2021, and created 2,600 jobs.
Hyundai’s SK On facility in Bartow County, Georgia, has a $5 billion investment with production beginning in 2025, generating 8,100 jobs.
Hyundai’s HMG-LGES Battery JV in Savannah, Georgia, involves a $4.3 billion investment with production starting in 2025 and creating 2,000 jobs.
Key states seeing massive investments in battery manufacturing capacity include Michigan, Georgia, Tennessee, Kentucky, and Indiana.
By 2028, over 1,000 gigawatt-hours of annual battery production capacity are expected to be in operation, enough to power 10 million electric vehicles.
A Financial Times report from August 2024 highlighted that 40% of over 100 clean energy projects, including battery plants, have been delayed.
By 2028, announced EV battery manufacturing projects are expected to support nearly 30,000 new jobs spread across the Southeast, Midwest, and Southwest regions.
EPA projections indicate that electric vehicles accounted for 12% of all U.S. vehicles produced in 2023, marking a 70% increase from the previous year.
source_title: Tracking the EV Battery Factory Boom in U.S. - OCBridge
