The project ties into broader efforts to attract battery, solar, and EV supply-chain companies across the Southeast.
Rivian has chosen a staged approach that involves launching the R2 from its existing Illinois facility first before using Georgia as a future scale-up platform.
A new plant gives Rivian room to build hundreds of thousands of vehicles annually once fully ramped.
The site can be configured around newer vehicle architectures rather than retrofitted around existing lines.
A Georgia location improves Rivian's footprint in the Southeast, which is an increasingly active corridor for EV and battery investment.
Larger planned output can help Rivian negotiate more effectively with suppliers over time.
Hyundai, Kia, Mercedes-Benz, BMW, battery suppliers, and materials companies have expanded across the region, strengthening the local talent pool and supplier network.
The site sits in Georgia's Stanton Springs area, a major industrial corridor with access to interstate routes and regional labor markets.
Before full construction, stages include site development such as grading, drainage, internal roads, and preparation of large building pads across the industrial campus.
Utility buildout will cover electrical capacity, water, wastewater, broadband, and other systems needed for high-volume manufacturing.
Building construction will include a body shop, paint shop, general assembly, logistics areas, offices, and employee facilities.
Manufacturing equipment installation will involve stamping, robotics, conveyance systems, battery-related equipment, and quality-control stations.
Hiring and training phases will cover technicians, engineers, production workers, maintenance teams, and plant leadership.
Validation steps include pilot builds, process testing, safety checks, and supplier readiness before customer vehicles are produced at scale.
The company has previously outlined plans for roughly 7,500 direct jobs at the Georgia complex when fully built out.

Those roles are expected to span vehicle assembly, battery-related operations, paint, stamping, plastics, maintenance, quality control, engineering support, warehouse operations, and plant administration.
The project is planned for Stanton Springs North, near Social Circle and Rutledge, east of Atlanta.
Georgia's incentive package for the Rivian factory has been reported at about $1.5 billion in potential value, combining state and local support.
The incentive structure includes tax abatements, infrastructure assistance, workforce development programs, land-related support, and other project-linked benefits.
The full value of incentives depends on Rivian meeting investment and hiring commitments over time rather than receiving the entire amount upfront.
Georgia's Quick Start program is expected to support recruiting and training for production and technical roles.
Public spending is tied to roads, site preparation, utilities, and access improvements needed for a factory of this size.
Property tax incentives are designed to reduce early project costs while still generating long-term revenue as the site develops.
Job creation and capital investment targets are central to the public support package.
The company has previously described the Georgia project as capable of producing up to 400,000 vehicles per year once fully built out.
The factory is expected to be developed in phases rather than reaching full capacity immediately after opening.
Rivian's existing plant in Normal, Illinois remains central to current production of the R1T pickup, R1S SUV, and commercial delivery vans.
The Georgia site is intended to add the scale needed for broader consumer adoption of next-generation vehicles, including the more affordable R2 platform.
A two-plant strategy separates premium adventure vehicles from more affordable vehicles targeting a wider market.
Rivian has been working to make its Illinois operations more efficient by simplifying vehicle designs, renegotiating supplier costs, and refining assembly processes.
Delaying the heavy construction ramp until 2026 reflects a strategy of prioritizing cash discipline while preparing the R2 launch and improving existing operations.
Tesla, Hyundai, Ford, General Motors, and other automakers are pushing for lower-cost electric models, domestic battery sourcing, and regional manufacturing scale.
source_title: Rivian breaks ground on $5B Georgia factory ahead of constru

entity_name: Rivian Stanton Springs North EV Plant
canonical_name: Rivian Stanton Springs North Manufacturing Campus
facts: Rivian is building a $5 billion manufacturing campus at Stanton Springs North, located east of Atlanta.
The campus spans roughly 9 million square feet across 1,800 acres of greenfield land.
Construction mobilization is scheduled to continue through 2026.
Vehicle production at the facility is targeted for 2028.
Rivian previously paused and then restarted the project to sharpen its capacity plan.
Rivian selected Clayco as the general contractor to build the plant.
Skidmore, Owings & Merrill (SOM) is serving as the design architect for the facility.
Jacobs is acting as the engineer of record.
The facility will be delivered in two phases using a design-build delivery method.
The plant is designed with a production capacity aiming to top 400,000 vehicles per year.
To capture its full state incentive package, Rivian must invest $5 billion and employ 7,500 workers at an average salary of $56,000 by the end of 2028.
Site preparation across the 1,800-acre site is a major earthwork and logistics component that sets the schedule before the first structure goes vertical.
The project is considered one of the largest economic-development projects in Georgia’s history.
The plant's stop-start path makes it a case study in how fragile even mega-incentivized manufacturing can be.
Rivian’s restart signals renewed confidence in U.S. EV demand at a time when the broader market has wobbled.
For the region, the plant is reshaping the local labor market by pulling thousands of skilled trades into the corridor between Atlanta and Athens.
The project reflects a wider reshoring wave concentrating the biggest U.S. construction jobs in advanced manufacturing, where only a handful of contractors can build at this scale.
Rivian serves as both the developer and owner/client for the project.
The current status of the facility is under construction.
Funding for the project comes from private sources.
source_title: Rivian Stanton Springs North EV Plant — Industrial in Social Circle - Exchange

facts: Electric vehicle maker Rivian has pressed pause on the construction of a major manufacturing plant in Stanton Springs, Georgia.
The company selected Clayco and Skidmore, Owings & Merrill to deliver the 1,800-acre complex.
In March, the company announced a pause in construction of the $5 billion facility.
Rivian stated that the plant remains an extremely important part of its strategy to scale production of R2 and R3 car models.
The timing for resuming construction is expected to be later to focus its teams on the capital-efficient launch of R2 in Normal, Illinois.
An email sent from the company in April underscored a commitment to the project.
During the pause period, Rivian anticipates performing some limited site development activities.
The depth and breadth of this work has not yet been determined.
source_title: Rivian puts construction of major Georgia EV plant on hold | News | Archinect

facts: Rivian will host two special events next month to mark the restart of construction of its $5 billion Georgia electric vehicle factory.
A community event is set for Sept. 14.
A formal kickoff ceremony is set for Sept. 16.
The kickoff ceremony will be held “in celebration of our strong partnership with Georgia and the planned resumption of construction in 2026.”
The kickoff ceremony will be attended by stakeholders and government officials, including Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp.
The plant near Social Circle is expected to start production in 2028.
The plant will have an annual capacity of about 200,000 units.
In March 2024, Rivian announced it was stopping construction on the Georgia factory project to save money.
Rivian decided to move production of its upcoming R2 SUV to its plant in Normal, Illinois, to expedite sales.
Rivian got the project back on track after winning final approval for a $6.6 billion Department of Energy loan in January.
Rivian cannot access the DOE loan funds until it resumes work on the Georgia factory.
The DOE loan funds are available through September 2028.
In July, Rivian announced it was opening a new East Coast headquarters in Atlanta later this year.
The new Atlanta headquarters will initially employ 100 people.
The new Atlanta headquarters will ultimately house 500 workers to support its operations in the state.
source_title: Rivian moving forward on $5B Georgia EV plant | Manufacturing Dive

facts: The halted Rivian plant construction near Social Circle, Georgia, has reshuffled EV component sourcing strategies.
Key manufacturing regions now seeing increased demand include China's industrial clusters in Shandong, Henan, and Guangdong provinces.
These areas offer mature EV supply chains with specialized zones for battery systems, power electronics, and chassis components.
Industrial clusters in Chongqing and Zhejiang provide advantages through vertical integration, with suppliers co-located near raw material processors and logistics hubs.
Shandong's automotive corridor has become particularly active, housing multiple Tier-2 suppliers with experience serving U.S. automakers.
The market advantages of these regions include established export protocols, reduced logistics costs through port access, and workforces with EV-specific technical training.
Suppliers in these clusters typically maintain buffer stock and flexible production lines to accommodate sudden supply chain shifts like the Georgia project pause.
Selecting reliable EV component suppliers requires rigorous evaluation of operational capabilities and compliance.
Essential criteria include verifying factory size (minimum 1,000m² recommended), staff count (40+ preferred), and annual revenue data.
Compliance certifications required include IATF 16949 automotive quality management and ISO 14001 environmental certifications.
Supply chain transparency requires tracing raw material origins and sub-supplier qualifications.
Industry standards mandate on-time delivery rates above 95%, response times under 4 hours for technical queries, and documented reorder rates exceeding 25% indicating customer retention.

Quality verification should include third-party factory audits with on-site production assessments, sample testing under automotive-grade stress conditions, historical performance analysis of online revenue trends and client retention metrics, and validation of "Verified Supplier Pro" status on B2B platforms.

Shenzhen IMR Technology Co., Ltd. is located in Guangdong, CN, has 12 years experience, 30+ staff, a 4.6/5.0 review score, ≤1h response time, 98.1% on-time delivery, $3,200,000+ online revenue, and 39% reorder rate.

Xiamen Aecoauto Technology Co., Ltd. is located in Fujian, CN, has 3 years experience, 9+ staff, a 5.0/5.0 review score, ≤1h response time, 100.0% on-time delivery, $2,200,000+ online revenue, and 40% reorder rate.

Henan Dongdong Automobile Service is located in Henan, CN, has 3 years experience, 60+ staff, a 4.9/5.0 review score, ≤3h response time, 100.0% on-time delivery, $1,100,000+ online revenue, and 29% reorder rate.

Zhongwu New Energy (Shandong) Group is located in Shandong, CN, has 3 years experience, 10+ staff, a 4.9/5.0 review score, ≤4h response time, 100.0% on-time delivery, $470,000+ online revenue, and 43% reorder rate.

Shan County Super Rich New Energy is located in Shandong, CN, has 2 years experience, 50+ staff, a 4.9/5.0 review score, ≤2h response time, 100.0% on-time delivery, $450,000+ online revenue, and 32% reorder rate.

Qingdao Uniland Import And Export is located in Shandong, CN, has 5 years experience, 20+ staff, a 4.6/5.0 review score, ≤3h response time, 100.0% on-time delivery, $350,000+ online revenue, and 19% reorder rate.

Chongqing Fengdao Auto Parts is located in Chongqing, CN, has 2 years experience, 6+ staff, a 5.0/5.0 review score, ≤2h response time, 93.8% on-time delivery, $170,000+ online revenue, and 25% reorder rate.
Analysis notes Shenzhen IMR Technology leads with 12 years' experience and strong revenue ($3.2M+), while Xiamen Aecoauto shows exceptional responsiveness (≤1h) and perfect quality ratings.
Zhongwu New Energy demonstrates the highest reorder rate (43%), indicating strong client retention.
For high-volume needs, Henan Dongdong's 60+ staff and $1.1M+ revenue provide scalability.
Response times emerge as a key differentiator, with top performers answering queries within 1-2 hours.
Clayco was selected as the general contractor for Rivian's $5 billion plant near Social Circle, GA.
The project initially launched in early 2024 but was paused when Rivian shifted focus to its Illinois facility.
Rivian secured a $6.5 billion loan from the Department of Energy to restart its Georgia plant construction after halting operations earlier this year.
Rivian is establishing its second U.S. manufacturing campus in Georgia, investing $5 billion in a carbon-conscious facility announced by Governor Brian Kemp in December 2021.
Most established suppliers accommodate factory visits with advance scheduling.
Manufacturers like Shenzhen IMR Technology (1,500m² facility) typically provide full production line access.
Virtual tours are increasingly available for international buyers.
source_title: EV Supplier Halts Georgia Plant Construction: Find Alternatives Now

facts: Uber pledged to invest more than £5 million ($6.5 million) in public electric vehicle charging infrastructure in London to persuade drivers to switch to electric cars.

Jamie Heywood, Uber’s regional general manager for northern and eastern Europe, stated that reliable, accessible charging near where drivers live is a key factor in deciding whether to switch to electric vehicles.
Addressing charging challenges for professional drivers now will help create a mass market for electric vehicles in the years to come, which is critical for the UK to achieve its net-zero goal.
The investment funds will be directed towards some of the poorest boroughs in London, which currently have only a fraction of the electric charge points found in wealthier parts of the city.
Uber acknowledges it will need to invest additional money to create sufficient charging infrastructure to persuade more drivers to go fully electric.
Uber has a fund of more than £100m raised through its self-imposed clean air fee in London, which has charged passengers 15p (20c) per mile since 2018.
The company aims to have all 45,000 cars on its app in London be electric by 2025.
Currently, only about 1,000 Uber vehicles in London are fully electric.
source_title: Uber set to invest millions in EV charging points in London | The Optimist Daily: Making Solutions the News

facts: The facility is located in Suwanee, GA, USA.
The facility manufactures innovative, industrial-grade powering solutions through a diverse product line of large-scale battery systems, chargers and uninterruptible power supplies.
Applications for its products include power generation facilities, substations, electrical utilities, transportation systems, communication networks and other enterprises with unique powering needs.
The facility was established in 2001.
The facility occupies 150,000 square feet of space.
The facility employs 200 people.
The facility provides critical backup power solutions for customers in challenging conditions.
The facility holds ISO 9001:2015 certification.
The facility holds ISO 14001:2015 certification.
source_title: EnerSys Suwanee GA | Industrial Battery Excellence

facts: Ozburn Electrical is a family-owned electrical company founded by Harold T. Ozburn in 1984.
The company has operated as a licensed electrician and electrical contractor in Madison, Georgia for over 30 years.
Ozburn Electrical provides commercial and industrial electrical services across Madison, Greensboro, Morgan County, and metro Atlanta, Georgia.
The firm offers EV Charging Stations including Level 2 EV Chargers and Fast DC EV Charging.
Ozburn Electrical provides Electric Vehicle (EV) Charger Installation including Level 2 and Level 3 Fast DC Charging for industrial, commercial, manufacturing, and retail facilities.
All electrical installations and work comply with the NEC National Electrical Code, State, County, Municipality, and Electrical Power Provider Guidelines.
The company's electricians complete a 2-year or 4-year electrical apprenticeship program established by the Independent Electrical Contractors Association (IEC), of which Ozburn is an active member.
source_title: Electrician in Madison GA | Ozburn Electrical Contractors

facts: Honda honored 52 North American suppliers during its annual Honda Supplier Conference in Columbus, Ohio, with awards for outstanding performance in supplying parts and materials in 2024.
During the two-day conference, Honda recognized 33 suppliers for supporting mass production and 19 suppliers for service parts serving Honda and Acura customers.
Honda purchased approximately $32.5 billion in parts for mass production operations from North American suppliers in 2024.
The 2024 mass production purchases supported the sales of more than 1.6 million Honda and Acura vehicles in North America.
Honda also purchased more than $1.96 billion in parts from its Service Parts suppliers in North America.

Mike Lapham, vice president of Procurement in the Purchasing & Supply Chain Center at Honda Development & Manufacturing of America, LLC, stated that the automotive industry is facing a challenging time and a once in a hundred-year period of transformation.
Lapham noted that Honda is pursuing its Second Founding to increase flexibility and agility to strike the right balance of internal combustion engine, hybrid and EV models to meet customer needs.

Honda does business with approximately 700 OEM suppliers across North America which provide parts to the company’s 13 plants in the region that produce Honda and Acura cars and light trucks and the engines and transmissions that power them.
The Piolax Corporation, located in Canton, Georgia, was recognized by Honda as a 2024 Mass Production Supplier Award Winner in the Excellence in Value category.
Honda honored 19 service parts suppliers with awards reflecting combined performance in quality, cost, and on-time delivery.

Jessica Hammontree, assistant vice president of the Parts Division at American Honda Motor Co., Inc., stated that despite market uncertainty and supply challenges, service parts suppliers enabled improved service levels.
Hammontree noted that Honda ranked number one in the industry for service parts availability for eight consecutive months.
Honda works with nearly 1,600 service parts suppliers in North America to provide service and accessory parts for more than 26 million Honda and Acura automobiles in operation in the United States.
Honda manages a national network of parts and distribution facilities that support customers by delivering high-quality service parts to Honda and Acura dealers nationwide.
Honda established operations in North America in 1959 and currently employs about 42,000 associates in the region engaged in development, manufacturing, sales and service support.
Honda operates 18 major manufacturing plants in North America and works with over 750 suppliers in the region to produce a diverse range of quality products.
In 2024, 99.6% of all Honda and Acura automobiles sold in the U.S. were produced in North America.
Honda conducts research and development activities at 23 facilities in North America where it fully designs, develops and engineers many of its North American-made products.
source_title: Honda Honors 52 Suppliers in North America for Outstanding Performance

facts: Ozburn Electrical is a family-owned electrical company founded by Harold T. Ozburn in 1984.
Ozburn Electric has been a licensed electrician and electrical contractors in Monroe, GA for over 30 years.
The company provides commercial and industrial electrical services with a 1-year warranty across Monroe, Georgia and surrounding cities.
Ozburn Electrical also serves Lawrenceville, Georgia.
The firm offers 24/7 emergency electrical services for commercial or industrial work in Monroe, GA.
Every electrician goes through interviews and tests including full character background checks, motor vehicle checks, drug, IQ and trade tests before joining the company.
Ozburn's Monroe, GA electricians are certified by completing a 2-year or 4-year electrical apprenticeship program established by the Independent Electrical Contractors Association (IEC).
The company serves as an active member of the Independent Electrical Contractors Association (IEC).
Ozburn Electrical provides industrial and commercial electrical services in Walton County, GA and all of metro Atlanta.
The company offers EV Charging Stations including Level 2 EV Chargers and Fast DC EV Charging.
Ozburn Electric provides Electric Vehicle (EV) Charger Installation services.
All electrical work is performed to the National Electrical Code (NEC), State, County, Municipality and Electrical Power Provider Guidelines.
The company's fully stocked trucks are ready to service industrial and commercial electrical work that same day if needed.

facts: A multi-billion dollar battery factory is under construction in Commerce, Georgia (North Georgia).
The US International Trade Commission (USITC) ruled that SK Battery must be punished for using trade secrets from a rival battery manufacturer.
LG Chem (now called LG Energy Solutions) accused SK Battery of hiring away its employees to steal lithium-ion battery technology.
SK Battery is building a $2.6 billion factory in Jackson County, Georgia.
The Jackson County development is expected to eventually generate 2600 jobs.
The state of Georgia agreed to provide $300 million in tax incentives for the project.
The plant was scheduled to open and become fully operational in 2022.
The facility covers 2.4M square feet along Interstate 85 in Jackson County.
The USITC ordered a 10-year importation ban on SK Battery for all lithium-ion batteries, battery cells, battery modules, and battery packs.
Many of the banned components are required to build electric vehicle batteries at the Jackson County facility.
John Hyun Jim, CEO of LG Energy Solutions, stated that SKI's total disregard of warnings and intellectual property rights gave them no choice but to file the case.
LG Energy Solutions expressed gratitude to the International Trade Commission for protecting its innovations and significant economic investments in the United States.
LG Energy Solutions' North American headquarters is located in Atlanta.
SK Battery had already signed deals to provide batteries for the electric Ford F-150 and a Volkswagen SUV being built in Chattanooga, Tennessee.

The ruling allows SK Battery a four-year window to manufacture batteries for Ford and a two-year window for Volkswagen, giving both automakers time to transition to new domestic suppliers of batteries.
The USITC originally ruled against SK Battery in February 2020 after an administrative law judge reviewed deleted emails discovered on SK's computers.
Deleted emails instructed SK employees to remove all materials related to the rival company and delete specific directives after completion.
State and local leaders were placed in an awkward position lobbying the full USITC for leniency toward SK Battery despite evidence of intentional non-compliance.
Parent company SK Innovation stated it would immediately enter into discussions with customers Ford Motor Company and Volkswagen regarding the ITC's decision.
SK Innovation expressed serious concerns about the commercial and operational implications for its EV-battery facility in Commerce, Georgia, which is expected to employ 2600 people upon completion.
SK Innovation believed the ITC ruling could seriously adversely impact President Biden's policies to combat climate change and expand US auto fleet electrification.
SK Innovation expressed a desire to have detailed discussions with Biden administration officials reviewing the ITC ruling and implementing EV and environmental policies.
source_title: Jackson County battery plant punished in trade secret controversy | FOX 5 Atlanta

facts: Club Car is headquartered in Augusta, Georgia.
Club Car delivered 443 vehicles to Bethpage Black for the 2025 Ryder Cup.
The fleet includes seven custom golf cars crafted exclusively for U.S. Captain Keegan Bradley, European Captain Luke Donald, and their vice-captains.
Fred Palmer serves as Vice President of Golf Business Development at Club Car.
Club Car has served as a Worldwide Supplier to the Ryder Cup since 2022, with a partnership dating back to 1991.
Club Car has partnered with the PGA of America since 1990.
Club Car's product portfolio includes vehicles for commercial, personal, and street-legal low-speed use.
The Club Car Vehicle Group includes the luxury electric vehicle brand Garia and the lightweight electric utility vehicle brand Melex.
Club Car has over six decades of history in superior design and performance.
source_title: Club Car Delivers Nearly 500 Vehicles for Ryder Cup 2025 at Bethpage Black

facts: Orbia’s Fluorinated Solutions business and Solvay signed a joint venture agreement on November 2, 2023.
The joint venture aims to create the largest polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF) production facilities for battery materials in North America.
The partnership provides security of supply for Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) compliant materials.
The partnership combines IRA-compliant materials with leading-edge PVDF technology for solutions critical to the North American EV transition.
The joint venture addresses considerable growth in demand for lithium-ion batteries driven by the U.S. EV and stationary energy storage market.
The partnership aims to establish a robust, secure, and local supply chain for lithium-ion batteries.
The partnership secures the supply of critical minerals and intermediate materials from Orbia.
Solvay will manufacture suspension-grade PVDF using Orbia's supplied materials.
Suspension-grade PVDF serves as a lithium-ion binder and separator coating in electric vehicle batteries.
Solvay contributes process technology and global market know-how to the joint venture.
The joint venture combines Solvay’s Solef® PVDF innovations with Orbia’s material assets and production expertise.
The delivered PVDF will allow electric vehicles to travel further on each charge.
The delivered PVDF will extend battery life.
The delivered PVDF will improve battery safety.
Ilham Kadri, CEO of Solvay, stated the project is a key milestone in Solvay's electrification strategy and emphasizes its global commitment to sustainable mobility.
Ilham Kadri stated the partnership positions Solvay to shore up an independent, sustainable EV battery supply chain in North America.
The project aims to create clean energy jobs.
Sameer Bharadwaj, CEO of Orbia, stated the partnership underscores Orbia's commitment to enabling the clean energy transition through investments in energy materials.
Sameer Bharadwaj stated Orbia has a unique 'mine-to-market' position with integration in key battery materials.
These integrated materials are needed to bolster North America's EV supply chain and ensure IRA compliance.
The joint venture will utilize two production sites.
One site will be located in Augusta, Georgia, for finished products.
The other site will be located in St. Gabriel, Louisiana, for raw material conversion to needed intermediates.
Both production plants are expected to become operational in 2026.
Orbia's participation in the joint venture reinforces its strategic position as a key supplier of battery materials.
Orbia supplies battery materials including electrolyte salts such as LiPF6.
Orbia supplies custom electrolyte formulations.
Orbia supplies specialty battery additives.
Orbia supplies recycled anodes.
source_title: Orbia and Solvay Finalize Joint Venture Agreement to Build PVDF Production Capacity in North America for Lithium-ion Batteries  | Orbia

56% of the ports are level 2 charging ports.
71 total Level 2 stations exist in the area.
44% of the ports are Level 3 stations.
55 total Level 3 stations exist in the area.

facts: Qcells has begun manufacturing solar cells at its new facility in Cartersville, Georgia.
The company describes the Cartersville site as the United States’ first and only fully vertically integrated solar manufacturing factory.
All production lines at the Cartersville plant are expected to reach full capacity by the third quarter of 2026.
Once fully operational, the facility will manufacture ingots, wafers, cells, and solar modules under one roof.
The start of cell production marks a significant milestone for domestic solar manufacturing, as most solar panels installed in the US still rely on imported components.
Qcells stated that the Cartersville site will become the largest operating solar cell factory in US history.
Module assembly operations at the facility are already running at full capacity.
The facility produces about 16,700 solar panels every day.
When fully ramped up, the Cartersville factory will produce 3.3 gigawatts (GW) of annual capacity each for ingots, wafers, and cells.
When fully ramped up, the Cartersville factory will produce 3.5 GW of solar modules annually.
Combined with Qcells’ expanded manufacturing facility in Dalton, Georgia, the company’s total US module production capacity is expected to reach 8.6 GW annually by the end of the third quarter.
That combined output is enough to generate the equivalent annual electricity needs of roughly 1.3 million American homes.
Andy Park, Global CEO of Qcells, said: “Producing the first solar cells at Cartersville is a milestone for Qcells and for American manufacturing.”
Producing key solar components domestically could provide customers with greater certainty around supply availability, pricing, and potential tariff-related disruptions.
The Cartersville operation could help project developers qualify for the federal Domestic Content Bonus tied to the Investment Tax Credit.
Because major solar module components are produced in the US, developers may find it easier to meet domestic sourcing requirements.

Qcells’ vertically integrated manufacturing approach allows it to claim production incentives under Section 45X of the Advanced Manufacturing Production Tax Credit across multiple stages of the solar supply chain, including ingots, wafers, cells, and modules.
The investment is expected to support thousands of manufacturing jobs in Georgia.
Together, the Cartersville and Dalton facilities are projected to employ nearly 4,000 workers.
This includes about 3,800 direct jobs across Bartow and Whitfield counties.
The Cartersville factory is the first US facility of its kind built in more than a decade.
The Cartersville factory includes what Qcells describes as the largest ingot and wafer production plant ever constructed in the country.
source_title: US' first vertically integrated solar factory begins cell production

facts: LG Energy Solution Vertech and Qcells announced a multi-year commitment to install reliable, affordable, American-made storage products for Qcells’ development projects across the country.
The agreement includes 5GWh of lithium-ion energy storage projects for utility-scale projects, as well as lifecycle services from LG Energy Solution Vertech.
This agreement supports Qcells EPC projects scheduled from 2028 – 2030.
In May 2024, Qcells entered into battery purchase agreements with LG Energy Solution Vertech totaling 4.8 GWh of BESS projects developed across the U.S.
The partnership secures energy storage systems for Qcells that meet domestic content requirements.
LG Energy Solution’s battery energy storage products are made in Michigan.
Qcells’ solar panels are made in Georgia.
Leveraging made-in-America products supports U.S. manufacturing jobs, strengthens the nation’s clean-energy supply chains, enhances grid reliability, and reduces exposure to trade risks and tariffs.
Chris Hodrick serves as CEO of Qcells EPC.
Qcells operates the largest solar panel manufacturing facility of its kind in the United States, based in Dalton, Georgia.
The company is building a second factory in Cartersville, Georgia, where it will manufacture ingots, wafers, cells and finished panels.
This second factory represents the largest investment in U.S. solar history to create an integrated, sustainable domestic supply chain.
Once fully operational, Qcells’ annual solar panel production capacity in the US will reach 8.4 gigawatts.
This capacity equals nearly 46,000 panels every day.
The output is enough to power more than 1.3 million American homes each year.
Jaehong Park serves as CEO and President at LG Energy Solution Vertech.
The energy storage systems included in this commitment are built using the latest LG Energy Solution containerized lithium-ion battery solutions with LFP cells manufactured at U.S. facilities.
LG Energy Solution is manufacturing up to 16.5 GWh of battery energy storage cells in Holland, MI.
Production at LG Energy Solution sites across North America is set to ramp up to around 50GWh by the end of 2026.
LG Energy Solution Vertech is an energy storage systems integrator backed by LG Energy Solution.
In 2022, LG Energy Solution purchased NEC Energy Solutions and established LG Energy Solution Vertech Inc.

Qcells EPC provides complete utility-scale turnkey solutions through the entire project lifecycle from modules, solar and energy storage project development, and Engineering Procurement and Construction (EPC) services to the US utility-scale market.
Qcells USA has a track record that includes 8.4 GW of sustainably produced module manufacturing capabilities in Georgia and nearly 2 GW of developed, constructed, and operating projects.
Qcells EPC is a flagship company of Hanwha Group, a FORTUNE Global 500 firm and a Top 7 business enterprise in South Korea.
source_title: LG Energy Solution Vertech and Qcells partner on 5GWh of US ESS

facts: There are 73 EV charging stations in Chattanooga, Tennessee.
The stations contain a total of 206 charging ports.
All chargers were verified as working this week.
There are 10 working DC fast chargers in the city.
There are 146 Level 2 ports distributed across the ChargePoint Network, Blink Network, and Non-Networked systems.
EV charging stations are located at Hamilton Place Mall (2100 Hamilton Place Boulevard), North Shore (301 Manufacturers Rd), and River City Company Riverfront Parkway Lot (190 Riverfront Pkwy).

Additional station locations include Chattanooga Airport (1001 Airport Road), Alternative Vehicles Test Facility on TVA Service Rd, Chattanooga Plant (8001 Volkswagen Dr), Finley Stadium (1835 Reggie White Blvd), and Chickamauga Service Center (4601 N Access Rd).
The page provides wholesale day-ahead EV charging prices for Chattanooga, refreshed daily via the open-source gridstatus library.
Infrastructure grade is calculated based on the DC fast charger ratio.
The grading scale for DC fast charger ratios is A (40%+), B (30–39%), C (20–29%), D (10–19%), and F (under 10%).
Density metrics data status is current as of July 5, 2026.
Data is sourced from the U.S. DOE Alternative Fuels Station Locator (AFDC).
As of July 2026, Chattanooga has 73 publicly accessible EV charging stations with 206 charging ports.
ChargePoint Network operates 53.4% of the stations in the area.
Blink Network operates 13.7% of the stations and is part of Tennessee's total of 1,241 stations statewide.
29% of ports (60) are DC fast chargers capable of adding over 100 miles of range in under 30 minutes.
71% of ports (146) are Level 2 chargers suited for longer stops.
Available charging connector types include CCS, Tesla (NACS), and CHAdeMO.
There are 12 different charging networks available in Chattanooga, providing excellent flexibility for EV drivers.
Nearby charging locations include Calhoun and Dalton in Georgia; Adairsville and Rome in Georgia; and McMinnville, Cleveland, Manchester, Ooltewah, Tullahoma, and Athens in Tennessee.
Data is sourced from the US DOE Alternative Fuels Station Locator (AFDC), maintained by NREL.
Data was last synced on July 5, 2026.
There are 3 hotels in Chattanooga with on-site EV charging, comprising 3 Tesla Destination chargers and 10 Level 2 ports.

On-site hotel charging is available at DoubleTree by Hilton Chattanooga Downtown (407 Chestnut St), Hampton Inn & Suites Chattanooga/Downtown (400 Chestnut St), Holiday Inn & Suites Chattanooga Downtown (434 Chestnut St), and Hilton Garden Inn Chattanooga Downtown (311 Chestnut St).
Tesla Destination chargers at the listed hotels provide a full charge in 6–10 hours overnight.

A spokesperson stated, "We are putting in charging stations where people are, where people are running errands. They're going grocery shopping, they go to work. That's where we put our charging stations."
source_title: 73 EV Charging Stations in Chattanooga, TN (2026)

facts: Tonya Hicks serves as the President and CEO of Power Solutions, Inc.
Power Solutions, Inc. was founded in 2000 by Tonya Hicks at age 28.
The company specializes in electrical contracting, facility maintenance, renewable energy, electrification, and EV charging solutions.
Power Solutions has expanded its operations internationally, with its success featured in Forbes Japan in November 2019.
Tonya Hicks was the first woman to become a Journeyman Inside Wireman Electrician in the International Brotherhood of Electrical Workers (IBEW), Local 917.
Tonya Hicks was the first Black Woman to become an IBEW Journeyman Electrician in the State of Mississippi.
During the pandemic, Tonya Hicks founded she-EV, Inc. to provide safer EV charging locations for women, elderly, and disabled persons.
The she-EV Car Club is scheduled to launch in 2024 as the first of its kind, providing community support, educational resources, and trusted products.
Tonya Hicks launched Women Do Everything LLC in 2015 to promote diversity and inclusion by training and supporting women in male-dominated industries through employment and entrepreneurship.
Tonya Hicks participated in the Leadership Atlanta Class of 2022.
Tonya Hicks is recognized as a Sustainability Ambassador for the City of Atlanta.
Tonya Hicks was named Minority Contractor of the Year by the National Association of Minority Contractors (NAMC) – Atlanta Chapter in 2021.
Tonya Hicks was included in Project Drawdown’s Drawdown Neighborhood Series for Atlanta, Ga., due to her expertise in climate change.
Tonya Hicks was selected to participate in the Leadership America program by Leadership Women in 2023.
Tonya Hicks was honored as one of the 2023 Top 100 Women in Construction by Top 100 Women.
