facts: Duracell batteries is the #1 trusted battery brand that offers Quantum and High Density Core, Duralock, Powercheck and Ion Core technologies.
The company's 309,000 sq. ft. facility in LaGrange uses high-speed, state of the art, advanced manufacturing processes that include combining electro-chemistry and mechanical functions.
The LaGrange facility produces all of the AA and AAA batteries for Duracell in North America.
The facility produces 1000s of batteries per hour.
The facility employs over 300 associates.
A recent construction development includes an 8 story expansion that is now the tallest building in LaGrange.
source_title: Duracell Plant Tour - LaGrange -  Georgia Manufacturing Alliance

facts: Georgia Undergrounding provides LaGrange, GA with underground utility and infrastructure construction services via tunnel construction services, horizontal auger boring and (HDD) horizontal directional drilling more commonly called directional boring.

The company specializes in the underground boring of utility pipelines for satellite, cable, TV, Internet, wi-fi, fiber optic, security, electric power, solar, wind, telephone, oil, gas, petroleum, steam, sewer, drain, water and irrigation crossings.

LaGrange, GA directional boring experts excavate an opening beneath the ground without disturbing the surface to bore underground pipes and conduit for utility lines of all types of lengths and diameters whether large or small.

The company has over 25 years of directional drilling experience and has bored and installed utility pipelines under trees, under city roads, under bridges, under railway tracks and even under rivers.
Most directional boring machines use drilling fluid while a few machines are designed to use either air or air and foam for drilling in rock.
Directional drilling fluid is generally a mixture of bentonite clay and water, with additives used to improve borehole performance.
Drilling fluid (mud) is classified as non-toxic and can be disposed of accordingly.
Upon reaching the exit point, the drill bit is detached and the end of the drill pipe is attached to a back reamer or hole opener to enlarge the borehole with consecutive passes.

When the borehole is between 25% - 50% larger than the pipe to be installed, the utility product pipe is pulled in while rotating the drill pipe and reamer using a swivel to prevent rotation of the pipe.
For some power, cable and telecommunications projects, the drill pipe itself becomes the conduit and is left in the ground upon reaching the exit point, known as “drill and leave”.

Directional boring is used for utility installation due to lower cost, directional capabilities, no access pit required, shorter project completion times, less traffic disruption, deeper product pipe installation possible, longer product pipe length installation possible, and being safer for the environment.

Underground boring preserves the integrity of existing landscape, allows curved or straight lines to avoid underground obstacles, is quicker than open cut excavation requiring fewer workers, and can drill underneath roads, buildings and waterways.

The company has bored telecommunications & power cable conduits, water lines, sewer lines, gas lines, oil lines, product pipelines and environmental remediation casings from 2” to 48” in size via directional drilling under city, county and state roads.
Additional methods for boring underneath roads include slick bore, dry bore, jack and bore, hammering, bentonite boring, rotary drilling, wet boring, mud jacking, slurry boring, and rotary boring.

Services in LaGrange, GA are categorized under NAICS Code 238910 (Boring for building construction), NAICS Code 237990 (Horizontal Directional Drilling), and various CSI Codes for trenchless utility installation and tunnel excavation.
source_title: LaGrange, Georgia Undergrounding - Georgia Undergrounding

facts: In May 2022, Hyundai Motor Group entered into an agreement with the State of Georgia to build its first dedicated fully electrified vehicle and battery manufacturing facilities in the United States.

HMG selected a 2,900-acre megasite in Bryan County for the project due to the area's quality of life, regional connectivity, global logistical connections and infrastructure, and existing workforce training programs.
In October 2022, HMG broke ground on the Metaplant, its first state-of-the-art U.S. smart factory.
Production at the Metaplant began in October 2024.
Once fully operational, more than 8,500 people will work on the megasite.
An additional 6,900 jobs will be created at offsite suppliers.
The economic development agreement totals more than $7.5 billion, making it the largest in Georgia state history.
The first Hyundai IONIQ 5 rolled off the production line at HMGMA in October 2024.
In March 2025, production expanded with the launch of the Hyundai IONIQ 9.
In June 2026, HMGMA launched the Kia Sportage Hybrid, marking the plant's first Kia model and first hybrid electric vehicle (HEV).
The facility is designed to accommodate a wide range of vehicle models and propulsion systems with minimal modifications.
The facility has the capacity to produce up to 10 different vehicles.
The Metaplant will ultimately manufacture vehicles for all three Hyundai Motor Group brands: Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis.
The megasite campus includes HLBMA, a joint venture battery manufacturing plant.
Five key affiliates on the campus are Hyundai Mobis, Hyundai Glovis, Hyundai Transys, Hyundai Steel, and Hyundai Material.
Another 22 suppliers operate nearby, creating an integrated network supporting vehicle production from battery manufacturing to final assembly.
A solar panel-covered parking area at the front of the campus generates approximately five percent of HMGMA’s electricity needs.
Inside the plant, robots and Automated Guided Vehicles (AGVs) work alongside employees known as Meta Pros.
Robots handle heavy lifting, while AGVs deliver parts throughout the facility to improve safety and efficiency.
Every stage of the manufacturing process, from order collection and procurement to logistics and production, is optimized using artificial intelligence and data-driven systems.
Every vehicle undergoes rigorous testing on HMGMA’s nearly two-mile test track before shipment.
HMGMA utilizes XCIENT heavy-duty hydrogen fuel-cell electric trucks to transport vehicle parts from regional suppliers daily.
Once complete, most vehicles are shipped to customers by rail, connecting the Metaplant to markets across the United States.
source_title: About Us - HMGMA

facts: Hyundai opened its Georgia “Metaplant” in 2025.
The company almost immediately started expanding the plant after opening it.
The $7.6 billion vehicle factory is located near Ellabell, Georgia.
The factory was originally designed to produce 300,000 electric cars per year.
The annual production target has been increased to 500,000 vehicles, which will be a mix of EVs and hybrids.
Expansion work to reach the new target is underway alongside live production.
Including the plant and two battery joint ventures with LG Energy Solution and SK On, Hyundai’s total investment in Georgia reaches $12.6 billion.
This represents the largest investment in the state’s history.
The plant is planned to create 8,500 jobs by 2031.
The IONIQ 9 three-row SUV is currently being produced at the plant.
Kia and some Genesis models are scheduled to join production in 2026.
A $4.3 billion battery cell joint venture plant with LG Energy Solution is scheduled to open in the first half of 2026.
The opening of the LG battery plant was delayed due to a federal immigration raid at the site.
The facility is designed as an integrated campus with stamping, body, paint, assembly, and battery operations located on or beside one site.
Federal immigration enforcement actions have stalled the battery plant’s schedule, placing the project at the center of current labor and immigration debates.
The developer and owner of the project is Hyundai Motor Group.
The current status of the plant is under construction.
The project is funded privately.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America — Industrial in Ellabell - Exchange

facts: Hyundai Motor Company is aware of the immigration enforcement action that took place at the construction site of its supplier, the HL-GA Battery Company in Bryan County, Georgia.
Hyundai is closely monitoring the situation while working to understand the specific circumstances.
Based on current understanding, none of those detained are directly employed by Hyundai Motor Company.
Hyundai is committed to full compliance with all laws and regulations in every market where it operates.
This commitment includes adherence to employment verification requirements and immigration laws.
Hyundai expects the same commitment from all partners, suppliers, contractors, and subcontractors.
The safety and well-being of everyone working at Hyundai sites, whether directly employed or working for suppliers, is a top priority.
Hyundai takes its responsibility as a corporate citizen seriously regarding incidents like this.
Incidents like this highlight the importance of robust oversight throughout the entire supply chain and contractor network.
Hyundai is reviewing its processes to ensure that all parties working on its projects maintain the same high standards of legal compliance demanded of itself.
This review includes thorough vetting of employment practices by contractors and subcontractors.
North America Chief Manufacturing Officer Chris Susock will now assume governance of the entire megasite in Georgia.
Hyundai will conduct an investigation to ensure all suppliers and their subcontractors comply with all laws and regulations.
Hyundai has zero tolerance for those who don’t follow the law.
Hyundai continues to invest in American manufacturing and create thousands of jobs.
These investments and job creations will be done in full accordance with U.S. law.
The approach reflects values of treating all people with dignity and respect.
source_title: Hyundai gives official statement after ICE raid

facts: Hyundai officially opened its new electric vehicle plant in Georgia on Wednesday.
The opening marks a major milestone in Hyundai's $7.6 billion investment in the state.
The facility is named Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
HMGMA will produce EVs, including the upcoming Ioniq 9, which is the company’s first three-row electric SUV.
Construction on the plant began less than two years ago.
The plant is expected to create 8,500 direct jobs by 2030.
Hyundai’s broader investment in Georgia, which includes a battery plant with SK On and 18 supplier facilities, has attracted more than $12.6 billion.
This broader Georgia investment is projected to generate over 58,200 jobs, according to the Center for Automotive Research.
Hyundai Motor Group Executive Chair Euisun Chung stated during the grand opening: “Together with the talented workforce at HMGMA, we are building the future of mobility with America, in America.”
The grand opening ceremony was attended by Georgia Governor Brian Kemp, Hyundai CEO Jose Muñoz, and Kia CEO Ho Sung Song.
The facility will manufacture EVs and hybrids for Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis brands.
Production at the plant had already started in October when the first 2025 Hyundai Ioniq 5 rolled off the assembly line.
The updated 2025 Hyundai Ioniq 5 features an extended range of up to 318 miles, refreshed styling, and a NACS charging port for Tesla Supercharger compatibility.
The next model to launch is the Ioniq 9, a larger three-row electric SUV expected to launch this spring.
The Ioniq 9 will feature a 110.3 kWh battery and deliver an estimated range of up to 335 miles.
The plant’s opening coincides with Hyundai’s broader $21 billion investment in the U.S., which was announced earlier this week.
Of the $21 billion, Hyundai plans to use $9 billion to increase production across Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis to 1.2 million vehicles.
Hyundai will allocate $6 billion of the broader investment to build a new steel plant in Louisiana.
During a press conference on Wednesday, Georgia Senator Jon Ossoff warned that potential policy changes could impact the EV industry.
Ossoff stated: “The Trump Administration’s war on electric vehicles poses a threat to EV sales, to the EV market, and to the automobile industry in Georgia and across the country.”
Ossoff cautioned that eliminating federal tax credits would put the U.S. at a disadvantage compared to China and other global competitors in the auto and technology sectors.
source_title: Hyundai Opens Georgia EV Plant, Gearing Up for Ioniq 9 Production

facts: Hyundai is constructing a $7.6 billion electric vehicle plant in Georgia that has cleared an environmental review concerning its water usage.
In August, the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers agreed to revisit Hyundai Motor Group Megaplant America’s (HMGMA) permit standards regarding water usage after a conservation group threatened to sue.
Last August, the Ogeechee Riverkeeper group pushed back against Hyundai’s permit, citing risks to the region’s drinking water from the plant’s 6.6 million daily gallons usage.
After reviewing Hyundai’s water usage for months, the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers concluded that HMGMA’s permit required no changes.
The Army Corps' memo stated that the impacts on the environment as a result of the permit were evaluated and found to be insignificant.
The memo noted a "long-term minor effect" on the water supply, marking a shift from the "negligible" label used in 2022.
Ogeechee Riverkeeper's legal director, Ben Kirsch, responded to the agency's findings regarding the permit decision.

Ben Kirsch stated that they do not yet know what ramifications will come from the decision and cannot offer substantive comments until reviewing the decision document, but they will continue to monitor actions around the permit closely.
Hyundai’s Georgia plant has experienced wastewater issues since it started operations.
The City of Savannah issued a permit to accept HMGMA’s wastewater on August 30, 2024.
The City of Savannah's water treatment facility stopped accepting the EV plant's wastewater by September 2024.
The city noted that the wastewater was affecting biological processes and clarified that it did not stop taking the Hyundai factory’s wastewater due to Hurricane Helene.
Hyundai received a Notice of Violation in October regarding the wastewater issue.
The City of Savannah is working with Georgia’s EPD and Bryan County to resolve the wastewater issue.
The environmental review clearance keeps Hyundai’s Georgia plant on track for its planned timeline.

Despite HMGMA’s water usage issues and wastewater hiccups, the Army Corps ruling signals confidence in the project’s environmental footprint, though local oversight persists amid water supply concerns.
source_title: Hyundai’s Georgia EV plant clears environmental review

facts: The Hyundai Metaplant is home to the award-winning, all electric IONIQ 5 crossover SUV.
HMGMA produces all IONIQ 5 models, with the exception of the N model, for the U.S. and Canadian markets.
The Metaplant began production of the IONIQ 5 in October 2024 when a white, 2025 SEL model rolled off the assembly line.
A private celebration was held for employees who signed the car.
It is housed at the Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia located on the megasite.
In March 2025, the all-electric 2026 IONIQ 9 became Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America’s second production vehicle.
The three-row SUV is often praised for its technology, design, comfort, performance and safety.
In October 2025, the Insurance Institute for Highway Safety (IIHS) awarded the 2026 IONIQ 9 with its highly coveted TOP SAFETY PICK+ prize.

In June 2026, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) reached three significant milestones with the launch of its first Kia model, its first hybrid electric vehicle (HEV), and the addition of a third vehicle to its production lineup.

The Kia Sportage Hybrid now joins the award-winning Hyundai IONIQ 5 and IONIQ 9, showcasing Hyundai Motor Group’s ability to build multiple brands and powertrain technologies on a single production line.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America produces the IONIQ 5, IONIQ 9 and Kia Sportage Hybrid models for the United States and Canadian markets.
HMGMA has the ability to produce up to 10 different vehicles.
As we ramp up to full production, we will announce new additions to our lineup.
source_title: Our Products - HMGMA

facts: Hyundai Motor Group will begin manufacturing the Kia Sportage Hybrid in the United States for the first time.
The vehicle will be produced at the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America in Georgia.
This marks the first hybrid vehicle and first Kia model to be produced at the Georgia EV plant.
Hyundai Motor Company announced Tuesday that it plans to manufacture the Kia Sportage Hybrid for the first time in the U.S. at Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
The Kia Sportage Hybrid is currently produced in South Korea.
Tony Heo, President and CEO of HMGMA, stated that the facility's flexible manufacturing system enabled the production of its first hybrid vehicle and first Kia model.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp, First Lady Marty Kemp, and Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson attended the production celebration.
Governor Kemp stated that HMGMA was announced as the largest economic plant in Georgia’s history, leveraging the state's two deep water ports.
HMGMA is Hyundai Motor Company's only EV plant in the U.S. and currently manufactures the Hyundai IONIQ 5 and IONIQ 9.
The Kia Sportage Hybrid features a futuristic, sleek design reminiscent of Hyundai's EV products and will come in five trims.
The Kia Sportage Hybrid achieves 42 miles per gallon, with a base MSRP of $30,490.
George Kennedy, product and technology communications manager at Kia America, emphasized competing on price and offering standard amenities typically found as add-ons on other brands.
Governor Kemp stated the announcement puts Georgia in a position to continue its success in ensuring quality jobs.
Governor Kemp highlighted the partnership between Kia America, HMGMA, and the local community as key to supporting long-term jobs in Georgia.
Approximately 200 facility employees ("Meta Pros") attended the celebration to mark preparation for the new powertrain.
The Kia West Point facility employs more than 3,000 team members, while HMGMA has nearly 2,000 metapros.
Kia is not revealing production numbers at this time, however manufacturing of the vehicle has begun.
HMGMA continues to ramp up production towards its goal of manufacturing 500,000 vehicles annually.
HMC President and COO José Muñoz reported on LinkedIn that Hyundai's hybrid sales are up 90% and EV sales are up 10%.
Hyundai sold 87,468 vehicles, up 3% year-over-year, while Genesis achieved its best May ever, marking 20 consecutive months of growth.
Sales of the Georgia-built IONIQ 5 are up 28%, and sales of the three-row IONIQ 9 are up 279%.
Kia North America President and CEO Sean Yoon highlighted the significance of introducing the vehicle at the metaplant.
Sean Yoon noted that Kia broke ground in West Point 20 years ago, praised Georgia's government for training support, and emphasized the need for production flexibility.
Kia Georgia President and COO Stuart Countess explained that the vehicle addresses a market gap for larger SUVs with hybrid technology.

Stuart Countess stated that consumer demand for technology and lifestyle fit drives interest in hybrids, noting the battery recharges while running, extended fuel range, and the plant's ability to build three powertrains to adapt to market shifts.
source_title: Georgia plant begins Kia Sportage Hybrid production

canonical_name: Rivian Manufacturing Facility (Stanton Springs North, Georgia)
facts: Rivian is scaling up its ambitions in the U.S. with a significant expansion of its upcoming manufacturing facility in Stanton Springs North, Georgia.
The company has increased its planned annual production capacity to 300,000 vehicles.
This represents an increase of 50 percent from the original 200,000 target, signaling stronger confidence in future demand and improved economies of scale.
The higher output is expected to lower per-unit production costs while leaving room for further expansion in later phases.
The Georgia facility will play a central role in Rivian’s next stage of growth, particularly with the introduction of its more affordable R2 platform.
Rivian is working closely with the U.S. Department of Energy through its Office of Energy Dominance Financing to revise a substantial federal loan tied to the project.
The updated financing package is valued at up to $4.5 billion, including approximately $4.0 billion in principal and nearly $500 million in capitalized interest.
This funding aims to strengthen domestic EV manufacturing while supporting construction and long-term employment in Georgia.
The loan structure aligns with Rivian’s updated factory design and long-term production roadmap.
Vertical construction of the Georgia facility is scheduled to begin in 2026.
Rivian expects to access the DOE loan by early 2027.
The company remains on track to start vehicle production in late 2028.
Initial construction efforts will focus on key infrastructure, including the stamping press area—one of the most complex and capital-intensive components of the plant.
Activity is expected to ramp up significantly as the main production buildings begin to take shape later in the construction phase.
The Georgia plant will be critical for manufacturing Rivian’s upcoming R2 models, which are designed to reach a broader segment of the EV market.
The R2 lineup is widely seen as a pivotal move to scale the brand beyond its premium offerings.
Rivian has partnered with Uber to produce a new generation of autonomous vehicles.
The facility is expected to build up to 50,000 robotaxis annually.
Robotaxi production is also slated to begin in late 2028.
Rivian’s expansion underscores the growing importance of domestic EV production as automakers race to scale capacity and reduce costs.
With federal backing and a clear long-term roadmap, the Georgia plant is positioned to become a major hub for both consumer EVs and future mobility solutions.

As competition intensifies in the global EV market, investments like this highlight how U.S.-based manufacturing continues to evolve—combining innovation, scale, and policy support to maintain leadership in the transition to electric mobility.
source_title: Rivian Expands Georgia EV Plant to 300,000 Units Annually

facts: Rivian will host two special events next month to mark the restart of construction of its $5 billion Georgia electric vehicle factory.
A community event is set for Sept. 14.
A formal kickoff ceremony is set for Sept. 16 “in celebration of our strong partnership with Georgia and the planned resumption of construction in 2026.”
The kickoff will be attended by stakeholders and government officials including Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp.
The plant near Social Circle is expected to start production in 2028.
The plant is expected to have an annual capacity of about 200,000 units.
In March 2024, Rivian announced it was stopping construction on the Georgia factory project to save money.
As part of that March 2024 decision, Rivian decided to move production of its upcoming R2 SUV to its plant in Normal, Illinois, to expedite sales.
The project got back on track after Rivian won final approval for a $6.6 billion Department of Energy loan in January.
The DOE loan funds are available through September 2028 but cannot be accessed until Rivian resumes work on the Georgia factory.
In July, Rivian announced it was opening a new East Coast headquarters in Atlanta later this year.
The new Atlanta headquarters will initially employ 100 people.
The Atlanta headquarters will ultimately house 500 workers to support Rivian's operations in Georgia.
source_title: Rivian moving forward on $5B Georgia EV plant | WardsAuto

facts: Rivian operates a plant in Normal, Illinois, formerly owned by Mitsubishi Motors North America.
Rivian produces the five-passenger pickup R1T EV (2021-), seven-passenger SUV R1S EV (2022-), and five-passenger SUV R2 EV (2026-).
Amazon is a customer for EDV 700 (2022-), EDV 500 (2023-), and EDV 900 (2023-) electric delivery vehicles.
Production of Enduro electric drive units began on February 3, 2023 in a new building on the south side of the Normal plant.
Rivian is planning a new plant in Georgia for EV R2, EV R3 and EV R3X vehicles.
Production at the Georgia plant is scheduled to begin in 2028.
New plans revealed on April 30 are for an initial 300,000 unit capacity as production starts in 2028.
The Georgia plant project is backed by a reduced DOE loan of USD 4.5 billion.
Construction at the Georgia plant started in September 2025.
With USD 6.6 billion DOE loan, plans are back on for production beginning in 2028.
Rivian announced a temporary halt to the Georgia plant project.
Rivian produced 57,000 units in 2023, up 135% year-over-year.
Rivian's sales surged but business losses continued in 2023.
Rivian achieved production and sales of over 20,000 units of three commercial vehicle models in 2022.
Rivian's U.S. sales increased 1,032.5% to 2,854 units in December 2022.
Rivian's total U.S. sales for all of 2022 increased 3,381.5% to 20,332 units.
Rivian's U.S. sales increased 71.3% to 4,890 units in December 2023.
Rivian's total U.S. sales for all of 2023 increased 146.5% to 50,122 units.
Rivian Automotive began production and delivery of pickups and SUVs in 2021.
Rivian began delivering pickups from June 2021, followed by deliveries of SUVs.
Rivian purchased a U.S. plant formerly owned by Mitsubishi Motors.
Rivian's total U.S. new car sales for 2024 were 53,451 units with a 6.6% share.
Volkswagen Group formed a partnership with Rivian and XPeng for SDV Development.
On June 27, Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe highlighted at an Investor Day that the company’s vertical integration means Rivian owns the electronics in the vehicle and the software stack.
Rivian's focus on autonomy will see the company control the full stack including camera sets and radar sets.
The Normal, Illinois plant was purchased from Mitsubishi in 2017 for USD 16 million.
Pilot-build vehicles were reported to go down the assembly line at Rivian’s Normal plant since the beginning of 2020.
Component pilot production for its own pickup truck began prior to 2020, as evidenced by images of stamped door surrounds tweeted by Rivian in July 2019.
On July 21, Rivian started adding Tesla/NACS cables to its Rivian Adventure Network (RAN).
The updated RAN enables Tesla vehicles and other brands with NACS ports to charge on Rivian’s charging network.
Rivian has started adopting NACS charge ports on its 2026 R1T and R1S vehicles.
NACS charge ports will be made standard on the upcoming R2.
Rivian is in the process of scaling up its charging network.
On May 12, Rivian introduced Rivian Assistant and Rivian Unified Intelligence to unlock hands-free utility with Connect+.
The AI-powered Rivian Assistant will roll out to all compatible Rivian Gen 1 and Gen 2 owners who subscribe to Connect+ or are in an active trial.
Rivian has created an AI system with the ability to think, predict, and grow more capable over time.
Rivian's total U.S. new car sales for 2025 were 45,489 units.
On November 7, Rivian released its financial results for the third quarter of 2024, reporting a net loss of USD 1.1 billion.
In Q3 2024, the company produced 13,157 vehicles at its manufacturing facility in Normal, Illinois and delivered 10,018 vehicles.
Total revenues for the third quarter of 2024 were USD 874 million, primarily driven by the delivery of 10,018 vehicles.
On June 27, Rivian announced the opening of three fast charging sites in Colorado and California, the first deployments in its nationwide Rivian Adventure Network.
These Level 3 DC fast charging sites will initially provide over 200 kW of power, allowing drivers to add as much as 140 miles of range in just 20 minutes.
The first Rivian Adventure Network site opened in Salida, Colorado.
On December 11 at its first Autonomy & AI Day, Rivian unveiled its proprietary, purpose-built silicon and outlined its roadmap for next-gen vehicle autonomy.
Rivian introduced an evolved software architecture underpinned by artificial intelligence (AI).
At the heart of Rivian’s technology strategy is the Rivian Autonomy Processor (RAP1), a custom 5nm chip.
On November 4, Rivian announced that its Q3 2025 net loss widened to USD 1.2 billion from USD 1.1 billion a year earlier.
Adjusted EBITDA for Q3 was USD (602) million, compared to USD (757) million for the same period last year.
In Q3 2025, Rivian produced 10,720 vehicles at its manufacturing facility in Normal, Illinois and delivered 13,201 vehicles.
source_title: Rivian : Search Results by Category - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: Kia Georgia's West Point facility has been operating since 2006.
The facility sits on a sprawling 2,200-acre site with a $3.2 billion investment.
The state-of-the-art manufacturing operation runs 24 hours a day.
The facility builds the Telluride, Sorento, Sportage, and the all-electric EV9 and EV6 SUVs.
Kia Georgia’s West Point facility and its suppliers have created more than 14,000 jobs across the region.
The facility has produced over 4.6 million vehicles to date.
The facility has an annual capacity exceeding 350,000 units.
Nearly 40% of all Kia sales in the United States start at the West Point facility.
Assembly specialists work on welding, stamping, and final vehicle assembly using advanced machinery while maintaining strict quality standards.
Production group leaders oversee specific areas of the assembly line, motivate teams, troubleshoot, and take ownership of station quality.
Maintenance technicians respond to production line breakdowns, diagnose problems, and handle preventative maintenance to ensure equipment runs safely and efficiently.
Kia Georgia partners with West Georgia Technical College and Georgia Quick Start for specialized maintenance certification programs that help young students access industry-relevant training.
Development specialists prepare requests for quotes, negotiate long-term agreements, and lead yearly cost reduction activities while building relationships with suppliers.
Procurement specialists coordinate purchasing activities between Kia Georgia and automotive parts suppliers, managing contract negotiations and delivery schedules.
Supply chain analysts ensure materials flow smoothly through the facility.
Quality engineers analyze data to identify manufacturing process improvements and work with suppliers to address issues before they impact production.
Quality engineering roles require expertise across multiple disciplines, including electrical, mechanical, and mechatronics.
The facility operates around the clock with multiple shifts offering flexibility for different lifestyles and schedules.
The 70,000-square-foot Kia Georgia Training Center provides customized, advanced industrial training to help employees keep up with current automotive technology.
Every employee receives comprehensive safety training, and the facility maintains rigorous protocols that protect team members at all times.
Employees are eligible for comprehensive health benefits on the first day of the month after their start date, including medical coverage, dental, and vision care.
The Employee Assistance Program provides free access to counseling, health and wellness programs, financial consultation, and legal consultation for employees and their dependents.
The company’s 401(k) plan becomes available 60 days following an employee's first day.
Paid time off benefits include vacation days, sick leave, and personal days.
Additional performance rewards and incentives include bonuses, profit-sharing, and recognition programs for employees who achieve specific goals or demonstrate outstanding performance.
Kia Georgia provides tuition reimbursement for employees pursuing approved courses, degree programs, or certifications that align with their current role or future opportunities.
Career development opportunities include training and workshops, mentorship programs, performance reviews, leadership development, job rotation, and networking events.
Relocation assistance supports employees moving for work-related reasons, including financial aid for moving expenses, temporary housing, and help with home transitions.
Kia Georgia supports veterans, active-duty service members, and military families through flexible learning options, career transition resources, and recognition of military experience.
The company focuses on building a "Customer-Centric, People-Focused Culture" that emphasizes empathy, collaboration, transparency, innovation, and continuous improvement.
Many employees begin in entry-level production roles and advance to team leadership, technical specialist positions, and management roles.
Mentorship programs pair experienced employees with newer team members to facilitate knowledge transfer and professional development.
Leadership development programs prepare high-potential employees for management positions by building skills in team motivation, strategic thinking, and operational excellence.
Job rotation opportunities allow employees to gain experience across different areas of the facility, broadening their understanding of the entire manufacturing process.
The company makes a deliberate effort to promote from within, rewarding employees who learn and grow.

Manufacturing Georgia is a program developed through a strategic partnership between the Georgia Association of Manufacturers and Unmudl, designed specifically to connect workers with employers like Kia Georgia.

Unmudl offers direct pathways to jobs with training aligned with skills Kia Georgia actively seeks, including mechatronics and automation training, manufacturing basics, and technical skills in electronics and industrial systems.

Kia Georgia has been recognized as an outstanding example of international manufacturing success in Georgia, earning praise from state officials and industry leaders for its rapid growth and community impact.
source_title: Employer Spotlight: Speed Toward A Fulfilling Career at Kia Georgia - Unmudl

facts: Workers at Kia Georgia’s assembly plant in West Point gathered on Thursday to celebrate the start of production for the 2025 EV9 SUV.
The 2025 EV9 SUV serves as Kia’s flagship electric vehicle.
The EV9 is the first saleable electric vehicle assembled in Georgia, according to Kia.
Governor Brian Kemp and Kia Georgia’s vice president James Watson drove the EV9 off the assembly line.
Governor Brian Kemp congratulated the Kia Georgia team on the achievement and thanked them for choosing Georgia.
Governor Kemp stated that state and local partners working with Kia demonstrates the value of such collaboration.
Georgia is working to become the "e-mobility capital of the nation."
The EV9 joins existing SUVs like the Telluride, Sorento, and Sportage being assembled at Kia Georgia.
An executive named Yoon stated that the launch reflects Kia’s commitment to the U.S. market and the state of Georgia.
Through "Plan S," Kia aims to be a leader in the e-mobility sector for years to come.
The EV9 has won accolades including World Car of the Year, World EV of the Year, and North American Utility Vehicle of the Year.
Nearly 15 years have passed since the first Kia vehicle rolled off the assembly line at the West Point facility.
Production of the EV9 SUV has resulted in approximately 200 additional jobs at the plant.
The West Point facility now has the flexibility to assemble both EVs and traditional products on the same assembly line.
Stuart Countess serves as the president and CEO of Kia Georgia.
Stuart Countess stated that the Kia Georgia team continues a mindset of turning challenges into opportunities.
Kia Georgia is adding its flagship EV to the facility and entering a new era focused on assembling high-quality vehicles in West Point.
source_title: Kia Georgia unveils flagship electric vehicle, the 2025 EV9 SUV

facts: Georgia recorded $24 billion in investments during fiscal year 2023.
The state received more than $27 billion in e-mobility and battery investments between 2018 and 2023.
Record-breaking exports surpassed $49.7 billion for the third consecutive year.
Georgia boasts a workforce of 5.3 million people.
The state has invested significantly in training programs, apprenticeships, and education initiatives to cultivate talent for evolving industry needs.
Georgia Quick Start is recognized as the nation’s top workforce training program and has trained over one million employees.

Lisa Nash, head of HR for Qcells in Dalton, stated that without Georgia Quick Start they would never be able to train the required volume of people, which would have significantly delayed their ramp up.
Brandon Chadwick serves as a Quick Start training coordinator preparing future Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) employees for Day One and beyond.
Quick Start training converts workers from industries like McDonald’s or retail into automotive manufacturing professionals.
Leading universities and colleges, including Georgia Tech and the University of Georgia, ensure a steady stream of qualified professionals for Georgia’s growth.

The GEORGIA MATCH Direct College Admissions Initiative sends personalized letters to rising high school seniors listing eligible public technical institutions, colleges, and universities to create a pipeline of qualified Georgians.
Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport is the busiest and most efficient airport worldwide.
The Port of Savannah is America’s largest container terminal.
Georgia’s logistics network includes strategic investments in air, sea, rail, and road transportation.
The Georgia Ports Authority operates an inland port system to move heavy products from inland sites to ports.
Georgia has more than 5,000 miles of rail.

Griff Lynch, President and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority, stated that GPA, the Georgia Department of Economic Development, and the Georgia Department of Transportation are working to foster responsible growth that delivers good jobs while preserving quality of life.
Anovion Technologies is building a synthetic graphite manufacturing facility in Southwest Georgia.
Chip Dunn, Executive Chairman of Anovion Technologies, cited access to waterway and land transport as a key reason for locating the company in rural Georgia.
The Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certification Program offers pre-qualified industrial sites with completed due diligence to streamline facility establishment.
About half of Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) suppliers were attracted through the GRAD Certification initiative.

Commissioner Pat Wilson of the Georgia Department of Economic Development stated that companies in e-mobility prioritize speed-to-market solutions and that investing in site development and GRAD certification helps communities prepare for new opportunities.
The Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority secured a 331-acre site at the intersection of two major highways.
The site became GRAD Select certified after completing one mass grading project, obtaining water access permits, and conducting traffic studies with the Georgia Department of Transportation.
HMGMA supplier Hwashin became the first tenant at the Dublin-Laurens County site.
Hwashin broke ground at the site in less than six months after announcing the selected location, joined by Governor Brian Kemp and local leadership.
The Georgia Center of Innovation drives innovation through collaborative public-private partnerships facilitated by state government investments.
CJ Foodville USA is establishing its first U.S. plant in Georgia.

Tony Hunsoo Ahn, President of CJ Foodville USA, cited Georgia’s well-established track record for attracting international investors and job-creators, particularly helping South Korean companies locate, as the reason for choosing the state.
Mr. Ahn noted that Georgia has cultivated a business-friendly environment, reduced regulatory constraints, and shown a constant willingness to engage with business leaders on a personal level.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development’s existing industries and regional project team serve as go-to resources for businesses operating in their region.

Stuart Countess, President & CEO of Kia Georgia, stated that partnerships with the Governor’s Office, the Department of Economic Development, Georgia Quick Start, the City of West Point, and Troup County have been tremendous.
The success of Kia Georgia ultimately helped Georgia secure the largest project in state history: the Hyundai Metaplant.
All factors that helped Kia Georgia thrive—workforce, connectivity, and partnerships—led Hyundai Motor Group to select Georgia for this generational project.
Hyundai expects HMGMA to come online a little over two years after announcing the project.
Hyundai is leveraging Georgia Quick Start at temporary training facilities and Quick Start’s Advanced Manufacturing Training Center for HMGMA.
Construction is underway on the Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia, which will be the first training center in Georgia dedicated exclusively to the EV market.
source_title: Analyzing a Decade of Economic Success in Georgia - Expansion Solutions

facts: Kia Motors Corp., South Korea's second-largest automaker, announced it will set up its first U.S. plant in Georgia.
Production at the new facility will begin in 2009.
The $1.2 billion factory is located in West Point, near the Alabama border.
The Georgia factory will employ 2,500 workers.
Georgia Gov. Sonny Perdue and Kia President and CEO Euisun Chung made the formal announcement at a ceremony in Seoul.
Locations in Mississippi and Tennessee had also contended for the plant.

Georgia Gov. Sonny Perdue stated that Kia's decision to locate in the state "is a testament to the tools, experience and know-how Georgia will deliver to one of the automotive industry's leading innovators."
Kia manufactures the Optima midsize sedan, the small-size Picanto, and the Sorento SUV.
Kia is an affiliate of South Korea's largest carmaker, Hyundai Motor Co., which operates a factory in Montgomery, Alabama, 75 miles away.
Proximity to the Hyundai site combined with a $258 million incentive package from the state helped sway Kia's decision, according to Georgia state officials.
Georgia has been negatively impacted by Ford Motor Co. and General Motors Corp. closing their plants in the state.
The new facility is expected to produce 300,000 vehicles per year at maximum capacity.
Five to six auto suppliers are expected to set up nearby the Georgia plant, resulting in the creation of an additional 2,000 jobs.
Kia exported three-fourths of its South Korean production last year and is pursuing an aggressive overseas expansion.
Besides the U.S. plant, Kia is building a second plant in China.
Kia expects its first European factory to start up in Slovakia at the end of the year-end.
The company supplies materials to partners for vehicle assembly in Malaysia, Indonesia, Vietnam, Russia, and Iran.
Kia President and CEO Euisun Chung stated: "Kia Motors has entered an aggressive growth phase in the U.S."
Kia expects its sales in the United States and Canada to increase 15 percent to 350,000 vehicles in 2006.
Kia expects its U.S. and Canadian sales to grow further to 800,000 by 2010.
Kia Motors America posted record sales for February and was 3.2 percent ahead of its record-setting 2005 sales pace at the time of the report.
In January, Kia reported that its 2005 net profit rose 2.9 percent to 680.9 billion won ($697 million) as revenue gained 4.6 percent to 16 trillion won ($16.4 billion).
Kia sold 1.27 million vehicles worldwide in 2005, which was 13.9 percent more than the previous year.
The company has invested more than $300 million in the United States over the last four years prior to this announcement.
Kia's U.S. facilities include a research and development center in Ann Arbor, Michigan.
Kia's U.S. facilities include vehicle proving grounds in Mojave, California.
Kia operates what it calls the largest automotive design studio in California, which it shares with Hyundai Motor America.
The company was constructing a $87 million U.S. corporate campus in Irvine, California, expected to open in December 2006, which will include a design center.
source_title: Kia Picks Georgia For U.s. Plant | Kia Forum

facts: Georgia Manufacturing Alliance closed out 2023 with a record number of networking opportunities, educational events and plant tours for its members.
More than 50 such experiences were offered throughout the state for GMA members in 2023 alone.
Founder and CEO Jason Moss started GMA in 2008 to grow Georgia’s manufacturing community and keep jobs in the state.
Two business-to-business networking events were held in January 2024, along with a plant tour of Iron Age Office that sold out with a growing waiting list.
A plant tour of Aristotle Metal Works is scheduled for Feb. 12, 2024.
GMA will celebrate its 16th anniversary at Creative Comforts in Athens on Feb. 20, 2024.
An educational panel discussion on the challenges and opportunities for local sourcing will be held on Feb. 22, 2024.
GMA members are scheduled to tour the Kia Georgia plant in West Point in March 2024.
A tour of the Gulf Stream plant in Savannah is planned for May 2024.
Twice-monthly networking and educational events are scheduled throughout the year for manufacturing professionals across the state.

Jason Moss stated that Georgia’s geographical location, climate, a major shipping port, airport, other transportation options, and a strong available workforce make the state an ideal location for manufacturers.
GMA provides monthly plant tours, educational sessions, tradeshows, and unique networking opportunities designed to help members make profitable business connections.

links.context: Membership page, 2024 calendar, and past tours/events

source_title: Georgia Manufacturing Alliance looks forward to a 2024 packed with opportunities - Shipping & Logistics Industry Today - EIN Presswire

facts: All electric vehicles manufactured by Hyundai Motor Group at its assembly plant near Savannah since April 1 qualify for a $7,500 federal tax credit now that their batteries are sourced from a northeast Georgia factory.
SK Battery America in Commerce is supplying lithium battery packs to the Hyundai Metaplant.
The Hyundai Metaplant is located along I-16 in Bryan County.
EVs are powered by batteries, not gasoline engines, and the battery pack is the largest component.
Before the deal with SK Battery, the IONIQ 5s coming off the Savannah assembly line failed to meet the tax credit’s requirement.
source_title: Hyundai’s ‘made in Georgia’ EVs just got a little more local | Chattanooga Times Free Press

facts: SK Battery America is a subsidiary of South Korea-based SK On.
SK Battery America is laying off hundreds of workers from its production facility in Commerce, Georgia.
The layoffs are occurring as electric vehicle battery demand slows and federal incentives dry up.
The company filed a Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification on Friday, notifying Georgia of job cuts affecting 958 people.
Affected workers will receive pay and benefits for the next 60 days.
Friday, March 6 was their last day of work.
The Commerce site originally had 2,566 workers before the reductions brought the headcount to 1,600.
Joe Guy Collier serves as SK Battery America’s spokesperson.
The company stated it will work with affected workers during the transition.
SK Battery America remains committed to Georgia and building a robust U.S. supply chain for advanced battery manufacturing.
The company is pursuing future customers in the Battery Energy Storage System arena.
The electric vehicle battery industry is navigating demand challenges, including slower sales and a pullback of federal incentives under the current administration.
Many automakers and battery companies are making cutbacks in response to these market conditions, according to the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas.
Companies are also looking to cater to growing data center demand for backup power and energy storage systems.
In December, Ford Motor Co., a customer of SK Battery America, announced it was shifting its electrification plans to include hybrid vehicles and smaller, more affordable EVs.
Ford also announced plans to grow its energy storage business.
Ford expects to record a $19.5 billion write-down due to the shift in electrification plans.
Ford dissolved its BlueOval SK battery joint venture with SK On as it shifts away from EVs.
Ford canceled the electric F-150 Lightning due to sluggish sales.
In 2023, SK Battery America said it would expand its Commerce site workforce by several hundred employees to meet rising EV demand.
The company had previously exceeded its goal of 2,600 employees at the Commerce site.
The Occupational Safety and Health Administration fined SK Battery America twice for exposing workers to respiratory hazards.
Law firm Strauss Borrelli announced it was investigating SK Battery for allegedly failing to notify the 958 employees per the WARN Act.
The WARN Act mandates that employers notify their workers in writing at least 60 days before a plant closure or job cuts take effect.
Spokesperson Joe Guy Collier stated that SK Battery America’s reduction complies with WARN guidelines.
source_title: SK Battery America lays off nearly 1,000 workers at Georgia plant | Manufacturing Dive

entity_name: LG Energy Solution and Hyundai Motor Group Joint Venture
facts: LG Energy Solution and Hyundai Motor Group are establishing a battery cell manufacturing joint venture in the U.S.
LG Energy Solution and Hyundai will build a $4.3 billion joint battery cell plant in Georgia.
HL-GA Battery Company LLC is announced as the title sponsor for the 2024 Industry Expo Project Report Out (PRO) Day.
Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy are building a $4.3 billion EV battery plant in the US.
Hyundai and LG announced a $4.3 billion plant in Georgia to build batteries for electric vehicles.
The page features a discussion titled 'What Can We Expect If Hyundai and LG Build an EV Plant in the US?'
The joint venture is recruiting talent, as indicated by 'JOIN OUR TEAM' and 'Careers' sections on the page.
source_title: News -

facts: Federal agents on Sept. 4 arrested hundreds of people at a sprawling battery plant construction site in Georgia in one of the Trump administration's largest immigration raids.
Agents from the Department of Homeland Security, FBI, Drug Enforcement Agency, and Georgia State Patrol took part in the operation at Hyundai's battery plant in Bryan County, southwest of Savannah.
Agents apprehended about 450 people suspected of being in the country illegally, according to an ATF X post.

DHS stated that Immigration and Customs Enforcement's Homeland Security Investigations executed a judicial search warrant for an ongoing criminal investigation into alleged unlawful employment practices and other federal crimes.
The agency did not clarify how many agents participated in the operation or the charges those detained face.
Steven Schrank, special agent in charge of Homeland Security Investigations Atlanta, stated at a news conference that they are making many arrests of undocumented individuals.
Schrank noted that many lawful employees, including United States citizens and lawful permanent residents, were encountered and released.
Schrank confirmed there were no injuries or uses of force during the operation and said more details would be released later.
The facility is the HL-GA Battery Company, a battery plant jointly created by LG Energy Solution and Hyundai Motor Group.
The plant is under construction next to the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America at a site spanning nearly 3,000 acres.
Hyundai's new $7.6 billion plant in a rural area near Savannah has promised to transform the local economy and workforce.
The facility is estimated to create around 8,500 jobs by 2030.
Hyundai spokesperson Bianca Johnson stated in an email that there was no impact to the metaplant or its production.
Johnson confirmed the law enforcement action took place at the HL-GA battery plant's construction site.
Johnson said Hyundai is cooperating with law enforcement and is committed to abiding by all labor and immigration regulations.
Mary Beth Kennedy, spokesperson for the HL-GA Battery Company, stated the company is cooperating fully with appropriate authorities regarding activity at their construction site.
Kennedy announced that they have paused construction to assist authorities' work but do not have further details at this time.
The Savannah Morning News was told to exit the facility as state troopers blocked access during the raid.
Images posted by ATF showed men and women in zip ties being escorted off the site.

Georgia State Patrol Capt. Crystal Zion said the department assisted federal officials with serving a criminal search warrant at the Hyundai Metaplant, directing further inquiries to Homeland Security Investigations.

The Georgia raid is one of the largest during President Donald Trump's second term as he promises a sweeping immigration crackdown using multiple federal agencies and local/state law enforcement assistance.
Raids have taken place across the country on farms, construction sites, restaurants, and car washes.
During Trump's first term in August 2019, agents arrested 680 people at seven Mississippi chicken processing plants, called the largest single-state immigration crackdown in a decade.
source_title: Hundreds arrested in immigration raid at Hyundai plant site in Georgia - AOL

facts: On September 4, 2025, hundreds of federal agents executed a federal search warrant at the HL-GA Battery Company, LLC in Ellabell, Georgia.
The search targeted evidence related to the unlawful employment of illegal aliens in violation of Title 8, United States Code, Section 1324.
Upon execution of the criminal search warrant, several people attempted to flee the location.
A handful of people ran into a sewage pond located on the premises during the raid.
Agents used a boat to retrieve individuals from the sewage pond.
One individual swam under the boat and tried to flip it over before being captured and identified as an illegal worker.
Over the course of the day, federal, state, and local law enforcement executed the search warrant and identified hundreds of illegal workers.
Law enforcement specifically identified over 475 people who were unlawfully working at the location.
United States Attorney Margaret E. Heap stated that over 400 agents participated in the operation and over 400 illegal workers were identified and detained.
United States Attorney Margaret E. Heap stated that the goal of the operation is to reduce illegal employment and prevent employers from gaining an unfair advantage by hiring unauthorized workers.
United States Attorney Margaret E. Heap stated that another goal is to protect unauthorized workers from exploitation.
The investigation was led by Homeland Security Investigations (HSI).

Assisting agencies included U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), Enforcement and Removal Operations (ERO), Federal Bureau of Investigations (FBI), Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF), Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA), Internal Revenue Service (IRS) Criminal Investigations, U.S. Department of Labor Office of Inspector General, United States Marshal Service (USMS), and Georgia State Patrol.
Assistant United States Attorneys Tania Groover, Ryan Bondura, Kelsey Scanlon, and E. Greg Gilluly, Jr., are investigating the case.
The investigation is ongoing.
All people involved are presumed innocent until proven guilty beyond a reasonable doubt.

This case is part of Operation Take Back America, a nationwide initiative that marshals the full resources of the Department of Justice to repel illegal immigration and eliminate cartels and transnational criminal organizations.
Operation Take Back America streamlines efforts from the Department’s Organized Crime Drug Enforcement Task Forces (OCDETFs) and Project Safe Neighborhoods (PSN).
source_title: DOJ Issues Press Release on Illegal Workers at Metaplant

facts: Hyundai Mobis introduced its expansion strategy in the North American market during a press conference at the North American International Auto Show (NAIAS) on Sept. 15, 2022.
Axel Maschka serves as Executive Vice President and head of Hyundai Mobis Global OE Sales.
The company's growth strategy is built on two keywords: Plant Expansion and Value Expansion.
Hyundai Mobis plans to expand its production facilities across the globe for prompt and timely supply to global automakers.
Hyundai Mobis will construct more EV dedicated sites in North America and invest in EV components and battery system assemblies to allow rapid and environmentally optimized local production.
Hyundai Mobis currently operates 44 production bases globally.
Hyundai Mobis's North American facilities are located in Alabama, Georgia, Ohio, and Michigan.
North America leading the electric vehicle shift to achieve carbon neutrality and green mobility is integral to the company's electrification production expansion plan.
Having a strong production capacity will be key to winning sales orders as the EV market continues to grow.
Hyundai Mobis has over ten years of expertise in producing high-quality electrification components.
Hyundai Mobis revealed its value expansion plan to increase its sales orders in the North American market with value-added technologies optimized specifically for electric and autonomous vehicles.
Hyundai Mobis presented future mobility technologies including the electric Complete Chassis Platform Module (eCCPM) and next-generation AR HUD.

Axel Maschka stated, "Hyundai Mobis has a wide range of innovations in its portfolio, and is ready for the future of mobility. Our vision is to bring the future to the present and make it more valuable."
Hyundai Mobis successfully mass-produced key electrification components including the battery system for the e-GMP platform used in Hyundai and Kia electric vehicles.
Hyundai Mobis also mass-produced PE modules.
Value-added products including the eCCPM module, electrification platform, AR HUD, and lighting grilles are drawing attention from clients in North America for their versatility and innovation.
Hyundai Mobis announced a goal of driving a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 36% in the North American market through the year 2030.
Hyundai Mobis reported total order volumes of $660 million USD in 2020.
Hyundai Mobis reported total order volumes of $1.4 billion USD in 2021.
Hyundai Mobis reported total order volumes of $1.7 billion USD in the first half of 2022.
Hyundai Mobis is ranked as the global no. 6 automotive supplier.
Hyundai Mobis is headquartered in Seoul, Korea.
Hyundai Mobis has outstanding expertise in sensors, sensor fusion in ECUs and software development for safety control.
Hyundai Mobis's product portfolio includes various components for electrification, brakes, chassis and suspension, steering, airbags, lighting and automotive electronics.
Hyundai Mobis operates an R&D headquarters in Korea.
Hyundai Mobis operates four technology centers in the United States, Germany, China and India.
