facts: EnChem America, Inc. is a U.S. corporation engaged in the development, manufacture, and sale of battery electrolyte materials.
EnChem America, Inc. is a U.S. subsidiary of EnChem Co., Ltd., a corporation organized under the laws of the Republic of Korea.
EnChem America is proposing a reverse merger / reverse take over (RTO) transaction with The Growhub Limited (TGHL).
The transaction will be structured as a reverse triangular merger where EnChem America becomes the surviving corporation and a wholly-owned subsidiary of TGHL.
The Private Company shall be treated as the accounting acquiror for financial reporting purposes, and the Transaction shall be accounted for as a reverse merger under U.S. GAAP.
The agreed equity value of EnChem America for purposes of the Merger is a minimum of USD 400,000,000.
Pre-Closing equity holders of EnChem America will receive Merger Shares constituting 85% of the equity of TGHL on a fully diluted basis immediately following Closing.
Pre-Closing equity holders of TGHL will retain 15% of the equity of TGHL on a fully diluted basis immediately following Closing.
Post-Closing ownership allocation shows EnChem at 85.0% and TGHL at 15.0%.
Total pro forma shares will be 168,147,986, comprising 142,848,176 TGHL Existing shares and 25,299,810 Merger Shares.
EnChem America holds a walk-away right to terminate the agreement without liability if the Closing VWAP is less than USD 4.00 per share within five Business Days of determination.

The Closing VWAP is defined as the volume-weighted average trading price of Public Company Common Stock on The Nasdaq Capital Market for the twenty consecutive trading days ending three trading days prior to the Closing Date, as reported by Bloomberg L.P.
The parties anticipate one or more capital raising transactions in connection with or following the Closing.
TGHL shall file a shelf registration statement registering an amount not less than USD 200,000,000 immediately upon becoming F-3 eligible.

Post-closing capital raises may utilize follow-on offerings, Equity Lines of Credit on form F-1 or S-1, PIPEs, registered direct offerings, confidential public offerings, at-the-market programs, or convertible debt offerings.
EnChem America shall deliver PCAOB audited financial statements prepared in accordance with U.S. GAAP on a mutually agreed date.
Upon closing, the entire Board of Directors of the combined company will be reconstituted with all seats filled by individuals designated by EnChem America (EnChem Designees).
The initial post-Closing Board shall consist of not fewer than five and not more than seven directors, with a minimum of three satisfying Nasdaq director independence requirements.
All incumbent senior executive officers of TGHL (including CEO, CFO, and other C-suite positions) will tender resignations effective at the Effective Time.
The post-Closing senior management team will be designated by EnChem America and delivered to TGHL no later than five Business Days prior to Closing.

An Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation will eliminate Class B Common Stock, remove super-voting rights, and vest full operational control in post-Closing management designated by EnChem America.
All Merger Shares issued to pre-Closing equity holders of EnChem America shall be subject to lock-up restrictions for 180 days following the Closing Date.

TGHL shall execute a Registration Rights Agreement to register resale of Merger Shares under the Shelf or a new registration statement upon expiration of the lock-up period, maintaining registration for not less than [36 months following expiration of the lock-up.
The Exclusivity Period runs from execution through 90 days, automatically extending by successive 30-day periods unless non-renewed.
During exclusivity, TGHL cannot solicit or negotiate alternative merger, business combination, sale of assets, or change-of-control transactions.
The Merger Agreement shall provide for termination if Closing has not occurred by the date 12 months following execution (the "Outside Date").
Each party shall bear its own legal, accounting, financial advisory, and other transaction costs; shared costs including SEC filing fees and printing shall be allocated equally.
The term sheet is dated May 28, 2026.
Signatories include Lester chan (Director of TGHL) on May 28, 2026, and Jung Kang Oh (CEO of EnChem America) on May 29, 2026.

The transaction complies with the Securities Act of 1933, Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Nasdaq Listing Rules including Rule 5635(a), Hart-Scott-Rodino Antitrust Improvements Act, and applicable CFIUS requirements.
Governing law is Delaware General Corporation Law with disputes subject to exclusive jurisdiction of the Court of Chancery of Delaware.
The Parties shall work together to structure the Transaction to optimize the tax treatment for each of the Parties.
Public Company stockholders must approve the issuance of the Merger Shares pursuant to Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(a).
Private Company shareholders must approve the Merger under applicable corporate law.
As soon as reasonably practicable following execution, TGHL shall file with the SEC a joint registration statement on Form S-4 or Form F-4 including a proxy statement.
Board resignations shall be delivered no later than two Business Days prior to the anticipated Closing Date and shall be irrevocable.

The Merger Agreement shall contain customary representations and warranties covering organization, good standing, authorization, capitalization, financial statements, Material Adverse Effect, intellectual property, material contracts, litigation, tax matters, compliance with laws, employee benefits, and related party transactions.

Pre-Closing covenants require each party to conduct business in the ordinary course, preserve material business relationships, cooperate in SEC filings, and refrain from actions impairing consummation.

Definitive documentation will include a Merger Agreement, Lock-Up Agreements, Registration Rights Agreement, Voting Agreements, Officer and Director Indemnification Agreements, and Amended Certificate of Incorporation and Bylaws.

The term sheet and all exchanged information are strictly confidential except to advisors subject to equivalent obligations, as required by law/SEC/Nasdaq, or for regulatory approvals; KOSDAQ disclosure rules in Korea override confidentiality where applicable.

Given the binding nature, both parties will work together to draft and issue press releases, public announcements, and filings; neither shall issue them without prior written consent except as required by law.
All terms become legally fully valid and enforceable obligations simultaneously with formal signing and sealing.

canonical_name: Advanced Materials and Manufacturing Technologies Office (AMMTO) Funding Opportunity DE-FOA-0003236
facts: On January 29, 2024, the U.S. Department of Energy's Advanced Materials and Manufacturing Technologies Office (AMMTO) released funding opportunity DE-FOA-0003236.
The funding opportunity provides $15.7 million to advance the domestic manufacturing of next generation batteries and energy storage.
This opportunity will advance platform technologies upon which battery manufacturing capabilities can be built.
Research and development under this opportunity will improve manufacturability and scalability of sodium-ion batteries, flow batteries, and nanolayered films for energy storage.
The funding opportunity will also integrate smart manufacturing technologies to increase productivity and lower the cost for domestic battery production.

Batteries are critical to the U.S. transition to a clean energy economy and to national competitiveness – for grid storage, the resilience of homes and businesses, the success of the domestic industrial sector, and the electrification of the transportation sector.
AMMTO estimates making up to 13 awards under this funding opportunity.
The opportunity encourages underserved communities and underrepresented groups to participate.
Applicants must submit a concept paper by 5 p.m. ET on March 4, 2024, to be eligible to submit a full application.
Topic Area 1: Platforms for Next Generation Battery Manufacturing is allocated $10.7 million total for 3–8 projects.
Topic Area 1 focuses on platforms that enable flexible, scalable, and highly controllable battery manufacturing processes through the development of advanced machines.
Subtopic 1.1 focuses on advanced processes and/or high-performance processing machines for low cost, large-scale, sustainable, commercial manufacture of sodium-ion batteries.

Subtopic 1.2 focuses on design and manufacturing of flow battery membranes, as well as system design and manufacturing for scale-up of flow battery production and cost-effective integration of flow battery systems.
Subtopic 1.3 focuses on scalable manufacturing processes and equipment for nanolayered films.
Topic Area 2: Smart Manufacturing Platforms for Battery Production is allocated $5 million total for 2–5 projects.

Topic Area 2 emphasizes development of broadly applicable smart manufacturing platforms that leverage processing and manufacturing data combined with computational learning technologies, such as artificial intelligence and machine learning.
Project teams under Topic Area 2 will focus on smart manufacturing processes and smart equipment capable of smart design, control, and monitoring of battery manufacturing processes.
Applicants must refer to the funding opportunity announcement for topic and subtopic specific eligibility requirements.
The submission deadline for full applications is May 7, 2024, at 5 p.m. ET.
DOE expects to issue selection notifications by August 27, 2024.
Award negotiations are expected to occur between October and December 2024.

links.context: Notifications for new EERE funding opportunities
Monthly updates on AMMTO funding opportunities, events, and news

source_title: AMMTO Releases $15.7 Million Funding Opportunity to Advance Domestic Manufacturing of Next Generation Batteries | Department of Energy

facts: Gov. Brian P. Kemp announced that Steves & Sons, Inc. will invest over $100 million and create 170 jobs over the next three years as it builds a new facility in Jackson County.
This is the company’s second expansion announcement in Georgia in the last year.
Based in San Antonio, Texas for six generations, Steves & Sons offers a variety of interior and exterior door styles and types.
Steves & Sons serves other leading millwork distributors across the country and has been recognized four times as vendor of the year by The Home Depot.
Last December, Steves & Sons announced a new facility in Turner County that will ultimately create 200 jobs in Ashburn.
Edward Steves, CEO of Steves & Sons, stated that Georgia has proven to be extremely helpful for the company's long-term manufacturing and distribution needs.
Sam Steves, President of Steves & Sons, added that Georgia's abundant wood fiber sources and world-class port in Savannah are critical to their expansion into Georgia.
The new 310,000-square-foot facility will be located near US-441 and Athens.
The facility will produce millions of interior molded door skins annually for use in new housing construction, repair, and remodeling.
The company will be hiring skilled operators, electricians, and engineers.
Scott Martin, Chairman of the Jackson County Industrial Development Authority, stated that Steves & Sons' decision to locate in Jackson County is proof of the area's thriving business climate.
Global Commerce Senior Project Manager Kristen Miller represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) on this competitive project.
The project was completed in partnership with the Jackson County Area Chamber of Commerce and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Steves & Sons will take advantage of Georgia Quick Start to fill newly created positions.

For over 156 years, Steves & Sons, Inc. has provided quality products for homes with an emphasis on doors, built on a commitment to quality, superior craftsmanship, style, and environmental responsibility expressed in long-lasting products incorporating recycled elements.
source_title: Manufacturer Steves & Sons, Inc. to Invest Over $100 Million in Jackso | Georgia

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp applauded Lockheed Martin’s positive economic impact over its 75 years of continuous operations in Marietta, Georgia.
The celebration event took place on June 17, 2026, at Lockheed Martin’s Cobb County manufacturing facility in Marietta.

Attendees included Governor Brian P. Kemp, First Lady Marty Kemp, representatives from the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), company officials, and state, local, and federal leaders.
The Marietta facility supports more than 5,600 Georgia jobs.
Lockheed Martin announced 1,200 new Marietta-based jobs as a milestone in its partnership with Georgia.
These new jobs are creating meaningful careers while supporting production of the C-130J Super Hercules and F-35 Lightning II aircraft.
Since 2019, Lockheed Martin has increased its Georgia workforce by more than 25%.
The company added over 1,200 jobs at its 8-million-square-foot Marietta facility alone since 2019.
Statewide, Lockheed Martin employs more than 6,300 people.
In 2025, Lockheed Martin contributed $4.5 billion to Georgia’s economy.
An additional 550 employees are based at Naval Submarine Base Kings Bay.
More than 100 employees are based at Robins Air Force Base.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Lockheed Martin has been a cornerstone employer and leader in delivering advanced capabilities for decades.

Trish Pagan, Vice President of Air Mobility & Maritime Missions and Marietta site general manager, cited Georgia’s favorable business environment, talent pipeline, university system, robust supplier network, and community support as critical strategic foundations.
The C-130J Super Hercules is Georgia’s top export and anchors the state’s second-largest manufacturing industry.
The aerospace and aviation industries generated more than $23.9 billion in GDP in 2024.
At its Marietta center of excellence, Lockheed Martin produces the C-130J Super Hercules and the F-35 Lightning II Center Wing Assembly.
The facility supports the ongoing sustainment of the F-22 Raptor, C-5 Super Galaxy, and P-3 Orion.
The Marietta facility is home to the Skunk Works® division, known for cutting-edge innovation.
The C-130J and F-35 programs alone support more than 20,000 direct and indirect Georgia jobs through robust supplier networks.

Ember Bishop Bentley, Executive Director of the Georgia Aerospace & Defense Alliance, highlighted the company's role in building a world-class ecosystem of manufacturers, suppliers, research institutions, and skilled workers.
Marietta has been the global home of C-130 Hercules production since 1955, hosting America’s longest-running military aircraft production line.
More than 2,700 C-130s have been built at the Marietta site and delivered to over 70 operators worldwide.
This manufacturing line is America’s last remaining production capacity for building aircraft for the airlift mission.
Careers at the Lockheed Martin Marietta facility span advanced manufacturing, engineering, AI, data analytics, IT, finance, operations, and more.
Sharon Mason, President and CEO of the Cobb Chamber of Commerce, noted that Lockheed Martin is one of Cobb County's largest employers and a cornerstone of the community's identity and economy.
Marietta employees give back 21,000 volunteer hours to schools and organizations each year.
Lockheed Martin invests in Georgia’s future workforce through partnerships with the University System of Georgia, Technical College System institutions, and K-12 STEM education.
These workforce initiatives align with Governor Kemp’s Top State for Talent initiative.
Katie Kirkpatrick, President and CEO of the Metro Atlanta Chamber, emphasized that Lockheed Martin's focus on high-tech jobs creates high-paying careers in the region.
Georgia’s nine military bases provide competitive advantages to Lockheed Martin and other defense and advanced manufacturing companies.
Nearly 2,000 veterans, reservists, and military spouses are employed directly by Lockheed Martin.
The Marietta facility stands poised to lead the next era of aerospace advancement for Georgia and the world.
source_title: Georgia Applauds Lockheed Martin’s 75 Years of Production, Impact | Georgia

facts: The Georgia Made® program celebrates manufacturers across Georgia whose products are used in daily life and globally.
To be Georgia Made® certified, at least 50% of the final product must be assembled and produced in Georgia.
The certification conveys a point of pride for products used in the state or carried via Georgia’s robust logistics network anywhere else in the world.
Choosing Georgia Made® products offers Georgians a fulfilling way to support their state's business community health.
Georgia works closely with manufacturing companies under the program to understand their needs and remove barriers, ensuring that prosperity benefits everyone.
The program provides opportunities for businesses to connect with leading logistics, research, and educational institutions throughout the state to explore partnership opportunities.
Upon certification, a company will be registered in the Georgia Made® database of companies.
Certified companies receive usage rights to the Georgia Made® logo on products, packaging, and promotional materials.
Eligibility requires at least 50% of the final product to be assembled or produced in Georgia, excluding food/agricultural products and those eligible for the Georgia Grown program.
Manufacturing under the program refers to human activities creating tangible or intangible goods, including product design, proprietary code, engineering, and industrial production.
Raw materials adapted into finished goods include wood, metal, minerals, recycled paper, plastic, and glass.
Finished goods may also be component parts incorporated into or assembled into other products or goods.
The program covers Georgia’s wide range of non-industrial products that meet the definition outside industrial or commercial facilities.
Companies whose products display the Georgia Made® label operate in sectors including aerospace, automotive, defense, and life sciences.
source_title: Georgia Made® | Georgia

entity_name: Department of Energy Critical Minerals and Materials Program
canonical_name: Department of Energy Critical Minerals and Materials Program
facts: The Energy Act of 2020 defines a “critical mineral” as any mineral, element, substance, or material designated as critical by the Secretary of the Interior, acting through the director of the U.S. Geological Survey.

The Energy Act of 2020 defines a “critical material” as any non-fuel mineral, element, substance, or material that the Secretary of Energy determines has a high risk of supply chain disruption and serves an essential function in one or more energy technologies, including technologies that produce, transmit, store, and conserve energy; or a critical mineral as defined by the Secretary of the Interior.
In 2023, the Department of Energy published a comprehensive assessment of various materials for energy to evaluate potential supply risk and importance to energy technologies.

DOE’s assessment considers supply risk factors including basic availability, competing technology demand, political, regulatory, and social factors, codependence on other markets, and producer diversity.
The assessment evaluates importance to energy technologies based on two factors: energy demand and substitutability.
The methodology incorporates material intensity of energy technologies for both currently widely deployed and innovative technologies that may reduce material requirements in the future.
Pursuant to Section 7002(a) of the Energy Act of 2020, the Secretary of Energy determines the Critical Materials List.

The current list includes critical materials from the Final 2023 Critical Materials List and the May 2025 amendment, as well as those on the 2025 final list published by the Secretary of the Interior acting through the director of the U.S. Geological Survey.
Non-elemental critical minerals designated by the USGS include natural graphite, metallurgical coal, phosphate, potash, and synthetic graphite.
Non-elemental critical materials designated by the DOE include natural graphite, metallurgical coal, electrical steel, and silicon carbide.

Critical minerals and materials serve energy applications in batteries, nuclear energy, fuel cells, solar cells, displays & optics, catalysts, semiconductors & microconductors, magnets & motors, steel production & strengthening alloys for aerospace/defense/high performance, lighting, general electronics components, lasers, and atomic clocks & GPS.

Elements and materials specifically listed for battery applications include aluminum, antimony, cobalt, copper, fluorine, graphite, lanthanum, lead, lithium, manganese, nickel, phosphate, silver, and vanadium.
The results of the 2023 DOE Critical Materials Assessment are presented in a criticality matrix to evaluate how critical each material is for the future of energy.
source_title: What Are Critical Minerals and Materials? | Department of Energy

facts: Astemo Americas is introducing new mobility technology to its North American customers.
The company is expanding its footprint in the Americas.
Formerly known as Hitachi Astemo Americas, the Tier One auto supplier's name has officially changed to Astemo Americas.
The name change aligns with the corporate headquarters' name change from Hitachi Astemo, Ltd. to Astemo Ltd.
Astemo has 80,000 employees worldwide.
Astemo operates more than 40 offices and manufacturing sites globally.
More than 14,000 of Astemo's employees are located in the Americas.

Astemo Americas CEO and President Tim Clark says the company aims to further strengthen its position as a leader in electrification and mobility intelligence as the industry moves into the age of the software defined vehicle.
The company is accelerating its investment in advanced mobility, including electrification, advanced driver-assistance systems, autonomous driving and advanced chassis technology.
Astemo has outgrown its technical center and headquarters in Farmington Hills, Michigan, and plans to expand to a new location in the near future.
Astemo is showcasing a wide range of new mobility technology to its major OEM customers in the Americas this summer.
Next-generation autonomous driving, EV, chassis, suspension, steering and brake technologies are being introduced to customers this month with presentations, displays and test-drive vehicles.

3D sensing-and-control, semi-active suspension, steer-by-wire and brake-by-wire are among the various technologies Astemo will have on display for use on future new-model vehicles sold in the Americas.

Astemo has been developing automated driving solutions using multi-camera system that utilizes proprietary AI based stereo vision technology which enables generation of 360° denser point cloud than conventional LiDAR along with color and brightness information.

The company is showcasing a full suspension system with AI-powered control logic using deep-learning algorithms that delivers improvements in ride-and-handling at a lower cost compared to similar systems today.
The system is equipped with a "preview" camera to prepare for bumps and potholes in the road ahead.

Additional suspension technologies being showcased include a performance series damper system focused on Off-Road vehicles and a Virtual Development Lab that supports customers' efforts in eliminating physical prototypes.
Astemo's latest generation of steer-by-wire technology features a hand-held device designed to replace a vehicle's steering wheel.
The Astemo Smart Steer-by-Wire System's software and hardware provide a pathway to the auto industry's move toward autonomous driving.
Shown for the first time in North America, Astemo's electro-mechanical brake-by-wire system, Smart Brake, reduces lag-time between driver input and brake response.
Its e-pedal eliminates the traditional hydraulic connection between the brake pedal and the vehicle's brakes.
The new system simplifies vehicle assembly, extends battery range and reduces maintenance by eliminating the use of brake fluid.
Astemo's Smart Brake is an innovative project supported financially by the French Government as part of France 2030 investment plan.
"Astemo, Mobility Beyond – From the Core of Mobility, Freedom" has become the new branding message and is featured on the company's global website and in other marketing materials.
The name Astemo, derived from "Advanced Sustainable Technologies for Mobility," underscores the company's focus on providing cutting-edge, sustainable mobility solutions.
Astemo is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.
Astemo is a joint venture between Hitachi, Ltd., Honda Motor Co., and JIC Capital, Ltd.

The Electric Business Division, Vehicle Business Division, and Motorcycle Business Division are engaged in the development, manufacture, sales, and service of automotive parts as well as transportation and industrial machinery and systems.
Astemo Americas, Inc. manufactures and markets engine management, electric powertrain and integrated vehicle controls for major automotive manufacturers worldwide.
Astemo Americas adheres to ISO/TS:16949:2009 industry standards.
source_title: Changes Are Underway at Astemo Americas - A Leading Auto Supplier

facts: Foresight Autonomous Holdings Ltd. (Nasdaq and TASE: FRSX) announced the signing of an agreement with Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. to test and evaluate autonomous-driving vision systems (ADAS).
Hitachi Astemo is a global Tier One supplier of engine management, electric powertrain and integrated vehicle-control systems for the auto industry.
The paid joint proof of concept project with Hitachi Astemo calls for the evaluation and testing of simulated and real-life autonomous driving scenarios.
The program will pair Foresight QuadSight® and Mono2Stereo™ technologies with existing Hitachi Astemo camera systems to improve distance measurement and object detection.
The project follows the successful evaluation of a QuadSight vision system prototype reported by the Company in March 2021.

Foresight’s proprietary Mono2Stereo software-based solution uses overlapping views of existing cameras with different fields of vision to create a three-dimensional stereo-vision system to improve object detection.

Haim Siboni, CEO of Foresight, stated that being chosen by Hitachi Astemo underlines the importance of stereoscopic technology in enhancing existing ADAS systems and its ability to amplify performance, provide better distance accuracy, and offer more robust active safety features.
Foresight's solutions can be deployed in vehicles equipped with Level 2 and Level 2-plus autonomy systems without requiring additional hardware and design changes.
Siboni believes teaming up with Hitachi Astemo could potentially lead to further collaboration with other automotive OEMs.

Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. manufactures and markets engine management, electric powertrain, and integrated vehicle control for major automotive manufacturers worldwide, adhering to ISO/TS:16949:2009 industry standards.
Foresight Autonomous Holdings Ltd. is a technology company developing smart multi-spectral vision software solutions and cellular-based applications.

Through wholly owned subsidiaries including Foresight Automotive Ltd., Foresight Changzhou Automotive Ltd., and Eye-Net Mobile Ltd., Foresight develops in-line-of-sight vision systems and beyond-line-of-sight accident-prevention solutions.

Foresight’s vision solutions include modules of automatic calibration and dense three-dimensional point cloud applicable to automotive, defense, autonomous vehicles, and heavy industrial equipment markets.

Eye-Net Mobile’s cellular-based solution provides real-time pre-collision alerts to enhance road safety and situational awareness for urban mobility by incorporating AI technology and advanced analytics.

The press release contains forward-looking statements regarding the agreement, testing of simulated and real-life autonomous driving scenarios, and potential future collaborations with other automotive OEMs.
The Company's headquarters and largest manufacturing facility are located in Kingsport, Tennessee.
Saflex™ Q series advanced acoustic interlayers enable weight reduction of vehicles.

Solar-absorbing Saflex™ PVB interlayer solutions, such as the Saflex™ S series, Saflex™ Solar Connect, and XIR™ Solar Control Technology, are ideal for electric vehicle ("EV") glass in cabin-forward designs and for larger sunroofs.
These solar-absorbing interlayer solutions reduce loads on air-conditioning systems and help maximize EV driving range.
Eastman began commissioning and startup activities of one of the world's largest polyester recycling facilities at its Kingsport manufacturing site in 2023.
Eastman introduced plastic waste into its Kingsport methanolysis facility in early 2024.
Eastman made progress on engineering, designing, permitting, and finalizing incentives for its third molecular recycling facility that is expected to be located in the United States.
On March 25, 2025, Hyundai Motor Group announced a significant investment of USD 21 billion in the United States from 2025 to 2028.
The commitment reflects the Group’s strategic focus on expanding its manufacturing capabilities, advancing future technologies, and enhancing energy infrastructure in America.
This latest U.S. investment builds on the Group’s existing allocation of approximately USD 20.5 billion since entering the U.S. market in 1986.

Hyundai Motor Group stated: “Hyundai Motor Group is deepening its partnership with the United States, reinforcing our shared vision for American industrial leadership. The Group’s investment and efforts will further expand our operations in the U.S. and grow our American workforce. Thank you to our American partners, employees, and communities. We’re proud to stand with you, and proud to build the future together.”

To reinforce its production capabilities, the Group will invest a total of USD 9 billion to establish an annual production capacity in the U.S. of 1.2 million vehicles across its automotive brands, Hyundai Motor, Kia, and Genesis.

The Group plans to invest in improving its production facilities, including Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama and Kia Autoland Georgia, to further enhance its customer-centric approach in delivering high-quality automobiles.

A total of USD 6 billion will be allocated to increase the localization rate of automotive components – including core parts for electric vehicles (EVs), such as battery packs – to form an auto cluster following expansion of the Group’s production facilities, as well as strengthening Group logistics to ensure robust supply chains and investing in steel production in the U.S.
Hyundai Steel, the Group’s steel affiliate, will construct an Electric Arc Furnace (EAF) steel mill in the state of Louisiana, capable of producing 2.7 million tons of steel annually.

This facility will produce low-carbon steel sheets using the abundant supply of steel scrap in the U.S. with the aim of enhancing the Group’s agility and flexibility in response to external uncertainties.

The Group will invest USD 6 billion to drive innovation and expand strategic partnerships with U.S. companies in areas including autonomous driving, robotics, artificial intelligence (AI), and advanced air mobility (AAM).

As part of its USD 6 billion commitment, the Group will also invest in energy infrastructure projects to secure new business opportunities and contribute to the development of sustainable energy generation.
Through these investments, the Group anticipates it will create 14,000 new direct full-time jobs in the U.S. by 2028.
The overall economic impact is expected to generate more than 100,000 direct and indirect job opportunities across related industries.

The Group plans to hold its Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America Grand Opening celebration in Georgia, U.S., later this week, marking the completion of the largest economic development project in Georgia’s history, just two and a half years after breaking ground.

HMGMA exemplifies HMG’s dedication to driving economic growth, furthering technological advancements, and delivering sustainable solutions to the U.S. market, creating or supporting over 570,000 jobs nationwide.
Hyundai Motor Group is a global enterprise that has created a value chain based on mobility, steel and construction, as well as logistics, finance, IT and service.
The Group employs about 250,000 people worldwide, with mobility brands including Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis.

links.context: Contact for Global PR Strategy & Planning

source_title: Hyundai Motor Group Commits to U.S. Growth with USD 21 Billion Investment

facts: Hyundai expanded its partnership with the H2GP Foundation to deliver the Hyundai Hydrogen RC Program to high school students in Bryan County.
Hyundai expanded its partnership with the SAE Foundation to deliver SAE International's award-winning A World In Motion (AWIM) PreK-12 STEM education program in the Bryan County School District.
Hyundai continued partnerships with the Georgia Historical Society and Coastal Empire Habitat for Humanity, donating funds to support education and housing affordability in Coastal Georgia.

These initiatives are part of Hyundai's corporate social responsibility program, Hyundai Hope, which seeks to uplift families and communities by fostering education, health, safety, and sustainability.
The Bryan County area is home to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA), Hyundai Motor Group's first dedicated electric vehicle mass-production plant.
Brent Stubbs, chief administrative officer of HMGMA, stated that Hyundai sees it as its responsibility to give back to local organizations creating positive change near the operating plant.
The expanded partnership with the H2GP Foundation will implement programming for a second year in Bryan County for the Hyundai RC Program.
The upcoming program year will feature 16 teams.
Two of Bryan County's teams recently competed at the H2GP World Finals.
Hyundai previously partnered with SAE International to implement the AWIM program in Bryan County fourth-grade classrooms.
The AWIM program will continue this year for over 800 fourth graders in the Bryan County School District.

Students will participate in the Skimmer challenge, designing and building fan-propelled paper sailboats while learning about air resistance, propulsion, surface area, teamwork, collaboration, and critical thinking.
The AWIM curriculum connects to HMGMA's renewable energy resources, emission reduction technologies, and career paths.
A donation to the Georgia Historical Society will support educational outreach programs and bolster resources for educators.

Dr. W. Todd Groce, president and CEO of the Georgia Historical Society, noted that Hyundai's generosity allows hundreds of thousands of Georgia students to study state and national history and supports events like the Georgia History Festival.

Brandon Ramirez, director of corporate social responsibility at Hyundai Motor North America, stated that expanding these partnerships aims to inspire career pathways in clean energy and the automotive industry.
Hyundai partnered with Coastal Empire Habitat for Humanity in 2023 to support affordable home ownership opportunities in the community.
The donation supports Habitat's mission of building homes, communities, and hope, aligning with Hyundai Hope's goal to enhance community health and quality of living.
Since 2022, Hyundai and its nonprofit organization Hyundai Hope on Wheels have donated over $6 million to local organizations and hospitals in Georgia.
Hyundai Motor America offers U.S. consumers a lineup of cars, SUVs, and electrified vehicles while supporting the Progress for Humanity vision.

Hyundai's significant U.S. operations include its North American headquarters in California, the Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama assembly plant, the all-new HMGMA, and several cutting-edge R&D facilities.
These operations, combined with those of Hyundai's 850 independent dealers, contribute $20.1 billion annually and 190,000 jobs to the U.S. economy according to a published economic impact report.

links.context: Hyundai Motor America news and updates

source_title: Hyundai Expands Education Initiatives in Georgia

facts: Hyundai Motor Group signed a 15-year power purchase agreement (PPA) with Matrix Renewables for its U.S. manufacturing facilities.
Matrix Renewables is an energy development company based in Madrid, Spain.
The Group will purchase 147 MW of generation capacity from Matrix Renewables’ Stillhouse Solar Project in Bell County, Texas.
The Stillhouse Solar Project has a total generation capacity of 210 MW.
Through this project, the Group expects to secure approximately 378 GWh of electricity annually.
The initiative anticipates a yearly reduction of 140,000 tons of carbon emissions.
The secured solar energy will support sustainable manufacturing at several Group-affiliated plants in the U.S., including the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
HMGMA is a factory dedicated to electric vehicles (EVs).
Other supported U.S. plants supply parts, steel plates, and sheets for the Group’s EVs.
Affiliates collectively joining the agreement include HMGMA, Hyundai Mobis North America Electrified Powertrain LLC, Hyundai Steel Georgia Inc., and Hyundai Transys Georgia Powertrain Inc.
These affiliates will strengthen a sustainable supply chain with renewable energy from parts to vehicles.
Starting with this PPA, the Group will undertake investments and contracts for renewable energy conversion at its U.S. facilities in 2024.
The Group plans to convert to renewable energy at its manufacturing facilities around the globe through collaboration with diverse parties.
HMGMA is Hyundai Motor Group’s first dedicated EV mass-production plant located in Bryan County, Georgia.
HMGMA will start producing EVs for automotive affiliates, including Hyundai Motor Company and Kia Corporation, in the fourth quarter of 2024.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group Secures Renewable Energy for Its U.S. Manufacturing Facilities

entity_name: Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System, LLC
canonical_name: Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System, LLC
facts: Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System, LLC will invest $9 million in expanding their seating manufacturing operations in West Point, Georgia.
The expansion will create more than 150 jobs in Troup County.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the investment and stated that Georgia's highly skilled workforce continues to attract manufacturing jobs in every corner of the state.
Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System manufactures seats and seat foam pads for the Hyundai Santa Fe, KIA Sorento, and other models.
In addition to providing seating structures for automobile manufacturers, the company also supplies seats to leading electric vehicle makers.
Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System President and CEO Sangkil Jung said they are excited for the new opportunity ahead as they expand their presence in the West Point community.
The expansion will take Hyundai TRANSYS’ employment total in Georgia to more than 1,890 jobs.
Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System is continuing to hire for careers in production seat assembly, foam, quality, and logistics.

Kevin Patrick, Chairman of the West Point Development Authority, thanked Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System for its continued investment and noted that automotive and existing industries are the backbone of local economic success.

Senior Project Manager Amanda Fields represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce division on this competitive project in partnership with the West Point Development Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that contributing more than $3 billion to Georgia’s economy, the state is committed to growing Georgia’s robust automotive ecosystem and creating solid manufacturing jobs of the future.

Wilson extended thanks to partners at local, state, and federal levels who have worked to foster Georgia’s pro-business environment, which has helped give automotive partners confidence to continue investing in the state.
Georgia is home to more than 200 automotive-related facilities and remains a national leader in advanced manufacturing.
Georgia is outpacing the United States in 10-year GDP growth in the manufacture of products including machinery, electrical equipment and components, and fabricated metals.
Hyundai TRANSYS is an automotive parts company that produces critical automobile parts, including transmissions, axles, and seats.
It is the only company in the world specializing in transmissions.
The company produces all types of automatic transmissions, manual transmissions, dual clutch transmissions (DCT), and continuously variable transmissions.
Hyundai TRANSYS also develops and produces top quality seats for Genesis, Santa Fe, Sorento, and other models.
source_title: Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System, LLC Expands Manufacturing Operations in Georgia | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Anduril Industries will invest $60 million in a new manufacturing and research facility in Atlanta.
The investment will create more than 180 new jobs in Fulton County over the next three years.
Anduril Industries is a defense technology company that specializes in building advanced technology for US and allied militaries.

Governor Kemp stated that Georgia is well established as a leader in aerospace and defense due to its top research universities, colleges, technical colleges, and large active military and veteran populations.
Anduril’s new Atlanta office will be home to Area-I, a Georgia-based manufacturer of advanced unmanned aircraft systems.
Anduril acquired Area-I in 2021, and the company operates as a wholly owned subsidiary of Anduril.
The new facility is located at 1435 Hills Place Northwest in Atlanta.

The facility includes more than 180,000 square feet of industrial buildings that are being transformed into mixed-use office, research and development, manufacturing, and production space across two buildings.
Area-I is hiring for a variety of engineering disciplines including aerospace, mechanical, software, electrical, and systems backgrounds for research and development.
The increased space for production will enable more hires for skilled mechanical and avionics technicians.
Matt Grimm, Chief Operating Officer of Anduril, stated that Atlanta has a highly skilled and educated workforce in aerospace, engineering, and robotics.
Matt Grimm noted that growing their presence in Georgia will allow the company to attract world-class talent to support its mission to transform defense capabilities for the US and its allies.
Aerospace products are Georgia’s top export at $9.19 billion in 2021.
The aerospace sector is the state’s second largest manufacturing industry, generating a $57.5 billion economic impact.
Georgia is home to more than 800 aerospace companies.
Georgia maintains a robust aerospace education pipeline with a dozen high schools that offer training in the field.
There are five technical colleges in Georgia with aviation programs.
Several universities in Georgia offer degrees in aerospace engineering.
Atlanta Mayor Andre Dickens stated that Atlanta continues to attract global companies looking to fuel their growth through its highly skilled, diverse tech talent and innovation ecosystem.

Robb Pitts, Chairman of the Fulton County Board of Commissioners, called the Anduril Industries announcement a big deal that speaks to the diversity of people, communities, and industries in Fulton County.

Metro Atlanta Chamber President and CEO Katie Kirkpatrick stated that Anduril is a welcome addition to the region's thriving community of knowledge-based businesses and will benefit from the innovative cluster of software-, hardware-, and engineering-driven businesses in metro Atlanta.
Project Manager Kasey Smith represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was completed in partnership with Select Fulton, Metro Atlanta Chamber, Invest Atlanta, the Georgia Center of Innovation’s Aerospace industry team, and Georgia Power.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Anduril Industries is a fantastic fit for Georgia’s aerospace and advanced manufacturing ecosystems.
Area-I has over a decade of history with the Georgia Center of Innovation and is a longstanding member of its Unmanned Systems Working Group.
Anduril Industries’ family of systems is powered by Lattice OS, an AI-powered operating system that turns thousands of data streams into a realtime, 3D command and control center.
Anduril is committed to bringing cutting-edge AI, computer vision, sensor fusion, and networking technology to the military in months, not years.
source_title: Anduril Industries to Invest $60M, Create 180 New Jobs in Fulton | Georgia

facts: Kia Georgia team members marked another historic day at its West Point facility on Tuesday, Feb. 24, 2026, with the start of production of the all-new 2027 Kia Telluride.
The ceremonial vehicle represented the first hybrid electric vehicle (HEV) assembled in the state of Georgia.
The same vehicle marked the 5 millionth vehicle produced at Kia Georgia.
The milestone ceremony was attended by Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp, team members, and state/community leaders.
Sean Yoon, President and CEO of Kia North America and Kia America, stated that Kia Georgia is the only plant in the world that builds the Telluride.
The facility has reached five million vehicles produced and introduced Kia's first US-assembled HEV.
Since beginning production in 2009, Kia Georgia has grown into one of the region's largest employers and a cornerstone of automotive manufacturing in the Southeast.
The Telluride is one of the industry's most awarded vehicles and serves as a flagship model for the brand.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp noted that the Telluride has been exclusively made in Georgia since its introduction in 2019.
The governor attributed the vehicle's success to a partnership approach between Kia Georgia, the local community, and the state.
Stuart Countess, Kia Georgia's President and CEO, emphasized that producing world-class gasoline-powered vehicles, EVs, and HEVs demonstrates the plant's versatility and talent.
Kia Georgia, Inc. is the first manufacturing site in North America for Kia Corporation, which is based in Seoul, Korea.
Kia Georgia has an annual production capacity of 350,000 units.
The facility is located on 2,200 acres in West Point, Ga.
Kia Georgia began mass production on Nov. 16, 2009.
Kia Georgia produces the Telluride, Sorento (excludes HEV and PHEV models), and Sportage (excludes HEV and PHEV models) SUVs.
Kia Georgia also produces the all-electric EV9 and EV6 vehicles.
source_title: Kia Georgia celebrates trio of major milestones, including Start of Production for 2027 Kia Telluride

facts: Passenger Car vehicle type includes Sedans, Coupes, SUV and Hatchback with a GVWR from Class 1: 6,000 lb or less (2,722 kg or less) to Class 2H: 9,001 - 10,000 lb (4,082 - 4,536 kg).
Truck vehicle type includes Pick Up, SUV with a GVWR from Class 1: 6,000 lb or less (2,722 kg or less) to Class 2H: 9,001 - 10,000 lb (4,082 - 4,536 kg).
Multipurpose Passenger Vehicle (MPV) vehicle type includes Minivan, SUV with a GVWR from Class 1: 6,000 lb or less (2,722 kg or less) to Class 2: 6,001 - 10,000 lb (2,722 - 4,536 kg).

facts: The Middle West Tennessee Clean Fuels Coalition is promoting fuel safety trainings throughout the state to increase safety and security in alternative fuel vehicle adoption.
The coalition is conducting these trainings in partnership with the National Fire Protection Association.
Additional information about the project can be obtained by contacting Middle-West Tennessee Clean Fuels.
More videos related to the initiative are provided by Clean Cities and Communities and FuelEconomy.gov.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Training Builds Safety and Security Into Alternative Transportation

facts: On May 21, 2014, Georgia Foreign-Trade Zone, Inc., grantee of FTZ 26, submitted a notification of proposed production activity to the Foreign-Trade Zones (FTZ) Board on behalf of Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corporation.
The proposed production activity is located within Subzone 26L in Rome, Jonesboro and Cartersville, Georgia.
The notification was processed in accordance with the regulations of the FTZ Board (15 CFR part 400), including notice in the Federal Register inviting public comment (79 FR 33723, 6-12-2014).
The FTZ Board determined that no further review of the activity is warranted at this time.
The production activity described in the notification is authorized, subject to the FTZ Act and the FTZ Board's regulations, including Section 400.14.
The authorization notice was dated September 22, 2014.
Elizabeth Whiteman, Acting Executive Secretary, signed the authorization notice.
source_title:
Federal Register
       :: 
      Authorization of Production Activity; Foreign-Trade Subzone 26L, Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corporation, (All-Terrain Vehicles), Rome, Jonesboro and Cartersville, Georgia

facts: The Southerfield Road Industrial Park has earned “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development” (GRAD) certification with “Select” status.
The site’s new “GRAD Select” status provides an additional seal of approval that makes it especially attractive for future industrial development.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Sumter County’s Southerfield Road Industrial Park represents another strong example of a GRAD-certified site helping strengthen Georgia’s competitiveness for new investment.

Pat Wilson noted that through deliberate planning and collaboration at the local level, sites like this support the state’s ability to attract high-quality industrial projects and ensure communities are prepared to meet the needs of expanding industries across Georgia.
The 136± acre industrial park is located north-northeast of downtown Americus in Sumter County, Georgia.
The site is bordered to the north by the Ted Baldwin Business and Technology Park.
The site is bordered to the east by Southerfield Road.
The site is bordered to the south by residential, municipal, and agricultural uses.
The site is bordered to the west by a Sumter County school system campus and additional agricultural and residential areas.
Jimmy Carter Regional Airport, with a 6,000-foot runway, is located just east of the site across Southerfield Road.
US 19 is approximately 3.7 miles west of the site.
Interstate 75 is about 33 miles east via US 280, which provides east-west access south of the site.
Rusty Warner, Executive Director of the Sumter County Development Authority, stated that GRAD certification reinforces the work being done to position Sumter County for long-term success.
Rusty Warner noted the goal has always been to remove barriers, strengthen sites, and make it easier for companies to choose this community with confidence.

Rusty Warner added that completing this level of due diligence improves competitiveness and shows planning ahead and investing in growth that brings quality jobs and lasting opportunity to the county.

David Baldwin, Chairman of the Sumter County Board of Commissioners, stated that GRAD certification is an important accomplishment for Sumter County because it reflects steady, responsible planning committed to for years.
David Baldwin noted the long-term goal has always been to prepare sites by doing due diligence up front so companies know the community is serious, ready, and able to support new jobs.

David Baldwin added that the certification strengthens their position, aligns with a vision for sustainable growth, and helps ensure Sumter County remains competitive for opportunities that will shape its future.
Completing the GRAD certification program with GDEcD is a proactive way for community planners and landowners to help catalyze economic growth and industrial development in their regions.

To qualify for GRAD status, available sites must meet the program’s due diligence standards, be reviewed by a third party, and earn final approval from a board of advisors comprised of public and private sector economic development professionals.
The “GRAD Select” status is an indication that a site has met or exceeded more rigorous certification requirements.
The GRAD Program offers more than 70 industrial certified sites that are ready for fast-track industrial projects thanks to comprehensive due diligence.
Georgia is investing over $21 million to help rural communities prepare new industrial sites through the Rural Site Development Initiative.
These grants support site improvements and the addition of new GRAD-certified sites.

links.context: Aerial views and property details for GRAD-certified sites

source_title: Southerfield Road Industrial Park Earns ‘Georgia Ready for Accelerated | Georgia

facts: Southern Company Services (SCS) and TerraPower built and installed a new test facility at TerraPower’s laboratory in Everett, Washington.
The Integrated Effects Test (IET) is the largest chloride salt system in the world.
The IET will be instrumental in helping to develop the team’s Molten Chloride Fast Reactor (MCFR) technology.
The installation of the IET was part of a seven-year, $76 million cost-shared project with the U.S. Department of Energy to further develop the MCFR system.
TerraPower and SCS plan to demonstrate the reactor in the early 2030s.
The IET is a multi-loop test facility that builds off of a series of smaller testing campaigns to inform its design.
The non-nuclear system is heated by an external power source and will be used to help validate thermal hydraulics and safety analysis codes needed to demonstrate molten salt reactor systems.
The IET supports the development of the Molten Chloride Reactor Experiment at Idaho National Laboratory, which will be the world’s first fast spectrum salt reactor.
The MCRE is being funded through DOE’s Advanced Reactor Demonstration Program.

Jeff Latkowski, TerraPower’s senior vice president of innovation programs, stated that the completion and installation of the IET is an important step to advancing TerraPower’s Molten Chloride Fast Reactor technology.
Latkowski noted that the MCFR will play a pivotal role in decarbonizing heavy industries.
Latkowski expressed pride in working with Southern Company, CORE POWER, and other partners to develop systems necessary to bring new reactors to market.

Dr. Mark S. Berry, Southern Company Services senior vice president of R&D, stated that Southern Company’s research and development program is committed to advancing next-generation nuclear as part of a diverse technology portfolio supporting the goal of a net-zero future for customers.
Berry noted they are honored to engage with TerraPower, the Department of Energy, and other team members to further this goal through the Integrated Effects Test.
CORE POWER, EPRI, Idaho National Laboratory, Oak Ridge National Laboratory, and Vanderbilt University all contributed to the IET project.
Southern Company and TerraPower are working together to develop a molten chloride fast reactor that uses liquid salts as both a coolant and fuel.
This design allows the reactor to operate at high temperatures to produce heat or clean power more efficiently than today’s reactors.
The team received a $45 million Advanced Reactor Concepts Award in 2015 from DOE to help advance liquid-fueled molten salt reactor technologies.
The goal of the 2015 award was to help re-establish expertise and research infrastructure that had been stalled since the 1960s with the termination of ORNL’s Molten Salt Reactor Experiment.
Major achievements under the award included more than 60 separate effects tests that led to the construction of the IET and the successful scale-up of its salt manufacturing processes.
Over the next few years, the project team will carry out the Integrated Effects Test to learn more about the fast-spectrum salt reactor and work toward the development and demonstration of the MCFR.
The Integrated Effects Test is a unique and flexible asset that will be utilized to help train a new crop of molten salt project personnel.

Southern Company's research and development organization and TerraPower will work with universities across the country to submit research proposals through DOE’s Nuclear Energy University Program to train students.
The university program will also support research into additional instruments, equipment, and components compatible with molten salt systems.
source_title: Southern Company Services and TerraPower Build World’s Largest Chloride Salt System | Department of Energy

facts: Amara Industries, a parent company to the Gracious Living group of companies, announced the acquisition of Thomson Plastics on Dec. 9, 2025.
Thomson Plastics is a recognized leader in large tonnage injection molding in the Southeastern United States.
Thomson Plastics currently operates from two locations in Georgia and North Carolina.
The strategic acquisition enhances Amara Industries' manufacturing footprint, automation capabilities, and technical depth to a total of five manufacturing facilities.
Thomson Plastics has clamping force capacities up to 3,700 tons.
Thirty percent of Thomson Plastics' fleet exceeds 1,000 tons in clamping force capacity.
Thomson Plastics is supported by advanced robotics and automation to deliver consistent performance across high-volume production environments.

Thomson Plastics' technical portfolio includes injection molding, gas-assisted molding, over-molding, insert molding, in-mold labeling, robotic part picking, pad printing, and heat transfer decorating.
Vince Orlando, Co-President at Amara Industries, stated that the addition of Thomson Plastics represents a strategic milestone for the organization.
The acquisition is a significant development in the North American injection molding industry.
The unified company will meet rising demand for high-performance molded products in key sectors including automotive, consumer goods, industrial, and commercial markets.
The expansion into the Southeastern United States strengthens Amara Industries' ability to provide customers with faster delivery, greater flexibility, and improved supply chain efficiency.
Amara Industries is a provider of comprehensive custom manufacturing solutions with a dedicated team of over 500 employees.
Amara Industries offers end-to-end capabilities spanning design, product development, engineering, and materials science.
Amara Industries specializes in injection molding, assembly, profile extrusion, decorating, and material compounding.
Amara Industries holds key certifications including ISO, IATF, and CTPAT.
source_title: Amara Industries Acquires Thomson Plastics

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy’s Office of Energy Dominance Financing issued a conditional commitment for a loan of up to $263 million to SHINE Chrysalis, LLC.
The loan supports the construction of Chrysalis, a high-volume medical isotope production facility in Janesville, Wisconsin.
The production facility will create a supply of reliable and secure medical isotopes made with fusion and fission technology.
Millions of patients rely on medical isotopes for life-saving diagnostic imaging and cancer treatments, but most of that supply comes from overseas with limited capacity to meet demand.
Utilizing fusion and fission technology, the facility will establish the only domestic commercial supply of molybdenum-99 (Mo-99).
Mo-99 is a life-saving medical isotope essential for diagnostic imaging; its decay product of technetium-99m (Tc-99m) is used in over 40,000 medical procedures in the United States each day.

EDF Director Gregory A. Beard said the SHINE Chrysalis project is vital to improving the nuclear supply chain and contributing to a strong next-generation nuclear workforce while onshoring critical production and improving national security.

Beard stated that using EDF’s loan authority to further commercialize a project long supported by DOE aligns with Trump’s policy of ensuring a reliable and secure domestic supply chain while lowering costs.

Thanks to President Trump and the Working Families Tax Cut, the announcement highlights the Energy Department’s role in advancing President Trump’s Executive Order, Reinvigorating the Nuclear Industrial Base.
The executive order aims to maximize the use of uranium through an innovative recycling process.
The project will support hundreds of construction and operation jobs, contributing to a strong next-generation American nuclear workforce.
SHINE has developed and demonstrated its technology through significant support from the National Nuclear Security Administration (NNSA) over the last 16 years.
With NNSA funding, the National Laboratories also played a key role in developing SHINE’s technology.

Dr. Matthew Napoli, NNSA’s Deputy Administrator for Defense Nuclear Nonproliferation (DNN), said SHINE is the key to ending reliance on imports of foreign-produced Mo-99 and ensuring U.S. patients have reliable access to American-made medical isotopes.

Napoli noted that NNSA’s leadership made the concept a reality by providing funding and technical support from national laboratories, enabling the company to go from an idea on paper to a commercial facility that is 75% complete.

The EDF conditional loan will get the project across the finish line, and SHINE’s market entry will be a major win for American nuclear medicine, fusion technology, and nuclear nonproliferation leadership.
EDF is committed to financing American energy and manufacturing projects that meaningfully contribute to U.S. energy security, grid reliability, and lowering costs for all Americans.
EDF empowers the private sector to invest in the future, help win the AI race, strengthen American industry, and restore American Energy Dominance.

While the conditional commitment indicates DOE’s intent to provide a loan, DOE and the company must satisfy certain technical, legal, environmental, and financial conditions before entering into definitive financing documents and funding the loan.
source_title: DOE’s Office of Energy Dominance Financing Announces Conditional Commitment for a Domestic Medical Isotope Manufacturing Facility | Department of Energy

entity_name: Tesla Global Engineering and AI Headquarters
canonical_name: Tesla Global Engineering and AI Headquarters
facts: Governor Gavin Newsom joined Tesla CEO Elon Musk on a tour of Tesla’s new global engineering and AI headquarters in Palo Alto.
Since 2009, California has supported Tesla to help create the future of the zero-emission vehicle (ZEV) market.
California will remain the home of Tesla as their new global engineering and AI headquarters works to develop the technology of the future.

Governor Gavin Newsom stated: “The future happens in California first. We’re changing the world through our historic investments, our conveyor belt for talent, and partnerships with companies like Tesla. The state continues to be the world’s innovation hub, charging the electric vehicle revolution, and dominating the industry in every category – all while keeping the same goal in mind: ensuring a cleaner, greener, and healthier place to live for future generations.”
California leads the nation’s ZEV market with nearly 1.4 million ZEVs sold as of December 2022, representing 40 percent of all ZEVs sold in the U.S.
California leads the nation in ZEV infrastructure, with 80,027 shared electric vehicle chargers and 63 hydrogen stations.
The state is home to 55 ZEV-related manufacturing companies, more than any other state.
Success of California’s programs has led to ZEVs becoming a top export and has spurred major advances in manufacturing and job creation.
California has the most high-quality, diverse workforce in the nation, including more engineers, scientists, researchers, and Nobel laureates than anywhere else in the nation.
source_title: Governor Newsom Welcomes New Tesla Global Engineering and AI HQ in California | Governor of California
