facts: Ericsson, Amazon Web Services (AWS), and Hitachi America R&D enabled a private 5G infrastructure trial at Hitachi Astemo Americas' electric motor vehicle manufacturing plant in Berea, Kentucky, USA.

The collaboration focuses on real-time digital video, artificial intelligence (AI), and edge-to-cloud technologies across a private 5G wireless network to transform manufacturing and improve productivity, efficiency, environmental impact, safety, and cost reduction.

The solution leverages Ericsson Private 5G side by side with the AWS Snow Family to provide foundational private cellular networks for establishing machine learning (ML) models within the Hitachi manufacturing complex.

Real-time video of a component assembly operation producing motor components for electric vehicles was fed across the Ericsson private 5G network to an AWS Snowball Edge device running Hitachi video analytics to detect defects earlier and reduce wasted material and lost production.
The trial installation using 5G wireless was completed in three days by setting up cameras on a live production assembly line.
Computer vision running on the private 5G network simultaneously inspected 24 assembly components, compared with one-by-one inspection using conventional approaches.
High-definition 4K cameras enabled the computer vision configuration to observe defects at the sub-millimeter level, providing far greater visibility than the human eye.
The high throughput and low latency of 5G was key to uploading huge volumes of video data from the cameras to an AWS Snow Family device for analysis to streamline decision-making.

Ericsson Private 5G is built on Ericsson's 4G and 5G radio and dual mode core technology, providing secure and reliable connectivity through a single server dual mode core pre-integrated for rapid time to service.
The combination of private 5G, cloud, and artificial intelligence/machine learning automated technologies has the potential to revolutionize manufacturing processes.

The trial demonstrated that the combination of 5G and cloud technologies is now making full-scale, global deployment of digital production line applications viable, including defect detection, quality inspection, robotics automation, real-time machine control, and augmented reality.

Ericsson hosted a webinar on Thursday, August 24, 2023, exploring the full deployment and highlighting how private 5G serves as a reliable, flexible, high-performing network for manufacturing use cases involving AI and machine learning automation models.

Hitachi Astemo Americas manufactures and markets engine management, electric powertrain, and integrated vehicle controls for major automotive manufacturers worldwide, adhering to ISO/TS:16949:2009 industry standards.

Thomas Noren, Head of PCN Commercial and Operations at Ericsson, stated that the collaboration demonstrates solutions deployable today in manufacturing and enterprise environments to deliver early adopter competitive advantages.

Sudhanshu Gaur, Vice President of R&D for Hitachi America and Chief Architect at Hitachi Astemo Americas, noted that the team explored and validated new use cases enabled by private 5G to show how smart factories can already function.

Chris McKenna, Global Lead for Private Wireless at AWS, explained that using the Ericsson Private 5G Network product and running AI/ML models on an AWS Snow Family device demonstrated a reliable and secure connection to run machine vision inferences on-site to detect defects earlier.
source_title: Ericsson works with AWS and Hitachi America R&D to showcase smart factory potential

facts: Battery separator manufacturer Hollingsworth & Vose announced on April 5 that COO Josh Ayer had been chosen to succeed Val Hollingsworth as the company’s CEO.
Hollingsworth is stepping down after 24 years in the post — and after more than 40 years with the company — but will continue as chair of H&V’s board.
Ayer joined H&V in 2009 as vice president and managing director for the Asia Pacific region, following 10 years at General Electric.
In 2015, Ayer became president of the engine and industrial filtration division, taking on the position of vice president and managing director of H&V’s Americas region in 2020.
Val Hollingsworth is a sixth-generation member of the Hollingsworth family. He first joined the company in 1976, working as a night shift supervisor and a production and plant manager.
Hollingsworth became president in 1997 and CEO in the following year.
During this time, H&V expanded from a mainly regional US business into a global company with 13 R&D and manufacturing facilities across three continents.
“We are delighted to be able to turn to Josh to lead H&V in our next chapter of growth and development,” Hollingsworth said.
“He has been an outstanding leader in his years at H&V and will do a great job as CEO.”
H&V has had several non-Hollingsworth family CEOs in its long history, and we are proud that there are seventh-generation members of our family working for H&V.

facts: Hollingsworth & Vose Company has been awarded the 2026 BCI Innovation Award by Battery Council International for its development of a unique glass fibrous matrix electrode.
The innovation is known as Glass Electrode Material (GEM).
GEM helps generate and support a unique microporous structure within the lead active mass of a battery to enhance chargeability performance and cycle life reliability without impacting water loss.

Roger Miksad, President and Executive Director of BCI, stated that battery innovation is thriving and H&V is a great example of technological leadership and product evolution that advances the capabilities of the storied lead battery.
For roughly two centuries, H&V has supported the economy of the future.
GEM technology improves recharge efficiency through enhanced charge acceptance, reducing fuel-related emissions while improving battery performance, durability, and cycle life reliability.
GEM has approximately three times higher Dynamic Charge Acceptance (DCA).
GEM enables next-generation start-stop systems that supports fuel economy gains of up to 5%.
The technology's fast recharge response makes it well suited for advanced 12-V architectures in hybrid (HEV, PHEV) and electric vehicle auxiliary systems.
The technology platform is applicable across multiple energy storage applications, including automotive batteries, motive power for forklifts, and stationary backup power solutions.
Hollingsworth & Vose developed GEM in partnership with ArcActive.

Nicolas Clement (R&D Director) and Dr. Amogh Karwa (Director of Product management) stated that GEM technology has true potential for creating a more sustainable future and will be a great tool for battery manufacturers for enhanced performance to enable lead batteries to compete better with alternative chemistries.
East Penn Manufacturing and Silicon Joule received Honorable Mention recognition for their joint work on the first commercial bi-pole battery based on lead chemistry and treated silicon wafers.
Battery Council International annually presents this award to celebrate innovation in equipment, processes, services, and products that advance the lead battery industry.
The award was established to honor industry thought leader Sally Breidegam Miksiewicz, the former CEO of East Penn Manufacturing Co.

BCI is the leading trade association representing the global battery industry, founded in 1924, advocating for battery manufacturers and recyclers, marketers and retailers, suppliers of raw materials and equipment, and battery distributors.

H&V is a global manufacturer of advanced materials used in filtration, battery, and industrial applications, headquartered in East Walpole, Massachusetts, U.S.A., and family-owned for seven generations.

New prices for filtration media will be effective with shipments starting July 1, 2013 or as contracts allow.
The increases will vary by product and region and will range from 3% to 5%.
H&V remains committed to providing its customers with the best combination of products, quality, consistency, value and support despite market fluctuations.
Established in 1843, Hollingsworth & Vose is a family-run company and a global leader in the supply of advanced materials for filtration, battery separator and industrial applications.
The company operates manufacturing sites and research centers in the Americas, Europe and Asia.

facts: Hollingsworth & Vose Company has been awarded the 2026 BCI Innovation Award by Battery Council International for its development of a unique glass fibrous matrix electrode.

The innovation, known as Glass Electrode Material (GEM), helps generate and support a unique microporous structure within the lead active mass of a battery to enhance chargeability performance and cycle life reliability without impacting water loss.
Roger Miksad, President and Executive Director of BCI, stated that battery innovation is thriving and H&V is a great example of technological leadership advancing lead battery capabilities.
GEM technology improves recharge efficiency through enhanced charge acceptance, reducing fuel-related emissions while improving battery performance, durability, and cycle life reliability.
GEM has approximately three times higher Dynamic Charge Acceptance (DCA).
GEM enables next-generation start-stop systems that support fuel economy gains of up to 5%.
The technology's fast recharge response makes it well suited for advanced 12-V architectures in hybrid (HEV, PHEV) and electric vehicle auxiliary systems.
The technology platform is applicable across multiple energy storage applications, including automotive batteries, motive power for forklifts, and stationary backup power solutions.
Hollingsworth & Vose developed GEM in partnership with ArcActive.

Nicolas Clement (R&D Director) and Dr. Amogh Karwa (Director of Product Management) stated that GEM technology has true potential for creating a more sustainable future and will be a great tool for battery manufacturers to enhance performance and enable lead batteries to compete better with alternative chemistries.
East Penn Manufacturing and Silicon Joule received Honorable Mention recognition for their joint work on the first commercial bi-pole battery based on lead chemistry and treated silicon wafers.
The BCI Innovation Award is presented annually to celebrate innovation in equipment, processes, services, and products that advance the lead battery industry.
The award was established to honor industry thought leader Sally Breidegam Miksiewicz, former CEO of East Penn Manufacturing Co.
Battery Council International (BCI) is the leading trade association representing the global battery industry, founded in 1924.

H&V is a global manufacturer of advanced materials used in filtration, battery, and industrial applications, headquartered in East Walpole, Massachusetts, U.S.A., and family-owned for seven generations.

facts: Hollingsworth & Vose is the oldest company in Tugboat Institute membership.
The company was founded nearly 300 years ago.
In its early days, they made paper for customers including Ben Franklin and Paul Revere.
Today, they are a global leader in filtration and energy storage systems.
Their customers include Tesla and Elon Musk.
Val Hollingsworth is the 7th generation leader and current Board Chair of Hollingsworth & Vose.

Val Hollingsworth shares highlights from his family’s company’s incredible journey and gives insight into the spirit of innovation that has driven the company since its founding in a Tugboat Institute talk.

facts: Honda offers a Co-op & Internship Program providing students with hands-on experience and exposure to Engineering and Business operations.
Honda provides Entry Level & Recent Graduates programs launching careers within immersive programs focusing on Accounting or Engineering.
Honda hires for Production, Technicians & Warehousing roles including Assembly, Skilled Maintenance and Technicians, Parts Distribution, and Materials Handling.
Honda has Design & Engineering roles creating products that enhance mobility and daily lives through Creative Design and Engineering.
Honda offers Supply Chain roles in Logistics, Distribution, Procurement, and Warehousing.
Honda employs Manufacturing Engineers ensuring quality, safety, and reliability through Manufacturing, Process, and Production engineering.
Honda has Sales & Marketing roles in Field, Operations, Advertising, Marketing, and Product Planning.
Honda has Information Technology roles focusing on Applications, Infrastructure, and Systems.
Honda has Corporate Operations roles in Accounting, Finance, Communications, HR, Legal, and Credit Union.
The company's culture is described as being permeated by a sense of mutual respect and mindfulness.
Honda offers medical, dental, and vision coverage through its Total Rewards program to support employees and their families at every stage.
A job listing for a Manufacturing Equipment Sr. Engineer position is located in Tallapoosa, GA (zip code 30176).

facts: The facility spans 89,000 square feet.
The facility is located in Bryan County, Georgia.
The facility can train 824 workers simultaneously for electric and hybrid vehicle production.
The facility uses simulation lines and high-voltage labs to prepare employees for Hyundai's advanced manufacturing processes.
EV assembly demands navigating high-voltage systems, automated processes, and digital quality controls that differ from traditional combustion engine factories.
The Hyundai Mobility Training Centre was unveiled on November 5th in Ellabell.
It is Georgia's first purpose-built EV training facility.
The centre is positioned adjacent to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America in Bryan County.
Trainees are prepared for roles at the Metaplant and its network of affiliate companies.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia's focus remains on bringing quality jobs to hardworking Georgians and connecting them to necessary skills amid record-breaking economic development statewide.
source_title: Hyundai's new EV training facility in Georgia: Boosting workforce for electric and hybrid vehicle production

facts: Georgia Tech will oversee nine projects within the larger Georgia AI Manufacturing (GA-AIM) technology corridor project.
The GA-AIM project aims to transform the Advanced Manufacturing Pilot Facility (AMPF) into the Artificial Intelligence Manufacturing Pilot Facility (AI-MPF).
Georgia Tech received a new $65 million grant from the U.S. Department of Commerce’s Economic Development Administration for the initiative.
The 24,000-square-foot AI-MPF facility will serve as a flagship component of the Georgia Tech Manufacturing Institute (GTMI).
The facility will serve as a testbed where basic research results are scaled up and translated into implementable technologies, coupled with education, and workforce training.
Targeted fields of interest for the project include additive/hybrid manufacturing, composites, digital manufacturing/Industry 4.0, and industrial robotics.
source_title: The Crest of the Wave: Advanced and Additive Manufacturing - Expansion Solutions

entity_name: Honda Development & Manufacturing of America, LLC (HDMA)
canonical_name: Honda Development & Manufacturing of America, LLC
facts: Honda is seeking qualified individuals with diverse backgrounds to join its team through the Engineering Development Program (EDP) targeting 2026 graduates.
The EDP is administered by Honda Development & Manufacturing of America, LLC (HDMA).
Over the last 25 years, HDMA's EDP has served as a critical program for developing entry-level engineers.
The program duration is one to two years and requires participants to rotate through various departments and roles throughout HDMA.
Successful graduates of the EDP will return to a designated "Home" department role after completing their rotations.
Program openings are available across multiple locations, including Tallapoosa, Georgia.
Quality Engineering positions are explicitly available in Ohio and Georgia.

Manufacturing Engineers lead, create, and implement technical activities and solutions across manufacturing operations while monitoring equipment and processes to ensure optimal performance and minimize operating expenses.

Equipment/Tooling Design Engineers design manufacturing equipment including stamping, hardware & software, and die & mold to ensure efficiency and stability for new models and technology introduction.

Materials Development Engineers focus on researching and developing resins, fabrics, and other non-metal materials within the Organics Group, specifically seeking materials or chemical engineering majors.
Safety Engineers apply scientific and practical knowledge to innovate, design, and improve systems, processes, equipment, and tools to align with daily operations, new models, and business plans.

Data Analytics Engineers review multiple data sources to evaluate, summarize, and draw conclusions identifying trends that lead to countermeasure activity across quality, delivery, safety, and cost information.
Applicants must hold a bachelor's degree from an ABET or ATMAE accredited program in Mechanical, Electrical, Industrial, or other equivalent engineering fields.
Candidates are preferred to have 3 months of co-op or internship experience in a relevant environment.
Eligible candidates must have less than 2 years of engineering work experience.
Honda does not provide sponsorship for employment visa status, including F-1 CPT/OPT, H-1B, and TN visas.
Compensation includes a competitive base salary based on variables like geographic location and work experience, paid overtime, and regional bonuses when applicable.
Benefits include industry-leading medical, dental, vision, and prescription plans, paid time off for vacation, holidays, and shutdowns, and company-paid short-term and long-term disability.
The company offers a 401K plan with a company match plus an additional contribution, along with relocation assistance if eligible.
Career growth offerings include advancement opportunities, career mobility, education reimbursement for continued learning, and training and development programs.

Additional employee offerings include tuition assistance, student loan repayment, lifestyle accounts, childcare reimbursement, elder care support, wellbeing programs, community service engagement, product programs, and free drinks onsite.
Honda's corporate vision targets 2030 goals focused on innovation, achieving a "zero environmental footprint," and realizing the "joy and freedom of mobility."
source_title: Engineering Development Program – 2026 Graduates – Career Development | NAU College of Engineering

facts: Hyundai announced a $2.7 billion expansion at its Georgia megasite two weeks after an immigration raid detained 475 workers.
The September 4, 2025 raid at the Hyundai-LG battery plant in Ellabell, Georgia was the largest single-location immigration raid in U.S. history.
The raid detained over 300 South Korean nationals and sparked diplomatic tensions between Washington and Seoul under Trump’s second term.
Federal agents descended on the Ellabell site with military equipment, helicopters, and ATVs during the raid.
Some detained workers held valid work permits according to leaked documents.
The Hyundai-LG battery plant opening was pushed from 2025 to 2026 due to the raid.
Seoul sent a charter plane to bring its citizens home following the detention.
Hyundai confirmed it will stick with the expansion plan first revealed at the plant’s March grand opening.
The expansion adds 200,000 units of annual production capacity over the next three years.
Total production capacity will jump from 300,000 to 500,000 vehicles annually by 2028.
The plant will build 10 different electric and hybrid models by 2028, up from two currently rolling off the line.
The expansion includes a new mid-sized pickup truck for Hyundai.
Hyundai currently employs 3,129 people at the Ellabell site.
Hyundai commits to reaching 8,500 workers by 2031, adding 3,000 new jobs.
Georgia promises $2.1 billion in tax incentives, which Hyundai must unlock by reaching the 8,500 worker threshold by 2031.
By 2030, Hyundai plans to build more than 80% of U.S.-sold vehicles domestically.
American content in Hyundai's U.S. vehicles will jump from 60% to 80%.
Rivian broke ground on a separate $5 billion EV plant east of Atlanta that will eventually produce 400,000 vehicles annually starting in 2028.
Georgia has attracted 22 EV-related facilities since the Inflation Reduction Act passed, representing $24.4 billion in investment and 26,400 jobs.
Governor Brian Kemp described Georgia's positioning as the 'electric mobility capital of America.'
President Trump posted on Truth Social calling for foreign companies to legally bring skilled workers while defending ICE's actions at the plant.
South Korean President Lee Jae Myung warned the raid could make Korean companies hesitant about U.S. investments.
South Korean Foreign Minister Cho Hyun fielded angry questions from lawmakers demanding retaliation.
America currently lacks enough workers who know how to build EV batteries at scale, exposing a workforce gap for foreign-owned facilities.
source_title: Hyundai Doubles Down: $2.7 Billion Georgia Expansion Proves ICE Raid Won’t Stop EV Push - DealerBar

facts: Hyundai Motor Group confirmed it will proceed with a $2.7 billion expansion plan at its automotive complex in Georgia.
The expansion announcement follows weeks after a large immigration raid disrupted operations at the same site.
The Georgia complex is located west of Savannah in Ellabell and serves as one of Hyundai’s key U.S. production centers.
The new investment will increase the plant’s annual production capacity by 200,000 vehicles over the next three years.
Once complete, total output at the site will reach approximately 500,000 vehicles per year.
Hyundai initially announced plans to enlarge the Georgia facility earlier this year during the plant’s grand opening.
The expansion is part of a broader investment program aimed at strengthening Hyundai’s role in the U.S. market.
Despite delays from the immigration raid, Hyundai reiterated that the expansion will proceed as planned.
The expanded plant will manufacture ten models of electric and hybrid vehicles in Georgia.
The facility currently assembles only two models but will eventually produce a mid-sized pickup truck for the U.S. market.
The $2.7 billion expansion brings Hyundai’s total investment at the Georgia site to $10.3 billion.
This figure excludes an additional $4.3 billion being spent with a joint-venture partner to build a battery plant at the same location.
Hyundai and its affiliates currently employ more than 3,100 people at the Ellabell site.
The company aims to increase its workforce to at least 8,500 employees by the end of 2031.
State and local governments have provided $2.1 billion in tax breaks and other incentives to support Hyundai’s expansion in Georgia.
This investment is considered one of the most significant single boosts to the U.S. automotive industry by a foreign automaker in recent years.
The Ellabell site will be the center of Hyundai’s domestic production increase, aligning with a goal to raise worldwide production capacity by over one million vehicles annually.
Hyundai has also announced plans to increase capacity at facilities in India and South Korea, plus additional production arrangements in Saudi Arabia, Vietnam, and North Africa.
U.S. authorities detained more than 300 workers during the immigration raid, most of whom were South Korean nationals.
The raid caused tension between the United States and South Korea, resulting in a brief diplomatic dispute.
The disruption raised concerns that such events could discourage future investment commitments in the U.S.
Hyundai emphasized that the raid will not alter its long-term strategic plans for the American market.
The immigration raid has delayed the opening of the new battery plant connected to the Georgia complex.
Company officials stated the battery facility, built with LG Energy Solution, will now open in the first half of 2026, later than originally expected.
Hyundai continues to stress its commitment to the U.S. market and Georgia despite the operational disruption.
The company describes its investments as a crucial part of its long-term strategy to localize production.
Hyundai aims to produce 5.6 million vehicles annually worldwide by 2030, with 60% of those models being electric or hybrid.
A portion of the Georgia plant’s expanded output will contribute to this global target, focusing on sales in North America, South Korea, and Europe.
source_title: Hyundai confirms $2.7 billion Georgia expansion after immigration raid raised investment concerns - Deftechtimes

facts: Honda announced plans to combine all of its automobile manufacturing facilities in the U.S. related to frame, engine, transmission, and related engineering and purchasing operations into one new company.
The new company will be named Honda Development & Manufacturing of America, LLC (HDMA).
HDMA will also integrate the automobile product development operations of Honda R&D Americas, LLC (HRA).
The unification of Honda’s manufacturing operations and merger with auto product development will be effective April 1, 2021.
Shinji Aoyama serves as chief officer of North American Regional Operations of Honda Motor Co., Ltd., and president & CEO of American Honda.
Auto design will become part of American Honda Regional Operations.

Automobile product design and certain market research functions currently part of the HRA Los Angeles Center will be integrated into the Regional Operations of American Honda Motor Co., Inc., based in Torrance, Calif.
HDMA will unite all auto creation functions with a focus on achieving seamless development of new products with improved efficiency and quality.

The changes will effectively bring all auto development, planning, purchasing, strategy and manufacturing into one company, strengthening what Honda calls a "one-floor" approach to the product development process.
Key business functions will be centralized to become more efficient and eliminate redundancies.
New product development will be executed with increased speed and accuracy.
Manufacturing characteristics will improve with a focus on quality, cost competitiveness and the ability to deliver vehicles that appeal to customers in a timely manner.

Auto-related Honda companies to be wholly or partially consolidated into HDMA include Honda of America Mfg., Honda Manufacturing of Alabama, Honda Manufacturing of Indiana, Honda Transmission Manufacturing of America, Honda Precision Parts of Georgia, Honda Engineering North America, Honda R&D Americas, and Honda Accessory America.
American Honda announced plans to streamline its Auto Sales and Parts & Service Field operations to enable a more agile response to local market needs.
In January 2021, Honda and Acura zone offices will be restructured in order to improve communication between Dealers, Zone management and National Sales and Parts & Service management.
The sales restructuring includes four new Regional Manager positions created to provide oversight of Honda Sales, Acura Sales and Parts, Service & Technical field operations.
The Regional Managers will become the key link between the company’s National and Field operations, ensuring better alignment among all staff who call on Honda and Acura dealers.
Decision making authority will be pushed down to local staff to more quickly address dealer and customer needs.
Honda of South Carolina Mfg., responsible for powersports manufacturing in the U.S., will formally become part of the Powersports business unit within American Honda.
Honda established operations in North America in 1959.

The company employs more than 40,000 associates in the region engaged in the development, manufacturing, sales and service support of Honda and Acura automobiles, Honda power equipment, Honda powersports products, the HondaJet advanced light jet and GE Honda HF120 turbofan engines.
Honda operates 19 major manufacturing plants in North America, working with more than 740 suppliers in the region to produce a diverse range of Honda products.
In 2019, more than 90 percent of the Honda and Acura automobiles sold in the U.S. were produced in North America, using domestic and globally sourced parts.
Honda operates 15 major research and development centers in North America which jointly fully design, develop and engineer many of the products the company makes in North America.
source_title: Honda Unifies Manufacturing Operations and R&D in North America; Sales Organization Streamlines Auto Sales and Parts & Service Field Operations

facts: Hyundai has officially opened its new electric vehicle assembly plant in Bryan County, marking the largest single economic development project in Georgia's history.
Before reaching full capacity, the company announced a plan for expansion at the facility.
The Hyundai Metaplant, located just outside Savannah, is Hyundai’s first dedicated mass-production facility for electric and hybrid vehicles.
The facility is expected to create 8,500 jobs over the next few years.
The project represents more than $12 billion in investment from Hyundai in Georgia.
The plant operates as a joint venture with LG Energy Solution, which will supply battery technology for the electric vehicles.
Hyundai officials state the facility will help the company reach its goal of selling more than 3 million fully electric vehicles per year by 2030.
Euisun Chung, executive chair of Hyundai, stated: "I have never been more optimistic about building the future of mobility with America, in America."
Company executives joined local and state leaders for the grand opening ceremony on Wednesday afternoon.
State officials have touted the project as a major economic win for Georgia, positioning the state as a key player in the nation’s growing EV industry.
Kemp stated: "This is yet another historic investment and a testament of Hyundai's belief in Georgia. A belief in our people."
The plant is already operational with vehicles rolling off the assembly line.
The facility was originally expected to produce over 300,000 vehicles annually.
During the grand opening, the company announced it could beat the original production target at 500,000 vehicles annually.
Hyundai Metaplant is currently producing the IONIQ 5 and IONIQ 9 models.
As of March 24, a little over 1,200 employees (excluding Korean expats) had been hired to work at the plant.
Total employees as of March 24: 1,232.
Employees from Georgia: 1,077.
Employees from out-of-state: 155.
Of the 1,077 Georgia-based employees, 33% come from Chatham County, 15% from Bulloch County, 8% from Effingham County, and 7% from Bryan County.
The facility is currently operating on one shift.
The goal is to eventually run the facility 24/7.
source_title: Hyundai's massive new plant celebrating opening today in south Georgia | FOX 5 Atlanta

facts: By 2050, the company aims to cut in half CO2 emissions from its products and business operations, including its production plants, in order to address greenhouse gas emissions that contribute to global climate change.
Built upon a state-of-the-art electric vehicle platform, the 2024 Acura ZDX debuts as an all-electric performance SUV that delivers our commitment to precision crafted performance.

The 2018 Honda Accord, the 10th generation of America's most popular car, earned the prestigious 2018 North American Car of the Year award and was the first model from a Japanese automaker to be made in America, beginning in November 1982 in Marysville, Ohio, with cumulative U.S. production of Accord exceeding 11 million vehicles over more than 35 years.

In April 2017, Honda showed for the first time all three vehicles in its Clarity series – the Clarity Fuel Cell Vehicle, Clarity Electric and Clarity Plug-In Hybrid – pointing the way toward a cleaner and more eco-conscious automotive future as the world’s first vehicle with three different electrified powertrains.
The 2016 Honda Civic is selected as the North American Car of the Year by a panel of automotive journalists, marking the second time a Civic has earned the honor.

As part of its vision to create a more sustainable mobility future, Honda introduced the Clarity Fuel Cell to customers in California in late 2016, representing the leading edge of zero-emissions vehicle technology with a hydrogen-powered range of up to 366 miles on a single tank, refueling in three to five minutes, and emitting only water vapor.

Honda set up shop in Silicon Valley over 15 years ago and expanded its facility in Mountain View, CA, partnering with entrepreneurial technologists to advance connectivity, telematics, safety, and autonomous driving.
The unveiling of the powerful, strikingly beautiful Acura NSX supercar causes a sensation in the automotive world.

Honda Transmission Mfg. of America fires up its brand-new wind turbines in Russells Point, Ohio, becoming the first major automotive manufacturing facility in the U.S. to get a substantial amount of its electricity from onsite wind turbines.

On March 20, the 10-millionth Honda Accord built in North America rolls off the assembly line at the Marysville Auto Plant as Honda celebrates a total U.S. production of 20 million Honda and Acura automobiles.
American Honda Motor Co., Inc. marks its 50th year of U.S. operations on June 11, growing from a first store in Los Angeles with six employees to astounding heights.

Honda breaks new ground introducing the 2009 FCX Clarity fuel cell vehicle, featuring a new, more compact fuel cell stack and becoming the world’s first fuel cell sedan capable of comfortably carrying four occupants.

Honda Manufacturing of Indiana begins producing the Civic Sedan, while Honda R&D Americas opens its Marine Engine Research Facility in Florida and Honda Aero begins construction on its North Carolina headquarters.
Honda R&D Americas opens its third design studio in California for the research and styling of future Acura production models.
The 2006 Honda Civic and 2006 Honda Ridgeline Truck sweep the North American Car and Truck of the Year awards as selected by a panel of automotive journalists in North America.

In December, the Honda Insight drives through uncharted territory to become the first mass produced hybrid vehicle available in North America, remaining the most fuel-efficient, non-plugin vehicle in its class for the next 17 years.

Honda launches Acura, the first luxury nameplate from a Japanese automaker, with sales of “precision crafted performance,” initially opening 60 U.S. dealerships and earning four consecutive JD Power and Associates Customer Satisfaction Index awards.
The initial Accord model is followed by an Accord Sedan in 1979, becoming the best-selling passenger car in America with American car buyers purchasing over 12.5 million from its launch until today.

Driven by the rallying cry of “Blue skies for our children” Honda engineers create a groundbreaking CVCC engine that makes the Honda Civic the first vehicle to meet the strict emissions standards of the new U.S. Clean Air Act without the use of a catalytic converter.
The Honda N600 is the first automobile we ever sold in America.
On June 11, American Honda Motor Co., Inc. is established in Los Angeles, California, as the first overseas subsidiary of Honda Motor Co., Ltd.
source_title: Honda Motor Company: Our History | Honda

entity_name: LG Energy Solution-Honda Fayette County Battery Plant
canonical_name: LG Energy Solution-Honda Joint Venture EV Battery Plant
facts: LG Energy Solution will sell its electric vehicle battery production plant to Honda Development and Manufacturing of America.
The plant is located on the Jefferson Township mega-site in Fayette County, Ohio.
The facility was previously a joint venture between LG Energy Solution and Honda.
Through a sale-leaseback arrangement, the plant will continue operations.
The $2.85 billion sale includes the building and related assets.
The transaction is scheduled to close in February 2026.
Honda previously purchased 454 acres from Martin Land Co. for $23.3 million in November 2022.
The investments are concentrated in Jefferson Township, west of State Route 729 and south of Interstate 71.
Fayette County Commissioner Tony Anderson stated that Honda had no specific obligation to inform the county of the purchase.
Commissioner Anderson noted the county's primary role regarding the site was providing infrastructure for water and the water treatment plant.
source_title: Honda to acquire LG Energy share of Fayette Co. battery plant in $2.85 billion deal - Record Herald

facts: Ford has Volkswagen as an EV partner.
FCA will form Stellantis with the PSA Groupe.
General Motors and Honda announced plans to link up in North America and jointly develop future products.
The two automakers signed a preliminary, non-binding memorandum of understanding to form a business and technical alliance.
The proposed partnership involves sharing vehicle platforms and propulsion systems, notably internal combustion, electric, and hydrogen fuel cell powertrains.
This announcement expands on an April deal wherein GM agreed to lend its Ultium EV platform to Honda for two EVs aimed at the North American market.
The alliance could signal the introduction of additional GM-based Honda EVs or vice versa.
A GM spokesperson confirmed that Ultium will account for one of at least four platforms the two automakers will share.
Powertrains for the shared platforms will likely match their company of origin.
GM and Honda expect to work on more than four vehicle architectures together, with some led by GM and others by Honda.
GM powertrains will be used in GM-led programs and vice versa.
Planning discussions for vehicles to be sold under both GM and Honda brands will begin immediately.
Engineering work is expected to begin in early 2021.
Honda statements strongly suggest an effective merger of the two automakers' research and development wings is on the table.
Collaborative R&D could also be used to advance other fields of automotive technology, specifically Advanced Driver Assist Systems (ADAS), infotainment, and vehicle connectivity.
Groundwork for integrating each automaker's connected car systems was laid in April when GM confirmed the pair of Ultium-based Hondas would utilize HondaLink rather than OnStar.
Honda will explore further co-development opportunities for future connected services, including GM's OnStar and infotainment.
Consolidation of behind-the-scenes operations such as manufacturing and purchasing is acknowledged as a possibility, with Honda citing significant potential cost savings.
James Cain serves as GM's Senior Manager of Communications.
source_title: GM and Honda Team Up to Build at Least Four Cars Together

facts: Honda is expected to announce a multi-billion dollar commitment to build electric vehicles and their parts in Ontario later this week.
The deal is expected to involve new facilities to build batteries.
The deal is expected to involve facilities to process cathode active materials, a component of the batteries used in electric vehicles.
The news was first reported by Bloomberg.
Ontario Premier Doug Ford called the deal the 'largest deal in Canadian history' during remarks at the annual First Nations Major Projects Coalition conference on Monday.
Ford teased that the deal will involve multiple facilities 'right across the region.'
Ford stated that 'everyone's going to benefit' from the deal.
The announcement is set to be made Thursday at Honda's plant in Alliston, Ont.
Details of government subsidies for the project could become public at the Thursday announcement.
Ontario Economic Development Minister Vic Fedeli suggested the massive auto deal is worth something in the ballpark of $15 billion.
Fedeli stated that EV investment in the province went from zero to $28 billion in three years.
Fedeli suggested the new deal would bring total EV investment in the province to around '$43 billion ... or a number in and around there.'
Last week's budget, which has yet to pass Parliament, pitched an EV supply chain investment tax credit that likely acted as a sweetener for Honda.
The government is proposing a 10 per cent tax credit on the cost of buildings used in three keys area of the EV supply chain: assembly, battery production and cathode active material production.
Finance Minister Chrystia Freeland offered no details about the anticipated deal but praised the budget's measures for attracting foreign investment.
Freeland expressed confidence that the tax credits would bring more significant investment to Canada in the days, weeks, and months to come.
The anticipated Honda deal is the latest in a series of announcements of projects building up the electric vehicle industry in Canada, mainly in Ontario and Quebec.
Last year, federal and provincial governments announced a number of deals with EV battery producers Northvolt, Volkswagen and Stellantis-LGES.

Governments have defended subsidizing EV production as a way to keep pace with measures contained in the Inflation Reduction Act, a $369 billion U.S. program that offers subsidies and incentives to American companies building electric cars.
Automotive Parts Manufacturers' Association president Flavio Volpe said supplier interest is high reacting to the deal.
Volpe stated that Honda does not make speculative term bets.
Volpe noted that in almost 40 years of making cars in Canada without failure or retreat, a Honda supply contract is extremely bankable.
source_title: Honda expected to announce multi-billion dollar deal to assemble EVs in Ontario: sources | CBC News

facts: Keisha Lance Bottoms officially launched her 2026 Georgia governor campaign with a direct challenge to Donald Trump’s policies.
Her platform focuses on Medicaid expansion, housing reform and support for working families across Georgia.
If elected, Bottoms would become the first Black woman governor in U.S. history.
She cited tariffs affecting more than 600,000 jobs tied to ports like Savannah and Brunswick.
She cited job losses at the Atlanta-based CDC.
She cited delays at the Social Security Administration under Elon Musk’s DOGE Service initiative.

Her platform includes expanding Medicaid, supporting teachers and first responders, holding corporate landlords accountable for rising housing costs and increasing access to college and career training for youth.

As Atlanta mayor, Bottoms led the city through the COVID-19 crisis, stood firm on the city’s mask mandate despite Republican opposition and redirected federal relief funds into violence prevention and youth employment programs.
She created an Office of Violence Reduction to tackle the root causes of crime.
During the 2020 George Floyd protests, Bottoms emerged as a national voice for calm and justice.
She condemned Trump’s immigration enforcement policies by blocking ICE detainee transfers in Atlanta.
Her entry reshapes the 2026 Georgia governor’s race, where Republicans like Attorney General Chris Carr and Trump-aligned Lt. Gov. Burt Jones are already making waves.
Trump narrowly won Georgia in 2024, but his endorsements have lost traction in past state contests, opening a lane for Democrats to make gains.
source_title: Keisha Lance Bottoms slams Trump in Georgia governor announcement

Eiji Fujimura serves as President & CEO and Director, American Honda Motor Co., Inc.
Kensuke Oe serves as President and Director, Honda Development & Manufacturing of America, LLC
Hideto Yamasaki serves as President & CEO, Honda Aircraft Company, LLC

Steven Bailey serves as Vice President of Parts, Service & Technical Operations, American Honda Motor Co., Inc.
