facts: KIA Motors Manufacturing Georgia, Inc (KMMG) produced its three millionth vehicle in the U.S.A., which was a white KIA 2020 Telluride SX Prestige.
KMMG is located in West Point, Georgia.
KMMG represents an investment of more than US$1.1 billion.
KMMG began mass production on 16 November 2009.
KMMG has an annual capacity of more than 340,000 units.
KMMG produces more than 40% of all KIA vehicles sold in the United States.
KMMG produced three million vehicles in less than 10 years.
The plant operates under a “One System, One Team” philosophy.
Jangsoo (Jason) Shin serves as the President and CEO of KMMG.
KMMG builds the Telluride, Sorento, and Optima models.
KMMG will celebrate its 10th year of production later in 2019.

KMMG's products have been recognized for outstanding quality, reliability, and performance by J.D. Power & Associates, Consumer Reports, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA), the Insurance Institute for Highway Safety (IIHS), and a variety of media outlets.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp praised KMMG as an example of hardworking Georgians performing at world-class levels.
Governor Brian Kemp attributed KMMG's continued investment to Georgia's pro-business atmosphere.
KIA Motors Corporation operates four manufacturing facilities in South Korea (Sohari, Hwasung, Gwangju and Seosan) with a combined annual capacity of more than 1,8 million units.
KIA Motors operates three manufacturing facilities at Yancheng in China with a combined annual capacity of nearly 900,000 units.
KIA Motors operates the €1 billion Zilina plant in Slovakia, which has an annual production capacity of 330,000 units and caters predominantly for the European production of Ceed and Sportage models.
Both the Zilina and Yancheng plants opened in 2007.
In 2016, KIA Motors opened a US$1 billion, 400,000 unit production facility in Monterrey, Mexico.
In January 2019, KIA Motors opened its latest production facility in Anantapur, located in India’s Andra Pradesh province.
The new Anantapur plant represents an investment of US$1 billion and has an annual capacity of 300,000 units.
Production at the Anantapur plant commenced in August 2019.
The first model produced at the Anantapur plant was the new KIA Seltos compact SUV.
Several additional models are destined to be produced in Anantapur.
source_title: KIA Motors produces its 3 millionth vehicle in the U.S.A. – iloveza.com

facts: RACEMARK International is a family-owned company operated for over 55 years.
Ginger Cannon Bailey serves as CEO of RACEMARK International and Co-Founder/CEO of GGBAILEY.
Bob Bailey is the Chairman & Founder of RACEMARK International.
Emily Bailey is Head of Sales & Director of New Business Development at RACEMARK International, and Co-Founder & President of GGBAILEY & Aftermarket.
Cannon Bailey is President of RACEMARK International, GmbH and RACEMARK Industries Kft.
The company's US facility is located at 1711 Hwy 41 South, Calhoun, GA 30701.
RACEMARK is a producer of the world’s finest automotive floor mats and interiors.
RACEMARK has joined Healthy Seas, an environmental initiative that recovers discarded fishing nets from the seas to regenerate them into new products.
RACEMARK is the first company from the automotive industry to partner with Healthy Seas.
Healthy Seas was founded by the non-profit organisation Ghost Fishing and two sustainable enterprises, Aquafil and Star Sock.
RACEMARK’s vision is to become the global leader in sustainable automotive floor protection by 2023.

RACEMARK aims to develop and sell products that create no waste and require no new material resources because they can be recovered at the end of their useful life and endlessly upcycled, recycled or regenerated.
Cannon Bailey serves as President of Racemark.
Since its launch in 2013, Healthy Seas has collected 453 tons of waste nets from the seas and from land.
The recovered fishing nets, sometimes called “ghost nets”, remain in the marine ecosystem for hundreds of years entangling and killing many marine animals including whales, turtles and birds.
Thanks to the ECONYL Regeneration System, the nylon in the fishing nets is regenerated into high-quality ECONYL yarn which is then turned into brand new sustainable products.
According to a joint report by the FAO and UNEP, an estimated 640,000 tons of fishing gear are left in oceans each year, accounting for one-tenth of all marine litter.
Racemark’s vision includes reducing its environmental impact, innovating products & processes, and expanding into new markets by 2023.
Racemark was the first company in the world to create a custom-fit carpet car mat.
The company was founded in 1972 by Bob Bailey, a Porsche of America team world class race car driver, on the principal of engineering excellence.
Today, Racemark is a 100% family owned and operated business that includes manufacturing locations in the United States, Germany, Hungary and Switzerland.
Racemark delivers features and product enhancements to achieve “best in class” parts for the global automotive industry.
Racemark’s customers include Porsche, Mercedes-Benz, BMW, Tesla, Acura, Audi, Bentley, Chrysler, GM, Honda, Hyundai, Kia, MINI, Mitsubishi, Toyota and Volkswagen.
Healthy Seas is an open initiative that welcomes new partners on board who are committed to circular economy, sustainability and marine protection.
The mission of Healthy Seas is to remove waste from the seas, in particular fishing nets, for the purpose of creating healthier seas and recycling marine litter into textile products.
source_title: Racing to sustainability: RACEMARK becomes first company from automotive industry to partner with Healthy Seas - EIN Presswire

facts: RACEMARK INTERNATIONAL LLC received a PPP loan of $993,497 approved on Apr 13, 2020.

RACEMARK INTERNATIONAL LLC is a limited liability company located in Calhoun, Georgia.

facts: Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire Europe announced the completion of a high-voltage winding wire facility in its existing plant in Bad Arolsen, Germany.
The opening ceremony for the new facility took place on November 8, attended by board members of Essex Europe and Furukawa Electric, customers, and local authorities.
A new investment focused on the production of high-voltage winding wires, an innovative product that offers benefits and efficiency for next-generation electric vehicle drives.
The innovative wire is based on extruded resin-coated rectangular enameled wire and offers properties that allow the automotive industry to shrink motors and increase electric vehicle efficiency.

Following strong interest from automakers and suppliers in recent months and technical exchanges about this new wire, Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire can now deliver samples for customers to test for integration into future developments.
Essex Magnet Wire is the world's leading provider of magnet wire, used by most major OEMs and industry leaders in the automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
Essex Magnet Wire has branches in 11 countries across three continents and operates globally while serving locally.

With 90 years of experience, Essex Magnet Wire delivers innovations that meet customer demands and is vertically integrated into the manufacturing process to ensure quality and seamless delivery services from global distribution centers.
The Essex MagForceX® innovation center drives technological advancement in magnet wire through research and development providing custom solutions for future challenges.
Furukawa Electric Co., Ltd. was founded in 1884 as a copper smeltery and wire factory and has become a pioneer of the latest technologies.
Furukawa Electric's products span telecommunications, electronics, automobiles, and construction, focusing on optics, plastics, and metals, with many achieving the highest global market share.
Furukawa Electric reported consolidated revenue of 843.3 billion JPY (approximately 7.8 billion USD) for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2017.
Superior Essex is headquartered at 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327 – USA.

facts: Superior Essex announced the expansion of its partnership with Waybridge, a raw material supply chain operating system.
The initiative supports Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire's Vision 2030 plan objectives focused on agile digitization of manufacturing facilities in Europe, North America, and Malaysia.

Superior Essex was one of the first users of the Waybridge platform, initially deploying it in two North American magnet wire production facilities in the United States and Mexico starting in August 2021.
The partnership expansion will implement Waybridge across nine new manufacturing facilities located in Canada, Germany, Italy, Serbia, and the United States.
Scott Wilde, Vice President of Strategic Sourcing for Superior Essex, stated that making innovation a core component of sourcing and achieving holistic sustainability has been a primary focus.
Scott Wilde noted that the company needed to streamline operations and predict supply chain issues like stockouts before they impacted customers or the company.
Collaboration with Waybridge radically changed how Superior Essex visualizes inventory and workflow, providing crucial predictive information to operate agilely on supply chain problems.

Inefficient supply chains and stockouts can increase logistics costs, damage brand image, and hinder future demand due to unexpected demand spikes, shipping/supplier delays, human error, and inaccurate inventory data.
Waybridge's Stock & Flow function aggregates real-time data from shipping forecasts, locations, ETAs, and production plans to offer predictive inventory capabilities.
In 2021 alone, Waybridge enabled Superior Essex to avoid four inventory stockout situations that would have directly impacted customers.
Thanks to Waybridge's interconnected platform, Superior Essex reduced company-wide Excel usage by 90% and procurement/tracking emails by 50%.

Scott Evans, CEO and Co-founder of Waybridge, stated that Superior Essex is a pioneer in raw materials and understands the importance of standardizing management on a common platform to benefit the company, clients, and suppliers.
Kyle McCarter, Chief Product Officer of Waybridge, noted that Superior Essex provided incredible feedback since the partnership began, allowing their product to evolve and benefit all clients.
Superior Essex currently requires all material management in North America to be handled through Waybridge.
Superior Essex plans to expand Waybridge technology to its Asian facilities in the near future.
Superior Essex is the parent brand of Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire, Superior Essex Communications, and Essex Brownell.
Superior Essex employs over 3,000 people across 11 countries on three continents.
Superior Essex is a global leading supplier of magnet wire and is pioneering communication fiber and Power over Ethernet (PoE) technology.
The company's technology is fundamental to disruptive technological advances in the electrification of automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
The partnership expansion was announced on May 11, 2022.
Superior Essex is headquartered at 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327.
source_title: Superior Essex, Essex Furukawa Partner With Waybridge On Agile Digitization Technology - Superior Essex – Corporate

facts: Essex Solutions, a US subsidiary of LS Corporation, has succeeded in its Pre-IPO investment attraction.
The total pre-IPO investment amount is USD 200 million (KRW 290 billion), noted as the largest for a single domestic project fund established in 2024.
The investment was structured as investors purchasing shares issued by the Mirae Asset-KCGI consortium via a third-party allocation paid-in capital increase.
The Mirae Asset-KCGI consortium acquired approximately 20% of Essex Solutions' shares.
Essex Solutions' pre-IPO market capitalization amounts to about USD 1 billion, or approximately KRW 1.45 trillion.
Leading investment firms Bain Capital, Goldman Sachs, and IMM participated in competitive bidding for the pre-IPO, which was directly led by LS Corporation.
Essex Solutions' predecessor, Essex Wire Corporation, was an American wire company established in 1930.
The predecessor acquired a telecommunications wire business in 1954 and later changed its name to Superior Essex (SPSX).
LS Group acquired SPSX in 2008 for approximately KRW 1 trillion.
SPSX has steadily invested in R&D in preparation for the electric vehicle era since turning a profit in 2016.
In 2020, Essex Solutions established a joint venture named 'Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire' with Japan's Furukawa Electric to advance into the global winding market.
EV coils are copper wires wound around electric vehicle drive motors to convert electrical energy into kinetic energy.
Essex Solutions acquired all of EFMW's shares from Furukawa Electric in April of last year and vertically integrated the group's winding corporations to launch Essex Solutions.
Main products include 'special windings for electric/hybrid vehicles' and 'special windings for large-capacity transformers'.
Special windings for electric vehicles are core materials for drive motors that require technology capable of withstanding high voltages.
Essex Solutions possesses the world's best technological edge in this area.
Products are supplied to the world's number one electric vehicle manufacturer and global leading automakers requiring high quality.
Essex Solutions holds an overwhelming market share of 1st place in North America, excluding China, where EV production is most active.
The company maintains production bases in global markets including North America, South America, Europe, and Asia to respond to regional protectionist measures.
Essex Solutions aims to increase its electric vehicle market share in North America to 70% and in Europe to over 50% within the next five years.
LS Group plans to complete the selection of an underwriter by early this year and pursue a full-scale listing within 2025.
The company initially planned to list on KOSPI but is also considering listing on NASDAQ due to strong interest from local U.S. investment banks.

An LS official stated that the market has recognized Essex Solutions' future business value following its large-scale pre-IPO success, which is essential in the era of electric vehicles and power super cycles.
The official added that Essex Solutions will continue to solidify its position as a leader in the winding industry through continuous R&D development and ultra-gap technological superiority.
source_title: LS Essex Solutions, $200 million pre-IPO success

facts: Superior Essex, a provider of wire and cable products and accessories, announced the launch of its Indoor Plenum Fiber Copper Hybrid cable for distributed antenna systems (DAS).
The DAS Hybrid cable consists of two discrete cables, one copper and one fiber, paired beneath an overjacket.
This dual-cable design allows the hybrid cable to transmit communications while also providing power to DAS Remote Units.
These cables are ideal for DSAs when the DAS remotes are powered from a centralized source.
The two-in-one design allows power and fiber cables to be pulled simultaneously, saving time and labor by eliminating the need for two separate pulls.
Unlike round hybrid cables with unjacketed copper and fiber, the discrete copper and fiber cables within the Superior Essex Hybrid cable can be easily separated and routed individually.
The copper conductors utilize an intuitive black and red color scheme which makes it easier to determine positive and negative connections.
This cable is UL 13 CL2P plenum rated, making it eligible for installation throughout a building, including air carrying plenum space.
When the cable is part of a NEC Class 2 power limited system some local building codes allow it to be installed without being enclosed in a raceway or armored.
Kim Purdie serves as director of product management, wireless products at Superior Essex.
Kim Purdie stated that the surge in DAS deployments places an increased demand on contractors to complete installations faster.
Kim Purdie noted that the Superior Essex hybrid cable helps reduce installation time by eliminating the need for two separate cable pulls.
source_title: Superior Essex Announces Launch of DAS Hybrid Cable - Broadband Communities

facts: Superior Essex Inc. announced the appointment of Daniel Choi as CEO effective March 1, 2022.
Choi will lead Superior Essex Inc. and all its subsidiaries, including Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire, Superior Essex Communications, Essex Brownell, Essex Energy Italy, IVA, and Hi-Wire.
Choi previously worked at LS Cable and System, the parent company of Superior Essex Inc., since 1999.
Prior to his appointment, Choi served as Regional President for North and South America at LS Cable & System.
Choi's previous roles include President of LS Cable & System USA for MV and LV Power Cable Systems, President of Cyprus Investments from 2008 to 2014, and SVP of PMO for Europe and China in 2015.
Choi holds a PhD in Materials Science and Engineering from Seoul National University and an MBA from Helsinki Business School.
Choi resides in Atlanta, Georgia, and serves as Chairman of the Korean Chamber of Commerce and Industry in Georgia.
Choi stated he is very grateful to take on the CEO position of this traditional and respected company and aims to build on past achievements while ensuring high quality and customer service.
The leadership change follows the resignation of Brian Kim, who served as CEO for nearly seven years.

During his tenure, Kim established core values, broke down business silos, expanded infrastructure for hybrid and PoE cables, increased production capacity, founded MagForceX Innovation Centers to focus on innovation and R&D, launched the strategic joint venture Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire, and oriented the company toward a sustainable future.
Superior Essex is the parent brand of Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire, Superior Essex Communications, and Essex Brownell.

Superior Essex is the world's leading provider of magnet wires and a leader in communication fiber applications and Power over Ethernet (PoE) technology.
The company contributes significantly to technological advances in the electrification of the automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
Superior Essex aims to pioneer smart building technology while advancing sustainability initiatives.

facts: Pirelli inaugurated its North American manufacturing headquarters in Rome, Georgia, in 2002.

European luxury automobile manufacturers including BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Audi, and Porsche aggressively established and expanded assembly plants across the American Southeast during the late 1990s and early 2000s.

Pirelli positioned its Rome facility within a geographic triangle defined by Atlanta, Georgia; Birmingham, Alabama; and Chattanooga, Tennessee to enable efficient logistics to regional automotive assembly centers.
The Rome plant was designed as a highly robotized production center focusing on premium, large-diameter specialty tires for the North American luxury SUV market.
Pirelli aims to secure Original Equipment (OE) tire supplier contracts for these prestigious European marques through its localized manufacturing presence.
Pirelli's chemical engineers develop synthetic rubber compounds specifically designed to reduce rolling resistance for new electric SUV models.
The Rome facility employs a diverse workforce of hundreds, including expatriate engineering specialists from Italy, Brazil, Germany, the United Kingdom, and Venezuela.
Pirelli's Rome plant is the first tire manufacturing facility globally to achieve Forest Stewardship Council (FSC) certification for sustainable natural rubber sourcing.
Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corporation (SMAC) officially opened its Rome, Georgia plant in 2001 and began production of Eiger and Vinson ATV models in early 2002.
SMAC transitioned into a 100% owned subsidiary of Suzuki Motor USA, LLC (SMO) in March 2024 to integrate Rome engineering teams with the national corporate structure.
The SMAC facility spans 35 acres and represents a $30 million investment, employing hundreds of associates who assemble over one hundred KingQuad 750AXi and 500AXi models daily.
Suzuki selected Rome for its proximity to the largest consumer ATV market in North America and access to a highly skilled regional labor pool and metallurgical suppliers.

Marglen Industries operates a polymer recycling facility in Rome that produces ultra-clean recycled PET (rPET) resin pellets for food-grade packaging and high-tensile polyester fiber for flooring and apparel.
Marglen's Rome facility installed a 95.2-kilowatt solar energy photovoltaic system on its rooftop in 2011 to offset polymer extrusion energy demands.
Summit Hill Foods was acquired by New York-based private equity firm EagleTree Capital with co-investments from Misland Capital Limited in November 2023.
Atrium Health Floyd, originally opened as Floyd County Hospital on July 4, 1942, combined with the Atrium Health network in 2019 and currently manages over 1,100 active clinical trials.
Rome's historical industrial base earned it the title of metallurgical "stove center of the South" before shifting to textiles, synthetic fibers, and heavy paper/containerboard manufacturing.
The International Paper Rome Mill remains a cornerstone of the local economy alongside historic facilities like the Massachusetts Cotton Mill and American Chatillon Corporation (later Celanese).

Georgia's state R&D tax credit under O.C.G.A. § 48-7-40.12 offers a 10% credit for research conducted within the state, featuring payroll withholding tax offsets for startups and expanding manufacturers.

Federal R&D tax credits under IRC § 41 subsidize Qualified Research Expenses (QREs) including W-2 wages for engineers, CAD designers, testing technicians, and raw materials consumed during prototype development.
Rome's strategic location at the confluence of the Etowah and Oostanaula rivers historically supported riverine transportation networks that facilitated regional industrial supply chains.

facts: Kubota Manufacturing of America celebrated the grand opening of its 700,000-square-foot facility in Gainesville.

The $190 million Kubota project will allow the company to produce 170,000 units a year.
The Kubota project brings 500 jobs to the Gainesville area.

OXERRA, a global manufacturer of iron oxide pigment, recently broke ground on its 120,000-square-foot facility.
The OXERRA distribution center will incorporate eco-friendly materials and energy-efficient lighting targeting LEED certification.
The OXERRA project is expected to be completed in January 2026.
Amazon will open a new package sorting center in Hogansville, Troup County.

The site will include an 18,000-seat arena, residential, retail and dining components.

facts: Rome, Georgia-based Suzuki Motor USA, LLC is celebrating 20 years of manufacturing KingQuad ATVs at the SMAC facility.
KingQuad ATVs first rolled off the assembly line in Rome in May 2002 and have been built exclusively in the U.S. ever since.
Suzuki has been building the award-winning KingQuad ATVs at a multi-million dollar, 35-acre manufacturing facility since 2002.

The SMAC facility is located in the center of the biggest ATV market in the world, allowing access to real-world rider feedback for product refinement and timely delivery of KingQuad ATVs to Suzuki dealers worldwide.
Suzuki has been an innovator in engineering since the company’s founding in 1909.
In the early 1980s, Suzuki led the industry with the creation of the first ever four-wheel ATV in 1983.

Kerry Graeber, vice president and division manager of MC/ATV Sales and Marketing, stated that building KingQuad ATVs in America for the past 20 years represents the commitment, hard work, and dedication of the people who build them at the Rome, Georgia facility.
The SMAC facility is one key to the continued success of Suzuki’s global ATV business.
KingQuad ATVs built at the facility are shipped to U.S. Suzuki dealers and nearly 30 countries worldwide.

facts: Morgan Advanced Materials has launched a thermal bag to counter the fire risk from overheating Lithium-Ion batteries due to thermal runaway.
The FireMaster battery bags went through an extensive period of testing in conjunction with Germanwings, part of the Lufthansa Group.
The FireMaster bag measures 500 mm x 500 mm.

The bag comprises two of Morgan’s specialist thermal materials to create a sealable, water-resistant compartment capable of containing the heat spread in the event of a primary-lithium or lithium-ion battery fire.
The US Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) has recorded an average of six incidents every year for the last 25 years involving batteries carried as baggage or cargo.
An increasing proportion of these FAA-recorded incidents are caused by lithium batteries.
Studies have proven that lithium battery fires cannot be adequately suppressed by halon fire extinguishing methods.
Both primary lithium and lithium-ion batteries are categorised as Class 9 hazardous materials.
Both the FAA and the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO) have restricted the transportation of lithium-based batteries as cargo.
The most recent ruling restricting lithium battery transport took effect from April 1, 2016.
Thermal runaway occurs when a lithium battery overheats past its point of thermal stability (150°C/302°F), resulting in fire, explosion, a pressure wave, or all three.
Thomas Schwarz is the sales director for Morgan’s thermal ceramics business.
The transportation of lithium-based batteries as cargo has been regulated by aviation authorities for more than a decade.
No limitations have been placed on the transportation of these products within the main aircraft cabin.
Thermal runaway, although a rare occurrence, can have a catastrophic effect on an aircraft in flight.
Passenger airlines have been proactive in trying to find a solution to this manageable danger.
The bag benefits from Morgan’s FireMaster Marine Plus technology, which was originally designed for use in the oil & gas sector.
FireMaster Marine Plus provides thermal protection from uncontrolled fire for sustained periods of time.
source_title: Thermal blanket has lithium-ion fires covered - Air Cargo News

facts: Amara Industries, a parent company to the Gracious Living group of companies, announced the acquisition of Thomson Plastics on Dec. 9, 2025.
Thomson Plastics is a recognized leader in large tonnage injection molding in the Southeastern United States.
Thomson Plastics currently operates from two locations in Georgia and North Carolina.
The acquisition enhances Amara Industries’ manufacturing footprint, automation capabilities, and technical depth to a total of five manufacturing facilities.
Thomson Plastics has clamping force capacities up to 3,700 tons, with 30% of its fleet exceeding 1,000 tons.
Thomson Plastics is supported by advanced robotics and automation to deliver consistent performance and efficiency across high-volume production environments.

Thomson Plastics' technical portfolio includes injection molding, gas-assisted molding, over-molding, insert molding, in-mold labeling, robotic part picking, pad printing, and heat transfer decorating.
Vince Orlando, Co-President at Amara Industries, stated that the addition of Thomson Plastics represents a strategic milestone for the organization.

Vince Orlando noted that the advanced molding capabilities, technical expertise, and culture of innovation will enhance their ability to serve customers with scale, speed, and precision across North America.
The unified company will meet rising demand for high-performance molded products in key sectors including automotive, consumer goods, industrial, and commercial markets.
The expansion into the Southeastern United States strengthens Amara Industries' ability to provide customers with faster delivery, greater flexibility, and improved supply chain efficiency.
Amara Industries has a dedicated team of over 500 employees.
Amara Industries offers end-to-end capabilities spanning design, product development, engineering, and materials science.
Amara Industries specializes in injection molding, assembly, profile extrusion, decorating, and material compounding.
Amara Industries holds key certifications including ISO, IATF, and CTPAT.
source_title: Amara Industries Acquires Thomson Plastics | The AI Journal

entity_name: Hyundai Meta Plant & Kia West Point Plant
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Georgia & Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia
facts: The state of Georgia is quickly turning into an automotive powerhouse.
Hyundai operates a brand-new manufacturing plant near Savannah called the Meta plant.
Kia operates an established assembly plant in West Point near Atlanta.
Hyundai's Meta plant exterior buildings are painted light sage green to blend with rural surroundings.
Every building at the Meta plant has rounded corners intentionally chosen to look more natural and less hard-edged.
The original plan for the Meta plant was to only make electric cars starting with the Hyundai Ioniq 5 and Ioniq 9.
EV sales did not materialize as expected, so Kia added the Sportage to the Meta plant for manufacturing volume.
The Meta plant is highly automated and described as the most automated facility visited by the author.
The Meta plant is currently operating at very low volume with lines moving at a snail's pace.
Production at the Meta plant started in October 2024.
Full line speed at the Meta plant is not expected until 2028.
The Meta plant needs more models to fill assembly lines and its advanced automation requires tweaking and adjustment.
Metal stamping at the Meta plant uses one massive transfer press line made by Rotem, a division of Hyundai.
There is only one transfer press line at the Meta plant with no backup if it goes down.
The single press line makes all panels for three models at the Meta plant.
Major A-class panels made on-site at the Meta plant include body sides, roofs, floors, and fenders.
Smaller stampings for the Meta plant are shipped in from suppliers.
No forklifts are used to move panel racks at the Meta plant; everything is handled by Automated Guided Vehicles (AGVs) and heavy-duty electric tuggers.
Welding at the Meta plant uses robotic automation with huge Fanuc robots picking up entire body-in-white structures.
The Meta plant is a brand-new facility currently ironing out operational kinks.
Line workers at the Meta plant were observed hammering door sills and Korean technicians discussing body-in-white assemblies.
Several bodies were pulled off the line at the Meta plant for repair loops.
Cars spend six to eight hours in the paint shop at the Meta plant using one gallon of paint per vehicle.
The paint shop at the Meta plant is not accessible on tours to prevent contamination from dust and dirt.
Final assembly at the Meta plant uses robots to take doors off for interior assembly and put them back on later.
Every car at the Meta plant rides on an AGV equipped with an automatic up-and-down lift to set ergonomic height at every workstation.
Hyundai Steel makes the steel used at the Meta plant.
Hyundai Rotem makes the presses used at the Meta plant.
Hyundai Glovis handles shipping, receiving, and logistics for the Meta plant.
Hyundai Mobis handles battery pack assembly for the Meta plant.
The Meta plant currently employs about 1,800 people.
The Meta plant is aggressively hiring to add a second shift, which will push employment to 2,600.
The Meta plant shipped about 6,000 cars the month before the visit.
At full capacity, Kia will receive about 40% of production from the Meta plant.
Current capacity at the Meta plant is 300,000 units.
Hyundai plans to boost Meta plant capacity to 500,000 units.
Hyundai is planning to add a second plant by knocking out an exterior wall to add another 250,000 units of capacity.
The extra space at the Meta plant will likely be tooled for upcoming midsize body-on-frame trucks and SUVs from Hyundai and Kia.
Kia's West Point plant began making cars 16 years ago.
The West Point plant spans roughly 1.7 million square feet under roof including stamping, paint, body shop, and final assembly.
The West Point plant is tooled to make 350,000 vehicles a year.
A single line at the West Point plant runs the Telluride, Sorento, Sportage, EV6, and EV9.
The West Point plant builds gas, hybrid, and pure EVs sequentially on one line.
The West Point plant employs 3,200 employees working three shifts, eight hours a day, five days a week.
The West Point plant does on-the-fly handoffs from shift to shift so the line never stops.
Daily maintenance at the West Point plant is squeezed into brief 30-minute lunch breaks and weekends.
The parking lot at the West Point plant is jam-packed due to no breaks between shifts, making it hard to find parking.
The West Point stamping shop runs two Rotem transfer presses.
Die sets for each vehicle at the West Point plant are color-coded.
Storage racks for stamped panels at the West Point plant are color-coded to each specific model.
Robots at the West Point plant pick panels off the press line and keep a last hit panel on a rack for each model to verify quality.
The West Point plant maintains about 36 hours of inventory in stamped panels.
The West Point body shop has zero batch building and builds different bodies one right after another to match sales orders.
A modular body buck at the West Point plant adapts on the fly to accommodate different models.
General assembly at the West Point plant has open sightlines with parts racks no higher than chest-high.
The West Point plant uses two separate trim lines where human operators assisted by mechanical lift equipment handle doors-on and doors-off processes.
Workers on the final line at the West Point plant rotate jobs every two hours to prevent fatigue and boredom.
The West Point plant paints 14 different colors, mostly white, black, and silver.
The final assembly line at the West Point plant is tightly packed with only about four feet of clearance between each car.
The West Point plant pumps out 1,500 cars a day.
Every vehicle at the West Point plant goes through a test on a 2.2-mile on-site track vital for testing lane-keeping system calibration.
The West Point plant features an on-site transmission assembly plant.
Kia sells transmissions from the West Point plant to outside competitors like Nissan and Volkswagen.
The West Point plant has a massive 13,000-spot parking lot with protective roofs built over part of the staging areas.
Protective roofs were added at the West Point plant after a severe hail storm caused massive inventory damage last year.
Solar panels were installed on the protective roofs at the West Point plant while management assesses cost-effectiveness of covering all cars.
The Meta plant is vast, highly automated, whisper-quiet, and utilizes AGVs for moving stampings and carrying cars.
The Meta plant is running at a snail's pace while slowly ramping up production and dialing in quality.
The West Point plant relies on hyper-disciplined human logistics and flexible tooling to churn out high-margin SUVs at maximum velocity.
The West Point plant uses about 17 labor-hours to make each vehicle including stamping, which is extremely efficient.
Hyundai and Kia are on track to make more than 1.4 million vehicles a year in the U.S.
This production volume would be more than what Toyota or Honda currently make in the U.S.
The plants demonstrate the kind of ambition the Hyundai Group has for the American market.
source_title: Insights & Observations: Inside Hyundai’s Meta & Kia’s West Point Plants - Autoline

facts: TI Fluid Systems plc, a supplier of thermal management and fluid systems based in Oxford, UK, has been acquired by Canadian automotive parts firm ABC Technologies.
The two companies plan to merge to form a single company called ‘TI Automotive’.
The acquisition was announced late last year and completed for a sum of over GBP1.8bn.
Following the merger, TI Automotive will move its headquarters to Auburn Hills, Michigan.
The Auburn Hills location was originally founded in 1922 as “Harry Bundy & Co”.
The combined company will have an annual turnover of USD5.4bn.
The combined company will have 34,600 employees across 26 countries.

facts: TI Fluid Systems is an automotive components manufacturer.
TI Fluid Systems posted interim profits that came in ahead of expectations on Thursday.
TI Fluid Systems said it was confident that adjusted underlying earnings margins would continue to expand throughout FY24.
TI Fluid's adjusted pre-tax earnings were up 2.7% year-on-year at €135.5m, beating market expectations for a print of €131.0m.
TI Fluid's EBIT margins expanded from 7.5% to 7.9%, above the 7.4% targeted by the group.
TI Fluid's revenues dropped 1.4% on a constant currency basis to €1.7bn.
TI Fluid's bookings increased 11% to €1.5bn.
TI Fluid's bookings included €600.0m worth of electric vehicle wins.
TI Fluid stated that productivity and efficiency measures put in place at the start of FY24 reaffirmed its confidence in expanding adjusted EBIT margins moving ahead.
TI Fluid increased its full-year adjusted EBIT margin expectation to above 7.6%.
The increase in margin expectation was notwithstanding a slight decline in revenue at constant currency due to the recent softening of the 2024 industry outlook.
As of 0925 BST, TI Fluid shares were up 11.46% at 124.84p.
source_title: Ti Fluid Systems posts H1 profit beat, reaffirms FY margin guidance  - Sharecast.com

facts: TI Fluid Systems is an automotive fluid systems manufacturer.
Full-year revenues and operating profits improved year-on-year in 2021.
Full-year revenues were up 5.6% at constant currency rates to €2.95bn.
Margin improved by more than a percentage point, rising from -6.3% to 4.3%.
Adjusted underlying earnings rose from a reported loss of €176.3m to a profit of €126.8m.
Earnings per share were EUR 2.76 cents, a marked improvement on the prior year's loss of EUR 48.88 cents.
Declared a dividend of EUR 3.39 cents, down from 2020's EUR 6.74 cent payment.
Adjusted free cash flow generation was €117.3m.
Net cash generated from operating activities was €215.1m.
The group ended the year with a strong balance sheet and a cash position of €499.1m.

Chief executive Hans Dieltjens reported a strong performance in 2021 despite ongoing pandemic impacts, lower production volumes, global supply disruptions, labour shortages, rising costs, and volatile customer orders.
TI Fluid anticipates that industry production volumes will remain uncertain and volatile in the near term given geopolitical conflicts and the macro-economic environment.
Annual global light vehicle production volumes were forecast to return to pre-Covid levels by the end of 2023.

entity_name: Ti Cold / Karis Cold / PermaCold Logistics Cold Storage Facility
canonical_name: Ti Cold / Karis Cold / PermaCold Logistics Cold Storage Facility
facts: A $60 million cold storage facility is being built in Darien, Georgia.
The project is a partnership between Ti Cold, Karis Cold, and PermaCold Logistics.
The facility will be located at Tidewaters Industrial Park, which is designated as a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) site.
The state's GRAD program allows for expedited industrial construction projects.
Governor Brian Kemp announced the groundbreaking on Wednesday.
Ti Cold serves as the cold storage builder and developer for the project.
Karis Cold is a private investment and development company with expertise in the cold storage industry.
The first phase of the project is expected to create 50 jobs upon completion.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that the announcement marks the largest known economic development win for McIntosh County and another success for rural Georgia.
Governor Brian Kemp noted that companies like Ti Cold, Karis Cold, and PermaCold Logistics, along with state ports and highway systems, support logistics industry jobs across the state.
Sam Tippmann is the founder and CEO of Ti Cold.
Sam Tippmann stated that his company has built in Georgia many times over the years and is thrilled to be working in McIntosh County.
Sam Tippmann highlighted that the project received incredible support from state officials, the McIntosh County team, and the city of Darien.
The facility will feature a flexible design capable of maintaining temperatures ranging from 40 degrees Fahrenheit to minus 10 degrees.
Structural steel pallet racks at the facility will accommodate more than 30,000 pallet positions.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development's Global Commerce team worked on the project in partnership with the McIntosh County Industrial Development Authority.
The Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia EMC, and Georgia Power also partnered with the GDEcD Global Commerce team on the project.
source_title: Kemp says groundbreakings mark development win for rural Georgia

facts: ABC Technologies (ABC) has announced the completion of its acquisition of TI Fluid Systems (TIFS).
The acquisition was completed for an enterprise value of over £1.8bn (US$2.38bn).
The combined business will be rebranded as TI Automotive.

The transaction brings together two complementary businesses to create a stronger, more diversified Tier 1 manufacturer supplying safety and performance critical products and highly engineered components.
TI Automotive will be led by ABC president and CEO Terry Campbell.
TI Automotive will be headquartered in Auburn Hills, Michigan.
TI Automotive will be managed through a regional operating model to ensure customer proximity and the agility to respond rapidly to local market dynamics.
ABC president and CEO Terry Campbell stated that the company has exceptional people, differentiated products, a deep commitment to safety, and a relentless focus on delivering for customers.
Terry Campbell added that the combination creates opportunities to drive innovation and manufacturing excellence while establishing foundations for future growth.
source_title: ABC Technologies acquires TI Fluid Systems | Automotive Powertrain Technology International

facts: Ti Fluid Systems is a leading global supplier of innovative automotive fluid storage, carrying, and delivery systems.
The company focuses on lightweight and sustainable solutions.
It has established itself as a trusted partner for many of the world’s top automotive manufacturers.
Ti Fluid Systems was founded in 2001.
The company designs and produces high-quality fluid management systems for the automotive industry.
Ti Fluid Systems is a publicly traded company listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol TIFS.
Shareholders of Ti Fluid Systems include institutional investors, individual investors, and corporate entities.
The company's main office is located in Johannesburg, South Africa.
Ti Fluid Systems offers job openings in various departments and locations, including South Africa.
Salaries at Ti Fluid Systems vary depending on position, experience, and location.
The company provides competitive compensation packages that include health insurance, retirement plans, and performance bonuses.
Ti Fluid Systems maintains manufacturing facilities and distribution centers in strategic locations across South Africa.
Sustainability is a key operational focus for the company, with a commitment to developing environmentally friendly solutions.
Facility tours are limited to authorized personnel only due to safety and confidentiality reasons.

facts: Rivian announced plans to increase the initial production capacity of its upcoming Georgia plant by 50%, bringing it to 300,000 vehicles per year.
The capacity increase was revealed as part of the company’s latest earnings report.
The update comes as the electric vehicle maker prepares for growth centered around its upcoming R2 platform.
The Georgia facility is located near Stanton Springs.
The facility is expected to play a key role in scaling production of Rivian’s more affordable, mass-market vehicles.
Originally planned with a lower starting capacity, Rivian says the increase is designed to improve cost efficiency from day one.
By building more vehicles at launch, the automaker aims to better absorb fixed costs across higher volumes—an important factor as they work toward profitability.
The plant is still on track to begin vehicle production in late 2028.
The expanded capacity applies to the first phase of development.
Rivian says the site will also allow for additional growth in later phases, giving it room to scale even further if demand for its vehicles continues to rise.
Supporting the project is a revised loan agreement with the U.S. Department of Energy.
Rivian now expects to access up to $4.5 billion in financing for the Georgia facility, down from earlier projections of approximately $6.6 billion.
Despite the lower total, the company says the updated structure offers more efficient capital deployment.
Rivian anticipates drawing its first funds from the loan as early as 2027, earlier than previously expected.
The Georgia plant will complement Rivian’s existing manufacturing operations in Normal, Illinois, where the company currently produces its R1T, R1S, commercial delivery vans.
As of last month, Rivian has also begun early production of the R2 at its Illinois facility, with deliveries to employees already underway and customer deliveries expected to begin in a few weeks.
source_title: Rivian Boosts Georgia Plant Capacity to 300,000 Vehicles Annually

facts: Researchers at the University of California San Diego developed a new upcycling process that converts retired lithium iron phosphate (LFP) battery cathodes into more powerful lithium manganese iron phosphate (LMFP) materials.
The upgraded LMFP material can store more energy while maintaining the safety and long life characteristics that made LFP batteries popular.
This recycling method upgrades existing cathodes into a better material rather than breaking old batteries down into raw materials to make new cathodes.
The process could cut down on waste, use less energy, and make retired EV batteries more valuable.
LFP batteries are among the most commonly used in electric vehicles and large energy storage systems because they cost less by avoiding expensive metals like cobalt or nickel.
LFP batteries currently make up almost half of the world’s lithium-ion battery market.
Most traditional recycling methods use high heat or strong chemicals, which are not environmentally friendly, use a lot of energy, and create waste and emissions.

Wei Li, the study’s first author and a postdoctoral researcher in Zheng Chen’s lab at UC San Diego, explained that conventional processes are not environmentally friendly due to high energy use and waste generation.
Chen’s research group previously developed a method to restore old LFP batteries to fresh LFP material, but the recycled batteries retained the same chemistry.
The new upcycling process offers a more valuable end use for spent batteries by upgrading them to LMFP instead of just regenerating them to LFP.
The recycling process begins by opening battery packs and removing the tightly rolled layers inside known as a jelly roll.
Researchers cut these layers into sheets, soak them in water, and gently shake the material to separate the cathode coating from the aluminum foil.
The separated aluminum foil can also be recycled separately.
The remaining cathode material is dried and ground into a fine black powder.
Researchers add lithium, manganese, and phosphate salts to provide the necessary ingredients for LMFP production.

A major challenge was that the added salts and original LFP material have incompatible crystal structures, which would cause non-uniform atomic distribution and worse electrochemical performance if mixed directly.
To solve this, researchers first prepare an intermediate compound called lithium manganese phosphate (LMP) with a crystal structure similar to LFP.
The powder is finely ground and mixed, then heated to allow LMP to form and mix evenly with the LFP.
During heating, manganese atoms slowly replace some iron atoms, creating a uniform LMFP structure.
A thin layer of carbon forms around each particle during the process, which helps conduct electricity and protects the material during many charging cycles.
The upgraded LMFP material stored more energy than the original LFP while maintaining its durability and safety.
Researchers tested the method using LFP batteries sourced from different manufacturers.
The team scaled up the process to kilogram quantities.
The recycled material performed well in both small lab coin cells and larger pouch cells, which are similar to batteries used in electric vehicles and large energy storage systems.
The research team plans to make the process more efficient and extract more material from it.
They also aim to improve the material’s composition and structure to boost performance and prepare the technology for large-scale recycling.
The research was published in the journal Joule.
source_title: US scientists turn dead EV battery waste into high-performing new battery

facts: Outgoing Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp headlined his final breakfast gathering of the state’s preeminent business and political leaders at the Georgia Chamber of Commerce's annual Eggs & Issues Breakfast at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta.
The breakfast serves as a preview for Kemp's State of the State address, which is set for 11 a.m. Thursday.
Among other proposals at the breakfast, the governor announced historic investments in affordability, transportation and energy infrastructure, and education and workforce development.
Georgia has maintained its coveted ranking as the No. 1 state for business and celebrated more Georgians working than ever before.
Georgia ports have broken new records for trade and movement of goods, making them the fastest growing on the east coast.
Kemp's administration returned over 9.7 billion dollars to taxpayers through tax cuts, homestead tax exemptions, multiple tax rebates, and suspended state gas taxes.
Making everyday life more affordable for hardworking Georgians will continue as a top priority for the upcoming legislative session.
Commissioner John King announced three auto insurers are lowering their rates in 2026 following the passage of tort reform legislation.
Georgia's Public Service Commission approved a three-year rate freeze for Georgia Power customers to ensure reliable, affordable energy.
The state is positioning itself as a national leader in renewable, reliable energy through smart, locally driven growth and investment while ensuring large load customers pay more.
Kemp announced his budget proposal will invest an additional 1.8 billion dollars to increase roadway capacity on I-75 south in Henry County.

The I-75 south funding will construct dedicated express lanes for both the North and South-bound directions to address bottlenecks for nearly 200,000 vehicles a day, including a quarter that are trucks hauling goods.

GDOT and SRTA will manage the I-75 south project, with initial estimates indicating that once completed, as much as 70 percent more vehicles will be able to pass through that corridor during rush hours.
Kemp announced an additional 200-million-dollar investment to continue improvements along Gorgia 316, commonly known as University Parkway, from Gwinnett County to Athens.
The GA 316 funding will go toward ongoing upgrades of intersections to interchanges, over passes, and other safer connections to create a true expressway.
The state will provide an additional 250 million dollars for community-level road projects through the Local Maintenance and Improvement Grant program this year.
In the prior two years, the state put a total of 515 million dollars into LMIG grants, doubling the initial investment.
There will be no match required from local governments for the new LMIG funding round.
The state will add another 100 million dollars to strengthen and improve bridges across rural communities in Georgia.

Kemp's amended budget proposal includes 35 million dollars in one-time money to create a natural gas infrastructure improvement fund within the Georgia Environmental Finance Authority, following Senate Bill 13.
The natural gas infrastructure fund will particularly benefit rural Georgia communities to improve quality of life and grow their ability to attract economic development projects.
Since taking office, Kemp's administration will have invested roughly 6 billion dollars in transportation, water, and energy infrastructure projects across the state.

Kemp announced that in the coming days his administration will propose the Education and Workforce Strategy Act to ensure unprecedented collaboration between the University and Technical College Systems, the Georgia Department of Education, and others continues.
Three new articulation agreements were announced between higher-ed systems in the fields of teacher preparation, cybersecurity, and accounting, building on a first compact in nursing.
The state will establish a cutting-edge Career Navigator application this year to connect students, job seekers, and employers across Georgia.
Labor Commissioner Bárbara Rivera Holmes is leading efforts to connect Georgians in every part of the state, including rural areas, with jobs coming to their communities.
Kemp announced the creation of a new Homelessness Response Grant program with a one-time state investment of 50 million dollars to tackle homelessness in Atlanta and other urban areas.

The Homelessness Response Grant will be coupled with funds from public, private, and non-profit partners, with awards going to local governments and nonprofit organizations addressing street-level homelessness.
Resources for the grant will go to emergency shelter and transitional housing, street outreach and engagement teams, and wraparound services like mental healthcare and drug rehabilitation.
Georgia will host a major influx of visitors this summer comparable to the 1996 Olympics, coinciding with the FIFA World Cup in Atlanta in the coming months.
source_title: Kemp outlines priorities for affordability, infrastructure, workforce

facts: RTX subsidiary Pratt & Whitney plans to invest $200 million to expand its Columbus, Georgia, manufacturing site.
The investment will add new equipment to boost output for both military and commercial engines.
The investment will fund a seventh isothermal forging press.
The seventh isothermal forging press is expected online in 2028.
The press will produce critical rotating compressor and turbine disks.
The press could increase production of these parts by approximately 30 percent.
The expanded production will support the geared turbofan (GTF) family and the F135 fighter engine, among others.
This expansion follows a $70 million project at the adjacent Columbus Engine Center.
The previous project added 81,000 square feet (7,525 square meters) for maintenance, repair, and overhaul.
The previous project raised GTF engine service capacity by 25 percent.
The Columbus site is located roughly 90 miles south of Atlanta.
The site supports both defense and commercial programs.
The site maintains GTF engines for the Airbus A320neo, A220, and Embraer E-Jets E2.
The site services F117 and F100 engines used in the C-17, F-15, and F-16.
Columbus Forge produces compressor airfoils and turbine disks for multiple engine programs, including the F135.
The F135 engine powers the F-35 Lightning II.
The expansion comes amid growing demand for narrowbody aircraft and sustainment services.
To date, over 2,600 GTF-powered aircraft have been delivered globally.
More than 1,300 F135 engines have been produced for international F-35 operators.
The Columbus site employs more than 2,600 workers.
The Columbus site has received over $1 billion in cumulative investment since 2008.
source_title: Pratt & Whitney to Invest $200M in Georgia Engine Manufacturing Site

facts: The Georgia Transportation Alliance, an affiliate of the Georgia Chamber of Commerce, selected Joseph Santoro to serve as its new Executive Director.
Joseph Santoro starts his new position on December 6 following a four-year tenure with the Council for Quality Growth.
Santoro previously served as Director of Policy & Government Affairs for the Council for Quality Growth.

Over the last four years, Santoro worked on local, regional, and state policies related to infrastructure planning, legislative initiatives, regulatory issues, land use, transportation, and environmental planning impacting the development and growth industry.
Santoro has over 10 years of government affairs experience working for membership-based organizations in metro Atlanta evaluating local, state, and federal policy priorities.
Santoro has been a Georgia Chamber Government Affairs Committee member since 2014 through various roles.
Santoro is a 2023 graduate of the Atlanta Regional Commission Regional Leadership Institute.
Santoro earned a B.S. in Public Policy from the Georgia Institute of Technology.

Santoro stated he looks forward to advancing the Board leadership's mission on identifying and promoting long-term transportation solutions to modernize and expand Georgia’s multimodal transportation system.
Santoro noted that the state’s transportation system fosters economic development, job creation, and enhances the quality of life for Georgia families.

Nick Juliano, Partner at Impact Public Affairs and Chair of the Georgia Transportation Alliance, stated that Georgia is at a critical moment for its transportation industry, particularly regarding freight and logistics.

Chris Clark, President and CEO of the Georgia Chamber of Commerce, described Santoro’s broad experience with statewide transportation stakeholders and trusted economic development insight as an ideal fit for the Executive Director role.
Clark highlighted that Georgia has extensive surface transportation networks, two world-class ports, and one of the largest railroad systems in the country.
Clark stated that Georgia’s transportation and logistics industry is an essential driver of the state's record-breaking economic success.
Clark expressed a goal to ensure Georgia remains the number one state in the country to do business.

The Georgia Transportation Alliance is the state’s only organization focused solely on identifying and promoting long-term transportation solutions specifically connected to economic development and job creation.
The alliance works closely with the Georgia Chamber of Commerce, the Georgia Department of Transportation, and other key stakeholders in Georgia and Washington, D.C.
The alliance's goal is to ensure an efficient and safe statewide multi-modal transportation system.

links.context: Learn more about the Georgia Transportation Alliance

source_title: Economic Development Expert Tapped to Lead Georgia Transportation Alliance - Middle Georgia CEO

facts: Georgia achieved an unprecedented milestone of being ranked the No. 1 state for business by Area Development magazine for 10 consecutive years.
The ranking is based on scores from approximately 50 leading site consulting firms across the U.S. in 14 categories.
Georgia placed in the top 10 for all 14 categories, earning the No. 1 spot in seven classifications and claiming the overall No. 1 ranking.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the milestone alongside constitutional officers, former Governor Nathan Deal, GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson, and other state and local leaders.
Governor Kemp stated that Georgia has brought record-breaking jobs and investments to all four corners of the state, especially to rural Georgia.

Dennis Shea, Publisher and President of Area Development, congratulated Governor Kemp, the Georgia Legislature, Commissioner Pat Wilson, and the GDEcD team for earning the No. 1 ranking for a 10th consecutive year.
Area Development Staff Editor Steve Kaelble noted that Georgia is uniformly at or near the top of most business location lists due to assembling building blocks that decision-makers look for.
Georgia earned a No. 1 ranking for the 14th year in a row for its workforce development program, Georgia Quick Start.
Georgia also earned a No. 1 ranking for competitive labor market.
The Georgia Quick Start program is offered through the Technical College System of Georgia and delivers customized training targeted at new and expanding businesses.
The Quick Start program helps attract companies in manufacturing, biotechnology, information technology, and other sectors by providing tech-savvy workers with specific skills.
Lt. Governor Burt Jones highlighted partnerships between the administration, Georgia General Assembly, and business communities as key to maintaining the top ranking.
Georgia's competitive advantages include an affordable cost of doing business and lower rates through a versatile energy mix.
Speaker of the House Jon Burns cited Georgia's logistics advantage, including the nation's fastest growing container port, the world's busiest airport, and a robust road and rail network.
The state continues to invest strategically in infrastructure to support its economy and maintain its business ranking.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson credited successive governors, local leadership, and the Georgia General Assembly for supporting a partnership approach to economic development.
The University System and Technical College System of Georgia have been working with economic developers to prepare Georgians for future workforce needs, giving the state a competitive advantage.
source_title: Georgia Ranked Number 1 State for Business for 10th Consecutive Year - AllOnGeorgia

facts: Held a ceremony on October 9 commemorating the 10-year anniversary of its establishment in Pendergrass, GA.
Jackson County Chairman of Commissioners Tom Crowe attended as a VIP guest.
State Department of Commerce International Investment Director Scott McMurray attended as a VIP guest.
President Akira Onishi from Toyota Industries Corporation Japan, TCGA's parent company, attended as a VIP guest.
Denso Managing Officer Katsuhisa Shimokawa attended as a VIP guest.
Consul General Sunaga attended as a VIP guest.
State Senator Frank Ginn, State Representative Tommy Benton, Jefferson Mayor Roy Plot, and economic development officials attended from the local community.
Approximately 500 company employees attended the celebratory event.
Founded in 2004 as a joint venture by Denso International America, Inc. (the US subsidiary of the Denso Corporation) along with Toyota Industries North America, Inc. (TINA, Indianapolis, Columbus).
Manufactures automobile air-conditioning compressors.
Employs approximately 580 employees.
In 2012, TCGA air-compressor production volume topped 1 million units.
In 2013, TCGA and TICA began a $190 million joint production expansion plan.
The 2013 joint production expansion plan would create 120 new jobs.
source_title: Consulate-General of Japan in Atlanta

facts: TACG and TICA are two entities of the Toyota Industries Group, an international corporation originating in Kariya, Japan in 1926.
Fortune magazine has recognized Toyota Industries Corporation as one of the World’s Most Admired Motor Vehicle Parts Suppliers.
In 2017, Georgia honored TACG and TICA as one of the State’s Top Automotive Suppliers.
More than 1,300 associates make up the heart of the two local companies and contribute a positive economic impact in Jackson County and the surrounding region.
The company's customers span across North America, representing many of the world’s most respected brands.
As the largest manufacturer of vehicle compressors for air conditioning systems, its products represent over 40 percent of the worldwide compressor market.
Toyota Industries Group companies are among the world’s leading makers of automobile air-conditioning compressors and AC/DC inverters.
TD Automotive Compressor Georgia (TACG) was established in 2004 as a joint venture between Toyota Industries North America Inc. and DENSO International America Inc.
TACG is a 943,000 square foot facility built on 150 acres of land in Jackson County, Georgia.

Toyota Industries Compressor Parts America, Co. (TICA) was established in 2012 to expand Toyota Industries Corporation’s production capacity for variable displacement type compressors in North America.
TICA is a 600,000 square foot facility which is physically connected to TACG, its primary customer.

In November 2020, the company expanded production to produce DC-AC inverters that allow electronics such as cellphones, laptops, job-site tools and small appliances to plug in and run off power from the vehicle.
The manufacturing process involves roles including assembly, logistics, electronics, CNC machine operation, sand & die cast operations, maintenance & engineering, and administration.
Toyota Industries Corporation was founded by Sakichi Toyoda, a visionary Japanese entrepreneur and inventor who invented the Toyoda Wooden Hand Loom in 1890.

The company expanded from textile machinery and materials-handling equipment into electronics, automobiles, and components, including automobile air-conditioning compressors and DC-AC inverters for the North American market.
In 2017, TACG and TICA were named Georgia’s Automotive Supplier of the Year.
Today, TACG and TICA have grown to approximately 1,300 associates working together to supply products to many of the world’s top vehicle manufacturers.
The Georgia-based company is located in Pendergrass and Jackson County.
The community is within an hour’s drive to Atlanta and minutes of Lake Lanier.

Local education opportunities include Jefferson and Jackson County school systems, the University of Georgia in Athens, the University of North Georgia and Brenau University in Gainesville, UNG’s Dahlonega Campus, and Georgia Tech in Atlanta.
source_title: About Us Toyota Industries Group

facts: Toyota Industries North America also operates under the name Toyota Inds Group Tacg-Tica.
The organization is located at 1000 Valentine Industrial Pkwy, Pendergrass, GA 30567.
This location is in Jackson County and the Jefferson, GA Metropolitan Area.
The company primarily operates in the Automobiles, New and Used business industry within the Automotive Dealers and Gasoline Service Stations sector.

The company is engaged in Manufacturing, Importing, and Exporting.

entity_name: Georgia Manufacturing Extension Partnership (GaMEP) at Georgia Tech
facts: Georgia is home to more than 200 automotive-related facilities.
The automotive industry contributes more than $3 billion to the state’s economy.
GaMEP at Georgia Tech supports the automotive industry by providing specialized assistance and expertise.
GaMEP helps companies meet quality, efficiency, and sustainability goals.
Quality Core Tools are available for planning, launching, and validating automotive products and processes.
Automotive manufacturers and suppliers must meet ISO 9001 quality system requirements in addition to IATF 16949 specific requirements.
GaMEP provides training on Advanced Product Quality Planning (APQP) for designing and developing new automotive products.
GaMEP provides training on Failure Mode Effects Analysis (FMEA) to determine potential failures with parts and processes.
GaMEP provides training on the Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) to prove reliable and consistent parts meet customer requirements.
GaMEP provides training on Statistical Process Control to verify if processes are operating in a controlled state and meeting customer requirements.
GaMEP provides training on Measurement System Analysis to recognize gauge variation and ensure calculations capture true process variations.
Receiving Quality Core Tools training helps reduce production defects, meet buyer demands, acquire new business, and increase profit margins.
The Georgia Tech Manufacturing 4.0 Consortium provides access to state-of-the-art advanced manufacturing research facilities to deploy manufacturing technologies and innovation into the market.
GaMEP offers customized automotive industry services including lean manufacturing implementation.
Customized services include Single Minute Exchange of Dies (SMED).
Customized services include quality management systems for ISO/IATF 16949 compliance.
Customized services include automation and Industry 4.0 technologies.
Customized services include supply chain and logistics optimization.
Customized services include Total Productive Maintenance (TPM).
Customized services include workforce training and development.
Customized services include sustainability and energy efficiency initiatives.
Customized services include risk assessment and operational resilience.
Customized services include Lean Six Sigma.

Partnering with GaMEP provides a trusted advisor with automotive expertise, access to proven tools and methodologies, improved efficiency, reduced waste, enhanced product quality, and competitiveness in a rapidly evolving market.
GaMEP hosts events and training courses designed specifically for automotive manufacturers.
GaMEP offers customized on-site training options.
Trenton Pressing is highlighted as a success story for connecting to new technology and strategic customers.
An article features a Q&A with Mike Stonecipher regarding EV Manufacturing in Georgia.
NuGen Systems, a manufacturer of rechargeable battery-based solutions started in 2016 by a co-founder, is highlighted as a success story for winning with safety and sustainability.
GaMEP divides the state into ten regions led by region managers who live and work within their served region.
source_title: Automotive Industry in Georgia | GaMEP at Georgia Tech

facts: Trenton Pressing broke ground on a $10 million facility to expand its manufacturing.
The expansion will add about 20 new jobs to the company's existing workforce of 220.
The new facility will be 105,000 square feet.
The project will help the company expand its assembly services and increase its manufacturing capacity.
Construction should be completed by December 2023, according to the company.
The expansion will allow the company to better service its growth and meet manufacturing needs.
The company's clients include BMW, Club Car, and ZF, among others.

links.context: Link to learn more about the expansion

source_title: Trenton Pressing is investing $10 million and adding about 20 more employees to its workforce of 220 - Greater Chattanooga Economic Partnership

facts: Trenton Pressing’s $12 million expansion project (which began last spring) was officially marked complete with a ribbon cutting on June 28.
The new facility adds 100,000 square feet to the automotive supplier’s manufacturing operations.

Hans Pfunder (chief operating officer) stated pride in creating parts and products for some of the best-known brands in the world right here in Dade County, Ga., noting the facility will allow them to put even more products in the market thanks to local talent.

Evan Stone (IDA executive director) emphasized retaining and growing their existing industrial base is high on the list of priorities in local economic development, stating that more jobs mean more opportunities for local talent to make a good wage, which creates economic mobility and success.
source_title: Two Ribbon Cuttings: Valley Venues And Trenton Pressing Celebrate Expansions – Dade County Sentinel

facts: Trenton Pressing was established in 1995, starting with its first pressing department.
The company has grown to three buildings and more than 192,000 sq. ft.
These facilities serve metal stamping processes, welding & assembly processes, and a warehouse & shipping department.
Trenton Pressing operates as a direct Tier 1 supplier to customers such as BMW and Club Car.
The company works collaboratively with customers as a strategic supplier to develop innovative solutions that add value and meet real business needs.
Management is committed to providing the highest quality parts and excellent manufacturing services.
Hans Pfunder is cited in the company's management message regarding quality and innovation commitments.
Trenton Pressing prioritizes continuous improvement, safety, quality, and customer satisfaction across its operations.
The organization aims to be profitable to guarantee employees' long-term job security.
Cross-training is available to allow employees to move up in the company.
Employees highlight a strong culture of teamwork, safety, and mutual support at the facility.
Trenton Pressing is committed to safeguarding employee health and well-being while giving back to communities.
The company maintains environmental protection commitments by preventing adverse impacts and exceeding legal requirements where possible.
source_title: About — Trenton Pressing

facts: Battery maker LG Energy Solution has agreed to sell its stake of its electric vehicle joint venture battery plant building in Ohio to Honda Development and Manufacturing of America for $2.85 billion, according to a Dec. 23 regulatory filing.

The plans for the joint venture EV battery plant were announced in August 2022 and included an initial investment commitment of $3.5 billion by Honda and LG Energy Solution to produce lithium-ion batteries for electric Honda and Acura models for the North American market beginning in 2026.
The transaction includes the building and infrastructure assets related to the joint venture, but excludes the land and equipment.
The joint venture will continue to use the facility under a lease agreement, with no changes to production or operational plans, according to the Korea Herald.
The deal is scheduled to close on Feb. 28.
The L-H Battery Co. joint venture between LG Energy Solution and Honda was formally established in January 2023 to produce battery cells in the U.S. for future Honda and Acura EVs.

Although the two companies planned an initial investment commitment of $3.5 billion in the Ohio plant, the total investment was expected to reach up to $4.4 billion, with a targeted annual capacity of 40GWh of battery cells.
According to LG Energy Solution’s regulatory filing, the deal to divest its stake in the battery plant building is intended to “increase the operational efficiency” of its joint venture with Honda.
The plant may expand production of batteries for energy storage system applications, according to the Korea Herald.

In October 2024, LG Energy Solution announced it will focus more on producing energy storage system batteries as part of its mid-to-long-term business strategy, which are in high demand due to the AI data center construction boom in the U.S.
LG Energy Solution said its long-term plans to develop advanced batteries for the auto industry will remain a priority.
Honda announced plans to establish an “EV Hub” in Ohio in March 2023, which included the construction of the joint venture battery plant with LG Energy Solution.

With changing market conditions, Honda and other automakers, including General Motors and Ford Motor Co., have revised their EV timelines and no longer require the battery capacity previously anticipated.
In October 2024, GM announced it was cutting thousands of jobs at its U.S. EV factories and battery plants amid slowing demand.
The plans include the laying off 550 workers at its Ultium Cells EV battery joint venture plant in Ohio and pausing production at a second Ultium Cells production site in Tennessee.
Last month, Ford Motor Co. and battery maker SK On reached an agreement to dissolve their EV battery joint venture “BlueOval SK.”

The JV was established in September 2021 as part of a planned $11.4 billion investment by the two companies to build three large-scale manufacturing plants in the U.S. to produce batteries for Ford’s future EVs.
Also last month, Ford terminated its multi-billion-dollar supply deal with LG Energy Solution for 75 GWh of battery cells due to weakening EV demand.

Although Honda will acquire LG Energy Solution’s multi-billion-dollar stake in the Ohio battery plant, the automaker has renewed its focus on launching new hybrid models as it pursues its more long-term electrification goals.
In a May 2025 press briefing, Honda’s Global CEO Toshihiro Mibe announced that the automaker was reducing its EV investments by $20.7 billion through 2030 to launch new hybrid models.
The automaker is targeting global sales of over 3.6 million vehicles by 2030, 2.2 million of which are expected to be hybrid models.
source_title: Honda buying LG Energy Solution’s stake in Ohio EV battery plant building for $2.85B | Utility Dive
