facts: On March 13th 2019, Valeo announced it is to invest €44M in a major RD&I investment programme.
The investment builds on the Group’s strong capability in Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS).
The programme positions Valeo at the forefront of developments in autonomous vehicles.
The investment will lead to the creation of 50 new R&D roles over the next three years.
The Irish R&D centre will grow to over 500 people and become one of the largest R&D facilities in Ireland.
The project is supported by the Irish Government through IDA Ireland.
Marc Vrecko, Valeo Comfort and Driving Assistance Systems Business Group President, stated that the Tuam facility is one of the leading Research centres in the Group.
With IDA Ireland support, Valeo continues to have a sustained and rewarding investment in Ireland.
The expansion aims to build on proven innovation in Product and Process R&D.
The investment will help fuel the global growth of the Vision Product Line.
The investment extends Valeo as a global leader for ADAS systems and related MaaS products.
Sean Canney TD, Minister of State for Rural Affairs & Natural Resources, welcomed the announcement.
Valeo is described as a substantial and valued employer in Tuam, Galway, and the West region.
The announcement demonstrates significant commitment by the company to the Tuam site.
The site positions itself at the leading edge of the development of autonomous vehicles.
The project shows that global companies can find skilled workers, necessary infrastructure, services, and a pro-business environment in regional locations.
IDA Ireland CEO Martin Shanahan described the project as focused on new emerging and disruptive advances in Autonomous Vehicles.
Valeo is characterized as a technology leader in autonomous technologies with a global remit.
The project helps in the development of a cluster in Ireland around AV.
The project will add significant value to the company’s operations in Tuam and benefit Tuam, Galway, and the West region substantially.
Valeo is an automotive supplier at the heart of three revolutions shaping the automotive industry: autonomous car, electrification of the powertrain, and digital mobility.
As a technology company, Valeo proposes innovative products and systems that contribute to the reduction of CO2 emissions and to the development of autonomous and connected cars.
In 2018, the Group generated sales of 19.3 billion euros.
In 2018, the Group invested 13% of its original equipment sales in Research and Development.
At December 31, 2018, Valeo had 186 plants, 21 research centers, 38 development centers, and 15 distribution platforms.
At December 31, 2018, Valeo employed 113,600 people in 33 countries worldwide.
Valeo is listed on the Paris stock exchange and is a member of the CAC 40 index.
Valeo is the global market leader and supplier of choice for sensors, including vision cameras and ADAS systems.
Automotive cameras act as the eyes of the vehicle, combined with advanced software algorithms and artificial intelligence to enable vehicles to make the right decision while driving.
The R&D centre in Tuam is the Group’s worldwide reference centre for vision systems.
The site includes a state of the art production facility alongside an R&D Centre of Excellence.
The Tuam centre has responsibility for the development and manufacture of automotive camera vision systems for global markets.
Products developed by the R&D centre in Tuam are also in production across the world at Valeo sites in Germany, Hungary, Mexico, and China.
Valeo is a major employer in the West region with a total workforce of over 1,000 people.
The 450-strong R&D centre consists of many highly skilled engineers and scientists across a range of disciplines.
The Tuam R&D team works on the latest technologies in Artificial intelligence and Deep Learning for application at the cutting edge of advancements in autonomous vehicles.
source_title: Valeo to invest €44M in Autonomous Vehicle RD&I, creating 50 jobs in its R&D centre  in Tuam, Irelan | IDA Ireland

facts: Sales reached 20,037 million euros in 2022, up by 16%.
Accelerated growth in ADAS (up 29% LFL) and electrification (up 32 % LFL).
Original equipment sales outperformed automotive production in all regions by 3 percentage points worldwide.
Strong growth in aftermarket sales, up 9% LFL.
EBITDA margin reached 12.0% of sales and EBIT margin reached 3.2 % of sales.
Generated free cash flow of 388 million euros.
Order intake increased by 48% to 32.6 billion euros, confirming growth prospects in electrification and ADAS.
Reduced greenhouse gas emissions in line with the “CAP 50” carbon neutrality roadmap.
Proposed a 2022 dividend of €0.38 per share to be presented at the next Shareholders’ Meeting.

CEO Christophe Périllat stated that all financial objectives set at the beginning of 2022 were achieved despite high inflation, an electronic components shortage, lockdown measures in China, and the conflict in Ukraine.
Growth in original equipment sales outperformed automotive production across all regions.
EBIT and EBITDA margins aligned with guidance published in February 2022.
Free cash flow generation of 388 million euros exceeded expectations.
Order intake growth included several particularly strategic orders in electrification and ADAS.
Performance confirms the successful first-year implementation of the Move Up strategic plan.
Targeting strong year-on-year sales growth in 2023 by leveraging acceleration in electrification and ADAS megatrends.
Aiming to curb inflation impact through productivity and cost reduction measures combined with price increases passed to customers.
Targeting 2023 sales between 22 and 23 billion euros.
Targeting an improved 2023 operating margin between 3.2% and 4.0%.
Targeting 2023 free cash flow generation of over 320 million euros.
Expecting a significant improvement in financial performance during the second half of 2023.

links.context: Download link for the press release

source_title: Press release – 2022 Financial Results | Valeo

facts: Valeo and Moodify, an Israeli company that develops scent solutions for improved personal performance and well-being, have announced their first collaboration to bring a solution for car malodor to the market.
The groundbreaking solution is based on the smart diffusion of a special scent called Moodify White.
The solution is based on 10 years of research at Israel’s top technology research institute.
Moodify White effectively masks the perception of foul smells in cars, like cigarettes, mold and decay.
The scent is made entirely of environmentally friendly, hypoallergenic and non-intrusive compounds.
The first version of Moodify White will be released in early 2020.
Samples of the solution will be available at the Consumer Electronics Show [CES] in Valeo and Moodify’s exhibit area from January 7 to January 10, 2020 in Las Vegas.

Georges de Pelsemaeker, Ph.D., health and wellbeing director at Valeo, stated that combining Valeo’s deep knowledge of the automotive industry and expertise in cabin air quality solutions with Moodify’s technological capacity enables them to bring an ultimate solution to bad smell in cars, a problem that pains the entire automotive supply chain.

Yigal Sharon, CEO of Moodify, described Moodify White as the harbinger of the digital scent revolution and noted that their technology uses neuroscience and chemo-signals to tailor-make scent solutions easily.
Moodify's investors include Toyota AI Ventures and Next Gear Ventures.
source_title: Moodify and Valeo Collaborate for Car Malodor Solution | Perfumer & Flavorist

facts: French global auto parts manufacturer Valeo plans to invest around ₹1,000 crore over the next five to six years in Tamil Nadu.
The planned expansion encompasses manufacturing facilities, aftermarket operations, and a global technical center.
Valeo plans to increase its talent pool by 3,000-odd employees in the Chennai region.
Valeo will increase its workforce in its state-of-the-art production facilities in Tamil Nadu for the expansion of its current product portfolio as well as new technologies.
Valeo’s aftermarket operations will witness substantial growth through expanded distribution networks and service centers in Tamil Nadu.
The expansion aims to provide enhanced accessibility to Valeo’s top-tier automotive products and services, catering to a wider customer base across the region.
Jayakumar G, group president and managing director of Valeo India, stated that the company has been in Tamil Nadu for over 25 years and expanded its diverse product portfolios in manufacturing.
Over the last 15 years, Valeo established a Global Tech Center for R&D and software activities in Chennai, citing it as testament to the local talent.

Jayakumar G plans to expand operations and R&D in the upcoming years and thanked the government for its continued support, praising the State’s conducive business ecosystem and proactive industrial policies.

At the Global Investors Meet 2024 (GIM 2024), Valeo’s stall in the French pavilion, in collaboration with Team France, will exhibit Four pillars of Valeo’s MoveUp Strategy: electrification acceleration, ADAS acceleration, lighting everywhere, and interior experience reinvention.
The aftermarket specialist Valeo Service will display a wide range of product portfolio for the aftermarket segment at GIM 2024.
In India, Valeo started its footprint in 1997 in Chennai for manufacturing Clutch and Friction Facing materials.
Valeo established its first R&D Centre in Chennai in 2005 for Powertrain Systems.
Valeo subsequently expanded with the Lighting Division, Thermal Systems, Wiper Division, Vision Systems Software Division, and Valeo Service.
Valeo is working with all mobility actors to deliver solutions for electric mobility in India.
source_title: French firm Valeo bets big on T.N., plans to invest ₹1,000 crore over next 5 years   - The Hindu

facts: Valeo has started construction of a new manufacturing facility in McAllen, Texas.
The French automotive supplier will invest $225m over the next five years in the site.
The facility will span 337,000ft2.
The project will create up to 500 jobs.
Production is due to begin in late 2027.
The McAllen facility supports one of Valeo's largest orders in its history.
The project is part of Valeo's "Elevate 2028" strategic plan focused on software-defined vehicle (SDV) technology.
The McAllen plant will produce General Motors' central compute unit, a liquid-cooled system that processes data from multiple sensors and systems to manage essential vehicle functions.

Jeffrey Shay, Valeo North America president, said: "We're proud to contribute technologies that enable the next generation of software-defined vehicles with this significant investment in the region and the future of automotive technology."
Jeffrey Shay added: "We're grateful for the opportunity to grow our business with GM in the state of Texas."

Kristin Toth, purchasing executive director, electrical systems, software and connectivity at General Motors, said: "A software-defined vehicle architecture is a key enabler to dramatically improve the customer experience through faster connectivity, richer entertainment options and more frequent updates."
Kristin Toth added: "Projects such as Valeo's plant in Texas will play a crucial role as we bring our next-generation electrical architecture to market."

Last month, Valeo announced plans to invest more than €200m ($236.8m) in India to expand electrification, advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) and artificial intelligence (AI) engineering activities under its Elevate 2028 strategy.
Valeo plans to triple its local revenue in India to about €700m by 2028.

The funding for the India expansion is about Rs21.49bn at current exchange rates and will enlarge Valeo's manufacturing and engineering presence in India to support domestic and export vehicle programmes.

In September, Valeo opened a new production site in Shanghai's Waigang Industrial Park, situated in Jiading District, to enhance its manufacturing capabilities for advanced driving assistance systems (ADAS) in China.
The article was originally created and published by Just Auto, a GlobalData owned brand.
source_title: Valeo breaks ground on $225m manufacturing plant in US

facts: SK On is headquartered in Seoul, South Korea.
SK On was spun off from SK Innovation in July 2021 and launched as an independent entity in October 2021.
SK On is a subsidiary of SK Innovation, which is a member of SK Group.
SK On's batteries are used for electric vehicles and energy storage systems (ESS).
From 2024 to 2025, SK Trading International, SK Enterm, and SK Enmove were merged into SK On.
In December 2022, SK On announced a new battery plant joint venture with Hyundai in Cartersville, Georgia.
In February 2023, SK On withdrew from a joint venture with Ford and Koç Holding in Turkey.
In August, construction started on a cathode material plant joint venture with EcoPro BM and Ford in Bécancour, Quebec.
Construction on the Bécancour, Quebec cathode material plant was paused multiple times.
In November 2024, SK Trading International, a crude oil and petroleum products affiliate, merged into SK On.
In February 2025, SK Enterm, a commercial tank terminal operator under SK Trading International, merged into SK On.
In March, SK On secured a battery supply contract with Nissan.
In April, Slate selected SK On as its battery supplier.
In July, SK On announced the acquisition of SK Enmove, a lubricant maker under SK Innovation.
In September, SK On completed construction on an all-solid-state battery plant in Daejeon, South Korea.
In November, SK On swapped stakes in its two joint ventures with Eve Power in China and took full ownership of the Jiangsu site.
In December, SK On and Ford announced the dissolution of their joint venture BlueOval SK, with SK On taking ownership of the Tennessee battery facility.
In March 2026, SK On laid off more than a third of its workforce at its plant in Commerce, Georgia.
In 2019, SK Innovation began construction of its first U.S. electric vehicle battery manufacturing facility in Commerce, Georgia.
The Commerce, Georgia plant is a 2.4 million-square-foot facility located on 283 acres along Interstate 85, approximately 65 miles northeast of Atlanta.
The Commerce, Georgia plant was completed in two phases: Phase 1 in 2020 and Phase 2 in late 2021, at a combined investment of approximately $2.6 billion.
The Commerce, Georgia plant supplies batteries to Volkswagen and Hyundai.
Following the October 2021 spin-off, the Commerce, Georgia plant became part of SK On.

SK On's manufacturing locations include Seosan (South Korea), Commerce (United States), Stanton (United States), Kingston (United States), Yancheng (China), Beijing (China), Changzhou (China), Komárom (Hungary), and Iváncsa (Hungary).
source_title: SK On - Wikipedia

facts: Valeo and MAHLE signed a Joint Development Agreement to expand their product range of magnet-free electric motors to upper segment applications.
The partnership will develop an innovative magnet-free electric axle system targeting upper segment electric vehicles with peak power ranging from 220 kW to 350 kW.
The new technology is named the iBEE system (inner Brushless Electrical Excitation).
Valeo will contribute expertise in electric motors, highly efficient inverters, and associated motor control laws.
MAHLE will contribute expertise on magnet-free rotors utilizing its MAHLE Contactless Transmitter (MCT) technology.
The companies are developing an innovative cooling concept to achieve an outstanding continuous to peak power ratio.
The carbon footprint of the new system is expected to be reduced by more than 40% versus a permanent magnet electric motor of equivalent power.
Testing of the first prototypes will be completed by the end of 2024.
Xavier Dupont, Valeo Power Division CEO, stated that MAHLE develops the EESM rotor and brushless excitation system, while Valeo provides dedicated control embedded in its inverter.
Martin Wellhoeffer, Member of the MAHLE Group Management Board, noted that the new e-axle will set a new benchmark in packaging and efficiency for EESM based e-axles.
Valeo's first EESM development was launched in 2022 in cooperation with a European OEM to create a new magnet-free electric motor generation.
Initial targets for the 2022 project included achieving an all-in-one architecture with a new stator and cooling technology to increase power density by 30% compared to current motors on the road.
A second initial target was to shrink the carbon footprint by 30% versus an electric motor equipped with magnets (PMSM).
A validation phase for more than 6 months was successfully completed, with results exceeding initial expectations in power and efficiency.
The performance matches that of permanent magnet electric motors for B/C segment vehicles while offering benefits like no rare earth materials and a lower carbon footprint.
MAHLE has been focusing on developing electric drive systems as part of its electrification strategy, leveraging its contactless transmitter technology for magnet-free electric motors.
Avoiding rare earth materials contributes to e-mobility sustainability and offers advantages in costs and resource security.
The key feature of the technology is inductive, contactless, and wear-free power transmission, allowing motor operation at benchmark efficiency levels.
source_title: Valeo and MAHLE expand their product range of magnet free electric motors to upper segment applications - MAHLE Newsroom

entity_name: SIEMENS Valeo EV PowerCharger 360/3500 HE I
canonical_name: SIEMENS Valeo EV PowerCharger 360/3500 HE I
facts: The SIEMENS Valeo EV PowerCharger 360/3500 HE I has an outbound voltage of 170-410 VDC.
The maximum amperage is 12A.
The maximum power output is 3.3 kW.
The AC input range is 82-275 VAC.
The unit is fully sealed with an IP67 rating.
It is equipped with water cooling.
It includes overvoltage protection.
The charging current can be controlled by a CAN bus signal.
The dimensions are 310 x 194 x 69 mm.
The weight is 3.5 kg.
The price is €920.00 excluding VAT.
There are 19 units in stock, with backorder availability.
source_title: New! SIEMENS Valeo EV PowerCharger 360/3500 HE I - EV Europe

facts: Valeo and Smovengo joined forces to remanufacture the motors and batteries of the 8,000 electric Vélib' bicycles that tour the streets of Greater Paris.
Smovengo operates Vélib' Métropole, a large-scale public bicycle sharing system in Paris and 64 of its communes.
Valeo offers a range of solutions for small mobility devices such as bicycles, scooters and electric rickshaws.
Valeo's commitment to the e-bike sector includes the supply of Valeo Cyclee electric motors and the circular maintenance of other essential components used in e-bikes.
This service is fully part of Valeo's circular economy strategy (4R) to expand the lifespan of existing mobility systems.
For over 40 years, Valeo has put its expertise in the design and manufacture of original parts at the service of remanufacturing.
Two Valeo sites specialising in remanufacturing have been selected for the partnership with Smovengo.
Valeo launched the Circular Electronics Lab in Nevers (France) in 2023 to set up a dedicated team combining flexibility, quality and competitiveness to incubate circular electronics projects.
The Nevers unit successfully launched the first ever remanufactured front camera and expanded its circular services for Small Mobility, starting with the repair of batteries for Smovengo.

The “Circular Innovation Factory” in Czechowice (Poland) serves as Valeo's Competence Centre for remanufactured products such as starters, alternators, dual mass flywheels, dual clutches and air conditioning compressors.
The Czechowice site is expanding its remanufacturing activity into the field of micromobility with the motors equipped on Smovengo’s electric bikes.
Since becoming the operator of Vélib' Métropole in 2018, Smovengo covers 65 municipalities and 450 km².
Every day, Smovengo's teams in the field and in the workshops repair 1,000 bikes to ensure a constant availability of the fleet.
Smovengo's R&D department steers efforts to design parts that are increasingly durable, reliable and refurbishable to reduce dependence on Asian suppliers.
Since 2020, Smovengo has implemented a complete industrial process for recycling spare parts, preparing all maintenance parts for a second life or recycled.
Currently, 30 employees are dedicated daily to testing and repairing these components at Smovengo.
A wheel production line in Alfortville produces the 2,500 wheels needed for fleet maintenance each week using new or recycled parts.
Smovengo was certified as a bicycle manufacturer in October 2021, allowing for the annual production of over 2,000 CE-certified electric and mechanical bicycles using new and recycled parts.
Smovengo entered into a partnership with Valeo to gain specific expertise and industrialisation capacity for repairing complex parts such as electric motors and batteries.

Eric Schuler, CEO of Valeo Service, stated the collaboration demonstrates Valeo's commitment to safer and more sustainable mobility and its capability to offer solutions for the electrification of all forms of mobility.

Jacques Greiveldinger, Managing Director of Smovengo, explained the partnership provides ecological, economic and logistical benefits, noting that motor and battery revaluation is only the first step to be extended to many other bicycle parts.
source_title: Valeo and Smovengo join to remanufacture  Vélib' e-bike motors and batteries, ETManufacturing

facts: Valeo inaugurated advanced manufacturing and assembly lines at its Pune facility.

The enhanced facility specializes in producing Valeo’s 3-in-1 Combo Unit, an integrated power electronics solution combining On-Board Chargers (OBCs), DC-DC Converters, and Power Distribution Units (PDUs).
These components are vital for EV efficiency, enabling smoother performance, space optimization, and reduced production costs for Indian automakers.
Xavier Dupont serves as CEO of Valeo Power Division and Group Executive Vice President.
Jayakumar G serves as Group President & Managing Director of Valeo India.
Dupont and Jayakumar G officiated the inauguration of the new lines at the Pune facility.
The 3-in-1 Combo Unit integrates smart automation, modular assembly processes, and sustainable manufacturing practices.
The On-Board Charger (OBC) converts AC grid power to charge high-voltage EV batteries ranging from 7kW to 22kW.
The DC-DC Converter steps down high-voltage power to 12V to support auxiliary systems and low-voltage batteries.
The Power Distribution Unit (PDU) distributes power efficiently across critical EV components.
Valeo aims to reduce India’s reliance on imported EV components and bolster local supply chains through this expansion.
Valeo has been operating in India since 1997.
Valeo maintains eight production sites and a global R&D center across Chennai, Bangalore, Pune, Sanand, and Gurgaon.
Valeo employs over 7,000 professionals in India, with nearly 50% working in engineering roles.
source_title: Valeo Boosts EV Production in Pune for India’s Green Future

facts: Valeo presented its third generation scanning LiDAR, set to make its market debut in 2024.
Geoffrey Bouquot serves as Valeo's Senior Vice-President, R&D and Strategy.

Bouquot stated that Valeo's third generation LiDAR is a major technological advance toward the autonomous vehicle and strengthens Valeo's technological and industrial leadership as the only supplier currently series-producing an automotive-grade LiDAR scanner.
Bouquot noted that the number one goal with the device remains to save lives on the road.
The new LiDAR delivers unrivaled performance in terms of range, resolution, and frame rate.
It reconstructs a 3D real-time image of the vehicle's surroundings at a rate of 4.5 million pixels and 25 frames per second.
Compared to the previous generation, the resolution has been increased 12-fold, the range 3-fold, and the viewing angle 2.5-fold.
The LiDAR can see things that humans, cameras, and radars cannot, enabling driving to be delegated to the vehicle in many situations including level 2 automation and above.
Vehicles equipped with the third-generation scanning LiDAR can manage emergency situations autonomously, including on highways at speeds of up to 130km/h.
The scanning LiDAR detects, recognizes, and classifies all objects located around the car, measuring speed and direction if they are moving.
It adapts to all light conditions and measures the density of raindrops to calculate the right braking distance.
It tracks nearby vehicles even when they are no longer in the driver's line of sight, using algorithms to anticipate trajectories and trigger necessary safety maneuvers.
The LiDAR protects people inside the car and those around it, such as pedestrians and cyclists.
Beyond the vehicles it equips, this LiDAR will alert other vehicles of road hazards via the Cloud.
Valeo designs and manufactures the entire system, including the hardware, software, and associated artificial intelligence that combines collected data to enable instant decision-making.
The software automatically adapts to the environment and improves its performance over time through regular updates.
Valeo's LiDARs are produced in Germany at its Wemding plant in Bavaria, where components are assembled with a micron level of precision.
The plant's production lines draw on Valeo's state-of-the-art expertise in optics, mechanics, and photonics.
300 engineers at Valeo are dedicated solely to this technology, for which over 500 patents have already been filed.
Valeo is the first and only company to produce a scanning LiDAR on an industrial scale, having already produced over 150,000 units.
99% of cars equipped with a scanning LiDAR scanner worldwide are equipped with a Valeo scanning LiDAR.
Up to 30% of premium new vehicles are set to reach level 3 automation by 2030, requiring them to be equipped with LiDAR technology.
Autonomous shuttles, robotaxis, delivery droids, autonomous trucks, and sectors including agriculture, mining, and infrastructure will also need to be equipped with one or more LiDARs.
The LiDAR market is expected to represent more than $50 billion by 2030.
Valeo's new third generation LiDAR will be a key contributor to this change in scale.
source_title: With its new generation LiDAR, Valeo makes autonomous mobility a reality

facts: For the second year in a row, Georgia had a record-breaking year for international trade.
GDEcD's 2022 end-of-year report showed that Georgia’s total trade reached $196 billion in 2022.
Georgia ranked seventh in the U.S. for the dollar value of trade.
The state’s top five trading partners were Korea, Mexico, Canada, Germany and China.
Aerospace products remained the state’s number one export.
Motor vehicles were the top imported product into Georgia.
$148 billion in imports passed through Georgia in 2022, representing a 20% growth from the previous year.
GDEcD leaders attribute the trade increase to the state’s appeal as a desirable location for businesses.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that 85 percent of exporters were small businesses, spanning from the Port of Savannah to the North Georgia mountains and everywhere in between.

Governor Brian Kemp noted that Savannah’s Port plays a major role in the state’s economic growth by providing infrastructure and capacity for businesses of all sizes to participate in the global economy.
Griff Lync, director of the Georgia Ports Authority, stated the GPA is working to fulfill last year’s promise to achieve a 60% increase in port capacity by 2025.

links.context: Related article about Brunswick Harbor modifications

source_title: Georgia breaks international trade record for second consecutive year

facts: Valeo is a pioneer in automotive technology.
Valeo showcased a range of cutting-edge innovations at CES 2024.

Executive Vice President of Corporate Strategy and CTO Geoffrey Bouquot stated that the pace of innovation in the auto industry is mind-blowing and that the showcased innovations are transforming how people will move in the near future.
Valeo has been present at CES for the past 10 years and returned in 2024 to showcase its latest innovations.
The company aims to share how its technology empowers tomorrow’s mobility and contributes to a greener, safer, affordable, and more connected automotive landscape.
Visitors could experience Valeo technology at two locations: booth CP17 on the Central Plaza and a live demonstration area in Piero’s parking lot at 355 Convention Center Drive.
source_title: Valeo Unveils Innovations at CES 2024 | Valeo Service

facts: Valeo, Atul Greentech Private Limited, and Honda Powerpack Energy Private Limited have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU).
Through the MoU, Valeo will provide electric power trains and a powertrain control unit for Atul's electric three-wheelers to work with Honda’s swappable battery solution.
Valeo will provide its 48V integrated compact electric powertrain system along with the Powertrain Control Unit for Atul's upcoming cargo and passenger three-wheelers launching in India soon.
Valeo’s engineers will provide technology integration support to help Atul go to market quickly with Honda’s swappable battery.

Valeo launched a fully integrated compact 48V electric powertrain system in India in 2020 to aid affordable electrification for small mobility vehicles, specifically two- and three-wheelers used for first- and last-mile connectivity.

Jayakumar G, Group President and Managing Director of Valeo India, stated that the tri-party engagement brings domain expertise together to accelerate three-wheeler electrification in both passenger and cargo segments.
Jayakumar G noted that Valeo's CO2-reducing technologies, strong engineering, and local manufacturing expertise will strengthen their partnership in the fast-growing Indian market and other markets.

Jayakumar G added that Valeo has the full spectrum of electric powertrain systems, from low to high voltage, powering vehicles ranging from two- and three-wheel urban vehicles to premium sedans and SUVs in India.
Valeo produces its electric powertrain units at its manufacturing facility in Pune, Maharashtra.
source_title: Valeo: Valeo signs MoU with 2 others to provide electric power trains - Times of India

facts: Vanguard National Trailer Corporation is a wholly owned subsidiary of Shenzhen-based China International Marine Containers (CIMC), a leading transportation equipment company.
The Vanguard factory in Monon, Indiana, employs over 400 people and contributes approximately 80 percent to the local gross domestic product.
Vanguard entered Monon in 2003 with new equipment, investment, and management after HPA Monon closed in May 2002.
The Vanguard factory occupies an area half the size of the town and contains lines of diverse semitrailers.
The two semitrailer companies in White County, Vanguard and Wabash, together produce most of the trailers in the United States.
Vanguard CEO Charlie Mudd states that hundreds of jobs were created in Monon thanks to the CIMC investment.

Mudd believes the Chinese investment brings wider development space and market access, enabling global sharing of ideas, information, resources, suppliers, and strategy across CIMC vehicle group companies.
Vanguard is expanding by opening another factory in Trenton, Georgia, creating about 200 jobs.
Charlie Mudd describes the expansion as a combination of Chinese knowledge and capabilities paired with American knowledge and opportunities, calling it "true collaboration."
Manufacturing director James Wilkerson notes that despite being Chinese-owned, the company is American-run and workers have felt little difference since the acquisition.
Vanguard produces semitrailers from iron sheets and frames designed to haul freight.
source_title: Chinese investment gives wheelpower to small US town - Chinadaily.com.cn

facts: Vanguard National Trailer Corporation, a manufacturer of semi-trailers, will expand into Dade County, bringing 400 new jobs to the area.
The company, based in Indiana, will invest $30 million in the expansion.
That expansion will require development of nearly 100 acres of land and the building of a new 350,000 square-foot facility near Trenton.
The new jobs will include both office and manufacturing positions.

Vanguard president and CEO Charlie Mudd stated the team is excited about expanding production capabilities and thanked the State of Georgia and the Georgia Department of Economic Development for their efforts to help with the project.
Vanguard manufactures dry freight and refrigerated semi-trailer vans for the North American transportation industry.
The company has about 47,000 employees world-wide.
source_title: 400 news jobs coming to Dade County |  | local3news.com

facts: Vanguard National Trailer Corp. is a leading semi-trailer manufacturer based in Monon, Ind.
The company invested about $30 million in a new manufacturing facility in Dade County.
Production started at the Trenton, Ga., plant in August 2016 with 70 employees.
Once operating at full capacity, the new location is expected to create 400 jobs.
Vanguard National Trailer Corp. broke ground on a nearly 100-acre site in the Dade County Industrial Park on September 3, 2015.
The company built a new 356,000 square-foot facility over the course of a year.
Vanguard manufactures dry freight and refrigerated semi-trailer vans for the North American transportation industry.
The newly created jobs include both office and manufacturing positions.
Vanguard National Trailer took advantage of Georgia’s Quick Start program, the nation's No. 1 workforce training program.

Georgia Governor Nathan Deal welcomed Vanguard to Dade County, stating: 'We are excited to welcome Vanguard National Trailer Corp. to Dade County. Their decision to establish a manufacturing and assembly plant in Georgia is a testament to our economic development assets that are critical in generating job opportunities for Georgians. I am confident that Vanguard Trailer Corp. will benefit from opening a new facility in our state and further prove why Georgia is a top destination for job creators.'

Georgia Attorney General Chris Carr, then Commissioner of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, stated: 'We are excited that Vanguard Trailer Corp. is locating to Georgia and taking advantage of one of the state’s greatest economic development assets in Quick Start. I am confident that Vanguard will be successful for many years to come and will find Georgia to be an excellent place in which to do business.'

GDEcD Director of Logistics, Energy, Agribusiness & Food Processing Brittany Holtzclaw supported the company on behalf of Georgia in partnership with the Dade County Industrial Development Authority, the Northwest Georgia Joint Development Authority, Georgia Power, and the Georgia Department of Transportation.
source_title: Top of Georgia | Success Stories

facts: In May 2014, Audia Group announced plans for a new manufacturing plant in Northwest Georgia.
Audia International, Inc. is a plastics compounding, color solutions, and distribution company with a 60-year history of customer focused growth.
Plans for the Walker County facility grew from an initial 240,000-square foot manufacturing facility to more than 300,000 square feet of space.
The facility will serve as the anchor tenant for the new 460-acre Walker County Business Park.
Audia first contacted the development office in August 2012 and initially sought an existing building before shifting to a new build-out.
Site preparation work had already begun, with operations expected to be up and running in the third quarter of 2015.
The company will invest over $50MM and create 200 jobs in Northwest Georgia when the build-out is complete.

Northwest Georgia Joint Development Authority project manager Keith Barclift noted that having an anchor tenant increases the marketability of the greenfield park by providing necessary infrastructure like roads cut and industrial-grade utilities in place.
Barclift commended Walker County Commissioner Heiskell for her foresight in ensuring the site's viability.
source_title: Top of Georgia | Success Stories

facts: A study by McKinsey & Company warns that Georgia cannot afford to ignore dangerous economic trends often masked by positive news.
Georgia became the ninth-largest state economy by 2017, rising from 17th place in 1977.
Atlanta fueled most of Georgia's recent expansion as one of 11 U.S. “superstar cities.”
Atlanta experienced a 3.4% average annual economic growth rate between 2012 and 2017.
The McKinsey study identifies transportation, education, and healthcare as the most pressing weaknesses threatening Georgia's continued economic growth.
Steve Reis, a senior McKinsey partner in Atlanta and co-author of the study, stated that the past is not a guaranteed guide to the future and emphasized the need to address identified shortcomings.
The report highlights workforce skills shortages and infrastructure gaps as critical threats to sustaining economic expansion.
source_title: McKinsey study flags threats to Georgia's economic growth

facts: Vernay Laboratories announced the grand re-opening of its Milledgeville manufacturing plant on April 22, 2025.

The re-opening event will feature a ribbon cutting ceremony, guided plant tours, and remarks from Vernay leadership, the Baldwin County Chamber of Commerce, and invited guests from Milledgeville College, Baldwin High School, and local government.
Doug Cumpston serves as the Plant Manager for the Milledgeville facility.
The updated facility represents Vernay’s commitment to operational excellence, local job growth, and long-term partnerships.
The Milledgeville plant will play an increasingly important role in Vernay’s global supply network.
The Milledgeville plant supports key customers in the Consumer Products and Mobility sectors.
Vanna Villata serves as Vice President of Operations at Vernay.
Vernay operates manufacturing facilities in Georgia across Milledgeville, Griffin, and Kennesaw.
Georgia provides Vernay with access to top-tier talent, strong community partnerships, and a robust industrial ecosystem.
Maurizio Trabucco serves as Director of Human Resources for North America at Vernay.
Vernay is implementing a broader initiative to enhance employee retention and talent attraction across its North American operations.
Vernay Laboratories was founded in 1946 and operates as a privately held firm.
The company designs, develops, and manufactures customized flow-control components and formulates its own compounds for specific applications.
Vernay’s products are utilized in millions of medical devices, automobiles, and consumer products.
The company produces more than 1 billion components per year.
Vernay maintains a library of more than 33,000 elastomeric materials and compound formulations.
The firm operates six manufacturing plants globally located in the U.S., the Netherlands, Italy, and China.

canonical_name: Georgia Electric Grid Modernization & Resilience Projects
facts: At least $1 billion in projects intended to modernize Georgia’s power grid remain in limbo as President Donald Trump seeks to halt congressionally approved funding for infrastructure and climate projects.

The money was spread across several projects and grants to upgrade existing high-voltage transmission lines with modern conductors and other “grid-enhancing technologies,” add new lines, and build more solar arrays and battery storage.

Federal money totaled more than $500 million, with local matches that would make more than double that figure available to invest in critical energy infrastructure as the state contends with rising energy costs.

Tim Lieuwen, executive director of the Strategic Energy Institute at Georgia Tech, noted a contradiction between some of Trump’s actions and his stated aims of supporting energy reliability and growth, but suggested the administration may change course after assembling a cabinet and analyzing projects against its own goals.

Lieuwen stated that building new energy infrastructure, including solar and wind, and encouraging domestic electric vehicles and battery manufacturing fulfill top-line priorities like domestic manufacturing and energy reliability.

Despite at least two judicial orders directing the federal government to resume funding after Trump sought to pause it via executive orders, the fate of the money awarded to Georgia remained unclear as of late Wednesday.
The funds were divided between the state, utilities, and nonprofits that could not or would not say if the funding had been restored.
The U.S. Department of Energy, the source of many of the federal funds, did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Kym Meyer, litigation director at the Southern Environmental Law Center, denounced the administration’s actions, citing a temporary restraining order that requires funds to be unfrozen and noting the refusal raises extremely serious constitutional issues.
Shayla Powell, spokesperson for the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, stated in an email that neither EPA nor the Department of Justice can provide further information on pending litigation.

Shane Hix, spokesperson for the Georgia Environmental Finance Authority (lead recipient for a $250 million grid resilience grant), said the agency was still waiting on further guidance from DOE and EPA about the availability of federal funds from the bipartisan infrastructure law and the Inflation Reduction Act.

The finance authority was poised to launch a federally funded home energy rebate program in March for income-qualified consumers to get up to $16,000 back on electric appliances, insulation, air sealing, heating and cooling, and other home improvements.

The Georgia Environmental Finance Authority was to receive the $250 million for grid improvements alongside a consortium that included nonprofit electric cooperatives and the Georgia Transmission Corp.

Blair Romero, speaking on behalf of Oglethorpe Power, Georgia Transmission, and Georgia System Operations, stated the award is “obligated and under contract,” with Oglethorpe and its family of companies agreeing to invest an additional $257 million.

Romero noted that the federal government has paused disbursement of funds from the bipartisan infrastructure law, and they are evaluating the potential impact on planned projects while awaiting further guidance from the U.S. Department of Energy.

Georgia Transmission was awarded another $98 million to upgrade transmission infrastructure and test new grid technologies with other nonprofit utilities, which will also match the $98 million in federal funding.
Barbara Hampton, president and CEO of Georgia Transmission, previously said the investments would help improve electric service, particularly in underserved and rural areas.

Georgia Power, the state’s largest utility with more than 2.7 million customers, was slated to receive $160 million that it planned to match for upgrading transmission lines with new conductors and modern technologies like dynamic line rating to improve efficiency.
Since the start of 2023, Georgia Power ratepayers have faced six hikes needed to pay for grid improvements, fuel costs, and the expansion of Plant Vogtle.
Jacob Hawkins, spokesperson for Georgia Power, said the company continues to evaluate developments to understand any potential implications to proposed projects undertaken on behalf of customers.
A nonprofit awarded $156 million to expand rooftop solar and battery storage for low-income Georgians suggested the funding pause remains in effect.

Alicia Brown, director of the Georgia BRIGHT program administered by the nonprofit Capital Good Fund, expressed disappointment that the pause came close to the anticipated launch but remained hopeful that a path forward can be found to deliver promised benefits without significant delay.
source_title: $1 billion Georgia electric grid updates on hold amid Trump funding freeze

facts: Georgia Power Co. now says the first of two nuclear reactors it’s building at Plant Vogtle near Augusta might not begin generating electricity until as late as March 2023.
The reactors will cost their owners nearly $30 billion.
Southern Co., the Atlanta-based parent of the Georgia utility, made the announcements as it released its annual earnings Thursday.
Southern Co. took a further $920 million loss on the reactors.
Southern Co. warned it could have to write off another $460 million depending on how a dispute with Vogtle co-owners turns out.
With the charge-offs, Southern Co. said it lost $215 million in the fourth quarter, or 20 cents per share.
Southern Co. earned a profit of $2.39 billion, or $2.26 per share, for the year.
source_title: $30B Georgia Power nuclear plant delayed up to 6 more months – WABE

facts: Governor Nathan Deal announced that voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc. will expand its existing operation in Bartow County, creating 150 jobs and investing $50 million in the local community.
voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc. is the supply base for the voestalpine Group, which produces ultra-high strength body-in-white components for premium car manufacturers.

Governor Nathan Deal stated that voestalpine’s decision to expand operations in Bartow County strengthens Georgia’s growing automotive industry and highlights the state’s extensive manufacturing capabilities.
Governor Nathan Deal noted that when he met with voestalpine executives in Germany, they discussed the benefits that Georgia can continue to offer the growing company.

Governor Nathan Deal said the business environment in Bartow County and throughout the state will help voestalpine maintain its position as a leader in automotive supply, while bolstering Georgia’s reputation as a top state for business.
The newly created jobs will range from advanced manufacturing to hot-forming and assembly positions.
The company currently employs approximately 100 Georgians at the facility.
The company offers extensive training opportunities, including an apprenticeship program with the students of the Bartow County College & Career Academy in Cartersville.

Philipp Schulz, managing director of voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc., stated that this investment is an important step in the growth strategy of their company in the U.S. and positions them well in the center of the southern automotive hub.
Philipp Schulz thanked state and local officials as well as the Georgia Department of Economic Development for their assistance in this project.
Philipp Schulz expressed excitement about expanding operations in Cartersville, where the company has found great partners for its operations.
voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc. is a subsidiary company of the Metal Forming Division of the German-based voestalpine Group.
The Metal Forming Division produces highly refined sections, tubes and precision strip steel products, as well as ready-to-install components made of pressed, stamped and roll formed parts.
Bartow County Commissioner Steve Taylor stated they are proud and thankful for voestalpine’s investment in Bartow County.

Bartow County Commissioner Steve Taylor noted that voestalpine was the first company to build in Highland 75 and their multiple additional phases, continued investment and creation of jobs shows that Bartow is a great location to do business.

Bartow County Commissioner Steve Taylor said the success of voestalpine and Highland 75 is a testament to the collaboration between Bartow County, the City of Cartersville and all their state and corporate partners.

Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) Senior Project Manager Wylly Harrison and Regional Project Manager Stephanie Scearce represented the Global Commerce Division in partnership with the Cartersville-Bartow County Department of Economic Development and Georgia Power.

GDEcD Commissioner Chris Carr stated that when global manufacturing companies like voestalpine choose to locate or expand their existing presence in Georgia, it is a testament to the international reputation of their top-ranked business climate.

GDEcD Commissioner Chris Carr expressed confidence that as the state’s automotive industry grows and manufacturing companies continue to invest, voestalpine will find the support it needs to successfully serve its customers around the world and remain competitive in the marketplace.
The voestalpine Group operates globally with around 500 Group companies and locations in more than 50 countries on all five continents.
The voestalpine Group has been listed on the Vienna Stock Exchange since 1995.
The voestalpine Group is one of the leading partners to the automotive and consumer goods industries in Europe and to the oil and gas industries worldwide using steel and other metals.
The voestalpine Group is the world market leader in turnout technology, special rails, tool steel, and special sections.
source_title: Leading automotive supplier to create 150 jobs in Bartow County - Expansion Solutions

facts: The voestalpine Group is a steel-based technology and capital goods group operating globally as one of the leading partners to the automotive and consumer goods industries.
The voestalpine Group contracted with Evans on the design-build construction of a new automotive metal forming plant in White, Georgia.
The facility's building is 48,000 square feet.
The building is composed of a conventional steel structure with concrete tilt-up wall panels and a mechanically fastened TPO roof system.
The facility is fully air-conditioned and holds manufacturing and office space.
The facility includes a QA/QC room containing a completely separate HVAC system with advanced humidity controls.
The production area features a 20-foot deep press pit designed to support an equipment load up to 570 metric tons.
The production area includes one 25-ton bridge crane.
The structure is supported by steel H-piles to handle the loads imposed by heavy equipment and coil storage.
Portions of the press pit and tunnel, and the coil storage areas are supported by 110 piles ranging in depth from 30 feet to 80 feet below Finished Floor Elevation (FFE).
The scope of work included complete design-build services, shell building construction with all finishes, and a comprehensive site work package.

facts: Lt. Governor Casey Cagle’s Ninth Annual Business and Education Summit was a two-day event that began on Monday in Dalton, Ga and concluded on Tuesday in Calhoun, Ga.
voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc. was named the 2016 Business Partner of the Year at the summit.

Lt. Governor Cagle stated that voestalpine Automotive is a true leader in strategic public-private partnerships necessary to unleash students’ full potential and provides world-class apprenticeship opportunities for the next generation.

Lt. Governor Cagle noted that voestalpine’s success is a clear indicator of what public school systems and private sector job creators can achieve when working together to create a dynamic and skilled workforce.
Sen. Bruce Thompson stated that the organization is at the forefront of workforce development in Georgia and expressed gratitude for opportunities provided to Bartow County students.
Sen. Bruce Thompson represents Senate District 14, which includes Bartow and Cherokee counties as well as portions of Cobb.

The summit brought together business, industry, and education leaders to discuss abandoning the one-size-fits-all model in public education and building a workforce to maintain Georgia’s status as the No. 1 state to conduct business.
School systems are implementing relevant career pathways with public-private partnerships to fill current gaps in Georgia’s workforce.
voestalpine sought out a partner to develop a skilled workforce prior to locating in Bartow County.

voestalpine is the first company in Georgia to organize an approved registered apprenticeship program exclusively for high school students, despite many U.S. Department of Labor Apprenticeship Programs being available for adults statewide.
This apprenticeship program serves as a superior model that can be replicated with other industries in the Bartow community and throughout the state.

The College and Career Academies initiative partners local school systems with technical colleges, other post-secondary institutions, and local businesses to develop high school and college-level programs training students for quality, in-demand jobs.
There are currently over 37 College and Career Academies in Georgia due to Lt. Governor Cagle’s initiative.
Approximately 20,000 students from different College and Career Academies across the state enjoy graduation rates in the high ninetieth percentile and 100 percent post-secondary job placement rates.
source_title: Lt. Governor Cagle Names Business Partner of the Year at Ninth Annual Business and Education Summit | WBHF

facts: voestalpine in Cartersville, Georgia, USA is a sub-supplier of parts and components to the automotive industry and has many leading OEMs as customers.
The facility produces structural parts for some of the world's chief OEMs whose production relies on having a constant flow of parts and components.
Production equipment includes two press-hardening lines from AP&T used to produce structural parts.
The two press-hardening lines have been in operation since 2017.
About a year ago, production was running around the clock without pause while manufacturing enormous volumes.
To secure production capacity and minimize unplanned downtime risk, voestalpine signed an extensive service agreement with AP&T in the spring of 2022.
The service agreement covers two press-hardening lines critical to the facility's production capacity.
The agreement includes regular preventative maintenance, 24/7 online support, and on-site assistance with priority response time at a fixed monthly cost.
voestalpine now has access to quick, qualified support in the evenings and on weekends, which they have benefited from on several occasions.
Most operational problems can be resolved remotely, but AP&T technicians can visit on-site at short notice if needed.
voestalpine is currently in discussions with AP&T about designing an agreement to guarantee uptime.

Potential future agreement terms could include handing over stocking spare machine parts to AP&T and having them guarantee parts delivery within a certain timeframe to free up warehouse space and reduce tied-up capital.
Fredrik Stålberg serves as Managing Director and CTO of voestalpine in Cartersville.
Magnus Svenningsson is the Director of Aftermarket Sales at AP&T.
Tobias Kronstedt is the Aftermarket Service Manager for AP&T in the US.
Andrea Clark is the President of AP&T North America.

links.context: AP&T's service agreements and other services

source_title: Voestalpine in Cartersville, Georgia, USA secures production with AP&T service commitment

facts: With the launch of the Volvo PV444, we introduced a new philosophy – one centered on people, practicality, and safety.
Our manufacturing plant in South Carolina does a lot more than just building cars.
It is also a significant economic player in Berkeley County.
It employs people, it draws in other busi...
source_title: Volvo News and latest updates | Volvo Cars

facts: Volvo Cars announced plans to ramp up production at its Ridgeville plant as part of continued investment in the facility.
Luis Rezende, president of Americas for Volvo Cars, stated the company is starting an ambitious journey to transform its U.S. business and expand growth in the country.
Charleston serves as the foundation to build Volvo's future, according to Rezende.
With $1.3 billion already invested in the plant over the last decade, Volvo plans to expand its output in the U.S. in 2026 by adding another car to the production line.
CEO Håkan Samuelsson said if the company is going to be serious and present on the American market, it also needs to be present industrially.
Volvo is ready to take the next step to make sure that the Charleston factory becomes the crucial part of its growth strategy.
Volvo first broke ground at the Ridgeville location in 2015.
The Ridgeville plant has a production capacity of 150,000 cars a year.
Plant officials stated that the facility is not utilizing full capacity.
In May, Volvo Cars made a "small reduction" to its workforce at the plant amid trade uncertainty.
The workforce change was part of a shift to "regionalization" in response to what a spokesperson described as "challenging macro conditions."
Officials claim the high-capacity plant, equipped with advanced technology and capable of producing multiple platforms and models, is the focus of renewed domestic investment.
CEO Håkan Samuelsson stated that employees are proudly assembling the company's flagship, the EX 90, which is built here for exports to around the world, mainly to Europe.
The plant currently only produces two cars: the fully electric EX90 SUV and the Polestar 3.
President of Americas Luis Rezende said the company aims to build where it sells, meaning big plans for Charleston.
Volvo aims to ensure that what is produced in Charleston over the next five years will reflect what customers buy.
Starting in late 2026, the plant will include production of Volvo's bestselling XC 60.
Officials said adding the XC 60 is a smart move since the company has sold over 27,000 units since the beginning of the year, a 20% increase from 2024.

South Carolina Governor Henry McMaster stated that Volvo's investment sends a message to international investors looking for places to invest hundreds of millions and billions of dollars that this is the place.
Governor McMaster, a Republican, presented awards to Volvo executives and thanked them for investing in South Carolina.
Governor McMaster noted that more and more people will be educated and trained to do jobs that become more sophisticated every day.
The governor emphasized that when people have working jobs, it supports happy families and generates tax money needed for roads and schools.
U.S. Representative Nancy Mace joined the event and said she is proud that Volvo is continuing to invest in the Lowcountry and will provide more job opportunities.
Rep. Mace noted there were two employees at the event who have been working with Volvo for 28 years.
She also met many folks this morning who have been working the line there for years.
The event marked Volvo's 10-year anniversary at the Ridgeville plant.
Rep. Mace highlighted the importance of education, not just college education in South Carolina, but also vocational training.
The Swedish company has plants around the world, with the Lowcountry location being key to its success in the U.S. today.
Chief Industrial Operations Officer Francesca Gambina stated that Charleston produces less than 5% of U.S. volumes with the rest coming from Europe.
Volvo aims to produce more than 50% of the U.S. volume right at the Charleston facility in five years.
The company plans to add a fully hybrid model to the South Carolina production line before 2030.

links.context: Volvo Cars plans for 'small reduction' at Ridgeville plant, spokesperson says
Volvo temporarily suspends production at SC plant citing parts shortages
Volvo Cars cutting 3,000 jobs to reduce costs

source_title: Volvo's Ridgeville plant gears up for expansion with new models and increased production

facts: Kingston, Ontario purchased two Mack LR Electric refuse models as its first battery-electric vehicles (BEVs) for its Solid Waste fleet.
The City of Kingston aligned these purchases with its vision of being “Canada’s Most Sustainable City” and furthering its commitment to climate action.
Volvo Trucks increased the use of recycled and renewable materials in its newest heavy-duty truck models to reduce manufacturing environmental impact.
Mack Defense received $47 million in programmatic funding to continue building the M917A3 Heavy Dump Truck at its Allentown, Pennsylvania facility.
U.S. Rep. Ryan Mackenzie announced the Mack Defense funding during an event at the facility.
The Mack Defense funding is part of a new five-year contract awarded in June 2025 for up to 450 trucks with a total contract value of up to $221.8 million.
Mack Trucks highlighted the driver-centric cab and interior design of the all-new Mack Granite, which was engineered from the ground up around the people behind the wheel.
Volvo Trucks is introducing over-the-air software updates for fleets that can run overnight, during driver breaks, or while parked without requiring drivers to stay with the vehicle.
The over-the-air software update feature will launch later in 2026.
The New York City Department of Sanitation and its nonprofit partner the Sanitation Foundation unveiled five new hand-painted Mack LR refuse vehicles during New York City’s Trucks of Art program.
Clinton Aluminum, the largest distributor of aluminum mold plate in North America, is using Mack MD7 medium-duty trucks to haul heavy materials due to their strong performance and dependability.
Clinton Aluminum plans to convert its fleet of 34 vehicles to an all-Mack fleet.
Volvo Autonomous Solutions (V.A.S) and DSV marked the start of autonomous freight operations in Texas, hauling the first commercial truckload using the Volvo VNL Autonomous.
The autonomous freight partnership between V.A.S and DSV has ambitions to expand to additional lanes over time.
Mack Trucks announced a comprehensive redesign of its PartsASIST online parts-ordering platform featuring a modernized user interface, faster performance, and new tools for customers and dealers.
The Mack MP13 engine is the most powerful and efficient engine Mack has ever produced and will be EPA 2027-compliant.
Mack showcased the MP13 engine during the Advanced Clean Transportation (ACT) Expo 2026 from May 4-7 at the Las Vegas Convention Center in Las Vegas, Nevada.
Mack Trucks added ABM and Lane Valente Industries as new full-service providers to its Turnkey Solutions program for battery-electric vehicle customers.
Volvo Trucks North America is expanding electric truck capabilities in North America by adding new capabilities to the Volvo VNR Electric.
A next-generation Volvo engine combines expanded power, strong fuel efficiency, and the lowest NOx levels Volvo has ever offered.
Mack Trucks announced the availability of the Mack ImpactShield, which features the first-ever use of Corning Fusion5 Glass in a Class 8 heavy-duty truck.
The Mack ImpactShield is standard on the all-new Mack Granite and delivers advancements in windshield durability, damage resistance, and total cost of ownership.
Gulf Highway Equipment announced plans to purchase 160 additional Mack MD Series cab and chassis units for its construction company and government agency clients.
Coke Canada Bottling more than doubled its fleet of Volvo VNR Electric trucks across the country.
source_title: Volvo Group North America Press releases | Volvo Group

facts: The Ridgeville plant had previously only housed production of the Volvo S60.
Production of the Volvo EX90 was originally scheduled to commence in the fourth quarter of 2023 but was delayed due to software issues.
During Volvo Cars’ Q4 2023 earnings conference call, CEO Jim Rowan stated that the EX90 is the company’s most technically advanced car ever.
Volvo prioritized ensuring its software “was as good as it possibly could be” before shipping the vehicle to customers.
Production of the Volvo EX90 has now commenced at the South Carolina facility.
First deliveries of the Volvo EX90 are expected in North America and Europe to commence in the second half of the year.
Volvo Cars’ US plant has an annual production capacity of 150,000 vehicles.
The company has made significant investments in its South Carolina factory in recent years.
Recent investments included significantly expanding the facility and refurbishing the body shop and paint shop.
The North American strategic production site now houses a battery pack production line as well.
Production of the Volvo EX90 will also take place in China, with deliveries scheduled to commence in November.
The Volvo EX90 will be available in three variants: Single Motor (RWD), Twin Motor (AWD), and Twin Motor Performance (AWD).

The Single Motor (RWD) variant features 205 kW power, 490 Nm of torque, accelerates from 0 to 100 kph in 8.4 seconds, has a top speed of 180 kph, uses a 104 kWh battery pack, and offers a WLTP range of approximately 580 km.

The Twin Motor (AWD) variant features 300 kW power, 700 Nm of torque, accelerates from 0 to 100 kph in 5.9 seconds, has a top speed of 180 kph, uses a 111 kWh battery pack, and offers a WLTP range of approximately 600 km.
Volvo Cars confirmed that the Twin Motor (AWD) variant's battery pack is supplied by CATL.

The Twin Motor Performance (AWD) variant features 380 kW power, 910 Nm of torque, accelerates from 0 to 100 kph in 4.9 seconds, has a top speed of 180 kph, shares the 111 kWh battery pack with the Twin Motor (AWD) variant (with 107 kWh usable), and offers a WLTP range of approximately 590 km due to higher power and torque.
Pricing for the Volvo EX90 starts at $76,695 in the US and €83,700 in Germany.
source_title: Volvo EX90 production starts in South Carolina - electrive.com

facts: Volvo Group is pursuing zero emission heavy duty construction machines and trucks through hydrogen fuel cell technology.
Its subsidiary Volvo Construction Equipment (Volvo CE) is establishing a new dedicated fuel cell test lab to accelerate R&D.

Volvo CTO Thomas Bitter stated that hydrogen fuel cells are a key enabler for sustainable solutions in heavier construction machines, supporting the company's Science-Based Targets and ambition to be 100% fossil free by 2040.

Battery electric powertrains are viewed as ideal for urban construction, shorter distances, and lower loads, while hydrogen fuel cells will complement them for larger machines requiring long haul, long distances, and short refueling stops.
Volvo emphasizes a life-cycle approach to hydrogen production, prioritizing green hydrogen from renewable energy over natural gas sourced hydrogen to reduce carbon footprint.
The new fuel cell test lab is located at the Volvo Construction Equipment Technical Center in Eskilstuna, Sweden.
This facility marks Volvo Group's first dedicated R&D center for fuel cell technology and its first site to test complete fuel cell units.
Volvo CE positions hydrogen fuel cells as one component of a three-pronged strategy alongside EV batteries and sustainable ICE fuels to achieve carbon neutrality by 2040.

Cellcentric, a joint venture between Daimler Truck AG and the Volvo Group, will accelerate the development, production, and commercialization of fuel cell systems for long-haul trucking and construction equipment.

Cellcentric aims to become a leading global manufacturer of fuel cells for mobile and stationary applications, including clean powertrains for commercial vehicles and emergency power for data centers, targeting climate-neutral transportation by 2050.

Volvo Trucks USA delivered two battery-electric VNR Electric trucks equipped with electric refrigerator units from Advanced Energy Machines to grocery chain Albertsons Companies at its distribution center in Irvine, California.
The delivery occurred on May 28, 2021, at a LEED-certified Albertsons store in Irvine, California, marking a step toward the company's sustainability goals.
Albertsons operates 1,400 Class 8 trucks nationwide, all certified under the EPA SmartWay program for high transportation sustainability and efficiency standards.
The Southern California Volvo Trucks fleet covers 335 stores from the Central Coast to the California-Mexico border.
Tim Burke, Albertsons vice president of transportation, noted the electric trucks will improve local air quality and provide a quieter working environment for drivers and staff.
source_title: Volvo Vows To Make Hydrogen Fuel Cell Construction Machines

facts: Greensboro, NC is cited as the dateline location for a June 29, 2026 press release regarding Kingston, Ontario’s purchase of Mack LR Electric refuse models.

The City of Kingston, Ontario, Canada, purchased two Mack® LR Electric refuse models to align with its vision of being “Canada’s Most Sustainable City” and furthering its commitment to leading climate action.
The Mack LR Electric trucks are Kingston’s first battery-electric vehicles (BEVs) in its Solid Waste fleet.

Volvo Trucks has increased the use of recycled and renewable materials in its newest heavy-duty truck models, reflecting the company’s effort to reduce the environmental impact from truck manufacturing.
U.S. Rep. Ryan Mackenzie announced that Mack Defense received $47 million in programmatic funding to continue building the M917A3 Heavy Dump Truck (HDT) at its Allentown, Pennsylvania, facility.
The funding is part of a new five-year contract awarded in June 2025 to Mack Defense for up to 450 trucks, with a total contract value of up to $221.8 million.

Volvo Group collaborates with startups that have advanced and innovative technologies, with Volvo Trucks tapping into a startup’s technology and agile pace to address an urgent customer need and deliver a valuable solution.

An invention from Volvo Group researchers has been introduced in the truck production environment, featuring new automated transporters that are a unique AI-powered solution setting a new standard for human and robot collaboration.
