facts: Volvo Cars' largest owner is China's Geely.
Volvo Cars could face a sales ban in the US under new legislation set to take effect next year that would prohibit software and hardware from countries such as China in cars on safety grounds.
The process for granting special permits is conducted on a case-by-case basis.
Granting of a special permit follows constructive discussions with the US Department of Commerce and other US representatives regarding Volvo Cars' corporate governance, technology and data security.
The US is one of Volvo Cars' largest markets.
Volvo Cars has a state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Charleston, South Carolina.
Volvo Cars has invested over $1.3 billion to date at its Charleston, SC facility.
source_title: Volvo Cars avoids US sales ban with special permit for connected cars | Sweden Herald

facts: In full-year 2023, Volvo increased car sales in the USA and Canada with the XC90 and XC60 the top-selling models.
From January to December 2023, Volvo Car USA and Canada sold 141,566 cars for the full year 2023 – an increase of 26 percent compared to sales in 2022.

Volvo’s recharge models — cars with a fully electric or plug-in hybrid powertrain — accounted for 28.3 percent of all Volvo cars sold in North America in 2023, an increase of 30.6 percent compared to 2022.
Volvo sales in Canada were at the highest level in fifty years.
For the full year 2023, Volvo sales in the US reached 128,701 cars, up 26.1 percent compared with the previous year.
The 2023 US sales period included a record year for sales through the retail channel in the US.
In the US, the XC90 midsize luxury SUV was the top-selling model, followed by the XC60 compact luxury SUV, and XC40 small luxury SUV.
Worldwide, the XC60 is Volvo’s most popular model.
In 2023, Volvo Canada set a new annual sales record with sales of 12,865 cars during the year.
The previous Canadian annual sales record was set in 1974 when 11,763 Volvo cars were sold in Canada.
Headquartered in Mahwah, New Jersey, Volvo Car USA LLC (VCUSA) is a subsidiary of Volvo Car Group of Gothenburg, Sweden (Nasdaq Stockholm VOLCAR B).
VCUSA provides sales, marketing, parts, service, technology, and training support to Volvo automobile retailers in the United States since the 1950s.
Operations also include a manufacturing campus in Ridgeville, SC that underlines the company’s long-term commitment to the U.S. as a key market.
VCUSA maintains offices in California and parts distribution and training centers across the country.
Volvo Car Canada Ltd. is a subsidiary of Volvo Car Group of Gothenburg, Sweden.
VCCL provides marketing, sales, parts, service, technology, and training support to the 37 Volvo automobile retailers across Canada.
Volvo Cars was founded in 1927.
Volvo Cars has sales to customers in more than 100 countries.
Volvo Cars is listed on the Nasdaq Stockholm exchange, traded under the ticker “VOLCAR B”.
Volvo Cars aims to become a fully electric car maker by 2030.
Volvo Cars has a commitment to an ongoing reduction of its carbon footprint, with the ambition to be a climate-neutral company by 2040.
As of December 2022, Volvo Cars employed approximately 43,200 full-time employees.
Volvo Cars’ head office, product development, marketing and administration functions are mainly located in Gothenburg, Sweden.
source_title: 2023 (Full Year) USA: Volvo US and Canada Car Sales by Model - Car Sales Statistics

facts: Gerald Jones Volvo Cars is a dealership located in Martinez, GA.
The dealership serves drivers throughout Waynesboro, Statesboro, Metter, Grovetown, Pooler, Richmond Hill, Rincon, Augusta, and Evans, GA.
It also serves nearby Clearwater, Aiken, and North Augusta, South Carolina.
The showroom features the All New 2025 & 2026 Volvo Lineup, including the Volvo XC60, Volvo XC90 Plug-in Hybrid, and Volvo XC60 Plug-in Hybrid.
The dealership offers pure electric cars that provide emissions-free driving without compromising range and power.
It offers plug-in hybrids that pair an electric motor and a combustion engine for worry-free driving.
It offers mild hybrids equipped with a combustion engine and an electric motor to reduce fuel consumption with no plug-in required.
The dealership provides Volvo lease options and financing plans tailored to customer budgets.
A dedicated Finance Department offers an online finance application for quick pre-approval from home.
The Service Department employs highly trained technicians who use advanced diagnostic tools and genuine Volvo parts to maintain vehicle performance.
Customers can use a user-friendly online tool to schedule service appointments at their convenience.
source_title: Volvo Dealership in Martinez, GA | Gerald Jones Volvo Cars

facts: Volvo is prioritizing the development of new crossovers to replace existing models while adding new members to its portfolio.
CEO Jim Rowan mentioned during an interview in November 2022 that electric sedans and wagons could hit the market after the SUV boom.
Chief operating officer Bjorn Annwall hinted in a new interview with Australian magazine Car Sales that a sports car is not completely out of the question.
Bjorn Annwall stated: "Every car brand needs a bit of spice that helps kind of push the boundaries of what the car really is, and I think Volvo should have those complimentary car products as well."
When asked whether the company intends to launch a new sports car, Annwall said: "Stay tuned."
Exterior design manager Orjan Sterner admitted it's fun to work on a "coupe or a super sports car" but cautioned profits must prevail at the end of the day.
The company is engineering volume-oriented vehicles that have a solid business case, so SUVs will remain the main focus for a while.
Volvo was among the first luxury automakers to announce the gradual demise of combustion engines.
Volvo plans to morph into a purely electric brand in 2030, five years after Jaguar.
With SUVs remaining the number one priority, it's likely too late in the game for an ICE-powered sports car.
Past performance vehicles include the P1800 Cyan and the Polestar 1 plug-in hybrid.
If Volvo green lights another performance machine, logic suggests it will likely be completely electric.
Car Sales speculates a sports car is unlikely to arrive until near the end of the decade or in the early 2030s.
source_title: Volvo Says We Should Stay Tuned For A Sports Car

facts: Sweden's Volvo Cars, which is controlled by Chinese auto maker Geely, will need up to two years to expand its U.S. car production in order to avoid hefty import tariffs.
CEO Hakan Samuelsson told daily Dagens Nyheter (DN) on Friday that the expansion timeline is necessary.
Volvo Cars is one of the most exposed automakers to U.S. President Donald Trump's auto tariffs as it imports most of its hybrid and electric models from Europe.
A Volvo spokesperson declined to comment when contacted by Reuters.
Samuelsson told DN that it would not be sustainable for the company in the long term to sell European-made cars in the United States at a 27.5% tariff.
Importing vehicles from the company's Chinese plants is "impossible" given the much higher U.S. tariffs on China.
In the short term, within one to two years, it will be about selling the cars the company already has.
The tariff situation will put pressure on profit margins and customers will also have to pay more.
Samuelsson last week said Volvo Cars was working towards increasing production in the U.S. as a response to tariffs.
source_title: Volvo Cars may take up to two years to expand US production to avoid tariffs, CEO tells daily DN

facts: Volvo Car USA and Canada reported total sales of 138,647 cars in 2024, down 2.1 percent from the previous year.
Electrified models, defined as those with a fully electric or plug-in hybrid powertrain, accounted for 35.4 percent of Volvo's total sales in 2024, up 22.3 percent from last year.
In December, Volvo Car USA and Canada sold 15,131 cars, up 2.1 percent from the previous year.
Electrified models represented 35.7 percent of Volvo's December sales, an increase of 44.5 percent compared to the previous year.
US car sales for Volvo reached 125,243 for the entire year of 2024, down 2.7 percent from the previous year.
Electrified models accounted for 34.3 percent of Volvo's total US sales in 2024, up 20.3 percent from last year.
December sales in the US reached 14,077 cars, up 3.4 percent over the same period last year.
Sales of electrified models represented 33.9 percent of Volvo's US December sales, an increase of 47.6 percent compared to the previous year.
In 2024, Canada set a new annual sales record for Volvo with 13,404 cars sold, up 4.2 percent compared with the previous record-setting year.
Sales of electrified models accounted for 46.1 percent of Volvo's Canadian sales during 2024, up 38.2 percent compared to last year.
December sales in Canada were down 12.7 percent from the previous year, with a volume of 1,054 cars.
Sales of electrified models accounted for 59.6 percent of Volvo's Canadian December sales, an increase of 24.1 percent compared to the previous year.
In the US, the XC60 compact luxury SUV was Volvo's top-selling model, with a record-setting 5,245 cars sold in December.
The XC90 midsize luxury SUV followed as the second best-selling model in the US in December, with 5,006 vehicles sold.
The XC40 small luxury SUV was the third best-selling model in the US in December, with 2,591 cars sold.
Mike Cottone, President of Volvo Car USA and Canada, stated that demand from customers for their cars continued through the end of the year, especially for electrified models.
Mike Cottone thanked retail partners, loyal customers, and valued employees for their commitment to Volvo Cars as the company continues to build momentum in the new year.
source_title: Volvo Car USA and Canada Reports Sales of 138,647 in 2024

facts: Volvo Group North America aims to achieve 100% fossil-free vehicles by 2040.
Volvo Group North America aims to achieve 35% electric vehicles by 2030.
Initiatives for fuel cell vehicles are set for the second half of this decade.
The company faces challenges in the heavy-duty zero-emission vehicle (ZEV) market, primarily related to charging infrastructure.
The company emphasizes early engagement with utilities and stakeholders.
The VNR Electric was launched commercially in December 2020.
The VNR Electric exemplifies their commitment to electrifying trucking with significant fleet orders and diverse duty cycle applications.
source_title: Volvo Group Electric Trucks In North America by Dawn Fenton | PDF

facts: Volvo Group North America achieved its goal of reduced energy consumption in the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) Better Buildings, Better Plants Challenge five years earlier than anticipated.
Volvo Group’s goal had been a 25 percent reduction in energy consumption at its eight U.S. manufacturing plants between 2009 and 2020.
By the end of 2014, Volvo Group had reduced energy consumption by 26.8 percent compared with its 2009 baseline.
Volvo Group was one of only 11 companies to meet its goal early.
Rick Robinson serves as the director of health, safety and environment for the Volvo Group North America.
Rick Robinson stated that environmental care is one of the Volvo Group’s core values and that reaching the milestone required the diligence and dedication of all employees.

The eight U.S. manufacturing facilities include: Volvo Trucks in Dublin, Virginia; Volvo Group Powertrain in Hagerstown, Maryland; Mack Trucks in Macungie, Pennsylvania; Volvo Construction Equipment in Shippensburg, Pennsylvania; Volvo Penta in Lexington, Tennessee; Volvo Bus in Plattsburgh, New York; Volvo Group Remanufacturing in Charlotte, North Carolina; and Volvo Group Remanufacturing in Middletown, Pennsylvania.

Energy Secretary Ernest Moniz commented that as the Better Buildings Initiative enters its fourth year, leaders continue to showcase how saving energy saves money, creates jobs, accelerates the nation’s competitiveness in the clean energy economy, and preserves the environment.

Three of Volvo Group’s manufacturing sites – Macungie, New River Valley, and Hagerstown – held the top three positions in the DOE’s Superior Energy Performance program among Platinum level partners at the time.
Since the DOE launched the Better Buildings Challenge in 2011, more than 250 partners have saved $840 million and saved 94 TBTUs of energy, representing six million tons of greenhouse gas emissions.
The Volvo Group is one of the world’s leading manufacturers of trucks, buses, construction equipment, and marine and industrial engines.
The company employs about 100,000 people globally.
The Volvo Group has production facilities in 19 countries and sells its products in more than 190 markets.
In 2014, the Volvo Group’s sales amounted to about $38.2 billion.
The Volvo Group is a publicly-held company headquartered in Gothenburg, Sweden.
Volvo shares are listed on Nasdaq Stockholm and are traded OTC in the U.S.
source_title: Volvo Group Achieves Energy Efficiency - FINCHANNEL

facts: Focus has shifted from purely growth to building resilience alongside growth, leveraging a broad portfolio and global presence to navigate cyclical markets, geopolitical uncertainty, and energy transition challenges.
The transition to zero-emission solutions is guided by a four-pillar approach: decarbonization, resilience, competitiveness, and national security.
Service business has grown at a 5% CAGR, now contributing high profitability and stability, with recurring revenues from service contracts and a growing installed base.
Operating margins have consistently exceeded 10% in recent years, with a focus on maintaining double-digit margins.
Net sales in 2025 reached SEK 479 billion (EUR 43 billion), reflecting robust performance.
The company is targeting growth in on-road freight, construction, urban logistics, mining, agriculture, and defense, with tailored strategies for each segment.

The on-road segment leads with high margins and market share gains in Europe and Brazil, with a strong push to replicate this in North America through new products, expanded capacity, and dealer investments.
Volvo Group targets a 25% North American truck share by 2030, driving growth with innovation and resilience.
Sales and margins fell amid FX and ZEV costs, but service and innovation remained resilient.
source_title: Volvo Group (VOLV) Investor Relations, Earnings Summary & Outlook

facts: Volvo Group North America reached a settlement with the California Air Resources Board (CARB).
The settlement resolves allegations by CARB regarding the adequacy of the description of certain emission controls on model year 2010-2016 engines installed in Volvo Group trucks sold in California.
Volvo Group's internal review found no evidence that anyone acted in bad faith, and the settlement is explicitly without admission of liability.
Volvo Group agreed to pay USD 12.5 million in civil penalties.
Volvo Group agreed to pay USD 71.0 million to CARB's Air Pollution Control Fund.
Volvo Group will spend USD 108 million on California emission-reduction projects under a plan to be submitted for CARB approval within a year.
Volvo Group will reimburse USD 5 million of CARB costs.
Volvo Group's operating income in the second quarter of 2026 will include a negative impact of USD 196.5 M (appr. SEK 1.8 Bn), which will be excluded from adjusted operating income.
Volvo Group's operating cash flow in the second quarter of 2026 will be negatively impacted by USD 89 M (appr. SEK 0.8 Bn).
The remaining cash flow effect is expected to occur over the coming five years.
Volvo Group proactively disclosed the issues that were the subject of CARB's concerns nearly a decade ago and has worked cooperatively with CARB to resolve the matter.
Volvo Group will make available software updates and a partial warranty extension for about 7,200 model year 2014-2016 engines in California as part of the settlement.
The Volvo Group is not aware of any additional investigations related to its engines' emissions compliance in the US.
The Volvo Group has always intended to comply with relevant regulations and conducts rigorous testing to ensure its engines are compliant with all emissions requirements.
There are no performance or safety issues associated with the engines in question.
source_title: Volvo Group North America has made a settlement with CARB

facts: Volvo Group plans to modestly scale back U.S. truck production after orders in North America declined by 34% year over year to about 11,100 units.
The OEM pointed to the lingering freight recession resulting in low levels of demand as the cause for reduced orders.
The company will implement “stop weeks” in Q1 rather than make more permanent structural changes.
CEO and President Martin Lundstedt announced the Q1 stop weeks during a Jan. 28 earnings call.
The move follows an undisclosed number of stop days in Q4, which resulted in under-absorption and weighed on the manufacturer’s overall performance for the period.
Truck deliveries fell 16% to 11,490 trucks during that same period.
CEO Martin Lundstedt stated the company will muddle through, keep focus, and wait for the North American market to recover to align capacity and inventory levels.
Volvo executives said recent ordering activity points to a gradual recovery in North America later in the year.
Carriers could prebuy vehicles due to uncertainty surrounding the Environmental Protection Agency’s 2027 emissions standards.
The company raised its 2026 guidance for truck registrations in North America to 265,000 units, an increase of 15,000 from the forecast issued in Q3 2025.
If realized, that figure would represent a slight improvement over the 257,968 registrations recorded in 2025 but would still fall well short of the 304,274 registrations seen in 2024.
Lundstedt noted that Volvo has observed stabilization in several markets but cautioned that geopolitical developments could still weigh on demand.
Lundstedt stated the company is starting to see somewhat better activity levels and noted there are a number of parameters supporting a potential recovery, though it may be too early to confirm.
source_title: Volvo Q4 truck orders fall 34% YoY in North America | Trucking Dive

facts: Volvo Group North America will lay off 550 to 800 employees.
A company spokesperson confirmed the layoffs to the Daily Voice on Friday, April 18.
The layoffs will happen at plants in Maryland, Pennsylvania, and Virginia.
The Swedish automaker cited the need to adjust to worsening economic conditions.
Heavy-duty truck orders continue to be negatively affected by market uncertainty about freight rates and demand, possible regulatory changes, and the impact of tariffs.
Between 250 and 350 employees will lose their jobs at Mack Trucks Lehigh Valley Operations.
The Macungie, PA, facility employs about 3,050 workers.
Another 250 to 350 jobs are being cut at Volvo Trucks New River Valley Operations.
The plant in Dublin, VA, has a workforce of around 3,400.
A smaller round of 50 to 100 layoffs is hitting Volvo Group Powertrain Operations.
About 1,800 people work at the Hagerstown, MD facility.
Volvo's layoffs come amid rising fears about the state of auto manufacturing under Trump's sweeping tariffs.
Trump has imposed 25% duties on imported cars, light trucks, aluminum, and steel, along with a 145% tariff on Chinese goods.
Vehicle data company Cox Automotive has warned that panic buying has dramatically lowered the US supply of new and used vehicles.
New-vehicle stock fell to 2.69 million units as of Tuesday, April 1, down 10.2% from the start of March.
Volvo said the layoffs were fueled by fewer purchases of its cars and trucks.
The company stated it needs to align production with reduced demand for its vehicles.
Other automakers have cut North American jobs since Trump's "liberation day" tariff announcement.
Stellantis has temporarily laid off about 900 US workers while halting production at some Canadian and Mexican plants.
General Motors said about 200 temporary layoffs have happened at an all-electric vehicle plant in Detroit "to align with market dynamics."
Another GM plant in Ontario will pause manufacturing, with a union leader saying there will be an "indefinite layoff of nearly 500 workers" once production resumes.
Volvo's website said it employs more than 19,600 workers in North America.
source_title: Volvo Laying Off 550-800 Employees At Plants In MD, PA, VA | Daily Voice

facts: Governor Terry McAuliffe announced that the Volvo Group will invest $38.1 million at the New River Valley (NRV) assembly facility in Pulaski County.
All Volvo Trucks models for North America are assembled at the NRV facility in Pulaski County.
The investment will fund several plant upgrades and the construction of a 36,000-square-foot Volvo Trucks Customer Experience Center on the NRV grounds.
Volvo will add 32 new jobs at the NRV facility as part of this investment.
Governor McAuliffe stated that growing the advanced manufacturing industry is a key part of efforts to create jobs, diversify and build a new Virginia economy.
The NRV facility is described as the largest Volvo truck manufacturing facility in the world.
Secretary of Commerce and Trade Maurice Jones said the announcement testifies to the great workforce and best-in-class business environment in Pulaski County and the Commonwealth.
Volvo Trucks provides complete transport solutions for professional and demanding customers, offering a full range of medium to heavy-duty trucks.
Customer support for Volvo trucks is secured via a global network of 2,200 dealers and workshops in more than 120 countries.
Volvo trucks are assembled in 16 countries across the globe.
In 2014, more than 117,000 Volvo trucks were delivered worldwide.
Volvo Trucks is part of the Volvo Group, one of the world’s leading manufacturers of trucks, buses and construction equipment, and drive systems for marine and industrial applications.
The Group also provides solutions for financing and service.
Volvo’s work is based on the core values of quality, safety and environmental care.

Göran Nyberg, president of Volvo Trucks North America, stated that the investment in the NRV facility and Customer Experience Center demonstrates commitment to employees and the Pulaski County community.
The Virginia Economic Development Partnership (VEDP) worked with Pulaski County to secure the project for Virginia.
Governor McAuliffe approved a $100,000 grant from the Commonwealth’s Opportunity Fund to assist Pulaski County with the project.
Governor McAuliffe also approved a $2 million performance-based grant from the Virginia Investment Partnership program, an incentive available to existing Virginia companies.
The company is eligible to receive state benefits from the Virginia Enterprise Zone Program, administered by the Virginia Department of Housing and Community Development.
Additional funding and services to support employee training activities will be provided through the Virginia Jobs Investment Program, administered by VEDP.
Joe Sheffey, Chairman of the Pulaski County Board of Supervisors, expressed pleasure in assisting Volvo with the effort and noted Volvo's 40-year presence in the area.
Delegate Nick Rush called the announcement fantastic news for Pulaski County and the New River Valley, praising Volvo Trucks as one of the best manufacturers in the world.

Senator Ben Chafin stated that the Volvo Group has been a valuable community partner in Pulaski County and the New River Valley Region, noting the new jobs are a positive step for Southwest Virginia's economy.
Press contact for the announcement is Brian Coy.
source_title: Governor McAuliffe Announces $38.1 Million Volvo Group Investment in Pulaski County | Virginia Economic Development Partnership

facts: Volvo Cars is restructuring its U.S. and Canadian operations.
The restructuring is part of an $88 million global effort to tighten spending, drive efficiencies, and update its workforce for an all-electric future.
Executive Cottone stated: 'Not any area of the business that is not impacted.'
The announcement regarding the job cuts and EV transition preparation was published on September 08, 2023.
source_title: Volvo to cut North American jobs to prepare for EV transition - Automotive News

facts: President Joe Biden visited the Volvo Group Powertrain Operations facility in Hagerstown, Maryland, for National Manufacturing Day on October 7.
During the visit, Biden met with Volvo Group executives, toured the facility, and spoke with employees.

Martin Weissburg, chairman of Volvo Group North America and Mack Trucks president, stated that the Hagerstown site exemplifies modern manufacturing by providing advanced transportation technologies to meet customer needs today and in the future.
Over the past 10 years, the Volvo Group has invested nearly $320 million in the Hagerstown site.
The Hagerstown campus produces engines, transmissions, and axles for Mack Trucks, Volvo Trucks, Prevost motorcoaches, and Volvo Buses sold in North America.

The facility recently began production of Modular Power Boxes (MPB) that are at the heart of the Group’s new zero-emission battery-electric vehicles, the Volvo VNR Electric and Mack LR Electric models.
President Biden toured the MPB subassembly, assembly, and testing areas while on campus.

Marcus Minkkinen, vice president and general manager at the Volvo Group Powertrain Operations in Hagerstown, stated that the site offers great jobs in a modernized facility that is transforming the transportation industry and highlighted the importance of manufacturing to the U.S. economy.
source_title: President Biden Visits Volvo Group Powertrain Operations in Hagerstown, Maryland | Markets Herald

facts: Volvo Group North America operates as the Swedish conglomerate’s commercial vehicle and industrial equipment arm across the United States, Canada, and Mexico.
Its portfolio includes trucks, buses, construction equipment, engines, and industrial power systems, as well as iconic U.S. brands such as Mack Trucks.
The group is separate from Volvo Cars, which produces consumer passenger vehicles.

Attackers compromised Conduent’s internal systems between October 21, 2024, and January 13, 2025, accessing highly sensitive personal information including full names, Social Security numbers, dates of birth, health insurance policy data, government issued ID numbers, and medical information.
Earlier filings indicated that the Conduent breach exposed data tied to approximately 10.5 million people in Oregon and another 15.5 million individuals in Texas alone.
Volvo Group North America confirmed that affected individuals are now receiving breach notification letters distributed by Conduent due to a third-party data exposure.
These notices include offers of at least one year of free identity protection services, encompassing credit monitoring, dark web surveillance, and identity restoration support.
Recipients are being advised to consider placing fraud alerts or credit freezes with major credit bureaus to reduce the risk of identity theft.
In an updated statement issued February 11, Conduent reiterated that the notification process is still ongoing.
Conduent discovered it was the victim of a cybersecurity incident in January 2025 and agreed to send notification letters on behalf of its clients by April 15.
A dedicated call center has been set up to address consumer inquiries regarding the incident.
At this time, Conduent has no evidence of any attempted or actual misuse of any information potentially affected by this incident.
The Conduent incident marks the second major third party driven breach disclosed by Volvo Group in recent months.

In August 2025, a separate compromise at IT services supplier Miljödata exposed the personal data of approximately 1.5 million individuals, including Volvo Group employees in both Sweden and the United States.
That incident involved the exposure of full names and Social Security numbers tied to internal staff records.
Security leaders say Volvo’s experience illustrates the cascading risks introduced by deeply interconnected IT and data service providers.
Piyush Sharma, CEO and co founder of Tuskira, said Volvo’s situation reflects a broader structural problem in enterprise security.

Attackers who compromise an IT provider like Conduent can often gain access to downstream environments or sensitive shared data, resulting in situations where one intrusion can trigger impacts down supply chains.

For large manufacturers like Volvo Group North America, even mature internal security programs cannot fully insulate organizations from the risk posed by vendors that process, store, or transmit sensitive data at scale.
Third party cyber risk management is increasingly shifting from a compliance exercise to a core operational priority for large manufacturers.
source_title: Volvo Group North America Hit by Data Exposure After Massive Conduent Breach Spreads Across Supply Chain

facts: Volvo Trucks North America begins production of its new regional hauler, the all-new Volvo VNR, at the company’s plant in Dublin, Virginia.
The new Volvo VNR is designed to meet the demands of urban and regional delivery operations.
The Volvo VNR was first unveiled in March 2025.
Order books for the Volvo VNR opened six months after its unveiling.
The first units rolled off the assembly line in February 2026.
The first units will be delivered to Clayton, a national single-family homebuilder.
Peter Voorhoeve is president of Volvo Trucks North America.
The Volvo VNR is built on a completely new platform that is 90 percent new compared with the legacy model.
The Volvo VNR is purpose-built for navigating congested city streets, distribution centers, and urban routes where visibility, precision, and agility are critical.
The all-new Volvo VNR is assembled at Volvo Trucks’ New River Valley Plant.
All Volvo trucks for the North American market are built at the New River Valley Plant.
The Virginia facility is Volvo’s largest manufacturing site globally.
The Virginia facility plays a central role in Volvo’s U.S. operations.
Volvo has recently invested $400 million in major upgrades at the plant.
The plant upgrades include the addition of a new 350,000-square-foot facility for cab welding.
The plant upgrades include upgraded paint and material flow systems.
Volvo has built trucks for the U.S. and Canadian markets in Virginia for more than 40 years.
The expansion supports Volvo’s growth plans in North America.
The expansion supports Volvo’s long-standing commitment to U.S. manufacturing and a skilled workforce.
The new Volvo VNR incorporates a comprehensive Volvo safety package with advanced onboard technologies designed to support operation in dense urban traffic and pedestrian-heavy environments.
The truck offers better forward visibility than the previous model, enabled by a sloped hood design and a larger panoramic windshield.
The truck incorporates industry-first safety innovations including integrated side curtain airbags.
The Volvo VNR is the safest regional truck ever produced by Volvo Trucks North America.
The new VNR delivers up to a 7.5 percent improvement in fuel economy compared with the legacy model.
The improved fuel economy is driven by enhanced aerodynamics, advanced powertrain features, and idle-shutdown systems.
Improved fuel efficiency helps lower operating costs while supporting sustainability goals in regional and local delivery applications.
The start of production of the Volvo VNR follows the launch of Volvo Trucks’ all-new long-haul Volvo VNL.
Together, the VNL and VNR reflect Volvo’s focus on application-specific trucks designed to improve efficiency, safety, and uptime across a range of fleet operations.
The all-new VNR features a tighter turning radius that makes it well-suited for navigating congested city streets, distribution centers, and urban routes.
Delivers up to a 7.5 percent increase in fuel efficiency compared to the legacy model.
Offers a tighter turning radius for improved maneuverability in regional and urban environments.
Available with the Volvo Active Safety Platform with Volvo Active Driver Assist Plus.
Available with a Camera Monitor System option that provides superior visibility compared to traditional mirrors.

The Camera Monitor System enhances safety during lane changes and backing maneuvers, features infrared technology for sharper low-light views to support night driving, and offers reference lines that indicate the rear of the trailer (including doubles) along with cameras that track the rear corners.
Driver awareness is enhanced through infra-red camera technology for low-light operation, which reduces glare from headlights of vehicles behind the truck.
Digital reference lines help drivers better judge trailer position and distance to surrounding objects.
The Auto Pan feature allows the camera view to follow the trailer through turns, supporting safer lane changes and improved visibility in dense urban conditions.
Provides multiple axle options, including Adaptive Loading, Electronically Controlled Suspension, and Global Rear Air Suspension.
Available in a variety of Class 8 configurations, including 4x2, 6x2, and 6x4 tractors, as well as straight trucks with multiple body types.

Available in two trim levels: the Core trim delivers a practical and durable interior for regional haul applications, while the Edge trim offers additional driver comfort and premium features for fleets seeking an enhanced in-cab experience.
Built on a 24-volt electrical architecture to support advanced safety and connectivity features.
Engine ratings include the VGT engine, ranging from 405-455 HP and 1450-1850 lb-ft of torque.
The D13 Turbo Compound engine harnesses a waste heat recovery system to capture lost energy in order to maximize engine efficiency.
Two additional low-NOx engine ratings are available for the D13.
Volvo Trucks North America began production of the all-new Volvo VNR in February 2026 at the company’s New River Valley Plant in Dublin, Virginia.
Volvo Trucks North America is headquartered in Greensboro, North Carolina.
Volvo Trucks North America is one of the leading heavy-duty truck manufacturers in North America.
Its Uptime Services commitment is delivered by a network of nearly 400 authorized dealers across North America and the 24/7 Volvo Trucks Uptime Center.
Every Volvo truck is assembled in the Volvo Trucks New River Valley manufacturing facility in Dublin, Virginia.
The Dublin, Virginia facility meets the internationally recognized ISO 9001 standard for quality.
The Dublin, Virginia facility meets the 14001 standard for environmental care.
The Dublin, Virginia facility holds a dual ISO 50001/Superior Energy Performance certification at the platinum level, indicating a sustained excellence in energy management.
Volvo Trucks North America provides complete transport solutions for its customers, offering a full range of diesel, alternative-fuel and all-electric vehicles.
Volvo Trucks North America is part of the Volvo Trucks global organization.
Volvo Trucks supplies complete transport solutions for discerning professional customers with its full range of medium- and heavy-duty trucks.
Customer support is provided via a global network of dealers with 2,200 service points in about 130 countries.
Volvo trucks are assembled in 12 countries across the globe.
In 2025 approximately 120,000 Volvo trucks were delivered worldwide.
Volvo Trucks is part of the Volvo Group, one of the world’s leading manufacturers of trucks, buses, construction equipment and marine and industrial engines.
The group also provides complete solutions for financing and service.
Volvo Trucks’ work is based on the core values of quality, safety and environmental care.
source_title: Volvo Trucks starts U.S. production of new regional hauler at Virginia manufacturing plant

facts: Wabash is expanding its network in the Southeast to improve customer access and service in the Atlanta metro area.
The company is opening a new Parts & Service center in the Atlanta area.
Wabash is expanding its dealer relationship with Fleetco.
Wabash is adding CS Truck and Trailer to its Preferred Partner Network.
The new Atlanta-area Parts & Service Center is strategically located in the Southeast’s trucking corridor.
The facility offers faster access to parts, service, and truck body upfitting.
The center provides Wabash’s Ready-to-Mount (RTM) truck bodies for dry freight and platform applications.
Wabash stated these RTM truck bodies provide rapid turnaround for in-stock chassis.
The Atlanta site officially opened on Sept. 18, 2025.
The Atlanta center joins existing Wabash service centers in California, Florida, Ohio, Pennsylvania, and Texas.
Fleetco, a long-standing Wabash dealer, will now represent Wabash in the greater Atlanta market.
This dealer expansion expands Fleetco's footprint across the Southeast.
The expansion gives customers additional access to new and used equipment and enhances local sales and service coverage.
Three CS Truck and Trailer locations in Georgia were added to the Preferred Partner Network.
The network addition extends expert service, maintenance, and access to Wabash Genuine Parts through authorized third-party dealerships.
The company said the Preferred Partner Network is designed to make high-quality parts and service more accessible through a unified national network.
source_title: Wabash Expands Southeast Network to Enhance Service

facts: Wabash (NYSE: WNC) is the visionary leader of connected solutions for the transportation, logistics and distribution industries.
President and Chief Executive Officer Brent Yeagy was recognized on IBJ Media’s second annual Indiana 250 list of the state’s 250 most influential business and community leaders.
Yeagy was recognized in the manufacturing, logistics and transportation category, which is one of 10 industry categories that constitute the complete list.

Influential leaders are identified as those “that others in the state, community or an industry look to for leadership; the people who are making the biggest difference in Indiana and who are known to be influential, visionary and impactful in their work.”

Reporters and editors from IBJ’s three news brands – Indianapolis Business Journal, Inside INdiana Business and Indiana Lawyer – compiled the list after reviewing nominations, researching organizations and interviewing leaders across the state.
Wabash is headquartered in Lafayette, Indiana.

Wabash designs, manufactures, and services dry freight and refrigerated trailers, flatbed trailers, tank trailers, dry and refrigerated truck bodies, structural composite panels and products, trailer aerodynamic solutions, and specialty food grade processing equipment.
source_title: Wabash CEO Named One of Indiana’s Most Influential Leaders | WNC Stock News

facts: Wabash National Corporation (NYSE:WNC) stock touched a 52-week low of $18.24 amid market shifts.
The manufacturer of semi-trailers and liquid transportation systems faced headwinds over the past year, reflected in a 1-year stock decline of 13.03%.
Wabash National was ordered to pay $462 million in damages following a product liability lawsuit related to a 2019 motor vehicle accident.
The company is contesting the verdict and considering all legal options, including appeals.
Wabash reported Q2 2024 earnings that met revenue expectations and surpassed earnings per share forecasts.
The company adjusted its full-year 2024 guidance to a midpoint of $2.1 billion in revenue and $1.55 in EPS.
Wabash declared a quarterly dividend of $0.08 per share, reinforcing its commitment to shareholder value.
DA Davidson maintained a neutral stance on shares of Wabash National, keeping the price target at $23.00.
The company has made improvements at its new South Plant.
Mike Pettit transitioned to Senior Vice President and Chief Growth Officer.
Patrick Keslin stepped in as Senior Vice President and Chief Financial Officer.
source_title: Wabash National stock hits 52-week low at $18.24 amid market shifts By Investing.com

facts: Wabash National Corp. has received orders exceeding $70 million from J.B. Hunt Transport Services Inc. for 6,000 53-foot DuraPlate domestic containers.
The order includes a smaller number of container chassis in addition to the domestic containers.
The 53-foot domestic containers feature a new design utilizing Wabash National's proprietary DuraPlate technology.
The containers have a stackable, high-cube design with an uninterrupted sidewall and interior dimensions resembling conventional trailers.
These will be the first 53-foot units in the J.B. Hunt fleet with sufficient strength to permit positioning on the bottom of a "double-stack" on the railcar.
Richard E. Dessimoz, acting CEO of Wabash, stated that J.B. Hunt selected the DuraPlate container for intermodal operations and the DuraPlate trailer for over-the-road operations.
Dessimoz expressed confidence that the DuraPlate material will prove to be an excellent performer in this new application.
Wabash said the containers incorporate features designed to reduce maintenance costs and increase durability.
These containers will be the first production domestic containers to use the DuraPlate sidewall material.
The DuraPlate material consists of a polyethylene plastic core sandwiched between two sheets of pre-painted high-strength galvanized steel.
Wabash claims the resulting panel is efficient, lightweight, and incredibly strong, offering advantages over aluminum plate and traditional sheet/post sidewall designs.
Kirk Thompson, president & CEO of J.B. Hunt Transportation Services, stated satisfaction with the performance and quality of their large fleet of DuraPlate van trailers in their truck business unit.
Thompson noted the opportunity to convert J.B. Hunt's intermodal container fleet to exclusively 53-foot equipment by replacing 48-foot equipment with stackable 53-foot DuraPlate containers.
Thompson stated that J.B. Hunt’s intermodal customers will have access to North America's largest fleet of exclusively 53-foot containers.
Rail partners will have the option to stack the new containers on top in traditional configurations or on the bottom of 53-foot rail cars.
With this addition, Wabash now offers a complete line of intermodal equipment including domestic containers, piggyback trailers, and bi-modal equipment.
source_title: J.B. Hunt orders 6,000 containers from Wabash National | FleetOwner

facts: Wabash National Corp. announced organizational changes within its Composites business, which designs and manufactures composite panels and doors, trailer aerodynamic solutions, portable storage containers, and mobile shelters.
Jeff Cole was appointed director of business development for Wabash Composites to lead all business development activities supporting the company's composite growth initiatives.

Jeff Cole previously worked at Solvay with sales responsibility for its Industrial Business Division and has spent his career in the global composite materials industry serving aerospace, aviation, automotive, and wind energy markets.
Frank Pendleton was appointed director of sales for Wabash Composites to lead the sales team toward strategic growth initiatives.
Frank Pendleton previously served as vice president and general manager at Scott Intermodal in Jacksonville, Florida, and has over 25 years in transportation industry sales and operations roles.
Joseph Perry was appointed sales manager for Wabash Composites to sell the product portfolio, including aerodynamic trailer solutions.
Joseph Perry previously worked at Michelin and Ziegler Tire for 10 years, managing sales teams and key customer accounts.
Blake Robertson was appointed director of manufacturing for Wabash Composites to lead manufacturing teams in Lafayette and Frankfort, Indiana.
Blake Robertson previously spent 23 years in the extrusion industry, most recently as vice president and general manager for the U.S. Midwest Region at Sapa.
source_title: Wabash National Corp. Announces Organizational Changes

facts: BLBD designs, engineers, manufactures, and sells school buses and related parts in the United States, Canada, and internationally.
It operates through two segments: Bus and Parts.
In addition to compressed natural gas and diesel-powered buses, the company also offers electric-powered school buses.
On September 19, BLBD delivered a total of 13 electric school buses to the Bowling Green Independent School District (BGISD) situated in Kentucky.
BGISD serves around 4,400 students across eight schools and initiated a pilot program aimed at expanding its school bus fleet by introducing zero-emission vehicles.
On May 25, BLBD marked a significant achievement with the inauguration of its EV Build-up Center at its primary U.S. manufacturing plant in Fort Valley, Georgia.
This facility spans approximately 40,000 square feet and has been established to meet the surging demand for electric school buses.
The company is taking significant strides to bolster its production capabilities for electric school buses, with the ambitious goal of ramping up daily production from four to 20 vehicles.
In terms of forward EV/Sales, BLBD is trading at 0.64x, which is 61.6% lower than the industry average of 1.67x.
The stock’s forward Price/Sales of 0.57x is 58.3% lower than the 1.36x industry average.
Likewise, the stock’s forward Price/Cash Flow ratio of 7.49x is 42.9% lower than the 13.10x industry average.
In the fiscal third quarter ending July 1, 2023, BLBD’s net sales increased 42.8% year-over-year to $294.28 million, while its gross profit grew 111.9% from the year-ago value to $45.75 million.
Its operating profit rose significantly year-over-year to $19.42 million.
The company’s net income and EPS amounted to $9.36 million and $0.29 versus a net loss and loss per share of $6.44 million and $0.20 in the prior-year quarter, respectively.
Analysts expect BLBD’s revenue for the fiscal fourth quarter ending September 2023 to increase 12.6% year-over-year to $290.11 million, while its EPS for the ongoing quarter is expected to be $0.48.
The company’s EPS is projected to improve by 15% per annum over the next five years.
BLBD has an excellent surprise history, surpassing its revenue estimates in each of the trailing four quarters.
The stock has surged 99.2% over the past year and 83.4% year-to-date to close the last trading session at $19.64.
BLBD’s POWR Ratings reflect this robust outlook.
The stock has an overall B rating, translating to Buy in the proprietary rating system.
The POWR Ratings assess stocks by 118 different factors, each with its own weighting.
It has a B grade for Growth and Quality.
In the 56-stock Auto & Vehicle Manufacturers industry, it is ranked #21.
source_title: 3 B-Rated Auto Buys Priced Under $30 AND Better Than…

facts: Reported EPS for Q1 2026 was negative $1.17, missing expectations of negative $1.01.
Brent L. Yeagy serves as president and chief executive officer, and Patrick Keslin serves as chief financial officer.
Mike Bennett transitioned out of the company as of April 8 after 14 years of service.
Q1 backlog increased 19% sequentially to $837 million.
Q2 2026 revenue guidance is set between $380 million and $400 million, with adjusted EPS guidance between negative $0.40 and negative $0.60 per share.
Plant idling actions announced in January 2026 are progressing as planned, with $3 million of costs recognized in Q1 2026.
Additional dry van capacity from the Lafayette South plant, completed in late 2023, provides scalable capability to produce approximately 10,000 incremental trailers versus prior upcycles.
The company targets a dry van market share of more than 25% in the first half of the cycle.
New upfit site openings are progressing in three major metroplexes designed to serve the Chicago, Atlanta, and Phoenix areas.
The company is already supporting major national accounts out of its Atlanta location.
At peak, additional upfit sites are expected to generate incremental revenue in the range of $10 million to $20 million per site with gross margins approaching 20%.
Q1 2026 consolidated revenue was reported as [inaudible].
The company shipped 5,378 new trailers and 1,527 truck bodies during Q1 2026.
Adjusted non-GAAP gross margin for Q1 was negative 2.6% of sales, and adjusted non-GAAP operating margin was negative 18.3%.
Adjusted non-GAAP EBITDA for Q1 was negative $38 million, or negative 12.5% of sales.
Adjusted non-GAAP net income attributable to common shareholders was negative $47.5 million, or negative $1.17 per diluted share.
The Transportation Solutions segment generated $250 million in revenue and reported an operating loss of $34.5 million on a non-GAAP basis.
The Parts and services segment delivered $54 million in revenue and negative $2 million of operating income on a non-GAAP basis.
Operating cash flow for Q1 was negative $33.7 million, resulting in negative free cash flow of $37.3 million.
As of March 31, total liquidity including cash and available borrowings stood at $165 million.
The company invested approximately $4 million in traditional capital expenditures in Q1 and returned $3.5 million to shareholders through quarterly dividends.
Wabash is engaged in discussions with banking partners to address its existing ABL facility ahead of September 2026 when it turns current.
Q2 2026 operating margin is expected at approximately negative 5%.
Positive adjusted EBITDA is anticipated for the full year 2026.
Q1 overall injury rate improved 7% versus 2025, and 19% versus the prior year, with total injuries declining 9% sequentially and 42% year over year.
The truck body business recovery profile is expected to trail dry vans by approximately six to nine months.
The company is working through a deployment schedule for a repositioned refrigerated van product, having made low-level capital purchases for long lead-time areas.
Additional hiring will be required in the later half of 2027 to add shifts as demand ramps up.

facts: Wabash National, a semi-trailer manufacturer, is closing its facility in Little Falls.
More than 50 jobs will be eliminated following the announcement of the Little Falls facility closure.
Wabash National first opened its Little Falls facility in 2017.
At opening, the company promised an $11 million investment to renovate the former Larson Boat Works plant and to employ over 150 people by 2022.
The Little Falls facility is shutting down less than 10 years after opening.
Wabash National announced the closure of the plant and subsequent layoffs due to "idling manufacturing operations" on Jan. 5.
Workers will be let go starting in early March, with final layoffs being finalized by April 17.
Little Falls mayor Greg Zylka stated that city officials had worked together with Wabash National for many years to bring the company to the area and celebrated its grand opening.
City officials are beginning meetings to help workers and the community affected by the plant's closure.
Mayor Greg Zylka noted that losing a large business hurts the community, especially with rising levy rates and cost of goods, which could be devastating to families.
The city has been in contact with various manufacturers interested in expanding existing businesses or welcoming new businesses and workers to the region.
City officials are working with Barrett Petfood to try to expand their facility.
Other companies have contacted the city looking for land to build.
Wabash National also announced it would be closing a plant in Goshen, Indiana.
The closure of the Goshen, Indiana plant will lay off an additional 144 workers.
source_title: Semi-Trailer Manufacturer Wabash National Closing Facility in Little Falls

facts: U.S. customs officials are poised to levy new duties on semitruck trailers made in China and Mexico after a yearlong legal battle fought by Savannah’s Great Dane LLC and two other American manufacturers.
The decision marks a small victory for the American employers against an onslaught of cheaper, less safe Chinese trailers that are heavily subsidized by Beijing.
Chris Hammond serves as executive vice president of Great Dane and is the great-great grandson of the 125-year-old company’s founder.
Great Dane originated as a Savannah-based factory that helped the U.S. Army transport heavy equipment between the U.S. mainland and European battlefields in World War II.
Great Dane has grown into one of the largest suppliers of refrigerated trailers in the nation.
The company operates 10 manufacturing facilities across the country, including a factory in Statesboro.
Great Dane supplies semitrailers to retail companies such as Walmart, Home Depot and Amazon with the semitrailers they need to move products around the country.
Great Dane, along with Wabash National Corp. and Stoughton Trailers LLC, presented data to the U.S. International Trade Commission regarding market conditions.
The three companies claim recent market headwinds are due to an unfair playing field rather than superior products or marketing by competitors.
The companies estimate that about 40% of the U.S. market for van trailers is supplied by China, Mexico and Canada combined.
Import data suggests foreign suppliers, mainly Chinese, boosted their sales in America from about 48,000 van trailers annually between 2015-2017 to 80,600 between 2022 and 2024.
Hammond attributes this competition to loopholes in trade law and a lack of enforcement by U.S. officials against state subsidies and exploitative wage practices by Chinese rivals.
According to Hammond, this competition has caused many other American trailer manufacturers out of business.
Hammond stated that Great Dane makes the safest and best products and is doing its level best to support good jobs, good manufacturing jobs, for Georgians.
Hammond emphasized that the company is simply asking for a level playing field.
The American Trailer Manufacturers Coalition, the lobbying organization for these three companies, petitioned the U.S. government to support U.S. manufacturers in two ways.
One petition goal was imposing countervailing duties on foreign rivals to close the price gap and offset foreign government subsidies or unfair wage competition.
The ITC determined that the U.S. trailer manufacturers have been materially injured by imports.
The Commerce Department instructed customs authorities to add duties ranging from 82.3% to 128.7% for trailers from China and 1.9% to 1.95% for trailers from Mexico.
A second Commerce decision set additional antidumping duties on specific Chinese companies intended to offset the amount by which a product is sold at less than fair value in America.
Those antidumping duties were set at 130% in a preliminary decision that is expected to be finalized later this summer.
Hammond said U.S. government action is a boon for his company and will enable Great Dane to hire more Americans and expand its output.
Hammond noted the decisions are important for national security considerations, as a thriving U.S. trucking trailer industry is necessary for America’s economy.
Hammond asserted that it is the job of the government to help businesses that have critical national security implications.
source_title: Savannah's Great Dane wins Chinese tariff battle - The Current

facts: Trailer builder Wabash National Corp. (NYSE: WNC) is refocusing its business on cold chain, home delivery, and parts and service, pushing resources to refrigerated vans needed to move vaccines and food.
Wabash anticipates that growth in home delivery will have staying power even as more Americans receive COVID-19 inoculations.
CEO Brent Yeagy stated on the company's fourth-quarter earnings call that new technology and products to keep goods refrigerated present significant organic growth prospects.
CEO Brent Yeagy said, "We've never been faced with a market that has this much potential," and noted that all potential growth avenues are currently on the table.
Potential growth avenues include expanding upfitting operations and acquiring technology or another manufacturing platform.

Wabash streamlined its portfolio by selling assets that do not offer strong strategic fit and is setting the stage to backfill by diversifying with expanded and new revenue streams in transportation, logistics, and distribution markets.
Wabash sold its Beall brand of tank trailer to MAC Trailer Enterprises in December.
Wabash is retaining its other tank trailer brands.
CEO Brent Yeagy stated the company is repositioning resources to accelerate organic efforts to create additional revenue in unprecedented ways for Wabash National.
Wabash anticipates expanded use of molded structural composite (MSC) technology beyond the 53-foot refrigerated van market.
MSC technology weighs less and offers better thermal protection than other trailer construction materials.
Wabash conducted an MSC pilot project with C&S Wholesale Grocers in 2020.
Using MSC technology, Wabash can make a 48-foot trailer 30% more thermally efficient than a traditional refrigerated trailer.
The improved thermal efficiency allows refrigeration units to run fewer hours, requiring fewer batteries for all-electric units.

CEO Brent Yeagy stated that MSC technology offers unique value through durability, reduced weight, and improved thermal efficiency, with consistent customer feedback highlighting benefits not found elsewhere.
Wabash is manufacturing Gruau refrigerated inserts for the Ford Transit van to serve the rapidly expanding food and grocery home delivery market.
Traditionally constructed refrigerated cargo vans use spray foam insulation that can release gas and allow mold intrusion.
Gruau inserts fit specific van models and provide 30% to 50% greater thermal efficiency.
CEO Brent Yeagy noted that participating in this space positions Wabash to better serve the smaller light-duty upfit market compared to its larger traditional truck body products.
CEO Brent Yeagy stated that President Joe Biden's green agenda, the lingering pandemic, climate change, and technology advances combine to create opportunity.
Yeagy stated that over the next four years, these factors open opportunities for Wabash to leverage innovation and existing technology to bring sustainable solutions to these markets.
Traditional truck bodies remain the main catalyst funding Wabash's expansion.
The value of Wabash's trailer order backlog rose to $1.5 billion at the end of Q4, up from $1 billion at the end of Q3.
Most of the backlog consists of dry vans, which dominate the Commercial Trailer Products division.
The average selling price for trailers was $27,000, remaining flat compared to the prior year.
CEO Brent Yeagy stated that Q4 shipments were in line with expectations and sees no structural impediments to moving forward in 2021.
Wabash shipped 10,610 trailers in Q4, down from 14,950 in the same period of the prior year.
Despite earlier concerns about finding workers during the pandemic, Wabash added 600 hourly employees during the October-December period.
CFO Mike Pettit stated the company is seeking 900 additional new hires for the current quarter.
Net sales for Q4 2020 were $404.1 million.
Operating income was $10 million, representing 2.5% of net sales.
Total revenue for the full year 2020 totaled $1.5 billion.
Full-year 2020 operating income was $22.5 million.
Q4 net income was $5.5 million, or 10 cents per diluted share.
Full-year 2020 net income was $7.8 million, or 15 cents per share.
Q4 operating EBITDA was $25.2 million, or 6.2% of net sales.
Full-year operating EBITDA was $73.6 million, or 5% of net sales.
source_title: Wabash National Refocuses Business On Cold Chain And e-commerce

facts: Wheelabrator Group designs and manufactures standard and custom shot blast machines for surface preparation of steel plates, structural shapes, bars, pipe and fabrications.
Preservation lines are available for blasting and priming plates and shapes.
The company provides surface preparation solutions for the structural steel, fabricated steel, aerospace, ship, railway, construction and more industries.

Wheelabrator Plus professionals conduct thorough assessments of installed blast equipment to determine if productivity, cost savings, safety or environmental improvements can be realized by upgrading to newer technology.
The company's facility is located at 1606 Executive Drive, LaGrange, GA 30240.
Brad Lorson serves as the Director of Wheelabrator OEM Sales- NA.
On May 15, 2026, Wheelabrator appointed shot blasting industry veteran Mike Reaves to head up its North American EMP (equipment modernization program) team as a technical specialist.
On July 10, 2025, LaGrange, Ga.-based shot blast equipment manufacturer Wheelabrator appointed Matt Schweitzer to head up its North American equipment sales business.
On May 29, 2024, Wheelabrator named Magdalene Höltke as senior vice president of its global wheelblast business.

On March 27, 2018, Wheelabrator Group Inc. consolidated its North American operations by creating a hub for centralized sales, service, engineering, and technology development at its LaGrange, Ga., facility.

On April 22, 2016, Wheelabrator developed a new HD rotary screen designed for blast machines operating in harsh environments, featuring harder steels in strategic locations, abrasive-resistant flighting and end bands, and DOM tubing.

On March 6, 2015, Wheelabrator introduced a concave blade for its 35 Ezefit blast wheel that achieves a narrow blast pattern and a tight “hot spot” at high abrasive speeds, suitable for processing narrow workpieces like wire, rod, tube, pipe, bar, and automotive parts.
Wheelabrator's structural steel shotblast cleaning systems are designed for descaling and cleaning structural shapes before or after fabrication.

A Wheelabrator rotary mesh table system is suitable for cleaning small parts offline using one operator and can process flame-cut shapes, fabrications, automotive parts, aluminum die castings, and forgings with an airless wheel blast process.

The Wheelabrator model 1824 structural preblast roll conveyor machine provides a flexible surface preparation method for structural shapes prior to fabrication, helping reduce wear and tear on cutting tools like band saw blades and drill bits.
On May 6, 2009, DISA Group merged with Wheelabrator Group, with both companies owned by Mid Europa Partners at the time.
The Wheelabrator Continuous Rocker Barrel machine is designed for inline cleaning of castings and forged parts, with production requirements ranging from less than 5 tons/hr. to more than 25 tons/hr.
Wheelabrator's WS 4/210 spinner hanger machine cleans paint hooks and prepares odd-shaped parts that do not adapt easily to batch machine processing.
source_title: Wheelabrator Group (USA)

facts: Wheelabrator’s Contract Manufacturing Service (WCMS) operation is a full service machining and fabrication facility dedicated to producing products to the highest quality standard at competitive prices.
The facility works with materials including carbon steel, alloy steel, stainless steel, and manganese steel.
WCMS Machine Shop equipment includes CNC machining centers, lathes and mills, a horizontal boring mill, a vertical turning mill, manual lathes, mills, and drills.
WCMS Fabrication Shop supports plasma or oxy-fuel burning, sawing, bending, punching, rolling, drilling, welding, blast cleaning, and painting.
WCMS operates within a 135,000 sq ft facility with an overhead crane lifting capacity up to 30 tons and a 33ft ceiling under hook.
The site features a wide range of Wheelabrator shot blast machines capable of cleaning products in either small or large batches.
Wheelabrator has been certified to the ISO9001 quality standard since 1997.
The company is classified as a Job Shop / Contract Manufacturer.
The facility employs between 200 and 500 people.
Wheelabrator Group is located in LaGrange, Georgia, 30240, United States.

The company's Centers for Product Innovation cover industries such as aerospace, automotive, foundry, shipping, construction, and medical.
Each center has a group of highly qualified engineers dedicated to delivering the right machine to fit detailed client requirements.
Wheelabrator specializes in the blast process.
The company can design and deliver complete automated materials handling solutions to include masking, delivery, loading and unloading of components.

facts: Wheelabrator Group reached a major milestone during the first quarter of 2014 with 1,000,000 hours without a lost time incident.
Over 200 Wheelabrator employees and guest will mark this special event during a safety luncheon at their LaGrange facility, March 13th.
Featured speakers will include Robert E. Joyce, President & CEO of Norican Group (parent company of Wheelabrator and DISA).
Poul Erik Arre, Director of HS&E, Norican Group from Denmark will speak.
Pierre Tanguay, VP Supply Chain, Wheelabrator Group will speak.
Judith Carr, Member of Chairman’s Board Georgia State Board Workers Compensation will speak.
Jim Thornton, Mayor City of LaGrange will speak.
Kathleen Parsloe, CPCU, ARM Senior VP Marsh USA, Inc. will speak.
Wheelabrator is the world’s leading surface preparation company, offering a complete range of equipment, services and parts.

Leading companies in the foundry, automotive, aerospace, energy, marine, rail, construction and many other industries have used Wheelabrator Group’s products and services to improve productivity and profitability for over 100 years.

With active customers in nearly 100 countries, Wheelabrator manufactures a full range of wheel-type shot blast machines, mass finishing equipment dedicated to the demands of the industry, and automated airblast solutions.
Technically advanced and designed for ease of maintenance, Wheelabrator Group machines range from standard versions to fully customized and integrated systems.

Wheelabrator Plus professionals will inspect your current blasting equipment and conduct a thorough assessment that includes reviewing the latest modernization options to determine if any productivity, cost savings, safety or environmental improvements could be realized as a result of upgrading to newer technology.
On March 13, 2014 an American Flag flew over the Washington D.C. Capitol in recognition of the 1,000,000 work hours for Wheelabrator.
This flag was sent to Wheelabrator with a certificate in recognition for the 1,000,000 work hours without a lost time incident; and will be displayed in the LaGrange, GA Wheelabrator lobby.
Wheelabrator, a global provider of surface preparation and finishing solutions, works closely with customers to deliver the best solutions, service and support worldwide.

Georgia Power nominated WIKA USA for the 2021 Movers & Makers Manufacturing and Supply Chain Awards in the Large Manufacturers (200+ employees) category.
WIKA USA was a finalist among 14 other excellent companies for the award.
WIKA USA has over 800 employees.
In 2020, WIKA USA manufactured pressure transducers for anesthesia machines, pressure gauges for oxygen manifolds, and pressure sensors for ventilators.
WIKA USA's headquarters is located in Lawrenceville, Gwinnett County.
WIKA USA collaborates with Gwinnett Technical College on programs in welding and management.
Business students at Gwinnett Technical College study WIKA USA's lean manufacturing and Kaizen approach.
Gwinnett Technical College assists WIKA USA with customer-service training.
WIKA USA has close ties with Georgia Gwinnett College, which is the alma mater of two of its graduate trainees.
WIKA USA participates in civic initiatives including Toys for Tots, canned food drives, Bed Race to end homelessness, and From Hunger to Hope during work hours.

facts: WIKA is a leading manufacturer of gauge thermometers and machine glass thermometers for industrial and process applications, from HVAC and refrigeration to pharmaceuticals and oil & gas.
Many of WIKA's industrial temperature gauges are produced in the Atlanta metro area.
Customers of WIKA's Atlanta-produced gauges are assured of industry-leading workmanship, competitive pricing, responsive customer service, and fast delivery times.
WIKA offers a portfolio of bimetal temperature gauges, gas-actuated temperature gauges, and expansion temperature gauges.

WIKA's temperature measuring instruments feature simple, robust, and cost-effective designs frequently used for industrial measuring tasks in heating, ventilation and air-conditioning, machine building, plant/vessel/pipeline/apparatus construction, and chemical/petrochemical industries.
WIKA provides a complete range of measuring instruments for electrical temperature measurement including thermocouples, resistance thermometers, temperature switches, and temperature transmitters.
The core of WIKA's electronic temperature measurement instruments is a sensor that converts measured temperature into an electrical signal.
WIKA thermocouples consist of two wires of different material welded together, with the welding point representing the actual measuring point.
WIKA resistance thermometers use platinum resistors (Pt100 and Pt1000) where measured resistance changes depending on temperature.
WIKA temperature switches provide control and analogue output signals to the process alongside temperature measurement.
WIKA temperature transmitters are mounted either on a DIN rail in a control cabinet or in a connection head directly at the measuring point.
WIKA's mechanical temperature measurement range comprises bimetal, gas-actuated, and expansion thermometers based on the expansion of temperature-dependent materials.
Bimetal thermometers from WIKA are composed of two bonded metal strips with differing expansion coefficients, where resulting bending measures temperature.
Gas-actuated thermometers from WIKA are filled with an inert gas or gas mixture, using temperature-dependent change in gas pressure to lead via a capillary to the temperature display.
Expansion thermometers from WIKA contain a liquid-filled measuring system that generates a pressure change when temperature changes, displayed via an integrated movement.

Mechatronic temperature measurement instruments from WIKA have additional equipment such as alarm contacts and switch contacts that trigger a switching operation when a pre-defined measured value is reached.

WIKA's mechatronic measuring instruments can be used for control and regulation processes via transmitted output signals, including mechanical temperature measurement with electrical output signals or switch contacts and mechanical temperature switches.
source_title: Temperature measurement - WIKA USA

WIKA supports U.S. manufacturing at its Lawrenceville location.

facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) announced its second production vehicle, the 2026 IONIQ 9, an all-electric, three-row SUV.
The 2026 IONIQ 9 will be the first all-new Hyundai vehicle assembled at the Metaplant.
The announcement follows June news regarding the 2025 IONIQ 5, an updated version of Hyundai’s award-winning electric vehicle, which will serve as the first production vehicle at the Metaplant.
With the IONIQ 9, HMGMA is doubling down on its electric lineup.
Production of the IONIQ 9 is slated to begin in Q1 2025, with the SUV hitting the market in Q2.
Oscar Kwon serves as the CEO of HMGMA.

Oscar Kwon stated: “We are thrilled to start building the all-new IONIQ 9 at the Metaplant. Every new vehicle we build brings an enhanced level of excitement for our Meta Pros, but to see the expansion of the acclaimed IONIQ lineup being manufactured locally is particularly special. As the only facility to build IONIQ 9s in North America, our Meta Pros will take pride in knowing every time they see the vehicle on the roadway in the U.S. that they took part in it being made.”
The IONIQ 9 was unveiled at AutoMobility LA 2024 in Los Angeles, alongside a simultaneous reveal by HMGMA at AutoNation Hyundai Savannah.
Meta Pros who contributed to the assembly of the preproduction vehicle were on-site to celebrate the launch.
Thanks to its U.S.-based assembly and battery sourcing, the 2026 IONIQ 9 is expected to qualify for the full $7,500 federal EV tax credit.
source_title: Hyundai's Metaplant Reveals 2026 IONIQ 9 as Newest Production Vehicle

facts: Dan Dubblestyne serves as the director of health, safety, and environment at Woodbridge Foam Corporation.
Woodbridge Foam Corporation is a private Canadian company that operates 50 to 60 facilities across approximately 12 countries.
The company's facilities largely serve the automotive market and manufacture seating and interior parts for buses, motorcycles, and golf carts.
Woodbridge Foam's core business is manufacturing polyurethane foam products, primarily producing car seats, bolsters, headliners, armrests, and headrests.
During the COVID-19 pandemic, Woodbridge pivoted its production lines to manufacture N95 face masks made from polyurethane foam.
The company collaborated with INOAC, a Japanese partner in one of its global joint ventures, to utilize an existing mask design for production.
Transitioning to PPE required splitting operations across multiple plants, with one facility producing the foam and another cutting and assembling it into final products.
Retooling did not require significant capital investment because foam cutting was performed on programmable CNC machines requiring only minor design modifications.
Woodbridge called back several idled teammates to return to work during the PPE manufacturing transition.
The N95 masks had to meet strict standards for sterilization, liquid penetration resistance, and particle filtration, requiring testing with federal and provincial agencies and laboratories.
Federal and provincial governments actively assisted Woodbridge in overcoming regulatory hurdles to transition mask production.
Plant workers received extra training through online programs focused on quality inspections and safe, consistent manufacturing processes for the new product.
The company implemented a Pandemic Response Toolbox and a Safe Work Playbook detailing protocols for temperature screening, facility access, PPE use, personal hygiene, case tracking, and containment.
An executive-level pandemic response team met daily to oversee operations and ensure all plants adhered to safety protocols.
Physical barriers were installed in facilities where employees had to work shoulder-to-shoulder to maintain safety standards.
Among approximately 7,500 global teammates, Woodbridge reported fewer COVID-19 cases than expected due to these safety measures.
Most manufacturing facilities resumed operations in late April or early May, though production volumes remained reduced as customers across the supply chain faced disruptions.
Pandemic response protocols implemented during the transition were expected to carry over well into 2021.
source_title: Woodbridge Foam Corporation Discusses Its Pivot to Producing… | ASUG

facts: Woodbridge defines automotive seat comfort through a combination of static and dynamic test factors.
Woodbridge specializes in fully-integrated foam solutions.
The company supports various automotive and non-automotive programs by combining products to deliver complete systems, modules, parts, and materials.
Woodbridge has a deep commitment to R&D science, enabling it to develop products and solve problems that other companies cannot.
Woodbridge is an expert in comfort, lightweighting, and craftsmanship, specializing in solutions for low VOC, structural integrity, cargo management, and acoustics.
Woodbridge has an extensive global footprint that allows customers to access its network of capabilities and expertise regardless of location or operational scale.
Woodbridge employs scientists, engineers, builders, and creators around the world.
The company runs a community involvement initiative called Woodbridge Cares, which inspires employees to contribute to their communities through volunteerism and philanthropy.
The state of Georgia attracts successful companies due to a pro-business environment, a talented workforce, world-class infrastructure, and unparalleled access to the world market.
Georgia offers a performance-based package of business assistance designed to foster success for companies and the Georgia communities they call home.
Strategic industries such as distribution, technology, manufacturing, telecom, processing companies, and their headquarters qualify for Georgia's Job Tax Credit.
Depending on the community's tier, companies must create between five and 25 net new jobs per year to qualify for the Jobs Tax Credit.
For each year (up to five years) the jobs are maintained, qualified companies can claim a tax credit with a value of $750 to $3,500 per job, per year.
Companies may claim a tax credit equal to 10% of their increased qualified research and development expense over gross receipts under Georgia's R&D Tax Credits program.
To qualify for the R&D tax credit, Georgia taxpayers must qualify for a research credit under the IRS code.
Emerging companies can apply for the R&D tax credit to offset their payroll withholding once all other tax liabilities have been exhausted for their first five years.
Georgia allows small businesses making capital investments of less than $410,000 to write off up to $102,000 of these expenses in the current year under Small Business Tax Relief.
For capital investments greater than $410,000, the Small Business Tax Relief write-off is reduced dollar for dollar.
Qualified equipment purchases are exempt from sales tax when the equipment is used in the manufacturing process under Georgia's Sales & Use Tax Exemption.
Under Georgia's local option Freeport law, 100% of qualified raw materials, work-in-process, and finished goods are exempt from property tax under the Inventory Tax Exemption.
Quick Start is Georgia's nationally ranked employee training program that provides customized training for new employees in skill-based jobs at no cost to qualifying companies.
The Quick Start training program is given to the company for its future use.
Quick Start provides training space, instructors, and all needed materials related to the program.
Quick Start has the potential to save companies millions of dollars in training costs.
Georgia Work Ready Hiring Assistance Program helps companies hire the best people and boost their bottom line.
The Georgia Work Ready program is available at no cost for companies meeting minimum hiring requirements.
The Georgia Work Ready program is accessible through the state's network of technical colleges.
Georgia companies can implement Work Ready in two ways: through job profiling and Work Ready Certificates.
Work Ready job profiles identify the job tasks and skill levels necessary to be successful in any job.
Companies match those job profiles to employees' Work Ready Certificates, which measure core skills, to ensure the right person is placed in the right job every time.
