facts: Adient announced the acquisition of a foam production plant in Romulus, MI on April 27, 2026.
The acquired facility joins Adient's network of 10 foam plants in the Americas.
The purchase brings Adient's global foam manufacturing footprint to 30 plants.
The acquisition agreement included the building, land, production equipment, inventory, and related assets.
Existing Romulus plant employees will transfer to become Adient employees.
The existing collective bargaining agreement with the UAW will remain in place for the transferred workforce.
Adient stated the acquisition supports its strategic growth plan.
The addition expands Adient's manufacturing footprint to serve multiple automaker customers.
Adient purchased the Romulus plant from automotive supplier Woodbridge.
Adient is a global leader in automotive seating with more than 65,000 employees across 29 countries.
Adient operates approximately 200 manufacturing and assembly plants worldwide.
Adient produces and delivers automotive seating for all major OEMs, ranging from complete seating systems to individual components.

facts: Georgia Gov. Brian P. Kemp announced that Woory Industrial Co., Ltd., an automotive parts manufacturer, will establish a new manufacturing facility in Dublin, Georgia.
The facility will create more than 130 new jobs.
The project involves an estimated $18 million in investment in Dublin-Laurens County.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Woory Industrial is helping the state become the 'e-mobility capital of the nation.'
Governor Brian P. Kemp credited Georgia's highly-skilled workforce and reliable infrastructure network for bringing historic levels of new jobs and investment to communities across the state.
Woory Industrial Company, known as Woory, was established in 1989.
Woory specializes in heating, ventilation, and air conditioning components and systems for the global automotive industry.
Woory is concentrating on developing new products for electric vehicles (EV) and hydrogen-fueled cars.

Jungwoo Kim, CEO of Woory Industrial Company, stated that the transition to electric vehicles represents a revolution in the automobile industry and that the U.S. EV market is key among all automotive markets.
Jungwoo Kim noted that over the past 10 years, Woory has grown into a specialized EV component company through continuous innovation.
Jungwoo Kim expressed confidence that Woory's experience and technology will contribute to community prosperity and appreciated the partnership of the State of Georgia and the City of Dublin.
Woory's new facility is located at 404 Kellam Road in Dublin.
The facility will be Woory's first U.S. manufacturing location.
The facility will produce electrical heaters, control units, and actuators for EV and internal combustion carmakers in North America.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in November 2023.
Woory's pioneering development of electric heaters over the last 10 years has positioned the company as a leader in this growing market segment.
Woory will be hiring personnel from all fields necessary for an automotive parts manufacturing plant.
Dublin Mayor Joshua Kight stated that fostering quality and sustainable growth is a goal of the City of Dublin.
Mayor Joshua Kight noted that Woory's commitment to repurposing an existing manufacturing building promotes infill development and creates new career paths for the community.
Dr. Fred Williams, Chair of the Board of Directors of the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority, welcomed Woory to the Dublin-Laurens County community.
Dr. Fred Williams highlighted Dublin and Laurens County's rapidly growing automotive sector, citing its geographic location on Interstate 16 and access to a skilled workforce.
Dr. Fred Williams stated that partnerships with area education systems and training providers will ensure training programs are in place to fill Woory's talent pipeline for years to come.
Project Manager Jacob Lee represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project is a partnership between Woory, the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that creating jobs and opportunities in rural communities is a priority for matching competitive projects with prepared sites.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson congratulated Dublin-Laurens County on bringing e-mobility jobs to middle Georgia.
For over a century, Georgia has fostered industry practices, partnership, and innovation, positioning the state as a leader in emerging technologies for the automotive and mobility industry.
From July 1 through December 31, 2022, the automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment in Georgia.
Since 2020, Georgia has pursued job creation opportunities along the entire EV supply chain, resulting in over $22.7 billion in investment and more than 28,400 jobs created.
source_title: GA: Woory to Open EV Parts Manufacturing, R&D Facility in Laurens County, Creating 130+ Jobs | Trade and Industry Development

facts: Republican Georgia Governor Brian Kemp announced that Woory Industrial Co., Ltd., an automotive parts manufacturer, would be opening a new manufacturing facility in Dublin, Georgia.
The new facility will create over 130 new jobs.
The company will invest $18 million in Dublin-Laurens County.
Woory Industrial Company specializes in heating, ventilation, and air conditioning components and systems for the automotive industry.
Woory has recently begun concentrating on the development of new products for electric vehicles (EVs) and hydrogen-fueled cars.
Jungwoo Kim serves as the CEO of Woory Industrial Company.
The company's new facility, located at 404 Kellam Road in Dublin, will be its first U.S. manufacturing location.
The facility will produce electrical heaters, control units, and actuators for EV and internal combustion carmakers in North America.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in November 2023.
Unlike internal combustion engine vehicles, electric vehicles do not have a heating source, requiring dedicated heaters for cabin heating and battery temperature maintenance.
Woory's pioneering development of electric heaters over the last 10 years has established it as a leader in this growing market segment.
From July 1 through December 31, 2022, the automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment in Georgia.
Since 2020, Georgia's pursuit of job creation along the entire EV supply chain has resulted in over $22.7 billion in investment and the creation of more than 28,400 jobs.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Woory Industrial is helping Georgia on the path to becoming the e-mobility capital of the nation.
CEO Jungwoo Kim stated that the transition to electric vehicles is the future and that the U.S. EV market is the key market among all automotive markets.
CEO Jungwoo Kim added that Georgia offers a good opportunity for Woory to advance as part of the vanguard of EV development.
source_title: Woory Industrial Company Opening New Facility In Georgia, Creating Over 130 New Jobs And Investing $18 Million | Doc and the Block

facts: Governor Brian Kemp announced that Woory Industrial Co., Ltd., an automotive parts manufacturer, will establish a new manufacturing facility in Dublin, Georgia.
The facility will create more than 130 new jobs and an estimated $18 million in investment in Dublin-Laurens County.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Woory Industrial is the latest job creator helping the state become the e-mobility capital of the nation.

Governor Brian Kemp noted that the emerging industry continues to bring historic levels of new jobs and investment to communities across the state thanks to a highly-skilled workforce and reliable infrastructure network.
Governor Brian Kemp expressed confidence that Woory will have a long and beneficial partnership with Dublin-Laurens County.
Woory Industrial Company, known as Woory, was established in 1989 and specializes in heating, ventilation, and air conditioning components and systems for the global automotive industry.
Woory maintains its traditional product lines while concentrating on developing new products for electric vehicles (EV) and hydrogen-fueled cars.
Jungwoo Kim, CEO of Woory Industrial Company, stated that the automobile industry is facing a revolution for the first time in 100 years and that the transition to EVs is the future.
Jungwoo Kim identified the U.S. EV market as the key market among all automotive markets.
Jungwoo Kim noted that over the past 10 years, Woory has successfully grown into a specialized EV component company through continuous innovation.
Jungwoo Kim stated that Georgia will be a good opportunity for Woory to take another step forward as part of the vanguard of EV development.
Jungwoo Kim expressed confidence that Woory's experience and technology will contribute to community prosperity and appreciated the partnership of the State of Georgia and the City of Dublin.
Woory's new facility is located at 404 Kellam Road in Dublin.
The facility will be Woory's first U.S. manufacturing location.
The facility will produce electrical heaters, control units, and actuators for EV and internal combustion carmakers in North America.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in November 2023.
Electric vehicles lack a heating source, so their heaters are required for cabin heating and maintaining battery temperature.
Woory's pioneering development of electric heaters over the last 10 years has made it a leader in this growing market segment.
Woory will be hiring personnel from all fields necessary for an automotive parts manufacturing plant.
Dublin Mayor Joshua Kight stated that fostering quality and sustainable growth is a goal of the City of Dublin.
Mayor Joshua Kight noted that Woory's commitment to placing an existing manufacturing building back into service helps promote infill development and creates new career paths.
Mayor Joshua Kight welcomed Woory to the Dublin community.
Dr. Fred Williams, Chair of the Board of Directors of the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority, expressed excitement about welcoming Woory to the Dublin-Laurens County community.
Dr. Fred Williams stated that Dublin and Laurens County have a rapidly growing automotive sector due to their geographic location on Interstate 16 and access to a skilled and trained workforce.
Dr. Fred Williams noted that partnerships with area education systems and training providers will ensure training programs are in place to fill Woory's talent pipeline for years to come.
Project Manager Jacob Lee represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was completed in partnership with the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that projects like Woory's first U.S. home in Dublin, Georgia are why the state prioritizes being on the cutting-edge of this transformational manufacturing opportunity to create jobs and opportunities in rural communities.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that Georgia has invested for decades to match competitive projects with prepared communities and sites, and congratulated Dublin-Laurens County on bringing e-mobility jobs to middle Georgia.

For over a century, Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged partnership and innovation, and positioned the state as a leader in developing emerging technologies for the automotive and mobility industry.
From July 1 through December 31, 2022, the automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment in Georgia.
The state has pursued job creation opportunities along the entire EV supply chain, resulting in over $22.7 billion in investment and the creation of more than 28,400 jobs since 2020.
source_title: Woory to Open EV Parts Manufacturing, R&D Facility in Laurens County - Valdosta CEO

facts: An automotive parts manufacturer plans to spend roughly $18 million to establish a new manufacturing facility in Laurens County.
The state is providing the company with comprehensive, customized training services.
South Korean-based Woory Industrial Company specializes in automotive heating, ventilation, and air conditioning components and systems.
The company expects to create more than 130 jobs at its new facility.
The new facility will be located at 404 Kellam Road in Dublin.
This will be the company's first U.S. manufacturing location.

A Georgia Department of Economic Development spokesperson stated that Georgia Quick Start has offered to provide comprehensive, customized training services designed to prepare new employees with the skills required to perform successfully at the facility.
The same spokesperson noted that the company qualifies for these services which have an in-kind, estimated market value of approximately $486,000 at full employment.
Training services will be provided over time as new employees are hired and trained.
In Dublin, the company plans to produce electrical heaters, control units, and actuators for North American electric and internal combustion vehicle manufacturers.
Officials said Woory was established in 1989.
Woory retains traditional product lines while focusing on developing new products for electric vehicles and hydrogen-fueled cars.
The company expects operations to start in November 2023.
Dublin Mayor Joshua Kight stated that fostering quality and sustainable growth is a goal of the City of Dublin.

Mayor Joshua Kight noted that Woory’s commitment to place an existing manufacturing building back into service helps to promote more infill development while creating new career paths for the community.
source_title: Georgia provides 'customized training services' to EV parts manufacturer • The Georgia Virtue

facts: Immersion Corporation signed a multi-year license agreement with Korean-based Woory Industrial Co. Ltd.
The agreement licenses Immersion's haptic technologies to Woory for automotive touchscreens manufactured by Woory and sold to automotive OEMs.
Woory Industrial is one of the leading automotive component suppliers in Korea.

Mr. Martin Heo, SVP at Woory, stated they are partnering with Immersion to add advanced haptic feedback components to their offering to provide customers with a larger breadth of products and capabilities.
Haptic technologies improve ease-of-use and screen navigation in automotive touchscreens, reducing distraction and improving safety.
Mr. Jared Smith, Interim CEO at Immersion, stated the company is pleased to grow its ecosystem with the addition of Woory Industrial haptic-enabled components for the touchscreen.
The adoption of haptics in automotive HMIs will lead to more innovative use cases and advanced implementations that showcase the important role touch plays as an interface in the car.
Immersion provides haptic technology solutions for automotive component manufacturers and tier 1 suppliers.

Immersion’s automotive haptic solutions provide tier-one suppliers with innovative use cases, reference designs for haptic implementations, software, and technologies to facilitate haptics in advanced automotive HMIs.
Haptic technologies increase the usability of touchscreens and provide a more sustainable and flexible design approach than mechanical buttons.
Immersion Corporation is the leading innovator of touch feedback technologies, also known as haptics.
The company invents, accelerates, and scales haptic experiences by providing technology solutions for mobile, automotive, gaming, and consumer electronics.
Haptic technologies create immersive and realistic experiences that enhance digital interactions by engaging users' sense of touch.
Immersion is headquartered in San Francisco, California, with offices worldwide.

links.context: Learn more about Immersion's automotive solution

source_title: Immersion Signs Korean-Based Woory Industrial to a New Multi-Year License for Haptic Technology Use in Automotive Touchscreens | IMMR Stock News

facts: Yachiyo, a Japanese auto parts supplier, will be locating not far from Cedar Village in the Carrollton community.
The company will hold a recruitment fair with the Georgia Department of Labor on April 5 in Waco to help fill jobs for their forthcoming plant.
The new plant will manufacture plastic fuel tanks and sunroofs for automobiles at its 130,000-square-foot facility.
The facility is located on the old Sony Driving Range property near the intersection of Columbia Drive and Beulah Church Road in Carrollton.

Yachiyo is recruiting for environmental health and safety coordinator, technical services manager, accounting assistant manager, purchasing coordinator, technical associates, quality assurance manager, shipping and receiving team leader, assembly team leaders, materials coordinator, final inspectors, and production associates.
Federal Occupational Safety and Health Administration regulations require that applicants be at least 18 years old and have at least a high school diploma or a General Education Diploma (GED).
Applicants are encouraged to bring a resume and will be required to complete a company application without assistance.
Yachiyo announced last September it would locate to the Carrollton community, using the 23.5-acre tract owned by the Carrollton Payroll Development Authority.
The company indicated at the time that it plans to fill about 230 new jobs.

Jim Camp, attorney for the Carrollton Payroll Development Authority, told the Carroll County Board of Commissioners on Sept. 26 that the company will bring some high-level management jobs with them, but the majority of the jobs will be hired locally and will range in scope from manual labor to very specialized jobs.
Yachiyo Industry was established in 1953 in Japan and has built an outstanding reputation as an automotive parts supplier.
The company now employs 6,800 workers worldwide, with five manufacturing operations in North America.
Yachiyo of America Inc. is headquartered in Columbus, Ohio.
The job fair is scheduled for 10 a.m. to 2 p.m. at the West Georgia Technical College Murphy Campus in Waco.

links.context: Georgia Department of Labor's Career Center contact for recruitment event information

source_title: New jobs coming to our Cedar Village, Carrollton community: Yachiyo Hiring for New Plant - Pentagon Homes Mobile Home Communities

facts: Yachiyo Industry Co., Ltd. reported revenue of 161,160 million JPY for FY ended Mar. 31, 2019, a 2.7% decrease from the prior year.
Operating profit fell 92.5% to 701 million JPY due to lower revenue, expenses to stabilize production at its US subsidiary, and recall costs for faulty fuel tanks.
Profit before tax was 928 million JPY, a 89.9% decrease from the prior year.
Profit for the year attributable to owners of the parent was (4,335) million JPY compared to 4,796 million JPY in the prior year.
The company transferred its vehicle assembly business to Honda Motor Co., Ltd. in April 2018 to consolidate managerial resources into parts businesses for sustainable growth.
Yachiyo Industry will strengthen development capabilities at its Zhongshan, Guangdong Province subsidiary to enhance local automaker support, aiming to increase stationed employees by March 2020.
The company's two Chinese subsidiaries in Wuhan and Zhongshan mainly produce fuel tanks and sunroofs, supplying Geely Automobile in addition to Honda Motor Co., Ltd.
US Yachiyo, Inc., a U.S.-based production subsidiary, will invest about JPY 2.8 billion to expand its plant building and install new forming equipment for plastic fuel tanks.
US Yachiyo boosted production with aging equipment, which caused earnings deterioration, before implementing the expansion.
US Yachiyo will start operation of the new forming equipment at the beginning of 2020.
Plastic fuel tanks have become lighter and functionally sophisticated in areas such as fuel transpiration prevention.
The company is considering developing and producing plastic fuel tanks for plug-in hybrid and electrified vehicles.
A flexible production system for high-performance plastic tanks will be established in the U.S.

Yachiyo Industry aims to develop two or three types of new exterior plastic automotive parts and establish mass-production technology by 2025, focusing on weight reduction as vehicle electrification accelerates.
The company is focusing on developing plastic tank production technology for fuel cell vehicles in Japan.
R&D activities include developing metal-honeycomb, high-precision fuel-cell carriers that comply with exhaust-gas regulations worldwide.
R&D activities include work on plastic fuel tanks with new structural designs.
R&D activities include work on new types of sunroofs and environmental technology for automotive parts.
R&D activities include work on special-needs vehicles and driver-assist systems.
R&D facilities include the Tochigi R&D Center (Tochigi Pref., Japan) for fuel tanks and sunroof systems for mass-market vehicles.
The Saitama R&D Center (Saitama Pref., Japan) was established in April 2012 for next-generation product R&D.
Yachiyo of America Inc. is located in Ohio, U.S., handling design and testing of sunroofs and fuel tanks manufactured in North America since its establishment in July 2008.
R&D expenditure was 4,282 million JPY for FY ended Mar. 31, 2019, down slightly from 4,305 million JPY in the prior year.
Overall capital expenditure reached 13,227 million JPY for FY ended Mar. 31, 2019, with the Americas region accounting for 3,580 million JPY.
Planned capital investments allocate 628 million yen to the Kashiwabara Plant in Saitama Pref., Japan from Mar. 2018 to Mar. 2020 to respond to new orders and renew facilities.
Planned capital investments allocate 504 million yen to the Suzuka Plant in Mie Pref., Japan from Mar. 2019 to Mar. 2020 to renew facilities and respond to new orders.
Planned capital investments allocate 461 million yen to the Kameyama Branch in Mie Pref., Japan starting May 2019 and ending Mar. 2020 to increase production capacity and renew facilities.
Headquarters in Saitama Pref., Japan will receive 148 million yen for operational management facilities from Jun. 2019 to Mar. 2020.
The Tochigi R&D Center will receive 145 million yen for R&D facilities from Sep. 2018 to Jan. 2020 to install testing facilities.
The Saitama R&D Center in Sayama, Saitama Pref. will receive 578 million yen for R&D facilities from Oct. 2018 to Mar. 2020 to install testing facilities and reduce energy consumption.
Goshi Giken Co., Ltd. in Kumamoto Pref., Japan will receive 892 million yen for production and R&D facilities from Apr. 2019 to Mar. 2020.
Uemura Tec Co., Ltd. in Kumamoto Pref., Japan will receive 146 million yen for production facilities from Apr. 2019 to Mar. 2020 to reduce energy consumption and streamline operations.
Goshi Tec Co., Ltd. in Kumamoto Pref., Japan will receive 305 million yen for production facilities from Apr. 2019 to Mar. 2020.
Planned capital investments allocate 4,084 million yen to US Yachiyo Inc. in Ohio, USA for production facilities from Mar. 2018 to Jan. 2020.

Yachiyo Manufacturing of America, LLC in Georgia, USA is allocated 1,241 million yen for production facilities from Mar. 2019 to Dec. 2020 to renew facilities, reduce energy consumption, and streamline operations.
Planned capital investments allocate 500 million yen to AY Manufacturing Ltd. in Ohio, USA for production facilities from Apr. 2019 to Mar. 2020.
Planned capital investments allocate 2,182 million yen to Yachiyo Zhongshan Manufacturing Co., Ltd. in Guangdong Prov., China from Apr. 2019 to Feb. 2020.
Planned capital investments allocate 1,493 million yen to Yachiyo Wuhan Mfg. Co., Ltd. in Hubei Prov., China from Apr. 2019 to Mar. 2020.
Planned capital investments allocate 753 million yen to Siam Goshi Manufacturing Co., Ltd. in Rayong Province, Thailand from Apr. 2019 to Mar. 2020.

Planned capital investments allocate 1,249 million yen to Yachiyo India Manufacturing Private Ltd. in Rajasthan, India from Apr. 2019 to Mar. 2020.
Sales forecasts for FY ended Mar. 31, 2020 project automotive parts revenue at 1,580 (in 100 million of JPY), a 2.0% decrease from actual results.
Sunroof sales forecasts for FY ended Mar. 31, 2020 project revenue at 450 (in 100 million of JPY), a 6.8% decrease.
Fuel tank sales forecasts for FY ended Mar. 31, 2020 project revenue at 463 (in 100 million of JPY), a 17.2% increase.

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation and DHL Supply Chain are moving forward with plans that could bring a new $60 million to $90 million distribution facility to Yamaha’s Newnan campus.
The proposed 784,700-square-foot building would be dedicated solely to Yamaha operations and marks a major investment in both logistics and the local community.

If approved, the facility would consolidate freight operations that currently run between Yamaha’s main plant and off-site centers, traffic that contributes to congestion along Highway 34 and surrounding intersections.
By relocating distribution onto the Yamaha campus, the project is expected to reduce truck traffic on local roads, improving both safety and traffic flow without cost to taxpayers.

Bob Brown, president of Yamaha Motor Manufacturing, stated: “This opportunity is about more than logistics. It’s about strengthening our supply chain while also being a good neighbor. We are working carefully through the necessary studies and permitting to ensure this project would benefit both Yamaha and the community for years to come.”
Company officials said the design includes sustainability features such as stormwater management systems, tree buffers and pervious surfaces to limit environmental impact.
Plans also call for roadway improvements, including new turn lanes and upgraded intersections.
Construction could begin as early as 2026, with operations starting in the first quarter of 2028 if the project receives required approvals.

Dave Rumler, president of automotive engineering and manufacturing at DHL Supply Chain North America, stated: “As North America’s leading contract logistics provider, DHL Supply Chain is committed to delivering integrated, end-to-end solutions that drive efficiency and resilience for our partners. We’re proud to collaborate with Yamaha on a project that not only enhances supply chain performance but also supports infrastructure improvements that benefit the Newnan community.”
Yamaha employs more than 2,000 metro Atlanta residents at its Coweta County facility, where it designs and builds golf cars, Side-by-Sides, WaveRunners and ATVs.
source_title: DHL to build new distribution facility on Yamaha campus | News | times-herald.com

entity_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America announced plans to immediately hire over 100 new employees in Newnan, Georgia.
The hiring is intended to assist with assembly line functions to keep pace with growing demand for recreational products assembled at the Newnan facility.
YMMC designs and assembles four key product lines: Golf Cars, All Terrain Vehicles (ATVs), Personal Watercraft, and Recreational Off-road Vehicles (ROVs/Side-by-Sides).
Vice President of Finance and Operations Support Bob Brown stated that demand for outdoor products increased as people sought family and friends activities while maintaining social distance.
The company is seeking eager new talent to immediately join the team to help assemble products for customers.
Brown noted that Yamaha's worldwide reputation stems from the quality, reliability, and durability built into each product, which results from employee dedication and skill.
The company aims to expand its production capacity through the addition of new team members.
Coweta County Development Authority President Trae Westmoreland described YMMC as the largest private employer in Coweta County and an essential community partner for over 30 years.
Westmoreland highlighted that industrial and economic growth go hand in hand and expressed excitement over increases in labor productivity and standard of living during unprecedented times.
Coweta County Development Authority Chairman Ronnie Clotfelter stated that YMMC has been an integral stakeholder for many years, providing a solid foundation for Coweta County.
Open positions include assemblers, material handlers, welders, certified forklift operators, and other roles.
Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation was formed in 1986 and began manufacturing units in 1988 at the Newnan location.
In January 2015, YMMC manufactured its 3 millionth unit.
The company celebrated 30 years of manufacturing at its Newnan location in June 2018.
Products are marketed through Yamaha Motor Corporation, USA (YMUS), a recognized leader in the outdoor recreation industry.

YMUS product offerings include Motorcycles and Scooters, ATV and Side-by-Side vehicles, Snowmobiles, WaveRunner Personal Watercraft, Boats, Outboard Motors, Outdoor Power Equipment, Power Assist Bicycles, Golf Cars, Power Assist Wheelchair Systems, Surface Mount Technology (SMT), Robotic Machines, Unmanned Helicopters, Accessories, Apparel, Yamalube products, and more.
YMUS sells its products through a nationwide network of distributors and dealers in the United States.
YMUS maintains a corporate office in California, two corporate offices in Georgia, facilities in Wisconsin and Alabama, and factory operations in Tennessee and Georgia.

Additional U.S.-based subsidiaries include Yamaha Marine Systems Company (with divisions Bennett Marine in Florida and Kracor Systems in Wisconsin), Skeeter Boats in Texas (with division G3 Boats in Missouri), and Yamaha Precision Propeller in Indiana.

source_title: YAMAHA HIRING OVER 100 NEW EMPLOYEES IN NEWNAN, GEORGIA - Coweta County Development Authority

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Company (YMMC) announced that Robert (Bob) Brown has been appointed President, effective December 30, 2024.
Bob Brown succeeds Takahiro (Taka) Imanishi, who served as President of YMMC since 2020 and returned to Japan for a new position.
Bob Brown began his career at YMMC in May 2019 as Vice President of Finance & Operations Support.
He was later promoted to Vice President of Manufacturing, where he helped develop the strategic plan to optimize Yamaha’s manufacturing footprint in North America.
His leadership has been instrumental in driving YMMC’s success in the ATV, Side-by-Side, Water Vehicle, and Golf Car businesses.
For nearly five years, Bob Brown has been a key figure at YMMC’s largest U.S.-based manufacturing facility in Newnan, Georgia.
He served as Chair of the Coweta Grand Prix, a soapbox derby fundraiser promoting STEM education through hands-on learning and collaboration.
He is an active member of the Newnan-Coweta Chamber of Commerce Board of Directors.
He is an active member of the Coweta Community Foundation Board of Directors.
Bob Brown led YMMC’s Corporate Social Responsibility Committee, identifying engaging and impactful projects for employees to give back to surrounding communities.
Bob Brown holds a degree in Accounting from Eastern Michigan University.
He completed the Global Executive Program facilitated by Hitotsubashi University, School of International Corporate Strategy.
Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Company (YMMC) employs more than 2,000 metro Atlanta residents to design and build recreational vehicles at its Newnan manufacturing facilities in Coweta County, Georgia.
All of the world’s Yamaha golf cars are manufactured at the facility in Coweta County, Georgia.
All of the world’s Yamaha Side-by-Sides are manufactured at the facility in Coweta County, Georgia.
Most of Yamaha's WaveRunners are manufactured at the facility in Coweta County, Georgia.
Most of Yamaha's ATVs are manufactured at the facility in Coweta County, Georgia.
Bob Brown stated he is honored to step into the role of President and looks forward to leading YMMC into a new era of growth and innovation.
YMMC embraces the concept of ‘Kando’ – creating experiences that deeply resonate, inspire joy, and inspire the heart and spirit.
The company aims to cultivate an enduring positive impact and build a stronger, more engaged community by bringing Kando to employees and community partners.
source_title: Bob Brown assumes role of President at Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation – Winters Media

The hiring will assist in many assembly line functions to keep pace with growing demand for recreational products assembled at its Newnan, Ga. facility.

Based in Coweta County, YMMC designs and assembles four key product lines: Golf Cars, All Terrain Vehicles (ATV’s), Personal Watercraft, and Recreational Off-road Vehicles (ROVs) or Side-by-Side vehicles.
Bob Brown, Vice President of Finance and Operations Support, stated that demand for outdoor products increased as people sought family and friends activities while maintaining social distance.
YMMC is seeking eager new talent to immediately join the team and assemble products for customers.

Brown noted that Yamaha's worldwide reputation for quality, reliability, and durability stems from employee dedication and skill, and the company is expanding production capacity through new team members.
Trae Westmoreland, President of the Coweta County Development Authority, described YMMC as the largest private employer in Coweta County and an essential community partner for over 30 years.
Westmoreland expressed excitement about increases in labor productivity and standard of living driven by the company's growth.
Ronnie Clotfelter, Chairman of the Coweta County Development Authority, stated that YMMC has been an integral stakeholder providing a solid foundation for Coweta County for many years.

facts: Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. is moving its U.S. headquarters from California to Kennesaw, Georgia.
The relocation follows roughly 50 years of operation in California.
The decision followed a meeting between Governor Brian P. Kemp and Yamaha executives in October of last year at the Southeast U.S./Japan (SEUS-Japan) annual gathering in Tokyo.
The relocation of the U.S. headquarters will begin this year and is expected to continue through 2028.
Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. manufactures ATVs, boat engines, personal watercraft, other motorized products, motorcycles, marine products, power products, and robotics.
The company currently employs over 2,300 Georgians across its existing operations in the state.
The Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America factory in Newnan is a 1.3-million-square-foot facility that opened in 1986 and currently employs roughly 2,000 Georgians.
Yamaha relocated its marine division to Kennesaw in 1999.
The company expanded its Kennesaw marine facility with a 75,000-square-foot Marine Innovation Center in 2023.
In 2019, Yamaha moved its motorsports operations to Georgia to benefit from close proximity to existing facilities.

Governor Brian P. Kemp welcomed the announcement and stated, “After many years of great partnership, we are honored and proud to welcome Yamaha’s American headquarters to the No. 1 state for business.”

Governor Kemp added that the move is a testament to what Georgia offers job creators and told other California-based companies looking for a better home, “we’ll give you plenty of reasons to keep Georgia on your mind.”
Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. was founded in 1955 and maintains its global headquarters in Iwata, Shizuoka, Japan.
source_title: Yamaha to move headquarters to Georgia

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMMC), based in Newnan, Georgia, broke ground on a new advanced and innovative “SMART” Warehouse.
The SMART Warehouse is the first-of-its-kind for Yamaha Motor Company worldwide.
Using state-of-the-art automated storage and retrieval systems, the new facility will be 200,000 square feet.
The warehouse will reduce response time for fulfilling dealer orders for Yamaha WaveRunners, ATVs, side-by-sides, and golf cars by more than 50 percent.
Mike Chrzanowski, president and CEO of Yamaha Motor Corporation, stated the facility will improve overall operational efficiency and material flow velocity to factory assembly lines.
Demand for all outdoor recreational products produced at the YMMC factory continues to exceed supply.
SMART is an acronym for Sequenced Material and Reduced Transportation.
The medium parts automated storage and retrieval system will retrieve and put away 240 containers per hour, compared to the current rate of 170 containers per hour.
The small parts automated system will handle 2,400 containers per hour, compared to the current capability of 550 containers per day.
New systems will enable Yamaha to reduce forklift usage by 50 percent using automatic conveyors and tuggers to move parts through the SMART center to factories.
The investment underscores Yamaha’s long-term commitment to YMMC employees, the City of Newnan, and Coweta County.
The facility will bring higher paid and more skilled jobs to Coweta County, including roles for robotics experts, software developers, and systems engineers.
The warehouse was designed by SSI SCHAEFER.
The facility will include an observation platform for groups such as students from local schools to learn about manufacturing and automated processes.
Yamaha expects to complete the SMART Warehouse in 2024.
source_title: Yamaha breaks ground on SMART Warehouse in Newnan, Ga. | Powersports Business

facts: Governor Brian Kemp announced that Yamaha Motor Corp., U.S.A. is officially making Georgia its permanent home.
Yamaha Motor Corp., U.S.A. is moving its national headquarters and financial services arm from California to Kennesaw, Cobb County, Georgia.
Governor Brian Kemp stated, “This is a long-awaited homecoming,” noting that Yamaha’s presence in Georgia dates back to 1986.
The company has long manufactured ATVs and watercraft at a facility in Newnan, Georgia.
The corporate headquarters relocation solidifies Georgia’s status as the “No. 1 State for Business.”
The move is expected to bring between 500 and 1,000 new corporate roles to the Kennesaw area.
The new corporate positions will be high-level roles in finance, legal, and executive management rather than assembly jobs.
Corporate leadership will now be located a short drive from Yamaha's massive Newnan factory and its state-of-the-art Marine Innovation Center.
The headquarters relocation positions Yamaha to tap into the talent pool provided by Georgia Tech and Kennesaw State University.
Consolidating its U.S. headquarters in Georgia ensures Yamaha's future growth is anchored in the Southeast, providing long-term tax revenue and community investment.
Yamaha is preparing to break ground on its new expanded headquarters facility.
source_title: Grand Homecoming: Yamaha Consolidates U.S. Headquarters in Georgia - A.T.L. News

facts: Yamaha Motor Corp., USA announced the donation of a UMAX Rally 2+2 utility golf car to the Burbank Police Department’s motorcycle training unit through the Yamaha Outdoor Access Initiative (OAI).
The connection between Yamaha and Burbank PD was made possible by longtime Burbank resident, motorcycle enthusiast, and legendary comedian and TV host Jay Leno.
Rafael Quintero serves as Chief of Police for the City of Burbank.
The Yamaha Outdoor Access Initiative has contributed more than $7 million to more than 535 projects across the U.S. since 2008.
Steve Nessl holds the title of Yamaha Motorsports Marketing Director.
YMUS maintains three corporate offices in Georgia, along with facilities in Wisconsin, Alabama, and Florida.
YMUS subsidiaries include Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC), based in Georgia, and Yamaha Jet Boat Manufacturing USA (YJBM), based in Tennessee.
YMUS owns Skeeter Boats in Texas, which includes the division G3 Boats in Missouri.

YMUS product offerings include Motorcycles and Scooters, ATV and Side-by-Side vehicles, Snowmobiles, Personal Watercraft, Boats, Outboard Motors, Outdoor Power Equipment, Power Assist Bicycles, Golf Cars, Power Assist Wheelchair Systems, Surface Mount Technology and Robotic Machines, Unmanned Helicopters, Accessories, Apparel, Yamalube products, and much more.
YMUS products are sold through a nationwide network of distributors and dealers in the United States.
Yamaha Motor Finance Corporation, USA offers financing solutions to support Yamaha Dealers and loyal Yamaha Customers nationwide and is based out of Cypress, CA and Marietta, GA.

facts: Byung Mo Ahn was promoted to Vice Chairman of Kia Motors Corporation on March 18, 2014.
Under Mr. Ahn's leadership, U.S. sales exceeded 500,000 units in both 2012 and 2013.
Mr. Ahn will continue overseeing Kia Motors America (KMA) and Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia (KMMG).
In his new role, Mr. Ahn serves as group vice chairman and CEO of KMA and KMMG.
Mr. Ahn will lead the implementation of the brand's long-term strategy in the U.S. as the product portfolio, customer base, and units in operation continue to expand.
Mr. Ahn previously served as the group president and CEO of KMA and KMMG since 2008.
During Mr. Ahn's tenure, Kia's U.S. sales have nearly doubled.
The West Point, Georgia-based manufacturing plant began production in 2009.
The West Point, Georgia-based manufacturing plant has produced more than 1.2 million vehicles.
In 2013, the Optima midsize sedan assembled at KMMG was KMA's best-selling vehicle.
The Optima set an all-time, single-nameplate annual sales record for the brand at 155,893 vehicles in 2013.
Sales of the iconic Soul urban passenger vehicle increased for the fourth consecutive year in 2013.
2014 marks Kia's 20th anniversary in the U.S.
Mr. Ahn's promotion furthers the organization's efforts to concentrate on quality, strengthen the brand, elevate the ownership experience, and identify new segments for growth.
KMA launched the Cadenza premium sedan in 2013.
KMA is currently introducing the K900 luxury sedan, signaling the beginning of a new era for the brand in the United States.
Mr. Ahn possesses more than 37 years of experience with the Hyundai Motor Group.
From 1999 through 2001, Mr. Ahn served as president and CEO of KMA.

Mr. Ahn previously served as executive vice president and COO of Hyundai Motor Company, responsible for overseas business operations including supervising the production activities of nine factories worldwide.
Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia, Inc. (KMMG) is the first manufacturing site in North America for Kia Motors Corporation based in Seoul, Korea.
KMMG has an annual capacity of 360,000 units.
KMMG is located on 2,200 acres in West Point, Georgia.
KMMG began mass production on November 16, 2009.
KMMG produces the Sorento CUV and the Optima mid-size sedan, two of the brand's top-selling models in the U.S.
source_title: Byung Mo Ahn promoted to Vice Chairman of Kia Motors Corporation - Kia Georgia

facts: Yamaha Motor Co. is moving its U.S. headquarters from California to Georgia after nearly half a century.
Gov. Brian Kemp announced that the Japanese company would be relocating to Kennesaw in the next few years.
The relocation will begin at the end of 2026 and last until the end of 2028.
The company's previous headquarters was located in Cypress, California, where it had been based since 1979 after acquiring the land in 1978.
Yamaha manufactures ATVs, boat engines, personal watercraft, and other motorized products.
Yamaha previously relocated its marine business to Kennesaw in 1999.
Yamaha previously relocated its motorsports business to Kennesaw in 2019.
Gov. Brian Kemp stated: "After many years of great partnership, we are honored and proud to welcome Yamaha's American headquarters to the No. 1 state for business."

Gov. Brian Kemp added: "This is another loud and clear testament to what we offer job creators from around the world. To any other California-based companies looking for a better home, we'll give you plenty of reasons to keep Georgia on your mind."
Yamaha currently employs over 2,300 people in Georgia.
The majority of Yamaha's Georgia employees work at its factory in Newnan.
The company stated the move is part of a structural reform aimed at improving the profitability of its U.S. operations.
The restructuring responds to cost increases resulting from U.S. tariffs and changes in the market environment.
source_title: Yamaha Motor Corp. moving its U.S. headquarters from California to Georgia - CBS Atlanta

facts: Yamaha Motor Co. is relocating its U.S. headquarters from Cypress, California to Kennesaw, Georgia.
The relocation is cited as necessary to cut costs and streamline operations amid rising tariff pressures.
The move begins this year and runs through 2028.
Yamaha will sell off its California fixed assets outright, including land, offices, and warehouses.

On Feb. 26, Yamaha stated it is "undertaking structural reforms aimed at improving the profitability of its U.S. operations in response to cost increases resulting from U.S. tariffs and changes in the market environment."
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp welcomed the news on March 10, calling Georgia the "No. 1 state for business" and stating it is a testament to what the state offers job creators.

Research by the Public Policy Institute of California shows 789 companies moved their headquarters out of California from 2011 to 2021, roughly 1.9% of all headquarters in the state at the start of that period.
The number of firms moving out of California increased over time while the number moving in declined.
California lost an estimated 77,600 jobs due to those headquarters moves between 2011 and 2021.
In 2025, Chevron, John Paul Mitchell Systems, and Realtor.com announced relocations to Texas.
In-N-Out Burger announced a move to Tennessee, with its CEO citing the difficulty of raising a family and running a business in California.
Recent California defectors include Oracle, Tesla, SpaceX, Charles Schwab, Hewlett Packard Enterprise, and Palantir.
U-Haul ranked California dead last among "growth states" for the fifth consecutive year for 2024.
Atlas Van Lines reported that 60% of its 2024 California shipments were outbound compared to just 40% inbound.
United Van Lines found similar findings with 58% outbound to 42% inbound in 2024.
California faces a projected $50 billion to $70 billion deficit in 2025-2026, a near reversal from the $97 billion surplus recorded in 2021-2022.
California levies the highest state income tax rate in the nation at 13.3% on top earners compared to 5.19% in Georgia.
California's corporate income tax rate of 8.84% is among the steepest in the country.
According to the Hoover Institution, businesses leave California due to taxes, regulations, litigation costs, labor costs, energy and utility costs, and employee cost of living.
California's permitting process is widely regarded as one of the most cumbersome in the U.S., leading to high compliance costs.
Georgia has been recognized as No. 1 for business by Area Development magazine for 12 consecutive years.
Gov. Brian Kemp stated Georgia's approach is to "trust job creators and get government out of the way."
Kemp identified the top three things that kill deals in Georgia as a burdensome tax code, slow permits, and lack of available sites.

Georgia invested heavily in its Rural Site Development Initiative, partnering with local development authorities to grade land, install infrastructure, and create ready-made sites before companies look at them.
Through the Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) program, the state does upfront heavy lifting so businesses are not waiting on infrastructure when ready to build.
Migration data shows a large decline in higher-income adults moving out of California, with 28% fewer leaving in 2024 versus the peak in 2021.
California's population has grown recently due to international immigration offsetting domestic outflows.
California retains advantages including the largest U.S. state economy, world-class research universities, deep venture capital networks, and unmatched tech talent concentration.

Researchers at the Public Policy Institute of California cautioned that headquarter relocations should not be viewed in isolation; far more headquarters launched (17%) or closed (30%) over the same period than relocated.
California had the highest net outflow of domestic companies across the U.S. from 2015 to 2025.
Yamaha's decision is a business decision weighing costs against returns rather than a dramatic act of protest.
source_title: Yamaha relocating HQ from California to Georgia

facts: Yamaha’s manufacturing facility in Newnan, Georgia, was featured in a new episode of the RFD-TV network program “Small Town, Big Deal.”
The episode featuring Newnan and Yamaha first aired on Thursday, June 11, at 8:30 p.m. Central Standard Time.
The show is co-hosted by Rodney Miller, a long-time leader in the agricultural machinery industry, and Jann Carl, former correspondent and anchor for “Entertainment Tonight.”
Co-host Rodney Miller stated that Yamaha has had a tremendously positive impact on Newnan from both an economic and philanthropic standpoint.
Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC) serves as Yamaha’s worldwide hub for off-highway vehicle (OHV) manufacturing.
The Newnan facility produces all of the company's Side-by-Side (SxS) vehicles and the majority of its ATV vehicles for national and international distribution.
The Newnan manufacturing site covers 280 acres with more than 1.3 million square feet of manufacturing facilities.
The facility employs approximately 1,473 workers.
YMMC executive vice president Mike Chrzanowski stated that Yamaha has invested more than $250 million into U.S. manufacturing over the past 15 years to create the Newnan facility.
Products manufactured at the facility include ATV or Side-by-Side vehicles, Golf Cars, and Personal Watercraft.
Yamaha strives to maintain the highest production quality and environmental standards while giving back to the community through corporate social responsibility programs.

facts: In 1994, YKK’s former chairman and CEO Tadahiro Yoshida established the YKK Group Environmental Pledge, which states that “The YKK Group proclaims that it will address and promote ‘harmony with the environment’ as the highest priority of its business activities.”

In 2009, YKK established an Environmental Compliance Committee within its North and Central America region to bring together managers to discuss environmental regulatory requirements and best practices.
The Environmental Compliance Committee is made up of environmental managers from each of YKK’s manufacturing locations in the U.S., Canada, and Latin America.

The committee meets quarterly to continuously look for ways to ensure employees at different sites are aware of applicable environmental regulatory requirements for compliance and environmental protection.
Each company within YKK’s North and Central America region has programs in place at their facilities to help ensure they are following applicable environmental laws.
Many of these regional facility programs include recycling, the elimination of hazardous wastes, and other sustainable manufacturing practices.
The YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. Field Technology Center (FTC) is located in Marietta, Georgia.
The FTC recycles its machines, scrap metal, spare parts, paper, and cardboard.
The FTC continues to replace outdated fluorescent lighting with more energy-efficient lighting.
The FTC eliminates waste solvents and paints from its machinery repainting process by having the work done outside of the facility rather than inside.
The FTC tracks its water and power consumption and separates wastewater from irrigation water.
The FTC continually promotes energy-saving and recycling programs among its employees.
source_title: YKK USA's Field Technology Center takes YKK's environmental pledge to heart - YKK Americas

facts: YKK AP AMERICA INC., a local subsidiary of YKK AP Inc. headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, announced that its new residential vinyl window production facility in Macon, Georgia, began operations on December 28, 2023.
Construction of the Macon Factory started in October 2022 and was completed in December 2023.
The factory is located at 100 YKK AP Way, Macon, Bibb County, Georgia, on a new site approximately 20 minutes by car from the former Macon Factory.
All manufacturing functions previously dispersed across two locations have been consolidated into this integrated production system for vinyl windows.
The facility aims to strengthen production and supply capabilities to increase sales of residential products throughout six southern U.S. states.
Production flow runs from the west side to the east side, following the order of extrusion of vinyl materials, processing of glass and windows, assembly, and shipping.
Automation technology adopted in Japan's APW Line will be implemented to increase productivity by 212% compared to current levels.
The factory includes a new hung window line for producing the upcoming StyleView® Classic series alongside existing vinyl window products.
GTP (Goods To Person) technology, involving shelf-transporting robots, has been added to reduce physically demanding tasks like heavy lifting and picking during production, warehousing, and shipment.
A remote support system, equipment specifications maintainable by local operators, and in-process inspections independent of human operators have been implemented to ensure safety and quality.

The building design incorporates increased natural light and improved roof and wall insulation for energy efficiency, with phased-in energy-generation technologies aimed at achieving carbon neutrality.
The facility's design is scalable to accommodate additional automation, increased capacity, and future business expansion.
An emergency evacuation site has been secured to protect employees from frequent southern U.S. tornadoes.
Main facilities include resin extrusion machines and production lines for laminate processing, insulating glass, and vinyl windows.
Annual production capacity is approximately 450,000 windows per year.
The total site area is 202,343 square meters from a recent acquisition.
The total floor area is about 36,000 square meters.
The building structure is steel construction and reinforced concrete, one story above ground.
Employment at the Macon Factory stands at 250 employees as of December 2023.
Total investment amount for land, building, and production facilities is $111.92 million USD (approximately 15.6 billion yen).
YKK AP AMERICA INC. was founded on April 24, 1991, and is led by President Oliver Stepe.
The company's broader employee count is about 1,200 as of the end of March 2023.

YKK AP AMERICA INC. operates plants in Dublin, GA; Macon, GA; Dallas, TX; Cincinnati, OH; and Lake Shore, Canada, with branches across multiple U.S. cities including Atlanta, Orlando, Baltimore, Greensboro, Boston, Chicago, Houston, and Novi.
The company sells aluminum architectural products for stores, residential/commercial buildings, curtain wall products, and residential vinyl windows.
source_title: YKK AP AMERICA INC. — Operations Begin at New Macon Factory | YKK AP Global Website

facts: YKK AP America Inc. opened a newly expanded commercial and residential design center in Atlanta.
The center, named Studio 101, launched in 2021 with a focus on commercial architectural projects.
The expansion allows YKK AP to feature a wider range of products, including residential doors and windows and a training center.

Officials say the training center is designed to provide customers with hands-on experiences to ensure proper installation of entrances, storefront, curtainwall, window wall, and door and window products.
The center is also designed to be a shared space for the architecture, engineering and construction community.
President and CEO Oliver Stepe stated that the newly expanded commercial and residential design center and training space brings to life a more creative, collaborative way to work.

Oliver Stepe highlighted the company's vision to frame the future by partnering more deeply with customers, partners and industry, and expressed honor at celebrating the space’s official opening with the local Atlanta community.
Studio 101 is located at YKK AP’s headquarters in Atlanta.
source_title: YKK AP America Unveils Newly Expanded Design Center

facts: YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. was honored with a 2021 GEAR Award by the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).
The award ceremony took place on January 14 at YKK's Macon, Georgia plant.
GDEcD named YKK the “Supplier of the Year, employing 200+ people” for its contributions to advancing Georgia’s automotive and mobility industries.
YKK produces a wide range of zippers, hook and loop fasteners, plastic hardware, and snaps and buttons.
These products are used for automobile seat assembly, truck tonneau cover hold-downs, and other applications.
YKK supplies these components to more than 100 automotive customers.
YKK operates a vertically integrated structure that allows the company to create its own materials and machinery.
This vertical integration ensures quality, dependable supply, and minimization of manufacturing costs.
GDEcD Chief Administrative Officer and General Counsel Andrew Capezzuto presented the award.
The 2021 GEAR award for Supplier of the Year recognizes companies that are good corporate stewards.
Macon-Bibb County Mayor Lester Miller stated that YKK is about investing in people and community.
Robert Stamps, Vice President and Business Development Leader – Transportation Fastening Materials at YKK, demonstrated various automotive fastening products.
Keith Kratz, Product Development Engineer at YKK, also demonstrated the products.
The ERGO-J® is a sheet fastener for securing the edges of motor vehicle seat covering material.
The ERGO-J® improves the ergonomic efficacy of seat assembly and reduces the rate of development of carpal tunnel syndrome for automotive workers.
The QUICKFIT® is a concealed hook fastener for automotive seats that enables operators to achieve a craftsmanship-like appearance when molded into seat foam.
The QUICKFIT® reduces seat weight compared to metal wire tie-down systems.
The 5CH CONCEAL® zipper forms a perfect seam when closed.
The 5CH CONCEAL® zipper allows seat manufacturers to significantly decrease the weight of seat back panels to improve fuel efficiency.
YKK Corporation of America President Jim Reed expressed gratitude for the growth of the automotive group.
YKK uses a cyclical process to contribute to the automotive industry, reinvest profits into employees, and remain a foundational pillar of the community.
YKK operates under its "Cycle of Goodness" philosophy and aims to give back to local communities through environmental initiatives.
YKK has pledged to reduce its emissions by 50% by 2030.
YKK aims to achieve net-zero emissions by 2050.
source_title: Macon-Bibb County Government

facts: YKK AP America Inc. is investing up to $125 million to expand its residential door and window offerings with a new manufacturing facility in Macon, Ga.
President Oliver Stepe says the operational goal is to double the company’s capacity while setting a forward course for the decades to come.

Macon has been the headquarters of YKK AP’s residential products business since 2007 and will be home to the approximately 400,000-square-foot residential door and window manufacturing facility set to break ground in October 2022.
Strategic planning for the project has been in the works for two years with final approvals coming down the line only three months ago.
Oliver Stepe says leveraging the desire and interest to do it is that they are trying to scale the business, noting they still have room to grow in the residential window business.
The company's currently-leased facilities were out of room and capacity, making the expansion a decision between continuing to grow or staying static.
Rising interest rates and a potential slowdown in housing starts are not deterrents for YKK AP because the project has been in the works for years.
Oliver Stepe notes that historically, the company's largest investments have occurred at times of financial challenges.
YKK AP acquired 50 acres in Macon on which the new facility will be constructed, leaving plenty of room for future growth.
While the operational goal is to double capacity in the residential market, the acquired acreage will allow for current capacity to be quadrupled in the future.
Oliver Stepe emphasizes that companies focusing on a disciplined approach in the market and with employees are the ones that will grow through economic cycles.
The new facility will utilize advanced sustainable manufacturing and enhanced work environments for employees.
Many of YKK AP's factories already use internally-designed and engineered machinery, which will continue at the new location.
The investment will see the introduction of a $20 million highly automated production line.
Glass sorting and additional automation are in the works for the new facility.
A good portion of existing machinery will be transferred to the new location once completed.
Employee-focused improvements include increased ambient lighting, outdoor space, 100% climate control, bright-colored walls, and multiple outdoor seating areas.
YKK AP will add more than 100 new jobs with the expansion, plus the 250 Macon employees set to transition to the new location.
Oliver Stepe says the company wants people to feel happy at work, focusing on comfortable and pleasing environments.
The new residential development will not take away from YKK AP's commercial offerings, as production, customers, and markets for the two areas remain in separate lanes.
JE Dunn Construction will serve as the construction manager for the project.
SSOE Group will serve as architects and engineers.
Scott Bodden, project director at JE Dunn Construction, says they look forward to collaborating with SSOE Group to support YKK AP’s growth and create jobs.
Scott Bodden notes the facility will be built on innovation, beginning with innovation in project delivery.
The facility is scheduled to be operational by Q1 of 2024.
source_title: YKK AP America Makes $125M Residential Investment

facts: YKK AP America Inc. will invest up to $125 million in an expansion that includes a new manufacturing facility and additional machinery and equipment in Bibb County.
The new facility will create 100 new jobs in addition to the 250 jobs YKK AP currently supports in Macon, Georgia.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the investment expansion on August 16.
YKK AP is a technology-oriented manufacturer of commercial façade systems and residential windows and doors.
YKK AP specializes in architectural products for commercial and residential buildings.
YKK AP entered the U.S. market in 1991 with a vertically integrated, commercial products factory in Dublin, Georgia.
YKK AP's headquarters are located in Atlanta.
YKK AP and its Ontario-based subsidiary Erie Architectural Products employ over 1,200 employees in North America.
Nearly 900 of YKK AP's employees are located within the State of Georgia.
Oliver Stepe is the President of YKK AP America Inc.
The new facility will be located at the Macon-Bibb I-75 Industrial Park.
The site is a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) certified site.
After completion, the company will relocate its operations and employees from its current site on Ocmulgee Boulevard to the new facility.
The Macon facility will become home to the company’s residential division, which manufactures vinyl windows and doors for builders and homeowners.
The facility will serve as a model plant for future North American manufacturing facilities.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in early 2024.
The company is currently hiring for numerous positions.
Governor Kemp stated that being the No. 1 state for business means ensuring companies already operating in Georgia can grow and thrive.
Oliver Stepe stated the expansion will introduce further vertical integration of vinyl extrusion, glass, and window production to manufacture high-quality windows and doors.
The investment builds toward YKK AP's long-term vision to be the leader in the residential window and door market.
Macon-Bibb County Mayor Lester M. Miller commented on the company being a long-running business partner making another investment in the community.
Senior Project Manager Candice Scott represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this project.
The project was completed in partnership with the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority and Georgia Power.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that YKK AP opened its first branch in Atlanta in 1986 and established its U.S. manufacturing presence in middle Georgia five years later.
Companies in advanced manufacturing like YKK AP created the second most new jobs in Georgia last year, according to GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson.
To earn GRAD certification, a property must undergo due diligence and complete specific certification requirements prior to a visit by a prospective business.
Through the GRAD program, Georgia has more than 60 industrial certified sites that are ready for fast-track industrial projects.
source_title: Governor Kemp: Building Materials Manufacturer YKK AP America Expands Residential Window Production Capabilities with New Facility in Bibb County

facts: YKK AP America Inc. installed a new goods-to-person (GTP) automation system at its residential window plant in Macon, Georgia.
The GTP system was installed at the 400,000-square-foot purpose-built facility’s shipping department to further reduce the manual movement of heavy windows.
Dennis Howard serves as senior manager of transportation and logistics, and Daymon Loyd serves as director of residential manufacturing for YKK AP.
The GTP system improves operations and quality and reduces time to load by 39%.
The system uses autonomous robots to move finished products from the end of the line to material storage units and into a fenced GTP field that houses final products.
YKK AP is making strategic investments in automation to reduce risk and improve safety across its operations.
For residential manufacturing, YKK AP's goal was to reduce material handling to improve quality, starting with the shipping department where most material handling occurs.
When the Macon facility opened in early 2024, YKK AP aimed for zero injuries, zero backorders, and 100% on-time delivery.
The facility currently achieves a 97% on-time delivery rate.
YKK AP invested in the GTP system to close the gap from 97% to 100% on-time delivery.
The facility handles 80% of its products with the GTP system, while the remaining 20% are too large for the material storage units.
Integrating the new autonomous system with YKK AP’s IT infrastructure was identified as the most significant deployment challenge.
Training for the system interface required an aggressive schedule focused on troubleshooting technical issues as they arose.
The Macon facility's residential manufacturing team made its first deployment of GTP technology, requiring flexibility to adjust original plans to accommodate the technology correctly.
Much of the capital equipment was staged on the manufacturing floor before activation, requiring time and trial-and-error for deployment.
source_title: Company News September 2025

facts: ZF Gainesville, LLC (also referenced as ZF Active Safety US Inc.) is located in Gainesville, GA, US.
The facility has multiple job openings for Engineer III - Plant Quality positions.
The Plant Quality Engineer III role is a full-time position closing on Thursday, July 2, 2026.
Job duties include managing customer zero-mile and warranty issues in customer portals.
Responsibilities involve facilitating Corrective Action Teams and performing internal quality investigations.
The role requires managing 8D investigations or similar corrective action processes derived from product and process audits.
Duties include maintaining internal indicators, process audits, and records for corrective actions.
The position involves preparing zero miles data and reporting status via electronic graphs.
The engineer serves as a customer contact for quality topics, including completion, submission, and approval of customer PPAPs.
Responsibilities include coordinating new project introductions, product/process launch activities, and ensuring adherence to company procedures and TS16949 requirements.
The role requires coordinating regular project team meetings and tracking progress against timing plans.
Duties encompass providing technical quality support to the project core team and creating quality documentation such as APQP, FMEA, PPAP, and MSA.
The position involves coordinating customer onsite activities including audits and run at rate.
Responsibilities include participating in product design reviews and supporting prototype builds.
The engineer performs large and small production assessments and conducts Cpk, Cmk, and SPC studies per AIAG Standards.
Duties include performing MSA and Gage R&R studies per AIAG Standards and managing the IMDS website.
Required qualifications include a Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Automotive Engineering, or a closely related field.
Candidates must have 3 years of experience in a manufacturing or quality setting.
Demonstrated experience is required with AIAG and IATF standards, including IATF 16949.
Experience with rapid problem-solving processes like 8Ds and APQP documents (PFMEA, Control Plans, Process Flow Diagrams) is required.
Knowledge of process control methods including SPC, standardized work, run-at-rate analysis, and process capability is necessary.
Required experience includes document revision control, MSA, Gage R&R, PPAP, external/internal process audits, and automotive component production.

facts: Federal energy officials announced a record $26.5 billion loan to electric utilities in Georgia and Alabama.
A total of $22.4 billion will go to Georgia Power and $4.1 billion to Alabama Power.
Both Georgia Power and Alabama Power are subsidiaries of Atlanta-based Southern Company, one of the nation's largest utilities.
The companies plan to use the loan cash to build new natural-gas fueled power plants, build new transmission lines, and upgrade existing power plants.
The utility expansion is driven by demand from computer data centers.
Energy Secretary Chris Wright said the loan will result in more than $7 billion in savings over decades from a lower, federally subsidized interest rate.

Chris Wright stated that federal officials are focused on driving down costs and the loan would help ensure Southern customers have access to affordable, reliable and secure energy for decades to come.

Wright and President Donald Trump have frequently advocated for fossil fuel-friendly policies, including orders over the past nine months to keep some coal-fired plants open past planned retirement dates, citing grid reliability.
Wright claims these orders have saved utility customers millions of dollars and helped keep lights on during last month’s winter storm.
Critics argue the orders are unnecessary and have raised electric bills as utilities operate older, more expensive plants.
Chris Womack, Southern’s chairman, president and CEO, said the loans will help lower the cost of grid investments that enhance reliability and resilience for customers.
The loan announcement comes amid scrutiny over rising utility bills, with electricity prices increasing faster than inflation in many states.
There is widespread opposition to new artificial intelligence data centers.
President Trump announced a “ratepayer protection pledge” against higher utility bills tied to AI during his State of the Union address on Tuesday.
Trump claimed tech companies will provide their own power for data centers and that prices will go down, but did not provide details.
It remains unclear if any tech companies have signed pledges to build their own power plants.
Wright stated on a Wednesday reporter call that every major company developing a data center has been in dialogue with federal officials.
Wright cited cooperation from Microsoft, Google, and Meta regarding data center power but did not specify written agreements.

Federal officials have previously provided utility loans, including $12 billion guaranteed by the first Trump administration and President Barack Obama’s administration for two nuclear reactors at Georgia’s Plant Vogtle, partially owned by Georgia Power.
Trump’s tax and budget bill last year reshaped the loan program to focus on increasing electricity generation and transmission capacity.
Loan guarantees under President Joe Biden previously focused on green energy goals.
Gregory Beard, director of the newly renamed Office of Energy Dominance Financing, said cutting interest rates and discarding Biden's policy will restore affordability.
The loan office will review individual projects for financial viability before approving them.
Beard stated officials will not build plants or deploy capital until sure it is right for the local community and ratepayer.
Requirements regarding project viability do not appear to be laid out in loan agreements released by Southern on Wednesday.

Jennifer Whitfield, an attorney for the Southern Environmental Law Center who represented Georgia Power expansion opponents, questioned the wisdom of the loans despite acknowledging they will save money for Georgians.
Whitfield stated that from a taxpayer perspective, the loan is a bailout paid for by every taxpaying citizen of the United States.
Any customer savings from the loan must be approved by the elected Public Service Commissions in Alabama and Georgia.
Commissioners last July approved a three-year rate freeze requested by Georgia Power, while Alabama commissioners approved a two-year rate freeze in December.
Company officials highlight these rate freezes amid nationwide utility requests for record increases, but opponents complain regulators locked in high prices and profits.
Voters removed two Republican incumbents from the Georgia commission in November due to complaints about rising bills.
Commissioner Peter Hubbard, one of two new Democrats, unsuccessfully tried to roll back approval for Georgia Power's expansion in recent weeks.
Hubbard stated that declining costs of solar, wind, and battery power could make new natural gas plants uneconomic over time.
Hubbard criticized the federal loan as locking the state into a costlier option that is not meeting the moment of affordability.
source_title: Feds Announce $26.5 Billion Loan for Electric Power Expansion in Georgia and Alabama
