facts: AWS announced an $11 billion investment to develop data centers in Georgia.
The initiative aims to support growing demand for artificial intelligence and cloud computing technologies while boosting the local economy and advancing sustainability goals.
The plan involves construction of state-of-the-art data centers in Butts and Douglas counties.
These facilities will leverage high-performance GPUs to handle complex workloads such as machine learning and generative AI models.
The project will create 550 high-paying technical jobs directly, including roles for data center engineers, network specialists, and operations managers.
Thousands of indirect jobs will be generated in construction and supply chains during the development phase.
Community leaders in Georgia praised the investment as a transformative development.
The Butts County Board of Commissioners and local entities collaborated for more than a year on this initiative.
The investment is described as the most significant in Butts County history.
AWS plans to power the new data centers with renewable energy, aligning with its global carbon-neutral objectives.
Solar farms will support the data centers, including the Bulldog Solar farm.
The solar farms will collectively generate enough clean energy to power approximately 123,000 U.S. homes annually.
A report from the Electric Power Research Institute predicts data centers could account for up to 9% of U.S. electricity consumption by 2030.
AWS is mitigating energy challenges by securing energy agreements with utilities across the country.
AWS is launching STEM education programs and technical training workshops to equip local communities with in-demand tech industry skills.
The Girls’ Tech Day initiative aims to inspire young women to explore careers in STEM fields and address the tech industry’s gender gap.
Fiber Optic Fusion Splicing Workshops are being offered to equip individuals with specialized skills for broadband infrastructure development.
In partnership with Augusta Technical College, AWS provided certification to dozens of participants in 2022, connecting them with employers in the broadband sector.
Think Big Spaces provides hands-on educational programs designed to give students practical exposure to STEM technologies and foster innovation.
Microsoft recently pledged $80 billion for data centers in fiscal 2025 to support AI advancements, reflecting escalating competition in the tech industry.
AWS leadership stated the multibillion-dollar investment is expected to create more than 550 well-paying jobs and drive significant economic growth for the state.
Since 2010, Amazon has invested $18.5 billion in Georgia.
Amazon's historical investments have contributed $20.1 billion to Georgia’s gross domestic product.
Amazon's historical investments have supported more than 34,000 direct jobs in the state.
The $11 billion commitment underscores Georgia’s thriving business ecosystem and positions it as a rising technology hub in the United States.
source_title: AWS Announces $11B Investment in Georgia for AI Infrastructure Growth! - All About AI

facts: Adient Automotive Seating Sdn Bhd is a subsidiary of Adient PLC (NYSE: ADNT), a global leader in automotive seating.
Its latest foam plant is located in Alor Gajah, Melaka, Malaysia.
The new RM10 million foam plant has started exporting headrests to Thailand and Indonesia.
The plant is equipped with state-of-the-art foam manufacturing technology to serve both the local market and for export.
Adient has been consistently investing in its Malaysian manufacturing operations since 1983 to improve and expand capability and capacity.
The new Alor Gajah foam plant elevates Adient's operations to become an automotive export hub for the Southeast Asia region.
Through this initiative, Adient aims to drive parts localisation, develop local suppliers, and create more job opportunities and export revenues for Malaysians and Malaysia.
AJ Dabydin serves as the Managing Director of Adient.
The plant's environmental strategy includes a rainwater harvesting system to reduce dependency on town water.
Solar panels were installed at the plant to produce electricity that powers various pieces of equipment.
The factory uses five-star power rated and low carbon emission equipment.
LED lighting is installed across the entire plant.
Environmental risk is managed through environmental management systems and certifications such as ISO14001.
The plant implements Hydra Manufacturing System, which is a first in Malaysia.
Hydra Manufacturing System collects vast amounts of production data from various equipment in real time onsite and remotely.
Visual analytics are used to improve right first time, reduce product defects and rework, and quickly identify root causes of potential issues from heat maps.
Adient focused on re-skilling and up-skilling operators for effective man-machine interaction due to the automation approach.
The plant layout allows for one-meter physical distance between workers and features carefully planned logistic routes to keep them safe.
The Adient Alor Gajah plant won the Automotive Services category at SBR’s Malaysia International Business Awards 2021.

Adient thanked the Ministry of International Trade and Investment (MITI), Malaysian Industrial Development Authority (MIDA), local authorities, customers, business partners, and its team for their support.
Adient plans to host an official opening ceremony of the new plant when circumstances permit.
Adient operates approximately 77,000 employees in 32 countries.
Adient operates 202 manufacturing/assembly plants worldwide.
Adient produces and delivers automotive seating for all major OEMs.
Adient's expertise spans from research and design to engineering and manufacturing of complete seating systems and individual components.
Adient's products are integrated into more than 19 million vehicles every year.
Adient Automotive Seating Sdn. Bhd. was incorporated on 7 September 1983.
Adient Malaysia currently operates three manufacturing plants, one engineering/prototype/testing lab, and one sequencing centre in the country.
Adient Malaysia has about 500 employees in the country.
Adient Malaysia's facilities are IATF 16949:2016, ISO 14001:2015, and ISO 45001:2018 certified.
source_title: ADIENT MALAYSIA AIMS TO BE SOUTHEAST ASIA AUTOMOTIVE SEATING FOAM HUB - drivingMotion

facts: Adient is a global leader in automotive seating solutions.
Adient has completed a significant expansion of its China Technical Center in Chongqing.

The expansion milestone was marked by a ceremony led by Adient President and CEO Jerome Dorlack, APAC Executive Vice President James Huang, and key leadership from the APAC and China teams along with staff at the China Technical Center.
The expansion is a major step in Adient’s long-term growth strategy in China.
The upgrade reinforces the company’s dedication to product innovation.
The upgraded center now boasts advanced facilities, such as a sled test lab and MAST test lab.
These facilities will enhance the company’s capabilities in engineering design, testing, validation, and product development.
The newly expanded center offers a collaborative, flexible, and digitally advanced environment for the Adient China engineering team.
This environment enables the team to create innovative automotive seating solutions that meet both local preferences and global automotive trends.
Jerome Dorlack stated that China is the largest automotive market in the world and a key growth driver for Adient.
The expansion highlights Adient’s commitment to blending global leading technologies with local market needs.
This strategy ensures the company continues to deliver outstanding products and services to both local and international customers while advancing innovations in electrification and smart features.
Dorlack expressed confidence in the future of China’s automotive sector, calling the expansion a clear demonstration of ongoing investment in the market.
Joe Wang, Vice President of China Seating, stated that the expansion is a significant milestone for their business as they continually strive to provide superior products and services to customers.

Mark Sutton, Vice President of APAC Engineering, commented that the upgraded facility will enhance their ability to develop cutting-edge products and strengthen their competitive position, supporting Adient’s sustained growth in China.
The enhanced technical center will collaborate closely with Adient’s global tech centers to lead innovation in the automotive seating industry.
Adient’s strategy focuses on driving business growth through technological advancements by working with OEM customers and partners.
Key exploration areas for these partnerships include comfort, connectivity, safety, reconfiguration, and sustainability.
These efforts aim to shape the future of mobility and propel the global automotive seating industry forward.
Adient has a workforce of around 70,000 employees across 29 countries.
The company operates more than 200 manufacturing and assembly plants worldwide.
Adient delivers automotive seating solutions for major OEMs and millions of vehicles annually.
The company’s offerings cover everything from complete seating systems to individual components.
Adient manages every stage of the automotive seat-making process.
source_title: Adient Expands China Technical Center to Fuel Innovation in Automotive Seating | Machine Maker - Latest Manufacturing News | Indian Manufacturing News - Latest Manufacturing News | Indian Manufacturing News - Machine Maker

facts: Adient plc (ADNT) began trading Oct. 31, 2016, when Johnson Controls spun off its automotive experience segment into this new company.
The company is the leading seating supplier to the industry with about one third of the global market.
It benefits from a diverse customer base and a strong international presence.
ADNT has been seeing strong revenue, driven by recovering industry volume and a healthy backlog.
It has also seen new business wins from auto companies such as Honda (HMC), Toyota (TM), and Nissan (NSANY), which should help drive revenue growth.
ADNT has a current ratio of 1.1 which indicates it has more than enough liquidity to handle short-term obligations.
The company has also shown strong growth as earnings have grown an average of 107.9% per year over the past three years.
Analysts forecast earnings to rise 5,625% this year, leading to a Growth Grade of B in our POWR Ratings system.
The stock also looks undervalued with a forward P/E of 7.92.
ADNT has formed key support at around $35 (green) over the past several weeks.
ADNT shares were trading at $41.61 per share on Monday morning, up $0.89 (+2.19%).
Year-to-date, ADNT has gained 19.67%, versus a 19.54% rise in the benchmark S&P 500 index during the same period.

facts: Adient PLC announced on July 15, 2025, the launch of its new mechanical massage seat solution.
The technology was introduced in GAC-Trumpchi's new PHEV model M8.
The system features an industry-first massage design intended to enhance seating comfort and provide superior relief from occupant fatigue.
The solution offers a 3D massage module that simulates professional kneading techniques, providing stronger effects than traditional pneumatic systems.
The system integrates both western fascia therapy and traditional Chinese medical philosophy for targeted back and lumbar relief.
Smart controls feature multiple modes and strengths, allowing for personalized massage experiences.
The compact design is compatible with heating and ventilation systems and supports over-the-air updates for continuous improvement.
Mass production is currently underway.
Plans are in place to expand the solution into mid- to high-end models in China.
The seating solution is gaining interest in the Americas and Europe markets.
The press release does not specify cost implications for automakers or end consumers.
There is no detailed timeline provided for when the technology will be available in markets outside China.
source_title: Adient PLC Unveils Innovative Mechanical Massage Seating Solutio

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp and the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) announced that through the third quarter of fiscal year 2022 (July 1, 2021 – March 31, 2022), job creation and investments resulting from economic development projects have already surpassed fiscal year 2021 year-end totals.
The 251 project locations supported by GDEcD’s Global Commerce team will result in the creation of more than 35,400 new jobs and $12.9 billion in investment for the state.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Georgia continues to be the preferred place to do business and expressed gratitude to job creators choosing to locate or expand operations in the Peach State.
The reported totals account for a 48% increase in jobs compared to three-quarters through the previous fiscal year and a 6% increase in jobs compared to the prior year’s overall total.
There was a recorded increase of 54% in total investment compared to the same time period last year.
Nearly 78% of both projects and investments were located outside the 10-county metro Atlanta area, and over half of the jobs created were also outside the metro Atlanta area.
Key industry sectors supported include automotive, advanced manufacturing, logistics and distribution, software and technology, and food processing.
Over the past year, Georgia’s electric vehicle (EV) and sustainability ecosystems have grown tremendously.
Georgia is continuing to recruit the entire EV supply chain to ensure an effective, efficient, and affordable environment for EV production.
Rivian has ongoing plans to develop a $5 billion campus at Stanton Springs North.
Rivian also plans an electric vehicle service center in Atlanta that will provide training on EVs.

SK Battery America has partnered with American lithium-ion battery recycling and engineered materials startup Ascend Elements to recycle both cell and module lithium-ion battery manufacturing scrap from SK’s Jackson County facility.
Ascend Elements announced its first commercial-scale battery recycling plant in Covington in January.

Aurubis, one of the leading recyclers of copper, precious metals, and non-ferrous materials worldwide, will invest $340 million in a ‘first-of-its-kind in the U.S.’ state-of-the-art recycling and secondary smelting facility for multi-metal recycling in Augusta.
Aspen Aerogels recently announced plans to locate their new 500,000-square-foot advanced manufacturing facility in Statesboro.
The Aspen Aerogels facility will produce PyroThin materials that provide thermal management, mechanical stability, and fire protection properties to EV battery systems.

Georgia Quick Start, the No. 1-ranking workforce training program, helped secure numerous projects and has provided specialized training tailored specifically to business needs for more than 40 years.
The Georgia Quick Start program is administered through the Technical College System of Georgia.
The Georgia Ports Authority’s deepwater and regional ports projects have played a key role in securing diverse projects across the state.
For the past 19 consecutive months, the Georgia Ports Authority has been reporting record-breaking numbers as it expedites plans for long-term growth.

The Savannah Harbor Expansion Project (SHEP) was recently completed, deepening a 40-mile stretch between the port and the Atlantic Ocean to accommodate super-sized freighters traveling from the expanded Panama Canal.
Inland ports are attracting investments, such as the Appalachian Regional Port driving Roper Corporation’s decision to invest $118 million in expanding its facilities in Walker County.
Other assets driving Georgia’s growth include Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport and the state’s extensive rail network.

The Georgia Department of Transportation maintains more than 1,200 miles of highway as part of Georgia’s highway and interstate system, which connects companies to 80% of the country in two days or less of driving time.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that a drive to create hope and opportunities for Georgia communities is at the heart of the Department’s mission, targeting new strategic industries for growth and supporting existing businesses’ expansions.
source_title: New Georgia Economic Development Records Set for Jobs, Investment in Nine Months - AllOnGeorgia

The director will be responsible for implementing and expanding a statewide technology and innovation-based economic development program.
The program encompasses a full range of sales, marketing, business recruitment, business development, community development, and public relations activities.
A bachelor's degree in the life sciences, engineering, or a closely related field is required for the position.
A master's degree in these fields is preferred.

facts: Archer Aviation is an eVTOL startup company led by CEO Adam Goldstein.
United Airlines and the U.S. Military are key partners of Archer Aviation, starting with deliveries to the USAF.
Grizzly Research published a bear report titled "We Believe Archer Aviation, Inc is a Lame Duck" on August 15, 2023.
Grizzly claimed Archer's DoD contract was noncompetitive with only $1.3 million in potential revenue for 2023.
Grizzly accused Archer of misrepresenting flight activity and recycling old video footage to exaggerate progress.
Grizzly criticized Archer's partnerships with United Airlines and Stellantis as being secured by giving out millions of shares and warrants for little compensation.
Grizzly claimed FAA experts estimated Archer would not receive Type Certification until at least 2028.
Grizzly claimed Archer settled a legal dispute with Wisk/Boeing by giving $100 million in free shares to Boeing.
Grizzly claimed Archer's San Jose lab and small-scale production facility was incomplete as of July 2023.
Archer delivered its first Midnight eVTOL aircraft to the U.S. Air Force on August 15, 2024, under a $142 million contract via the AFWERX Agility Prime program.
The U.S. Air Force plans to use the Midnight aircraft for personnel transport, logistics, and rescue operations.
The DoD contract includes pilot training, maintenance, and certification support.
Archer continued manned test flights of the Midnight eVTOL aircraft post-August 2023.
United Airlines made a $10 million pre-delivery payment for 100 of Archer’s eVTOLs.
Stellantis committed $150 million in equity in January 2023 and became Archer's exclusive manufacturing partner for the Midnight eVTOL.
Stellantis led a $215 million funding round for Archer as of August 2023.
In July 2024, N.V. Stellantis bought 17 million shares of Archer for $54 million.
The FAA issued final airworthiness criteria for the Midnight aircraft on May 23, 2024.
Archer received a Part 145 Maintenance & Repair Certificate and a Part 135 Air Carrier Certificate from the FAA.
As of September 2024, Archer successfully completed 402 test flights ahead of schedule.
For-credit flight testing was expected to begin in early 2024.
The settlement with Wisk/Boeing evolved into a collaboration agreement making Wisk Archer's exclusive provider of autonomous flight technology.
Boeing made a significant investment in Archer during the $215 million funding round alongside United Airlines and ARK Invest.
Archer completed installation of its high-volume battery pack manufacturing line at the San Jose Integrated Test Lab in May 2024, with capacity for up to 15,000 battery packs per year.
Archer's first conforming Midnight aircraft was expected to be completed in Q4 2023.
Archer began construction on a high-volume manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia in March 2023.
The Covington, Georgia facility is projected to support production of up to 650 eVTOL aircraft per year.
The Covington facility is on track for completion in 2024.
The Covington facility is nearly 400,000 square feet.
Construction of the Covington facility was expected to be completed by the end of 2024 as of July 2024.
The Midnight eVTOL's planned entry into service is in 2025.
Stellantis increased its investment in Archer by $55 million following successful flight milestones.
Short interest in Archer Aviation stock dropped from nearly 30% to 22.28%.
Benchmark reaffirmed a "buy" rating with a $12.00 price objective on August 9th.
Cantor Fitzgerald reaffirmed an "overweight" rating with a $10.00 price objective.
JPMorgan Chase & Co. increased its price objective from $5.00 to $6.00 and gave an "overweight" rating on July 24th.
HC Wainwright started coverage with a "buy" rating and $12.50 target price on September 3rd.
Archer Aviation has a consensus rating of "Moderate Buy" and a consensus target price of $9.00 according to MarketBeat.com.

Christopher Coes, acting under secretary of transportation for policy at the U.S. Department of Transportation, announced at Honeywell’s Advanced Air Mobility (AAM) Summit on Sept. 19 that the final special federal aviation regulation (SFAR) for powered-lift pilot certification and operations is expected to be published in the "next month."
The FAA technically has until Dec. 16 to finalize the SFAR regulation.
First movers like Joby Aviation and Archer Aviation are aiming for a 2025 commercialization of eVTOLs.
source_title: Archer Aviation (ACHR) Is A Table Pounding Buy As The Grizzly Research Report Caused Over ~27% Short - Oak Park Journal

facts: Industry-first high-volume facility for manufacturing eVTOL aircraft strategically located adjacent to the Covington Municipal Airport.
Archer and Stellantis are set to join forces to begin manufacturing Archer’s Midnight eVTOL aircraft in 2024.
On February 28th, Archer closed on the local incentive transaction for the project that, along with a Georgia REBA grant, totals ~33% of the planned capital investment.
On March 2nd, Archer began construction on Phase 1 of the project -- build out of a ~350,000 square-foot facility capable of producing up to 650 aircraft per year.
The planned approximately 350,000 square-foot facility is strategically located adjacent to the Covington Municipal Airport and will be capable of producing up to 650 aircraft per year.
This type of high-volume manufacturing facility is believed to be a first for the eVTOL industry.
The Covington facility will be capable of being expanded by an additional 800,000 square feet, which is estimated to support long-term production targets of up to 2,300 aircraft per year.

In January, Archer announced an expanded relationship with Stellantis, one of the world’s leading automakers and owner of brands like Jeep®, Ram and Maserati, under which the two companies will join forces to manufacture the company’s production aircraft, Midnight.

Archer brings its world-class team of eVTOL, electric powertrain and certification experts while Stellantis will contribute advanced manufacturing technology and expertise, experienced personnel and capital to the partnership.

This combination is intended to enable the rapid scaling of aircraft production to meet Archer’s commercialization plans, while allowing Archer to strengthen its path to commercialization by helping it avoid hundreds of millions of dollars of spending during the manufacturing ramp up phase.
The goal is for Stellantis to mass produce Archer’s eVTOL aircraft as its exclusive contract manufacturer.

Adam Goldstein, Archer’s Founder and CEO, stated: 'We are thrilled to be taking this next important step as we work closely with Stellantis to ramp up our high-volume manufacturing capabilities at this new facility in Covington, GA.'

Adam Goldstein also stated: 'The key to unlocking the promise of what the eVTOL aircraft industry can bring is scale, and we are thrilled by the support the State of Georgia and Newton County are providing to this first of its kind facility in such an exciting new industry.'
Archer plans to hold a ceremonial event in April in Covington, GA to mark the milestone.
Archer is designing and developing electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft for use in urban air mobility networks.
Archer's team is based in Santa Clara, CA.
source_title: Archer

facts: Archer Aviation is an aerospace company with a market cap of $4.75 billion developing electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft to transform urban air mobility.
The company ended 2024 with its strongest ever cash position and significant progress in manufacturing, certification, and commercial readiness.
Cathie Wood's flagship ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) accounts for 2.7% of Archer Aviation stock.
Archer is set to report its first-quarter fiscal 2025 earnings on May 12.
Archer stock is down 9.4% year to date, while the broader market has fallen 3.1%.
Wall Street forecasts the stock to soar around 50% from current levels.

Archer’s flagship model is the Midnight, designed for dense urban environments using a combination of electric propulsion and advanced AI-powered flight software specifically for short-distance flights.
The company envisions air taxis alleviating urban congestion while lowering carbon emissions.
As of now, Archer has not generated revenue from commercial operations.
Adjusted EBITDA losses totaled $368.9 million in 2024.
With the launch of Midnight, Archer is actively preparing for revenue-generating operations in late 2025.
The company remains committed to obtaining certification from the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA).
Archer is pursuing a dual-path commercialization strategy by deploying Midnight aircraft outside the U.S., primarily in countries that do not require FAA type certification such as the UAE.
This international track is supported by a new partnership with Abu Dhabi Aviation.
Partnerships with Etihad for pilot training and Falcon Aviation for infrastructure development help solidify the foundation for scalable operations in the UAE and beyond.
Domestically, Archer is collaborating with United Airlines (UAL) and Southwest Airlines (LUV) to establish urban air mobility services.
Archer has collaborated with Palantir Technologies (PLTR) to leverage its Foundry and Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP) to accelerate the scaling of its aircraft manufacturing capabilities.
Archer expanded into defense via a partnership with Anduril to collaboratively develop a next-generation hybrid propulsion vertical lift aircraft for both military and civilian applications.
The Midnight is fully assembled and currently undergoing final ground tests at Archer’s Salinas, California test facility.
Archer's new Georgia manufacturing facility is now complete and will produce up to 10 Midnight aircraft this year.
The company expects an adjusted EBITDA loss of $95 million to $110 million in the first quarter of 2025 due to ongoing investment in engineering, production, and commercialization.
Archer ended 2024 with $835 million in cash and cash equivalents, resulting in a liquidity position of more than $1 billion.
This liquidity was largely enabled by successful equity raises in 2024 that raised $820.4 million.

Management stated that the company’s liquidity does not yet include Stellantis’ potential $400 million capital commitment to support scaling up production of the Midnight aircraft, which Archer expects to finalize in the first quarter.
Analysts predict revenue will be around $28.1 million in 2025 before rising by 404.9% to $142 million in 2026.

Canaccord Genuity analyst Austin Moeller reiterated a “Buy” rating, citing strategic momentum, innovative edge, the Midnight unveiling, plans for a New York Air Taxi Network, collaborations with aviation industry leaders, and the aircraft’s low noise profile.
Overall, Archer Aviation stock is rated a “Moderate Buy” on Wall Street, with five analysts rating it “Strong Buy,” two saying “Moderate Buy,” and two rating it “Hold.”
The average target price of $11.39 is 32% above current levels.
The street-high estimate of $13.5 suggests the stock can rally around 56% over the next 12 months.
Archer appears well-positioned to lead urban air mobility as it transitions from development to commercial operations in the second half of 2025.
source_title: Should You Buy Archer Aviation Stock Before May 12?

facts: Stellantis invested an additional $55 million into Archer following the achievement of its transition flight test milestone last month.
This latest investment builds on Stellantis completing a series of open market purchases of 8.3 million shares of Archer’s stock in March of this year.
During 2023, Stellantis invested $110 million into Archer through a combination of open market stock purchases and investments under the companies’ strategic funding agreement.
Construction nears completion on Archer’s high-volume manufacturing facility in Covington, GA.
The facility is on track to be completed later this year.
The first phase of the build out is a ~350,000 square foot facility on an ~100 acre site.
The facility is designed to support production of up to 650 aircraft annually.
This capacity would make it one of the largest manufacturing facilities by volume in the aircraft industry.
Archer’s goal with this facility is to establish a factory that can support its planned commercial ramp by leveraging Stellantis as its contract manufacturer.
Stellantis CEO Carlos Tavares applauded the innovation, expertise and hard work of the engineering and manufacturing teams from Stellantis and Archer.
Archer CEO Adam Goldstein stated that Stellantis’ commitment has been unrivaled, citing its foresight to provide manufacturing expertise and capital needed to accelerate Archer’s business objectives.
Stellantis has been a strategic partner to Archer since 2020 through various collaboration initiatives.
Stellantis has been an investor in Archer since 2021.
Archer has leveraged Stellantis’ deep manufacturing, supply chain, and design expertise in connection with designing, developing, and commercializing its eVTOL aircraft.

Archer’s goal is to transform urban travel, replacing 60–90-minute commutes by car with estimated 10–20-minute electric air taxi flights that are safe, sustainable, low noise, and cost-competitive with ground transportation.
Archer’s Midnight is a piloted, four-passenger aircraft designed to perform rapid back-to-back flights with minimal charge time between flights.
Stellantis is executing its Dare Forward 2030 strategic plan aiming to become a carbon net zero mobility tech company by 2038.
source_title: Archer

facts: On November 14, 2022, Archer Aviation Inc. announced plans to locate its manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia, adjacent to the Covington Municipal Airport.
The selection of the site represents a key next step as Archer works towards its goal of being first in the industry to bring up a high-capacity eVTOL production facility.
The facility will be built on a 96-acre site.
Archer plans to initially build out a 350,000 square-foot facility capable of producing up to 650 aircraft per year.
The planned eVTOL aircraft manufacturing facility is expected to create over 1,000 jobs over the long term.
Construction is expected to be complete by the end of 2023, with initial production expected to begin in 2024.
Construction is expected to be completed in the first half of 2024 with initial production expected to begin in the second half of 2024.
The facility will be capable of being expanded by an additional 550,000 square feet, which is estimated to support production of up to 2,300 aircraft per year.
Archer intends to invest $118M over the next 10 years.
Archer will receive an incentive package totaling 33% of its capital investment commitment.
The incentive package includes land conveyance, tax incentives, and a Georgia REBA grant.
Archer completed a comprehensive survey of potential manufacturing sites across the United States before selecting Georgia.
Key site selection considerations included local labor market talent availability, utility availability, ability to conduct seamless flight test operations, construction costs, and logistics.
Archer has entered into an LOI with Synovus Bank to obtain financing for the project.
Synovus Bank is the largest bank headquartered in Georgia.
Archer anticipates that Synovus will provide financing for the project.
Archer CEO Adam Goldstein stated that Georgia was selected after many months of discussion and evaluation.
Goldstein noted that the U.S. has long led the world in aerospace technology and Georgia has played a vital role in that industry.

Goldstein stated that Archer's eVTOL technology can transform how urban and rural communities live and commute, and the factory can create pathways to highly skilled manufacturing jobs and other ladders of social and economic opportunity.
Goldstein expressed gratitude to Governor Kemp along with Bob Kosek, Sara Gershon, Serra Hall, Frank Turner, their teams, and Archer colleagues for their collaboration.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Georgia has made nurturing innovation a priority as it works with businesses to define the future.
Governor Kemp noted that Georgia's established leadership in the aerospace industry was essential to landing Archer Aviation in Newton County.
Governor Kemp highlighted that Archer can take advantage of Georgia's signature workforce training program Quick Start to help fill over 1,000 long-term jobs in Covington.
Georgia is home to more than 800 aerospace companies.

Georgia maintains a robust aerospace education pipeline with a dozen high schools offering field training, five technical colleges with aviation programs, and several universities offering degrees in aerospace engineering.

The Georgia Center of Innovation for Aerospace, a strategic arm of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, has cultivated an ecosystem of opportunities for innovative companies like Archer for more than 10 years.
Archer designs and develops electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft for use in urban air mobility networks.
Archer's team is based in Santa Clara, California.
source_title: Archer

facts: Archer Aviation broke ground on its facility in Covington, Georgia, where it will produce its flagship electric air taxis in partnership with Stellantis.
The facility will span 350,000-square-feet and produce up to 650 aircraft per year.
The facility has room to expand and support production of up to 2,300 aircraft annually.
The air taxis, known as Midnight, are expected to be used by United Airlines in 2025.
Adam Goldstein is Archer’s co-founder and CEO.
Goldstein said the Covington facility will be the “industry’s first high volume eVTOL manufacturing facility.”
Archer and Stellantis selected Covington for its site after a comprehensive survey of potential markets across the U.S.

The companies considered “the availability and diversity of talent in the local labor market, ability to conduct seamless flight test operations, construction costs and logistics” when selecting the site.
Construction will be completed in two phases.
In the first phase, the facility will span 350,000 square feet and produce 650 aircraft yearly.
The second phase will build out an additional 500,000 to 800,000 square feet.
The expanded facility will have the ability to produce up to 2,300 aircraft yearly.
Goldstein stated that production will be balanced against many other factors to ensure vehicles coming off the line are immediately put into service.

The total state incentive for the Covington project is 33% of the planned investment.

facts: Archer Aviation unveiled the production aircraft, Midnight, a pilot-plus-four-passenger eVTOL aircraft, during its Open House event in Palo Alto, CA.
Archer and customers such as United Airlines will use the Midnight to carry passengers around busy urban areas, including to and from airports.
Midnight is the evolution of Archer’s demonstrator eVTOL aircraft, Maker, which has validated its proprietary twelve-tilt-six configuration and key enabling technologies.
The aircraft features a 47-foot wingspan with a dozen propellers – six in front and six on the trailing edge.
The front six propellers rock five blades capable of fully tilting forward for cruise flight.
The six stationary propellers on the back are two-bladers, pointing upwards, and lock into a minimum-drag configuration in horizontal flight.
The electric aircraft is designed to be safe, sustainable, and quiet, with an expected payload of over 1,000 pounds.
It can carry four passengers plus a pilot.
The glossy black-and-gray eVTOL air taxi is capable of top speeds up to 150 mph (241 km/h).
It can fly as far as 100 miles (160 km) on a charge.
It is optimized for back-to-back short-distance trips of around 20 miles (32 km), with a charging time of approximately 10 minutes in-between.
Midnight’s seats are constructed out of “flax” fiber, a natural plant that requires very little irrigation and is highly absorbent of CO2.
Archer’s design uses fabric made from recycled contents like plastic bottles.
The electric motors used in Midnight have significantly less moving parts than those found in a gas turbine or piston engine, allowing it to operate with less maintenance and lower overall risk.
Designed to cruise at approximately 2,000 feet, the design of Midnight is almost 1,000 times quieter than a helicopter, according to the company.
Midnight is all-electric, resulting in zero operating emissions.
Archer is committed to sourcing renewable energy whenever possible to power its aircraft.
The company is working to certify Midnight with the FAA in late 2024.
After certification, Archer plans to use it as part of its urban air mobility (UAM) network, which it plans to launch in 2025.
In August, Archer completed Midnight’s preliminary design review covering all aspects of the aircraft’s specifications and manufacturing requirements.
The preliminary design review enabled Archer to determine that the design is feasible for Type Certification and commercialization.
source_title: Archer Aviation unveils its full-size Midnight eVTOL air taxi

facts: Archer Aviation is positioning itself as a pioneer in the urban air mobility sector, which is projected to reach $1.5 trillion by 2040.
The company's flagship Midnight eVTOL aircraft completed its first transition flight in 2024, achieving speeds over 100 mph and vertical landing capabilities.
Archer aims to secure FAA Type Certification for the Midnight by late 2025, with three of four required licenses already secured.
Commercial operations for the Midnight are planned to begin as early as 2026 upon certification.
A 400,000-square-foot Georgia manufacturing facility began output in early 2025.
The Georgia facility targets an initial production rate of 2 aircraft per month, ramping to over 100 aircraft per month by 2027.
Production scaling leverages automotive expertise and supply chain best practices from a partnership with Stellantis.
Stellantis could provide up to $400 million in manufacturing support for the Georgia facility.
Archer's defense division, Archer Defense, secured a $142 million contract with the U.S. Air Force's Agility Prime program to fund hybrid VTOL military aircraft development.
The defense market for such aircraft is projected to hit $79 billion by 2033.
United Airlines placed a $300 million order for 250 Midnight aircraft and took a $50 million equity stake in Archer.
Analysts estimate the Midnight's price at $5 million per unit, with economies of scale expected to drive down costs as production ramps.
The Midnight features electric propulsion and Distributed Electric Propulsion (DEP) with twelve independent propellers for redundancy and noise reduction.
The aircraft utilizes a dual VTOL/CTOL (conventional takeoff and landing) capability to extend range and reduce operational expenses.
Archer's strategy includes repurposing existing airports and helipads to avoid costly vertiport construction and accelerate deployment.
Archer holds a $1.1 billion cash balance following a $301.8 million capital raise in early 2025, which should fund operations through mid-2026.
Net losses widened to $198.1 million in Q4 2024, highlighting the need for cost discipline during scaling.
Current valuation stands at approximately $2 billion, with stock volatility driven by regulatory updates and partnership news.
Competitors advancing similar eVTOL programs include Joby Aviation and Airbus.

facts: Archer Aviation is scaling production activities across a 700,000 square foot footprint split between its San Jose facility in California and an upcoming manufacturing campus in Covington, Georgia.
The Covington, Georgia manufacturing campus is being developed in partnership with Stellantis.
The Georgia facility is expected to serve as the company's primary production site once fully operational.
The Georgia facility will feature advanced automation and volume manufacturing capabilities designed to support mid-decade scale-up.
Archer Aviation aims to reach a production goal of 50 Midnight eVTOL units annually by 2026, aligning its manufacturing footprint across California and Georgia.
As of the third quarter of 2025, six Midnight units were under assembly with three nearing final buildout.
Archer Aviation ended the third quarter of 2025 with more than $450 million in cash and short-term investments.
The company's financial runway includes funds from strategic partners Stellantis and United Airlines.
United Airlines has placed a conditional order for up to 200 Midnight aircraft, reinforcing long-term commercial demand.
Archer Aviation is working closely with the Federal Aviation Administration to achieve type certification for the Midnight aircraft by late 2025 or early 2026.
The company must deploy ground operations and training protocols for pilots, maintenance crews, and customer service before its 2028 target date for full network launch.

facts: Santa Cruz-based Joby Aviation and Toyota launched a joint venture to commercially produce air taxis.
The companies will work together on productivity, quality and costs and move toward mass production of Joby's electric vertical takeoff aircraft.
Toyota made a nearly $400-million investment in the company in 2020.
Toyota has since increased its backing of the company to $900 million.
Toyota Chair Akio Toyoda stated that taking on this challenge with Joby is meaningful and their strengthened relationship is an important step forward in realizing the future mobility society.
Joby's all-electric vertical takeoff vehicles are designed to hold four passengers and a pilot.
The vehicle can travel at up to 200 mph.
The vehicle uses six tilting propellers to achieve vertical takeoff before switching to forward flight.
In February, Joby announced a partnership with Uber to start service in the United Arab Emirates this year, bringing on-demand air taxi rides to the country.
Joby plans to expand to the U.S. after the completion of its final stage of Federal Aviation Administration testing.

Prior to its full FAA certification, Joby is hoping to launch early flight operations later this year as part of a White House program that will bring flights to several states, including New York, Texas and Arizona.
Flights in California will not begin until after obtaining FAA certification.
Joby has been in a fierce battle to be the first with taxis in the sky with its Northern California competitor Archer Aviation.
The two companies are involved in overlapping lawsuits, with Joby alleging corporate espionage against Archer.
Archer filed a suit alleging dubious ties to China that sparked an investigation into Joby by the U.S. International Trade Commission.

Joby's chief executive and founder JoeBen Bevirt stated Toyota has been by Joby's side for nearly a decade, providing invaluable guidance and support as they built the foundation for manufacturing their aircraft.
Joby Aviation's shares have fallen more than 30% this year but climbed 3% on Tuesday to $8.92.
source_title: Joby Aviation Creates a Joint Venture With Toyota To Build Air Taxis | Aviation Pros

facts: Electric vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) aircraft are gradually bringing urban air mobility closer, impacting travel for tourists and local residents.
Archer Aviation is submitting applications to the U.S. government’s eVTOL Integration Pilot Program (eIPP).
The eIPP aims to introduce electric air taxis in the largest cities in the U.S.
Huntington Beach, California, has been given Archer Aviation as the sole provider of air taxi services for its plans.
Archer Aviation's applications span several states, including California, Texas, Florida, Georgia, and New York.
These cities have demonstrated a clear commitment to creating infrastructure needed for air taxis to thrive.
Archer's strategy focuses on setting up operational teams locally, developing necessary infrastructure, and collaborating closely with local public safety and emergency response agencies.
As the sole air taxi operator in Huntington Beach's submission, Archer is pioneering advanced air mobility (AAM) to transform urban landscapes.
The collaboration with Huntington Beach is a stepping stone for Archer's long-term goal of offering electric air taxi services.
Archer's involvement aligns with the National Advanced Air Mobility (AAM) Strategy.
The AAM Strategy aims to safely integrate eVTOL aircraft into the national airspace securely and efficiently.
The strategy provides 40 recommendations for certification, operations, and infrastructure development.
The FAA will review initial submissions and begin making decisions in mid-2026.
Integration of eVTOLs into the commercial sector is poised to significantly boost the aviation industry.
eVTOL aircraft are designed to be environmentally friendly, efficient, and highly flexible.
eVTOLs could serve as a viable solution for urban congestion.
For tourism, eVTOLs offer fast, novel ways to experience cities from above, including quick transfers to attractions, rapid airport connections, or scenic tours.
Tourists will benefit from shorter travel times and the ability to travel in style.
Cities like Huntington Beach are already popular among tourists for coastal views and relaxed lifestyle.
Archer has spent years collaborating with federal, state, and local agencies to influence aviation regulations.
This collaboration includes influencing the development of Special Federal Aviation Regulations (SFAR) for air taxis.
Archer's proactive approach contributed to shaping policy and laying groundwork for integrating eVTOLs into America’s national airspace system.
Archer works closely with the U.S. Department of Transportation (DOT) and the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA).
This close work allowed Archer to establish a clear path to market.
Archer's CEO is Adam Goldstein.
CEO Adam Goldstein expressed excitement, noting the focus has shifted to execution: building, deploying, and flying eVTOL aircraft in the U.S.
The FAA prepares to announce selected applicants for the eVTOL program in 2026.
Archer is poised to take a leadership role in the air mobility revolution.
The partnership between Archer Aviation and Huntington Beach reflects growing excitement around electric air taxis.
Potential of eVTOLs to revolutionize urban transport systems could have significant benefits for tourism.
Tourists could soon fly in eco-friendly, efficient air taxis to destinations, bypassing traffic while enjoying scenic beauty.
This represents a new frontier of innovation for the aviation sector.
Introduction of eVTOLs could lead to development of a new market segment: air taxis for tourists.
This aligns with increasing demand for fast, convenient, and sustainable travel options.
As more cities join the eVTOL Integration Pilot Program, rapid growth in the tourism industry is expected as destinations invest in air taxi infrastructure.
As the timeline for the eVTOL Integration Pilot Program unfolds and regulatory frameworks solidify, possibilities for eVTOL technology in tourism appear limitless.
Key partnerships are in place with significant progress being made on the regulatory front.
The emergence of electric air taxis, driven mainly by Archer Aviation, could have a substantial impact on the aviation industry, especially the tourism sector.
Outcomes will include reductions of travel times and environmental footprint.
Tourists will be treated to a new and thrilling way of seeing places from the air.
The next few years will determine how eVTOLs will be integrated into commercial aviation.

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. is building a nearly 400,000 square foot high-volume manufacturing facility in Covington, GA.
The facility is located adjacent to the Covington Municipal Airport.
Construction phase one, inclusive of grading and foundation work, was completed earlier this year.
Phase two, involving the erection of exterior walls and roof, is nearing completion.
Phase three (interior fit out) and phase four (equipment load in and power on) are on track to be completed in the coming months.
Archer reaffirmed an expected construction completion date by the end of 2024.
The facility will support planned production of up to 650 Midnight aircraft annually in partnership with Stellantis.
This production volume would make it one of the largest manufacturing facilities by volume in the aviation industry.
Archer recently showcased its Midnight aircraft at Atlantic Aviation’s new terminal at DeKalb–Peachtree Municipal Airport.

Guests at the showcase included government officials from Georgia’s Office of the Governor, Georgia’s Congressional Offices, the City of Atlanta, Georgia’s State Legislature, the Federal Aviation Administration, and other local governments.
Adam Goldstein, CEO and founder of Archer, stated that the company is tracking towards completing the manufacturing facility by the end of the year.
Once complete, Archer will focus on ramping production up to 650 aircraft a year.
Archer aims to be the first in the eVTOL industry to achieve scaled manufacturing.
The Midnight aircraft is an electric vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) aircraft built around Archer’s proprietary electric powertrain.
The Midnight is designed to offer passengers a sustainable, low-noise, and safe alternative to ground transportation.
The Midnight travels at speeds up to 150 mph, turning hour-long ground commutes into minutes in the air.
The Midnight is designed for back-to-back flights of 20-50 miles with minimal charge time in between.
The Midnight is 100x quieter than a helicopter at cruising altitudes and nearly inaudible from streets below.
The Midnight features redundant systems allowing Archer to target similar safety levels as commercial airliners.
The Midnight is designed to carry a pilot, up to four passengers, and carry-on luggage.
Archer is continuing to progress plans with Stellantis to mass-produce the Midnight aircraft together.
source_title: Archer Aviation Showcases Midnight Aircraft In Atlanta As It Nears Completion Of Its High-Volume Manufacturing Facility In Covington, Georgia | FinancialContent

facts: Archer Aviation unveiled full details of its proposed Miami metropolitan area air taxi network.
The plan combines newly-built vertiports and existing landing sites to create a citywide network for eVTOL aircraft.
Archer's Midnight aircraft is a piloted aircraft designed to carry four passengers and perform high-frequency journeys with minimal charge time between flights.
The company says the Midnight is ideal for built-up urban communities such as Miami, where traffic congestion can be a significant inhibitor to growth.
The new network aims to connect major population and business centers of the greater Miami conurbation, including Miami, Fort Lauderdale, Boca Raton, and West Palm Beach.
Flights between these four centers will be short, 10–20-minute eVTOL flights designed to bypass ground-based traffic congestion using the soon-to-be-certified Midnight aircraft.

The network will link the four major centers with the area's three primary airports: Miami International Airport (MIA), Fort Lauderdale–Hollywood International Airport (FLL), and Palm Beach International Airport (PBI).
Other strategically important general aviation airports in the area will also be linked to decrease transfer times while reducing road infrastructure traffic.

Partnerships with property development, infrastructure, and real estate partners across the region will work on developing existing helipad networks and building newly constructed dedicated vertiports.
West Palm Beach real estate developer Related Ross plans to partner with Archer to develop a vertiport within its under-construction West Palm Beach downtown development.
Related Ross aims to create a new premium mobility hub in the region's fastest-growing commercial center.
Archer intends to collaborate with Dragon Global, a private investment firm based in Miami, Florida, which invests in venture capital and real estate projects.
Dragon Global's Magic City Innovation District in Miami's Little Haiti area plans to develop a vertiport site for air taxis in one of Miami's largest mixed-use technology and innovation communities.
Hard Rock Stadium and Apogee Golf Club have said they are ready to adapt existing helipads to handle next-generation eVTOL aircraft such as the Midnight.

Hard Rock Stadium is a premier sports and entertainment destination home to the Miami Dolphins NFL team, University of Miami Hurricanes, Formula 1 Miami Grand Prix, Miami Open, and other major global events.
Apogee Golf Club is one of the US's most prestigious golf clubs featuring three 18-hole courses designed by renowned architects.

Miami Mayor Francis Suarez commented that Miami attracts visionaries, embraces breakthrough technology, and turns bold ideas into real impact, noting his work with Archer to advance an air-taxi network that elevates Miami as a global capital for innovation and mobility.

Steven Ross, CEO and Chairman of Related Ross, stated the partnership will accelerate South Florida region connectivity and improve access to key locations by integrating Archer's eVTOL across its locations.
Bob Zangrillo, Founder of Dragon Global and partner in the Magic City Innovation District, said partnering with Archer advances a multimodal, future-ready transportation ecosystem for South Florida.
Archer is slowly gaining a firm grip on global cities wishing to be at the forefront of eVTOL evolution, with plans for public passenger eVTOL flights in 2026 if all goes according to plan.
At November's Dubai Airshow, Archer stated Abu Dhabi will become its "second home" in the near future and should see public eVTOL flights in 2026.
Abu Dhabi will be the first place globally where the Midnight eVTOL passenger aircraft will be built outside of the United States.
At Dubai Airshow 2025, Archer signed an exclusive tripartite deal with The Helicopter Company and Red Sea Global to facilitate eVTOL operations rollout across Saudi Arabia from 2026.
The Dubai Airshow deal focuses on development, testing, and potential integration of Archer's eVTOL aircraft into Red Sea Global's operations as one of the first deployments in Saudi Arabia.
In October, Korean Air signed an exclusive agreement with Archer Aviation to commercialize its Midnight aircraft in South Korea.
Under the memorandum of understanding, Korean Air plans to purchase up to 100 Midnight aircraft for deployment across government and commercial applications.
The collaboration supports South Korea's ambition, announced by the Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, and Transport in 2022, to make eVTOL operations a national priority.
source_title: Archer Aviation unveils more details on its Miami network

facts: On 20 March 2024, Stellantis N.V. and Archer Aviation Inc. announced that Stellantis recently completed a series of open market purchases of ~8.3 million shares of Archer’s stock.
The share purchase signals Stellantis’ continued confidence in Archer’s plans to bring electric vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) aircraft to market beginning in 2025.
Stellantis’ increased investment follows CEO Carlos Tavares’ recent visit to Archer’s headquarters and manufacturing facilities in Santa Clara, California.
In January 2023, Stellantis announced its goal to mass produce Archer’s Midnight aircraft as its exclusive contract manufacturer.
The exclusive manufacturing partnership allows Archer to strengthen its path to commercialization by helping it avoid hundreds of millions of dollars of spending.
Construction on the first phase of Archer’s high-volume manufacturing facility in Georgia remains on track to be completed later this year.
The first phase of the Georgia facility is a build out of ~350,000 square feet on a ~100 acre site.
The Georgia facility is designed to support production of up to 650 aircraft annually.
At full capacity, the Georgia facility would make it one of the largest manufacturing facilities by volume in the aircraft industry.
Archer’s Midnight aircraft is designed to be safe, sustainable, quiet and carry four passengers plus a pilot.
The Midnight aircraft is optimized for back-to-back short distance trips of around 20-50 miles, with a charging time of approximately 10 minutes in-between.

Stellantis CEO Carlos Tavares stated: “Archer has established itself as a market leader in the electrification of aviation and we believe that together we can usher in the next transportation revolution.”
Archer founder and CEO Adam Goldstein stated: “We are on the cusp of changing the way the world moves in the sky.”

Adam Goldstein added: “Archer and Stellantis are working side by side to realize a once-in-a-generation opportunity to redefine urban transportation and deliver tremendous value to the world’s cities and our shareholders.”
source_title: Energy, Automobile, EV, Renewable News | Stellantis Increases Strategic Shareholding In Archer Aviation

facts: Archer Aviation announced it will invest $118 million to construct a plant near Atlanta, Georgia.
The aircraft plant will be built adjoining an airport in Covington, Georgia.
The company plans to eventually hire up to 1,000 people at the facility.
Archer Aviation is developing a battery-powered vertical takeoff and landing craft with six propellers that holds four passengers and a pilot for short urban flights.
United Airlines announced it would operate the aircraft on a route from downtown Manhattan to Newark Liberty International Airport in New Jersey, cutting travel time to 10 minutes.
The U.S. Air Force is evaluating Archer's aircraft for possible use.
Archer Aviation is seeking Federal Aviation Administration certification and expects it could be certified by 2024.
The Georgia site aims to produce 650 aircraft annually, with production capacity potentially rising to 2,300 per year.
Archer expects to receive an incentive package worth some $40 million from Georgia and local governments.
The incentive package includes the donation of 96 acres of land at Covington Municipal Airport, a state cash grant, and other tax incentives.
Archer chose Georgia due to the availability of local workers, the ability to conduct test flights, affordable construction costs, and good highway, rail, and air connections.
Archer CEO Adam Goldstein stated that their eVTOL technology can transform urban and rural commuting and create pathways to highly skilled manufacturing hubs and economic opportunity.
Georgia Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that economic recruiters specializing in aerospace worked with Archer on the project.
source_title: Archer Aviation will build major factory in Georgia

facts: Archer Aviation has received the certificate of occupancy for its high-volume eVTOL manufacturing facility, ARC.
ARC is connected to the Covington Municipal Airport in Georgia.
The facility is located in Newton County, Georgia.
Archer is currently working to complete the tooling load-in for the initial manufacturing line at ARC.
Production at ARC is planned to begin in early 2025.
Archer aims to ramp production to a rate of two aircraft per month by the end of 2025.
Archer built ARC in close partnership with Stellantis.
Archer and Stellantis are continuing to progress plans to mass-produce Archer’s Midnight aircraft.
Stellantis is contributing capital, advanced manufacturing technology, expertise, and experienced personnel to the project.
The partnership aims to scale ARC's production capacity to 650 aircraft annually by 2030.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia remains committed to working with innovative companies like Archer to create quality jobs and opportunities for years to come.
U.S. Congressman Mike Collins of Georgia sits on the House Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure and the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
Congressman Mike Collins stated that the facility's completion highlights Georgia’s leadership in innovation, advanced manufacturing, and job creation.
Congressman Collins noted the project will drive progress, deliver economic benefits for Georgia, and create opportunity for everyone involved with and around the facility.
Adam Goldstein is Archer’s founder and CEO.
Goldstein stated that the facility's completion marks a shift in the industry from R&D into commercialization.
Goldstein noted that construction on ARC is complete and the team is focused on execution and scaling after production starts early next year.
Goldstein praised the Archer team and partners for completing the project quickly and on budget.
Arnaud Deboeuf is Stellantis's Chief Manufacturing and Supply Chain Officer.
Deboeuf stated that supporting Archer’s electric aircraft growth with Stellantis's deep manufacturing expertise is a great opportunity for both companies.
Deboeuf congratulated the Archer team on the milestone, calling it a significant step in taking clean mobility to the sky.
ARC will be used to produce Archer’s eVTOL aircraft, Midnight.
The Midnight aircraft is designed to offer passengers a sustainable, low-noise, and safe alternative to ground transportation in congested cities around the world.
source_title: Archer Completes Construction of eVTOL Manufacturing Facility - Advanced Air Mobility Intl

facts: Began production of enclosed cargo trailers in January 2008 in Hazlehurst, Georgia.
Has manufactured and sold over 75,000 trailer units since its inception.
Products feature a true all-tube steel perimeter frame made with the highest quality materials coupled with exceptional fit-and-finish and quality workmanship.
Employs 150+ individuals that devote significant time and effort to making its products.
Operates bifurcated production lines across two physical plants to accommodate high-volume production demands without compromising quality standards.
Focuses on value rather than price, with the ultimate goal of providing customers with the best trailer at a cost that is most economically valuable.
Corporate mission is summarized by the Four Ps: People, Process, Product, and Price.
Company motto and corporate mission statement is 'Just Do The Right Thing.'
source_title: About Us - Arising Industries, Inc. Trailer Manufacturer in Fitzgerald, GA

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Salesforce, Inc. will expand its Georgia footprint with a $15 million investment expected to create more than 250 new jobs in Fulton County by the end of 2028.
The expansion further strengthens Georgia’s position as a national leader in technology, innovation, and business growth.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that Salesforce’s decision to expand in the No. 1 state for business is the latest direct result of Georgia’s unwavering commitment to fostering innovation and economic opportunity.
Governor Brian Kemp cited the state’s pro-business environment and workforce investments as continuing to pay off as companies choose to operate and grow in communities across the state.
Salesforce is a global leader in AI-powered customer relationship management (CRM) and trusted cloud technology, helping organizations connect with customers in innovative, data-driven ways.
The company currently supports more than 2,000 jobs in Georgia.
Salesforce serves clients across financial services, healthcare, retail, manufacturing, logistics, and the public sector.
Tanya Counter, Site Lead for Salesforce Atlanta, stated that Atlanta has always been a cornerstone of Salesforce’s growth story.
Tanya Counter highlighted the company’s confidence in Atlanta’s extraordinary talent and commitment to building an inclusive, values-driven workplace.
Salesforce’s Atlanta operations are headquartered at Salesforce Tower Atlanta, 950 East Paces Ferry Rd NE.

Atlanta Mayor Andre Dickens emphasized that Atlanta offers diverse talent, global connectivity through Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport, and a welcoming community for companies to expand and thrive.

Fulton County Board of Commissioners Chairman Robb Pitts stated that the investment demonstrates confidence from globally premier companies in the community and will create opportunities for residents and the local economy.

Metro Atlanta Chamber President and CEO Katie Kirkpatrick noted that the expansion underscores the strength and vibrancy of the workforce, citing a deep pool of talented professionals and thriving communities.
The expansion project was supported by the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team, Invest Atlanta, Fulton County, the Metro Atlanta Chamber, and Georgia Power.
GDEcD Senior Regional Project Manager Caroline Kinchler represented the state agency in supporting the project.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that by supporting industries that drive technology and innovation, Georgia ensures businesses succeed while boosting local economies.
Salesforce helps organizations of all sizes become agentic enterprises by integrating humans, agents, apps, and data on a trusted, unified platform.
Salesforce trades on the NYSE under the ticker symbol CRM.
source_title: Salesforce to Invest $15 Million in Georgia Expansion - Expansion Solutions

entity_name: Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Program
canonical_name: Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Program
facts: During the 2021 Georgia Economic Developers Association (GEDA) Annual Conference in Savannah on September 21, 2021, the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) announced new ‘GRAD Certified Select’ sites across the state.
Economic developers representing each newly recognized and newly renewed ‘GRAD Certified Select’ property were presented with a special certificate.
Completing the GRAD certification program with GDEcD is a proactive way both community planners and land owners can help catalyze economic growth and industrial development in their regions.

To qualify for GRAD status, available sites must meet the program’s due diligence standards, be reviewed by a third party and earn the final approval of a board of advisors comprised of public and private sector economic development professionals.
The ‘GRAD Certified Select’ status is an indication that a site has met or exceeded more rigorous certification requirements.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that since starting this program, thousands of Georgia jobs have been created on GRAD Certified sites.

Pat Wilson commended all communities across the state that have taken the initiative to go through the GRAD Certification process and for achieving the ‘Select’ status, thanking them for their partnership and commitment to creating jobs for Georgians and spurring economic growth.
Newly announced ‘GRAD Select’ sites include Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority’s Georgia Highway 257 Rail Served Site in Dublin.
Newly announced ‘GRAD Select’ sites include Development Authority of Peach County’s Robins International Industrial Park in Byron.
Newly announced ‘GRAD Select’ sites include Development Authority of Columbia County’s White Oak Business Park – Phase 2 in Appling.
Newly announced ‘GRAD Select’ sites include Development Authority of Polk County’s Cedartown North Business Park – Phase 3 in Cedartown.
Renewed ‘GRAD Select’ sites include Douglas-Coffee County Economic Development Authority’s Satilla Park in Douglas.
Renewed ‘GRAD Select’ sites include Worth County Economic Development Authority’s Worth Industrial Complex in Sylvester.

GDEcD Deputy Commissioner, Global Commerce Scott McMurray stated that Amazon, Facebook, Starbucks, and Q CELLS are just a few of the companies in Georgia that have benefited from GRAD Certified properties.

McMurray noted that by earning ‘GRAD Certified Select’ status, communities are telling companies they’re ready to work with them on their next big project and looking forward to seeing future jobs and companies these development-ready sites will attract.
The Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Program offers more than 68 industrial certified sites that are ready for fast-track industrial projects, thanks to prior diligence.

GDEcD serves as the state’s sales and marketing arm, lead agency for attracting new business investment, encouraging expansion of existing industry and small businesses, locating new markets for Georgia products, attracting tourists, and promoting the state as a destination for arts, film, music, and digital entertainment projects.

links.context: Aerial views and property details for recognized sites

source_title: | PressReleasePoint

facts: Aurubis Richmond poured its first melt this week, marking the launch of full-scale operations at the company’s $850 million Augusta facility.
The milestone positions the plant to chip away at America’s chronic copper shortage while supplying other critical metals.
David Schultheis, Aurubis president and managing director, stated that the U.S. currently imports about 1 million tons of copper each year.
The facility expects to fill about 10% of that copper import gap.
Aurubis Richmond now employs more than 200 workers.
The company expects to reach 240 employees by mid-2026.
Construction currently draws about 500 people to the site daily, in addition to jobs tied to suppliers and contractors.
The company has logged 2 million man-hours during construction.
Construction activities included installing 3,500 metric tons of steel.
Construction activities also included laying 25 miles of pipe.
Phase 2, approved in late 2022, will double intake capacity to more than 180,000 tons of recycling material.
Phase 2’s first melt is set for February 2026 and will add the “metallurgical part,” specifically the furnaces.
Aurubis’ multi-metal production includes copper, nickel, tin, and zinc.
These metals are vital to data centers, electrification, energy production, and electric vehicles.
Employee Kristian Hughes of Beech Island expressed pleasure with the mission of processing copper and various other metals at a better rate.
Aurubis CEO Toralf Haag called the plant “an industrial powerhouse” producing essential metals.
Haag stated that the U.S. can now reassert ownership of essential metals like copper, nickel, tin, and precious metals, which are the backbone of advanced manufacturing.
Without these metals, the U.S. would lose global competition, security, and competitiveness, according to Haag.
Advisory board Chairman Prof. Dr. Fritz Vahrenholt said the company once considered expanding into China but decided against it due to mismatched interests.
Vahrenholt cited America’s advantages as secure, competitively priced energy and fewer bureaucratic hurdles compared to Germany.
He noted Germany “punishes us with high CO2 taxes” and made the mistake of phasing out nuclear energy.
The company is delighted with President Trump’s plans to quadruple U.S. nuclear capacity by 2050 and his opposition to carbon dioxide taxes.
Congressman Rick Allen praised the project, linking it to winning the economic war with China by matching and beating China in the race to energy.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp sent a video message thanking Aurubis and local officials for welcoming the project.
German Ambassador to the U.S. Jens Hanefeld noted that Aurubis Richmond complements Germany’s two existing copper smelters, compared with China’s 65.
Hanefeld’s trip to Augusta was his first outside Washington, D.C., since his appointment.
Hanefeld stated the company is delighted to be part of creating joint resilience and views it as an investment in a common security partnership.
source_title: Aurubis fires up $850 million Richmond plant

facts: The company that built the plant in Richmond County, near Augusta, says it's the largest German investment in Georgia.
source_title: Aurubis opens metal recycling plant in Richmond County, Georgia - Atlanta Business Chronicle

facts: Aurubis AG, a 150-year-old German-based company, broke ground on June 18, 2022, on the United States’ first large secondary smelter for multimetal recycling.
The new facility, Aurubis Richmond, is located in Augusta, Richmond County, Georgia.
Total investment in the Augusta project is estimated at €300 million (approx. $320 million).
This represents the largest single investment made by a German company in Georgia history.
The plant is expected to be fully operational by mid-2024.
Once fully online, Aurubis Richmond will have the capacity to process about 90,000 tons of complex recycling materials annually.
Processed materials include printed circuit boards, copper cables, and other recycling materials containing metals such as copper, nickel, tin, and other industrial and precious metals.
The facility will create over 120 new direct jobs.
Aurubis Richmond is expected to become a significant employer in greater Augusta, GA, and a major player in the North American supply chain.

Roland Harings, CEO of Aurubis AG, stated that the US currently produces about 6 million tons of recycling materials containing valuable metals, which are currently shipped to Asia and Europe or trucked to local landfills.
Harings added that the company wants to take advantage of this potential to ensure these materials get reused as critical input materials for many US industries.
Aurubis’ metals are crucial enablers for global market trends such as renewable energy, digitalization, and electric vehicles.
The facility will reduce waste and direct key metals to industries including solar and wind renewables, along with electric vehicle and battery manufacturers.
Copper is 100-percent recyclable without any loss in performance, but industrial-scale secondary smelter recycling requires significant long-term investment.
The plant utilizes state-of-the-art environmental technology that meets or exceeds the highest State of Georgia and federal government standards.

Aurubis Richmond will have a very low impact on regional air quality, contributing less than 0.2% share of dust emissions in Richmond County (based on actual emissions at Aurubis' recycling site in Germany).

Governor Brian Kemp welcomed Aurubis as one of more than 500 German companies with operations in Georgia and congratulated state and local economic development teams for bringing the sustainability-focused company to Augusta.
Kemp noted that the groundbreaking represents what is made possible in Georgia by strong partnerships and world-class business assets.
The facility demonstrates a gold-standard commitment to sustainable growth and the circular economy.
source_title: Aurubis AG starts construction of a € 300 million (approx. $ 320 million) multimetal recycling plant in Augusta, Georgia

facts: Aurubis has allocated an extra $276 million to double the capacities of its secondary smelting facility in Georgia.
The German-based metals firm announced its decision to invest 640 million euros ($707 million) to construct the smelter in Augusta, Ga.
With a planned upgrade resulting in an additional recycling module, this site will have an estimated capacity of 180,000 tons per annum.
When construction began in mid-2022, it was projected that 300 million euros ($331 million) would be required to complete the project.
The project was originally designed to process up to 90,000 tons per year.
After deliberation in December, Aurubis concluded that a major capital investment was necessary for its new plant.
The new plant will recycle printed circuit boards, cables, and other scrap materials.
On March 27th, Aurubis formally announced it had established an agreement with German equipment manufacturer SMS Group to install a second recycling module.
Aurubis CEO Roland Harings stated that the expansion delivers on the company's "Driving Sustainable Growth" strategy.
The strategy aims to keep strategically important and high-demand mineral raw materials in the resource cycle.
SMS Group’s technology is being installed as part of Aurubis’ new plant in America.
The equipment utilizes a top-blown rotary converter (TBRC) to extract raw materials such as copper, nickel, tin, and platinum group metals from complex recycling materials.
These extracted resources are recovered in downstream processes.
The recovered resources are essential for future technologies, renewable energy transition, sustainable transportation options, and digitalization.
Aurubis CEO Roland Harings stated that bundling SMS Group’s plant know-how with Aurubis’ expertise creates an environment for highly efficient metal recovery.
Harings noted the partnership allows them to take advantage of the enormous potential of the US recycling market.
He added that backing from a strong partner like SMS Group puts them in an excellent position to realize the project professionally and on time.
SMS Executive Board member Michael Rzepczyk stated that their plants are helping Aurubis assume a leading role in multimetal recycling in the US.
Rzepczyk noted that doubling production volume at the new US site takes recycling to the next level in the US.
He added that together they are building a more sustainable future by closing value chains and maximizing recovery of valuable metals from recycling materials.
Aurubis released a quarterly report revealing its decision to increase spending to fund the added recycling system, update existing infrastructure, and cover inflation costs.
The company is injecting an additional 250 million euros ($276 million) into the secondary processing system.
The company is also injecting an extra 90 million euros ($99 million) for inflationary and cost adjustments.
Operations at the Augusta plant will commence in 2026.
Company projections suggest that starting in the 2026-27 fiscal year, a total of 170 million euros ($188 million) will be earned before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization (EBITDA).
In June of 2022, Aurubis’ subsidiary, Aurubis Richmond, received necessary air quality permits from Georgia authorities.
Shortly after receiving permits, construction on the project began.
A press release from Aurubis stated that construction is currently running at full steam.
The project faced heavy objections from Savannah Riverkeeper and other environmental groups despite securing permits and contracts.
Savannah Riverkeeper executive director Tonya Bonitatibus stated in a press release that the air cannot stand any more contamination.
Bonitatibus called it absurd to continue bringing highly polluting industries into the community, arguing people of Augusta do not want to suffer bad air for a few jobs.

The environmental group's statement continued that it is impossible to comment meaningfully about the project without understanding the interconnectivity of permits and their allowable pollutant loads.
The Georgia Department of Natural Resources released an air permit narrative document outlining specifics of the company’s technology.
Using its European facilities, Aurubis will be able to turn blister copper into industrial and precious metals.
A portion of these items will be sold directly back to the American market.
