Aurubis Richmond has flexibility in its recycling technology due to its modularity.
This capability allows extra components to be added, enabling expansion to cater to further requirements.
These features secure the firm's place as a leader in the US recycling market and provide freedom to increase capacity whenever necessary.
Aurubis CEO Roland Harings stated that the modular system allows planning a prudent market strategy to respond flexibly to US needs.
Harings declared that Aurubis will benefit from constant market growth in the US.
Harings declared that economic growth and comprehensive corporate responsibility go hand in hand for the group’s investment in Georgia.
The facility will resolve intended growth targets and adhere to America’s strictest environmental standards.
The project will provide nearly 200 new job opportunities.
Beginning in 2024, phase one of the plant’s construction will be completed and ready for operations.
Two years afterwards, by 2026, module two is set to come online.
source_title: Aurubis Doubles its Smelter Capacity in Georgia - ITAD Daily

facts: Aurubis AG launched a groundbreaking metal recycling facility in Richmond, Georgia.
The facility represents an $800 million investment.
It is Europe's largest copper producer's first major US copper investment on American soil.
The facility is designed to process up to 180,000 metric tons of complex recycling materials annually.
It will transform electronic waste, copper cables, and other metal-bearing products into valuable materials.
The plant will produce 70,000 tons of high-grade blister copper with 98-99% purity.
The facility employs sophisticated processing technology to recover nickel, tin, and precious metals from waste streams.
Production at the facility began in September 2025.
Full operational capacity is expected by the first half of 2026.
When fully operational, the plant will be one of the most advanced metal recycling facilities in North America.
US domestic copper demand reaches approximately 1.8 million tons annually.
Roughly 50% of US copper demand is imported from international sources.
The facility addresses vulnerabilities by establishing a reliable domestic source of recycled copper for industries ranging from electronics manufacturing to renewable energy development.

Copper's strategic importance continues to grow across defense and military applications, renewable energy infrastructure, electric vehicle manufacturing, and critical telecommunications and grid infrastructure.
Recycling complex waste materials domestically keeps valuable resources within the US economy while reducing reliance on international supply chains subject to geopolitical tensions or disruptions.
The Richmond facility's 70,000-ton annual production capacity will offset approximately 7-8% of current US copper imports when operating at full capacity.
Electric vehicle production consumes 2-4 times more copper than conventional vehicles.
Aurubis has explicitly stated intentions to prioritize US customers for the copper produced at Richmond.

The facility's operational flexibility allows for direct sales to US customers at competitive market rates, further processing through Aurubis' international smelting network, and integration into value-added product manufacturing.
The facility recovers nickel, which is critical for batteries, stainless steel, and aerospace applications.
The facility recovers tin, which is essential for electronics manufacturing and soldering.
The facility recovers precious metals including gold, silver, and platinum group metals with high value-to-weight ratios.
Electronic waste is processed via thermal processing and separation to yield high-grade blister copper.
Copper cables are processed via mechanical processing and smelting to yield nickel, tin, and precious metals.
Metal-bearing products are processed via advanced sorting and metallurgical recovery to yield refined metals for industrial applications.
The facility creates immediate construction jobs and long-term industrial positions.
The facility creates high-skill technical positions in metallurgical processing.
The facility creates supporting logistics and administrative roles.
The facility creates indirect employment through supplier relationships.
Copper recycling typically requires up to 85% less energy than primary production from mined ores.
Copper recycling significantly reduces greenhouse gas emissions per ton of metal produced, water usage, land disruption, and waste entering landfills.
Aurubis CEO Toralf Haag stated the company sees significant potential for additional projects in the United States.
Industry analysts note the Richmond facility may represent just the first phase in a broader North American strategy for the European metals giant.

Aurubis CEO Toralf Haag said: "With the high availability of recycling material and good local conditions, the American market offers Aurubis appealing prospects that we will continue to consider closely in the future as well."

Factors making the US market attractive for continued metal recycling expansion include growing waste streams, policy support for domestic manufacturing and supply chain security, a strong manufacturing base requiring reliable metal inputs, and well-developed transportation networks and utility systems.

The facility incorporates highly automated sorting and processing systems, sophisticated metallurgical recovery techniques, advanced material handling capabilities, and efficient energy management and recovery systems.

The Richmond facility demonstrates scale efficiency, multi-metal focus, integration capabilities connected to international processing networks, and strategic positioning to serve growing North American demand.

The facility establishes new standards for metal recycling in North America that will likely influence industry practices for complex material processing, regulatory approaches to waste management and recycling, investment patterns in circular economy infrastructure, and manufacturer engagement with recycled material streams.
The facility helps address critical material availability concerns for clean energy technologies, advanced electronics manufacturing, defense applications, and transportation electrification.

The facility will process up to 180,000 metric tons of complex recycling materials annually, including printed circuit boards, copper cables, and various metal-bearing products containing valuable recoverable metals from consumer and industrial sources.

High-grade blister copper (98-99% purity) produced at Richmond can be further refined to meet the same quality specifications as primary copper for electronics, electrical equipment, and infrastructure applications.
According to a detailed Aurubis Richmond facility factsheet, the plant's environmental performance will significantly exceed regulatory requirements while establishing new standards for the industry.

The article contains forward-looking statements regarding market impacts and facility performance, noting actual results may vary based on operational factors, market conditions, and regulatory developments.
source_title: Aurubis US Copper Recycling: Transforming Domestic Supply

facts: Aurubis AG and SMS group GmbH signed a supply contract for a second plant module at the new Aurubis Richmond recycling plant in Georgia as part of their strategic partnership.
The modular system design allows for flexible operations and quick growth in the dynamic US recycling market.
Aurubis is considerably accelerating its growth course in the future recycling and circular economy market.
The additional equipment will increase the capacity of the metal recycling plant in Richmond from an annual 90,000 tons to 180,000 tons of complex metal scrap materials per year.

Aurubis CEO Roland Harings stated that the expansion stage delivers on the company’s “Driving Sustainable Growth” strategy while keeping strategically important and high-demand mineral raw materials in the resource cycle.
SMS group recycling equipment will be installed in the second module of the new US plant.
The core technology is the top-blown rotary converter (TBRC), a state-of-the-art system for processing complex recycling materials.
The TBRC enables recovery of raw materials like copper, nickel, tin, precious metals, and platinum group metals in additional downstream stages.
Recovered metals are crucial raw materials for future technologies including the energy transition, sustainable mobility, and digitalization.
SMS Executive Board member Michael Rzepczyk stated that Aurubis is assuming a leading role in multimetal recycling in the US with the planned doubling of production volume at the new site.
Michael Rzepczyk added that the partnership closes value chains and maximizes recovery of valuable metals from recycling materials to build a more sustainable future.
Additional components can be added to the Aurubis Richmond plant to expand capacity precisely to meet future requirements.
The modular system allows Aurubis to plan a prudent market strategy and respond flexibly to US market needs.
Aurubis is investing about €640 million for both phases of the project to build the first secondary smelter specializing in multimetal recycling in the US.
Groundbreaking for the Aurubis Richmond plant took place in mid-2022, with construction running at full steam.
Stage one of the plant is scheduled to go online in the first half of 2024.
The TBRC from the signed contract is destined for the second stage, which is anticipated to start production in 2026.
Aurubis processes roughly 1 million tons of recycling material annually as a leader in multimetal recycling.
The Aurubis Richmond plant will add another 180,000 tons of annual processing capacity.
The Georgia investment will comply with the highest environmental standards in the US and create around 200 new jobs.
Aurubis AG is a leading global provider of non-ferrous metals and one of the largest copper recyclers worldwide.
The company processes complex metal concentrates, scrap metals, organic and inorganic metal-bearing recycling materials, and industrial residues into high-quality metals.
Aurubis produces more than 1 million tons of copper cathodes annually, along with wire rod, continuous cast shapes, profiles, and flat rolled products made of copper and copper alloys.
Aurubis also produces precious metals, selenium, lead, nickel, tin, and zinc.
The product portfolio includes sulfuric acid and iron silicate.
Aurubis has around 6,900 employees and production sites in Europe and the USA.
SMS group generates worldwide sales of around €2.6 billion and employs 14,500 people.
Aurubis shares are listed on the Prime Standard Segment of the German Stock Exchange, the MDAX, the Global Challenges Index (GCX), and the STOXX Europe 600.
source_title: Aurubis and SMS group forge ahead with the first multimetal recycling plant in the US - SMS group GmbH

facts: Aurubis began commissioning its first US multimetal recycling plant in September 2025.
The facility produces strategic metals including copper, nickel, tin, and precious metals for American energy infrastructure, data centers, AI applications, and the defense industry.
The project created more than 240 jobs in Georgia.
The investment amount is approximately €740 million.
During the expansion stage starting in 2026, the site will process up to 180,000 t of complex recycling material annually.
The complex recycling material includes circuit boards, copper cable, and other metal-bearing products.
The Georgia site serves as a springboard for further US growth, including options for value chain expansion and new strategic partnerships.
Aurubis is scaling its value creation close to crucial resource and customer streams of the future.
source_title: Focused Growth - Aurubis Annual Report 2024/25

facts: Germany-based copper producer and recycler Aurubis AG has listed a doubling of capacity at its under-construction smelter in Richmond, Georgia, among its capital expenditure (capex) plans for the current fiscal year.
Aurubis achieved operating earnings before taxes (EBT) of 532 million euros ($565 million) in its fiscal year 2021/22, which closed at the end of September.

For the upcoming 2022/2023 fiscal year, Aurubis projects profits in the 400 million euros ($425 million) to 500 million euros ($531 million) range, representing a decline in EBT of 6 percent to 24 percent.
Profitable conditions have prompted Aurubis to move forward with several previously announced capex plans and announce three additional capex projects.

The company cites implementing its “Driving Sustainable Growth” strategy, including the ASPA recycling project, BOB capacity increase for nickel and copper in Belgium, and the United States plant Aurubis Richmond in Georgia.
Aurubis approved three further strategic projects with an investment volume of around 530 million euros ($563 million).
One project involves investing to expand the modular recycling system at the U.S. site in Georgia.
With a second recycling module, Aurubis will double its processing capacities at the Georgia location.
Further investment adjustments for infrastructure requirements and inflation were approved for the ongoing Georgia project.

In Germany, the Complex Recycling Hamburg (CRH) project will include extensive investments in processing capacity of complex recycling materials and intermediate products at the group's headquarters in northern Germany.
CRH will optimize material flows at the Hamburg site and form the basis for additional strategic projects.
The third newly announced expenditure concerns an expansion of an existing solar park at the Aurubis site in Pirdop, Bulgaria.
For the Oct 1, 2021 to Sept 30, 2022 fiscal year, the company benefited from significantly higher metal results with higher prices for industrial metals like copper, tin, and nickel.
Former Metallo facilities now part of Aurubis have increased its presence in the tin and nickel markets.
Higher refining charges for other recycling materials and strong operating performance at the Pirdop site ensured a strong overall result.

CEO Roland Harings states: “As the most efficient and sustainable smelter network in the world, we have the metallurgical expertise and offer the best process and recycling solutions to recover strategically necessary future metals for the circular economy.”
Aurubis says it is very well positioned financially and operationally and remains optimistic for the 2022/23 financial year despite economic slowdown and rising energy prices.
source_title: Aurubis capex plans include added US capacity - Recycling Today

facts: Aurubis is adding one of the world’s largest state-of-the-art multi-metal recycling facilities to Augusta.
Cal Wray serves as President of the Augusta Economic Development Authority.
The Augusta Economic Development Authority hosted a panel discussion addressing Aurubis's investment.
Aurubis's investment is the largest single investment by a German company in Georgia.
The project will create up to 1,000 jobs.
The project has a 1 Billion dollar impact.
Aurubis aims to expand Georgia’s electromobility value chain through sustainable development and raw material security.
The new Augusta facility will produce materials required in lithium-ion batteries and other electronics.
The facility is located at the Augusta Corporate Park.
The facility spans 150 acres.
Further expansions are expected at the Augusta facility.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development is tracking the project's progress through a blog on its website.

links.url: Accelerated Investment in Aurubis’ Augusta Multi-metal Recycling Facility Strengthens Georgia’s Position in EV, Sustainability.
links.context: Georgia Department of Economic Development blog post tracking project progress

source_title: Tracking Aurubis’ progress - Augusta Business Daily

facts: German metals producer Aurubis has opted to double capacity at its Georgia plant.
Aurubis signed an equipment contract for 180,000 tons of capacity.
The contracted capacity doubles the amount from Aurubis's initial announcement for the facility.
source_title: Aurubis opts to double capacity at Georgia plant – Carolina Recycling Association

facts: Aurubis AG formally signed an agreement with the European Investment Bank (EIB) for a €200 million investment loan on September 11, 2025.
The five-year loan will support two strategic projects: expanding copper refining at its Bulgarian site and scaling up metal recycling and environmental protection at its Hamburg plant.
Steffen Hoffmann, Chief Financial Officer at Aurubis, stated the financing commitment acknowledges the social relevance and outstanding quality of their investment projects.
The loan's attractive terms compared to market conditions enable Aurubis to further diversify its financing base.

Nicola Beer, Vice President of the European Investment Bank, said securing critical raw materials like copper and expanding recycling activities in Europe are essential to Europe’s resilience, sustainable development, and industrial competitiveness.
Copper is described as driving innovation in electric cars and renewable energy.
The EIB loan is the first following the adoption of its new broad-ranging critical raw materials strategy and supports the rollout of the EU Critical Raw Materials Act.
Both projects reinforce sustainable metal production and support key EU policy objectives such as economic growth, regional development, and sustainable business practices to create new jobs.
The financing commitment represents the first EIB financing for the sector since the adoption of the new EIB Group strategy to secure Europe’s access to critical raw materials earlier this year.
Aurubis is investing €120 million to expand the tankhouse at its Bulgarian production site and boost cathode production to cover growing European demand for copper.
Aurubis anticipates commissioning in fiscal year 2025/26 for the Bulgarian expansion.
This is the largest single investment at the Bulgarian plant since its acquisition in 2008.
The Bulgarian expansion represents an around 50% increase in production at the site to 340,000 tons of refined copper per year.
The expansion will support Bulgarian economic development, boost exports, and create additional jobs.
Copper plays a crucial role in enabling the energy transition, AI and data centers, and underpins Europe’s security architecture.
With increased production capacity, Aurubis is positioned to better serve Europe’s rising demand for copper in the future.
Experts predict that global demand for copper will double by 2050.
The loan will also back the €190 million Complex Recycling Hamburg (CRH) investment program at the North German site.
Aurubis expects operations to begin in the coming fiscal year for the CRH facility.
The new Aurubis facility will process around 30,000 additional tons of recycled material along with a larger amount of complex internal smelting intermediates.
The CRH project closes both internal and external material loops and reinforces the company’s contribution to the circular economy.
The EIB Group Strategic Initiative for Strengthening EU’s Critical Raw Materials (CRM) Supply Chain was adopted in March 2025.

The initiative aligns with the EU’s Critical Raw Materials Act, setting an ambitious annual lending orientation of EUR 2 billion to support critical raw materials across extraction, processing, recycling, and substitution.
It delivers a targeted 9-point action plan to catalyse investment, support innovative and sustainable technologies, and strengthen the entire CRM value chain inside and outside the EU.
Aurubis AG processes complex metal concentrates, scrap metals, organic and inorganic metal-bearing recycling materials, and industrial residues into metals of the highest quality.
Aurubis produces more than 1 million tons of copper cathodes annually.
From copper cathodes, Aurubis produces wire rod, continuous cast shapes, profiles, and flat rolled products made of copper and copper alloys.
Aurubis also produces precious metals, selenium, lead, nickel, tin, zinc, sulfuric acid, and iron silicate.
Aurubis has around 7,100 employees and production sites in Europe and the US.
source_title: Aurubis AG: Aurubis secures € 200 million from EIB to drive recycling and copper production | Corporate - EQS News

facts: The State of Georgia maintains full-time representation in a dozen international markets.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development and its economic development partners capitalized on the 2024 SelectUSA Investment Summit to demonstrate why Georgia stands out to international companies seeking to locate or expand in the U.S.
Georgia has emerged as a leader in the e-mobility sector, with more than $27.3 billion in investments since 2018, creating over 32,200 new jobs.
Major Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) making significant investments in Georgia include Kia, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA), Rivian, and Archer Aviation.
Battery manufacturers at the forefront of battery innovation in Georgia include SK Battery America and LG Energy Solution.
Recyclers and fuel cell manufacturers advancing recycling and hydrogen fuel technologies in Georgia include Aurubis AG, Ascend Elements, and Plug Power.
The state’s traditional automotive sector exports reached $4.1 billion in 2023.
The Georgia Ports Authority’s Brunswick facility is the second-busiest automotive Roll-on/Roll-off port in the U.S.
Aerospace and defense products are Georgia’s top export, totaling $11.1 billion in 2023.
The state ranks fifth in aerospace exports in the U.S.
In 2023 alone, aerospace investments in Georgia surpassed $500 million.
Notable aerospace giants contributing to the sector include Gulfstream Aerospace Corporation, Lockheed Martin, and Embraer.
Innovative startups pushing boundaries in urban air mobility and advanced aerospace technologies include Archer Aviation and Hermeus.
Defense leaders driving advancements in defense technology include Anduril Industries and Northrop Grumman.

The state’s appeal is enhanced by its affordable cost of doing business, available sites, and robust workforce pipeline from institutions like the University System of Georgia and the Technical College System of Georgia.
The Georgia Center of Innovation supports these sectors with industry-specific expertise and strategic partnerships.
Georgia ranks among the Top 10 U.S. states for food processing GDP, contributing over $11 billion to the state’s economy.
Major companies in Georgia’s food processing industry include Pilgrim’s, Chick-fil-A, The Coca-Cola Company, Yakult, Nestlé, and Ferrero.
Baked goods are prepared fresh daily at King’s Hawaiian and CJ Foodville.
Companies like Local Bounti and Pure Flavor are leading advancements in controlled environment agriculture (CEA), significantly reducing land and water use.
Georgia’s food processing industry benefits from the eighth-largest refrigerated capacity space in the U.S.
There are 22 technical colleges across the state ensuring a steady, skilled workforce pipeline.
Georgia’s agricultural legacy includes leading production in peanuts, pecans, and poultry.
For over a decade, Georgia has established itself as a prime location for global business growth, offering diverse opportunities across key industries.
source_title: From Global to Local: Industries that Put Georgia on the Map - Albany CEO

facts: Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp is set to visit Germany and Poland next week on a weeklong business mission aimed at shoring up relationships with key partners and driving new investment relationships.

Mr. Kemp recently outlined his legislative and budgetary priorities for 2025 in local appearances and the annual State of the State address, which focused on lowering taxes, boosting infrastructure spending, and achieving tort reform.
The governor emphasized the importance of international partnerships to job-creation efforts across the state.

Mr. Kemp stated that over the last six years, international companies have invested over $38 billion in Georgia, creating more than 58,000 new opportunities for Georgians in every corner of the state, with communities outside of metro Atlanta attracting over 80 percent of those jobs.
The visit occurs as Germany faces a political crisis with snap elections scheduled for Feb. 23 and announced that 2024 brought a second straight year of recession.

Despite struggles in Europe's largest economy and public issues with auto-makers like Volkswagen that have large plants in the Southeast, Georgia remains committed to the German market where it has been present for decades.

Mr. Kemp said the mission is an opportunity to reinforce collaboration with Germany while opening dialogues with emerging markets to ensure a brighter future for Georgians as the state prepares to return money to taxpayers and invest in public safety and education.
Automotive suppliers from Germany have invested more than $830 million in Georgia over the last two decades, according to the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
Manufacturers like Volkswagen and BMW have set up shop in the region.
Atlanta is home to the North American headquarters of both Porsche and Mercedes-Benz.
Georgia’s European office was opened in 1973 by then-governor Jimmy Carter and is now located in Munich.
This trip will be at least the third visit to Germany for Mr. Kemp.
On a previous visit, Mr. Kemp visited Porsche in Stuttgart to see production of the all-electric Taycan.

More recently, he traveled to Hamburg to view smelting operations of Aurubis, which just opened an $800 million smelting operation in Augusta, representing the state’s largest German investment project ever.
At the grand opening, Mr. Kemp called Aurubis “one of my favorite projects ever,” noting it exemplifies the promise of Georgia’s global partnerships.
Six other German investors are on Mr. Kemp’s public itinerary, and his office stated he will also attend diplomatic and relationship-building opportunities.
BASF maintains a manufacturing presence in Georgia with operations in Attapulgus, Edgar, and Savannah.
Gerresheimer established a production facility in Peachtree City in 1993 and expanded in 2009 with a Technical Competence Center (TCC), announcing additional expansions in December 2023 and May 2024.
Carcoustics established Carcoustics USA South Inc. in Buford in 2017, including a central research center and manufacturing presence.
KIRCHHOFF Automotive opened an assembly facility in Lawrenceville in 2023, set to create 73 jobs.
GEDIA Automotive Group announced an advanced manufacturing facility in the greater Dalton area in 2020 that will begin production focusing on parts for electric vehicles.
KIRCHHOFF Automotive and GEDIA Automotive Group are based in North Rhine-Westphalia, the only German state with a trade office in Georgia.

The press release did not specify which firms the governor plans to visit in Poland, though a defense element is possible as Poland's trade with Georgia increased from $118 million five years ago to $1.2 billion in 2023 following delivery of Marietta-made Lockheed Martin C-130J aircraft.
On previous overseas trips, Mr. Kemp has visited National Guard troops.
Following the governor's return, the community will have two opportunities to hear from German Consul General Melanie Moltmann about Germany’s political and economic situation.
Mr. Kemp attended his second appearance at the World Economic Forum’s annual conference in Davos, Switzerland, last January, with the event scheduled again for next week.
Following that trip, he traveled to Switzerland to reinforce relationships with Swiss investors, accompanied by members of the Georgia team.
The economic development department will accompany the governor on the Germany mission.
source_title: Gov. Kemp to Visit Germany, Poland on Business Mission  - Global Atlanta

facts: Aurubis Richmond achieved a strategic milestone with the ramp-up and start of production at its new US site on September 24, 2025.
The facility will produce key strategic metals including copper, nickel, tin, and precious metals in Georgia.
These metals are essential for expanding data centers, AI applications, energy infrastructure, high-tech products, and the defense industry.
American industry currently requires approximately 1.8 million tons of copper per year.
Experts estimate copper demand will rise nearly 30% over the next five years.
The United States currently imports about half of the copper it processes.
Multimetal recycling at Aurubis Richmond is positioned to quickly close this supply gap and reinforce American supply chain independence.
A "First Melt" celebration was held on September 24, 2025, attended by US Representative Rick W. Allen, German Ambassador Jens Hanefeld, and other political and business figures.

Dr. Toralf Haag, CEO of Aurubis AG, stated the company is the frontrunner in multimetal recycling and processing in the US and will continue expanding its expertise in producing key strategic raw materials domestically.

Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp noted in a video message that Aurubis Richmond is now a significant employer for Georgia and plays a major role in the American multimetal supply chain, standing as a testament to American manufacturing independence and resiliency.
The project involves an investment of approximately $800 million.
Aurubis will create more than 240 jobs in Georgia through this facility.
The plant is designed to process up to 180,000 tons of complex recycling material annually.
An expansion stage for the facility is scheduled to begin in 2026.
Recycling materials processed at the site will include printed circuit boards, copper cables, and other metal-bearing products.
Aurubis Richmond recovers critical metals from these materials on-site to close the value chain locally.
Because the plant will be supplied locally, it will help keep valuable raw materials within the domestic market.

US Representative Rick W. Allen emphasized that the facility is a strategic site in Georgia’s 12th District that reinforces America's ability to supply itself with metals critical for national security, infrastructure, and technology leadership.

David Schultheis, President and Managing Director of Aurubis Richmond, described the facility as an important player in the North American multimetal supply chain and thanked Georgia policymakers and governmental authorities for their strong support and smooth approval processes.
The new plant utilizes cutting-edge technology that meets the latest environmental standards enacted by Georgia state and federal authorities.
Operations will maintain high consideration for water, air, and soil quality.
The facility is designed as a flexible "one-stop shop" capable of receiving a broad range of metal-bearing recycling materials from US suppliers.
Aurubis AG is a leading global provider of non-ferrous metals and one of the largest copper recyclers worldwide.
The company processes complex metal concentrates, scrap metals, organic and inorganic metal-bearing recycling materials, and industrial residues into high-quality metals.
Aurubis produces more than 1 million tons of copper cathodes annually.
From its copper cathodes, the company manufactures wire rod, continuous cast shapes, profiles, and flat rolled products made of copper and copper alloys.
Additional metals produced by Aurubis include precious metals, selenium, lead, nickel, tin, and zinc.
The company's portfolio also includes sulfuric acid and iron silicate.
Sustainability is a fundamental part of Aurubis's strategy, integrating sustainable conduct into its corporate culture regarding natural resources and ecological conduct.
Aurubis employs approximately 7,100 people globally and operates production sites in Europe and the US with an extensive worldwide distribution network.
Aurubis shares are listed on the German Stock Exchange (Prime Standard Segment), MDAX, Global Challenges Index, and STOXX Europe 600.

links.context: Digital press kit for Aurubis Richmond

source_title: Aurubis Richmond: First US multimetal recycling plant starts production of strategic metals | Corporate - EQS News

facts: Aurubis scrapped its existing dividend policy.
The company announced 530 million euros ($562.44 million) in growth-oriented investments.
The new investments will expand plants in the U.S. state of Georgia and in Hamburg, Germany.
For fiscal year 2021/22, Aurubis reported operating earnings before tax (EBT) of 532 million euros.
This EBT figure is up from an adjusted 381 million euros in the previous year.
In November, CEO Roland Harings stated that strong growth in copper demand from the renewables and electric vehicle sectors compensated for a decline in demand from the construction sector.
Aurubis trimmed its operating EBT forecast corridor for fiscal year 2022/23 to 400 to 500 million euros.
The previous operating EBT forecast corridor was 500 to 600 million euros.
The company will pay dividends for fiscal year 2021/22 at a rate of 1.80 euros per share.
source_title: Copper smelter Aurubis plans 530 million euros in new investments, ETAuto

facts: Experts have been researching autonomous vehicles (AVs) and the future of the workforce for over a decade.
Research has found that AVs will create new jobs spanning many skill levels and areas of expertise, countering narratives that they will eliminate jobs.
The leading automation companies were founded in the U.S., and maintaining global leadership will strengthen American workers’ ability to compete globally.
The average trucker is nearly 50 years of age, and the industry is struggling to attract new workers.
Automated trucking alone could increase GDP by more than $68 billion over 30 years.
The AV industry has already introduced thousands of jobs into the American economy.
The AV industry could create up to 450,000 more jobs over the next 15 years.
Many new AV-related jobs will not require a 4-year college degree and will span multiple areas of expertise and interest.
AVs will remove transportation barriers to employment for individuals with disabilities.
Workforce changes from AV deployment will occur gradually over several decades rather than overnight, minimizing impacts on individual careers.
"The true value of automation lies in its ability to enhance human capabilities, not replace them."
source_title: US AV Jobs Coalition

facts: Since 1982, Big Tex Trailers Inc. has grown from a small-town trailer shop into a multimillion-dollar enterprise.
Over a thousand intelligent employees wake up each morning and call Big Tex Trailers home.
The company's success strategy involves hiring great people, providing them support, and pushing them to reach their full potential.
The company prioritizes employee stability, safety, and professional growth over just compensation.
source_title: Big Tex Trailers Careers

facts: Big Tex Trailer Manufacturing LLC filed a WARN notice in 2026 with the state of Georgia on February 5, 2026.
The filing relates to Big Tex Trailer Manufacturing LLC’s Georgia operations and is sourced directly from official state WARN records.
The layoff event is classified as a permanent closure.
100 employees are affected by the layoff.
The layoff date is January 16, 2026.
The notice date is February 5, 2026.
source_title: Big Tex Trailer Manufacturing LLC Layoff in GA — Layoff Details | Layoff Tracker

facts: Rivian's initial Georgia plant capacity has been increased to 300,000 units annually.
The new capacity is up from the previously planned 200,000 units.
Production at the Georgia factory is on track to begin in late 2028.
Rivian announced the capacity increase as part of its first-quarter earnings release.
The company now plans to borrow $4.5 billion through the Department of Energy to build the facility, down from a previous plan of $6.6 billion.
Rivian will begin drawing on the DOE loan in 2027.
The Georgia factory will produce vehicles based on Rivian's midsized platform, including the R2 model.
The plant will also produce a robotaxi variant as part of a 50,000-vehicle partnership with Uber.
Production of Rivian's first mass-market crossover has begun at an expanded facility in Normal, Illinois.
Rivian's Illinois facility has the capacity to build 155,000 R2 vehicles annually.
CFO Claire McDonough stated that a combined capacity of 515,000 vehicles is expected to help the company achieve positive free cash flow.
In the first quarter, Rivian reported $1.38 billion in revenue and a net loss of $416 million.
Rivian is relying on the R2 model to achieve meaningful economies of scale that the higher-priced R1T and R1S models could not.
Rivian's initial plan for the Georgia plant called for two construction phases, each yielding 200,000 units of production capacity.
CFO Claire McDonough stated on the earnings call that the Georgia plant will have room for future expansions.
source_title: Rivian Boosts Initial Georgia Plant Capacity To 300,000 Units

facts: The Georgia Ports Authority Board has approved an expenditure of $127 million to build the Blue Ridge Connector, an inland rail terminal in Gainesville, Ga.
The Blue Ridge Connector will link Northeast Georgia with the Port of Savannah’s 35 global container ship services and is scheduled to open in 2026.
The facility will serve a region important for the production of heavy equipment, food, and forest products.
Funding for the Blue Ridge Connector combines GPA internal capital with a Federal Maritime Administration grant of up to $46.8 million.
Norfolk Southern Railroad will connect the Blue Ridge Connector facility to GPA’s Mason Mega Rail terminal in Savannah.

Counting this project, GPA has invested more than $374 million in rail capacity, including the Port of Savannah’s on-dock Mason Mega Rail Terminal and the Appalachian Regional Port in Northwest Georgia.
Approximately 18-20 percent of GPA’s container cargo moves by rail, with the remainder handled by truck.

Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp stated that inland terminals have a proven track record of powering economic development for rural Georgians by extending port services to manufacturing and supply chain operations.
GPA President and CEO Griff Lynch noted that the investment will help customers streamline their supply chains while reducing congestion on Georgia highways.
Improved rail service to the region will increase transportation efficiency and act as a magnet for jobs and economic development, according to GPA leadership citing the Appalachian Regional Port.
Every container moved by rail avoids a 600-mile roundtrip by truck between Savannah and the Gainesville area, reducing the carbon footprint for cargo owners.
Hybrid electric rubber tire gantry cranes will be used at the new facility.
The board approved $44.5 million to build a 300,000 square-foot facility on Garden City Terminal with offices and refrigeration facilities to support U.S. Customs and Border Protection.
Savannah provides the only on-port CBP inspection facility in the U.S., lowering costs and time for port customers by eliminating the need to transport cargo to an off-site inspection facility.
GPA Chairman Kent Fountain emphasized that the long-term partnership with CBP is important to the safe and secure operational flow of cargo through the gateway container terminal.
GPA reopened Berth 1 at Garden City Terminal to vessel traffic following a two-year renovation project.
The renovated dock now allows the facility to serve vessels in the 16,000+ TEU range.
With all berths now online, vessel service has returned to previous velocities, with ship queues cleared.
GPA is conducting a $262 million expansion program at the Port of Brunswick.
Construction is complete on 350,000 square feet of near-dock warehousing that serves auto and machinery processing on the north side of Colonel’s Island Terminal.
Three additional buildings representing 290,000 square feet and 122 acres of Roll-on/Roll-off cargo storage space are under construction on the south side of the island.
GPA maintains a two-pillar strategy of development and operations, with the Port of Savannah focused on container trade and Brunswick specializing in Ro/Ro cargo.
GPA anticipates trade of approximately 430,000 twenty-foot equivalent container units for November, representing an 18.5 percent increase over the same month in 2019.
Georgia’s ports and inland terminals support more than 561,000 jobs throughout the state annually.
The ports contribute $33 billion in income, $140 billion in revenue, and $3.8 billion in state and local taxes to Georgia’s economy.
GPA anticipates investing $4.5 billion over the next ten years as part of its port master plan to expand cargo handling capabilities for future supply chain requirements.
$6 million will be donated to communities located near the Port of Savannah to support a multi-year, local workforce housing initiative.
CNBC ranked Georgia #1 in the U.S. for infrastructure in America’s Top States for Business in 2023.
Area Development has ranked Georgia as the Top State to Do Business for ten consecutive years.

links.context: Contact email for Chief Communications Officer Tom Boyd
Phone number for Chief Communications Officer Tom Boyd

source_title: GPA invests in rail connections, terminal capacity - Georgia Ports Authority

facts: TMC Transformers USA Inc. announced it would invest more than $15.3 million over the next five years in a new manufacturing facility in Waynesboro, Georgia.
The investment will create at least 110 jobs.
Operations have already started at the company’s first facility in Burke County.
The new plant is expected to be operational at the beginning of 2026.
TMC is now hiring for roles in management, administrative staff, production technicians, operators, testers, sales and quality control.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that after meeting with TMC's leadership in Italy, they were truly impressed by the company's warmth, hospitality and enthusiasm for their decision to invest in Georgia.
Pat Wilson noted that the transformers manufactured in Burke County are critical in addressing energy infrastructure needs for the state and the nation.

This expansion follows recent manufacturing expansions in Georgia, including Shinsung Petrochemical's investment of an additional $11.2 million in a new facility in Bryan County, creating 30 new jobs.
GF Casting Solutions AG announced a more than $184 million investment in Augusta to produce lightweight components for the mobility and energy industries, creating 350 new jobs in Richmond County.
StandardAero began work on a $33 million expansion of its business aircraft maintenance operations in Augusta, which will create 90 new jobs in Richmond County.
source_title: TMC Transformers Invests in New Georgia Manufacturing Facility | Manufacturing.net

facts: Bosch is a global leader in engineering and technology with strong positions in automotive solutions, consumer goods, and smart technologies.
In 2025, Bosch is focused on sustainable mobility, digitalisation, AI, and connected solutions, while investing in EVs, IoT, and Industry 4.0 innovations.
Bosch has a commitment to sustainability that includes carbon-neutral operations and hydrogen fuel cell investments.
Founded in 1886 with electrical equipment repairs, Bosch quickly became a pioneer in automotive innovations like the magneto ignition system and spark plug.
Bosch became the first global industrial company to achieve carbon neutrality in 2020 across 400+ locations.
Bosch generated over €91.6 billion in revenue in 2024, showcasing its scale and financial strength.
Bosch is a leading supplier of automotive components worldwide with a strong presence in electrification and mobility solutions.
Annual R&D investment exceeds €7 billion, driving breakthroughs in AI, IoT, and Industry 4.0.
Bosch is a leader in smart mobility, hydrogen fuel cells, and autonomous driving solutions.
A strong patent portfolio ensures Bosch maintains a technological edge over competitors.
Unlike many competitors, Bosch is privately owned, allowing it to reinvest profits into innovation rather than short-term shareholder gains.
Over 400,000 associates worldwide drive innovation at Bosch.
A large portion of Bosch's revenue comes from automotive components, making the company vulnerable to downturns in the auto industry.
Shifting demand to EVs puts added pressure on Bosch as rivals like Tesla and BYD scale faster.
Any slowdown in car production directly impacts Bosch's profitability.
R&D costs often surpass €7 billion annually, squeezing short-term margins.
Competitors with leaner cost structures can undercut Bosch in pricing.
Rising operational costs may slow expansion into emerging markets.
Global competitors in EV and AI, such as Tesla and Siemens, have moved faster in scaling advanced solutions.
Economic downturns reduce consumer spending on cars and appliances, impacting Bosch's sales.
Increasing regulatory demands around emissions, sustainability, and compliance add financial strain to Bosch.
Competitors with niche focus, such as EV-only players, may adapt faster to regulatory changes than Bosch.
The global shift toward electric vehicles presents a major opportunity for Bosch's automotive division.
Bosch can expand its EV components, battery management systems, and charging infrastructure solutions to meet rising demand.
Collaborations with automakers and government initiatives for clean mobility can strengthen Bosch's market position.
Competing with global giants like Siemens, Honeywell, ABB, and Schneider Electric pressures Bosch's market share in automotive, industrial, and consumer products.
Emerging startups in smart home tech, EV components, and automation are agile competitors capturing niche markets.
Raw material cost fluctuations, semiconductor shortages, and logistics disruptions can impact production and profitability.
Stricter environmental, safety, and energy regulations in the automotive and industrial sectors increase operational complexity.
Changes in international trade policies, tariffs, and sustainability requirements can affect production and global expansion.
Core tension: Bosch's reliance on the automotive sector exposes it to market volatility, while rising costs and faster-moving competitors in EVs and AI threaten its leadership position.
Future outlook: Bosch is well-placed to thrive by expanding into EVs, hydrogen technology, and smart connected solutions.
Recommendations include collaborating with EV manufacturers, governments, and tech firms to scale green and digital solutions faster.
source_title: Bosch 2026 SWOT: Key Insights & Analysis | IIDE

facts: Bosch announced it is acquiring US chipmaker TSI Semiconductor.
Bosch is investing $1.5 billion to produce silicon carbide (SiC) semiconductors and enable the adoption of EVs.
Bosch has been transforming its business as the auto industry shifts to sustainable, zero-emission EVs.
Bosch has invested heavily in introducing new products such as electric powertrains, charging solutions, and electric drives to meet the growing demand for EVs.
Mike Mansuetti is President of Bosch in North America.
Mansuetti explained last year that the auto industry is “rapidly evolving” toward electric vehicles.
In October, Bosch revealed a new $250 million investment to extend its Charleston, South Carolina, campus by roughly 75,000 square feet to build and assemble electric motors.
Mansuetti added, “Local production helps advance our customer’s regional electrification strategies and further supports the market demand for electrification.”
Bosch is expanding its North American manufacturing capabilities with plans to acquire Roseville, California-based chipmaker TSI Semiconductors.
Bosch will invest $1.5 billion in the Roseville site to convert and prepare the facility for the auto industry’s future.
Starting in 2026, Bosch will produce the first chips on 200-millimeter wafers based on silicon carbide (SiC) material at the new site.
Bosch has been producing SiC chips since 2021 at its Reutlingen location in Germany.
Bosch expects to have an average of 25 of its chips in every new EV by 2025.
SiC chips enable greater range and more efficient recharging, losing up to 50% less energy.
The full scope of the project is made possible and will rely heavily on federal funding opportunities according to Bosch.
The Biden administration is investing heavily to promote North American manufacturing and the adoption of sustainable transportation and clean energy in the US.

The Inflation Reduction Act, passed last August, provides incentives and rebates to purchase EVs (up to $7,500 for new and $4,000 for used) and other clean energy alternatives (example: 30% solar tax credit).
The Chips and Science Act, signed into law last year, invests in US chip-making capabilities to strengthen the domestic supply chain and enable clean energy adoption.
Vice President Kamala Harris provided a statement regarding the Bosch acquisition and investment.

The $1.5 billion investment will bring down costs, strengthen the electric vehicle supply chain, help rebuild American manufacturing, and create economic opportunity for working families of California.
It will put more electric vehicles on the road, a priority Harris worked for since serving in the US Senate.
All of this was made possible by the Administration’s Invest in America agenda.
Harris stated that House Republicans are working to undo progress and want to defund American manufacturing and ship jobs overseas.
President Biden and Vice President Harris will never let that happen.

According to a recent Financial Times analysis, over $200 billion in private investments in the US has been announced in the eight months since the Chips and Science Act and Inflation Reduction Act were signed.
The Biden administration is aiming for a 50% electric vehicle sales share by 2030.
source_title: Bosch to acquire chipmaker to meet SiC chip demand for EVs

facts: Bosch Sensortec plans to integrate complete 9-axis inertial sensor complements along with more powerful ARM-based microcontrollers into a single package while aiming for the same power envelope as the hub alone.

Stefan Finkbeiner, chief executive officer and general manager of Bosch Sensortec, announced upcoming trends in micro-electro-mechanical systems (MEMS) at the MEMS Executive Congress in Scottsdale, Arizona.
Bosch Sensortec plans to add environmental sensors including volatile-organic-compounds (VOCs), barometric pressure, humidity, and temperature into a 2.5-millimeter package.
i-Blades has a smartphone case that includes Bosch's environmental sensors and is popular in regions with poor air quality like China.
The i-Blades smartphone case won the MEMS & Sensors Technology Showcase, earning a place in the MEMS Hall of Fame.

Bosch aims to make its environmental sensors cheap and small enough for factory inclusion in smartphones, as well as for use in air purifiers and thermostats by lowering their price/performance ratio.

Bosch's consumer-grade MEMS division and its automotive division are sharing resources for integrated navigation modules that are small and cheap like consumer devices but meet higher automotive MEMS standards.
The Bosch automotive division announced a new super-high precision SMP580 barometric pressure sensor that significantly reduces fuel consumption and emissions at the same price.

The Bosch automotive division introduced a new SMP580 barometric pressure sensor designed for immersion in automatic transmission oil, which results in smoother shifting, fast sensing with a response time of less than 1ms, and a smaller package sealed for the lifetime of the car.
source_title: Bosch to integrate 9-axis inertial sensor complements - EDN Asia

facts: The economic environment and market conditions for Bosch Mobility have recently become more challenging.
The global vehicle market continues to see subdued development.
A lack of a regulatory framework is making it difficult to establish new technologies, such as hydrogen.
Market penetration of emerging technologies such as electromobility and automated driving is experiencing considerable delays.
Demand in Bosch’s sales markets is shifting extensively to regions outside Europe.
Ongoing structural changes and very high price and competitive pressures exist in the global automotive industry.
Bosch Mobility faces an annual cost gap of around 2.5 billion euros worldwide in its Mobility business.
Bosch intends to use all levers to reduce costs at all levels quickly to close the cost gap.
The company sees potential to reduce costs through productivity gains from artificial intelligence in manufacturing and engineering.
Cost reduction measures include reducing material and equipment costs, reducing capital expenditure, and making logistics and global supply chains more efficient.
Structural and personnel adjustment measures are unavoidable.
Bosch anticipates a further reduction of around 13,000 jobs, particularly at its Mobility locations in Germany.
Time frames for necessary adjustments vary and extend until the end of 2030.
A sharp decline in demand has caused significant overcapacity in administration and sales as well as in development and production.
Stefan Grosch is a member of the Bosch board of management and director of industrial relations.
In Europe and Germany, Bosch’s strong presence makes it impossible to maintain its current high headcount.
Power Solutions and Electrified Motion divisions are particularly affected by the cuts.
Affected locations include Feuerbach, Schwieberdingen, and Waiblingen in the greater Stuttgart area, as well as Bühl and Homburg.
Jobs are also to be cut in Bosch’s corporate functions, administration, sales, and subsidiaries.
Bosch remains committed to Germany as an industrial location.
Stefan Grosch states that Germany is and will remain central to Bosch regarding the number of associates, but positioning must be more efficient to hold own against tough global competition.
Increased competitiveness is a prerequisite for securing orders in Germany, and thus headcount in the country.
At Feuerbach, development, sales, administration, and the Power Solutions plant for powertrain components are affected.
A stagnating automotive market and worldwide decline in diesel proportion significantly impact the Feuerbach plant, which currently produces mostly diesel products.
Demand for hydrogen technology products commercialized recently is too low due to considerable delays to the ramp-up of the hydrogen market in Europe.
Underutilization of production capacity and overall workforce overcapacity exist at the Feuerbach site.
Some 3,500 jobs are to be cut at Feuerbach by the end of 2030, including around 1,500 at the plant.
At Schwieberdingen, Power Solutions, Electrified Motion, and Mobility Electronics divisions will cut around 1,750 jobs in sales, purchasing, administration, and development by the end of 2030.
A negative order situation and slow ramp-up of emerging technologies increase pressure to adjust costs and structures at Schwieberdingen.
At Waiblingen, Bosch plans to phase out production for connector technology by the end of 2028, currently employing around 560 people.
The Waiblingen plant mainly produces connectors based on thermoplastic and silicone rubber for the global automotive industry.
Production volumes and headcount at Waiblingen have been declining for many years, making the plant uncompetitive.
Subsidiaries Bosch Healthcare Solutions GmbH and Bosch Industrial Additive Manufacturing at Waiblingen are not affected by the planned measures.

At Bühl/Bühlertal location, where Bosch develops, commercializes, and manufactures electric drives for low voltage applications for European automakers, around 1,550 jobs will be cut by the end of 2030.
Job cuts at Bühl affect sales, purchasing, administrative functions, development, and manufacturing.
At Homburg location, Bosch plans to cut around 1,250 jobs by the end of 2030 and largely consolidate operational activities of the Power Solutions division in the eastern part of the plant.
Consolidation at Homburg aims to leverage synergies and reduce structures.
A stagnating automotive market, worldwide decline in diesel vehicles (including dominant commercial vehicle business), and delay in hydrogen market ramp-up significantly impact Homburg.
Bosch has presented necessary measures and personnel adjustments to employee representatives and informed associates.

Stefan Grosch states that despite urgent need for action, they stand by agreements with employee representatives and will quickly discuss necessary measures at individual locations for socially acceptable solutions.
The company plans to begin discussions with employee representatives responsible at individual locations immediately.
Markus Heyn is a member of the board of management of Robert Bosch GmbH and chairman of the Mobility business sector.
Markus Heyn states that geopolitical developments and trade barriers such as tariffs lead to considerable uncertainty and more intense competition.
Bosch aims to seize growth opportunities wherever possible and ensure Mobility locations worldwide remain viable.
Markus Heyn is convinced that Bosch Mobility can prevail in the highly competitive global market but must pave the way now using its own resources to secure competitiveness as time is pressing.
source_title: Measures that aim to close the cost gap at Bosch Mobility – further job cuts unavoidable - Bosch Media Service

facts: Bosch completes the sale of its starter motor and generator division that will now be known as SEG Automotive.
The newly formed SEG Automotive belongs to the Zhengzhou Coal Mining Machinery Group with immediate effect.
SEG Automotive is now employing 8,000 employees at 16 sites throughout the world.
Besides continuing the business with starter motors and generators, the company wants to turn towards electrification, too.
Together with a group of investors, SEG Automotive plans to deliver solutions for electrification such as the BRM (Boost Recuperation Machine).
The BRM recuperates energy from braking to distribute it through the 48 Volt on-board system.
source_title: Bosch subsidiary turns into SEG Automotive - electrive.com

facts: Bosch generated sales revenue of 90.5 billion euros in 2024, with an EBIT margin from operations of 3.5 percent.

Stefan Hartung, chairman of the board of management of Robert Bosch GmbH, stated that growth markets such as electromobility developed much more slowly than expected, negatively impacting business development due to lack of sales and underutilized capacity.
By 2030, Bosch aims to be among the leading suppliers in its areas of business in key markets, striving for average annual growth of between 6 and 8 percent with a margin of at least 7 percent.
Bosch is acquiring the HVAC business of Johnson Controls and Hitachi for around 8 billion U.S. dollars to expand its presence in growth markets such as the U.S. and Asia.
Bosch sold large parts of its Building Technologies division’s product business for security and communications technology to focus on systems integration.
In North America, Bosch ended its 2024 fiscal year with $17.4 billion in consolidated sales, representing growth of around six percent compared to the previous year.
Paul Thomas, president of Bosch in North America, stated that North America and the United States specifically is an important growth market for Bosch.
The Mobility business sector achieved $10.7 billion USD in North America in 2024, a five percent increase from 2023.
Bosch continues to invest in and book customer wins related to technologies supporting regional needs such as trucks/SUVs and hybrid technology.
Bosch is progressing on plans to manufacture the second generation silicon carbide chips on 200mm wafers in Roseville, California in 2026.

In Energy and Building Technology, preliminary results in North America showed $1.9 billion USD, a 46 percent increase from 2023, including plans to acquire HVAC solutions business for residential and light commercial buildings from Johnson Controls expected to close during 2025.
For the Industrial Technology business sector, Bosch posted $1.4 billion USD in North America in 2024, a 12 percent decrease year-over-year.
Electromobility, hydrogen, and sustainable technologies remain a growth business and focus of innovation for Bosch.

Bosch is testing a cryogenic pump in the U.S. that compresses up to 600 kilograms of liquid hydrogen per hour, allowing trucks to fill up with enough hydrogen for the next 1,000 kilometers in just 10 minutes.
Around 5,000 AI experts at Bosch are working on making AI suitable for everyday use, including an AI-assisted emergency call service for elevator users.
Bosch expects to generate sales of more than 6 billion euros with software and services by the beginning of the next decade, with two-thirds coming from the Mobility business sector.

Bosch is advancing software-defined mobility through systems like Vehicle Motion Management, which enables brake-by-wire braking systems where an electronic brake pedal operates without a mechanical connection.
As of December 31, 2024, the Bosch Group employed approximately 417,900 associates worldwide, nearly 3 percent lower than the previous year (-11,500).
In Germany, headcount fell by around 3 percent (-4,400) to just over 129,800 as of December 31, 2024.

Markus Forschner, member of the board of management and chief financial officer of Robert Bosch GmbH, stated that worldwide growth will remain moderate and the global economy is not expected to pick up again before 2026.
Bosch assumes the economy will grow by just 2½ percent in 2025 and aims to achieve its target margin of 7 percent by 2026.
source_title: The 2024 business year: Bosch held back by market developments - Bosch Media Service US

facts: Bridgestone Bandag will expand its operations in Abilene, Texas with a $60M investment.
The expansion adds 50,000 square feet to the company's existing 200,000 square foot facility.
The project focuses on developing an automated operation for rubber mixing and production.
The Development Corporation of Abilene (DCOA) presented “Project Eagle II” to the City Council of Abilene.
The City Council approved a $6 million incentive package from the DCOA to support the expansion agreement.
Bridgestone Bandag is expected to retain 179 employees through the expansion project.
The project will create an additional 25 jobs with average salaries above the City of Abilene's average salary.
Over the next 10 years, the $60 million investment is estimated to add over $21 million in taxes for the City of Abilene, Taylor County, and the Abilene Independent School District.
Over the next 10 years, the total estimated indirect and direct economic impact of the company's retention and expansion is $1.81 billion.
Bridgestone Bandag has been a part of the Abilene community since 1971.

The expansion supports Bridgestone’s E8 Commitment, which consists of eight values starting with “E” (Energy, Ecology, Efficiency, Extension, Economy, Emotion, Ease, and Empowerment) to realize a sustainable society.
Barry Owens, senior vice president of the Bridgestone Americas Manufacturing Group, stated the expansion is driven by growing demand for products and the team's commitment to innovation and quality.
The company aims to build greener retreads while helping maximize fuel efficiency for fleets.
The Abilene community supports the facility with four higher education institutions, a technical college, and a community college.
Abilene is located in Taylor County, Texas, directly on I-20, approximately two and a half hours from the Dallas/Fort Worth metro area.
The city has a population of 126,518 people and a labor shed of over 140,000.
Bridgestone Americas, Inc., the U.S.-based subsidiary of Bridgestone Corporation, is headquartered in Nashville, Tennessee, and employs more than 50,000 people across its worldwide operations.
source_title: The Development Corporation of Abilene Announces Bridgestone Bandag’s $60M Investment - Expansion Solutions

facts: Bridgestone Bandag broke ground on the expansion of its retread tire plant in Abilene, Texas.
The project represents a $60 million capital investment into Abilene.
The expansion will add 50,000 square feet to their existing 200,000 square-foot facility.
Misty Mayo, President and Chief Executive Officer of the DCOA, stated that Bridgestone Bandag has continued to invest in Abilene for over 50 years.
The expansion project is expected to retain 179 full-time employees.
The project will create an additional 25 full-time positions for Abilene.
Over the next 10 years, the $60 million project is estimated to add over $21 million in taxes for the City of Abilene, Taylor County, and Abilene Independent School District.
Abilene Mayor Weldon Hurt stated that Bridgestone Bandag continues to grow and invest in the City of Abilene.
Over the next 10 years, the total estimated indirect and direct impact of the retention and expansion is $1.81 billion.
Sam Vinson, DCOA Board Chair, noted that Bridgestone Bandag has chosen to stay, grow, and prosper in Abilene, Texas.
The expansion will focus on the development of an automated operation for rubber mixing and production.
Billy Fralick, Plant Manager of the Abilene Plant, stated the expansion will allow them to grow production capabilities to meet customer needs.
The plant has received community support over the last 52 years.

The project supports Bridgestone’s E8 Commitment, which consists of 8 values starting with “E” (Energy, Ecology, Efficiency, Extension, Economy, Emotion, Ease, and Empowerment) to realize a sustainable society.

Chris Daniel, Executive Director of Bandag Manufacturing at Bridgestone Americas, stated the expansion is driven by growing demand for products and the team’s commitment to delivering innovation and superior quality.
The company aims to build greener retreads while helping maximize fuel efficiency for fleets.
The project taps into Abilene’s skilled labor force in the manufacturing industry.
Abilene has four universities, a technical college, a community college, and state-of-the-art training facilities for high school and college students.
Abilene is located directly on I-20, about two and a half hours from the Dallas/Fort Worth metro, in the south-central United States.
The city has a population of 126,518 people and a labor shed of over 140,000.
Bridgestone Americas, Inc. is the U.S.-based subsidiary of Bridgestone Corporation, headquartered in Nashville, Tenn., and employs more than 50,000 people across its worldwide operations.
Bandag has been responsible for keeping approximately 300 million tires out of the waste system since 1957.
The Bandag retreading process has saved four billion gallons of oil since it began 50 years ago.
The Development Corporation of Abilene (DCOA) is the economic development driver of Abilene, leveraging the DevelopAbilene partnership to market the city for business expansion.

facts: Carcoustics, an auto-industry supply company based in Germany, will spend $6 million on a new manufacturing facility in Buford, Georgia.
The company plans to create 200 jobs over the next few years.
Governor Nathan Deal announced the project on Tuesday.
Carcoustics develops and produces acoustic and thermally effective components for the automotive industry and other companies.
Its customers include major manufacturers of cars, trucks, domestic appliances, agricultural machinery, and HVAC systems.

Stefan Vallentin, project manager at Carcoustics, stated the company is happy to locate its new plant in Georgia due to great international transport connections and a logistical location between BMW in South Carolina and Mercedes in Alabama.
The company identified potential customers throughout the South, including Volkswagen, Mercedes, Volvo, Nissan, Honda, Toyota, Kia, and Hyundai.
Gwinnett County Board of Commissioners Chair Charlotte Nash welcomed Carcoustics to Gwinnett County, noting it joins a strong and growing base of international companies in the area.

Governor Deal emphasized that Georgia offers automotive industry suppliers resources to compete worldwide, citing a well-trained workforce, a thriving automotive industry, and the nation’s top business environment.
Governor Deal highlighted efforts to build international relationships as yielding positive results with German companies locating in Georgia.

facts: Gov. Nathan Deal announced on June 13, 2017, that Carcoustics will create 200 jobs and invest $6 million in a new Buford facility over the next five years.
The newly created jobs will include positions in manufacturing.

Gov. Nathan Deal stated that Georgia offers automotive industry suppliers like Carcoustics resources to compete worldwide, including a well-trained workforce, a thriving automotive industry, and the nation’s top business environment.
Gov. Nathan Deal expressed pride in seeing another German company locate in Georgia as efforts to build international relationships continue to yield positive results.
The international Carcoustics Group model combines a central research center with a manufacturing structure to provide proximity to major markets for quick global product availability.

Stefan Vallentin, project manager at Carcoustics, stated the company chose Georgia for its great international transport connections and logistical location between BMW in South Carolina and Mercedes in Alabama.
Potential customers for Carcoustics include Automotive Tier I/ Tier II suppliers, Volkswagen, Mercedes Vans, Volvo, and Asian OEMs like Nissan, Honda, Toyota, Kia, and Hyundai.
The Georgia location offers new possibilities for Carcoustics' growth strategy, especially in the U.S., according to Stefan Vallentin.
Gwinnett County Board of Commissioners Chair Charlotte Nash welcomed Carcoustics to Gwinnett County, citing its ideal mix of talent, resources, and location for international companies.

Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) Director of International Investment Nico Wijnberg represented the Global Commerce division in partnership with Partnership Gwinnett, the Metro Atlanta Chamber, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.

Hala Moddelmog, president and CEO of the Metro Atlanta Chamber, noted that Carcoustics joins international companies recognizing metro Atlanta’s prime location, rich resources, and skilled talent pool.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Carcoustics is joining a growing number of German automotive manufacturers in Georgia and expressed excitement about continued economic development opportunities in Gwinnett County.
Carcoustics is an innovative, midsize company headquartered in Leverkusen, Germany.
The company develops and produces solution-oriented acoustic and thermally effective components for the automotive industry.
Carcoustics offers its expertise in a wide range of technologies to other industrial sectors, with customized solutions at the heart of its product portfolio.
Carcoustics’ customers include major manufacturers of cars, trucks, domestic appliances, agricultural machinery, and HVAC systems.

facts: Club Car announced plans on Friday, Oct. 13 to expand into a new 105,000 square foot facility in Appling.

facts: Constellium Automotive USA is a premier manufacturer of advanced aluminum solutions for the automotive industry.
The company operates its state-of-the-art facility in White, Georgia.
It specializes in comprehensive aluminum solutions that span from initial design concepts through end-of-life recycling processes.
Its product portfolio includes automotive body sheet, extrusion-based components, automotive specialties, and innovative battery solutions.

The company leverages cutting-edge manufacturing technologies to deliver high-performance aluminum components that enable automakers to achieve critical weight reduction goals while maintaining structural integrity and safety standards.

Constellium Automotive USA has established itself as a trusted partner to major automotive manufacturers through its commitment to sustainable mobility solutions and continuous innovation in materials engineering.
Its advanced research and development capabilities focus on next-generation automotive applications, including electric vehicle battery systems and lightweight structural components.
The White facility serves as a strategic manufacturing hub providing just-in-time delivery and technical support to automotive customers across the southeastern United States.

Through an integrated approach to aluminum processing and fabrication, Constellium Automotive USA delivers customized solutions that enhance vehicle performance, fuel efficiency, and environmental sustainability while meeting stringent automotive quality and certification requirements.
