facts: In 2024, Continental celebrates important milestones in its three U.S. tire plants.
The Clinton, Mississippi plant is celebrating 5 years of operation.
The Sumter, South Carolina plant is celebrating 10 years of operation.
The Mount Vernon, Illinois plant is celebrating 50 years of operation.
Continental has invested around $1.5 billion USD in its U.S. tire manufacturing operations over the last decade.
Continental’s U.S. tire plants have an annual production capacity of more than 16 million tires per year.
Continental’s U.S. tire plants employ more than 6,700 employees.

Jochen Etzel, CEO of Continental Tire the Americas, stated that since 1988 the company has strategically and consistently enhanced its manufacturing footprint to support growth in the American market.
The Mount Vernon plant produced its first tire in 1974 and employs a team of over 3,900 people.
The Mount Vernon plant's product portfolio includes both passenger light truck tires (PLT) and truck tires (TT).
The Sumter plant officially opened in 2014, employs approximately 1,300 employees, and focuses on producing PLT tires.
The Clinton plant officially opened in 2019, employs around 700 people, and produces TT tires.
Continental serves both original equipment and replacement customers from its U.S. tire plants.
Continental has additional manufacturing locations in Aldora Mills and Barnesville, Georgia, which produce tire cord.
Hoosier produces racing tires at a location in Plymouth, Indiana.
The U.S. market is identified as a strategic growth market and an important component of Continental Tires' 'Vision 2030' strategy program.
Continental was recently named 'America's Best Large Employers' by Forbes for the eighth time.
Mary Ann Kotlarich serves as Head of Brand Communication and Public Relations for Replacement Tires The Americas.
Katharina Bühmann serves as Spokeswoman for Technology, Innovation & Original Equipment at Continental Tires.
source_title: In 2024, Continental celebrates important milestones in its three U.S. tire plants - Continental AG

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp and GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson announced that Georgia achieved a record year for economic development investments and job creation in fiscal year 2022 on August 10, 2022.
Investments during FY22 were 94 percent above prior economic development records.
Job creation during FY22 was 53 percent above prior economic development records.
Total investments in expansions and new locations during FY22 (July 1, 2021 – June 30, 2022) totaled more than $21.2 billion.
A total of 51,132 jobs were created statewide through 358 projects supported by GDEcD’s Global Commerce team during FY22.
Excluding the two largest projects in state history – Rivian and Hyundai Motor Group – Georgia jobs for the year totaled 35,532.
Excluding the two largest projects in state history – Rivian and Hyundai Motor Group – Georgia investments for the year totaled $10.7 billion.
The Rivian and Hyundai Motor Group projects were part of 12 automotive projects that created a total of nearly 16,000 new jobs across the state.
Eighty-five percent of the investments came from outside the 10-county metro Atlanta region.
More than 30,000 new jobs came from outside the 10-county metro Atlanta region.
Companies choosing to expand in Georgia accounted for 74 percent of total projects.
New locations created 33,846 jobs across the state.
The advanced manufacturing sector added more than 10,000 new jobs in the state.
Advanced manufacturing includes manufacturers and suppliers for the electric vehicle (EV) industry.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America’s (HMGMA) development includes EV battery manufacturing along with affiliate suppliers on-site.
Component recyclers Aurubis and Ascend Elements have joined HMGMA as part of Georgia’s efforts to build a sustainable, reliable supply chain for its electric mobility ecosystem.
Foreign direct investment (FDI) accounted for over $8 billion in investments created in fiscal year 2022.
The top five FDI sources were South Korea, Germany, Japan, France, and the Netherlands.
Korea-based projects represented the highest job creation and investment from a single country.
Germany produced the second most jobs and investments after Korea.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson led economic development missions to the United Kingdom, South Korea, and Germany in FY22.
Meetings were held with economic development drivers like Hyundai Motor Group and SK On.
GDEcD offers "Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development" (GRAD) certification, which recognizes pre-qualified industrial sites where due diligence is completed prior to a prospect visit.
Georgia has certified more than 60 industrial sites as ready for fast-track industrial projects through the GRAD program.
Georgia’s Quick Start program provides specialized workforce training tailored specifically to business needs and has operated for 55 years.
Logistics and distribution capabilities are supported by access to Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport, the Port of Savannah, and a robust network of highways and rail lines.
source_title: Georgia Shatters Investment and Job Records in FY22 | Development Authority of Peach County

entity_name: Georgia EV Tax Rules and Charging Incentives
canonical_name: Georgia EV Tax Rules and Charging Incentives (2026)
facts: The rules for buying an electric vehicle (EV) in Georgia have shifted dramatically following recent federal tax restructurings and localized state updates under the Georgia Economic Growth and Tax Relief Act.

The focus for Georgia EV buyers in 2026 has pivoted away from point-of-sale state rebates toward massive vehicle loan interest deductions, targeted regional charging infrastructure credits, and localized utility rebates.
Eligible taxpayers can now deduct up to $10,000 in loan interest annually on a qualifying vehicle purchase under the new above-the-line tax deduction.
To qualify for the loan interest deduction, the passenger vehicle must have a Gross Vehicle Weight Rating (GVWR) under 14,000 lbs and undergo final assembly within the United States.
The $10,000 loan interest deduction begins phasing out for modified adjusted gross incomes (MAGI) over $100,000 for single filers or $200,000 for married couples filing jointly.

Locally built Georgia vehicles that qualify for the assembly rule include the Hyundai IONIQ 5 and IONIQ 9 rolling out of the Hyundai Metaplant in Bryan County, as well as the Kia EV6 and EV9 assembled in West Point, Georgia.
The Alternative Fuel Vehicle Refueling Property Credit (Section 30C) covers 30% of the combined cost of charging hardware and professional electrical installation, up to a maximum credit of $1,000.
The federal Section 30C charging station tax credit is legally scheduled to expire for any residential property placed in service after June 30, 2026.
To claim the Section 30C credit, the charger must be fully installed and operational before the deadline, and the installation must be located within an eligible non-urban or low-income census tract.

Buyers purchasing EVs online via Clicklane can bundle the purchase price of OEM home charging hardware directly onto the vehicle's original invoice to make the interest paid on that equipment generally deductible under new tax laws.
Georgia Power, the state’s largest utility provider, offers a residential EV charger rebate delivering up to $200 back for installing a dedicated Level 2 (240-volt) home charger.
The Georgia Power rebate requires installation by a licensed electrician and submission of the application along with receipts within six months of the installation date.
Both Georgia Power and various local Electric Membership Corporations (EMCs) across Georgia offer specialized EV Time-of-Use (TOU) electricity tariffs.
Switching to an EV TOU rate allows overnight charging during super-off-peak windows, typically from 11:00 PM to 7:00 AM, at a fraction of standard daytime energy pricing.
Commercial/business enterprise buyers can utilize Section 179 Capital Deductions up to $31,300 for vehicle purchase support.
Commercial buyers qualify for a 6% charging port cost credit up to $100,000 per port under the Section 30C framework, which also expires on June 30, 2026.
Commercial buyers can claim a Vehicle Conversion Tax Credit equal to 10% of alternative fuel conversion costs up to $2,500.
Pursuant to the Georgia Economic Growth and Tax Relief Act, legacy state programs like Credit 111 (Business Enterprise Vehicle Tax Credit) were officially repealed on January 1, 2026.

Nalley Automotive’s Clicklane platform automatically identifies vehicles matching assembly prerequisites, links buyers to an active marketplace of over 30 lenders for competitive interest rates, and itemizes local county taxes down to the exact penny.

Every online EV purchase through Nalley Automotive is protected by a 7-Day, No-Questions-Asked, Money-Back Return Policy that includes pickup from the buyer's driveway and a full refund if logistics do not align with daily routines.
source_title: EV Tax Rules and Charging Incentives for Georgia Buyers (2026) - Nalley Automotive

facts: Continental Tire the Americas (CTA) announced plans to build a new tire manufacturing plant in Sumter County.
The new plant project involves a $500 million investment.
The Sumter County plant is expected to generate 1,700 new jobs in South Carolina over the next 10 years.
Nikolai Setzer serves as a member of Continental’s Executive Board and head of Continental’s global tire business.
Growing demand for Continental and General brand passenger and light truck tires in the U.S. drove the investment decision.
The first phase of production capacity at the new plant will be nearly 5 million tires upon reaching full production levels in 2017.
A second phase is expected to increase the plant’s total capacity to 8 million units per year by 2021.
Governor Nikki Haley praised the $500 million investment for creating 1,700 new jobs in South Carolina.
CTA’s passenger and light truck tire business volume in the Americas region increased nearly 20 percent over the past two years.
Bobby Hitt serves as Secretary of Commerce for South Carolina.
The Sumter County announcement marks the fourth recruitment of 500 or more jobs announced in South Carolina this year.
The new manufacturing facility will be located at a greenfield site off U.S. Highway 521 in Sumter County.
The announcement was made at a news conference at the Sumter Opera House.
Jochen Etzel serves as CEO of Continental Tire the Americas.
Jim Newsome is president and CEO of the South Carolina State Ports Authority.
Greg A. Thompson is Chairman of the Sumter Development Board.
The $500 million investment represents the largest industrial investment in Sumter County history.
Eugene R. Baten serves as Sumter County Council Chairman.
Joseph McElveen Jr. is Mayor of Sumter.
The Coordinating Council approved a $31 million grant for the purchase and improvements of the plant site.
The project was awarded a $4 million federally funded Community Development Block Grant through Sumter County to offset road and public infrastructure improvement costs.
Continental Tire the Americas is also announcing an investment of more than $4 million at its Lancaster County, South Carolina headquarters.
The Lancaster County headquarters currently employs nearly 350 people and opened in 2009.
The Lancaster investment includes a 16,000-square-foot building addition, bringing the total facility size to 91,000 square feet.
The Lancaster County expansion will create 80 new jobs over the next four years.
Continental reported sales of €26 billion in 2010.
Continental supplies brake systems, powertrain and chassis components, instrumentation, infotainment solutions, vehicle electronics, tires, and technical elastomers.
Continental employs close to 160,000 people across 45 countries.
The S.C. Department of Commerce won the Gold Shovel Award and Deal of Year Award from Area Development magazine this year.
The S.C. Department of Commerce has recruited companies including Boeing, Bridgestone, Monster.com, Heinz, ZF Group, BMW, and Google Inc. to South Carolina.
source_title: SC: Continental Tire the Americas LLC Announces New Plant in Sumter County, to Create 1,700 New Jobs | Trade and Industry Development

facts: Continental announced its decision to discontinue operations at its Aldora Mills textile manufacturing plant in Barnesville, Georgia by the end of 2026.
The Aldora Mills facility employs approximately 230 people.
The plant produces textile reinforcement materials, including tire cord fabric, hose yarn, and knitted fabric.
These materials are used exclusively within Continental’s Tires and ContiTech group sectors.
Continental acquired the Aldora Mills facility in 1988.
The decision to discontinue operations follows a comprehensive business review aimed at safeguarding the company’s profitability, competitiveness, and business performance in the Americas region.
The plant has not been cost competitive for many years despite continuous turnaround efforts.
Ongoing changes in global market dynamics and supply chains necessitated the discontinuation decision.
Continental reaffirms its commitment to its customers and partners in the United States.
Continental operates tire manufacturing plants in Plymouth, Indiana; Clinton, Mississippi; Sumter, South Carolina; and Mount Vernon, Illinois.
Continental’s U.S. tire plants have an annual production capacity of more than 16 million tires.
Continental employs more than 8,800 people in the U.S. across its Tires and ContiTech group sectors.
Over the last decade alone, Continental has invested around 1.5 billion USD in its tire manufacturing operations in the United States.
In October, Continental announced a more than 85 million USD investment to expand manufacturing operations at its ContiTech plant in Mount Pleasant, Iowa.
In September 2024, Continental broke ground on its first fully owned tire distribution center in the Dallas–Fort Worth metroplex.
The new Dallas–Fort Worth distribution center is scheduled to open in early 2026.
The new distribution center will enhance service levels and customer support across the region.
The new facility complements seven partner-owned distribution centers that currently serve the U.S. market.
Continental’s main priority regarding the Aldora Mills closure is to support all affected employees.
In collaboration with local partners, Continental will offer career counselling and access to employment resources for affected workers.
source_title: Continental to Discontinue Operations at Georgia Textile Plant - TRAC

facts: Daechang Seat Corp. USA is a supplier specializing in automotive seat frames.
Daechang Seat Corp. USA will invest $72.5 million in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
The new facility will be located in close proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
The investment will create over 500 jobs.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that another HMGMA supplier coming to Georgia highlights the impact projects like the Hyundai Metaplant have beyond their initial investment.
To date, suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant project reach at least nine counties.
Governor Brian Kemp expressed excitement about adding Daechang Seat Corp. USA to Georgia's growing manufacturing base centered around Chatham County and the Savannah region.
Daechang Seat Corp. (DSC) was established in 1979 in Kyungju, South Korea.
DSC has become one of the largest seat frame producers in the world.
Globally, DSC employs more than 5,000 people in seven countries.
The company’s production process includes fully automatic lines with arc welding and spot-welding machines.
Jinsuk Lee is the CEO of Daechang Seat Savannah Corp.

Jinsuk Lee expressed appreciation to the State of Georgia, local communities, and the City of Savannah for DSC's role in creating improved opportunities and employment prospects within Chatham County and the Savannah area.
Jinsuk Lee stated DSC's primary objective is to drive growth through facility expansions to transform DSC into a global entity.
DSC currently holds a prominent partnership with Hyundai Transys and Hyundai Mobis at HMGMA.
DSC hopes to collaborate with even more projects in the future.
DSC’s new facility will be located at the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
The company will be hiring for assembly operators, equipment technicians, mechanical engineers, and electrical engineers.
Hiring will occur through local job fairs and website postings in the future.
Griff Lynch is the Georgia Ports Authority President and CEO.
Griff Lynch noted that DSC is only minutes from the Port of Savannah, ideally located to take advantage of Georgia’s world-class connectivity.
The Georgia Ports Authority aims to provide the best global gateway for U.S. supply chains and welcomed DSC as a job creator in Georgia’s automaking sector.
Trip Tollison is the Savannah Economic Development Authority President and CEO.
Trip Tollison announced that Daechang Seat Corp. USA will be the third Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America supplier to locate in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
The project brings 500 new jobs and $72.5 million in capital investment to Chatham County.
Trip Tollison described Daechang Seat Corp. USA as a well-established global company and expressed anticipation for their growth and community integration.
Alyce Thornhill is the Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy at the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team.
Alyce Thornhill represented GDEcD on this competitive project in partnership with the Savannah Economic Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
Pat Wilson is the GDEcD Commissioner.
Pat Wilson stated that countless partners are helping transform Georgia into a center of the automotive and mobility industry and welcomed DSC in Savannah.
Pat Wilson highlighted Savannah as an established global gateway and noted Chatham County has leveraged that advantage to create a bustling community.
As the market transitions to electric vehicles, Georgia has pursued the entire supply chain.
Georgia has created more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs since 2018.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent when compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
DSC has recently ventured into the electric vehicle sector and continues to expand its scope.
DSC anticipates greater opportunities for growth with the completion of new manufacturing facilities, providing larger installation spaces.

facts: Hyundai Motor Group's Georgia supplier list expanded over the past few weeks with three additional auto part makers announcing investments to establish manufacturing facilities in the state.

Hyundai’s upcoming EV metaplant in Ellabell has a projected cost of $5.5 billion.

Production at Hwashin's Dublin facility is expected to begin in late 2025.
Daechang Seat Corp. USA announced an investment of $72.5 million to build a manufacturing facility in Savannah.

The joint battery cell manufacturing plant will have a capacity to produce 300,000 EVs annually.
Hyundai broke ground on its Georgia manufacturing campus over a year ago.
Construction has recently begun on Hyundai's production-related buildings at the campus, with foundation work nearly complete as of last month.
Hyundai's Georgia manufacturing campus is a nearly 3,000-acre megasite.
The campus will use renewable resources to power its mass production.
Using renewable resources at the campus will help Hyundai reach its goal of becoming carbon neutral by 2045.

Governor Kemp noted that Georgia’s automotive industry employs over 50,000 hardworking Georgians and will continue to grow as e-mobility suppliers choose all corners of the state.
South Korea was one of the top three international sources of job creation in Georgia during Fiscal Year 2023.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated: “Economic development is a team sport that is driven by the local level. Congratulations to Harris County and our community partners for helping bring Daesol Ausys to West Georgia.”

Commissioner Wilson added: “The automotive industry is providing generational change in the region and across the state. We’re excited to see the continuation of this growth and look forward to the opportunities that Daesol Ausys will provide to the people of Harris County.”

facts: Daesol Ausys Georgia is a South Korean automobile parts supplier that announced it will build a new manufacturing facility near Kia’s factory in West Point.
The company is a supplier for Kia, Hyundai Motor Group, and General Motors.
Daesol Ausys Georgia will build a $72 million factory in Harris County, roughly 80 miles southwest of downtown Atlanta.
The new plant will employ more than 140 workers.
The facility will manufacture luggage boards and covers for electric vehicles.
Kia’s new electric EV9 SUV is expected to roll off the West Point production lines next year.
Governor Brian Kemp stated in a news release that the project continues the ring of growth around Kia’s auto plant, which the state aims to recreate with two other massive EV factories.

Governor Brian Kemp said, “West Point has become an automotive capital in its own right... Our state’s automotive industry employs over 50,000 hardworking Georgians and will continue to grow as e-mobility suppliers choose all corners of the state for the jobs of the future.”
Founded in 2017, Daesol Ausys specializes in designing and manufacturing motor and EV interior components and accessories.
The company plans to open its new facility at the Northwest Harris Business Park off Ga. 103 in December 2024.
Kia expects to launch its three-row SUV called the EV9 next year, which requires a $200 million expansion to the Korean automaker’s existing factory.
When Kia’s West Point plant opened nearly 15 years ago, it became Georgia’s only vehicle manufacturing plant.
Hyundai Motor Group, Kia’s parent company, is building its $5.54 billion “Metaplant” near Savannah.
EV startup Rivian expects to begin construction soon on its $5 billion EV factory in southern Morgan and Walton counties, an hour east of Atlanta.
Georgia has emerged as a leader in EV production.
Since 2018, the state has attracted $25 billion in investment and 30,000 jobs in the EV industry, according to Kemp’s office.
This fiscal year, suppliers flocking to locate near the forthcoming Georgia factories represent $2 billion of that investment.
Daesol Ausys will likely qualify for job creation tax credits and worker training through Georgia Quick Start.
A Georgia Department of Economic Development spokesperson said negotiations involving discretionary incentives, such as grants, remain active.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that DAS Corp. will establish a new manufacturing facility in Metter.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant resulted in over $2 billion in investment last fiscal year alone, helping Georgia achieve a third straight year of record-breaking economic growth.
Governor Kemp noted that Georgia's logistics assets, including a reliable network of rail lines and highways, connect companies like DAS to key business partners statewide and globally.

DAS specializes in designing and manufacturing seating systems, safety seating components, and seating structures.
DAS is a key supplier for Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia (KMMG) and Hyundai Motor Group.

The new DAS manufacturing facility is located in the Candler County Industrial Park in Metter.

Operations are projected to begin in the latter half of 2024.

DAS intends to establish collaborations with local employment services as part of its hiring process.
Information about employment opportunities is available online and via email.

Glyn Thrift, Chairman of the Candler County Board of Commissioners, thanked DAS for choosing Candler County as its new home and stated they will provide a boost to spur more economic development.
Griff Lynch, President and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority, stated the authority is proud to support the growing automotive manufacturing presence in the state to drive economic activity and jobs.
Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The GDEcD partnered with the Candler County Industrial Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Electric Cities of Georgia, and Georgia Quick Start for this project.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated Georgia is well-positioned for Hyundai’s supplier network due to years of consistent investment in site preparation, workforce solutions, and connectivity.

In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.

facts: The state of Georgia defines a small business as one that is independently owned and operated with less than 300 employees or a revenue of less than $30 million.
Up to 99% of companies in Georgia fall under this small business category and are vital to the state’s economy.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) offers extensive suggestions and assistance for enhancing business operations.
GDEcD provides resources on its website for business licenses, suppliers, contractors, loans, certifications, and connections for entrepreneurs.
Businesses may be eligible for certification as a women-owned or minority-owned business through GDEcD.
Georgia Centers of Innovation (COI) is divided into five centers that assist the industries of Aerospace, Energy Technology, IT, Logistics, and Manufacturing.
COI helps businesses navigate the business environment, find eligible research grants, and handle legalities for working with universities on projects.
The Georgia Mentor Protégé Connection (MPC) offers a one-year partnership program between corporate mentors and aspiring entrepreneurs.
MPC corporate mentors come from companies with headquarters in Georgia, and eligible small businesses must be nominated by past mentors.
The Georgia Chamber of Commerce is the state’s largest business advocacy group, with 73% of its members being small businesses.
The Chamber provides tools including a Small Business Advisory Council that offers a Webinar Series and other benefit programs.
Local chambers serve almost every city and county in Georgia to support small businesses in their respective areas.

The announcement follows Rivian and Hyundai committing to electric vehicle factories in Georgia.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Denkai America will locate its new manufacturing facility and North American Headquarters in Richmond County.
The company’s initial investment will be $150 million to establish an electrodeposited (ED) copper foil production facility, with a focus on electric vehicle (EV) battery foil.
The project will be located on a 115-acre site.
Denkai America aims to invest a total of $430 million and create 250 new jobs over the next five years.

Governor Brian P. Kemp stated: "Georgia is proud to be selected as the home of Denkai America, further strengthening our state's status as a leader in the emerging electric mobility and existing aerospace, defense, and technology industries."

Governor Brian P. Kemp added: "We have been laser focused on bringing quality jobs to highly-trained, hardworking Georgians across the state, and we look forward to seeing one-of-a-kind companies like Denkai thrive in Georgia’s top-ranking pro-business environment."
Denkai America’s ED copper foil materials have traditionally been used in printed circuit board (PCB) applications, primarily for the aerospace and defense industries.
The company's ED copper foil materials are now also used in lithium-ion batteries powering electric vehicles.
Denkai has operated for more than 60 years with team members worldwide and operations in Japan and North America.
Heday Nakajima, Ph.D., Denkai Group President and CEO stated: "This investment highlights our commitment to leading ED battery foil manufacturing in North America for many years to come."
Heday Nakajima added: "We are grateful to the teams in Richmond County and the State of Georgia for making this project possible."

Nobu Masuda, Denkai America’s President and CEO stated: “We are thrilled to expand our manufacturing presence into Augusta, which has the skilled and talented work force our company needs for growth.”
Construction is expected to begin in September 2022 as the first part of a three-phase investment.
The facility will be fully operational by December 2024.
The company will be hiring for skilled industrial technicians, production managers, and engineers.

Steven Kendrick, Chairman of the Augusta Economic Development Authority stated: "The Development Authority of Augusta, Georgia, is excited to welcome Denkai America and its North American Headquarters to the Augusta Corporate Park."
Steven Kendrick added: "We know they will be a great corporate citizen and bring high-skilled jobs to the growing list of companies that call Augusta and the Augusta Corporate Park home."
Steven Kendrick stated: "We look forward to the great jobs and investment that they will bring to Augusta."
Steven Kendrick thanked the Augusta Commission and the Augusta Utilities Department for their support and hard work to make this happen.
Barton Lowrey, Global Commerce Director of Corporate Solutions and Cyber Security at the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), represented GDEcD on this competitive project.
The project was completed in partnership with the Augusta Economic Development Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.

Georgia Quick Start is the Technical College System of Georgia’s nationally ranked workforce training program that has provided specialized training tailored specifically to business needs for more than 40 years.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated: "As the adoption of electric mobility becomes widespread, the State of Georgia is committed to harnessing this generational opportunity to ensure that Georgians employed in today’s automotive industry will have access to these jobs of the future here at home."
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson added: "Denkai America plays a vital role in the EV ecosystem, and we are excited to welcome this innovative company to Augusta and Richmond County."

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated: "This investment is also further evidence of how Georgia’s commitment to building lasting international relationships continues to drive economic partnerships across our state, including the recruitment of the wider electric mobility value chain."

Georgia’s prime location, extensive infrastructure, skilled workforce, and business-friendly climate has made it an attractive location for a diverse array of rapidly developing industries focused on creating a sustainable future.
Georgia is cultivating a vertically integrated supply chain that will help companies increase efficiencies by reducing the reliance on imported materials.
EV-related projects have contributed more than $12.6 billion in investments and more than 17,800 new jobs to Georgia since 2020.
Augusta Corporate Park is a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified park.
To earn GRAD program certification, a property must undergo due diligence and complete specific certification requirements prior to a visit by a prospective business.

Completing the GRAD certification program with the Georgia Department of Economic Development is a proactive way both community planners and landowners can help catalyze economic growth and industrial development for the regions they serve.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Denkai America to Locate New Manufacturing Facility, N.A. Headquarters in Richmond County :: I-Connect007

facts: AUGUSTA, GA – Nippon Denkai has decided to close and liquidate its copper foil manufacturing plant here, the last such factory in the United States.

Denkai America Inc., the US-based subsidiary of the Japanese company, will close just two years after its parent company heralded the site as the future of foil production, with tens of millions of dollars in investments planned and hundreds of new jobs expected.
The company cited financial problems stemming from semiconductor shortages, decreased exports of domestically manufactured batteries, lower demand for smartphones, and other factors for the decision.
The continued losses, coupled with loan payments coming due and dried-up sources of new capital, have rendered the company insolvent.
Denkai America announced in 2022 the planned investment of some $150 million in the plant to make copper foil.
It took combined losses in the 2023 and 2024 fiscal years of about $23 million tied to the plant.

For its fiscal year ending March 2025, decreased sales and a rapid increase of copper prices, plus an impairment loss related to the Augusta plant, led to first-half consolidated ordinary losses of approximately 1.5 billion yen ($9.9 million) and a consolidated net loss of approximately 5 billion yen ($33 million).

In addition to the deterioration of its performance in and after the fiscal year ended March 2023, the company’s cash flow gradually deteriorated as a result of proceeding with the reconstruction of manufacturing facilities in the American subsidiary’s previous plant in Camden, SC, and investments in foil manufacturing facilities in the Augusta plant.
Attempts to find additional funding have stalled, Denkai said in a statement.
In November, the company filed a petition for commencement of civil rehabilitation proceedings.
Under the supervision of the Tokyo District Court, Nippon Denkai will continue its business while seeking a buyer.
Sumitomo Mitsui Banking is providing up to 2 billion yen as DIP financing for the company’s future fund-raising.
Nippon Denkai is being delisted from the Tokyo Stock Exchange.
source_title: ATW News - Printed Circuit Engineering Association Magazine

facts: The full programme for the 3rd Off-Highway & Power Generation USA 2026 conference is now live.
The event features a comprehensive two-day agenda focused on energy-agnostic decarbonisation across non-road machinery and stationary power.
The conference will take place on 20–21 May 2026 at the Sheraton Indianapolis City Centre Hotel.
The event will convene regulators, OEMs, fuel specialists, powertrain engineers, and technology providers to examine meaningful emissions reduction in practice.
Delegates can review the confirmed speaker line-up and detailed session structure spanning ICE, electrification, renewable fuels, and power generation technologies.

Off-highway machinery remains heavily reliant on diesel, yet alternatives like electrification, hybridisation, biofuels, and e-fuels are advancing rapidly with technical, economic, and lifecycle considerations.
Power generation is under renewed scrutiny due to exponential energy demand growth from data centres and AI infrastructure.
The 2026 programme addresses performance, durability, regulation, and commercial viability trade-offs rather than focusing on a single technology.
Day One of the conference focuses on Regulation, Powertrains, and Electrification.

Day One speakers include Kim Heroy-Rogalski (Chief, Mobile Source Regulatory Development Branch at California Air Resources Board), Allen Schaeffer (Executive Director of Engine Technology Forum), and Dr Graham Conway (Director, Industrial Sustainability at Opportune LLP).
Powertrain development contributions feature Professor Dr Greg Shaver (Purdue University), Dr Danan Dou (John Deere Power Systems), and Chris Sharp (Southwest Research Institute).
Afternoon session speakers include representatives from Proventia, Cambustion, Volvo Construction Equipment, Ymer Technology, and Convergent Science.
Sessions will address topics including hydrogen combustion NOx variability, advanced exhaust aftertreatment, thermal management for electrification, and simulation-led optimisation tools.
Day Two shifts focus to Power Generation Under Pressure and decarbonisation.

Day Two confirmed contributors include representatives from Johnson Matthey, Rolls-Royce Solutions America Inc., Cummins Inc., Dinex Emission Inc., EPRI, Neste US, Innospec Fuel Specialities, and Kaizen Clean Energy.

Day Two sessions will examine the evolution of the stationary power market, emissions from gensets in data centre applications, emerging fuels and gas turbines, renewable diesel as an immediate carbon reduction pathway, dispatchable off-grid microgrids, and catalyst technologies ready for future fuels.
A concluding leaders’ panel will address how the power generation industry can make meaningful emissions reductions while meeting urgent customer demand.
The conference was launched to provide a neutral meeting place for non-road stakeholders during polarised energy debates.
The event is organised by Emissions Analytics, an independent real-world emissions testing specialist.
Registration is now open for delegates wishing to attend in Indianapolis on 20–21 May 2026.
Delegate passes include access to all presentations, electronic copies of materials, refreshments throughout, and an evening networking reception.
Group bookings are available for the conference.

Emissions Analytics is an independent global leader in real-world emissions testing and analysis, providing evidence-led insight across tyres, powertrains, and fuels to support industry and policymakers.
source_title:
Off-Highway & Power Generation USA 2026 Programme Now Live as Industry Prepares to Meet in Indianapolis This May
— Emissions Analytics

facts: Down to Earth Trailers (D2E) is centered in Baxley, Georgia.
Southern Trailer Depot in Douglas, Georgia, provides Down to Earth Trailers as part of its cargo trailer inventory.
Since its inception in 1999, Down to Earth Trailers has built cargo haulers for customers across the nation.
Down to Earth Trailers manufactures a wide range of car trailers, ATV trailers, landscape trailers, utility trailers, gooseneck trailers, and enclosed cargo trailers.
Down to Earth trailers are designed as workhorses for landscaping businesses, car enthusiasts, and general hauling needs.
The company operates with a strict code of ethics and practices honest business and integral operations.
Down to Earth builds its trailers to withstand heavy use and ensure longevity through quality craftsmanship and top-tier components.
Down to Earth utility trailers are ideal for plumbers, tradesmen, lawn care businesses, and general hauling needs.
Utility trailer options include 6x12 single axles, 7x16 tandems, and larger sizes.
Down to Earth utility trailers feature open-air designs with rear loading ramps and tie-down points for easy loading and securement.
Customers can add compartments to utility trailers to store smaller items like cords, trimmers, and gas tanks.
Down to Earth landscape/dump trailers are designed for hauling loose elements like dirt, gravel, mulch, and yard debris.
The dump trailers feature a smooth tilt and containment design with solid side walls built to a standard 24-inch height.
The axles on Down to Earth dump trailers come equipped with a built-in braking system to handle heavy loads.
Dump trailer options include various bed sizes and power lift configurations.
Down to Earth expanded its lineup to offer enclosed cargo trailers with a range of sizes and payload capacities.
Enclosed cargo trailers are constructed to strict standards using top-of-the-line materials for excellence and longevity.
Enclosed trailer customization options include additional tie-downs, lighting upgrades, extra payload capacity, and storage options.

facts: Eaton Corp. announced a $50-plus million investment to expand U.S. manufacturing capacity in Virginia.
The expansion positions the power-management firm to meet surging data-center demand driven by rapid AI and cloud growth.
The company will build a 350,000-square-foot facility near Richmond to produce critical power-distribution equipment for data centers.
Eaton aims to boost output and create about 200 new local jobs starting in 2026.
Eaton will manufacture static transfer switches, power distribution units, and remote power panels at the new site.
The expanded capacity responds to a record number of new data-center approvals in Virginia this year.
The new campus will more than double Eaton's footprint in the Richmond area.

Consolidating and upgrading production across static power infrastructure aims to support data centers handling increasing AI-driven workloads and deliver scalable power solutions to North American customers.
Since 2023, Eaton has invested over $1.2 billion in North American manufacturing.
The Virginia investment reflects the company's broader push to support rising demand for electrification and data infrastructure.
Virginia Gov. Glenn Youngkin welcomed the announcement, stating it underscores local companies' readiness to meet growing power requirements.
Henrico's economic development officials added that the project strengthens the region's manufacturing base and job market.

The company focuses on electrification, digitalization, and energy transition trends to drive growth across mobility end markets.
Eaton is making significant investments in research and development driving advancements in energy storage, electrical vehicle charging infrastructure, and renewable energy integration.

Arnold joined Eaton in 2000 as Vice President and General Manager of the Cutler-Hammer's electrical business unit.
Craig Arnold was appointed President and Chief Operating Officer (COO) of Eaton Corporation in 2015.
Paulo Ruiz became President and Chief Executive Officer effective June 1, 2025.

Core business segments include Electrical Americas, Electrical Global, Aerospace, Vehicle, and eMobility.
Electrical Americas and Electrical Global focus on regional electrical products, systems, and services for North America and international regions.
Aerospace, Vehicle, and eMobility are grouped under an Industrial/ Mobility-focused leadership structure that consolidates mechanical and transportation businesses.

The Mobility Group comprises the Vehicle and eMobility segments, including the Roadranger division.
Eaton produces clutches, automated and mechanical transmissions, and hybrid power systems.

Eaton's hybrid power systems are mounted between the UltraShift automated manual transmission and clutch, featuring an electric motor/generator connected to a power inverter using lithium-ion batteries, controlled with an electronic control module.
The system has a fail-safe that reverts to conventional engine-powered operation should some fault occur.
Eaton's truck segment is involved in the design, manufacture and marketing of powertrain systems and other components for commercial vehicle markets.
Key products for the truck segment include manual and automated transmissions, clutches, drive-line components, and hybrid power.

Eaton's automotive segment produces superchargers, engine valves, valve train components, cylinder heads, locking and limited-slip differentials, heavy-duty drive-line components, fuel, emissions, and safety controls, transmission and engine controls, spoilers, exterior moldings, plastic components, and fluid connectors.
Eaton's revenue was US$24.88 billion in 2024.
Eaton had 94,000 employees as of 2024.
source_title: Eaton Corporation - Wikipedia

facts: Eaton Corporation is a power management company that works with customers on efficiency, safety, and sustainability.
The company was founded by Joseph O. Eaton, Henning O. Taube, and Viggo V. Torbenson as the Torbenson Gear and Axle Co. in 1911.
The Torbenson Axle Company was incorporated in 1916, and a year later its largest customer, Republic Motor Truck Company, bought out the company.
In 1923, Eaton bought the company back from Republic, changing the name to Eaton Axle and Spring Company.

Eaton's diversified operations include electrical products, systems and services for power transmission, distribution, and control, lighting products, wiring products, hydraulics for industrial and mobile equipment, pneumatic systems for commercial and military use, and powertrain systems.
Eaton is publicly traded on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol ETN and is a member of the S&P 500.
The company ranks #194 in the Fortune 500.
Eaton has over 100,000 employees.
Eaton reported $22.23 billion in revenue in 2013.
Eaton's corporate headquarters is located at 2000 Apollo Dr, Cleveland, OH 44142-4102.
The CEO of Eaton Corporation is Alexander M. Cutler.
source_title: Eaton Corporate Office: Phone, Address & HQ | CorporateOfficeHQ

facts: Eaton will display technologies to support the zero emissions journey in the commercial vehicle market Sept. 20-25 at the IAA Transportation show in Hannover, Germany.

Scott Adams, president of Eaton’s eMobility business, states that Eaton provides a vast portfolio of EV technologies for full battery electric, fuel cell, and hybrid commercial vehicles, as well as EV charging infrastructure solutions.
Eaton’s eMobility business offers a new Flex Power Distribution Unit (PDU) for high-voltage electrified commercial vehicles designed to handle multiple load requirements.

The Flex PDU integrates Eaton’s Breaktor® circuit protection technology, which improves vehicle safety, protects components from overcurrent conditions, is resettable like a circuit breaker, and reduces cost for both OEMs and consumers.

The Flex PDU leverages sealed and corrosion-resistant terminals and connectors from Royal Power Solutions, a global leader in power distribution and transmission solutions that Eaton acquired earlier this year.
Eaton’s Bussmann® series fuses are integrated into the Flex PDU to meet requirements for high-powered electrified vehicles with ratings up to 1,000 volts of direct current (VDC) and 600 amps.
The Flex PDU provides OEM diagnostic information on power consumption to maximize performance and efficiency, while its integrated design reduces component count and complexity.

Eaton’s new 4-speed transmissions for medium- and heavy-duty electrified commercial vehicles provide superior performance on grades and acceleration, offer flexible gear ratios, improve system efficiency, and enable longer vehicle range and battery life.
Adams notes that Eaton supplies transmissions for multiple global commercial vehicle manufacturers and continues to build on its heritage as a top supplier of commercial vehicle transmissions.
Eaton recently introduced a 48-volt DC-DC converter to power accessories such as antilock brakes and lighting for commercial vehicles with 48-volt electrical systems.
Eaton’s DC-DC converters are operational in ambient temperatures up to 85 degrees Celsius and boast 97% design efficiency.
The DC-DC converter portfolio covers low- to high-voltage systems and can be tuned for particular duty cycles via advanced digital control architecture and firmware adaptation.
In March 2021, Eaton acquired Switzerland-based Green Motion SA, a leading designer and manufacturer of electric vehicle charging hardware and related software.

Eaton’s EV charging infrastructure expertise includes the production and operation of charging stations, designing management software for charging networks, and serving as an electric mobility service provider.
Eaton’s “Buildings as a Grid” strategy helps fleet managers devise fast-to-implement, cybersecure, and scalable EV charging strategies that can add more capacity when needed.
The strategy may include on-site EV charging infrastructure, on-site renewables like solar panels, and options for fleet drivers to charge at home or while traveling.
Eaton transforms workplaces or depots into energy hubs through digital and physical infrastructure, serving as a gateway to electrifying site functionality and investing in the energy transition.
Eaton was founded in 1911, has been listed on the NYSE for nearly a century, reported revenues of $19.6 billion in 2021, and serves customers in more than 170 countries.
source_title: Eaton to showcase innovative electrified commercial vehicle technologies at IAA Transportation show - Korea Newswire

facts: Eaton Corporation plc announced a proposed transaction to combine its Mobility Group with Dana Incorporated in a Reverse Morris Trust transaction.
Definitive agreements were dated June 10, 2026, and customer FAQs describing leadership plans were posted on June 11, 2026.
The combined automotive and commercial-vehicle business is expected to generate approximately $11 billion in revenues.
Eaton expects the transaction to close in Quarter 1, 2027, subject to customary regulatory approvals, Dana shareholder approval, and other closing conditions.
The companies will operate independently until closing, with the Mobility Group planned to transition to the Dana brand upon completion.
Eaton's Mobility Group is valued at about $5.1 billion, while the combined company is valued at over $10 billion.
The combined business is expected to generate roughly $11 billion in 2026 pro forma revenue and $1.7 billion in estimated adjusted EBITDA.
Run-rate cost synergies are estimated at $250 million.
At closing, Eaton will receive approximately $1.1 billion in cash distribution.
Eaton shareholders are expected to own at least 50.1% of the combined company's shares.
The deal is intended to be tax-free for U.S. federal income tax purposes.
Leadership appointments for the combined company include Byron Foster as incoming Chief Executive Officer and Timothy Kraus as Chief Financial Officer.
Erin Rowse will serve as Chief Human Resources Officer upon close.
Dana's eight-member board will be expanded to include three additional directors designated by Eaton.
The combined Mobility/Dana company will create a larger engineered solutions provider for commercial and light vehicle OEMs and aftermarket channels.
The transaction is framed as part of Eaton’s 2030 growth strategy.
After closing, Eaton will focus on its Electrical and Aerospace businesses.
The combined company will operate as Dana Incorporated and continue to trade under the ticker DAN.
Upcoming SEC filings include a Form 10, Form S-1/S-4, Schedule TO, and Form S-4.
Company statements direct employees to virtual information sessions and a Mobility Group Transition Hub for updates.
The communication includes customary forward-looking cautionary language about approvals, integration risks, financing, and other uncertainties.
source_title: ETN SEC Filings - Eaton 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K Forms

facts: The U.S. government is investing heavily to enable far more resilient and sustainable infrastructures.

With the passage of the American Rescue Plan Act (ARPA), Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA) and Inflation Reduction Act (IRA), there are unprecedented opportunities—through direct funds, grants, tax credits, tax incentives and rebates—for organizations to maximize the return on investment (ROI) of critical infrastructure and clean energy projects.
Navigating the intricacies of federal funding and tax incentive mechanisms can be a formidable challenge for those unfamiliar with the procedures and processes.
Qualified clean energy projects that utilize U.S. equipment and materials have the potential to earn bonus tax credits of up to 10%.

Eaton was awarded more than $26.6 million in investment tax credits from the U.S. Internal Revenue Service as part of the Qualifying Advanced Energy Project Tax Credit (48C) funded by the Inflation Reduction Act.

These tax credits were awarded for Eaton’s manufacturing investments totaling more than $200 million to support clean energy projects and workforce training in Nacogdoches and El Paso, Texas, and Waukesha, Wisconsin.
As an experienced Government contractor, Eaton prides itself on ethical conduct and doing the right thing for its customers and suppliers.
Eaton has implemented a robust government compliance program focused on ensuring compliance with all applicable Government procurement laws and regulations.
source_title: Build America, Buy America Act | Domestic Preference | Eaton

facts: Birmingham-based Five South Architecture will lead the design of Ecoplastic America’s new manufacturing facility.
The new manufacturing facility is located in Statesboro, Georgia.
Ecoplastic America is a major supplier for the new Hyundai Metaplant near Savannah, Georgia.

facts: EcoPlastic is investing a large amount of capital to build an electric vehicle (EV) parts factory dedicated to EVs in Georgia, USA.
The company plans to raise the necessary funds through loans and capital increases.
Once the US factory begins full-scale operations in October next year, it is estimated that the annual sales of the US subsidiary will exceed 300 billion KRW by 2025.
EcoPlastic will issue 7 million new shares through a rights offering followed by a general public offering of forfeited shares.
The planned issue price per share is 3,890 KRW, raising a total of 27.2 billion KRW.
For every one existing share, 0.21 new shares will be allocated.
The largest shareholder, Seojin Automotive, plans to subscribe to 100% of the allocated shares using bank loans or its own funds.
All raised funds will be invested in 'EcoPlastic America.'
In line with the US government's strong EV industry promotion policies, EcoPlastic will build an EV parts factory in Georgia.
The Georgia factory will have an annual capacity of 300,000 units.
The total expected investment amount is 254.9 billion KRW.
Of the total investment, 173 billion KRW will be raised through loans, and the remainder will be invested using internal funds and capital raised through stock issuance.
Hyundai Motor Company is EcoPlastic's largest customer.
Hyundai has set a goal to complete its dedicated EV factory in the US by 2025.
Hyundai has selected EcoPlastic as its partner for entering the US EV market.
EcoPlastic supplies 60% of Hyundai's plastic bumpers for the domestic market.
Hyundai accounted for 68% of EcoPlastic's sales in the first half of this year.
Projected sales of the US subsidiary are 313.9 billion KRW in 2025, 476.3 billion KRW thereafter, and 601.1 billion KRW after that.
EcoPlastic expects to grow into a major affiliate with the US subsidiary sales surge starting in 2025.
On a consolidated basis, EcoPlastic recorded sales of 1.0173 trillion KRW in the first half of this year, an 18.2% increase compared to the same period last year.
Once EV parts production begins in the US, EcoPlastic's sales growth rate is expected to accelerate further.
EcoPlastic's large-scale borrowing for investment is considered a risk factor.
As of the first half of this year, EcoPlastic's debt ratio reached 407.5%.
Total borrowings amount to 280 billion KRW and have steadily increased since 2020.
Interest expenses have been rising: 8.7 billion KRW in 2020, 8.8 billion KRW in 2021, and 11.1 billion KRW in 2022.
Due to the impact of interest rate hikes, interest expenses exceeded 7 billion KRW in the first half of this year.
Considering the operating profit of 38.5 billion KRW recorded last year, the interest expense is not insignificant.
EcoPlastic's operating profit margin has been increasing: 1.0% in 2021, 2.1% in 2022, and 3.5% in the first half of this year.
During the same period, the cost of goods sold ratio decreased from 95.8% to 93.4%.
Due to large sales volume, profitability improvements lead to significant profit increases, though parts manufacturers face difficulty with dramatic margin improvements.
EcoPlastic has signed standby underwriting agreements with KB Securities, Shinhan Investment Corp., and others to ensure no disruptions in raising investment funds.
If forfeited shares occur after the general public offering, a forfeiture fee of 7.0% may be incurred.
If Hyundai produces and sells EVs locally in the US as planned, EcoPlastic's sales and profits are expected to increase, enabling stable growth.
source_title: EcoPlastic Follows Hyundai Motor into the U.S. Market with a 255 Billion KRW Investment Plan - The Asia Business Daily

facts: Since May, Ecoplastic America has launched its careers website, hit the ground running with community outreach events, and started training its team in everything from safety and paint to leadership development.
Kevin Campbell is Ecoplastic America's new Talent Acquisition and Public Affairs Manager who started working for the company this spring.
Ecoplastic America is hosting open interviews starting Tuesday, June 25, 2024, from 2pm to 6pm at its Statesboro office located at 25 Siebald Street in Downtown Statesboro.
The Statesboro office is located beside the Judicial Annex, behind the Courthouse.
Applicants must apply for the 'Production Team Member' job post on the company's website before attending the open interviews.
Ecoplastic America will continue holding open interviews every Tuesday from 2pm to 6pm at the Statesboro office following June 25, 2024.
Production Team Members will work hand-in-hand with experienced Production Supervisors to ensure production lines run smoothly and efficiently.
Sabrina Vasher is Ecoplastic America's Director of Human Resources.
The company is seeking candidates who can perform administrative tasks, provide manufacturing support, have specialized skills in some areas, and are willing to learn.
Ecoplastic America is committed to building a team and then training them to perform at their highest potential.
Ecoplastic America's team attended Georgia Southern University's inaugural CPE Leadership Conference in May.
The conference featured dynamic discussions on talent retention, diversity, effective communication, boosting morale, and optimizing performance.
Dr. Brent Stubbs, Senior Manager of Learning & Development at Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, presented at the conference.
Justin Farquhar, Vice President of Workforce Savannah Economic Development Authority, presented at the conference.

Sabrina Vasher shared her expertise alongside Jamie Calloway, HR Manager for Ajin-Joon of Georgia, in a panel discussion on HR dynamics in Korean-owned Tier 1 Hyundai supplier companies in South Georgia.
The Ecoplastic America team has completed specialized robotics paint operations training by Sames.
The team has also completed safety training by Georgia Quick Start.
Ecoplastic America views its commitment to training team members as part of the company's culture and vision for the future.
Maurice Williams is Ecoplastic America's Senior Manager of Paint Operations.
Thomas Kim is the CEO of Ecoplastic America.
Creating a collaborative, safety-conscious, and engaged team with a positive work culture continues to be a top priority for the company.
Ecoplastic America will be hiring 500 employees from the area.
The company is investing more than $205 million in its Bulloch County facility.
All positions are located in Bulloch County, Georgia.
source_title: Ecoplastic America's Careers website is LIVE and they can't wait to meet new candidates!  - Grice Connect

facts: Not all of the 8,500 employees expected to work at Hyundai Motor Group’s Metaplant America will be working on the first day of production.
Almost 6,000 additional jobs are projected for electric vehicle and battery manufacturing suppliers across Georgia.
Area economic development officials expect at least a five-year ramp-up to reach full job creation numbers.
Trip Tollison, president and CEO of the Savannah Economic Development Authority, stated it will be a five- to seven-year hire-up process.
The plant is not expected to hire 14,000 people in one year.
During a tour of the construction site in northern Bryan County on Oct. 25, 2023, HMG Metaplant CEO and President Oscar Kwon said the plant is on track to start production in early 2025.
Hyundai Motor Company Global President and Chief Operating Officer Josè Muñoz added that production could start sooner than early 2025.
The groundbreaking ceremony for the project took place on Oct. 25, 2022.
The complex is called a “meta” plant because it will include several HMG-affiliated suppliers and another corporation’s battery factory alongside the vehicle assembly plant.
Officials say the facility may produce electric vehicles under Hyundai, Kia, Genesis, and Ioniq labels.

The Savannah Economic Development Authority, based in Chatham County, and development authorities in Bulloch, Bryan, and Effingham counties participate in the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority (JDA).
The JDA, together with the state of Georgia, purchased and provided the site for the project.
The incentive package is the largest in Georgia’s history and includes $2.1 billion worth of tax breaks.
Eric Johnson, a retired architect and former state senator, serves as project director at the site for the JDA and the state.
When first announced in May 2022, the HMG plant was predicted to create about 8,100 jobs through a $5.54 billion investment.
The addition of an on-site battery plant in a joint venture with LG Energy Solution adds 400 expected jobs.
The total expected investment for the project was boosted to $7.59 billion.
Off-site suppliers represent a further $2.2 billion investment across nine different counties.
The total investment from Hyundai Motor Group and its suppliers is approaching $10 billion.
Parts suppliers are projected to employ about 6,000 workers.
HMG states that the Metaplant and off-site suppliers together will create a total of 14,476 jobs at full production.
Company representatives reported that 290 people have been hired for the HMG Metaplant so far.
The current hires include 182 salaried and 108 hourly employees.
Some hired workers are in training at other HMG plants, including Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama in Montgomery and facilities in South Korea.
The count of 290 hires does not include 53 South Korean nationals assigned to the plant, including CEO Oscar Kwon.
Both HMG and its battery joint venture partner LG Energy Solution are headquartered in South Korea.
Approximately 2,000 contractor employees are currently working to build the manufacturing complex, excluding them from job-creation figures.
The average salary for production jobs, including those at the HMG plant, battery plant, and all affiliates, will be $58,105.
This predicted average salary is 25% higher than the current average Bryan County annual wage of $47,000.
Bulloch County’s comparable average salary is $45,032, based on a $21.65 average hourly wage from Department of Labor reports.
The predicted Metaplant wage is 29% higher than Bulloch County’s average.
As of the article's publication date, the Metaplant’s career job search page listed 95 job descriptions.
A “production team member” job listing stated a salary of from $22.40 per hour.
Benjy Thompson, CEO of the Development Authority of Bulloch County, agreed with the five- to seven-year timeline to achieve full job numbers.
HMG supplier factories are being located in nine counties, including areas far from the four JDA counties and their immediate neighbors.
Supplier announcements have been made in Henry County (McDonough) and Dublin.
Three Hyundai suppliers are building plants in Bulloch County along U.S. Highway 301 south of Statesboro and near the I-16 interchange.
Joon Georgia Inc. is predicted to create 630 jobs.
Ecoplastic America Corporation is projected to create 456 jobs.
Hanon Systems is projected to create 160 jobs.
source_title: Company says Metaplant employees can expect annual pay of $58k - Bryan County News

facts: Annual economic impact of manufacturing in Bulloch County surpassed $1.17 billion in 2023 according to a Georgia Southern University study.
The Georgia Southern University study was commissioned by the Development Authority of Bulloch County.
Manufacturing economic impact in Bulloch County increased by 32 percent from a similar analysis conducted in 2020 prior to the COVID pandemic.
Manufacturing currently supports more than 4,000 jobs in Bulloch County with an average annual salary of $56,339.

Benjy Thompson, CEO of the Development Authority of Bulloch County, stated that the county’s manufacturing sector provides significant value to citizens and that manufacturers invest a great amount of capital in facilities while providing good-paying and well-benefitted jobs.
The Development Authority of Bulloch County partners with Bulloch County, the City of Statesboro, Bulloch County Schools, and education/workforce partners for manufacturer recruitment and support.
Local manufacturing employers included in the analysis are Briggs & Stratton, Great Dane Trailers, Cardinal Glass, and other small and large industrial businesses.
Benjy Thompson noted that additional industry partners coming online in the next few years will grow manufacturing's impact on the county, providing more jobs and economic growth.
Hanon Systems is building a factory in Gateway Regional Industrial Park.
Hanon Systems is one of three tier-one suppliers to Hyundai Motor Group’s Metaplant, which is under construction in Bryan County, that are building facilities in Bulloch County.
Two other non-Hyundai related businesses are also building plants in Bulloch County.
New plants in Bulloch County have job creation commitments totaling a projected 1,600 employees.
Promised capital investments for the new Bulloch County plants total $1 billion.
Aspen Aerogels announced its project on Feb. 17, 2022, with construction nearly complete underway on a 90-acre site in Bruce Yawn Commerce Park.
Aspen Aerogels committed to a $325 million investment and at least 250 manufacturing jobs for its Bulloch County plant.
Aspen Aerogels’ plant will produce aerogel insulation used in and around electric vehicle batteries, though it is not linked to the Hyundai factory.

Ecoplastic America Corporation, a supplier of injection-molded plastic automotive body parts to Hyundai Motor Group, intends to build a $205 million plant on a site owned by the DABC south of Statesboro.
Ecoplastic America promises to create 456 jobs phased in over eight years, with site preparation currently underway.
Ajin USA, a supplier of metal auto body parts to Hyundai, committed to invest $317 million and eventually employ 630 people at its Joon Georgia plant.
Ajin USA’s 83-acre site is located beside the Aspen Aerogels site in Bruce Yawn Commerce Park.
revalyu Resources, a Germany-headquartered company not connected to Hyundai, plans to build a PET plastic-bottle recycling plant on a 43-acre site in Gateway Regional Industrial Park.
revalyu Resources is expected to create 71 jobs and require a $50 million investment in Phase 1, which is the only phase counted in current totals.
Hanon Systems, Ecoplastic America Corporation, and Ajin USA are all tier-one suppliers because they deal directly with Hyundai Motor Group.
A tier-two supplier provides materials to a tier-one supplier, and subsequent tiers supply further down the chain.
Manufacturers in Bulloch County provide significant revenue to both Bulloch County and Bulloch County schools.
Gateway Regional Industrial Park, which includes several manufacturing facilities and a Walmart Distribution Center, paid more than $3.5 million in property taxes in 2023.
source_title: Manufacturing impact in Bulloch exceeds $1 billion - Statesboro Herald

facts: Bulloch County Commissioners voted to finalize a 15-year tax abatement for a manufacturer coming to Bulloch County as part of the Hyundai Meta Plant.
EcoPlastic America Corp, a Korean based company, announced its intention to come to Bulloch County in January of this year.
The new facility will offer 456 jobs to the community.
The facility is located at 4822 Highway 301 South.
The project is reportedly a $205 million investment.
EcoPlastic America Corp is a supplier to Hyundai and Kia.
According to Area Development, the company currently employs more than 770 employees and recorded sales revenue of more than $700 million in 2021.
Commissioners approved a tax abatement structure of 100% for ten years followed by a 50% abatement for five years after that.
The Bulloch County Development Authority agreed to provide a $2.28 million discount on the $4.312 million property.
BCDA is slated to apply for a $1.6 million grant from the ONEGeorgia Edge Grant program for the project.
The project includes $30,000 allocated for training future EcoPlastic America Corp employees, which must take place at Ogeechee Technical College.
BCDA will provide a temporary office for EcoPlastic America Corp for a cost up to $15,000 if requested by the company.
Prior to the company taking on the property, the Development Authority conducted a $56,565 wetlands delineation study.
The company is not expected to reach its full 456 jobs until years 8-15 in Bulloch County.
Employment for years one through seven will scale gradually from 80 employees to 435.
The Development Authority entered into an MOU with EcoPlastic America Corp for the location of a manufacturing project in Bulloch County.
The facility will manufacture automotive exterior and interior parts including bumpers, garnishes, consoles and trims for electric vehicles.

The MOU parties are the Development Authority and EcoPlastic America Corp, with requests made to the Board of Commissioners and Board of Tax Assessors to acknowledge the MOU and agree to applicable provisions.
The MOU includes a 100% abatement of County ad valorem taxes for 10 years and a 50% abatement for an additional 5 years as an inducement for location.
The tax abatement excludes school taxes and taxes for the County’s fire district.
County attorney Jeff Akins stated that the MOU is similar to incentives offered to other manufacturers recently.
Bulloch County Development Authority Chair Billy Allen welcomed EcoPlastic, noting confidence that their location, workforce, and quality of life will help them succeed.
Commissioners approved the abatement unanimously with no discussion after staff recommended approval.
source_title: Commissioners Approve 100% Tax Abatement for New Manufacturer in Bulloch County • The Georgia Virtue

facts: Ecoplastic Corp. will build a new automotive parts plant in Bulloch County.
The project represents a $205 million investment.
The plant will create 456 jobs.
Gov. Brian Kemp announced the project on Thursday.
The plant will supply the Hyundai electric vehicle manufacturing facility being built near Savannah.
Ecoplastic was established in 1984.
The company produces plastic automobile parts for original equipment manufacturers and other suppliers.
Products include bumpers, consoles, trims, and plastic molds.
Ecoplastic expects to begin operations by October of next year.
The company will be filling positions in human resources, finance, parts development, quality control, facility maintenance, injection, and paint production.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development worked with the Development Authority of Bulloch County, the Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia EMC, and the Technical College System of Georgia to land the project.
Two months ago, auto parts manufacturer Joon Georgia announced it will invest $317 million in a new manufacturing plant in Bulloch County that will create 630 jobs.
Joon Georgia is the Hyundai EV plant’s first confirmed supplier.

Gov. Brian Kemp said, “We’re proud to welcome Ecoplastic to our growing ecosystem of manufacturers, logistics professionals, suppliers, and more, building on the record-breaking jobs and investments that are on their way to the Peach State.”
Gov. Brian Kemp added, “This great company … will find a welcoming community of hardworking Georgians and close proximity to our ports system.”
source_title: Hyundai lure continues: Bulloch County lands second auto parts supplier - The Current

The company operates a plant in Dublin, GA.
Parts are shipped from the Dublin, GA plant to OEM and Tier 1 customers in the US, Canada, and Mexico.
Erdrich USA is part of the Erdrich Group, a Germany-based enterprise with five plants in Germany, the Czech Republic, China, and the USA.

facts: Erdrich Umformtechnik is one of the world’s leading manufacturers of complex metal parts and subassemblies for the automotive industry.
Erdrich contracted with Evans to design-build their first U.S. based production facility in Dublin, Georgia.
The 123,000 sf building is composed of a structural steel frame and concrete tilt-up walls wrapped in insulated metal panels with TPO roofing and ESFR sprinkler systems.
The structure includes 95’ and 120’ spans with four 15’ deep press pits and 500 linear feet of conveyor supply trenches.
Inside the fully air-conditioned facility is approximately 100,000 sf production and assembly space featuring two mezzanines.
The remaining 23,000 sf is used for office and administrative purposes.
Nine overhead cranes were installed throughout the manufacturing areas to aid in metal forming and assembly operations.
Scope of work included design-build services, shell building construction with finishes, and a comprehensive site work package.

Holds a 4.7-star overall rating from 9 reviewers.
Former employee Richard Shontell experienced a layoff with no warning on January 22, 2020.

Assistant Fire Chief Rick Browning of Faught Col. Fire Dept. in Lamar County, Texas, reported that his volunteer fire department operates three Fouts Brothers fire trucks, two tankers, and one rescue truck.

facts: Fouts Fire offers fire tankers and pumpers factory-direct in Georgia with immediate delivery lead times to any Georgia fire department.

William “Junior” Ingram serves as the factory-direct specialist and fire sales manager for Fouts Fire, covering Georgia and several other states including Arkansas, Colorado, Hawai’i, Kansas, Missouri, Nebraska, Nevada, North Carolina, Oklahoma, Utah and the Virgin Islands.

Available apparatus models include 2000 Gallon Tanker, 3000 Gallon Tanker, 4000 Gallon Tanker, Fouts FOUR, Commercial Crew Pumper, FB-94 Pumper, Light Duty Rescue, Medium Duty Rescue, and Heavy Duty Rescue.
Fouts Fire delivers standardized fire apparatuses engineered to handle the operational demands of fire stations in Georgia for urban, suburban, and wildland situations.
The company addresses escalating fire equipment costs by providing new trucks that are dependable without compromising quality.
Pricing information and full unit specifications can be obtained by filling out a “Request a Quote” form on any apparatus page.

For areas with limited municipal water access in Georgia, high-capacity tankers provide reliable water supply, while compact mini pumpers are ideal for rapid deployment across varied terrain including urban and rural areas.
Crew cab and commercial chassis pumpers deliver cost-effective, dependable performance for everyday firefighting operations in Georgia.
Fouts Fire apparatus meet or exceed National Fire Protection Association (NFPA), Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), and Georgia state standards.
New fire apparatus are delivered directly to specified delivery locations throughout Georgia through William “Junior” Ingram.
Fouts Fire leads the United States as a high-performance fire apparatus manufacturer, delivering emergency vehicles renowned for durability, reliability, and consistent production timelines.
The company provides standardized fire apparatus designed to meet operational demands while upholding high industry standards of quality and performance.

Georgia fire departments and fire chiefs consistently choose Fouts Fire for efficient manufacturing, reliable delivery, transparent production tracking, engineering solutions balancing operational effectiveness with cost-conscious design, NFPA-compliant construction with EPA-certified engine systems, simplified procurement through factory-direct sales, and extensive support infrastructure for parts availability, maintenance, and ongoing fleet care.
