facts: BRPH is an expert in complex manufacturing facilities for the world’s most innovative manufacturers and is known for its decades of specialized aviation expertise.
BRPH is consistently ranked nationally among manufacturing design firms.

BRPH has served a growing list of industries, emphasizing companies requiring clean and secure manufacturing environments, automation, Industry 4.0 technology integration, and specialized process and tooling equipment installation.
BRPH's portfolio includes operations centers ranging from 10,000 square feet to 1.3 million-square-foot assembly buildings.
BRPH designs a host of ancillary facilities for research and development, warehousing and supply chain integration, and customer delivery centers.

BRPH supports the development of greenfield facilities from site selection and master planning through process layout, concept development, full design, engineering, and construction, including projects executed on a fast-track and design/build basis.
BRPH's manufacturing experts and industrial engineers consult on process flow improvement and facility consolidations to improve efficiency and unlock capacity in ways that advance the bottom line.
BRPH teams pay close attention to production efficiency, energy consumption, and facility configurations that allow for future expansion.
BRPH designers strive to help clients create safe, attractive, and engaging workplaces to address growing issues in manufacturing sector workforce development and employee retention.

Rich Winters, President of FOX Factory, Inc., stated that BRPH worked with their team members to design a 'Factory of the Future' to allow process flexibility as manufacturing evolved to meet future market demands, and that BRPH always provided services with the highest level of professionalism and responsiveness.
source_title: BRPH - Manufacturing

facts: A European provider of warehousing and automation solutions is set to invest $36 million in a new factory in northwest Georgia.
Stow Group says it will hire 200 workers at the facility in northwest Georgia.
The region's reputation as the “carpet capital of the world” in the 1970s helped make Georgia the No. 1 destination for Belgian firms.
Georgia set up an office in Brussels under then-Gov. Jimmy Carter, partly in response to flooring investments flocking to the region.
Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson cites the state's strong “manufacturing legacy” and proximity to an inland port as key factors.
Stow’s main customers are warehouses involved in “intralogistics,” which have congregated near the inland port.
Stow’s facility in Gordon County will focus on the “highly automated production” of racking systems as part of customized storage solutions for customers in logistics, food, life sciences, and more.
Plant Manager Michael Morgenroth confirmed the facility's focus in a news release from Gov. Brian Kemp’s office.
The investment does not appear to include Stow’s Movu Inc. autonomous mobile robot division, which lists an Illinois U.S. headquarters.
Back in Belgium, Movu operates out of a separate innovation center opened in 2023 in Lokeren.
The Georgia site will be Stow’s first facility in the United States.
Stow is based in Spiere-Helkijn, Belgium, in the West Flanders province near the French border.
The global company employs 2,000 “Rack Stars” across 11 factories and sales offices in 21 countries.
Stow's international footprint includes 20 locations in Europe and one in Australia.
The 45-year-old company’s products are sold in more than 60 countries.
The investment comes as steel from Europe faces a 50 percent tariff.
CEO Jos De Vuyst stated that expanding production in the U.S. is a key milestone to serve customers faster, enhance local partnerships, and commit to innovation and sustainability.
Three open jobs are currently listed in Georgia, including two at the Adairsville plant and one supply chain and purchasing manager role in Atlanta.
More production roles are to be announced in profiling, stamping, welding, powder coating, automation, and assembly.
Logistics, administrative, and management roles are also on the way.
source_title: European Firm to Invest $36M in Northwest Georgia Factory - Global Atlanta

facts: Hyundai Motor Group is joining forces with a South Korean battery maker to build a $4 billion factory in Georgia to manufacture electric vehicle batteries.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp's office said that the White House does not understand how economic partnerships are made after President Biden touted Hyundai's new manufacturing plant.
President Biden praised Hyundai's investment in Georgia, despite supporting MLB pulling its All-Star Game out of the state because of voting law changes.
Hyundai breaks ground on a $5.5 billion electric car plant in Georgia.
The new $5.5 billion Hyundai plant serves as a political boost for incumbents Gov. Brian Kemp (R-GA) and Sen. Warnock (D-GA), who are facing Stacey Abrams and Herschel Walker respectively.
source_title: Georgia

facts: Fox Factory Holding Corp. is headquartered in Duluth, Georgia.
The company employs approximately 4,100 people globally as of January 2025.
Its largest facility is located in Gainesville, Georgia, spanning 336,000 square feet and serving as a key hub for the Powered Vehicles Group.
The Gainesville facility was completed in late 2021 and encompasses manufacturing, warehousing, distribution, and office functions.
Powered vehicle operations were relocated from Watsonville, California, to the Gainesville, Georgia site in early 2022.

Fox Factory operates 27 plants worldwide—15 in the U.S. for the Aftermarket Applications Group, two in the U.S. and one in Italy for the Powered Vehicles Group, and ten across multiple countries for the Specialty Sports Group—totaling approximately 5.4 million square feet as of January 3, 2025.
The Powered Vehicles Group (PVG) and Aftermarket Applications Group (AAG) accounted for approximately 63% of net sales in fiscal 2024, totaling $882.9 million out of $1.394 billion.
The Specialty Sports Group (SSG) segment represented about 37% of fiscal 2024 net sales at $511.1 million.
Approximately 79% of fiscal 2024 sales ($1.097 billion) were derived from North America, while international markets in Europe (12%) and Asia (8%) show growth potential.
Leadership is headed by Chief Executive Officer Michael C. Dennison, who assumed the role in June 2019 after joining the company in 2018 as President of the Powered Vehicles Group.
The executive team includes Chief Financial Officer Dennis C. Schemm.
Core operations are managed through subsidiaries such as Fox Factory, Inc., and recent additions like Marucci Sports, LLC.

The supply chain prioritizes premium materials such as aluminum, magnesium, steel, and carbon fiber, sourced from a concentrated group of vendors, with 26% of components procured from the top ten suppliers in fiscal 2024.
Fox Factory demonstrates vertical integration through in-house manufacturing of critical technologies, including shock valving and damping systems.

The company's global distribution network reaches over 16,000 retail dealers and distributors worldwide, facilitating sales through original equipment manufacturers (44% of 2024 net sales), aftermarket channels (49%), and direct-to-consumer (7%).

Fox Factory serves as an original equipment manufacturer (OEM) supplier to major brands, including Polaris for UTVs like the RZR series, Can-Am for models such as the Maverick, and Ford for the F-150 Raptor since its 2010 debut.
Aftermarket offerings include complete kits for vehicles like Jeep Wranglers and Toyota Tacomas.
The company's Live Valve electronic damping technology uses sensors to adjust compression and rebound in real-time by sampling terrain and driver inputs up to 400 times per second.
In 2020, Fox Factory introduced Intelligent Quick Switch (iQS), an electronic system for UTVs that enables on-the-fly preload and damping adjustments via a dashboard switch.
In 2023, Fox Factory acquired Custom Wheel House for $131.6 million, incorporating brands such as Method Race Wheels and Tensor Tires.

In November 2023, Fox Factory acquired Marucci Sports for an enterprise value of $572 million, with the brand generating $192 million in 2024 sales, representing approximately 14% of the company's total $1.39 billion revenue for the fiscal year.
The Gainesville, Georgia facility achieved ISO 14001 certification for environmental management in 2022 and maintained it through 2023 audits, alongside ISO 45001 for occupational health and safety.
These measures align with broader compliance to regulations from the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and California Air Resources Board (CARB).
Fox Factory was founded in 1974 by Bob Fox in Scotts Valley, California.
The company went public via an IPO on NASDAQ under ticker FOXF in August 2013, selling 9,857,143 shares at $15 each for gross proceeds of approximately $147.8 million.
Revenue expanded from $272.7 million in fiscal 2013 to $366.8 million in fiscal 2015.
Corporate headquarters were relocated to Duluth, Georgia, in 2018 to optimize logistics and proximity to key markets.
A Taiwan branch was established in 2011 focused on manufacturing suspension components to shorten supply chains and support Asian production needs.
In 2015, the company formed RFE Holding (Canada) Corp. through acquisitions to expand distribution and manufacturing capabilities in North America.
In 2014, Fox Factory acquired assets of Race Face Performance Products and Easton Cycling for approximately $30.2 million.
Later in 2014, the company acquired assets of Sport Truck USA for approximately $43 million.
In 2015, Fox purchased assets of Marzocchi's mountain bike suspension product line from SR Suntour for an undisclosed amount.
In 2017, Fox acquired assets of Flagship Inc., doing business as Tuscany Motor Company, for $53.4 million in cash.
In 2019, Fox Factory acquired substantially all assets of Ridetech for approximately $14 million, financed via cash and newly issued stock.
In February 2020, Fox Factory announced the acquisition of SCA Performance Holdings, Inc., completed later that quarter for $328 million plus $13 million in contingent performance-based payments.
Fox Factory positions its bicycle suspension as a premium OEM and aftermarket solution, supplying components to leading brands such as Specialized, Trek, and Santa Cruz.
In December 2024, Fox Factory acquired Marzocchi, reviving the brand to offer additional mountain bike suspension products including forks and shocks that complement the FOX lineup.
Core operations prioritize segment-specific autonomy while maintaining centralized oversight from headquarters in Duluth, Georgia.
source_title: Fox Factory — Grokipedia

facts: Gov. Brian Kemp and Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson announced that Tift County’s Brian Marlowe will be the new deputy commissioner for rural Georgia.
Marlowe has been the president and CEO of the Tift County Development Authority.
Marlowe will lead the Governor’s Rural Strike Team, an initiative the governor announced last year to improve the economy in rural parts of the state.
The initiative aims to market large rural industrial sites to new businesses.
The effort involves creating specific marketing plans for a site and targeting specific industries.
The program includes training local leaders in business recruitment.
State rankings show Georgia's least developed areas, based on poverty rates, unemployment rates, and income, are overwhelmingly rural.
The exception to the rural development ranking is Clarke, Clayton, and Dougherty counties.
Almost all of those less-developed counties are located south of Interstate 20.
Gov. Kemp stated he believes they can land big projects in rural communities across the state.
Kemp expressed confidence that Marlowe will help continue to move the needle in rural Georgia.
Kemp was elected in 2018 with overwhelming support from rural, white voters.
The announcement coincided with Republican efforts to maintain their majority in the state House, including targeting a handful of rural districts.
Marlowe has led business recruiting efforts in Tift County for 10 years.
Before his role in Tift County, Marlowe worked in Grady County.
Marlowe was a member of Kemp’s transition team.
Kemp cited Marlowe's advocacy for improved internet service, health care, and worker training.
Marlowe will be Wilson’s seventh deputy commissioner.
Wilson cited Marlowe's success in Tifton, including Coca Cola Bottling Co. United's decision to locate a $60 million, 200-job distribution facility there.
Wilson stated they needed someone with a passion for growing rural communities and providing hope and opportunity.
Marlowe is scheduled to start his new role on Dec. 1.
Wilson was unsure whether Marlowe’s office would be located in Atlanta or somewhere else.
source_title: Gov. Kemp announces leader for rural Georgia economic development effort | FOX 5 Atlanta

facts: The Georgia Transportation Alliance, an affiliate of the Georgia Chamber of Commerce, selected Joseph Santoro to serve as its new Executive Director.
Joseph Santoro starts his new position on December 6 after a four-year tenure with the Council for Quality Growth.
Prior to this appointment, Santoro served as the Director of Policy & Government Affairs for the Council for Quality Growth.

Over the last four years at the Council for Quality Growth, Santoro worked on local, regional, and state policies related to infrastructure planning, legislative initiatives, regulatory issues, land use, transportation, and environmental planning impacting the development and growth industry.
Santoro has over 10 years of government affairs experience working for membership-based organizations in metro Atlanta evaluating local, state, and federal policy priorities.
Santoro earned a B.S. in Public Policy from the Georgia Institute of Technology.
Santoro has been a Georgia Chamber Government Affairs Committee member since 2014 through various roles.
Santoro is a 2023 graduate of the Atlanta Regional Commission Regional Leadership Institute.
Santoro and his wife Kayla live in the City of Atlanta where they recently purchased their first home.

Nick Juliano, Partner at Impact Public Affairs and Chair of the Georgia Transportation Alliance, stated that Georgia is at a critical moment for its transportation industry, particularly regarding freight and logistics.

Chris Clark, President and CEO of the Georgia Chamber of Commerce, noted that Santoro’s broad experience with statewide transportation stakeholders and trusted economic development insight make him an ideal fit for the role.

Chris Clark highlighted that Georgia has extensive surface transportation networks, two world-class ports, and one of the largest railroad systems in the country, which are essential drivers of the state's record-breaking economic success.

The Georgia Transportation Alliance is the state’s only organization focused solely on identifying and promoting long-term transportation solutions specifically connected to economic development and job creation.

The Georgia Transportation Alliance works closely with the Georgia Chamber of Commerce, the Georgia Department of Transportation, and other key stakeholders in Georgia and Washington, D.C., with the goal of ensuring an efficient and safe statewide multi-modal transportation system.

links.context: Website for the Georgia Transportation Alliance

source_title: Economic Development Expert Tapped to Lead Georgia Transportation Alliance - LaGrange CEO

facts: The company operates out of Duluth, Ga.
Fox Factory’s Speciality Sports Group (SSG), which includes its bicycle-product business, reported sales down 8.7% in the first quarter compared to the same period in 2025.
The decline in SSG sales reflects distributor and dealer inventory destocking and a difficult prior-year comparison due to the industry’s first half 2025 order pull-forward.
The $10.5 million or 8.7% decrease applies to Specialty Sports Group (“SSG”) net sales.
The SSG division includes Fox's bicycle business through the Fox, Marzocchi, Ride Concepts, RaceFace, Easton cycling and Lizard Skins brands.
SSG also includes Fox's Marucci business, which sells baseball and softball gear.
Company-wide, Fox’s first quarter sales were up 3.9% to $368.7 million.
The company recorded a net loss for the quarter of $15 million, an improvement on its first quarter 2025 loss of $259.7 million.
Company-wide gross margin was 28.9%, compared to 30.9% in the first quarter of fiscal 2025.
The decrease in gross margin was primarily driven by the net impact of tariffs and shifts in product line mix.
Fox CEO Mike Dennison stated that Q1 would be a tough column for SSG, particularly in bikes, given the strength seen in the first half of the prior year due to industry order pull-forward in 2025.
Dennison noted the bike environment feels much like last year, with channel inventory improved but volatile, and demand signals remaining muted as consumers are cautious.
Dennison highlighted progress on new customer relationships and product expansion, particularly in categories like e-bikes, where they see long-term opportunity.
Dennison commented on the changing landscape in OEMs, stating it is both a challenge and an opportunity for Fox.

Regarding new OEM customers in the bike market, Dennison cited volatility and significant declines among many players, forecasting stable to slightly up sales for Fox due to product diversification across premium categories between e-bikes and normal mountain bikes.

Dennison also mentioned the development of new customers, noting a fairly significant rotation of new players versus traditional players, indicating disruption in the industry that Fox is benefiting from through relationships with new players and their new products.
Dennison mentioned that the Middle East war has disrupted some of its SSG suppliers and customers, without sharing specifics, with impacts expected to be seen in the second-quarter financial report.

Fox stated there is “potential” it will recover tariff costs paid under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA), but uncertainty about timing and size means it is not including potential recovery in forecasts.
source_title: Sales in Fox Factory's SSG unit down 9% in first quarter | Bicycle Retailer and Industry News

facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies has been honored with a 2020 Top Workplaces Award from the Detroit Free Press.
The award represents the third consecutive year since 2018 that employees at Freudenberg-NOK have identified the company as a great place to work.
Freudenberg-NOK runs the business operations for Freudenberg Sealing Technologies in the Americas.
Employees working at the innovative sealing and electric mobility technology company participated in an anonymous survey sponsored by the Detroit Free Press, which resulted in the honor.
They broke into applause at a recent virtual town hall meeting when they learned of the award.
In a difficult, pandemic-influenced year, Freudenberg-NOK continued to improve its survey scores to move up in Top Workplaces ranking.
Matthew Portu serves as President of Freudenberg-NOK.
Matthew Portu stated that the survey results provide welcome testimony from employees that they consider Freudenberg-NOK an employer of choice despite 2020’s extraordinary business challenges.
Matthew Portu noted that the company continued to embrace excellence, flexibility, innovation, diversity and open communications that benefited employees, customers and business partners.
Matthew Portu expressed gratification by the results and achievements of Freudenberg-NOK as reflected in this competition.
Matthew Portu stated that the company will use these survey results to look for new opportunities for improvement and growth.
Freudenberg-NOK ranked 43rd among 55 midsized Michigan companies which participated in the program, moving 11 spots higher on the list compared to its 2019 results.
Freudenberg-NOK has approximately 5,000 employees working at 20 facilities across the Americas.
Small, medium and large companies participate in the Top Workplace competition in separate categories; 150 companies in total were honored with Top Workplace status.
The Detroit Free Press Top Workplaces Program has been a part of the corporate work scene in Michigan for the past 13 years.

Companies are nominated and rated by their employees through an anonymous survey conducted by Energage, a Free Press research partner that tracks key workplace practices that influence how employees feel about their companies.

Freudenberg employees participating in the 2020 survey indicated satisfaction with the company in a variety of areas including culture, benefits and pay, career opportunities, financial stability, customer relationships, leadership and technology.
source_title: Freudenberg-NOK named a top workplace third year in a row | Sealing & Contamination Control Tips

facts: Freudenberg Sealing Technologies develops solutions for electromobility and fuel cells and focuses on the hydrogen economy.
The company's sealing technology is used in cars, buses, trains, airplanes, excavators, pumps, electric toothbrushes, percussion drills, tunnel boring machines, wind turbines, and soda bottles.
Freudenberg Sealing Technologies focuses on Industry 4.0, robotics, digitalization, and automation.
One section of the page states the company employs around 13,000 people worldwide, while another section states it has 51,000 employees globally.
Freudenberg aims to be climate-neutral by 2045.
As a family-owned company, Freudenberg's corporate culture emphasizes diversity, trust, and mutual respect.
The company provides personal development opportunities through its Academy for technical, leadership, or methodological skills.
Employees use the Workday personnel management system to determine how to strengthen their strengths and set the course for their professional careers.
Freudenberg offers flexible work arrangements to accommodate different life stages and career expectations.
Oemer Sucu serves as Manager Finance, Controlling and SCM at Freudenberg Sealing Technologies.
source_title: Careers at Freudenberg

facts: Freudenberg-NOK announced it will support college students designing single-passenger vehicles for the 2015 SAE Supermileage Competition with custom seals and engineering resources.
For a third year, Freudenberg-NOK will support the Supermileage event as both a Gold Sponsor and a component supplier.
The company has engineered a new series of custom Energy Saving Seals (ESS) to fit the latest Briggs & Stratton engine that students use to power their Supermileage cars.
The seals are part of the company’s Low Emission Sealing Solutions (LESS) portfolio and are custom-designed to fit the crankshaft on the small four-cycle engine.
Global automotive manufacturers are intensively focused on improving fuel economy and lowering vehicle emissions, mirroring the goals of the student vehicles.
Luis Lorenzo, senior vice president, Technology and Innovation at Freudenberg-NOK, stated that Supermileage is a prime example of what the auto industry is pursuing.
In last year’s competition, the company supplied ESS seals that reduced friction by 57 percent.
Because a newer engine model will be used in the 2015 competition, Freudenberg-NOK will design and produce new ESS seals to fit the updated single cylinder engine.
Lorenzo added that the company is actively pursuing trends like light weighting and fuel saving technologies through events like Supermileage.

In addition to providing friction-reducing seals, the company will sponsor two students and their vehicle from the top-performing team at the Center for Automotive Research’s Management Briefing Seminars (CAR MBS) in August.

In the 2014 competition, the top team was Concordia University from Montreal, Quebec, Canada, which received the Endurance Award for achieving the highest average fuel economy with at least five complete runs.
Students will have the opportunity to participate in the CAR MBS Student Program with all expenses paid.

Alex Laroche, a Concordia University engineering student and 2014 CAR MBS participant, said Freudenberg-NOK gave them the opportunity to start a relationship with auto industry leaders and build their network.
The 2014 team received publicity after being featured in an interview with Autoline.tv, which was appreciated by their university, association, and sponsors.
SAE International’s Supermileage Competition takes place June 4-5 at Eaton Corp.’s proving grounds in Marshall, Mich.
source_title: Freudenberg-NOK Renews Support For Collegiate Fuel Economy Competition – UnderhoodService

facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies will increase its testing capabilities with the addition of a new materials laboratory at its Findlay, Ohio manufacturing plant.

The new laboratory is part of an overall plant expansion taking place in 2015 and will provide the company’s Fluid Power Division with new material characterization, failure analysis and mechanical property test capabilities.
The laboratory will consolidate and expand an existing engineering test laboratory at the facility.
Dr. Theodore G. Duclos, president of Freudenberg-NOK, stated that research and development are core values at the company and the laboratory investment supports its mission, customers and markets.
The Findlay expansion, announced last month, will add 43,000 square feet to the existing factory.
The new lab will account for approximately 2,000 sq. ft of the total expansion.
The laboratory represents an investment of approximately $450,000 in equipment and building expenses.
The entire project represents an investment of approximately $8.6 million in construction and equipment upgrades.
The expansion will create 25 new jobs at the Findlay plant.
The project is expected to be complete by winter 2015.
The company officially celebrated the expansion with a groundbreaking ceremony attended by executives, elected officials, employees, and media.
The Findlay facility produces approximately 700 plastic parts for customers including vehicle manufacturers and tier one and two suppliers using state-of-the-art manufacturing processes.
The new laboratory will perform analysis and testing of parts the plant is making from plastics.
These parts include thrust washers, seals, and other components used in the automotive industry.
The company is installing a new engineering test rig in the lab to measure torque loss.
Data from the test rig will help Freudenberg-NOK further improve custom engineered sealing solutions that are reducing CO2 emissions and improving fuel economy.
The 25 new jobs created will include chemist, chemical engineer, and quality technician positions.
The company worked with the state of Ohio, the Findlay-Hancock County Economic Development authority, Jobs Ohio, and the Regional Growth Partnership to secure workforce incentives.
The expansion will help the facility increase its compression and injection molding product production.
The facility's compression and injection molding products include seal rings, thrust washers, pistons, and assemblies.
The plant currently manufactures 185 million seals and related components annually.
The company anticipates the new addition will increase production by approximately 25 percent a year.
source_title: Freudenberg-NOK expands with new laboratory in Findlay - Fluid Power World

facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies (FNST) showcased a portfolio of products that enhance the safety, durability and performance of electric vehicles at the 2012 Battery Show in Detroit, MI, from Nov. 13-15.
FNST is one of the largest producers of elastomeric seals and custom molded parts.
FNST is pursuing advanced electric vehicle technologies as part of its commitment to sustainable products and processes.

FNST displayed a variety of components at the show that leverage the expertise and products of its own divisions and other Freudenberg companies including Freudenberg-NOK Mechatronics and Freudenberg Nonwovens.

Dr. Luis Lorenzo, vice president, Advanced Product Engineering, FNST, stated that the company is working in collaboration with customers to ensure that electric vehicle technologies evolve to the next level of safety and reliability.

Developing alternative technological solutions that address the functional requirements of the system and the cost effectiveness of different approaches is one of FNST's key drivers, according to Dr. Lorenzo.

With operations in the Americas, Europe and Asia-Pacific markets, FNST can tap a vast network of chemistry, design, product engineering and manufacturing experts to provide solutions for the global vehicle electrification industry.
The company uses nearly two dozen polymer families and more than 2,000 raw materials to provide premium battery protection technologies.
FNST provides products including battery pack gaskets, cell frame sealing, plug-and-seal technologies, compensation elements and liquid detectors.
Breathable nonwoven elements were among the technologies displayed at The Battery Show.
Breathable components produced from Freudenberg nonwoven materials optimize the ability of the battery system to repel water, oil, and dirt contamination.
The structure of the fabric creates a “deep filter” affect in the battery unit that ensures the exchange of gases even when exposed to environmental contaminants.

Juergen Mall, market segment manager, E-Mobility, FNST, stated that FNST engineers work on-site with auto manufacturers and their teams have been integrated into the vehicle electrification development process.
FNST offers products capable of performing over a broad range of temperatures and environments.
Customers rely on FNST's expertise in areas like material testing and product validation.
source_title: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies showcase nonwoven materials - Nonwovens Industry

facts: Freudenberg-NOK officially announced a 43,000-foot factory expansion at its Findlay, Ohio plant.
The expansion could result in retaining 148 jobs and creating 25 new jobs at the facility.
Final company decisions are dependent on formal approval of all State of Ohio workforce and incentive programs.
The company expects to invest approximately $8.6 million in construction and equipment upgrades.
Expansion and construction will be complete by winter, 2015.
Dr. Theodore G. Duclos, president of Freudenberg-NOK, stated the investment aims to better serve existing and new customers with quality sealing products.
Freudenberg-NOK leaders worked with JobsOhio, state officials, and local officials on incentives to support the expansion.
JobsOhio Managing Director Glenn Richardson noted the decision demonstrates how companies view Ohio as a great investment location.
JobsOhio and the Regional Growth Partnership will work closely with Freudenberg-NOK as they expand their presence in Hancock County.
Findlay-Hancock County Economic Development Director Anthony Iriti cited the positive business climate and outstanding workforce in Findlay and Hancock County as key factors.
Findlay has become the company’s lead facility for adopting Single Cavity Net Shape Molding, a breakthrough manufacturing approach.
The single cavity approach uses compact, custom-engineered one-cavity injection and compression molding machines rather than multi-cavity machines to produce crucial thermoplastic parts.
Roy Schroeder, Lead Center Manager for Fluid Power Automotive in Findlay, highlighted the plant's pursuit of world-class manufacturing processes and market expansion.
The expansion will help increase compression and injection molding product production, including seal rings, thrust washers, pistons, and assemblies.
The plant currently manufactures 185 million seals and related components annually.
The new addition is anticipated to increase annual production by approximately 25 percent.

facts: GEDIA Automotive Group is bringing its first manufacturing plant in the Southeast to Northwest Georgia.
Gov. Brian Kemp announced the project late last week.
GEDIA Automotive Group will invest $85 million in a state-of-the-art plant in Whitfield County.
The facility will create 200 jobs in the greater Dalton area.
The facility will produce parts for electric vehicles.
Gov. Brian Kemp stated that Georgia is a national leader in manufacturing, logistics and workforce training.
Gov. Brian Kemp expressed confidence that the family-owned business would be pleased with its decision to join the growing electric vehicle ecosystem in Georgia.
Gov. Brian Kemp noted that the state’s consistent investment in its Germany-based European office is paying off.
GEDIA supplies lightweight automotive parts to Volkswagen, BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Volvo and other automakers around the world.
The Georgia plant will be GEDIA Automotive Group’s second manufacturing facility in the United States.
GEDIA Automotive Group already operates a plant in Michigan.
State Commissioner of Economic Development Pat Wilson was traveling in Germany to meet with government officials and trade partners when the announcement was made.
GEDIA’s German headquarters is located in the Bavaria region.
Markus Schaumburg, one of two GEDIA Automotive Group CEOs, called the project an essential step into the future for the company.
Markus Schaumburg stated that moving closer to original equipment manufacturers allows the company to serve the American market even better.
The new plant is expected to begin operations during the third quarter of next year.
source_title: German auto parts maker building plant in Georgia | News | rockdalenewtoncitizen.com

facts: The mountainous terrain of Northwest Georgia makes for a steep hill to climb in terms of developing this region.
Billions of dollars in investment is headed for the hills this year in a variety of industries including solar, ecommerce, car manufacturing and product distribution.
In the last year, this region has been grappling with workforce shortages and supply-chain delays that interrupted project plans.
The region has exponential growth in clean-energy industries with two major electric vehicle operations that opened in the last two years.
A $6.5 million facility from Turkish company TEKLAS opened in Gordon County.
An $85 million facility from German company GEDIA opened in Whitfield County.

The region is attractive to EV developers and traditional auto parts suppliers due to its proximity to car manufacturers in the state, such as Kia, and others in the Southeast such as BMW in South Carolina and Volkswagen in Tennessee.
Kathy Johnson is president and CEO of the Gordon County Chamber of Commerce.
Bartow County has seen billions of dollars in growth in green industries.
SK On and Hyundai Motor Group announced in December 2022 that they will be investing $4 billion to $5 billion to develop an electric vehicle battery plant in Bartow County.
The SK On and Hyundai Motor Group battery plant project will create more than 3,500 new jobs.
The SK On and Hyundai Motor Group battery plant project is one of the largest economic development projects in state history.
South Korean solar energy manufacturer Qcells announced a $2.5 billion expansion in Bartow County.
The Qcells expansion will create 2,500 new jobs for Bartow County.
Together, these two projects will boost Bartow’s employment population by more than 10%.
Melinda Lemmon is executive director of the Cartersville-Bartow County Department of Economic Development.
Duluth Trading Company established a distribution and fulfillment facility in Bartow County in 2022.
Hyundai and SK On operate as a joint venture for their battery plant project.
Shaw Industries in Whitfield County is the third-largest manufacturing facility in the state with more than 6,000 employees.
Shaw Industries wrapped up a $20 million, three-phase expansion last year, creating an additional 55 jobs in Catoosa County.
Mohawk Industries in Gordon County has 3,200 employees.
Mohawk Industries is one of the largest floorcovering manufacturers in the country, producing carpeting, vinyl and hardwood flooring among other floorcovering materials.
Mohawk Industries has called Calhoun home since 1992.
Floorcovering companies in Gordon County are shifting to having in-house design and marketing roles for photography, videography, catalog design and print products.
The recent TEKLAS site and the proximity to the I-75 corridor has helped attract other industries to Gordon County.
Gordon County expects close to 15 million square feet of distribution facilities will be ready for move-in between Q4 of this year and Q1 of 2024.
Pam Powers-Smith is president of the Rome-Floyd Chamber of Commerce.
The Rome-Floyd Chamber of Commerce had almost 65 ribbon-cuttings last year.

Floyd County is unveiling a workforce strategic plan for the community in 2023, which includes a new workforce website in partnership with Georgia Power that will connect employers with prospective employees.
Many communities are putting new emphasis on infrastructure such as expanded broadband, highway access and workforce training to address labor shortages.
Companies in Gordon County are looking at ways to address labor shortages from an educational standpoint, but also through automation and artificial intelligence resources.
Catoosa County is preparing to unveil its Catoosa County College and Career Academy in August 2023, which will partner with area businesses to train high school students for local careers.
Amy Jackson is president and CEO of the Catoosa County Chamber of Commerce.
The Catoosa County College and Career Academy board has been working with area businesses for the last three years to form partnerships for internships and potential jobs upon graduation.
More than 82% of the workforce in Catoosa County commutes out of the county to get to their jobs.
Meghan Trusley is executive administrative assistant to the Catoosa County manager.
Marianne Wood is executive director of the Polk County Chamber of Commerce.
Chris Thomas is president and CEO of the Polk County Development Authority.
Polk County has two college and career academies that it is trying to connect directly to major employers in the area, such as Parker Meggitt, Tip Top Poultry and HON.
Parker Meggitt is an aircraft fuel-tank manufacturer located in Polk County.
HON is an office chair manufacturer located in Polk County.
Polk County has GRAD-certified 240 acres of industrial land that is seeing increased interest from EV manufacturers following the SK On and Hyundai development announcement.
Chattooga County will spend much of this year rebuilding after significant flooding in late 2022.
Cindy Rivers McGraw is executive director of the Chattooga County Chamber of Commerce.
Gov. Brian Kemp called the late 2022 flooding in Chattooga County a 500-year storm when he visited the county last September.
The late 2022 flooding forced dozens of businesses to close their doors in Chattooga County.
Chattooga County has been able to help 25 households through local flood-relief grants.
Blake Elsberry is Chattooga County sole commissioner.
Chattooga County is developing affordable housing and opening the first freestanding rural emergency room in the state, which is an $18 million project.
The 50-acre North Georgia Business Park in Catoosa is located directly off of I-75 and has room for two medium-sized industrial or manufacturing facilities.
Dan Wright is Catoosa County manager.
A new Love’s Travel Center is being built adjacent to the North Georgia Business Park site, which will create 50 to 60 jobs.

Chattanooga-based White Oak Enterprises teamed with Ohio-based Tenby Partners to develop a Class A industrial park near Chattanooga that can accommodate up to 1.1 million square feet of built-out space.
Cloud Springs I-75 Industrial Park is about 40 minutes away from the Appalachian Regional Port in Murray County.
Jeff Londis is co-founder of White Oak Enterprises.
The goal for the Cloud Springs I-75 Industrial Park development is to support the onshoring of industries, particularly in the EV space.
Based on an economic impact study, White Oak Enterprises and Tenby Partners anticipate a capital investment of more than $100 million by the time the property is fully developed.
source_title: Northwest Georgia: Steadily Pushing Forward - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that GEDIA Automotive Group will build an advanced manufacturing facility in Whitfield County.
GEDIA is a family-owned international company that specializes in making state-of-the-art automotive parts.
The facility will begin production with a focus on parts for electric vehicles.
GEDIA will invest $85 million in this project.
The project will deliver 200 jobs to the greater Dalton area.
The site is located at a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) certified property in Whitfield County.
Governor Kemp stated that Georgia is a national leader in manufacturing, logistics, and workforce training.
Kemp expressed confidence that GEDIA will be pleased with joining the growing electric vehicle ecosystem in Georgia.
Kemp noted that the state’s consistent investment in its Germany-based European office is paying off.
The announcement was supported by Georgia’s government partners in the Free State of Bavaria, one of Georgia’s strategic partner regions.
Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) Commissioner Pat Wilson traveled to Germany to meet with government officials, tourism and trade partners.
Commissioner Wilson met at GEDIA headquarters and visited other companies in the final stages of the decision-making process on entering the U.S. market.
GEDIA has more than 100 years of experience supplying lightweight structural automotive parts to Volkswagen, BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Volvo, and numerous other world-renowned companies.
GEDIA is headquartered in Germany.
This facility will mark GEDIA’s first site in the Southeast and its second facility in the United States.
Markus Schaumburg, one of two GEDIA Automotive Group CEOs, stated that moving closer to original equipment manufacturers allows the company to serve the American market even better.
The new innovative production facility will have 200 planned positions.
Helmut Hinkel, GEDIA Automotive Group's additional CEO, stated that the heart of the plant in Whitfield County will be a hot-forming line with patented TemperBox®.

The patented TemperBox® technology takes tailored tempering technology to the next level, enabling the creation of body components with lower weight and significantly higher crash safety performance while conserving energy and material.
GEDIA will use state-of-the-art press hardening, tempering, and automated welding processes to produce parts for electric vehicles.
Operations are expected to begin in the third quarter of 2021.
Whitfield County Board of Commissioners Chairman Lynn Laughter welcomed GEDIA to Whitfield County’s industrial park.
Laughter noted that the location is in the heart of the Southeast automotive manufacturing market, providing a significant advantage for the company.
GEDIA will offer opportunities for management and staff positions, highly skilled technicians, and production workers.
Additional information about the project will be available by the beginning of the fourth quarter of 2020.
GEDIA will build and occupy a 180,000-square-foot facility on a GRAD Certified property in Carbondale Business Park.
To earn a GRAD program certification, a property must undergo due diligence and complete specific certification requirements prior to a visit by a prospective business.
Completing the GRAD certification program is a proactive way for community planners and landowners to help catalyze economic growth and industrial development.
Since the inception of GDEcD’s GRAD certification program, more than 8,000 jobs have been created on GRAD sites.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development was represented in the project by Director of International Relations Nico Wijnberg.
Wijnberg partnered with the Georgia Electric Membership Corporation, Whitfield County, and Georgia Quick Start.
Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that investing in a GRAD certified property was undoubtedly a main driver in GEDIA’s site selection process.
source_title: German automotive parts manufacturer to locate site in Whitfield County | News | griffindailynews.com

facts: Leishen Power is a new leading intelligent high-efficiency powertrain solutions provider officially launched.
Leishen’s products include a world-class modular hybrid powertrain platform, high-efficiency dedicated hybrid transmissions, new hybrid engines, and new E-drive systems.
The new powertrain brand is part of Geely’s commitment to sustainability, offering consumers sustainable low emission powertrain solutions.

On November 01, 2021, in Hangzhou Bay, Geely Auto Group officially released its global powertrain brand “Leishen Power” and its new world-class modular intelligent hybrid powertrain platform, Leishen Hi-X.
The new advanced Leishen hybrid platform contains an ultra-high 43.32% thermal efficiency engine.
The platform features the world’s most advanced 3-speed Dedicated Hybrid Transmission (DHT).
The platform offers a 40% reduced fuel consumption NEDC rating.
The platform includes full powertrain FOTA (Firmware Over the Air) update capabilities.
The core product of Leishen Power is the Leishen Hi-X platform, which offers a comprehensive upgrade in fuel economy, power, comfort, and intelligence.
As a modular intelligent hybrid powertrain platform, Hi-X is the industry’s most adaptable and expandable powertrain in terms of space, levels of electrification, and power delivery.
The Hi-X can be used for A-segment to C-segment models.
The Hi-X supports HEV/PHEV/REEV configurations for brands within Geely Auto Group.
The next generation DHE1.5(1.5TD) is a dedicated hybrid engine developed by a 500-person global engineering team over 5 years.
The DHE1.5 will be the world’s first mass-produced supercharged direct injection hybrid dedicated engine.
The new engine adopts four advanced technologies including high-pressure direct injection, a supercharger, a Miller-cycle, and low-pressure EGR.
The engine achieves the world’s highest thermal efficiency for a mass-produced engine of 43.32%.
The engine's indicated thermal efficiency has reached 52.5% and offers the highest torque and power within its class.
The world’s most advanced 3-speed DHT Pro is a core module of the Hi-X platform and will be the world’s first mass-produced 3-speed hybrid transmission.
The DHT Pro integrates two E-motors (P1+P2), transmission, and E-controllers while weighing only 120kg.
With a maximum torque output of 4920 N•m, the DHT Pro achieves a torque to weight ratio of an astounding 41N•m/kg.
A new high-efficiency DCT EVO has been developed from Geely’s 7DCTH with improved torque efficiency and performance while reducing weight.
A new E-Drive motor has been developed according to global luxury performance standards adopting a modular design and offering high-efficiency, high reliability, and fully customizable solutions.
Leishen E-Drive adopts an 800V e-motor, silicon carbide power module, oil-cooling efficiency of 92% and maximum power of 475kW.
The E-Drive motor can enable models to achieve 0-100km/h acceleration in under 3 seconds.
In an Hybrid Electric (HEV) configuration, the Hi-X reduces fuel consumption by 40% with an NEDC rating as low as 3.6L/100km.
Hi-X is the first hybrid platform capable of FOTA improvements of the entire hybrid powertrain system and offers as many as 20 intelligent driving modes.
The Hi-X features a smart parallel drive mode which improves the efficiency of power delivery from both its engine and e-motors.
Hi-X parallel drive mode can be entered in at speeds as low as 20km/h, much lower than other hybrid powertrain systems on the market which can only enter parallel drive modes at speeds above 70km/h.
The difference enables Hi-X to have a 20% improved system efficiency.
The DHT Pro offers a catapult start feature improving standing acceleration by 50%.
Through smarter utilization of its 3 gears, acceleration between 80km-120km/h is improved for high-speed overtaking by releasing 60% of reserved power.
The first models to utilize the Leishen Hi-X platform powertrain system will be Geely Auto’s flagship “Star” series that includes the Xing Yue (Tugella) and Xing Rui (Preface) products.
Over the next three years, Geely Holding Group’s family of brands will offer a diverse variety of electrified powertrain options including HEV, long-range PHEV, and range-extended EVs.
Through Leishen Power, Geely re-doubles its commitment to sustainable development and accelerating the realization of full carbon neutrality.
The products and technologies available from Leishen Power enable automakers to offer users the most advanced, most efficient, electrified powertrain solutions in the world.
Leishen Power breaks with the one-sizes fits all mindset on reducing emissions and provides consumers with low emission sustainable mobility without sacrificing user experience.
Global Powertrain Systems is located in Newnan, GA.
A candidate interviewed at the Global Powertrain Systems plant in May 2012.
The application and interview process took 3 weeks.
The interview involved sitting and talking to human resources and touring the plant.
An HR representative tried to talk a candidate out of working there during the interview.
Candidates were not required to have a diploma, pass a drug test, or take a skills test for the position.
source_title: 1 Global Powertrain Systems Interview Questions & Answers (2026) | Glassdoor

facts: Stellantis India’s CEO and MD is Shailesh Hazela.
The company's next 10 years will be shaped by flexible manufacturing, localisation, and future-ready platforms.
Hydrogen is already part of Stellantis India’s present-day planning.
Stellantis has started working on a hydrogen pilot project in Europe.
If the hydrogen product meets Indian requirements, it can be manufactured at the Hosur plant.
The hydrogen solution could fit into Citroën or Jeep brands where customers are looking for that solution.
India needs everything at the right value, which matters for adoption.
Hydrogen is considered the next chapter in Stellantis’ multi-energy strategy, not a disruption.
Stellantis platforms must be adaptive, future-proof, multi-energy, and scalable to define the next decade.
The Hosur plant has flexible production lines capable of making multiple products on the same line.
In Thiruvallur, all four Citroën products are manufactured on one flexible line.
Stellantis often stretches existing production line capabilities instead of immediately building new ones.
The company sometimes increases worker hours and optimizes lines to get more production out.
It is too early to comment on new lines or products beyond the recently launched three X-series.
Stellantis India operates a multi-city engineering ecosystem with over 4,000 engineers across Chennai, Pune, Hyderabad, and Bengaluru.
These engineers work on global software, cloud systems, connectivity layers, and platform programmes.
The teams work on programmes across brands, platforms, and continents, covering engineering, manufacturing, IT, and supply chain.
The Hosur plant is the physical heartbeat of Stellantis India.
The Hosur plant spans 18 acres.
The plant operates on three shifts a day, six days a week.
It produces up to 374,000 gearboxes annually.
It produces up to 300,000 engines annually.
Nearly 1,500 employees work at the Hosur plant.
The plant produced its first 6-speed manual gearbox in 2018.
The plant builds ED engines, the 1.2-litre naturally aspirated engine, and the 1.2-litre turbo Gen 3.
The plant produces multiple gearbox models.
The gearboxes serve five major Stellantis brands: Citroën, Peugeot, Opel, DS, and South American models.
Citroën connects India and export markets for these components.
Part of the 5-speed gearbox production is exported to South Africa and Morocco.
The plant has achieved 90 per cent localisation.
Stellantis works with 138 suppliers, most located in Tamil Nadu.
Ninety-five percent of Hosur plant production goes to plants outside India.
The plant is committed to supporting every domestic requirement.
Hosur has become an indispensable node in Stellantis’ global supply chain across Europe, the Middle East, Africa, Brazil, and Argentina.
Hosur is the global centre of competence for 6-speed and 5-speed manual gearboxes.
Hosur is also the global centre of competence for the 1.2-litre engine.
The plant functions as an engineering campus with design, simulation, and calibration happening on-site.
Exports from India’s supplier base are expected to rise from about ₹4,000 crore to ₹10,000 crore next year.
Stellantis nurtures suppliers and builds their capability to supply locally and globally.
Stellantis has invested nearly ₹11,000 crore in India over the years, with more in the pipeline.
The company’s investment approach is demand-driven, targeting capacity, capability, or new technology needs.
The Hosur plant uses AI-enabled vision systems to scan for imperfections.
Machine-learning robotics adjust to production changes in real time at the plant.
These technologies are used as everyday tools rather than futuristic experiments.
The plant has the lowest energy consumption per gearbox unit.
Hosur uses 92 per cent renewable energy.
The plant saved 10,622 tonnes of CO₂ in 2024.
The plant is targeting a CO₂ saving of 14,000 tonnes in 2025.
Employees at the plant come from 16 states.
Eighteen percent of the plant's employees are women.
Stellantis built its own training academy for plant employees.
Directly and indirectly, the plant has created 4,500 jobs.
The plant optimizes space inward using vertical storage, compact machining, and condensed pathways rather than expanding outward.
Stellantis operates a global map of 44 engine and gearbox plants.
Hosur’s capacity is comparable to 45–50 per cent of those 44 plants.
If Stellantis produces 6 million vehicles globally, the 300,000 engines from Hosur represent 5 per cent of the world’s engine capacity.
Hosur will continue to support Stellantis with all internal combustion engines and manual gearboxes for as long as needed.
source_title: Stellantis’ Hosur plant emerges as a global powertrain hub, ETManufacturing

facts: Magna Powertrain of Muncie has announced plans for a 50,000 square foot expansion at their Cowan Road facility.
The company is investing $8.9 million in equipment and $2.9 million in real estate for the expansion.
The expansion includes 21 new jobs and $655,000 in new annual payroll.
The expansion will allow Magna to expand their manufacturing capacity to fulfill customers’ increased production requirements.
The expansion will better equip them to handle additional future growth.
Stephen Brand serves as the General Manager at Magna Powertrain.

Stephen Brand stated: “With the continued investment of Magna Powertrain, combined with the support from our business and municipal partners, we will continue to increase our competitive advantage and execute our plan for sustained growth.”
Groundbreaking for the expansion is anticipated in the coming months.
Once construction is complete, the company will hold an opening event to recognize the partners who have assisted with Magna’s growth in Muncie.
Magna Powertrain opened the Muncie plant in 2005.
The company has invested nearly $100 million and created more than 300 jobs at the Muncie location.
The company also expanded in 2011, occupying the former Delaware County speculative building on Fuson Road.
The planned expansion will bring Magna’s Muncie footprint to 431,500 square feet.
The company received assistance from the Muncie-Delaware County Economic Development Alliance in the procurement of local and state incentives.
Jay Julian serves as President & Chief Economic Development Officer at the Muncie-Delaware County Economic Development Alliance and Chamber of Commerce.

Jay Julian stated: “Magna, a top employer, has made a momentous investment in the community, and we are pleased to assist them with our available resources. Growth of existing business is the keystone of a successful economic development strategy.”
Todd Donati serves as President of the County Commissioners.

Todd Donati stated: "Magna is creating jobs and increasing our tax base, which is extremely valuable to our communities success. Magna has far exceeded its expectations when they first moved to our community and the commissioners and council continue to support them in every way possible. We are very grateful to have this valuable asset, not only with their growth but with their community involvement."
The Muncie-Delaware County Economic Development Alliance (EDA) is a private economic development organization dedicated to the growth and prosperity of Muncie-Delaware County, Indiana.

The EDA’s purpose is to allocate and leverage economic development resources to sustain and enhance the economic growth, vitality and global competitiveness of Muncie-Delaware County as a superior business location.
The EDA’s administrative entities are Delaware Advancement Corporation and the Muncie-Delaware County Chamber of Commerce.
Magna is the most diversified automotive supplier in the world.

The company designs, develops and manufactures automotive systems, assemblies, modules and components, and engineers and assembles complete vehicles, primarily for sale to original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) of cars and light trucks.
Magna operates in three geographic segments: North America, Europe, and Rest of World (primarily Asia, South America and Africa).

Magna’s capabilities include the design, engineering, testing and manufacture of automotive interior systems; seating systems; closure systems; metal body & chassis systems; mirror systems; exterior systems; roof systems; electronic systems; powertrain systems as well as complete vehicle engineering and assembly.
The company has 294 manufacturing operations and 87 product development, engineering and sales centres in 26 countries on five continents as of Q1 2012.
source_title: IN: Magna Powertrain Expands, Hiring Again, Total Investment Reaches $100M | Trade and Industry Development

facts: Hyundai Glovis Georgia, LLC is a logistics company headquartered in Seoul, Korea and part of the Hyundai Kia Automotive Group.
Its predecessor company, Hankook Logitech Co. Ltd was formed in February 2001.
The company supplies ocean transportation logistics advice, cargo space, loading/unloading, and packaging services.
Hyundai Glovis changed its name to Hyundai Glovis in June 2003.
Hyundai Glovis provides ocean transportation, air transportation, inland transportation, logistics consulting, storage, packaging services as well as supply chain management services.
Since 2011, the company has launched an auto parts recycling business named OnECO, which mainly consists of distribution of reuse and remanufactured auto parts.
The headquarters is located at 6101 Sorento Road, West Point, Georgia, United States.
Hyundai Glovis has 1035 employees.
Revenue ranges from $100 million to $250 million.
The company operates under NAICS code 488510.
Hyundai Glovis's industry is Logistics And Supply Chain.
Taesung Kim serves as the Chief Operating Officer of Hyundai Glovis.
source_title: Hyundai Glovis - Phone, Email, Employees, CEO, VP, 2024

facts: GLOVIS EV Logistics America is an integrated logistics company located in Savannah, Georgia.
The company provides Tier 1 logistics, warehousing, and inventory services to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
GLOVIS EV Logistics America was established on October 6, 2022.
The Operations Specialist role is based at the Vehicle Processing Center (VPC) in Ellabell, GA.
The position reports to the VPC Senior Manager.
Work schedule is Monday through Friday from 6:30 AM to 3:00 PM or 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM, with possible weekends and overtime.
Responsibilities include overseeing UC/UBC installation processes and vehicle loading for both UC/UBC and Signoff areas.
The role involves managing employees, payroll, attendance, and disciplinary actions while assigning manpower to achieve production goals.
The specialist must effectively conduct meetings to communicate production, quality, safety, and cost requirements.
Duties include ensuring timely completion and quality of work by team members.
The position requires overseeing, ordering, and ensuring accountability of tools and appropriate materials.
Periodic audits are performed to ensure installations are effectively executed.
Proper documentation must be completed on each install according to customer specifications and quality standards.
The role is responsible for maintaining payroll during the shift and assigning overtime as warranted.
Requires understanding of automotive installations and maintaining SWIS documents.
Involves administrative activities, record keeping, managing spreadsheets, and tracking data to show department improvements.
Daily, weekly, and monthly audits and reports are required.
The specialist supports other departments to ensure operational success and participates in continuous improvement activities, safety walks, and production meetings.
Overtime may be assigned to support HMGMA lines and production as needed.
Qualifications include a high school diploma, some college, or college degree.
1-2 years of management experience is preferred, preferably in an automotive or production environment.
Candidates must have a good understanding of Microsoft applications like Excel, Word, and PowerPoint.
The role requires strong written and verbal communication skills along with good interpersonal skills.
Employees routinely use standard office equipment including laptops and cell phones.
The job generally requires long periods of sitting, typing, and working at a computer.
Occasional lifting of up to 30 pounds may be required.
Total rewards include medical, dental, and vision coverage.
A 401(k) Plan is offered with $1/$1 matching up to plan provisions.
Generous paid time off is provided as part of the benefits package.
The estimated base salary range for the Operations Specialist is $55,700 to $61,600 per year.
Hyundai GLOVIS EV conducts pre-employment screenings including employment verification, physicals, drug screens, and criminal background checks.
New hires are verified through E-Verify.
The workplace is designated as Drug Free and Tobacco Free.
The company will be certified under the CTPAT Program through Homeland Security.
source_title: Glovis EV Logistics America Operations Specialist in Ellabell, GA | 1164128556 | Snagajob

Physical address is 50 Anton Haering St., Lavonia, GA 30553.
Business hours are Monday through Friday from 7:00 AM to 5:00 PM, with Saturday and Sunday closed.
Maintains a 3.8-star overall rating derived from 13 customer reviews.

facts: Haering Precision USA LP was recognized as the 2024 Impact Business of the Year by the Hart County Chamber of Commerce.
The award highlights businesses with over 50 employees that have made a significant economic and community impact through job creation, corporate citizenship, and community engagement.
Dustin Chambers, Commercial Plant Manager at Haering Precision, stated that receiving the Impact Business of the Year award reflects the team's dedication to making a difference.
Dustin Chambers noted that Haering believes in investing in people, developing talent, investing in future generations, and supporting initiatives contributing to Hart County's well-being.
Last fall, Haering participated in National Manufacturing Week by hosting and participating in key events emphasizing manufacturing's connection to education, innovation, and community engagement.
The Haering plant is located in the Gateway 2 Industrial Park.
Every year, the Haering plant hosts the Haering Precision Skills Invitational in partnership with North Georgia Technical College.

The Skills Invitational pairs participants from local high schools with senior precision machining students from North Georgia Tech in a pro-am style contest testing technical skills, creativity, and teamwork.
The award was presented at the Chamber’s Annual Awards Banquet on February 21, 2025.

Dwayne Dye, former Director of Economic Development for the Hart County Industrial Building Authority, emphasized Haering’s commitment to offering career opportunities and its role as a driving force in the local economy during the presentation.
Dwayne Dye stated that the potential for long-term positive impact for local area students, apprentices, workforce, and the Northeast Georgia Region is only the beginning.
The company remains committed to workforce development, education, and innovation to ensure continued growth and success for employees and the community.
source_title: Haering Precision USA LP Named 2024 Impact Business of the Year by Hart County Chamber of Commerce - 92.1 WLHR

facts: Haering Precision USA LP partnered with North Georgia Technical College (NGTC) to host the second annual Haering Precision Skills Invitational in March 2025 at NGTC’s Clarkesville campus.
The event brought together high school students from across Northeast Georgia for a dynamic, hands-on competition aimed at inspiring the next generation of manufacturing talent.
Students from Hart, Franklin, White, and Union counties participated in the pro-am style invitational.
Each participating high school student was paired with a precision machining student from NGTC to complete real-world challenges together.
Teams worked on challenges designed to reflect modern manufacturing processes, including interpreting technical drawings, operating CNC equipment, and fabricating a custom wax stamp.
The competition was judged by industry and educational professionals who evaluated teams on technical accuracy, creativity, teamwork, and overall execution.
The top three students were recognized and received prizes including professional tools to begin their careers in manufacturing.

Dustin Chambers, Commercial Plant Manager at Haering Precision, stated the goal is to create meaningful, hands-on experiences that connect students with career opportunities in high-tech manufacturing.
All participating students have been invited to complete a paid internship or work-based learning program at Haering Precision’s Lavonia facility.
These programs provide direct exposure to cutting-edge production environments and serve as a pathway into high-demand technical careers.
The 2025 Haering Precision Skills Invitational is part of Haering’s ongoing commitment to workforce development, technical education, and community collaboration.

facts: HEXPOL announced the acquisition of Kirkhill Rubber from founder Edward Reker, his family, and co-owners.
Kirkhill Rubber is an industry leader with experience in aerospace, automotive, medical, and other demanding industry segments.
Kirkhill Rubber has most recently expanded into silicone and perfluoroelastomer compounds.
Kirkhill Rubber has annual sales of approximately 46 million USD.
Kirkhill Rubber brings extensive knowledge in highly engineered elastomers, specialty equipment, and complete laboratory testing facilities.
Kirkhill Rubber's state-of-the-art facility in Long Beach, California will be its sole manufacturing plant.
Production at Kirkhill Rubber's Downey, California location will be transferred to Long Beach, California.
Production at Kirkhill Rubber's Athens, Georgia location will be transferred to other HEXPOL sites in the US.
The facilities in Downey and Athens are not included in the acquisition transaction.
The acquired business has an EBITDA margin below the HEXPOL Group but is expected to positively impact earnings per share.

Tracy Garrison, President of HEXPOL Compounding NAFTA, stated the acquisition complements HEXPOL Compounding in the US and strengthens their presence with rubber compounds through highly skilled people and advanced processes.
Kirkhill's recent purchase of the Long Beach, California facility increases the ability to provide superior products and service to customers.
Edward Reker, President of Kirkhill Rubber, will continue with HEXPOL after the acquisition.
Edward Reker noted immediate benefits from access to HEXPOL’s global material reach, expanded development resources, and advanced quality systems.
Mikael Fryklund, CEO of HEXPOL Group, described the acquisition as a step in expanding and strengthening the group within its core business.
The acquisition price amounts to approximately 49 million USD on a cash and debt-free basis.
The transaction is funded by a combination of cash and existing bank facilities.
The acquired business will be consolidated into HEXPOL from September 2018.
A smaller performance-based consideration will be paid later if certain criteria are met.
HEXPOL is a world-leading polymers group with positions in advanced polymer compounds, gaskets for plate heat exchangers, and plastic/rubber materials for truck and castor wheel applications.

HEXPOL's customers include systems suppliers to the global automotive and engineering industry, construction industry, energy/oil and gas sector, medical equipment manufacturers, and OEM manufacturers of plate heat exchangers and forklifts.
HEXPOL is organized into two business areas: HEXPOL Compounding and HEXPOL Engineered Products.
HEXPOL Group sales in 2017 amounted to 12,230 MSEK.
HEXPOL Group has approximately 4,400 employees across eleven countries.

canonical_name: Georgia Economic Development Incentives & Financing Programs
facts: BLS & Co. periodically revises state incentive pages to provide current information on legislative and regulatory developments affecting available programs.
Businesses that create net new full-time jobs may be eligible for an annual non-refundable and non-transferable tax credit of $1,250 to $4,000 per new job for 5 years.

The Georgia facility must be engaged in a specified operation or the headquarters of a company engaged in a specified industry, including manufacturing, warehousing, distribution, logistics, software development, contact centers, and others.
The exact value of the as-of-right Job Tax Credits depends on job creation and the development tier of the county or census tract where the project is located.
Unused Job Tax Credits may be carried forward for up to 10 years.
A company may claim either the Job Tax Credit or the Investment Tax Credit, but not both.

The Quality Jobs Tax Credit is an as-of-right program offering tax credits to companies that create at least 50 net new jobs within a 24-month period with wages at or above 110% of the county average wage.
Quality Jobs Tax Credits range from $2,500 to $5,000 per job, per year for 5 years.

The credit amount depends on qualifying jobs' average wage compared to the county's average wage, ranging from $2,500 for wages at 110%-120% of the county average up to $5,000 for wages at 200% or greater.
Quality Jobs Tax Credits can be used to offset up to 100% of state corporate income tax liability.
If Quality Jobs Tax Credits exceed a company's tax liability in a given year, the excess may be used against state payroll withholding.

Companies that create at least 1,800 net new jobs over six years and invest a minimum of $450 million or have an annual payroll of at least $150 million may claim the Mega Project Tax Credit of $5,250 per job, per year for 5 years.
Companies investing at least $600 million are granted an additional two years to meet Mega Project job creation requirements.
Companies investing at least $800 million are granted an additional four years to meet Mega Project job creation requirements.
The Mega Project must pay an average wage above specified minimums or show high growth potential.
Mega Project Tax Credits can offset up to 100% of state corporate income tax liability, with excess credits applied to state payroll withholding.
Claiming the Mega Project Tax Credit forgives claiming any other credit besides R&D and retraining tax credits.
Premium Tax Credits are used against annual premium tax applied to insurance companies and require satisfying job creation requirements specific to county tiers.
The Premium Tax Credit award ranges from $750 to $3,500 per eligible job per year for up to five years.
Unused Premium Tax Credits can be carried forward for 10 years.
The Investment Tax Credit provides tax credits to companies engaged in manufacturing or telecommunications support that have operated in Georgia for at least 3 years.
Businesses with at least $100,000 of qualified investment may be eligible for an Investment Tax Credit ranging from 1% to 8%.
The actual Investment Tax Credit depends on the development tier of the county and the type of capital expenditures made.
Investment Tax Credits can offset up to 50% of state corporate income tax liability, and eligible companies in Rural Counties within Tiers 1 to 2 can apply credits to payroll withholding.
Unused Investment Tax Credits can be carried forward for up to 10 years.

Port Activity Bonus Tax Credits are available to companies qualifying for the Job or Investment Tax Credit that increase imports or exports through a Georgia deepwater port by a specified amount over an indicated year.
The base year port traffic minimum for Port Activity Bonus is 75 net tons, 5 containers, or 10 TEUs.
The Port Activity Bonus provides an additional $1,250 tax credit per job, per year for up to five years when used with Job Tax Credits.
When used with the Investment Tax Credit, the Port Activity Bonus increases the credit to the equivalent of a Tier 1 location regardless of the actual tier level.
Businesses may claim a Research and Development Tax Credit equal to 10% of qualified R&D spending in Georgia minus a base amount.
The R&D Tax Credit can offset up to 50% of net Georgia corporate income tax liability after all other credits have been applied.
Excess R&D credits can be applied to state payroll withholding and carried forward for up to 10 years.
The Life Sciences Manufacturing Tax Credit is a bonus added to the Job Tax Credit for manufacturing pharmaceuticals, medicines, and medical equipment/supplies in Georgia.
Recipients of the Life Sciences Manufacturing Tax Credit must also be eligible for the Job Tax Credit.
The Life Sciences Manufacturing Tax Credit equals an additional $1,250 for eligible jobs and may offset up to 100% of corporate income tax liability.
Excess Life Sciences Manufacturing Tax Credits may offset state payroll withholding and can be carried forward for 10 years.

Georgia exempts the 6%-9% sales tax that manufacturers must make for their operations, covering expenses like manufacturing machinery and equipment, repair to industrial machinery, raw materials and packaging, energy used in manufacturing, primary material handling equipment, pollution control equipment, and clean room equipment.
Data centers are exempt from the 6%-9% sales tax if they create a specified number of new high-paying jobs and meet a minimum qualified investment threshold that depends on the county.
The sale of certain computers is exempt when the total qualifying purchases of a high-tech company exceed $15 million.
Effective January 1, 2024, any entity claiming the data center or high-tech sales tax exemption must pay 10% of all sales and use tax imposed on the first $15 million.
Distribution centers are eligible for the 6%-9% sales tax exemption when they invest $5 million in the purchase or expansion of a facility in qualifying expenses.
A distribution center cannot have retail sales above or equal to 15% of the facility's total revenues to qualify for the exemption.
Businesses that retrain employees to use new equipment or learn new technology skills may qualify for a Retraining Tax Credit equal to 50% of direct training expenses.
The maximum Retraining Tax Credit is $500 per full-time employee per approved training program per year, with a total cap of $1,250 per employee per year.
Retraining Tax Credits can offset up to 50% of state corporate income tax liability and unused credits can be carried forward for up to 10 years.
Retraining programs must be approved by the Technical College System of Georgia.
The Quick Start Employee Training incentive provides training space, instructors, and all needed training materials for new employees in skill-based jobs at no cost to qualifying companies.
Quick Start helps companies assess, select, and train new employees for expansion or relocation projects.
Direct property tax abatements are statutorily banned in Georgia, but local communities can provide relief through bond-leaseback transactions of real and personal property.
The public ownership structure enables a negotiated Payment-In-Lieu-Of-Taxes (PILOT) to effectuate a temporary partial tax exemption.
Counties and Municipalities in Georgia have the option of enacting local property tax exemptions for four classes of inventory at 20%, 40%, 60%, 80%, or 100% under Level One Freeport Law.
Class One inventory covers goods in the process of being manufactured or produced, including raw materials and partly finished goods.
Class Two inventory covers finished goods manufactured within Georgia held by the manufacturer for a period not exceeding 12 months.
Class Three inventory covers finished goods stored in a warehouse, dock, or wharf destined to be shipped outside Georgia for a period not exceeding 12 months.
Class Four stock in trade covers fulfillment center inventory stored in a warehouse for a period not exceeding 12 months.
The incentive page was last updated in April 2023.
