facts: Hyundai Motor Group will unveil its artificial intelligence (AI)-converged robotics business strategy at CES 2026 — driven by its next-generation all-electric humanoid robot Atlas.
The Atlas robot will be demonstrated for the first time at the upcoming exhibition, which runs for four days from Jan. 6 in Las Vegas.
Hyundai identifies Atlas as the cornerstone of its software-defined factory (SDF) initiative.
In 2022, Hyundai Motor Group Executive Chair Chung Euisun drew attention at CES by appearing onstage with Boston Dynamics' four-legged robot Spot.
The group had previously acquired a controlling stake in the robotics developer for $880 million (1.3 trillion won), positioning the sector as its next major revenue stream.

Under the theme “Partnering Human Progress: AI Robotics,” Hyundai will outline its commercialization strategy for AI robots to accelerate their integration into daily life and smart factories at CES 2026.
Atlas — also developed by Boston Dynamics — will take center stage at CES 2026.

The state-of-the-art humanoid will begin test operations at Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, the company's latest EV factory, in 2026, autonomously handling basic tasks like sorting and moving auto parts.
The carmaker will also unveil a strategy to expand its AI robotics ecosystem by converging robots with the carmaker’s software-defined factories across the globe.

An official from Hyundai Motor said SDFs are high-tech smart factories operated by datasets and software, and the next-generation manufacturing facilities are capable of maximizing flexibility and agility in every production process.
In 2023, the carmaker opened its first-ever SDF in Singapore — Hyundai Motor Group Innovation Center Singapore.
The facility can produce some 30,000 EVs annually, and more than 70 percent of its assembly and inspection process has already been automated through AI and robots.
Hyundai Motor Group aims to evolve from one of the world's largest automakers into a comprehensive robotics solutions provider over the long term.
An official stated the company will strengthen its position in the AI robotics by engaging in every single robot manufacturing process from its initial development to training and operation.
At the carmaker’s exhibition hall, visitors will be able to experience the firm’s virtual AI robotics research environment and receive a tech briefing on cutting-edge AI robotics technologies.
Hyundai Motor Group attends CES biennially.
During CES 2024, the carmaker focused on promoting its vision for future mobility, such as hydrogen cars and software-defined vehicles.
CES 2026 will be a venue for the carmaker to express its next-generation technological direction, driven by the AI and robotics.
The carmaker also aims to revive its self-driving ambitions by drawing global attention to its renewed robotics vision at the world's largest tech fair.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group to unveil AI robotics strategy at CES - The Korea Times

Nearly 6,000 new jobs will be located mostly in communities outside of large urban areas.

Woosuck Kim stated that Hyundai Industrial has more than four decades of experience providing comfortable and safe experiences for drivers and passengers with some of the world’s biggest automotive brands.
Hyundai Industrial is excited to be part of the e-mobility transition happening in Georgia.
Hyundai Industrial looks forward to working with Coweta County to create mutually beneficial growth for the community and future employees.
A press release from Governor Kemp’s office stated that Hyundai Industrial purchased an existing building at 83 Amlajack Way in Coweta County.
The building will be retrofitted to produce arm and head rests for OEMs.
One of the OEM customers is Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
Hyundai Industrial will focus on filling manufacturing production roles.

Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged collaboration and innovation, and positioned itself as a leader in developing and harnessing emerging technologies for the automotive and mobility industry for over a century.
Georgia has pursued the entire EV supply chain, creating more than $25.1 billion in investments and 29,000 jobs since 2020.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in Georgia’s automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to fiscal year 2021.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.

Hyundai is duking it out with Ford Motor Co. for second place in US electric vehicle sales this year, behind Tesla Inc.
President and co-Chief Executive Officer Jaehoon Chang, 58, stated that the company was on the right track and very strong this year.

Hyundai is trying to be flexible and optimize and protect production despite a chip shortage.

facts: Governor Brian Kemp and Hyundai Motor Group Executive Char Euisun Chung announced that Hyundai Motor Group will open its first fully dedicated electric vehicle and battery manufacturing facility in Georgia.
The facility will be located at the megasite in Bryan County, Ga.
Hyundai Motor Group will invest $5.54 billion to open the factory.
Other suppliers will invest around another $1 billion in the project.
The project will deliver approximately 8,100 new jobs to the area.
Construction on the facility is expected to begin in January 2023.
Full production is expected in the first half of 2025.
Governor Brian Kemp stated the project will be a huge economic boost to Bryan County and the entire state of Georgia.

Governor Kemp said, “This is going to be the largest economic plan we have ever done in the state’s history. From a state perspective, it’s just incredible even bigger than the Rivian plant, that we announced several months ago. 8,100 jobs, $5.5 billion in initial investment. We know there will be an addition $1billion dollars from Suppliers who are going to bring, hundreds and hundreds, if not thousands of other jobs.”
Members of the Richmond Hill Bryan County Chamber of Commerce expressed excitement about the announcement.
Chamber chairwoman Emily Cannon noted that new businesses directly related to the EV plant at the megasite will be expected, along with businesses for workers such as restaurants.

Emily Cannon said, “And then after the direct industry comes, then is when you get additional restaurants and more mom and pop shops that join in and fill in with the community. So we can expect down the road to see this slow and homogenous growth that comes around a large investment like this.”
County leaders stated that economic incentives for Hyundai are not finalized yet but should be announced in June.
The megasite development footprint grew by about 700 acres to fit development needs following last year's land purchase announcement.
Trip Tollison, President and CEO of the Savannah Economic Development Authority (SEDA), explained the megasite expansion.

Trip Tollison said, “We’re going to, part of the announcement is an extra billion dollars of investment with a bunch of jobs attached to that that are coming as well. So we’re still getting all those details out. But we’ll see a lot of supplier visits over the next several months as they look to also locate here as well.”
The overall job count and investment associated with the venture will grow over time.
The Kia plant in West Point, Ga., serves as an example of how automotive plants boost local economies.
Mayor Steve Tramell noted that West Point's downtown was 90 percent empty fifteen years ago but is now 90 percent full due to the Kia plant.
Mayor Tramell said, “Fifteen years ago, this downtown was 90 percent empty. Empty shops every where you could see. Now, it’s 90 percent full.”
West Point has a nighttime population of about 3,600 that swells to 10-12,000 daily due to plant workers.
The Kia plant brought restaurants on nearly every block and several hiring agencies to West Point.
Local businesses in West Point adapted to the plant's presence, such as shoe stores selling steel-toed shoes to plant employees.
Communities along the road from the Kia plant have seen economic growth.
Mayor Tramell said, “Everything you plan for, you get something else. It might not be what you want it to be. But what comes in, is a good thing......”
Rep. Buddy Carter attended the announcement and released a statement supporting Hyundai's choice of Georgia.

Rep. Carter said, “I am thrilled that Hyundai chose Georgia as the home for its latest factory. Not only will this investment bring thousands of jobs to the First District, but it will also boost America’s manufacturing economy, spurring innovation and global economic competitiveness within the region. With this investment, Georgia will be a leader in the electric vehicle industry. The future of transportation is right here in Bryan County.”
Georgia Senator Ossoff released a statement supporting the announcement.
Senator Ossoff led an economic delegation to South Korea back in November.

Senator Ossoff said, “When I led an Economic Delegation to South Korea last fall, attracting Hyundai’s investment in Georgia was a key goal. This multibillion-dollar opportunity for Georgia will create thousands of jobs and grow Georgia’s reputation as a world leader in automotive and clean energy manufacturing.”

facts: Hyundai Mobis will present more than 30 mobility convergence technologies at CES 2026 in Las Vegas from January 6 to 9.
The company will host an invitation-only private booth exclusively for pre-invited customers to focus on new business opportunities.
Hyundai Mobis is specifically targeting key clients from across North America for its CES booth.
Senior executives from multiple major global automakers, including heads of purchasing and technology, have confirmed visits to the booth.
A dedicated meeting room on the third floor of the West Hall at the Las Vegas Convention Center will be used to facilitate deeper discussions with visiting executives.
The CES showcase highlights include the updated M.VICS 7.0 cockpit integrated solution and the X-by-Wire electronic control technology.
M.VICS 7.0 integrates advanced infotainment technologies featuring a holographic windshield display, a vertically expandable 18.1-inch large display, and a redesigned center-console interface.
The X-by-Wire integrated solution enables electronic steering and braking control without mechanical connections via a single controller for optimized driving dynamics and built-in dual safety.
Additional showcased technologies include an augmented-reality head-up display (AR-HUD), low-power display solutions, and cost-efficient, high-performance EV drive systems.
The Holographic Windshield Display (HWD) featured in M.VICS 7.0 won the CES 2026 Innovation Award from the Consumer Technology Association (CTA).

HWD is developed in collaboration with German optics specialist Zeiss and is the world's first system to utilize holographic film to transform the entire front windshield into an ultra-large display surface.
The HWD technology allows drivers to view key information directly on the windshield while enabling passengers to watch separate content like videos or games without distracting the driver.
Hyundai Mobis is currently co-developing the HWD technology with major global customers and aims to begin mass production in 2029.
The company plans to ramp up promotional efforts across North America and Europe through CES, international exhibitions, and roadshows.
Hyundai Mobis aims to increase its share of global OEM sales in its core component business to 40% by 2033.
Hyundai Mobis is ranked as the global no. 6 automotive supplier and is headquartered in Seoul, South Korea.

The company possesses expertise in sensors, sensor fusion in ECUs, software development for safety control, and manufactures components for electrification, brakes, chassis, suspension, steering, airbags, lighting, and automotive electronics.
Hyundai Mobis operates its R&D headquarters in Korea along with four technology centers located in the United States, Germany, China, and India.

facts: Close to 900 new jobs are coming to West Point, Georgia.
Recent expansions and developments in the city's automotive industry are expected to increase West Point's population.

West Point Mayor Steve Tramell stated that after years of idling following the departure of the textile industry, Kia and its suppliers are moving into West Point, adding jobs, housing, and community growth.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp announced Monday a $9 million investment at Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System to expand its operations.
A new $240 million facility is being built at Hyundai TRANSYS.
Job expansion is occurring at Jin-Tech America, a supplier of interior automotive parts.
West Point Development Authority director Meghan Duke expressed excitement about the next generation of automobiles being built in West Point.
Both Hyundai TRANSYS and Jin-Tech America provide parts to West Point’s Kia facility.
The city is building a new apartment complex near the West Point Coca-Cola facility.
Many new homes are being constructed in the Villages of Harris Creek neighborhood on Highway 103.
Johnny's Pizza owner Ben Hamilton expressed excitement about increased foot traffic and investment in West Point businesses.
Meghan Duke reported downtown growth driven by lunch-hour crowds visiting local businesses due to project momentum.
Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System is accepting job applications for its expansion.

source_title: West Point preparing for huge economic developments

facts: On December 9, 2024, LPO announced a conditional commitment for a loan guarantee of up to $305.54 million to finance Project IceBrick.
Project IceBrick is a virtual power plant (VPP) consisting of up to 193 cold thermal energy storage installations at commercial buildings across California.
The loan guarantee includes $303.69 million of principal and $1.85 million of capitalized interest.

The TES cells, which are the main component of the IceBrick systems, will be manufactured for this project (and future US installations) entirely in the US by contractors located in three states: Texas, Iowa, and California.
The project has the potential to create more than 200 jobs, including over 170 peak construction jobs.
Over the 5-year construction period, Nostromo will create more than 870 annual job equivalents.
Nostromo Energy submitted its loan application to LPO in Summer 2022.
If finalized, Project IceBrick would provide customers with efficiency as a service by freezing a water-based solution during hours when electricity supply is at its most abundant and clean.

The IceBrick system would store and later use the ice to support cooling of the building during hours of peak demand, when the power grid faces highest demand and electricity production is dirtiest and most expensive.

Nostromo’s Cirrus software platform allows the IceBrick systems to operate as a VPP by orchestrating multiple energy assets to function in concert with one another or participate as individual assets.

The flexibility of this load shift technology provides resilient power capacity and serves as a load stabilizing complement to intermittent clean energy assets central to California’s 100% clean energy by 2045 goal.

This investment supports the Biden-Harris Administration’s Justice40 Initiative, which sets a goal that 40% of the overall benefits of certain federal investments in clean energy flow to disadvantaged communities marginalized by underinvestment and overburdened by pollution.

LPO borrowers are required to develop and ultimately implement a comprehensive Community Benefits Plan that ensures meaningful engagement with communities and labor groups to create good-paying jobs and improve resident and worker well-being.
Nostromo will target installing at least 20% of these projects in disadvantaged communities, further improving the reliability of local systems.
As a VPP, Project IceBrick supports a higher rate of grid asset utilization, tempering cost increases for Californians who face some of the highest electricity bills in the nation.
Project IceBrick represents the third VPP project LPO has announced and the first to use cold TES.
VPPs are aggregations of electrified, grid-connected devices, including grid interactive efficient buildings.
VPPs reduce utilities’ reliance on natural gas peaker plants and reduce strain on transmission and distribution infrastructure by intelligently time-shifting and shaving electricity demand.

By shifting commercial cooling loads away from times of peak usage, when electricity is at its most carbon-intensive, Nostromo’s IceBrick systems would allow California’s bulk power system to avoid up to 500 thousand tonnes of CO2 emissions over the project’s lifetime.
With Project IceBrick, Nostromo will share with its customers a portion of the expected cost savings attributable to this aggregate shift in building cooling load.
Nostromo plans to earn additional revenue by having the VPP participate in wholesale energy and capacity markets.
At full scale, the project could provide the equivalent of approximately 170 MW (450 MWh) of behind-the-meter storage capacity for hotels, offices, data centers, and other commercial buildings.
Individual IceBrick thermal storage cells are modular and compact, allowing for installation in a variety of building types as well as locations within a building, such as in a basement or on a roof.
Nostromo’s VPP software can control operation and performance of IceBrick systems either as standalone systems or in concert as a VPP.
LPO’s conditional commitment to Nostromo demonstrates the success of President Biden’s Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) in accelerating deployment of clean energy and storage systems.

The loan guarantee, if finalized, will be offered through the Innovation Energy category of LPO’s Title 17 Clean Energy Financing Program, which includes financing opportunities for projects that deploy new or significantly improved high-impact clean energy technologies.

Interest in projects like IceBrick is also supported by the IRA’s Section 48 Investment Tax Credit, which allows businesses to deduct a significant percentage of the installation costs of TES and other clean technology installations from their federal taxes.

While this conditional commitment indicates DOE’s intent to finance the project, DOE must complete an environmental review, and the company must satisfy certain technical, legal, environmental, commercial, and financial conditions before the Department can decide whether to enter into definitive financing documents and fund the loan guarantee.
source_title: LPO Announces Conditional Commitment to Nostromo Energy to Enhance Grid Reliability and Cut Air Conditioning Energy Waste for Large Buildings in California | Department of Energy

facts: Southern Company will build or upgrade an estimated 16.7 GW of energy generation capacity from gas, nuclear, hydropower, storage, and transmission projects across two states, providing direct cost savings to customers.
In February 2026, EDF closed an unprecedented $26.5 billion in loans to support two wholly owned subsidiaries of Southern Company—Alabama Power and Georgia Power.
The investment is funded under President Trump’s Working Families Tax Cut.
The project will lower American energy costs, create thousands of jobs, and increase grid reliability in Georgia and Alabama.

As of the loan closing, more than 10,000 megawatts (MW) of new generation and upgrades to existing generation are planned to be added across Georgia and Alabama to provide base load power and forecastable, dispatchable capacity for electric customers.
New natural gas generation projects underway in Georgia include Plants Bowen, Wansley, McIntosh and Yates, among other sites.

Southern Company is making investments to modernize and upgrade hydroelectric generation facilities from the Coosa River in Alabama, to the mountains of north Georgia to the lakes and rivers of central and western Georgia.

Following the completion of Vogtle Units 3 and 4—the first new nuclear units constructed in more than 30 years and also supported by previous DOE loans—Southern Company is upgrading Vogtle Units 1 and 2 to enable those units to deliver additional energy for customers for decades to come.
Southern Company is planning for additional future upgrades at Plant Hatch and Plant Farley.
The historic investment will expand and enhance a diverse generation mix to deliver over 16 gigawatts (GW) of firm reliable power to the electrical grid.

The project includes 5 GW of new gas generation, 6 GW in nuclear improved through upgrades and license renewals, hydropower modernization, battery energy storage systems, and over 1,300 miles of transmission and grid enhancement projects.
The loan will deliver over $7 billion in electricity cost savings to millions of American customers in Georgia and Alabama.
The loan will create and sustain thousands of jobs across the two states.
Southern Company is listed as the owner of the project.
Project locations span various sites across Alabama and Georgia.
The EDF Tech Sector for this initiative is Utilities.
Financing is provided through the Energy Dominance Financing Program under a loan structure.
The total loan amount is $26.5 billion, issued in February 2026.
U.S. jobs supported are estimated at thousands at the time of closing.
Customer savings are estimated at $7 billion at the time of closing.
source_title: Southern Company | Department of Energy

facts: Kautex Textron GmbH & Co. KG (Kautex), a Textron Inc. (NYSE: TXT) company, announced it has received an order from a leading automotive OEM for a full-BEV thermoplastic composite lower battery housing unit.
The lower housing is part of the company’s Pentatonic battery enclosure portfolio, facilitating battery electric and hybrid electric vehicle production.
Joerg Rautenstrauch serves as President & CEO of Kautex.
The Kautex Pentatonic product line offers lightweight, customizable EV solutions made from thermoplastic composite or composite metal hybrids.
These solutions enhance the thermal management of the battery pack and contribute to overall lightweight design, boosting vehicle performance and extending driving range.

The lower housing seals and protects the vehicle battery while meeting extensive structural and stringent thermal requirements, including crush and bottom impact testing, and external and internal fire tests.
The housing will be produced for a major BEV platform spanning across multiple vehicle brands.
The Pentatonic solution offers lower cost compared to conventional metal counterparts due to reduced components from high functional integration and a leaner bill of material.
The thermoplastic composite material helps lower the carbon footprint of the part, while the lightweight housing reduces carbon emissions during driving.
The multi-material approach provides corrosion resistance without compromising performance throughout the vehicle's lifespan.
This order marks Kautex’s second Pentatonic business win, following an initial success in January 2023 with a full-BEV underbody protection skid plate that entered serial production in 2024.
The Pentatonic product line has won the SPE Grand Innovation Award in Europe, as well as the Lingxuan and Gasgoo Innovation Awards in China.
Kautex has deployed three on-road test vehicles in the US, Japan, and Europe to validate full-BEV and hybrid Pentatonic enclosures across various terrains and weather conditions.
These test vehicles have collectively been road-tested for over 100,000 miles.
Kautex operates as a Tier One automotive supplier with 30 plants in 13 countries.
Kautex is a pioneer in the design and manufacture of traditional and hybrid fuel systems, engine camshafts, and plastic industrial packaging solutions.
Kautex is expanding its portfolio to offer thermoplastic composite and composite-metal hybrid battery systems, as well as assisted and autonomous drive vehicle cleaning systems.
Textron Inc. is a multi-industry company leveraging a global network of aircraft, defense, industrial, and finance businesses.
Textron owns brands including Bell, Cessna, Beechcraft, Pipistrel, Jacobsen, Kautex, Lycoming, E-Z-GO, and Textron Systems.
source_title: Kautex Textron Wins Significant BEV Battery Housing Contract with Leading Automotive OEM

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE), through its Loan Programs Office (LPO), announced the closing of a $2.5 billion loan to Ultium Cells LLC on December 12, 2022.
The loan will help finance the construction of new lithium-ion battery cell manufacturing facilities in Ohio, Tennessee, and Michigan.

Ultium Cells is a joint venture between General Motors and LG Energy Solution that will manage battery cell production at the three facilities to address growing U.S. consumer demand for electric vehicles (EVs).
The project is expected to create approximately more than 11,000 good-paying jobs across the three facilities, including 6,000 construction jobs and 5,100 operations jobs.
The newly-organized Warren, Ohio facility will include more than 700 United Auto Worker jobs.
In October, President Biden launched the American Battery Materials Initiative alongside $2.8 billion in grants from DOE to build out the battery mineral and material supply chain.
The loan supports President Biden’s goals to reach net zero emissions by 2050 and have EVs make up half of all new vehicle sales by 2030.

U.S. Secretary of Energy Jennifer M. Granholm stated that the loan will jumpstart domestic battery cell production needed to reduce reliance on other countries, support widespread EV adoption, and cut carbon pollution from gas-powered vehicles.

U.S. Senator Sherrod Brown (OH) stated that investing in American production and Ohio workers is part of a pro-American, pro-worker industrial policy and will help Ohio lead the country and world in producing sustainable technology and electric vehicles.

U.S. Senator Debbie Stabenow (MI) stated she authored the Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing Program to ensure clean vehicles and innovative technologies are made at home, noting the investment will create thousands of good-paying jobs, strengthen the supply chain, and help address the climate crisis.
U.S. Representative Tim Ryan (OH-13) stated his advocacy for the Mahoning Valley to become the nation’s hub of electric vehicle production yielded a major DOE investment in the workforce.
In July, LPO announced a conditional commitment for the loan to Ultium Cells to manufacture large format, pouch-type cells that use state-of-the-art chemistry to deliver more range at less cost.
The battery cells can be arranged in different combinations to provide clean, reliable energy for pickups, SUVs, family vehicles, luxury vehicles, and commercial vehicles.
Ultium Cells plans to use this technology in coordination with General Motors’ work to eliminate 100% of tailpipe emissions from its new U.S. light-duty vehicles by 2035.
The project supports GM’s plans to install capacity to produce more than one million EVs annually in North America and make its global products and operations carbon neutral by 2040.
Today’s announcement marks LPO’s first closed loan exclusively for a battery cell manufacturing project under the Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing (ATVM) program.

Financing from the ATVM program complements historic investments from the President’s Bipartisan Infrastructure Law, including $7.5 billion for EV charging infrastructure and more than $7 billion for critical minerals supply chains necessary for batteries, components, materials, and recycling.

The Inflation Reduction Act appropriates $3 billion for the costs of direct loans to remain available through September 30, 2028, and expands uses to medium- and heavy-duty vehicles, maritime vessels, aviation, and other transportation modes.
The Inflation Reduction Act provides a clear demand signal for EV batteries as the auto industry builds future vehicles in America.

As of the end of October, DOE attracted 98 active applications across all LPO’s new and improved programs, totaling over $104 billion in requested loans and loan guarantees for projects across the country.
source_title: U.S. Department of Energy Announces $2.5 Billion Loan to Ultium Cells for Three Domestic Battery Cell Manufacturing Facilities | Department of Energy

facts: The Alternative Fuels Data Center provides a Medium- and Heavy-Duty Vehicle Search tool for finding and comparing alternative fuel vehicles, engines, and power sources.
BYD C10MS (45' double decker) is an electric vehicle with a 159-mile electric-only range and a 446 kWh battery capacity, seating up to 59 + 18 passengers.
The BYD C10MS utilizes two BYD Asynchronous Motors rated at 3-Phase 180 kW each.
According to the manufacturer, the BYD C10MS requires 3–3.5 hours for charging and includes a 12-year battery warranty.
BYD C8M (35') is an electric vehicle with a 149-mile electric-only range and a 313 kWh battery capacity, seating up to 41 passengers.
The BYD C8M utilizes two BYD Asynchronous Motors rated at 3-Phase 180 kW each.
According to the manufacturer, the BYD C8M requires 2–2.5 hours for charging and includes a 12-year battery warranty.
BYD C8MS (35' double decker) is an electric vehicle with a 125-mile electric-only range and a 313 kWh battery capacity, seating up to 39 + 12 passengers.
The BYD C8MS utilizes two BYD Asynchronous Motors rated at 3-Phase 180 kW each.
According to the manufacturer, the BYD C8MS requires 2–2.5 hours for charging and includes a 12-year battery warranty.
BYD Class 6F Box Truck is an electric vehicle with a 250-mile electric-only range powered by a BYD 390 kW electric motor.
The BYD Class 6F Box Truck has a gross vehicle weight rating of 26,000 lbs, produces up to 523 HP and 2,360 lb-ft of torque, and is available with either a 281 kWh or 343 kWh battery.
According to the manufacturer, the BYD Class 6F Box Truck supports charging capacity up to 120 kW, charges from 20%-80% in 2-3 hours, and includes an 8-year battery warranty.
BYD Creator Type C is an electric vehicle with a 170-mile electric-only range and a 282 kWh battery capacity, seating up to 77 passengers.
The BYD Creator Type C uses a BYD AC Synchronous Motor rated at 260 kW and features bi-directional V2G charging capability.

According to the manufacturer, the BYD Creator Type C is available with either an 188 kWh or 282 kWh battery, takes 9-15 hours for Level 2 charging or 1.5-2.5 hours for DC Fast charging depending on battery size, and includes a 12-year battery warranty.
BYD Dreamer Type D is an electric vehicle with a 155-mile electric-only range and a 255 kWh battery capacity, seating up to 81 passengers.
The BYD Dreamer Type D uses two BYD AC Synchronous Motors rated at 150 kW each and features bi-directional V2G charging capability.

According to the manufacturer, the BYD Dreamer Type D is a 40-foot vehicle that supports Level 2 charging up to 19.2 kW (taking 14.5–15 hours) or Level 3 charging up to 110 kW (taking 2.5–3 hours), and includes a 12-year battery warranty.
BYD K11M (60') is an electric vehicle with a 193-mile electric-only range, seating for 55 passengers, and utilizes two BYD AC/DC Asynchronous Motors rated at 3-Phase 180 kW each.
According to the manufacturer, the BYD K11M features a 576 kWh battery that requires 4-4.5 hours to charge and includes a 12-year battery warranty.
BYD K7M (30') is an electric vehicle seating up to 22 passengers, utilizing two BYD Asynchronous Motors rated at 3-Phase 90 kW each.
According to the manufacturer, the BYD K7M features a 213 kWh battery providing up to 158 miles of range, requires 1.5–2 hours to charge, and includes a 12-year battery warranty.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Vehicle Search

facts: Mercedes-Benz USA (MBUSA) is headquartered in Atlanta, GA.
The Daimler Board of Management appointed Dimitris Psillakis as Head of Marketing and Sales, Mercedes-Benz Cars North America and CEO of MBUSA, effective January 1, 2021.
Psillakis reports to Britta Seeger, Member of the Board of Management of Daimler AG and Mercedes-Benz AG, responsible for Marketing and Sales.
Psillakis succeeds Nicholas Speeks, who will leave the company after more than 40 years.
Psillakis will be based in Atlanta, GA.
In his new role, Psillakis assumes overall responsibility for Mercedes-Benz Cars in the United States and Canada.
He leads MBUSA's more than 1,600 employees and more than 380 dealer partners throughout the U.S.
He also oversees an additional 1,200 employees and almost 60 authorized dealers throughout Canada.
Psillakis will continue as President and CEO of Mercedes-Benz Canada for the remainder of the year.
For the interim period, MBUSA’s VP of Marketing Drew J. Slaven will serve as acting Head of Marketing and Sales, Mercedes-Benz Cars North America and CEO of MBUSA.
Psillakis joined Daimler AG in 1992 as a management trainee with Mercedes-Benz Hellas and was promoted to general manager of passenger cars in 2001.

In 2009, Psillakis moved to Mercedes-Benz do Brasil Ltda to serve as managing director for passenger cars in South America and Vans Sales Brazil, where his team tripled sales volume and reached the #1 spot in Brazil over six years.

In 2015, Psillakis joined Mercedes-Benz Korea (MBK) as President and CEO, expanding the South Korean market to the 5th largest globally for Mercedes-Benz Cars while achieving top customer satisfaction ratings.
MBUSA is responsible for the distribution, marketing, and customer service for all Mercedes-Benz products in the United States.
MBUSA offers drivers a lineup of 15 model lines ranging from the A-Class sedan to the flagship S-Class and the Mercedes-AMG GT R.
MBUSA is also responsible for Mercedes-Benz Vans products in the U.S.

Britta Seeger stated that Dimitris is a proven leader who successfully expanded the growth of Mercedes-Benz in key markets around the world and will continue to advance North American operations during a time of unprecedented transformation.
source_title: Mercedes-Benz USA Announces Dimitris Psillakis as Head of Marketing and Sales, Mercedes-Benz Cars North America and CEO of MBUSA

facts: Rivian will expand its manufacturing operations, locating its second U.S. plant in the state of Georgia.
A carbon-conscious campus is planned east of Atlanta, in Morgan and Walton Counties.
The project represents a $5-billion site development and manufacturing investment.
The plant will eventually employ more than 7,500 workers.
Once ramped, the Georgia facility will be capable of producing up to 400,000 vehicles per year.
Construction on the facility is expected to begin in summer 2022.
The start of production is slated for 2024.
Site considerations included logistics, environmental impact, renewable energy production, availability and quality of talent, and fit with Rivian company culture.

Rivian Chief People Officer Helen Russell stated that the company is happy to partner with Georgia on the new manufacturing site to build next-generation products important for scaling the business, noting that the work together is driven by a compelling purpose and rooted in building a highly collaborative environment that creates a true sense of belonging.
Helen Russell expressed excitement for Georgians to bring their creativity and ideas to Rivian, stating that alongside Illinois, Georgia is the place for Rivian to continue to scale and thrive.
Rivian’s parcel for the new site will be almost 2,000 acres and include abundant natural space.
As with its facility in Normal, Rivian will develop community engagement and workforce training programs in the area.
Rivian is also scaling capacity at its existing plant in Normal, Illinois.
Rivian’s Normal plant was recently approved for a 623,000-square-foot expansion, bringing the total footprint to approximately 4 million square feet.
There are further plans to extend warehouse, storage, and production capacity onsite at the Normal facility.
Rivian plans to hire an additional 800-1,000 employees in Normal by the second quarter of 2022.
Rivian and Georgia are planning 2022 town hall events near the manufacturing site for area residents to learn about the company and site plan.
Rivian offers industry-leading compensation, generous benefits, and equity in the company for every team member.
New Normal and Georgia-based positions were posted on December 16, 2021.

links.context: Project RFIs, RFPs listings, and supply chain outreach inquiries

source_title: Rivian to site second manufacturing plant in Georgia

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp applauded Morgan Stanley’s continued progress in growing its workforce across several locations in the metro Atlanta area.
The company confirmed it is close to reaching its goal set in 2020 of adding 1,800 Georgians to its workforce.
Many of these new positions are located at Morgan Stanley's Alpharetta campus.
More than 4,000 Georgians currently work across the firm’s offices in metro Atlanta and statewide in a variety of roles and functions.
Roles include Technology, Research, Human Resources and Operations and support roles for Morgan Stanley at Work and E*TRADE from Morgan Stanley.

Governor Kemp stated Georgia is the No. 1 state for business for nine years running and continues to attract companies from diverse industries that bring opportunity to communities all across the state.

Kemp noted Morgan Stanley continues to grow its presence in Georgia because the state's workforce and partnership approach lead to success, ensuring Georgia remains at the cutting edge of the financial services industry.
Morgan Stanley is a leading global financial services firm providing investment banking, securities, wealth management, and investment management services.
The company has offices in more than 42 countries, with employees serving clients worldwide including corporations, governments, institutions, and individuals.
The company’s presence in Georgia was bolstered by its acquisition of E*TRADE Financial Corporation in 2020.
Ekene Ezulike, Global Head of Corporate & Enterprise Services at Morgan Stanley, stated Alpharetta expansion will play a key role in growing and broading their talent base to support operations.
Ezulike appreciated the support of the State of Georgia and looked forward to deepening commitment to the Atlanta Metropolitan area.
Katie Kirkpatrick, President and CEO of the Metro Atlanta Chamber, welcomed Morgan Stanley's growth in metro Atlanta, citing the strength it adds to both the fintech and financial services community.
Kirkpatrick committed to working with partners to ensure success and seamless integration into the region while continuing to build the top business ecosystem in the country.
Alpharetta Mayor Jim Gilvin stated Alpharetta was selected for Morgan Stanley's growth due to shared core values including 'do the right thing', and looked forward to strong community connections.

Robb Pitts, Chairman of the Fulton County Board of Commissioners, called Fulton County a leading destination for the nation’s best talent and noted the announcement speaks to community quality and workforce strength.

Director of Statewide Projects Barton Lowrey and Senior Project Manager John Soper represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce division on this project with the City of Alpharetta, Select Fulton, Metro Atlanta Chamber, the University System of Georgia, and Georgia Power.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated it is gratifying when a company heavily invested in the state doubles down and chooses to grow again.

Wilson noted Morgan Stanley’s decision to significantly expand their campus and add well-paying jobs to the fintech ecosystem testifies to diverse talent from education systems, and exemplifies the state’s strength in attracting and retaining talent due to high quality of life.
source_title: Gov. Kemp Applauds Morgan Stanley’s Commitment to Georgia Job Creation | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Novelis Inc. broke ground on a $36 million investment to expand and upgrade recycling capacity at its Greensboro, Ga. facility.
The investment includes adding state-of-the-art equipment for aluminum scrap recycling.
The project features a new baghouse for improved dust mitigation.
The upgrades include enhanced designs for safer and more efficient traffic flow within the facility.
Novelis expects to complete the expansion project by fall of 2021.
The investment expands Novelis' capabilities in automotive closed-loop recycling, a process that takes aluminum scrap created during stamping and recycles it for new vehicle production.
Closing the loop preserves the value of the alloy, reduces cost, minimizes environmental impact, and establishes a secure supply chain.
The Greensboro facility will continue to recycle used beverage cans for the beverage packaging industry.

Marco Palmieri, SVP and President of Novelis North America, stated that automakers are choosing aluminum as a sustainable material of choice, prompting investments to increase recycled content in new vehicles and reduce carbon emissions.
Palmieri noted the investment aligns with Novelis' purpose of "Shaping a Sustainable World Together" and demonstrates a commitment to helping customers meet their sustainability targets.
The facility upgrades will enhance safety systems by more effectively separating pedestrian walkways from mobile equipment traffic.
Beatriz Landa, Novelis Greensboro Plant Manager, stated the team consists of 145 employees in Greensboro with a proud tradition of offering innovative solutions in aluminum recycling.
Landa added that the investment enables the facility to continue modernizing its capabilities to serve an even broader customer base for years to come.
Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp congratulated Novelis on the expansion, noting that any home-based company expansion is good news for business in Georgia.
Governor Kemp highlighted Novelis' 40-year history in Greensboro has created countless opportunities for hardworking Georgians in the region.
Congressman Jody Hice stated manufacturing is an important ingredient to Georgia's thriving economy and expressed thrill at Novelis growing its footprint in the 10th District.
Hice described Novelis as a global leader in the aluminum industry and the largest recycler of aluminum in the world.
The Greensboro facility opened in 1980 and is responsible for pioneering the majority of Novelis' recycling technology.
Novelis Inc. is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, operates 23 facilities in 9 countries, has approximately 11,000 employees, and recorded $12.3 billion in revenue for its 2019 fiscal year.

Novelis is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited, an industry leader in aluminum and copper, and the metals flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group, a multinational conglomerate based in Mumbai, India.

Novelis partners with customers in the automotive, beverage can, and specialties industries to deliver sustainable lightweight aluminum solutions throughout North America, Europe, Asia, and South America.
source_title: Novelis Invests $36 Million to Expand, Upgrade Aluminum Recycling Capabilities in Greensboro, Ga.

facts: U.S. Secretary of Energy Chris Wright joined executives of Century Aluminum to celebrate progress toward constructing a primary aluminum smelter in Inola, Oklahoma.
The project is supported through a grant from the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE).
February 10, 2026 marks the date of the announcement and celebration.
The project demonstrates the economic viability of domestic smelting and reduces U.S. reliance on imports.
Secretary Wright stated the project will deliver America’s first new aluminum smelter of the 21st century.
Secretary Wright attributed the project's realization to President Trump’s commitment to revitalizing the manufacturing base and reducing reliance on foreign suppliers.
Century Aluminum’s joint venture demonstrates how President Trump’s economic policies encourage global companies to partner with U.S. firms, build in America, and create good-paying American jobs.
Once complete, the plant will be the largest-ever primary aluminum production plant in the U.S.
The plant has the potential to produce over 500,000 tons of high-quality, primary aluminum per year.
Approximately 20,000 tons of the annual output will be high-purity aluminum suitable for national defense applications.
On January 26, 2026, Century Aluminum announced plans to jointly develop the plant with Emirates Global Aluminum (EGA).
EGA’s investment to help develop the multi-billion-dollar smelter project in Oklahoma was secured by President Trump.
The investment is part of a strategic partnership announced between the United Arab Emirates and the United States in Abu Dhabi on May 15, 2025.
In 2025, DOE’s Office of Clean Energy Demonstrations (OCED) awarded $500 million to advance construction of the first new primary aluminum smelter in the United States since 1980.
The project reduces dependence on foreign imports.
In 2024, the United States imported 5.46 million metric tons and exported 3 million metric tons of downstream aluminum products and scrap.
This resulted in net imports of 2.46 million metric tons in 2024.
There are currently four primary aluminum smelters operating in the United States that produce 683,500 metric tons.
This production is down from America’s historical ranking as the world’s leading producer when 33 sites were in operation producing up to 5 million tons per year.
President Trump is ending this trend and re-shoring manufacturing by safeguarding and expanding domestic production of critical minerals and materials through tariffs and other trade policies.
These trade policies have made investments like this in American projects possible.
source_title: Energy Department Awardee to Build First American Aluminum Smelter Since 1980 | Department of Energy

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd. (PAS) and Arm announced a strategic partnership to standardize automotive architecture for Software-Defined Vehicles (SDVs).
The two organizations share a common vision for creating a software stack with the necessary flexibility to meet current and future needs of the automotive industry.

They aligned on this vision through their active participation in SOAFEE, an industry-wide initiative promoting collaboration for the development of standardized software within the automotive market.
Under this new partnership, PAS and Arm will apply and expand the VirtIO device virtualization framework to decouple automotive software development from hardware.
This initiative aims to accelerate development cycles in the automotive industry.
The announcement was issued from Yokohama, Japan & Cambridge, England.

links.context: Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd. corporate communications office

source_title: Panasonic Automotive Systems en Arm werken samen om Software-Defined Vehicles te standaardiseren

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on September 7, 2023 that Pratt Industries will build a new production facility in Warner Robins (Peach County), Georgia.
The new location will create more than 125 jobs and over $120 million in investment.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that expansions like this account for more than 70 percent of last year's economic development projects.

Pratt Industries is based in Atlanta, is the fifth-largest corrugated packaging company in the United States, and is the largest privately owned producer of 100 percent recycled containerboard in the world.
Anthony Pratt, Global Executive Chairman for Pratt Industries, stated that the new facility will bring the company's total workforce in Georgia to over 2,100 employees and 11,700 nationwide.
The new factory will be the company’s 13th site in Georgia and will bring the company’s total investment in Georgia to over $800 million.
Anthony Pratt stated that Georgia has substantially grown its role as a regional hub for supporting businesses and manufacturing needs across the Southeast.
The facility will be located at Robins Industrial Park, a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) certified site in Warner Robins within Peach County.
The planned facility will be 496,000 square feet and will be one of the most modern of the company’s 72 factories spread across 25 states.
The facility will produce corrugated boxes using 100 percent recycled containerboard, largely sourced from the company’s mill in Conyers, Georgia.
Operations are expected to begin in late 2024.
Warner Robins Mayor LaRhonda W. Patrick stated that Pratt's investment will be the largest single private investment in the history of the city and will provide over 100 new careers.

Peach County Board of Commissioners Chairman Martin Moseley stated that the project brings opportunities for good jobs to citizens and will be an asset to other industries in Middle Georgia that rely on Pratt's products.

Senior Project Manager John Soper represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project in partnership with the Development Authority of Peach County and Georgia Power.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that by investing in site preparation and obtaining GRAD certification, communities are better positioned to attract investments that create jobs aligned with their strategic goals.
Pratt Industries was founded in the U.S. more than 35 years ago and operates manufacturing facilities in more than 25 states.

The company operates an extensive recycling company to supply six modern 100 percent recycled paper mills located in Conyers, Georgia; Staten Island, New York; Valparaiso, Indiana; Shreveport, Louisiana; Wapakoneta, Ohio; and Henderson, Kentucky.

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) has been headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia since 1998.
PCNA was established in 1984.
PCNA serves as the exclusive U.S. importer for the Porsche 911, 718 Boxster, 718 Cayman, Macan, Cayenne, Panamera and Taycan.
PCNA operates a Porsche Experience Center in Atlanta featuring a module-based 1.6 mile driver development track, business center, and fine dining restaurant, 356.
PCNA supports 193 independently owned and operated Porsche dealerships in the U.S., including supplying parts, service, marketing, and training.
PCNA is an indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of Porsche AG, which is headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany.
Porsche boasts some 30,000-plus motorsport wins to date.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Sewon America, a subsidiary of Korean-based Sewon Precision Industry Co., will create 740 new jobs in Rincon.
Sewon America will invest more than $300 million in a new Effingham County manufacturing facility.
This project is the largest known private investment in the City of Rincon, to-date.
The announcement fulfills the commitment to create jobs and investment in all four Savannah Harbor Interstate-16 Joint Development Authority (JDA) counties.
This fulfillment is a result of Hyundai’s Metaplant location in Bryan County.
Hyundai’s Metaplant is the largest economic development project in state history.
Suppliers for the Metaplant have now committed over $1.8 billion in investment.
Supplier investments surpass initial estimates by at least $800 million.
Governor Brian P. Kemp celebrated delivering on the promise to create jobs in all four JDA counties and the growth of a valued partner.
On his first international trip as governor to Korea, he congratulated Chairman Kim on the company’s then-recent expansion and discussed continuing to work together.
Those discussions led to this announcement as Sewon America continues to grow their Georgia operations.
Speaker of the House Jon Burns represents part of Effingham County and played a role in securing the project and engaging Sewon America executives.
Lt. Governor Burt Jones commended Governor Brian Kemp, Sewon America, and stakeholders on the historic economic development announcement for Rincon.
Lt. Governor Burt Jones noted investments by companies like Sewon build on Georgia’s commitment to remain the No. 1 state in which to do business while attracting a robust workforce.
Sewon America operates one of the largest automobile body parts manufacturing plants in North America.
The company opened a manufacturing facility in LaGrange in 2008, which has since doubled in size.
Following completion of the Rincon facility, Sewon is expected to support 1,600 jobs in Georgia.
Vice Chairman Kim stated Sewon America was founded 15 years ago in west Georgia and expressed excitement about building another facility in east Georgia as one family in two locations.
Growth is possible thanks to full support from the Sewon team, Governor Kemp, the Georgia Department of Economic Development, and Effingham County.
Sewon’s new facility will be located at the Grande View industrial park in Rincon.
The new manufacturing location will produce electric vehicle (EV) body parts for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs), including Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in 2025.

Rincon Mayor Ken Lee welcomed Sewon America and commended partners at the Effingham County Industrial Development Authority for bringing the largest employment and private investment project in the city’s history.
Chairman of the Effingham County Industrial Development Authority (IDA) Troy Smith stated Sewon America selected Grande View industrial park for significant investment and future growth.
The IDA chairman noted the investment will allow current and future generations to have fulfilling, well-paying careers without leaving Effingham County.
Executive Director of the Georgia Ports Authority Griff Lynch called the announcement terrific news for Effingham County and Georgia and stated the authority is excited to support Sewon’s growth.
Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy Alyce Thornhill represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
GDEcD partnered with the Effingham County Industrial Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia EMC for this project.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted Sewon integrated into and lent industry experience to Georgia’s automotive community through work with local colleges, Georgia Quick Start, and statewide initiatives like Georgia Made.
Sewon is reinvesting in Georgia while supporting Hyundai Motor Group’s operations from Effingham County, which has the skilled workforce, infrastructure, and collaborative spirit to support growth.
On October 25, 2022, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground in neighboring Bryan County on its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility.
Georgia is positioned as a hub for the electric mobility industry, building on assets that make the automotive industry successful.
Since 2020, more than 35 EV-related projects have announced more than $21 billion in investment and 26,700 jobs in Georgia.
Sewon America was established in 2008 in LaGrange as a Georgia company primarily operating in the Motor Vehicles and Car Bodies industry within the Transportation Equipment sector.
Sewon produces stamped body components and is celebrated for high-quality automotive parts, continuing this tradition in the new Rincon facility.
source_title: Sewon America Announces 740 new jobs in Effingham County – Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority

facts: Georgia ranks No. 1 in Logistics and Infrastructure according to Area Development (2025).
Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport serves more than 150 U.S. destinations, 70 international destinations, and 40 countries.
The Port of Savannah hosts more than 30 ship calls per week.
The Port of Brunswick ranks No. 1 for roll-on/roll-off cargo.
Georgia has over 4,600 miles of active rail lines and two Class I railroads, boasting one of the most extensive freight rail systems in the U.S.
Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport is the world’s busiest and most efficient passenger airport, with an average of 2,100 arrivals and departures moving 286,000 passengers daily.
The airport's cargo operation includes three main air cargo complexes totaling 1.8 million square feet of cargo handling space.
Each airport cargo complex is equipped with its own refueling and support systems as well as direct access to four interstates: I-75, I-85, I-285, and I-20.
Delta Air Lines is headquartered in Atlanta.
Delta serves seven of Georgia’s nine regional commercial airports and offers nonstop flights to more than 200 domestic and international destinations.
The Port of Savannah is the western-most port on the Atlantic, offering faster access to major inland markets and the largest refrigerated capacity on the East Coast.
The Port of Brunswick is the nation’s No. 1 Ro/Ro port, with 1,700 acres of dedicated auto and heavy equipment processing space.
Georgia has more than 20,000 miles of major interstate-connected highway.
Georgia's inland ports extend the reach of deepwater seaports by shifting freight to rail and lowering transportation costs.
Appalachian Regional Port provides a direct CSX rail link between the Port of Savannah and the Southeast.
The Blue Ridge Connector is a Norfolk Southern-served terminal opening in 2026 that will connect directly to the Port of Savannah.
Georgia has the eighth-largest refrigerated space capacity in the U.S. at 300+ million cubic feet, allowing perishables and temperature-sensitive products to move safely and efficiently.
Half percent of the world’s top 3PLs operate in Georgia, including Americold.
Georgia was the first state in the U.S. to precisely map the availability of broadband services and identify areas that need greater access.
State-led and regional initiatives are aimed at closing broadband access gaps across the state.
Two of the nation’s largest fiber optic trunk lines intersect in Atlanta, along with two major research lines.
This broadband access directly facilitates the continuing growth of Georgia’s “Transaction Alley,” which extends from Columbus in west Georgia, across Atlanta, to Georgia’s eastern border in Augusta.
Georgia’s three primary utility providers are Georgia Power, Georgia Electric Membership Corporation (Georgia EMC), and Electric Cities of Georgia (ECG).
These utilities offer reliable, affordable energy to businesses of all sizes with long-term, advanced planning to accommodate large load users and provide responsive service crews.
Through the Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) program and the Rural Site Development Initiative (RSDI), Georgia maintains a portfolio of development-ready sites statewide.
These site readiness programs reduce risk, minimize delays, and support long-term operational stability in both urban and rural markets.
source_title: Infrastructure | Georgia

facts: Solvay Specialty Polymers USA plans to shift operations to Augusta, GA, to produce PVDF, a plastic component used in lithium-ion batteries.
Synesqo, the new parent company of Solvay Specialty Polymers, received a $178M grant from the U.S. Department of Energy for the facility.
The proposed expansion will be located in Augusta's 30906 zip code, identified as one of the most vulnerable areas in the city.
Solvay is currently one of the largest emitters of pollution in Augusta and ranks among the city’s top 5 polluters to air, water, and land by RSEI Hazard score.
The facility discharges approximately 65,000lb of Chlorobenzene into the air every year, a chemical on the EPA’s substance priority list for human health threats.
EPA reports indicate Solvay increased carbon emissions at its Augusta site by over 70% in the last decade.
The planned expansion includes construction of a fossil-fuel powered boiler that could produce enough heat for about 13,410 homes per year, potentially generating around 50,000 tons of CO2.
In June 2023, Solvay agreed to pay $393 million to the state of New Jersey to settle allegations of contaminating water sources with PFAS chemicals.
Prior to 2010, Solvay discharged over 100,000 pounds of PFAS (PFNA) into New Jersey’s air and waterways.
In 2013, the Delaware River was found to have a higher concentration of PFNA than anywhere else in the world due to Solvay's operations.
Solvay has a documented history of withholding chemical toxicity data from regulators and the public, often citing trade secrets or proprietary information.
The company discovered in 2006 that its PFOA chemical was toxic in animal studies but did not report findings to the EPA until 2012.
Solvay found PFAS chemicals accumulating in workers' blood in 2011 but delayed reporting this data until 2019.
In 2021, the Environmental Working Group petitioned the EPA, alleging Solvay violated federal law by withholding crucial health hazard information.
The expansion aims to support the global transition to renewable energy and EV batteries, but local communities fear worsening environmental and health impacts in an already disadvantaged area.
Experts like Martin Scheringer, a professor and renowned PFAS expert at ETH Zurich, argue that industries relying on PFAS require rigorous oversight due to their track record of long-term harm.

The page recommends that before Solvay operates in Georgia, the city should establish independent third-party testing, increased public transparency for all findings, and enhanced regulatory enforcement placing the burden of proof on Solvay.
Richmond County's Sci4Ga Burden Score is ranked as the 3rd worst, with 80% of its census tracts having stroke rates higher than the national average.
Citizens in Augusta's highest-burden and lowest-income census tracts live on average 1-4 years less than an average Georgian.
Augusta's environmental burdens are historically rooted in racial segregation, industrialization (textiles, paper manufacturing, chemical production), and urban planning decisions.
source_title: Environmental Justice Problems and Solutions in Augusta – Science for Georgia

facts: Georgia Tech and Stryten Energy LLC announced the successful installation of Stryten Energy’s Lead Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) at the Carbon Neutral Energy Solutions Laboratory (CNES).
The CNES building is located in the North Avenue Research Area of the Georgia Tech campus.
The CNES houses the Strategic Energy Institute (SEI), an interdisciplinary research institute focused on energy research.
SEI hosts multiple research groups dedicated to renewable energy and energy infrastructure-related topics.
The BESS installation aims to create a living-learning lab on campus that supports research and real-world applications of medium-duration energy storage solutions.
Lead BESS was selected for this initial installation due to its cost-effectiveness, high discharge rates, and recyclability.
Extensive research demonstrates the reliable performance of lead BESS.
The BESS integrates renewable energy sources into the existing power mix, providing stable backup power and reducing grid dependency during peak hours.
The system includes additional components and software capable of bi-directional charging, allowing current to flow into the battery for charging and out to power the grid or microgrid.
Christine Conwell serves as SEI’s interim executive director.
Georgia Tech's strategic plan envisions its campus as a dynamic laboratory and experimental test bed where sustainable practices are seamlessly integrated into operations.
Partnerships with organizations like Stryten create mini ecosystems that yield valuable situational data to help chart a path for innovative energy research.
Scott Childers is vice president of essential power at Stryten Energy.
The BESS deployment targets commercial and industrial microgrids paired with EV charging stations to help achieve U.S. energy goals.
The installation demonstrates the advantages of lead BESS from cost savings, technology, environmental, and safety perspectives.
Richard Simmons serves as SEI’s director of research and studies.
Simmons called the Stryten lead BESS system an enabling piece of the Distributed Energy Resources (DER) puzzle.
At the CNES lab, Georgia Tech researchers can control charging and discharging cycles for the battery in coordination with the existing Solar PV array and a new EV charging test bed.
This research tool allows the time-shifting of peak solar input by several hours to meet late afternoon building loads.
The system stores renewable energy for the overnight charging of campus vehicles.
Research focuses on DERs' impact on the grid, their contribution to system reliability, and their effect on energy costs during the transition to clean energy.
Simmons hopes the lead BESS will be one of several living lab battery pilots at Georgia Tech.
Along with regional partners, researchers are exploring similar R&D and testing projects involving flow batteries for longer-duration storage.
Researchers are also exploring lithium-ion BESS that may integrate second-life EV battery modules for grid resilience.
Georgia Tech is one of the top public research universities in the U.S., developing leaders who advance technology and improve the human condition.

Georgia Tech researchers work across the energy value chain in basic and applied science in EVs, photovoltaics, hydrogen, carbon capture, industrial decarbonization, grid security and resilience, and related social sciences.
Georgia Tech is consistently ranked among the top universities in the nation for graduating underrepresented minorities in engineering, physical sciences, and energy-related fields.
U.S. News & World Report ranked Georgia Tech as the No. 1 public university and No. 3 overall in energy and fuels research.
Stryten Energy is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
Stryten's stored energy technologies include advanced lead, lithium, and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers, and energy performance management software.

Email address for Stryten Energy corporate communications

source_title: Georgia Tech and Stryten Energy Unveil Installation of Lead Battery Energy Storage System for Advanced Research

facts: Stryten Energy LLC is a U.S.-based energy storage solutions provider headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
Lead BESS offers a safe, proven and reliable solution for microgrids and EV-charging applications.
The company will highlight the benefits of its lead battery energy storage systems (BESS) at Battcon 2024 in Miami.
Stryten Energy’s Lead BESS technology is designed to safeguard against power outages, ensuring continuous operation of critical infrastructure and applications when traditional power sources fail.
The technology can act as a critical grid support tool, providing rapid response capabilities to mitigate fluctuations, stabilize voltage and enhance overall grid resilience.

Stryten Energy’s Lead BESS technology can be sized to meet a wide range of applications today – from commercial and industrial to utility scale – and easily support future expansion to meet evolving power and energy storage requirements.
Scott Childers, Vice President, Essential Power at Stryten Energy, stated that lead batteries are dependable for keeping the lights on, even when other power sources are offline.

The technology already has an established domestic supply chain and a complete circular economy, providing a sustainable and cost-effective energy storage solution for businesses requiring energy resiliency for their operations.

Visitors to Booth #133 will see the key product powering Stryten Energy’s Lead BESS systems: E-Series Absolyte® AGP:2-volt valve-regulated lead-acid (VRLA) battery cells proven for telecommunications, UPS, electric utility, railroad and renewable energy applications.
Absolyte AGP cells are designed and optimized for standby float or cycling applications and are available with an ampere-hour capacity of 104-4800Ah.
The Absolyte AGP provides superior float life (20 years at 25ºC) and cycling capabilities (1200 cycles to 80% depth of discharge).

Stryten Energy helps solve the world’s most pressing energy challenges with a broad range of energy storage solutions across the Essential Power, Motive Power, Transportation, Military and Government sectors.
The company partners with some of the world’s most recognized companies to meet the growing demand for reliable and sustainable energy storage capacity.
Stryten powers everything from submarines to subcompacts, microgrids, warehouses, distribution centers, cars, trains and trucks.

Stryten's stored energy technologies include advanced lead, lithium and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers and energy performance management software that keep people on the move and supply chains running.
An industry leader backed by more than a century of expertise, Stryten has The Energy to Challenge the status quo and deliver top-performing energy solutions for today and tomorrow.
source_title: Stryten Energy to Showcase Lead Battery Energy Storage System Technology at Battcon 2024

entity_name: U.S. Department of Commerce AD/CVD Investigations on Aluminum Extrusions
canonical_name: U.S. Department of Commerce Antidumping and Countervailing Duty Investigations
facts: On October 25, 2023, the U.S. Department of Commerce announced the initiation of antidumping duty (AD) and countervailing duty (CVD) investigations of aluminum extrusions from China, Indonesia, Mexico, Turkey, Colombia, Dominican Republic, Ecuador, India, Italy, Korea, Malaysia, Taiwan, Thailand, UAE, and Vietnam.

The petitioners are the U.S. Aluminum Extrusions Coalition and the United Steel, Paper and Forestry, Rubber, Manufacturing, Energy, Allied Industrial and Service Workers International Union (Pittsburgh, PA).

The Coalition consists of 14 U.S. aluminum extruders: Alexandria Extrusion Company (Alexandria, MN); APEL Extrusions Inc. (Coburg, OR); Bonnell Aluminum (Newnan, GA); Brazeway (Adrian, MI); Custom Aluminum Products (South Elgin, IL); Extrudex Aluminum (North Jackson, OH); International Extrusions (Garden City, MI); Jordan Aluminum Company (Memphis, TN); M-D Building Products (Oklahoma City, OK); Merit Aluminum (Corona, CA); MI Metals (Oldsmar, FL); Pennex Aluminum (Wellsville, PA); Tower Extrusions (Olney, TX); Western Extrusions (Carrollton, TX).
Bonnell Aluminum is located in Newnan, GA.
China has an alleged dumping margin of 376.85%.
Colombia has an alleged dumping margin of 165.25%.
Dominican Republic has an alleged dumping margin of 28.29%.
Ecuador has an alleged dumping margin of 42.79-63.21%.
India has an alleged dumping margin of 39.05%.
Indonesia has an alleged dumping margin of 88.53%.
Italy has an alleged dumping margin of 41.67%.
Korea has an alleged dumping margin of 43.56%.
Malaysia has an alleged dumping margin of 25.89-27.51%.
Mexico has an alleged dumping margin of 76.68-82.03%.
Taiwan has an alleged dumping margin of 60.25-67.86%.
Thailand has an alleged dumping margin of 76.73%.
Turkey has an alleged dumping margin of 48.43%.
UAE has an alleged dumping margin of 42.29%.
Vietnam has an alleged dumping margin of 41.84%.
Alleged subsidy rates for China, Indonesia, Mexico, and Turkey are above de minimis (less than 1% for developed countries, less than 2% for developing countries).
Petitions were filed on October 4, 2023, with Commerce initiation dates on October 24, 2023.
ITC preliminary determinations are scheduled for November 20, 2023.
Commerce preliminary AD determinations are due March 12, 2024, and CVD preliminary determinations December 28, 2023.
Commerce final AD determinations are due May 28, 2024, and CVD final determinations March 12, 2024.
ITC final determinations are scheduled for July 12, 2024 (AD) and April 26, 2024 (CVD).
Issuance of AD orders is scheduled for July 19, 2024, and CVD orders for May 3, 2024.
The investigations cover HTSUS subheadings 7604.10.1000 through 7610.90.080.
Commerce maintains 671 AD and CVD orders providing relief to American companies and industries impacted by unfair trade.
source_title: Initiation of AD and CVD Investigations of Aluminum Extrusions from Multiple Countries

facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA), in cooperation with Glovis America, deployed Hyundai XCIENT heavy-duty hydrogen fuel-cell electric trucks for clean logistics operations.
Initially, a total of 21 XCIENT trucks will be in operation at the facility.

Oscar Kwon, HMGMA CEO, stated that the company is committed to creating sustainable transportation solutions and that the hydrogen fuel-cell trucks support Metaplant release zero emissions while offering a cleaner alternative to traditional freight logistics methods.
The deployment represents an industry-leading initiative to reduce HMGMA's carbon footprint and promote sustainable practices in daily logistics operations.
The use of zero-emissions hydrogen fuel cell technology demonstrates Hyundai Motor Group’s ongoing commitment to responsible environmental stewardship.
The Hyundai XCIENT hydrogen fuel-cell Class 8 heavy-duty trucks will transport vehicle parts from HMGMA suppliers across the region to the Megasite on a daily basis.
During the initial rollout, the XCIENT trucks will transport parts between suppliers and the on-site Consolidation Center before expanding to a broader network of suppliers.
The 21 XCIENT vehicles will represent more than one-third of the Glovis America truck fleet at HMGMA.

Earlier this year at CES, Hyundai Motor Group announced HTWO, its new hydrogen value chain business brand encompassing businesses and affiliates for production, storage, transportation, and utilization of hydrogen.

Jim Park, SVP of commercial vehicle and hydrogen business development at Hyundai Motor North America, stated that deploying XCIENT for logistics is one of the main initiatives of HTWO to showcase a closed-loop hydrogen logistics ecosystem and improve manufacturing environmental sustainability.
HTWO Logistics, a joint venture between Hyundai Motor Company and Glovis America, will install a mobile hydrogen refueling station at the Megasite for efficient refueling.
A hydrogen production and refueling station is being developed for the Megasite and will be announced at a later date.
HMGMA is Hyundai Motor Group’s first dedicated Electric Vehicle mass-production plant located in Bryan County, GA.
The new plant will feature a highly connected, automated, and flexible manufacturing system that organically connects all elements of the EV ecosystem to realize customer value.
The Georgia facility will become an intelligent manufacturing plant where order collection, procurement, logistics, and production are optimized utilizing AI and data.
The innovative manufacturing system is designed to create a human-centered work environment with robots assisting human workers.
Hyundai Motor Group is a global enterprise with about 250,000 employees worldwide, operating mobility brands including Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Sewon America, a subsidiary of Korean-based Sewon Precision Industry Co., will create 740 new jobs in Rincon.
Sewon America will invest more than $300 million in a new Effingham County manufacturing facility.
The Savannah JDA was recognized as Business Facilities magazine’s Bronze Deal of the Year Award recipient on Jan. 12, 2023, for the Hyundai Motor Group announcement.
Micron Technologies received the Gold award and Chevron Phillips Chemical received the Silver award in the same category.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Seoyon E-HWA, a global auto parts supplier that manufactures both interior and exterior components, will create 740 new direct and indirect jobs.
Seoyon E-HWA will invest almost $76 million in a new manufacturing facility in Chatham County.
Hyundai Motor America kicked off the Progress Project for the second year in honor of Black History Month.
The Progress Project by Hyundai provides financial assistance to black-owned small businesses in a region, with this year's focus on Savannah, Georgia.
Savannah is home to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, a new dedicated EV and battery manufacturing plant currently being built in Bryan County.

The Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority (Savannah JDA) announced the appointment of Maria Whitfield, a veteran human resources executive, to the position of Director of Workforce.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Ecoplastic Corporation, an automotive parts manufacturer, will create 456 new jobs.
Ecoplastic Corporation will invest approximately $205 million in a new manufacturing facility in Bulloch County.
The Savannah JDA tapped Wadley Donovan Gutshaw Consulting, LLC (WDGC) to conduct a Regional Workforce Study and Comprehensive Strategic Plan for the Savannah region.

Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hyundai Mobis, one of the world’s largest automotive suppliers, plans to invest $926 million in an Electric Vehicle (EV) Power Electric system plant in Bryan County.
The Hyundai Mobis plant will eventually employ at least 1,500 people.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Joon Georgia, Inc., an automotive parts manufacturer, will create 630 new jobs and invest $317 million in Bulloch County.
Plans for the Joon Georgia facility mark the first confirmed supplier for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America in Bryan County.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, LLC is working alongside the Georgia Department of Labor to secure its workforce.
Some 8,000 plus job openings will be open over the next several years at the Hyundai plant.
More than a dozen positions are available now at the Hyundai plant, including Global Trade Manager, Procurement Management Assistant Manager, and Treasury Manager.

facts: Inalfa Roof Systems manufactures and distributes sunroof and open roof systems for the automotive industry.
Inalfa Roof Systems is located in Auburn Hills, Michigan, within the Detroit Metropolitan Area.
Inalfa Roof Systems operates as a private company in the retail, transportation, warehousing & logistics, and logistics/wholesalers/retail distributors sectors.
Estimated annual revenue for Inalfa Roof Systems is $5.0-25M.
