facts: Gov. Nathan Deal announced that Inalfa Roof Systems, a leading global supplier of vehicle roofing systems, will open a manufacturing plant in Cherokee County.
The plant represents a $17.1 million investment and will create 300 jobs for Georgians.

Gov. Nathan Deal stated that Georgia's auto industry has been cultivated for more than 100 years, and the state's central location, deep workforce resources, and logistics infrastructure have attracted well over 300 companies and give the state a competitive advantage in this manufacturing sector.
Inalfa Roof Systems supplies sunroofs and open roofing systems to automotive manufacturers including Hyundai Kia, Ford, General Motors, Volvo, Chrysler Group, and BMW Group.
The facility is strategically located off Interstate 75 at Cherokee 75 Corporate Park, in close proximity to automotive manufacturers throughout the Southeast.
Danny Colaris, vice president of North American Operations for Inalfa Roof Systems, stated that after a four-year search for a location in the South, Cherokee County was found to be the ideal site.

Danny Colaris noted that state and local economic development teams, as well as Georgia Power, were extremely helpful in introducing the company to the strategic location and business advantages in Georgia.
The Cherokee County facility will be Inalfa's fourth plant in the U.S. and its first in the Southeast.
Inalfa Roof Systems has its global headquarters in Venray, The Netherlands, and a global workforce of 3,000 employees.
Inalfa’s North American headquarters is located in Michigan, and it operates a total of four plants in Michigan and Mexico.
Worldwide, Inalfa Roof Systems also has facilities in Slovakia, South Korea, China, Brazil, and Japan, and is currently expanding its footprint in Poland.
Building plans and preparation for construction for the Cherokee County facility have begun.
The plant is projected to begin operating in January 2014.
The new Georgia facility will provide job opportunities in engineering, operations and supply chain, purchasing, and manufacturing associates.
Hiring for these positions will begin in the fourth quarter of 2013 and continue through the second quarter of 2015 until all lines are installed and the plant is fully operational.
Marshall Day, chairman of the Cherokee Office of Economic Development, stated that Inalfa Roof Systems is the first business to locate in the Cherokee 75 Corporate Park.
Marshall Day thanked project partners at CBRE, Rooker, GDEcD, and Georgia Power for their work in making the project a reality.
Marshall Day noted that the new investment and jobs created will have a significant impact on Cherokee and the northwest Atlanta region.
Wylly Harrison is the project manager for the Georgia Department of Economic Development, which partnered with the Cherokee Office of Economic Development on the project location.
Chris Cummiskey, Commissioner of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, stated that investment by international companies creates jobs and contributes to the state’s economy.
Chris Cummiskey noted that commitments from top-notch businesses like Inalfa help other investors understand how Georgia can help them become successful in the U.S. market.
source_title: Inalfa Roof Systems Investing $17M in Georgia, Creating 300 Manufacturing Jobs - Middle Georgia CEO

facts: Gabriel and Inalfa plan to invest Rs 170 crore to set up a new manufacturing facility.

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy closed a $26.5 billion loan package to Southern Co.
The loan is for building or upgrading 16.7 GW of grid resources
The grid resource upgrades include 5 GW of new gas generation
It is the largest loan in DOE’s history
Officials say it will deliver over $7 billion in cost savings for customers of Southern subsidiaries Georgia Power and Alabama Power
Southern serves 9 million customers across the Southeast
Southern announced that its five-year multistate spending plan now sits at $81 billion as it works to meet growing electricity demand
Chris Womack is Southern’s chairman, president and CEO
The DOE loan will support 6.3 GW of nuclear through upgrades and license renewals
The DOE loan will support 1 GW of hydropower modernization
The DOE loan will support battery energy storage systems
The DOE loan will support over 1,300 miles of transmission distribution system projects
A DOE fact sheet specifies the loans will result in 5.3 GW of new gas generation and nearly 500 MW in gas capacity upgrades
The additions include three new natural gas turbines totaling 1.3 GW at the Yates Power Plant in Georgia
The Yates Power Plant turbines are set to be online by the end of 2027
Southern will add additional generation at three other plants by the end of 2030
Chris Womack said the loans “will help lower the cost of investments in our grid that will enhance reliability and resilience for the benefit of our customers.”
A DOE statement says the loans represent “the largest government investment aimed at directly lowering consumer energy costs and increasing grid reliability.”
The agency said the loans are estimated to reduce Southern’s interest expenses by over $300 million annually
Georgia Power’s base rates are currently frozen through at least the end of 2028 under an agreement the state’s Public Service Commission approved in 2025
Alabama Power’s rates are also frozen through 2027 under a voluntary moratorium the utility requested

facts: Business process consultant jobs in Georgia are in steady demand, with the strongest concentration in financial services, logistics, healthcare administration, and technology across a market that ranges from entry-level analysts to senior transformation leads.
Atlanta anchors the bulk of hiring for these roles, with secondary activity in Alpharetta and Savannah, where employers like NCR Voyix, Fiserv, and UPS have long-established Georgia operations.
Process improvement, ERP implementation support, and operational efficiency consulting are the specialties appearing most consistently in Georgia listings.
Qcells is listed among the top companies hiring for business process consultant roles in Georgia, with 9 current openings.
The Home Depot, Georgia-Pacific, Infosys, and Manus are also listed as top hirers in this category.
Manufacturing and Consulting & Professional Services are the top industries hiring for these roles, each accounting for 19 openings.

Business process consultants in Georgia earn a median of about $99,600 a year, based on May 2025 Bureau of Labor Statistics wage data, ranging from around $61,010 for the lowest 10% to over $172,850 for the top 10%.
Atlanta, Covington, and Alpharetta account for the largest share of business process consultant openings in Georgia.
About 34% of business process consultant openings tied to Georgia are remote or hybrid as of July 2026.
Most roles require a bachelor's degree in business, industrial engineering, or a related field, along with proficiency in process mapping tools such as Visio, Lucidchart, or equivalent software.
Experience with ERP platforms including SAP, Oracle, or Microsoft Dynamics is commonly sought, and Lean Six Sigma Green Belt or Black Belt certification is strongly preferred by employers.
A specific job description for Jack Link’s (Link Snacks) outlines a Business Process Control Analyst role on the 2nd shift, working Monday through Thursday from 3:30 PM to 2:30 AM.

The Jack Link’s position offers a starting hourly rate of $18.58 - $25.54 and includes benefits like medical, dental, vision, life and disability insurance, 401k participation, paid holidays, and paid time off.
Georgia does not require a state-issued license for business process consultant roles.
source_title: Business Process Consultant Jobs in Georgia | Migrate Mate

facts: Local Lavonia industry Kautex has announced it will expand its current facility this summer.

Tyler Miles, Director of Operations for Kautex, announced the expansion is to accommodate a new product line called SCR Systems for diesel trucks and vehicles.
The SCR System holds a urea fluid that ejects into the catalytic converter for diesel systems, mixing with nitrous oxide to reduce carbon emissions by producing water instead.
The SCR System will be installed in both diesel trucks and diesel-powered vehicles.
Kautex is starting production of VW small products, including the Golf, Jetta, and Passat, marking significant growth in the smaller vehicle segment.

facts: Kautex Textron GmbH & Co. KG, a Textron Inc. company, has announced a significant business win with a leading automotive original equipment manufacturer (OEM).
The order is for a full-BEV (Battery Electric Vehicle) thermoplastic composite lower battery housing unit.
The component is part of the Kautex Pentatonic battery enclosure product line.
The thermoplastic solution offers advantages over metal, including lower cost, higher functional integration, and a reduced carbon footprint.
This marks the second major business win for the award-winning Pentatonic product line.
The component will be used on a major BEV platform that spans multiple vehicle brands.
The Kautex Pentatonic product line provides lightweight and customizable solutions for the growing EV market.
Pentatonic products are made from thermoplastic composite or composite-metal hybrids.
These products are designed to enhance the thermal management of the battery pack.
Their lightweight properties contribute to improved vehicle performance and an extended driving range.
The lower battery housing is a critical safety component, designed to seal and protect the vehicle’s battery.
It meets extensive structural requirements and stringent thermal standards, including crush and bottom impact testing, as well as both external and internal fire tests.

Compared to conventional metal alternatives, this thermoplastic composite solution achieves a lower cost by reducing the number of components through high functional integration, resulting in a leaner bill of material.
The material helps lower the part’s carbon footprint, while the lightweight design contributes to reducing carbon emissions during vehicle operation.
The multi-material approach ensures excellent corrosion resistance without compromising performance over the vehicle’s lifespan.
Joerg Rautenstrauch serves as President & CEO of Kautex.
He noted that this order from a leading automotive OEM marks a significant milestone for the company.
This contract is Kautex’s second Pentatonic business win.
Its first success came in January 2023 for a full-BEV underbody protection skid plate, which entered serial production in 2024.
The Pentatonic portfolio has received numerous accolades, including the SPE Grand Innovation Award in Europe and the Lingxuan and Gasgoo Innovation Awards in China.
Kautex has validated the performance of its Pentatonic enclosures by deploying three on-road test vehicles in the US, Japan, and Europe.
These test vehicles have collectively covered over 100,000 miles across various terrains and conditions.
Kautex is a Tier One automotive supplier with 30 plants in 13 countries.
The company is a pioneer in designing and manufacturing fuel systems, engine camshafts, and industrial packaging.
For the new era of mobility, Kautex is expanding its portfolio to include thermoplastic composite battery systems and cleaning systems for assisted and autonomous vehicles.
Textron Inc. is a multi-industry company known for its global network of aircraft, defense, industrial, and finance businesses.
Textron’s brands include Bell, Cessna, Beechcraft, Pipistrel, Jacobsen, Kautex, Lycoming, E-Z-GO, and Textron Systems.
source_title: Kautex Textron Wins Major BEV Contract - The EV Report

Lund International products are marketed under the brand names of AMP Research, AVS, Belmor, Bushwacker, LUND, Rampage Products, Roadworks Manufacturing, Roll-N-Lock, Stampede Automotive Accessories and Tonno Pro.
The company is an employer of over 1,000 American workers.
Lund International serves as a premier designer and U.S.-based manufacturer of innovative accessories for trucks and Jeeps of all classes.

Chief Executive Officer Mitch Fogle stated the new television series will take viewers on a journey to experience truck and Jeep lifestyle, shows, events, and products in use addressing common everyday problems.

The company's new television series will feature installations and applications of its products, serving as a resource with perspectives from people who designed and built the parts and accessories popular with enthusiasts.

facts: President Donald Trump visited the Lehigh Valley on Tuesday to laud his administration’s tariff policies for reviving U.S. manufacturing.

Former President Joe Biden previously visited the Macungie, Pa., facility and promised regulatory changes requiring government contractors to buy “substantially” American-made products would support the company’s growth.
As of 2026, the “Buy American” rule requires 65% of materials used to be domestically produced.
The domestic material requirement under the “Buy American” rule rises to 75% in 2029.
This threshold expands upon a rule change from Trump’s first term that increased the requirement from 50% to 55%.
Mack Trucks has been manufacturing in Pennsylvania for more than a century.
The “Buy American” rule makes Mack Trucks an appealing choice for government contractors, including ongoing negotiations for a supply of 15,000 trucks.
The potential cost of the 15,000-truck contract could not be confirmed.
During his July 2021 visit, Biden stated: “We brought this economy back from the brink, checks in people’s pockets, shots in people's arms, tax cuts for working families with children.”
Biden also stated during that visit: “We designed our strategy to not only to provide a temporary boost but to lay the foundation for a long-term boom that brings everyone along.”
In 2021, Biden’s $1 trillion infrastructure spending plan was intended to “rebuild the backbone of the country” amid pandemic-induced unemployment and supply chain shortages.
Criticism of the former administration’s recovery plans helped flip the swing region back to Trump's favor after Biden won it in 2020.

On Tuesday, Trump stated: “You are the ones who are making America great again and you’re building the heavy-duty machinery that keeps our economy rolling, our factories moving and our industries roaring all across the nation.”
Company President Stephen Roy stated that Mack Trucks now supports 2,800 employees.
Mack Trucks has invested more than $400 million in facilities over the past decade.
Mack Trucks has invested more than $1 billion in new truck lines.
Mack Trucks recalled more than 150 workers amid strong demand.

Republican officials and administration representatives present at the visit included State Treasurer Stacy Garrity, U.S. Reps. Dan Meuser and Ryan Mackenzie, NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman, and Acting U.S. Secretary of Labor Keith Sonderling.
These officials echoed the message that tax cuts, trade policy, energy production, and workforce development are helping revive American industry.
Rep. Ryan Mackenzie pointed to $47 million he helped secure for Mack Defense to build more heavy-duty trucks in the Lehigh Valley.
The $47 million funding protects union jobs, strengthens domestic supply chains, and supports skilled manufacturing workers.
Trump stated his administration’s trade policies are designed to protect companies like Mack from foreign competition.
Tariffs on foreign steel, aluminum, copper, automobiles, and heavy-duty trucks are used as tools to encourage building in the U.S. and hiring American workers.
In early 2025, months into Trump’s second term, Mack Trucks announced layoffs of up to 350 workers amid tariff uncertainty.
Ultimately, 170 workers were laid off.
150 laid-off workers were brought back later in 2025.
source_title: Lehigh Valley’s Mack Trucks a presidential favorite for economic stumping | Pennsylvania | thecentersquare.com

facts: The Georgia Transportation Alliance is an affiliate of the Georgia Chamber of Commerce.
Georgia economic development veteran Joseph Santoro was selected to serve as the new Executive Director of the Georgia Transportation Alliance.
Joseph Santoro starts his new position on December 6.
Prior to joining the Georgia Transportation Alliance, Joseph Santoro had a successful four-year tenure with the Council for Quality Growth.
Joseph Santoro previously worked as the Director of Policy & Government Affairs for the Council for Quality Growth.

Over the last four years at the Council for Quality Growth, Santoro worked on local, regional, and state policies related to infrastructure planning, legislative initiatives, regulatory issues, land use, transportation, and environmental planning impacting the development and growth industry.

Joseph Santoro has over 10 years of government affairs experience working for membership-based organizations in metro Atlanta evaluating local, state, and federal policy priorities and setting forth recommended pathways to successfully advocate on behalf of the Board leadership and membership.
Joseph Santoro earned a B.S. in Public Policy from the Georgia Institute of Technology.
Joseph Santoro has been a Georgia Chamber Government Affairs Committee member since 2014 through various roles.
Joseph Santoro is a 2023 graduate of the Atlanta Regional Commission Regional Leadership Institute.

Joseph Santoro stated he looks forward to joining the team to continue advancing the mission of the Board leadership on identifying and promoting long-term transportation solutions to modernize and expand Georgia’s multimodal transportation system.
Joseph Santoro noted that the state’s transportation system fosters economic development, job creation, and enhances the quality of life for Georgia families.

Nick Juliano, Partner at Impact Public Affairs and Chair of the Georgia Transportation Alliance, stated Georgia is at a critical moment for its transportation industry, particularly as it relates to freight and logistics, and expressed happiness over Santoro's hire.

Chris Clark, CCE, President and CEO of the Georgia Chamber of Commerce, stated Joseph’s broad experience working with statewide transportation stakeholders and his trusted economic development insight make him the ideal fit to serve as Executive Director of the Georgia Transportation Alliance.

Chris Clark noted that with extensive surface transportation networks, two world-class ports, and one of the largest railroad systems in the country, Georgia’s transportation and logistics industry is an essential driver of record-breaking economic success.
Chris Clark stated he looks forward to collaborating with Joseph to ensure Georgia remains the number one state in the country to do business.

The Georgia Transportation Alliance is the state’s only organization focused solely on identifying and promoting long-term transportation solutions specifically connected to economic development and job creation.
The Georgia Transportation Alliance works closely with the Georgia Chamber of Commerce, the Georgia Department of Transportation and other key stakeholders in Georgia and Washington, D.C.
The goal of the Georgia Transportation Alliance is ensuring an efficient and safe statewide multi-modal transportation system.
source_title: Economic Development Expert Tapped to Lead Georgia Transportation Alliance - Tifton CEO

facts: HL Mando has been named General Motors’ Supplier of the Year for 2024.
This marks the fifth consecutive year and the eleventh time overall that HL Mando has earned this GM award.
HL Mando was recognized in the Brake Apply & Controls category for its Motor on Caliper (MoC) product.
The MoC product is manufactured at HL Mando’s Alabama plant.
HL Mando was praised for its localization efforts and technological innovation.
Less than 1% of GM’s suppliers receive this Supplier of the Year accolade.
The award was presented at General Motors’ 33rd annual Supplier of the Year event on April 8, 2025, in Phoenix, Arizona.
HL Mando stood out among 92 companies from 12 countries honored as exceptional GM partners.
As part of HL Group, HL Mando is a Tier 1 supplier specializing in Software Defined Vehicle (SDV) technologies.
HL Mando’s North American journey began in 1996 with an R&D center in Michigan.
HL Mando has manufacturing facilities in Alabama and Georgia, plus a second R&D center focused on future automotive advancements.
HL Mando has invested approximately $700 million in North American operations.
Jeff Morrison, GM’s global chief procurement officer, praised the suppliers’ innovation and resilience for delivering advanced technology to GM customers.
The Supplier of the Year award evaluates performance in safety, innovation, and resilience, alongside alignment with GM’s values.
Jason (Jae-hyuk) Kim, Head of HL Mando Americas, expressed pride in localization efforts and excitement for future collaborations with GM in automotive software and autonomous driving.
source_title: HL Mando Secures Fifth Consecutive GM Supplier Award - The BRAKE Report

facts: MAT Holdings Inc. is a diversified global manufacturing, marketing, and distribution company headquartered in Long Grove, Illinois.
The company operates across three continents and employs over 13,000 people worldwide.
MAT Auto Group manufactures suspension components including shocks and struts under the Gabriel® brand for the North American market.
MAT Auto Group also manufactures suspension systems under the Reyco Granning brand for the North American market.
The MAT Foundry Group engineers and manufactures cast iron products that serve passenger vehicle, commercial vehicle, and industrial applications.
The MAT Friction Group serves passenger, commercial, and heavy-duty vehicle markets with braking components including brake pads, shoes, shims, and back plates.
MAT Industries operates domestic manufacturing facilities to produce high quality air compressors, air tools, and accessories for retailers under several popular brands.
MAT Engine Technologies engineers and manufactures gasoline powered outdoor power equipment for the North American retail market.
Midwest Air Technologies provides fencing products and lawn and garden accessories to the hardware and home center market.
MAT Logistics provides logistic solutions to companies selling through retail channels and maintains 7 distribution centers with over 2.1 million square feet of floor space across the U.S.
MAT Holdings Inc. has averaged double digit revenue increases over the past 5 years through organic initiatives and acquisitions.
The company plans to drive organic growth and profitability in the consumer group through shared activities across operating companies, new product development, and distribution services.
Auto Group results will be driven by synergies across the Friction Group and aftermarket opportunities in Mexico, Europe, and the US.
New auto platform sales are expected to drive OE/OEM/OES growth for the MAT Foundry and Friction Groups.
Recent acquisitions contributing to synergies and sales growth include Mincer HD, Prazisionstechnik Resources Altenburg (PTR), MAT Friction Noyon, and MAT Commercial Vehicle Products.
Steve Wang serves as the CEO of MAT Holdings Inc.
On May 19, 2022, MAT Auto Group supported Valeo Service Africa & Overseas by delivering products from its Roulund Braking Division in India to reduce environmental impact.
On April 26, 2022, brake manufacturing at a UK location began undergoing a digital transformation using Artificial Intelligence to improve production visibility and resolve defects.
source_title: MAT Holdings, Inc

facts: MAT Holdings, Inc. is a privately held, globally diversified manufacturing, marketing, and distribution company providing products to Automotive and Consumer business categories.
The Automotive segment includes the MAT Foundry Group, Friction Group, and Suspension Components Group.
The MAT Foundry Group manufactures cast iron parts for automotive and industrial applications.
The Friction Group supplies friction braking components.
The Suspension Components Group supplies suspension components and systems.
For over 30 years, the company has operated multi-faceted manufacturing, distribution, and sourcing facilities in Asia, Europe, and the United States.
MAT Auto Group manufactures suspension components including shocks and struts under the Gabriel® brand for the North American market.
Its suspension systems meet specifications for tractor, trailer, emergency response, bus, military, and motor home manufacturers.

The MAT Foundry Group consists of 7 companies on 3 continents engineering and manufacturing cast iron products for passenger, commercial, heavy duty vehicle markets, industrial applications, and racing.
The foundry group produces over 350,000 tons of iron per year across 7 foundries with 1,750 employees.
The Foundry Group maintains 8 machining facilities across Europe, Asia, and Central America.
The Foundry Group supports OEM and aftermarket customers with R&D including 80 process and development engineers.

The MAT Friction Group consists of 8 companies on 3 continents serving passenger, commercial, and heavy duty vehicle markets with braking components including pads, shims, back plates, brake shoes, and linings.

The friction group produces over 24 million sets of passenger brake pads per year, 2.5M sets of PV shoes per year, 200,000 sets of CV brake pads, and 4M pieces of CV linings per year across twelve factories.
The Friction Group maintains three R&D centers and 42 dynamometers for product development and testing.

MAT Industries operates domestic manufacturing facilities offering air compressors, air tools, and accessories under brands such as Powermate®, Industrial Air®, Industrial Air Contractor®, Sanborn®, and private label.
Its air compressors are sold through major home centers, hardware co-ops, independents, and farm and fleet stores around the US.
MAT Engine Technologies engineers and manufactures gasoline powered outdoor power equipment for the North American retail market under the Powermate® and Southland® brands.
The company offers two and four cycle engines that are EPA and CARB compliant.
Product offerings include walk behind edgers, field trimmers, garden tillers, blowers, hand-held trimmers, blowers, and walk behind cultivators.
Midwest Air Technologies is a leading producer of fencing products, lawn and garden accessories, and pet accessories.
It manufactures residential, chain link, and agricultural fencing under the YardGard® and FarmGard® brands.
It produces trellis's, shepherds hooks, and other lawn and garden accessories under the Gilbert & Bennett® brand.
MAT Logistics provides strategic logistic solutions for moving goods and maintains 7 U.S. facilities strategically positioned from coast-to-coast.
MAT Logistics operates over 2.1 million square feet of floor space to service North America including Canada, the contiguous 48 United States, Alaska, Hawaii, and Puerto Rico.
The company's philosophy focuses on creating value through process improvement, global supply & distribution, scale and scope economies, and engineering and marketing expertise.
MAT Holdings, Inc. has averaged double-digit revenue increases over the past 5 years through acquisitions and organic growth.
A key success factor is the ability to find synergies in acquisitions and assimilate them quickly to enable higher performance and ROI.
Midwest Air begins importing and selling aftermarket brake rotors.
MAT acquires a majority stake in Eurac Holdings.
MAT establishes GRI Engineering for the sales of brake rotors.
MAT acquires Roulunds Braking and Meneta.
MAT founds Danblock Brakes.
MAT founds MAT Foundries Europe after acquiring assets of Sakthi Germany.
MAT founds MAT Dalian Auto Parts.
MAT acquires Dania, Fomar Poland and Ride Control, LLC (Gabriel).
MAT founds MAT Brakes India.
MAT acquires MincerHD.
MAT purchases assets of Frager Group and renames it to MAT Machining Europe.
MAT acquires assets of PTR Germany.
MAT acquires two Federal-Mogul factories and renames them to be MAT CV Products and MAT Friction Noyon.
source_title: MAT Holdings, Inc - About

facts: Legacy Mats is a supplier of Georgia Ground Protection Mats and temporary road mats in Georgia.
Their mats are crafted to withstand the demands of heavy machinery and Georgia’s varied climate.
They offer a comprehensive range of mats including access, bridge, composite, crane, 3 ply laminated, CLT, and pipeline skids.
Mats are available for rent or purchase in new or used condition.
Their 3-PLY laminated mats are renowned for strength and resilience against harsh weather and heavy-duty use.
The company emphasizes reliability, affordability, sustainability, and eco-friendly products.
Georgia’s construction sector heavily relies on access mats to support heavy equipment operations and provide stable platforms.
These mats are vital in areas with soft ground or sensitive landscapes to prevent damage and ensure regulatory compliance.
The state’s construction industry uses a wide range of mats including 3 Ply Laminated, CLT, Composite, Crane, Timber, and custom-designed mats.
3 Ply Laminated Mats in Georgia are widely used for durability and robustness, comprising three layers of bonded wood to create solid platforms for heavy machinery.
8'x14' 3 Ply Laminated Mats have an approximate quantity of 20 per full truckload and weigh approximately 1850.00 lbs per mat.
8x16' 3 Ply Laminated Mats have an approximate quantity of 17 per full truckload and weigh approximately 2300.00 lbs per mat.
Other names for 3 Ply Laminated Mats in Georgia include Truck Mats, Access Mats, Swamp Mats, Logging Mats, and Waffle Mats.
Cross-Laminated Timber (CLT) Mats are increasingly popular in Georgia for their remarkable strength and eco-friendliness.
CLT Mats are composed of crosswise stacked lumber layers that evenly distribute weight to support heavy construction equipment.
Composite Mats combine materials like plastic and fiberglass, offering a lightweight, durable, and rot/corrosion-resistant solution for Georgia’s construction sites.
Mega Deck Composite Mats (4.25"x7.5'x14') have an approximate quantity of 40 per full truckload and weigh approximately 1,030 lbs.
Dura Base Composite Mats (4.25"x8'x14') have an approximate quantity of 40 per full truckload and weigh approximately 900 lbs.
Dura Deck Composite Mats (.5"x4'x8') have an approximate quantity of 500 per full truckload and weigh approximately 86 lbs.
Crane Mats in Georgia are crucial for ensuring a stable foundation for crane operations and are made from sturdy woods like oak.
12″x4’x16′ Crane Mats have an approximate quantity of 12 per full truckload and weigh approximately 3,800 lbs.
12″x4’x20′ Crane Mats have an approximate quantity of 10 per full truckload and weigh approximately 4,800 lbs.
12″x4’x24′ Crane Mats have an approximate quantity of 8 per full truckload and weigh approximately 5,750 lbs.
Traditional Timber Mats in Georgia are crafted from dense hardwood to provide a robust platform for heavy machinery while minimizing soil compaction and preventing erosion.
8″x4’x16′ Timber Mats have an approximate quantity of 20 per full truckload and weigh approximately 2,300 lbs.
8″x4’x18′ Timber Mats have an approximate quantity of 18 per full truckload and weigh approximately 2,600 lbs.
8″x4’x20′ Timber Mats have an approximate quantity of 16 per full truckload and weigh approximately 2,875 lbs.
Pipeline Skids in Georgia play a vital role in pipeline construction and maintenance by providing a stable base for pipes to safeguard them from ground moisture.
Legacy Mats offers partnerships with leading distributors to ensure reliable foundations for projects across industries including renewable energy and pipeline construction.
New mats are constructed with premium materials for maximum strength and longevity, suited for Georgia's varied environments.
Used mats undergo thorough inspection and quality assurance to meet Georgia’s rigorous standards while offering cost-efficiency and environmental responsibility.
Legacy Mats offers adaptable rental and lease options for ground protection mats tailored for diverse project needs in Georgia.
Rental services include expert consultation, delivery and installation support, and flexible terms catering to Georgia-based projects.
The company presents a Mat Buyback Program in Georgia that allows clients to convert surplus access mats into cash with fair pricing and hassle-free processing.
Legacy Mats provides efficient transportation and logistics services across Georgia for the prompt delivery and installation of ground protection mats.
source_title: Georgia Ground Protection Mats : Legacy Mats

facts: Mercedes-Benz USA (MBUSA) is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia.
MBUSA has 829 employees.
MBUSA was founded in 1965.
MBUSA's postal address is One Mercedes-Benz Drive, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA.
MBUSA is responsible for the distribution, marketing, and customer service for all Mercedes-Benz products in the United States.
MBUSA offers drivers the most diverse lineup in the luxury segment with 18 model lines.
The lineup ranges from the sporty GLA SUV to the flagship S-Class.
The lineup includes dynamic all-electric vehicles from Mercedes-Benz.
MBUSA is also responsible for Mercedes-Benz Vans in the United States.

facts: Mercedes-Benz commits $7 billion-plus to its US manufacturing future.
$4 billion of the investment is directed specifically to the Tuscaloosa plant by 2030.
Atlanta is established as Mercedes-Benz's North American headquarters, anchored by a new research and development hub.
The new GLE and GLS world premieres occurred at the Tuscaloosa, Alabama facility on March 31, 2026.
The event marked the assembly of the five-millionth vehicle since production began at the plant in 1997.
The Tuscaloosa plant is operated by Mercedes-Benz US International (MBUSI).
The plant opened in 1995 and began series production in 1997 with the M-Class SUV, marking the first major Mercedes-Benz plant located outside Germany.
MBUSI directly employs approximately 5,800 people.
Senator Katie Britt noted direct employment of around 6,100 MBUSI workers in Alabama and indirect employment of close to 60,000 Alabamians through supplier and services relationships.
Ola Källenius, Chairman of the Board of Management of Mercedes-Benz Group AG, was part of the original team at the Alabama plant in the 1990s and subsequently served as its head.
Current production rates at the Tuscaloosa plant are around 260,000 vehicles per year.
The plant exports approximately 60% of its annual output to markets across the globe.
Investment allocations will cover production system upgrades, automation, electrification-ready infrastructure, and capacity expansion.
Mercedes-Benz sold 303,200 passenger cars and 40,000 vans in the United States in the most recent reporting period, making it the company's second-largest global market.
The GLC SUV will join the Tuscaloosa production roster with US production scheduled for late 2027.
Current Tuscaloosa production includes the GLE, GLS, GLE Coupe, AMG performance variants, Mercedes-Maybach GLS, EQE SUV, EQS SUV, and Mercedes-Maybach EQS SUV.
The new GLE and GLS arrive with updated AI-driven technologies, refined powertrains, and premium connectivity features.
The Mercedes-AMG GLE 53 HYBRID made its world debut at the event.
MBUSA is moving up to 500 roles from various US locations into a new Atlanta R&D hub as part of a strategic multi-million-dollar investment.
The Atlanta base centers on the existing '1MB' facility in Sandy Springs, which has housed approximately 800 Mercedes-Benz employees since 2018.
The transition to add up to 500 further roles to the Atlanta headcount is expected to be complete by August 2026.
The new R&D hub will leverage the engineering talent pool around the Georgia Institute of Technology and collaborate with the region's startup ecosystem and tech sectors.
Mercedes-Benz operates approximately 400 US-based suppliers and sources its demand for steel and aluminum almost exclusively from within the country.
The company supports an estimated 160,000 jobs across the US economy, encompassing 10,600 direct roles of which around 8,000 are in manufacturing.
An additional EV infrastructure investment of approximately $1.2 billion is under way to expand a high-speed charging network across North America.
The MBUSI plant at Vance, near Tuscaloosa, functions as the global hub for Mercedes-Benz SUV production, with two-thirds of its output leaving the country.
source_title: Mercedes invests $7bn in US manufacturing with $4bn Tuscaloosa plant upgrade and new Atlanta R&D hub

facts: Mercedes-Benz is strengthening its presence in metro Atlanta with a major headquarters expansion.
The company will create up to 500 new jobs at its North American headquarters in Sandy Springs.
Since relocating its North American headquarters from New Jersey in 2018, Mercedes-Benz has become an integral part of the local business community.
The latest expansion will centralize key corporate functions by relocating roles from California and the suburbs of Detroit.
Mercedes-Benz is investing millions to build a state-of-the-art research and development center near its 1MB campus.
The new research and development center will accelerate innovation and create new, high-skilled jobs.

Gregg Simon, senior vice president of project and partner development at the Metro Atlanta Chamber, stated: 'This project is a great example of why companies choose metro Atlanta. What began with Mercedes’ headquarters relocation in 2015 has evolved into a decade of growth — from the stadium partnership to expansions in Sandy Springs and the new R&D hub at Georgia Tech.'
Georgia has been ranked the number 1 state for business for 12 consecutive years.
source_title: Mercedes-Benz Deepens Roots in Metro Atlanta with Major Expansion - Metro Atlanta Chamber

facts: The Mercedes-Benz Group has a broad global footprint, operating in nearly every country and running over 30 production facilities worldwide.
Revenue dropped nearly 30% in 2020 due to the pandemic but bounced back to reach $157.82 billion in 2023, lagging behind the industry average of $170.30 billion in 2023.
Revenue experienced a negative compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 2.95% from 2019–2023.
Free cash flow (FCF) was $11.05 billion in 2023, far above the industry average of $3.32 billion.
FCF jumped from $0.71 billion in 2019 to $20.57 billion in 2021 before settling at its current position.
Among competitors, Volkswagen leads in CFO with $20 billion, followed by BMW ($17.36 billion) and Volvo ($3.86 billion).
Capital expenditure (CapEx) fell from $11.17 billion in 2019 to $7.11 billion in 2022, reflecting a CAGR of -6.71%.
In 2023, the company increased CapEx by 19%, reaching $8.46 billion, which is still below the industry average of $12.44 billion.
The company is deliberately scaling back fixed costs while prioritizing high-impact areas.
Current ratio was 1.26 in 2023, demonstrating strong liquidity and comfortably exceeding the industry average of 1.18.
Strategic focus includes efficiency, electric mobility, and global market leadership.
source_title: Mercedes-Benz Treasury Performance: Cash Flow & Capital Spending

facts: Mercedes-Benz USA LLC and Penske Automotive Group (NYSE:PAG) announced discussions to transition the distribution rights, management, sales and marketing activities of smart USA from Penske Automotive Group to Mercedes-Benz USA.
The transition is expected to occur by the end of the second quarter of 2011.
The proposed change marks a further step in implementing the future growth strategy of the smart brand.

The move reflects last year's reorganization at smart headquarters in Germany, where smart is now managed as an independent brand but fully integrated into Mercedes-Benz Cars business unit of Daimler AG.

Mercedes-Benz President and CEO Ernst Lieb stated excitement about working toward integration of smart into the MBUSA organization and looking forward to working with dealer partners to exceed customer expectations for the vehicle.
Ernst Lieb noted that adding smart, combined with the Autohaus dealer initiative, will help build upon the momentum the Mercedes-Benz brand achieved in the U.S.

Penske Automotive Group Chairman Roger Penske stated that introducing the smart brand to the U.S. has been a unique opportunity and highlighted delivering over 45,000 vehicles over the last three years.

Roger Penske explained that aligning smart distribution with Daimler worldwide is the logical next step for the smart brand in the U.S., enabling growth through the Mercedes-Benz dealership network and leading in conservation and meeting future fuel economy standards.
Dr. Annette Winkler, Vice President smart, credited Roger Penske and his team for successfully paving the way for the smart brand in the US.

Dr. Annette Winkler stated that integrating smart wholesale into their own national sales organization is an important part of the global growth strategy for smart and a step towards success in the US market.

The addition of the fuel efficient smart fortwo and smart fortwo electric vehicles to Mercedes-Benz USA's product portfolio underscores the company's commitment to meet even more stringent fuel economy standards in the future.
The smart fortwo achieves EPA estimated 41 mpg in highway driving.
Manufactured by Daimler AG, smart is currently sold in more than 40 countries.
More than 1.2 million smart fortwo have been sold worldwide since 1998.
Smart has established itself as the think tank for urban and electric mobility for Daimler AG.
Smart plays an important role in Daimler AG's plans to make innovative future mobility concepts such as car2go available in Europe and the United States.
source_title: Mercedes-Benz USA Intends to Transition the Distribution of smart from Penske Automotive Group

facts: Gov. Nathan Deal announced that Mercedes-Benz USA will relocate its headquarters to Atlanta.
The relocation will create at least 800 jobs.
Mercedes-Benz USA will invest approximately $74 million in the move.
Georgia Governor Nathan Deal stated that Mercedes-Benz USA's decision to move to Georgia is a home run for the state due to its international name recognition and unmatched reputation for quality.
Gov. Deal expressed looking forward to building upon the new partnership and adding Mercedes-Benz to the state's automotive sector.
Gov. Deal noted that Georgia is home to many growing, globally connected businesses due to its top-ranked economic environment, making it the No. 1 state for business three times over.
Gov. Deal stated that Mercedes-Benz will find in Georgia a state that will help its bottom line and people who will welcome its team and their families with open arms.

facts: Milliken & Company is a global manufacturing leader focused on materials science that delivers products across textile, flooring, chemical, and healthcare businesses.
Milliken & Company’s Chemical Division has been a leading global supplier of coatings, chemicals, and advanced additive and colorant technologies since its conception in 1957.
The Chemical division was founded to supply proprietary processing chemicals within Milliken’s operations and has expanded into diverse industries ranging from plastics to children’s art products.

Milliken & Company is a globally recognized leader in polymer additives and colorants with expertise to help brand owners and converters achieve sustainability objectives.

In fall 2022, Milliken introduced its DeltaFlow Viscosity Modifier for recycled polypropylene (rPP) at the K 2022 trade fair, based on latest viscosity-modification chemistry.
On October 19, 2022, Milliken & Company and PureCycle Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ: PCT) jointly announced they created the first fully sustainable concentrate for PP on the market.
The concentrate based on PureCycle materials allows for greater use of 100% recycled content.
When used in combination with PureCycle’s recycled PP, the formulation produces a carbon footprint (GHG) approximately 35% lower than that of virgin PP.
On October 19, 2022, Milliken & Company announced its science-based net-zero targets have been approved by the U.N.-backed Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi).
SBTi is a collective of international organizations dedicated to helping companies set emission reduction targets consistent with climate science and the Paris Agreement.

On October 12, 2022, Milliken & Company partnered with Finnish flexible packaging giant Huhtamaki Group to develop a more sustainable, mono-material laminate for use in tubes targeting cosmetics, toothpaste, and other personal-care applications.
Milliken introduced its RESIST™ XTR colorants designed for extreme conditions and demanding applications at the K 2022 trade fair.
The addition of RESIST™ XTR 9798 builds on Milliken’s existing portfolio while contributing to a more sustainable future.

At the upcoming K 2022 show, Milliken partnered with European recycling leaders EREMA and PreZero to highlight the use of Hyperform® HPN® performance additives in recycled polypropylene (rPP) compounds.
The common purpose of the partnership with EREMA and PreZero is to close the loop and advance circularity.
On September 15, 2022, Milliken & Company achieved a gold rating on its 2022 EcoVadis assessment.
Milliken’s EcoVadis score places it in the top 5% of more than 90,000 organizations rated by EcoVadis across environmental, social, and ethical considerations.
At Pack Expo International 2022, Milliken showcased novel plastic additives aimed at moving the circular economy forward.

Milliken offers customers opportunities to achieve sustainability goals through lightweighting, upgrading recycled content, conserving energy, simplifying recycling with mono-materials, and reducing carbon footprint via inter-material replacement of less-sustainable resins.

facts: pSemi showcases the world’s first 4-level buck converter for battery charging applications alongside new charge pump and two-stage step-down DC-DC converters.
The 4-level buck converter enables higher input voltages in 4-level buck mode and mid-to-low input voltages in 3-level buck mode.
The device can be operated in fixed ratio, capacitor divider mode with divider ratios 2 and 3 when the input voltage is a programmable power source (PPS).
Current delivery for the 4-level buck converter is up to 6A per device, with the option to parallel devices to achieve faster charging times across all operation modes using a 1 mm height inductor.
This design allows systems to achieve equivalent power delivery and faster battery charge times regardless of the type of USB_PD, PPS, or Qi wireless charging source.

pSemi will showcase these breakthroughs during the 2025 Applied Power Electronics Conference (APEC) on March 16-20, 2025, at the Georgia World Congress Center in Atlanta at Murata Electronics’ Exhibit booth #915.

Laurence McGarry, director product line manager at pSemi, will present details regarding the world’s first 4-level buck converter during a presentation at APEC on March 18, 2025, from 3:45 p.m. ET – 4:15 p.m. ET in Expo Theatre 4.
pSemi announced the PE24111, a 3.3Vin two-stage regulator capable of supplying up to 20A, ahead of the APEC Conference.

The PE24111 targets applications requiring high efficiency in a low-profile form-factor under 1.2mm and consists of a two-phase interleaved charge pump followed by an interleaved buck regulator stage.
This power system greatly reduces dependency on inductance for high efficiency solutions in small-footprint and height-constrained form-factors.
The PE24111 output voltage can be adjusted between 0.35 and 0.85V using external feedback resistors or an external DAC.
The PE24111 is ideal for step-down DC-DC converter solutions for low-profile point-of-load regulators, high-density optical transceiver modules, core supplies, ASICs, and FPGAs.
To achieve higher power delivery, the PE24111 can be connected in parallel up to four devices with synchronization capability.

Tatsuya Kubo, senior product marketing manager at pSemi, will highlight the PE24111 during a presentation entitled “Advances in 2-stage Buck Regulator Bring Higher Power Delivery to Optical Transceivers” at APEC on March 19, 2025, from 12:45 p.m. ET – 1:15 p.m. ET in Expo Theatre 3.
The PE25213 is an ultra-high efficiency charge pump configurable to divide down an input voltage by two or three and delivers up to 10A with peak efficiency up to 99%.
The PE25213 uses pSemi’s patented adiabatic or lossless switching architecture to maximize efficiency and reduce solution size.
The PE25213 supports an input voltage range of 5.7V to 15V in divide-by-2 mode and 8.4V to 15V in divide-by-3 mode.

The PE25213 is primarily used as a front-end converter to convert a two- or three-cell battery input to a 1-1.5S output for downstream regulators to improve overall system efficiency and extend run time.
The PE25213 offers a unique auto-switch mode to change the divide-down ratio during operation to avoid a downstream under-voltage lockout event at heavy system loading during low battery conditions.
The PE25213 comes in a 4.545 mm × 2.715 mm 47-pin WLCSP package with a pinout designed to be fully compatible with Type III PCB design to reduce system cost.
The PE25213 targets 2 and 3S battery input systems such as laptops and 12V input systems such as datacenter and networking applications.
The PE24111 and PE25213 are available for sampling now and will be demonstrated alongside the latest innovations in multi-level technology at APEC in Atlanta, Georgia, on March 16 – 19, 2025.
pSemi is a leader in semiconductor integration providing high-performance RF, analog and mixed signal solutions with a 30-year legacy of technology advancements.

pSemi’s solution portfolio spans power management, connected sensors, antenna tuning, and RF frontends which enable advanced modules for smartphones, base stations, personal computers, electric vehicles, data centers, IoT devices, healthcare and more.
pSemi is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Murata Electronics North America, Inc., headquartered in San Diego with additional offices spanning North America, Europe, and Asia.
source_title: pSemi, a Murata Company, Takes Power Conversion to the Next Level at APEC 2025

facts: NVL is the leading producer of innovative, sustainable aluminum solutions and the world's largest recycler of aluminum, with a strong emphasis on providing low-carbon aluminum solutions.
The company operates a global network of 32 advanced facilities, including 14 recycling centers and 11 innovation centers.
NVL employs 13,190 people to serve diverse markets such as beverage packaging, automotive, aerospace, and specialties across North America, Europe, Asia, and South America.
NVL anticipates long-term growth in aluminum demand driven by increased adoption of electric vehicles (EVs), which utilize higher amounts of aluminum.
Management estimates global automotive aluminum sheet demand will grow at a compound annual growth rate of 7% from 2023 to 2028.
Ducker Carlisle projects North American automotive aluminum sheet demand to increase by approximately 40% from 2022 to 2030, aligning with NVL's growth estimates.
Demand in Europe is expected to grow at a slightly higher rate, with the Asian market increasing at a steeper rate from a lower baseline in 2023.
Easing supply chain challenges and pent-up consumer demand are supporting strong near-term demand for automotive aluminum sheet.
NVL has identified approximately USD 6.4 billion in debottlenecking, recycling, and new capacity capital investments to meet growing customer demand and align with sustainability commitments.
These investments include approximately USD 4.7 billion in North America, USD 300-450 million in Europe, USD 450 million in Asia, and USD 850 million in South America.
NVL currently has approximately USD 4.9 billion of investments under construction through 2027 to increase recycling and rolling capacity and profitability.
In October 2021, NVL announced a USD 130 million investment in its Oswego, New York, plant to increase hot mill capacity by 124 kt and enhance batch annealing capabilities for automotive sheet.
In January 2022, a USD 365 million investment was announced for a new recycling center in Guthrie, Kentucky, to reduce carbon emissions by over one million tonnes annually.
A USD 65 million investment in a recycling and casting center at UAL joint venture in South Korea was announced in February 2022, expected to reduce carbon emissions by more than 420 kt annually.
In October 2022, NVL broke ground on a USD 4.1 billion greenfield rolling and recycling plant in Bay Minette, Alabama, to support beverage packaging and automotive aluminum sheet demand.

NVL is undertaking smaller debottlenecking projects, including a USD 150 million investment at its Logan JV plant for an additional 80 kt of rolling capacity, and a USD 50 million investment at its Pindamonhangaba, Brazil plant for an additional 70 kt of rolling capacity.

NVL allocated USD2.8 billion between 2012 and 2022 for capital expenditures and the Aleris acquisition, significantly expanding its rolling, recycling, and automotive finishing sheet production capacity.
The company is engaged in a new phase of organic investments (fiscal years 2023-2027) with a planned USD4.9 billion to further enhance recycling, rolling capacity, and profitability.
As of fiscal 2024, USD1.2 billion has already been deployed from the current investment phase.
Flat-rolled products shipments increased from 3,123 kt in fiscal 2016 to 3,673 kt in fiscal 2024.
NVL's financial leverage ratio improved from 4.7x in March 2016 to 2.3x in March 2024.
As of March 31, 2024, NVL's cash, cash equivalents, and availability under committed credit facilities totaled USD 2.3 billion.
The company anticipates capital expenditures to rise to approximately USD 1.8 to 2.1 billion for fiscal 2025, with around USD 300 million allocated for expected maintenance.

facts: Fiscal 2026 was marked by operational disruption from Oswego plant fires, causing significant production interruptions and losses, but core performance remained resilient with rapid recovery and Oswego hot mill restart ahead of schedule.
Strategic investments in Bay Minette, Alabama, are progressing well, with cold mill commissioning begun in March and full plant commissioning on track for later in the year.
Global cost efficiency initiatives delivered over $200 million in run rate savings, with a new target of $350–$400 million by FY 2028.
Q4FY26 Adjusted EBITDA per tonne rose 10% year-over-year to $544, despite operational disruptions.
Full-year FY26 net income attributable to common shareholder was $15 million, down 98% year-over-year; excluding special items, $476 million, down 38%.
Q4 net sales rose 4% year-over-year to $4.8 billion, driven by higher aluminum prices, despite a 12% decline in rolled product shipments due to Oswego fires.
Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 was $459 million, down 3% year-over-year, with per ton EBITDA up 10% to $544.
FY26 net sales increased 7% to $18.4 billion, adjusted EBITDA fell 9% to $1.6 billion, and net income attributable to the common shareholder was $15 million.
Adjusted free cash flow for FY26 was an outflow of $2.4 billion, mainly due to $2.3 billion in capex and $1.1 billion Oswego fire impact.
Rolled product shipments for the year were 3,557 kilotonnes, down 5% year-over-year.
Oswego is expected to ramp up quickly to pre-fire levels, supporting pent-up demand and normalizing shipments in H2 FY27.
Bay Minette project remains on track for full commissioning in the second half of the calendar year.
Full-year capex for FY27 is projected at $2.1–$2.4 billion, with Bay Minette and Oswego repairs as key drivers.
Positive free cash flow is expected by the end of FY27, with deleveraging to follow as major projects complete.
Long-term EBITDA per ton target of $600 remains on track within two to three years as Bay Minette ramps up and cost savings are realized.
Adjusted EBITDA fell 19% on higher scrap costs, but sales rose and packaging demand stayed strong.
Record beverage packaging demand and investments drove growth, but margins remain pressured by scrap costs.
Adjusted EBITDA rose 19% to $500 million, despite Sierre flooding charges in Q1.
Net income up 27% to $163M, Adjusted EBITDA down 9%, Bay Minette CapEx at $5B.
Net loss of $160 million driven by Oswego fires; cost efficiencies and insurance support resilience.
Net sales up 13%, but margins fell on higher costs; recovery expected as savings ramp up.
Q2 revenue up 5%, but margins pressured by Sierre flooding and rising scrap costs.
Global aluminum leader launches NYSE IPO; parent retains 92.5% control, no proceeds to company.

facts: Novelis Inc. announced it will invest $365 million to build a highly advanced recycling center for automotive in North America.
The new facility will have an annual casting capacity of 240 kt of sheet ingot.
The facility is expected to reduce the company's carbon emissions by more than one million tons each year.
The new recycling facility will be built adjacent to Novelis' existing automotive finishing plant in Guthrie, Kentucky.
The center will enable Novelis to grow its closed-loop-recycling programs with more automotive customers in North America.
Through closed-loop recycling, the company takes back aluminum remaining after automotive parts are stamped from sheets and remakes it into the same product for new vehicle production.
The center will also have the capability to process aluminum from vehicles at the end of their lifecycle.
Using recycled aluminum as input material requires only 5 percent of the energy used to make primary aluminum, thus avoiding 95 percent of the carbon emissions associated with production.
Steve Fisher serves as President and CEO of Novelis, Inc.
Novelis aims to be the world's leading provider of low-carbon, sustainable aluminum solutions that advance its business, industry and society toward the benefits of a circular economy.
The new recycling center will be equipped with advanced shredding and sorting technology.
The facility will include energy-efficient innovations to support Novelis' sustainability goal to reduce energy intensity by 10 percent by 2026.
Groundbreaking for the recycling center is scheduled for early 2022.
Commissioning of the facility is expected in 2024.
When fully operational, the expansion will add approximately 140 new jobs in Guthrie.
Novelis grand opened its automotive finishing plant in Guthrie in October prior to this announcement.
The Guthrie automotive finishing plant currently employs 150 people and will grow to 190 over the next two years.
Tom Boney serves as Executive Vice President and President of Novelis North America.
Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear stated that the investment helps Novelis achieve sustainability goals and supports carbon-reduction targets for automotive customers.
Gov. Beshear noted that Novelis is proving to be a great fit for Todd County and the surrounding area.
Novelis has a 37-year history of creating jobs in Kentucky and investing in local communities.
Novelis employs 1,600 people in the state of Kentucky.
In addition to the Guthrie automotive finishing facility, Novelis operates an aluminum beverage can recycling plant in Berea.
Novelis also operates the Logan Aluminum joint venture in Russellville.
Novelis Inc. is a global leader in the production of innovative aluminum products and solutions and the world's largest recycler of aluminum.
Novelis had net sales of $12.3 billion in fiscal year 2021.
Novelis is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited, an industry leader in aluminum and copper.
Hindalco Industries Limited is the metals flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group, a multinational conglomerate based in Mumbai.

The announcement contains forward-looking statements regarding the investment, emission reductions, and job creation, with risks detailed in Novelis' Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended March 31, 2021.
source_title: Novelis to Build $365 Million Recycling Center to Support North American Automotive Customers and Reduce Carbon Emissions by More Than One Million Tons a Year :: Novelis Inc.

facts: Novelis Inc. announced it will invest USD 2.5 billion to build a new low-carbon recycling and rolling plant in Bay Minette, Alabama, USA.
The highly advanced facility will have an initial capacity of 600 kilotonnes of finished aluminum goods per year.
The facility's capacity will support strong demand for aluminum beverage packaging and automotive solutions.
Mr. Kumar Mangalam Birla, Chairman of the Aditya Birla Group and the Novelis Board of Directors, stated the investment marks the start of another transformational growth phase for Novelis.
Novelis continues to invest in each of the markets it serves, including beverage can, automotive, aerospace, and specialties, across all geographies.
More than half of the capacity of the new facility will be used to serve growing demand for aluminum beverage can sheet in North America, driven by consumer preference for more sustainable packaging.

Steve Fisher, President and CEO of Novelis Inc., stated the investment demonstrates a commitment to grow alongside customers and meet their needs for low-carbon, highly sustainable aluminum solutions.
Novelis is well-positioned to efficiently expand capacity at this facility in the future above the 600kt announced to capture ongoing strong demand.
The decision to build a fully integrated, greenfield recycling and rolling plant is backed by strong North American demand for flat-rolled, low-carbon aluminum from can makers and beverage companies.
Aluminum beverage cans, bottles, and cups are models of sustainable packaging and the circular economy.
An average "can-to-can" lifecycle is just a couple of months, with a recycled can able to be back on store shelves in as little as 60 days.

Ron Lewis, Ball Corporation’s chief operating officer for global beverage packaging, stated Novelis’ new plant will add much-needed domestic production of sustainable aluminum in North America while decreasing the carbon footprint of products created.
The facility will be the first fully integrated aluminum mill built in the U.S. in 40 years.
The plant is expected to create up to 1,000 high-paying, advanced careers in modern manufacturing.
The facility aims to be net carbon neutral for Scope 1 and 2 emissions.
The plant will be powered primarily by renewable energy, use recycled water, and operate as a zero-waste facility.
The plant will rely on railroad transportation, which can reduce logistics-related carbon emissions by up to 70% compared to road transport.
The plant will make significant use of advanced automation and digital technologies, including artificial intelligence, augmented reality, and robotics.
With the addition of a new recycling center for beverage cans, Novelis will soon be able to recycle 90 billion cans globally, up from the 74 billion used beverage cans the company currently recycles.

Novelis has been working to develop circular economies for aluminum through state and federal public policies, as well as partnerships with customers and other stakeholders on new approaches that encourage U.S. consumers to recycle more often.

John Murphy, Chief Financial Officer of The Coca-Cola Company, stated the announcement of this new low-carbon recycling and rolling facility will benefit the Coca-Cola system, its customers, and consumers while reducing environmental impact.
Site work is under way now as of the publication date.
The company expects to begin commissioning in mid-2025.
In addition to the beverage can market, the facility will serve the automotive market, where aluminum is the fastest growing material as automakers make plans to achieve sustainability goals.
source_title: Novelis Inc to Build USD 2.5 Billion Low Carbon Aluminum Recycling and Rolling Plant

facts: Atlanta-headquartered Novelis Inc.- the global leader in aluminium rolling and recycling announced its decision to invest $36 million to expand and upgrade recycling capabilities at its Greensboro, Ga plant.

Novelis plans to install a state-of-the-art equipment for aluminium scrap recycling and a new baghouse for improved mitigation of dust.
Further, the investment will also work on designs to ensure a safer and more efficient traffic flow, thereby enhancing the facility’s safety systems in a big way.
Meantime, the facility will continue to recycle used beverage cans as usual.
Marco Palmieri, SVP and President, Novelis North America noted that the investment demonstrates the company’s commitment to meet sustainability targets.

Beatriz Landa, Plant Manager, Novelis Greensboro stated that the investment will allow the company to continue its modernization of the facility and its capabilities so as to serve its broad customer base for many more years to come.

Meantime, Governor Brian P. Kemp congratulated Novelis on its exciting expansion in Greensboro. He reminded the countless opportunities created by the facility over the years and hoped that the company achieves continued success in the years to come.

facts: Novelis is the leading producer of innovative, flat-rolled aluminum products and the world's largest recycler of aluminum.

The company’s business strategy is built on sustainability, with an ambition to be the world’s leading provider of low-carbon, sustainable aluminum solutions that advance the company’s business, the aluminum industry and society as a whole toward the benefits of a circular economy.

facts: Novelis Inc. is a leading sustainable aluminum solutions provider and the world leader in aluminum rolling and recycling.
Steve Fisher serves as president and CEO of Novelis Inc.
Novelis reported fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2026 financial results.
Net sales for the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2026 increased 4% versus the prior year period to $4.8 billion, mainly driven by higher average aluminum prices.
Rolled product shipments decreased 12% to 844 kilotonnes in the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2026.
Lower fourth quarter shipments were driven by Oswego production disruption and softness in some specialty markets due to geo-political conditions.
Net loss attributable to common shareholders was $84 million in the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2026, compared to a net income of $294 million in the prior year period.

The quarterly net loss decrease was primarily due to $630 million in pre-tax net losses related to the Oswego fires, partially offset by favorable metal price lag from rising average local market aluminum premiums.
Net income attributable to common shareholders, excluding special items, was down 13% year-over-year to $227 million in the fourth quarter.
Adjusted EBITDA decreased 3% year-over-year to $459 million in the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2026.

Fourth quarter adjusted EBITDA was impacted by an estimated negative $53 million resulting from production interruptions at Oswego and a net negative tariff impact, partially offset by lower aluminum scrap prices and higher Sierre flood insurance recoveries.
Net sales increased 7% versus the prior year to $18.4 billion in full fiscal year 2026, primarily driven by higher average aluminum prices.
Rolled product shipments decreased 5% to 3,557 kilotonnes for full fiscal year 2026 due mainly to the Oswego fires.

Fiscal year 2026 net income attributable to common shareholders decreased 98% versus the prior year to $15 million, primarily driven by the Oswego fires, restructuring charges, and unrealized derivative losses.
Net income attributable to common shareholders, excluding special items, was down 38% year-over-year to $476 million for full fiscal year 2026.
Adjusted EBITDA decreased 9% to $1.6 billion in fiscal year 2026 driven by an estimated negative $104 million resulting from the Oswego fires and $143 million from tariffs.
Full year adjusted EBITDA was partially offset by higher product pricing, lower SG&A costs, and favorable foreign exchange.
Net cash used in operating activities was an outflow of $193 million in fiscal year 2026, compared to a net cash inflow of $951 million in the prior year period.
The operating cash flow outflow was largely related to impacts from the Oswego fires and higher working capital from rising aluminum prices.
Adjusted free cash flow was an outflow of $2.4 billion in fiscal year 2026, compared to a prior year period outflow of $737 million.
The reduction in adjusted free cash flow is mainly driven by impacts from the Oswego fires and a 39% increase in total capital expenditures to $2.3 billion in fiscal year 2026.
Total capital expenditures were mainly related to the Company's U.S. greenfield rolling and recycling plant in Bay Minette, Alabama.
The Bay Minette plant is expected to commission in the second half of this calendar year.
The Company had a net leverage ratio (Adjusted Net Debt / trailing twelve months Adjusted EBITDA) of 4.1x at the end of fiscal year 2026.
Total liquidity stood at $2.8 billion as of March 31, 2026, consisting of $1.3 billion in cash and cash equivalents and $1.5 billion in availability under committed credit facilities.
Dev Ahuja serves as executive vice president and CFO of Novelis Inc.
Novelis expects to return to positive free cash flow by the end of fiscal 2027, setting a firm path towards deleveraging.
A fire broke out at the Novelis plant in Oswego, New York on September 16.
A second significant fire occurred at the Oswego plant on November 20 in a location where repair work from the September fire was taking place.
Everyone working at the Oswego plant was safely evacuated with no injuries to employees, contractors or first responders during either event.
Both Oswego fire events were contained to the hot mill area and did not impact the rest of the plant.
Novelis expects the Oswego hot mill to be back in service in the next few weeks, positioning the company to support pent-up demand and normalize shipments over time.
source_title: Novelis Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Fiscal Year 2026 Results - Financials and Investment

Novelis expects to complete the public offering after the SEC completes its review process, subject to market and other conditions.
