facts: Novelis Inc, the US-based fully owned subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Ltd., has filed to offer 45 million (4.5 crore) shares for an initial public offering (IPO) with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).
The IPO price band is estimated to be between $18 and $21, Novelis said in a filing with the US SEC.
Hindalco is aiming to raise $810-945 million funds through this offering.
At the higher end of the price band, Novelis is targeting a valuation of up to $12.6 billion in its US IPO.

Assuming the green shoe option is exercised, whereby it could offer another 6.75 million shares or a 1.1% stake in case of overallotment, the total proceeds for the sole shareholder of Novelis are estimated to be in the range of $931.5 million to $1.08 billion.
Billionaire Kumar Mangalam Birla's Hindalco Industries will own about 92.5% of the company after the IPO.
Novelis has applied to list its common shares on the New York Stock Exchange, or NYSE, under the symbol 'NVL'.
The shares will be offered by Novelis' sole shareholder, AV Minerals (Netherlands), a 100% subsidiary of Hindalco Industries.
Novelis would not receive any proceeds from the sale of shares by its sole shareholder.
Following the offering, Hindalco will own approximately 92.5% of Novelis' outstanding share capital.
Hindalco will therefore control approximately 92.5% of the voting power of the company's outstanding share capital, as per the document filed with the US SEC.

Novelis is a 'foreign private issuer' under applicable Securities and Exchange Commission rules, and as a result, will be subject to reduced public company reporting requirements for this prospectus and future filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
The basis net debt of $4.35 billion, as per the F1 document, the enterprise valuation of the company is estimated to be in the range of $15.2 billion to $17 billion.
Morgan Stanley, BofA Securities and Citigroup are the joint book-running managers for the proposed IPO.
Wells Fargo Securities, Deutsche Bank Securities and BMO Capital Markets are acting as additional book-running managers.
BNP Praibas, Academy Securities, Credit Agricole CIB, PNC Capital Markets LLC and SMBC Nikko will be co-managers for the issue.
Hindalco acquired Novelis in 2007 and delisted it from the exchanges in the US.
Novelis said in February it had filed confidentially for the listing with the SEC.
Novelis is the world’s biggest maker of flat-rolled aluminium products, used in an array of goods, from cars to soda cans.
source_title: Novelis files to offer 45 million shares in US IPO, price band at $18-21 per share - CNBC TV18

facts: Novelis Inc., a leading sustainable aluminum solutions provider and the world leader in aluminum rolling and recycling, announced the start up of its new roll forming development line.
The new development line will help Novelis meet industry demand for a process that can produce large volumes of high-strength aluminum auto parts.

Located at the company’s Automotive Customer Solution Center in Novi, Michigan, a suburb of Metro Detroit, the new state-of-the-art roll forming development line will provide Novelis with the unique advantage of conducting full scope, year-round research and development on roll forming in house.

The round-the-clock access to the line will facilitate unmatched innovation and expedite the process to ensure complex, high-strength aluminum roll forming is developed, and process robustness is proven more swiftly.
As the automotive industry moves toward electrification, it is demanding faster production of lighter, greener parts.
With the roll forming process, strips of aluminum are incrementally formed in a continuous fashion to form a desired shape having various degrees of detail and strength.
Offering a process that is scalable and customizable, aluminum roll forming provides significant opportunities for production of high strength aluminum at scale.

While roll forming is not new to the industry, its ability to produce complex high strength aluminum parts has yet to be fully developed and will require innovation and investment to maximize its potential.
Novelis’ introduction of its own roll forming development line is a critical step to moving the technology forward.

The shift to the mass production of aluminum-intensive vehicles is a natural next step as the industry prioritizes electrification and sustainability, as well as other benefits of aluminum, such as increased safety, reduced risk of corrosion and improved performance.

“The auto industry understands the benefits of aluminum and is looking to Novelis to develop technology that supports their growth initiatives,” said Jamie Zinser, Vice President, Global Automotive, Novelis Inc.
“One of these technologies is roll forming. We are introducing this development line to accelerate research and development, not to become a roll forming company.”
“Our goal is to work with our automotive and Tier I partners to innovate and develop aluminum sheet technology that will support the electrification movement.”
source_title: Novelis accelerates aluminum automotive part innovation with introduction of roll forming development line

facts: Novelis Inc. announced the expansion of its research partnership with the Georgia Institute of Technology (Georgia Tech) Novelis Innovation Hub.
The initiative focuses on materials and process research to help Novelis reach a net zero carbon footprint by 2050.
Extended cooperation builds on programs to accelerate innovation via high throughput development methodologies coupled with artificial intelligence.
Programs aim to develop new generations of electric vehicle (EV) batteries, making use of aluminum to improve their performance.
Extended programs will target improved performance with higher amounts of recycled aluminum in batteries.

Philippe Meyer, Senior Vice President and Chief Technology Officer of Novelis Inc., stated the collaboration provides an opportunity to accelerate R&D and boost EV battery performance (energy density) and recyclability, while lowering costs for automakers and battery manufacturers.
Meyer stated they want to partner closely with Georgia Tech to help transform their industry to reach committed objectives toward sustainability.
The expanded partnership continues ongoing technical research while initiating new strategic programs.
New strategic programs include circular economy solutions or de-carbonization of the industrial footprint.
Programs also promote entrepreneurship and incubation of new ventures.
This will drive accelerated commercialization of new products, resulting in broader relationships with Georgia Tech's business and commercialization groups.

Raj Gopalaswamy, Global Technology Director of New Domains at Novelis Inc., stated the aluminum industry must increase the pace of innovation to develop solutions demonstrating aluminum's superior sustainability benefits.

Gopalaswamy noted research partnerships with institutions like Georgia Tech help fulfill growing needs for aluminum applications that help customers meet sustainability goals faster and more efficiently.

Shreyes Melkote, Executive Director of the Novelis Innovation Hub at Georgia Tech, stated collaboration between academia and industry will help speed development of transformative solutions to meet sustainability challenges.

Melkote expressed looking forward to expanding the partnership with Novelis to address critical scientific, technological and workforce needs by leveraging Georgia Tech faculty and students in sustainable technologies, advanced materials and manufacturing, artificial intelligence, data sciences and robotics.
Julia Kubanek, Vice President for Interdisciplinary Research at Georgia Tech, views Novelis as a critical partner for Georgia Tech to accelerate product innovation across multiple sectors.

Kubanek stated the Institute remains committed to fostering world-class partnerships collaborating with Novelis, citing mutual interests in sustainability, clean energy and creative design of materials.
Novelis Inc. is driven by its purpose to shape a sustainable world together.
Novelis is a global leader in innovative products and services and the world's largest recycler of aluminum.
Novelis partners with customers in the aerospace, automotive, beverage can and specialties industries.
Novelis delivers solutions maximizing lightweight aluminum benefits throughout North America, Europe, Asia and South America.
Novelis is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited.
Hindalco Industries Limited is an industry leader in aluminum and copper, and the metals flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group.
The Aditya Birla Group is a multinational conglomerate based in Mumbai.
source_title: Novelis Expands Research Partnership with Georgia Tech :: Novelis Inc.

facts: Novelis Inc. is the world's leading provider of sustainable aluminium solutions and a global leader in aluminium rolling and recycling.
Roberta Soares has been named Regional President for South America, effective June 1.
Cary Chenanda has been named Regional President for North America.
Francisco Pires, the former President of South America, will now serve as Chief Transformation Officer.
Tadeu Nardocci, previously interim president of North America, will transition to Executive Vice President of Manufacturing Operations.
Steve Fisher serves as President and CEO of Novelis.
As head of the South America region, Roberta Soares leads all aspects of business that had net sales of $2.46 billion in fiscal year 2024.
The Novelis South America region operates two plants and employs approximately 1,700 people serving beverage packaging and speciality markets.
Soares was most recently Vice President of Operations at Novelis South America before her appointment.
Prior to that role, Soares spent four years as Vice President of Finance and IT at Novelis South America.

Nardocci is a 45-year veteran of Novelis with deep expertise in capital expansion projects.

Novelis expects the collective leadership to bolster its commitment to operational excellence, customer service, innovation, and sustainability.

facts: According to Recycling Today, Novelis has received financial assistance from the Oswego County Industrial Development Agency to help cover the costs of repairing its Oswego, New York, aluminum facility following multiple fires in 2025.
The IDA approved the application on January 7.
The Atlanta-based company filed for assistance on December 29, 2025, detailing $255 million in total repair costs.
These costs include $187 million for building repairs, $61 million for equipment and machinery, $2 million in engineering fees, and $5 million in other expenses.
Novelis noted in its application that it had already invested $165 million in the project as of the filing date.
The IDA granted Novelis an exemption from sales and use taxes associated with the renovations, with the financial assistance through exemptions estimated at $15 million.
The need for repairs stems from two major fires on September 16, 2025, and November 20, 2025, with a smaller incident occurring between those dates.
The September fire affected the hot mills structural surroundings, primarily the roof, and other plant areas were impacted by the 5 million gallons of water used to fight the fire.
Novelis initially anticipated a three-month recovery and a December 2025 restart.
The November fire was contained to the hot mill and hot mill motor room—the same general area as the first fire—and damaged the hot mills finishing mill.

Novelis stated in its application that the finishing mill parts are highly specialized, all need to be inspected for reconditioning or rebuilding needs, and due to this, a recovery timeline is still to be determined.
Novelis says the project will help retain 1,122 full-time and four part-time employees at the site.
The current annual Oswego County payroll and benefits expenditure is approximately $180 million.
The company stated the project will also support its continued local spending on services and the local supply chain.
The Oswego facility produces more than a billion pounds of aluminum sheet annually for the automotive, beverage can, and building and construction markets.
Novelis supplies several automakers, including Ford.
source_title: Novelis Gets Tax Exemption for $255M NY Plant Fire Repairs | 2026 Update - News and Statistics - IndexBox

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that automotive parts manufacturer NVH Korea will establish a new facility in Locust Grove during an economic development trip to the Paris Air Show this week.
The project will create over 160 new jobs.
The project will bring $72 million in investment to Henry County.
This is the second economic development project Governor Kemp has announced this week alone.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Georgia's reliable infrastructure network and great workforce allow job creators to find success in every community in the state.

Governor Brian P. Kemp highlighted the incredible growth of Georgia's e-mobility and battery industries as a testament to supporting large suppliers like NVH Korea, calling them the latest partner in making Georgia the epicenter of the industrial revolution.
NVH Korea is an automotive parts manufacturer based in the City of Ulsan and was established in 1984.
The company specializes in automotive noise, vibration, and heat-control parts, which it currently produces in Korea, Europe, and India.
The company operates a manufacturing facility that produces floor mats, cargo mats, and cargo trays in Georgia.
The new facility will supply battery parts for electric vehicles (EVs) in the United States for manufacturers such as Hyundai Mobis, Kia Georgia, and Hyundai Motor Group.

Ja Kyum Koo, Chairman and CEO of NVH Korea, stated that Georgia was chosen as the company’s first EV battery component manufacturing site and that cooperation between state agencies and company staff helped guide the project.
Ja Kyum Koo noted this is the first step in contributing to the electrification of the mobility industry and expressed excitement about joining the Locust Grove and Henry County community.
NVH Korea’s new facility will be located at Gardner Logistics Park on Colvin Drive in Locust Grove.
The facility will produce EV battery system components that protect, connect, and sense the performance of battery cells.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in the second quarter of 2024.
Mayor Robert Price of the City of Locust Grove stated that NVH’s investment will increase the manufacturing jobs base and integrate the company into Georgia's electric vehicle ecosystem.
Carlotta Harrell, Chair of the Henry County Board of Commissioners, stated the announcement will add quality manufacturing jobs for citizens and continue to diversify the local economy.

Project Manager Jacob Lee represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project in partnership with the Henry County Development Authority, Georgia EMC, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that an economic transformation is happening across Georgia as manufacturers create future jobs aligned with cutting-edge technologies and industry trends.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson welcomed innovators establishing their first U.S. or e-mobility manufacturing presence in Georgia, noting they enrich the local industry mix, provide well-paying jobs, and build on the statewide mobility ecosystem.

For over a century, Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged collaboration and innovation, and positioned itself as a leader in developing emerging technologies for the automotive and mobility industry.
From July 1 through December 31, 2022, the automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment in Georgia.
Since 2020, Georgia's pursuit of job creation along the entire electric vehicle supply chain has resulted in more than $22.7 billion in investments and the creation of over 28,400 jobs.
source_title: GA: EV Parts Supplier NVH Korea to Create Over 160 Jobs, Invest $72 Million in Henry County | Trade and Industry Development

facts: Korean NVH Korea (Chairman Gu Ja-gyeom) is establishing its first US production facility in Georgia.
On the 22nd, Georgia Governor Brian Kemp announced that auto parts supplier Korean NVH Korea will invest $72 million in Locust Grove, Henry County.
The project is expected to create over 160 new jobs (subhead notes 150 jobs).
Governor Kemp stated that Georgia's remarkable growth in the e-mobility and battery industries has enabled support for large suppliers like NVH Korea.
Governor Kemp, currently attending the Paris Air Show in France, noted this is the second economic development project secured by Georgia this week alone.
Governor Kemp expressed pride in welcoming NVH Korea as a partner in Georgia, describing it as the center of the cutting-edge industrial revolution.
NVH Korea is headquartered in Ulsan and was founded in 1984.
The company currently operates production facilities in Korea, Europe, and India.
NVH Korea specializes in manufacturing automotive noise, vibration, and heat control parts.
The Georgia plant plans to supply EV battery parts to Hyundai Mobis, Kia Georgia, and Hyundai Motor EV plants.
Chairman Gu Ja-gyeom expressed pleasure at selecting Georgia as the company's first EV battery parts manufacturing site.
Chairman Gu stated this marks the first step in efforts to contribute to the electrification of the mobility industry and thanked Henry County and Georgia for their cooperation.
The new facility will be located at Gardner Logistics Park on Colvin Drive.
Production at the new facility is scheduled to begin in the second quarter of next year.

facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) is the company’s third U.S. production hub.
HMGMA began full production in Ellabell, Georgia, in October 2024.
The factory spans 2,900 acres.
The site currently produces Hyundai’s Ioniq 5 and Ioniq 9 electric SUVs.
Plans are in place to expand production to Kia and Genesis models in the future.
HMGMA plans to add 8,500 on-site jobs.
This makes it Georgia’s largest economic development project to date.
At present, the site employs 1,623 workers referred to as "Meta Pros".
85% of the current workforce is from Georgia.
65% of the current workforce is from the greater Savannah area.
HMGMA has a production capacity of up to 300,000 vehicles each year.
The plant can scale up to produce 500,000 vehicles annually when it reaches its goal of 8,500 workers.
Hyundai invited journalists to tour the production facilities to observe various stages of the car-building process.
The facility utilizes a streamlined operation where humans and robots work side by side.
Every vehicle begins as thousands of individual parts that pass through countless steps before arriving at dealerships.
Trained specialists examine every nook and cranny of each car to ensure it meets the highest quality standards.
A drive test is one of the final steps in the car-building process, and every car must pass through the facility's test track before continuing in production.
Hyundai allowed media personnel to drive through the test track for the first time during this tour.
The test track includes driving over various bumps to listen for any unusual noises.
The test track includes "figure 8" maneuvers to assess steering wheel responsiveness.
The test track tests all braking functions on both uphill and downhill inclines.
A full-speed brake check is a required part of the testing process.
The track session also tests active safety and driver assistance functions, including lane-keeping assist features.
Before reaching the production line, employees must complete on-site training at a Quick Start facility known as the Hyundai Mobility Training Center.
Trainees receive field-specific hands-on training that previews the actual production line.
The training center features classrooms with instructors and a daily class schedule similar to college courses.
Training stations include general assembly, exterior quality testing, and a disassembly area.
Trainees use an S.T.E.P. simulator featuring a large touchscreen while walking on a slowly moving conveyor belt.
source_title: Inside Hyundai’s Metaplant: What It Takes to Power the Future - Korea

facts: Manufacturing represents 10 percent of Georgia's state gross domestic product.
Total manufacturing GDP totaled nearly $62 billion in 2019.
The manufacturing industry accounts for 92 percent of exports across the state.
Manufacturing employs more than 380,000 people across Georgia.
Georgia's location and logistics network allows companies to reach 80 percent of the United States in hours by truck, train, or plane.
Companies can access the world via Georgia's ports.
According to the biannual Georgia Manufacturing Survey, over the past 12 years manufacturers have continuously identified marketing as one of their top needs.
GaMEP is a program of Georgia Tech’s Enterprise Innovation Institute.
GaMEP has been in operation since 1960 with the singular goal of enhancing global competitiveness for Georgia manufacturers.

GaMEP's existing service toolkit includes leadership development, technology adaptation, quality systems, energy, sustainability, and implementation of lean tools to improve processes across the plant.
GaMEP has added marketing services covering everything from strategic plan development through tactical execution and improving a company’s digital presence.
Katie Takacs serves as the GaMEP group manager for marketing.
Many small to mid-size manufacturers either don’t have an internal marketing team, have one person supporting marketing, or are project managing multiple agencies.
Oneda Corp. is a Columbus, Georgia-based precision metal stamping manufacturer primarily for the automotive and heating and air industries.
Oneda Corp. worked with GaMEP to develop and implement a strategic marketing plan.

The Oneda project grew to include writing customer success stories, launching a new website with improved usability and content, planning, creating LinkedIn content, and training Michael Brooks to manage social media.
Michael Brooks is Oneda’s manager of marketing, customer service, and logistics.

Katie Takacs stated, “What we’ve found is that many small to mid-size manufacturers either don’t have an internal marketing team, have one person supporting marketing, or the company is project managing multiple agencies.”

Michael Brooks noted, “If you’re not on social media, and you’re not keeping up with that, you will lose your audience,” adding that younger audiences check websites and social postings to determine if content meets their needs before deciding on a business relationship.

facts: Oneda Corporation is a contract manufacturer located in Columbus Georgia (Southeast).
The company utilizes the latest technologies in precision metal stamping, assembly and die designs.
Oneda specializes in innovative robotic transfer and progressive tooling capabilities with presses sizes ranging from 25 to 500 tons.
The company serves industries with competitive performance and cost effective metal stampings.

From prototyping of initial design to full mass production, Oneda is a customer driven and process centered organization striving to ensure the highest standards of quality, customer satisfaction and timelines of all projects.

Working in partnership with customers, Oneda uses combined extensive experience in flexible manufacturing processes to significantly reduce tooling expenses, improved overall unit costs and increase production capacity.
Oneda uses advanced comprehensive engineering and design experience to achieve unique manufacturing techniques in factory automation.
Oneda has developed tooling technology and manufacturing capabilities to produce several different part numbers all at once within the same process.
This new methodology provides significant cost saving advantages.

Students in the area are served by the Fayette County School System, which includes three high schools: McIntosh High School, Starr’s Mill High School, and Whitewater High School.

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems believes time spent in a car should be more than just moving from one place to another, aiming to redefine the mobility experience with a focus on people and their lifestyles.
The company created the “WELL Cabin” project and initiatives with the “WELL Cabin GranLuxe” to explore the future of cabin spaces.
The WELL Cabin GranLuxe is a concept vehicle based on the Toyota HiAce featuring a 55-inch large transparent OLED display, a proprietary entertainment system, and a refined living space.
The WELL Cabin GranLuxe was exhibited at Tokyo Auto Salon 2025, attracting significant attention.
Panasonic Automotive Systems' core business is OEM, working with car manufacturers to deliver products that exceed their expectations.
The company shifted its focus from B-to-B business to considering end users by examining people's actions and lifestyles beyond traditional mobility assumptions.
Research identified a clear need for “the desire to rest while on the move,” particularly a short 30-minute power nap for businesspeople, which inspired cabin environment exploration.
The company previously developed the “Luxe” concept before the WELL Cabin GranLuxe to help passengers switch moods or build anticipation for their next experience.
The team utilized “Interpretation” methods, often used by tour guides, to introduce cultural or natural value inside the vehicle as entertainment during travel.
Product planning involved setting up personas and interviewing users to envision ideal in-cabin environments tailored to specific cases.
Designs were inspired by executives using short naps or meditation between business meetings to reset their minds, leading to spaces that allow relaxation while supporting productivity.

The cabin experience was designed to share information with vegan tourists about sushi without seafood or explain Japanese cultural connections to enhance the journey beyond simply learning information.
Designer Kanji Muto focused on shaping “human states” such as mental calmness or clarity by combining elements like light, sound, and scent.
The original “Luxe” concept was designed for executives with an emphasis on a relaxing environment for small groups.

The WELL Cabin GranLuxe broadened its focus to include tourists, inbound travelers, and affluent customers while drawing inspiration from Japanese cultural concepts of “hare” (special occasions) and “ke” (everyday things).
The exterior design motif was inspired by a cruise ship sailing across the open sea to convey elegance, relaxation, and luxury.
Panasonic collaborated with Italdesign, a renowned automotive design studio in Italy, to ensure the vehicle's exterior would leave a lasting impression on international audiences.
The company envisions advanced autonomous driving reducing commuting time to zero, making cabin spaces chosen for desired experiences increasingly important as remote work reduces unwanted travel.

Panasonic believes that in the era of autonomous driving, cars will sense people inside, read their state of mind and body, and actively respond, making the design of human states more important than ever.

Satoshi Tsuchiya joined the company in 2006, worked on advanced development of displays and video LSIs as well as IVI and CID systems, has been involved in product planning since 2019, and is currently the project manager for the “WELL Cabin” project in the Cabin UX Business Development Office.

Yusuke Shiotani joined the company in 2025, is engaged in co-creation activities with car OEMs and product planning for “WELL Cabin”, holds a Travel Service Supervisor qualification, and handles planning and customer engagement for inbound-focused business.
Kanji Muto joined the company in 1993, is engaged in interior design development in the Cabin UX Business Development Office, and is currently responsible for the design development of “WELL Cabin”.
source_title: Designing with a focus on human states: an experience-driven approach to expanding the possibilities of mobility | Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd.

facts: At CES 2022 on Jan. 4, 2022, Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America announced a collaboration with Totem USA to bring Totem's first UL-certified eBike, "Zen Rider," to the U.S. market.
In 2019, Panasonic received the industry's first e-Bike safety standard certification (UL2849) from UL, a not-for-profit safety standards development organization and leading safety science company.
The Totem USA eBike "Zen Rider" is powered by Panasonic and provides consumers with an affordable, family-friendly option to meet growing demands of the eBike market.

Research from NPD indicated a growth rate for electric bicycles of 240% in the 12 months leading up to July 2021, with sustained interest expected after pandemic spending on cycling and sporting equipment.

Derek Arciniaga, senior manager of business development at Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America, stated they are proud to announce Totem USA's first eBike with a UL-certified Panasonic system to expand mobility transformation in the United States.
The Zen Rider features a low step-through mounting design and a motor/battery system especially suited for urban areas.
This marks Panasonic's second UL 2849 certified motor & battery system.
Totem USA, in collaboration with Panasonic, is prepared to design, develop, and produce custom eBike offerings for companies interested in UL-certified eBikes.
The Panasonic drive system is suitable for two-wheel and three-wheel city, touring, or cargo eBike variants.

Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America is a division company of Panasonic Corporation of North America and a leading global supplier of automotive infotainment and connectivity system solutions.
Panasonic Automotive is headquartered in Peachtree City, Georgia, with sales, marketing, and engineering operations in Farmington Hills, Michigan.
Panasonic Corporation of North America is based in Newark, New Jersey, and serves as the principal North American subsidiary of Osaka, Japan-based Panasonic Corporation.
Panasonic was named one of Interbrand's Top 100 Best Global Brands of 2021.
Totem USA is located in McKinney, Texas, and operates as a division of the Golden Wheel Group, which is the fourth largest manufacturer in the world of bicycles and eBikes.

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd. is a premier supplier of automotive and mobility technologies for the world’s leading OEMs.
The company announced it will change its name and brand to Mobitera to better represent its commitment to creating a safe, comfortable and sustainable mobility experience.
The name and brand change will be effective on April 1, 2027.
Masashi Nagayasu serves as the chief executive officer (CEO) of Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd.
Nagayasu stated that Mobitera reflects their vision to power the future of mobility with high-quality technologies that elevate and enrich the human experience.
He noted the organization is evolving to meet the needs of today's rapidly changing market while staying true to its core values.
In December 2024, Panasonic Automotive Systems began a strategic partnership with the Apollo Group.
The partnership aims to position the organization for accelerated growth by strengthening software development capabilities.
The collaboration focuses on driving the evolution of the in-vehicle cockpit space and optimizing their overall business portfolio.
The rebrand to Mobitera is positioned as the next step in positioning the organization for success in this pursuit.
The new corporate name will be Mobitera Inc.
The name Mobitera fuses mobility from their business domain, the Latin word iter (path), and the Japanese word terasu (to illuminate).
These words express a wish to shine light upon the future.
The central i in Mobitera highlights a vow to keep individuals the focus of all they do.
The initial M symbolizes the aspiration to illuminate the future.
The corporate color is Mobitera Blue-Green, developed to symbolize the harmonization of people, society, and the planet.
The company's mission is to create a sustainable mobility society to promote a better life for everyone.
The company's vision is to be the Joy in Motion design company.
Domestic and overseas consolidated subsidiaries will also change their corporate names to align with the new Mobitera brand name, effective April 1, 2027.
Panasonic ITS Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera ITS Inc.
Panasonic Automotive Electronics Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera Solutions Inc.
Panasonic System Networks Evaluation Technology Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera Evaluation Technologies Inc.
Harima SANYO Industries Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera Wel Inc.

Panasonic Automotive Systems Reynosa Mexico S.A. de C.V. will become Mobitera Reynosa Mexico, S.A de C.V.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Monterrey Mexico S.A. de C.V. will become Mobitera Monterrey Mexico, S.A. de C.V.

Panasonic Automotive Shared Services Company Mexico, S.A. de C.V. will become Mobitera Shared Service Company Mexico, S.A. de C.V.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Europe GmbH will become Mobitera Europe GmbH.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Czech, s.r.o. will become Mobitera Czech, s.r.o.
OpenSynergy GmbH will undergo no name change.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Asia Pacific Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera Asia Pacific Co., Ltd.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Malaysia Sdn. Bhd. will become Mobitera Malaysia Sdn. Bhd.
Panasonic Automotive Systems India Pvt. Ltd. will become Mobitera India Pvt. Ltd.

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd. is a leading supplier of cutting-edge automotive and mobility technologies for global OEMs.

CEO Masashi Nagayasu stated that 'Mobitera reflects our vision to power the future of mobility with technologies that elevate and enrich the human experience.'
The company entered a partnership with the Apollo Group in 2024 aimed at accelerating growth through enhanced software development and optimizing its business portfolio.
The name 'Mobitera' is a fusion of 'mobility,' the Latin word 'iter' (meaning path), and the Japanese word 'terasu' (to illuminate).
The central 'i' in the Mobitera name and logo represents keeping individuals at the heart of everything the company does.

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America, a division of Panasonic Corporation of North America, announced a collaboration with Totem USA to bring Totem’s first UL-certified eBike to the U.S. market, “Zen Rider.”
In 2019, Panasonic received the industry’s first e-Bike safety standard certification from UL (UL2849).
The Totem USA eBike “Zen Rider” is powered by a Panasonic system and offers an affordable, family-friendly option for the growing eBike market.
NPD research indicated a 240% growth rate for electric bicycles in the 12 months leading up to July 2021, with sustained interest expected following pandemic-era cycling spending.

Derek Arciniaga, senior manager of business development at Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America, stated: “We’re proud to announce Totem USA’s first eBike, with a UL-certified Panasonic system, to expand mobility transformation in the United States.”
The Zen Rider features a low step-through mounting design, making its motor and battery system especially suited for urban areas.
Panasonic’s second UL 2849 certified motor and battery system includes a 2-Axle motor plus battery eBike system.
The drive system provides 75 Nm of max torque and a 404 Wh battery.
Pedal assistance operates up to 15mph, aligning with city or local ordinances that enforce 15mph speed limitations.
Totem USA is prepared to design, develop, and produce custom eBike offerings for companies seeking UL certified eBikes in collaboration with Panasonic.
The Panasonic drive system supports two-wheel and three-wheel city, touring, or cargo eBike variants.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America is a leading global supplier of automotive infotainment and connectivity system solutions.
Panasonic Automotive is headquartered in Peachtree City, Georgia, with sales, marketing, and engineering operations in Farmington Hills, Michigan.
Totem USA is located in McKinney, Texas, and operates as a division of the Golden Wheel Group, the 4th largest manufacturer globally of bicycles and eBikes.

entity_name: Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America (PASA)
canonical_name: Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America
facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America (PASA) is an industry-leading global supplier to automotive OEMs for infotainment systems and advanced connected car solutions.
PASA's clients include Ford, GM, Chrysler, Daimler, Fiat, Tesla, Honda, and Toyota.
The company focuses on delivering technology and engineering expertise that enhances consumer experience and safety inside the vehicle.
PASA is hiring for an AI Architect role to lead the design, development, and governance of next-generation artificial intelligence systems across its enterprise.
The AI Architect will own PASA's enterprise AI architecture spanning generative AI, agentic AI frameworks, LLM orchestration, MLOps pipelines, and responsible AI governance.

The role involves translating cutting-edge AI capabilities into real-world automotive applications including connected vehicles, in-vehicle infotainment (IVI), ADAS, manufacturing automation, and supply chain intelligence.
Responsibilities include architecting and governing enterprise AI strategy covering generative AI, agentic/multi-agent systems, LLM orchestration, computer vision, and predictive analytics.
The position requires designing multi-agent AI architectures with cognitive components including reasoning engines, memory systems, tool interfaces, and goal management loops.

The role entails leading the selection, evaluation, and integration of modern AI platforms, LLMs (e.g., GPT-4o, Llama 3, Gemini, Claude), agentic frameworks (LangChain, LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, OpenAI Agents SDK), and vector databases.
The architect will define and maintain reusable AI architecture blueprints including RAG patterns, prompt engineering standards, context engineering practices, and fine-tuning pipelines.
The position involves building and scaling MLOps and LLMOps pipelines supporting continuous model development, evaluation, deployment, monitoring, and retraining in production environments.

The role requires establishing and enforcing responsible AI governance frameworks covering model explainability, bias detection and mitigation, guardrails design, AI safety protocols, audit trails, and regulatory compliance (EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF).
The architect will define KPIs and evaluation frameworks to measure AI solution accuracy, reliability, ethical performance, and business impact.
The position involves partnering with automotive engineering teams to deliver AI-driven use cases across connected vehicles, ADAS, IVI, manufacturing quality control, and supply chain optimization.
The role includes architecting edge AI and embedded inference solutions for low-latency, on-device AI processing in automotive environments.
The architect will develop and maintain PASA's enterprise data strategy for AI including data pipelines, knowledge graphs, vector stores, and real-time streaming architectures.
The position requires mentoring and growing a high-performing team of AI/ML engineers and data scientists while championing a culture of innovation and responsible AI.
The architect will represent PASA at industry conferences, standards bodies, and ecosystem partnerships to bring cutting-edge research back to the organization.

The role involves designing scalable, secure, and cost-optimized AI/ML architectures on cloud platforms such as Microsoft Azure, Amazon Web Services, or Google Cloud Platform including data lakes/lakehouses, distributed compute, networking, and HA/DR patterns.
The position requires architecting end-to-end AI platforms by integrating data engineering, MLOps, and deployment pipelines using tools like Apache Spark, Kubernetes, and Azure Machine Learning.
Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, or related technical field with a Master's or Ph.D. preferred.
Applicants need 10+ years of experience in software engineering, data science, or solutions architecture plus 4+ years in a dedicated AI/ML architecture or senior AI engineering leadership role.

The role requires strong experience designing and delivering cloud-based data and AI platforms on Microsoft Azure, Amazon Web Services, or Google Cloud Platform with proven expertise in building scalable architectures and implementing HA/DR strategies.
Candidates must have demonstrated experience designing and deploying enterprise-scale AI/ML systems in production rather than just prototypes.
The position demands deep expertise in LLM architecture, prompt engineering, context engineering, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and fine-tuning workflows.
Applicants need hands-on experience with multi-agent frameworks including LangChain/LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, or OpenAI Agents SDK.
The role requires experience working with leading LLMs including OpenAI GPT-4o, Meta Llama 3/4, Google Gemini, Anthropic Claude, or Mistral.
Candidates must have a strong understanding of agentic AI cognitive architecture covering planning loops, memory management (short-term, long-term, episodic), tool use, and goal decomposition.
The position requires experience designing guardrails and safety systems for autonomous agentic workflows operating without continuous human intervention.
Applicants need familiarity with vector databases and embedding stores such as Pinecone, Weaviate, Chroma, or pgvector.
The role demands strong experience with cloud AI/ML platforms including AWS SageMaker, Azure Machine Learning/Azure OpenAI Service, or Google Vertex AI.
Candidates must possess expertise in MLOps and LLMOps practices and tooling such as MLflow, Kubeflow, Weights & Biases, and CI/CD pipelines for ML.
The position requires strong Python programming skills along with proficiency in SQL and familiarity with Spark for large-scale data processing.
Applicants need experience with containerization and orchestration tools including Docker, Kubernetes, and Helm.
The role requires knowledge of real-time data streaming and event-driven architectures such as Apache Kafka and Spark Streaming.
Candidates must have deep knowledge of responsible AI principles including model explainability (SHAP, LIME), bias detection and mitigation, fairness frameworks, and model risk management.
The position demands familiarity with AI regulatory frameworks including EU AI Act, NIST AI Risk Management Framework (RMF), and ISO/IEC 42001 AI Management Systems.
Applicants need experience defining enterprise AI governance policies, audit trails, data privacy controls (GDPR, CCPA), and ethical AI deployment standards.
The role requires the ability to design agentic AI governance structures including accountability frameworks for autonomous agents, human-in-the-loop controls, and fail-safe mechanisms.

Benefits include great Medical/Dental benefits, company-matched 401K retirement savings, annual bonus program, educational assistance, relaxed dress code, PASATalks Speaker Summits, leadership & mentorship programs, High5 reward recognition program, and onsite happy hours.
The job posting indicates a hybrid work model (#LI-HYBRID) and allows candidates to work from home (#LI-CW1).
The position is identified by Job ID REQ-152089.
source_title: AI Architect in United States | Panasonic Automotive

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd., a premier supplier of automotive and mobility technologies for the world’s leading OEMs, announced it will change its company name and brand to Mobitera.
The rebrand reflects a commitment to creating a safe, comfortable, and sustainable mobility experience.
The corporate name change will be effective on April 1, 2027.
Masashi Nagayasu serves as the chief executive officer (CEO) of Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd.
CEO Masashi Nagayasu stated that Mobitera reflects their vision to power the future of mobility with high-quality technologies that elevate and enrich the human experience.
In December 2024, Panasonic Automotive Systems began a strategic partnership with the Apollo Group.

The partnership aims to position the organization for accelerated growth by strengthening software development capabilities to drive the evolution of the in-vehicle cockpit space and optimize their overall business portfolio.
The rebrand to Mobitera is positioned as the next step in positioning the organization for success in this pursuit.
The name Mobitera fuses mobility from their business domain, the Latin word iter (path), and the Japanese word terasu (to illuminate).
The central i in Mobitera highlights a vow to keep individuals the focus of all they do.
The new corporate logo features an initial M symbolizing the aspiration to illuminate the future, with a central i symbolizing keeping individuals at the forefront.
The corporate color is named Mobitera Blue-Green, developed to symbolize the harmonization of people, society, and the planet.
The company's mission is to create a sustainable mobility society to promote a better life for everyone.
The company's vision is to be the Joy in Motion design company.
Domestic and overseas consolidated subsidiaries will change their corporate names to align with the new Mobitera brand, also effective on April 1, 2027.
Panasonic ITS Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera ITS Inc.
Panasonic Automotive Electronics Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera Solutions Inc.
Panasonic System Networks Evaluation Technology Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera Evaluation Technologies Inc.
Harima SANYO Industries Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera Wel Inc.
Panasonic Automotive Systems America, LLC will become Mobitera America, LLC.
Panasonic Automotive Systems de Mexico S.A. de C.V. will become Mobitera De Mexico, S.A de C.V.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Reynosa Mexico S.A. de C.V. will become Mobitera Reynosa Mexico, S.A. de C.V.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Monterrey Mexico S.A. de C.V. will become Mobitera Monterrey Mexico, S.A. de C.V.
Panasonic Automotive Sales Company de Mexico, S.A. de C.V. will become Mobitera Sales Company de Mexico, S.A. de C.V.
Panasonic Automotive Shared Services Company Mexico, S.A. de C.V. will become Mobitera Shared Service Company Mexico, S.A. de C.V.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Europe GmbH will become Mobitera Europe GmbH.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Czech, s.r.o. will become Mobitera Czech, s.r.o.
OpenSynergy GmbH will remain unchanged.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Asia Pacific Co., Ltd. will become Mobitera Asia Pacific Co., Ltd.

facts: Representatives from the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) arrived at Panasonic Automotive Systems offices in Peachtree City on Oct. 13 to present company officials with the 2020 Georgia Emerging Automotive Recognition (GEAR) Award for Automotive Innovation.
The GEAR Award is conferred to a company demonstrating innovation through emerging technologies for the automotive industry, a new product design, or a new/updated manufacturing process.
Panasonic Automotive President Scott Kirchner stated that developing innovative products for safer roadways and a transformed mobility experience is the company's core mission.
Scott Kirchner noted that the company relies on remarkable talent and culture, viewing employees as its most sustainable competitive advantage.

Scott Kirchner expressed honor at receiving the award recognizing Panasonic Automotive employees' innovation and dedication, and stated a desire to continue being part of Georgia's emerging automotive footprint.
GDEcD noted that the automotive industry is rapidly evolving from ride-sharing apps to increasing electric vehicle purchases, with Panasonic innovating to meet customer needs.
GDEcD described Panasonic Automotive as an integrated solutions leader in infotainment, connected mobility, and electrification, remaining at the intersection of current industry changes.

The award noted that Panasonic's innovations in battery advancements, new display technologies, and 5G communication performance validated across the entire vehicle have helped numerous OEMs adapt to the modern consumer.
Panasonic's Georgia facility is located in Peachtree City and employs roughly 1,500 people.
Award criteria require that the recognized innovation must have been made at the organization's Georgia location.
Georgia is home to more than 200 automotive-related facilities.
In the last five years, Georgia located 102 automotive companies, creating 8,103 jobs.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America (PASA) is an industry-leading global supplier to automotive OEMs for infotainment systems and advanced connected car solutions.
PASA's clients include Ford, GM, Chrysler, Daimler, Fiat, Tesla, Honda, Toyota, Nissan, and many others.

facts: MiddleGround Capital announced the closure of its second transaction in its inaugural private equity fund – the acquisition of Peterson Spring.

The company is based in Southfield, Michigan.

MiddleGround Capital co-founder John Stewart and Partner Scot Duncan have over 30 years of combined experience working in operational roles at Toyota Motor Company, including leadership positions in engineering, maintenance, production and manufacturing.

The investment aims to help the company increase operational efficiency, provide necessary resources to fuel long-term growth, and ensure manufacturing employment opportunities for generations to come.

facts: PAC Racing Springs, a division of Peterson Spring based in Detroit, Michigan, announced the acquisition of Schroeder Torsion Bar Manufacturing.
Schroeder Torsion Bar Manufacturing has over 50 years of heritage in industry-leading manufacturing and technology for torsion bar and sway bar manufacturing.
Schroeder Torsion Bar Manufacturing was founded by Gordon Schroeder in Burbank, California, with deep roots in circle track racing history.
The acquisition combines a cumulative 150 years of experience in high-performance spring products.
PAC Racing Springs utilizes a total system approach coupling expertise in engine valve train component design, materials, and processing to expand into torsion bar product manufacturing.
Jason Youd, PAC Racing Springs’ Manager of Business Development, stated the company is proud to bring the quality of Schroeder Product lines and Manufacturing into their company.
Gary Schroeder expressed excitement for PAC Racing Springs to carry on the tradition of Schroeder Torsion Bar Manufacturing and technology.
PAC Racing Springs is the aftermarket and racing manufacturer division of the Peterson Spring Company, headquartered in Southfield, Michigan.
Peterson Spring manufactures springs, wire forms, spring stampings, and coiled retaining rings for automotive, industrial, recreational, appliance, agriculture, and lawn/garden markets.
Peterson Spring has 12 locations across the USA, Canada, Mexico, and the United Kingdom.
PAC Racing Springs specializes in engine valve springs, suspension springs, and recently expanded into sway bar and chassis suspension systems.
PAC Racing Springs draws on over 100 years of manufacturing experience from the Peterson Spring Group of companies to improve materials and high-performance processing.

facts: Peterson Spring is part of the Peterson American Corporation, an international group of spring makers.

Its customer base spans automotive OEM and powertrain applications, chassis, and other sectors such as appliances, agriculture, lawn and garden machinery, medical equipment, furniture, heavy equipment, marine, and auto sport.

It emphasizes an integrated design and manufacturing capability to support projects across multiple industries.

facts: Georgia-Pacific announced on May 14 that most positions at its Cedar Springs mill would be eliminated by Aug. 1, with plans to permanently close the site this year.
The plant closure will eliminate approximately 535 jobs in Early County.
The closure is expected to strip away about $6.5 million from tax revenue in Early County, according to an analysis completed by David Bridges.
David Bridges, former president of Abraham Baldwin Agricultural College and a rural Georgia advocate, hosted the economic outlook town hall for the Early County Development Authority.
Bridges stated the closure will have devastating ripple effects across Georgia, Alabama, and Florida.

Early County historically generated sales tax returns of about $5 million annually, compared to an average of $2 million from surrounding counties, largely due to Georgia-Pacific's retail value and economic movement.
The county faces a property tax shortfall equivalent to replacing two-to-three mills.
Bridges' analysis utilized historical data from Georgia-Pacific, county finances, and expertise from Georgia State University and the University of Georgia.
Nearly all areas of Early County will be affected by the closure, including retail, schools, health care, and banking sectors.
Blakely, the Early County seat, will lose about $200,000 in revenue from Georgia-Pacific's natural gas purchases.
The local school system will experience revenue loss and likely see enrollment decline as families are forced to relocate.
An additional 206 jobs are predicted to be lost in the county due to income loss, requiring layoffs by the city of Blakely and the school district.
Local hospitals and medical centers will lose approximately 535 privately insured patients, significantly impacting their revenue streams.
The timber industry is experiencing a chain reaction as far north as LaGrange, with loggers forced to transport wood farther distances and facing lower prices due to lost abundant supply.
A logger from LaGrange reported that the closure has killed his market.
Georgia-Pacific's Cedar Springs mill closure was in development for five years, accelerated by Hurricane Michael.
U.S. paper and paperboard capacity declined by 1.6% in 2023 to 79.7 million tons, compared to an average annual decline of 0.9% since 2014, according to the American Forest & Paper Association.
The plant was largely depreciated and lacked necessary investments for compliance and operation over recent years.
Retired mill worker William Cleveland noted that workers sensed the closure when repairs stopped on some equipment.
Bridges stated it is unlikely a new paper producer will move into the mill due to its depreciation.
Dave Atkins, chairman of the Blakely Downtown Development Authority, emphasized the need to diversify revenue streams and capitalize on local assets like the river.

Early County Commission Chairman Hank Jester highlighted White Oak Pastures in Bluffton as a model for diversification beyond traditional beef production through regenerative practices, retail products, and agritourism.
City and county leaders are navigating budget processes to make difficult financial choices following the closure.
Early County Commissioner Jeffery Haynes is seeking federal aid, citing past recoveries from Hurricane Michael and the COVID-19 pandemic.
State Rep. Gerald Greene (R-Cuthbert) and Sen. Freddie Powell Sims (D-Dawson) attended the town hall and pledged to seek state help for Early County.
Sen. Sims encouraged residents to approach the Capitol about the county's needs.
Bridges urged making a compelling case to the governor for short-term help, emphasizing that the community must move forward rather than give up.
source_title: Economic impact study on Georgia-Pacific Cedar Springs closure paints bleak picture for Early County

source_title: Pirelli Tire North America Headquarters | SalesTools AI

facts: The Motor Enclave announced a sponsorship agreement with Pirelli Tire North America to become "The Official Tire" of the 200-acre experiential motorsports venue.
The venue is conveniently located at the corner of I-4 and I-75, less than 15 minutes from downtown Tampa.
Pirelli will receive exposure in key locations throughout the development, most notably its trademark yellow and red branding throughout the newly named "Pirelli Pit Lane".
All events on the 1.6-mile Hermann Tilke designed performance circuit begin and end at the Pirelli Pit Lane.
The Pirelli Pit Lane will incorporate a visually stunning "Pirelli Podium" where members and guests can capture photos to commemorate their visit.
The podium will showcase authentic Pirelli Formula 1 tires.
Brad Oleshansky, Founder and CEO of The Motor Enclave, stated they are elated to secure Pirelli as one of their premiere sponsors.
Oleshansky noted that Pirelli is synonymous with innovation and quality, aligning with their mission to build and operate the premiere motorsports venue in the world.
Oleshansky added that Pirelli's heritage in racing and current partnership with Formula 1 ties in beautifully with their desire to deliver unique experiences to automotive enthusiasts.
Andre Oosthuizen, Chief Marketing Officer of Pirelli Tire North America, stated they have been tracking the development of The Motor Enclave Tampa since it was announced in 2019.

Oosthuizen mentioned that after seeing the success of Brad Oleshansky's initial project in Michigan, M1 Concourse, they knew his next iteration would be the perfect platform to highlight Pirelli's full product line.

Oosthuizen expressed excitement about demonstrating their full product line across the multitude of driving surfaces at The Motor Enclave, including the performance circuit, vehicle dynamics pad, and off-road experience.
Pirelli was founded in Milan in 1872 and stands as a global brand known for cutting-edge technology, high-end production excellence, and passion for innovation drawing heavily on its Italian roots.
The company's product range consists of innovative tires for cars, motorcycles, and bicycles due to solid partnerships with the most prestigious manufacturers.
These collaborations allow Pirelli to develop tailor-made tires depending on the model to satisfy the specific needs of sophisticated consumers.
Pirelli Tire North America includes a Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS) facility and research and development center at its Rome, Georgia headquarters.
Pirelli Tire North America also includes a state-of-the-art manufacturing plant in Silao, Mexico.
Pirelli Tire North America maintains sales and marketing offices in New York City, Los Angeles, Detroit, Montreal, and Mexico City.
The company manufactures, distributes, and markets original equipment and replacement tires for export and domestic car/motorcycle applications.
The Motor Enclave is the premier developer of experiential motorsports venues in North America.
The Tampa development includes a 1.6-mile Hermann Tilke designed driving circuit.
The Tampa development includes a 2-acre vehicle dynamics pad.
The Tampa development includes a 100-acre off-road experience with miles of purpose-built trails.
The Tampa development includes a 37,000 square foot corporate event center.
The Tampa development features the largest Private Garage community in the world with more than 300 privately owned luxury garages.

facts: Gov. Roy Barnes started production at the Pirelli tire plant in Rome on Saturday.
The first production P Zero Nero tire came off the company’s modular integrated robotic system (MIRS) less than 10 minutes after start.
Gaetano Mannino is president and chief executive officer of Pirelli North America.
This is the fourth MIRS operation in the world for Pirelli.
Georgia’s long history of automotive leadership was a key determining factor for locating Pirelli's headquarters in Rome, according to CEO Gaetano Mannino.
Pirelli decided to build a new MIRS facility and headquarters in the United States approximately two years prior to the article's publication.
Pirelli investigated many possible sites but chose Rome, Georgia because state, county, and city representatives did a fantastic job promoting the area.
Northwest Georgia has attracted a growing number of automotive suppliers and support facilities since Mercedes-Benz and Honda opened plants in Alabama.
Gov. Roy Barnes touted his education reform package, which aims to produce highly skilled workers for future technology jobs.
Barnes stated that a highly skilled workforce, not land or buildings, brings businesses to Georgia.
Barnes said fostering high-tech industrial growth solidifies Georgia’s standing in the automotive industry and places Rome on the map as a high-tech manufacturing center.
Garry Fricks is chairman of the Floyd County Commission.
Ronnie Wallace is chairman of the Rome City Commission.
The plant is 400,000 square feet and includes Pirelli’s North American headquarters and research and development facility.
The project represents a $140 million investment in Greater Rome.
The highly automated MIRS module reduces the 14-step tire manufacturing process to three steps.
The MIRS system can turn out a tire every three minutes.
MIRS requires only about one-fifth of the space and uses one-third of the energy as conventional tire manufacturing.
A MIRS line can be changed over to produce a new product in about 20 minutes.
A MIRS line can make as few as four tires for specific applications.
When all 20 MIRS modules slated for the Rome plant are installed, it will be Pirelli’s largest MIRS operation.
The Rome plant currently has three MIRS modules: one in production, one in final commissioning, and one under installation.
Andrew Kearton is director of manufacturing at Pirelli.
Pirelli plans to have 15 MIRS modules in production within three years.
There is room to add five additional modules beyond the planned 15.
The plant is located on enough land in the Floyd County Industrial Park to eventually have 40 MIRS modules in production.
Pirelli expects to have 300 workers in Rome within three years.
About 250 of the future workers will be production workers, and the remaining 50 will be corporate personnel.
All headquarters employees are currently in Rome.
Approximately 35 production workers are currently on the job.
Others are currently in training and more are being recruited every month.
Andrew Kearton praised Georgia’s Quick Start training program for providing training to all of Pirelli’s first Rome workers.
Pirelli sent its first 13 people to the United Kingdom to train on MIRS modules, but they participated in a Quick Start training program before going.
Craig McDaniel is chairman of the Greater Rome Chamber and president of Coosa Valley Technical College.
Pirelli officials had temporary offices at Coosa Valley Tech’s Business Expansion Center while the new plant was under construction.
Pirelli announced its plans to build the Rome plant in April 2001.
Ground was broken in September 2001.
Corporate personnel moved into the new headquarters in April 2002.
Test production began in June 2002, three weeks ahead of schedule.
source_title: Pirelli tire plant comes on lin | Local New |  | northwestgeorgianews.com

facts: Pirelli has been given the go-ahead to begin manufacturing its latest series of tires in the United States with help from the Italian government.
The company’s plant in Rome, Georgia will soon start manufacturing connected tires using Pirelli’s Cyber Tyre technology.

These new tires include sensors that can collect data and work in conjunction with a vehicle’s on-board electronics, including the ABS and stability control systems, to enhance driving dynamics and safety.
The Italian government stepped in to limit China’s control over Pirelli by using its ‘golden powers’ rule established to protect companies of national strategic interest.

Last month, Italy imposed several curbs on Sinochem, noting that none of the eight board members that Sinochem has previously appointed will be allowed to hold top corporate positions like chairman or chief executive.
Sinochem has been instructed that it cannot exert any influence over Pirelli, and Pirelli doesn’t need to share sensitive information with the Chinese company.
For more than a year, there had been concerns about Pirelli in the US because the Chinese company Sinochem holds a 34.1 percent stake in the tire giant.
The US government clamping down on Chinese technology made it seem likely Pirelli would have to build these tires elsewhere.
Pirelli’s plant in Rome, Georgia can currently produce up to 400,000 tires annually.
The company did not say how many tires using its Cyber Tyre tech will be added to its production.
Claudio Zanardo, Pirelli North America chief executive, stated that the start of Cyber Tyre production in the Rome, Georgia plant is a significant milestone for Pirelli in this country.

Zanardo noted the move reflects Pirelli’s commitment to bringing advanced technologies like Cyber Tyre closer to the market and further strengthening its industrial footprint and innovation capabilities in the United States.
The development will help offset some of the impacts of US tariffs on the tire manufacturer.
source_title: Pirelli’s Connected Tires Will Be Made In Georgia, The US Almost Blocked Them Over China | Carscoops

facts: Pirelli Tire North America Inc. is launching an all-weather tire line, the WeatherActive.
The WeatherActive is the company's first with all-weather capabilities.
The announcement originates from Rome, Ga.
The launch was announced on September 19, 2022.
source_title: Pirelli launches all-weather tire line for N.A. market - Tire Business

facts: Pirelli is reinforcing its long-term commitment to the United States with a key step in its product and industrial strategy.
The Georgia plant will be enhanced by the production of connected tires featuring Cyber™ Tyre technology.
Investment and output expansion details for the project will be communicated in the coming months following the finalization of development plans.
Pirelli made the announcement while participating in the SelectUSA Investment Summit, a flagship event promoted by the U.S. Department of Commerce.
Cyber™ Tyre is being showcased at the summit as a core innovation shaping the future of smart mobility.
Cyber™ Tyre is the world's first hardware-and-software system capable of collecting data and information from sensors embedded in tires.
The system processes sensor data through Pirelli's proprietary software and algorithms.
It communicates in real time with the vehicle's electronics to enable new functionalities integrated with driving and control systems.
These functionalities enhance the driving experience, increase safety levels, and support connected infrastructure.
Cyber™ Tyre was showcased at the Georgia State booth during the SelectUSA summit.
Pirelli has been established in Georgia for more than 20 years.
Claudio Zanardo, CEO of Pirelli North America, stated that the start of Cyber™ Tyre production in the Rome, Georgia plant is a significant milestone for Pirelli in the country.
The production start reflects Pirelli's commitment to bringing advanced technologies like Cyber™ Tyre closer to the market.
It further strengthens Pirelli's industrial footprint and innovation capabilities in the United States.
To enhance Rome as a high-tech production site, Pirelli is finalizing the introduction of the latest version of the MIRS (Modular Integrated Robotized System) production process.
The MIRS process will be the most advanced manufacturing process for high-end, premium production within the entire Pirelli Group.
The MIRS process is exclusive to the Georgia factory.
The new process enhances robotized production capabilities, increasing productivity, flexibility, and quality.
It is a highly digitalized system that enables a direct link between product design and its industrial application.
This development lays the groundwork for growth in Pirelli's production capacity in Rome.
The Rome, Georgia plant specializes in high-value tire production for the North American market.
The facility also hosts a dedicated R&D center to strengthen technological development and product innovation.
The facility is recognized for responsible sourcing practices, including the use of FSC® (Forest Stewardship Council®)-certified natural rubber.

source_title: PIRELLI WILL START CYBER™ TYRE PRODUCTION IN GEORGIA UNDERLINING THE STRATEGIC IMPORTANCE OF THE U.S. MARKET | Morningstar

facts: Pirelli North America announced a new partnership with The Ray, a non-profit based in Atlanta.
The Ray has helped develop a net-zero highway testbed on an 18-mile stretch of Interstate 85 in West Georgia.
The testbed is located near the Pirelli factory in Rome, Georgia.

The partnership aims to advance research and solutions in technology for road safety, circular economy, EV charging infrastructure, renewable energy, and environmental and social impacts of tires and roads.
As part of the non-profit’s Advisory Board, Pirelli will contribute to strategic thinking around roadways of the future.
Pirelli is investigating future projects with The Ray to research the impact of rubber-modified asphalt.
Pirelli is investigating the use of tire-derived aggregate for stormwater galleries.
Pirelli is joining a multi-tiered partnership focused on tires and the circular economy.
This partnership focuses on driving education, innovation, solutions development, and sharing of best practices.
Maureen Kline serves as Vice President, Public Affairs and Sustainability at Pirelli North America.
Maureen Kline stated that being part of sustainability solutions extending beyond just the product is an important part of their commitment to climate action.
Pirelli believes tire design will play a key role in shaping the future of mobility.
The company has set goals to increase the renewable and recycled content of tires.
The company has set goals to reduce environmental impacts.
Pirelli is focusing on mitigation strategies for broader impacts of its products, including end-of-life tires and tire and road wear particles.
Allie Kelly serves as Executive Director of The Ray.
Allie Kelly stated that Pirelli leads from Georgia with an advanced manufacturing facility that was the first FSC-certified tire plant in the world.
Allie Kelly noted that The Ray leads and scales net-zero transportation infrastructure from The Ray Highway in Georgia.
The Ray is a non-profit organization and living innovation lab focused on sustainable highway transportation and setting new standards for roadways of the future.
Current projects at The Ray include the use of highway rights-of-way for solar arrays.
Current projects at The Ray include cloud-based data management for connected vehicle safety and efficiency.
Current projects at The Ray include drones used as first responders.
Current projects at The Ray include pollinator gardens for endangered species.
Current projects at The Ray include further deployment and impact of bioswales.
The Ray defines net-zero in transportation as “Zero Deaths, Zero Waste, Zero Carbon and Zero Impact.”

The Ray facilitates building and scaling clean transportation and energy projects with transportation agencies, including Georgia and other state Departments of Transportation, tollway and turnpike agencies, regional collaboratives, and local governments.
Pirelli was founded in Milan in 1872.
Pirelli Tire North America includes the Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS) facility and research and development center at its Rome, Georgia headquarters.
Pirelli Tire North America includes a state-of-the-art manufacturing plant in Silao, Mexico.
Pirelli Tire North America has sales and marketing offices in New York City, Los Angeles, Detroit, Montreal, and Mexico City.
The company manufactures, distributes, and markets original equipment and replacement tires for export and domestic car/motorcycle applications.
The Ray is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit charity and net-zero highway testbed located on 18 miles of Interstate 85 between Lagrange, Georgia and the Georgia-Alabama state line.

The stretch of interstate is named in memory of Ray C. Anderson (1934-2011), a Georgia native recognized as a leader in green business who challenged his company, Interface, Inc., to pursue a zero environmental footprint.

The company is responsible for overseeing the distribution, marketing, and customer service operations for Porsche vehicles in the North American market.
With a focus on delivering high-performance luxury vehicles, Porsche Cars North America works closely with dealerships and customers to provide a seamless and exceptional driving experience.
The company also offers various services and programs to enhance the ownership experience for Porsche enthusiasts across the region.

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) is headquartered at One Porsche Drive, Atlanta, GA 30354 USA.
PCNA was established in 1984 and has been headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia since 1998.
PCNA serves as the exclusive U.S. importer for the Porsche 911, 718 Boxster, 718 Cayman, Macan, Cayenne, Panamera, and Taycan.
PCNA supports 192 independently owned and operated Porsche dealerships across the U.S.
PCNA supplies parts, service, marketing, and training to its supported dealerships.
PCNA operates as an indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of Porsche AG, which is headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany.
Porsche Digital, Inc. maintains an Atlanta, Georgia office co-located with the PCNA headquarters.
The Porsche Digital Atlanta office develops the "My Porsche" sports car owners site, a central e-commerce platform, and digital services for wholesale and retail operations.
Klaus Zellmer holds the title of President and CEO at Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA).
Christian Friedl is the Head of the Porsche factory in Zuffenhausen.
Robert Ader serves as Vice President Customer Relations at Porsche.
The "My Porsche" platform functions as a centralized customer portal bundling offerings and services for individual cars and is available as a mobile app.
Porsche plans to enroll its all-electric Taycan model in the "Porsche Track Your Dream" tracking service in the future.

The "Porsche Track Your Dream" digital service allows customers to track their vehicle through 14 milestone events, including order creation, freeze point for changes, production updates, vessel departure from Germany, port entry into the U.S., and dealership arrival.
Initial rollout of the tracking service targets new car customers in the USA, Germany, Great Britain, Canada, Switzerland, and Spain, with additional markets to follow in coming months.
PCNA operates the first Porsche Experience Center in North America in Atlanta, featuring a 1.6-mile driver development track, a business center, and Restaurant 356.
Porsche Digital GmbH was founded in 2016 as a subsidiary of Porsche AG to focus on innovation, digital product development, and venture investments.
Porsche Digital's global headquarters are located in Ludwigsburg, Germany, with additional offices in Berlin, Tel Aviv, and Shanghai.

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) announced third-quarter retail deliveries totaled 18,403 cars in the U.S.
PCNA's overall sales for the first nine months of 2025 reached 57,099 cars, up 5.6 percent year-to-date over 2024.
The 718, Macan, and Panamera models posted significant gains year-to-date.
Third-quarter sales of the Taycan and Macan Electric performed well.
Porsche Approved Certified Pre-Owned (CPO) sales in the U.S. for Q3 reached a new PCNA quarterly record of 12,232 units.
Year-to-date CPO sales stood at 35,439 units, representing a 13 percent increase compared to the same point in 2024.
Timo Resch serves as President and CEO of PCNA.
Timo Resch stated that Porsche remains strong in the United States due to customer support and loyalty during unprecedented change.
Timo Resch emphasized that choice is vital for customers and highlighted the upcoming new Cayenne Electric joining the Cayenne gasoline and plug-in hybrid models by year's end.
Compared to Q3 2024, PCNA sales decreased by 4.9 percent, driven in part by restricted supply.
The Macan was the top-selling model in Q3 with 6,599 deliveries.
The Cayenne was the second top seller in Q3 with 4,294 deliveries.
The 911 was the third top seller in Q3 with 3,448 deliveries.
PCNA follows the U.S. Auto Industry Sales Release Schedule issued by Motor Intelligence.
Third quarter new car sales for PCNA began on July 1, 2025, and ended on September 30, 2025.
ALL 911 model recorded 3,448 July-September 2025 sales and 8,872 year-to-date sales.
ALL 718 model recorded 1,719 July-September 2025 sales and 5,398 year-to-date sales.
ALL TAYCAN model recorded 1,553 July-September 2025 sales and 3,636 year-to-date sales.
ALL PANAMERA model recorded 790 July-September 2025 sales and 3,410 year-to-date sales.
ALL CAYENNE model recorded 4,294 July-September 2025 sales and 14,621 year-to-date sales.
ALL MACAN model recorded 6,599 July-September 2025 sales and 21,162 year-to-date sales.
Total PCNA sales were 18,403 for July-September 2025 and 57,099 year-to-date.

facts: Porsche Cars North America plans to invest $1,723,032.00 to build out 42,734 square feet of new space in Atlanta, Georgia.
The company plans to occupy the new space at 1 Porsche Dr in Atlanta on or about October 1, 2023.
According to state and local development sources, the investment covers the build-out of the new corporate facility.
Porsche Cars North America states it thrives on a foundation of precision engineering fueled by the American entrepreneurial spirit.
The company carries on the vision of founder Ferry Porsche by delivering impassioned experiences tied to the cars it builds.

Porsche aims to fuel 'Porsche Passion' as a brand built on being better together as a diverse and gifted workforce united in the desire to be the world's most aspirational brand and most empowering employer.
Each team member at Porsche Cars North America brings a unique perspective and master craft to the workforce.
The company boasts engineers, brand builders, marketing minds, business development leaders, analytical aficionados, and corporate professionals in the automotive business.
Porsche's goal is to lead its industry in performance and customer satisfaction.
Kjell Gruner serves as the Chief Executive Officer of Porsche Cars North America.
