facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) has announced a new multi-million dollar investment to further develop its North American headquarters and U.S. footprint in Georgia.
The new 33-acre development is located at the Northeast corner of Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.
The development adds to the company’s existing 27-acre headquarters.
Construction for the first stage of the development starts this month.
The first stage will be a standalone Porsche Service Center, independently developed, owned and operated jointly by Hennessy Porsche North Atlanta and Porsche Atlanta Perimeter.
Over the next three years, PCNA will invest in a new Porsche Classic Factory Restoration facility as well as a track extension for the Porsche Experience Center Atlanta.
Kjell Gruner, newly appointed President and CEO of PCNA, stated that Porsche is committed to the United States and its American hometown of Atlanta.
The expansion aims to enhance the customer experience by expanding the campus for faster service at a unique location next to the world’s busiest airport.
This development starts a longer-term expansion of the successful Porsche Experience Center Atlanta, which has already welcomed over 270,000 visitors since opening in 2015.
In partnership with two Atlanta dealers who will operate the service center, PCNA announced this next phase of development to lay the groundwork for additional economic activity in the region.
Governor Brian Kemp stated it is a pleasure to see Porsche grow their U.S. footprint in Georgia in partnership with their two Atlanta area dealers.
Georgia’s automotive industry is a $3 billion economic driver in the state, and Porsche is an important part of its success story.
Governor Kemp thanked Porsche for their continued commitment to Georgia and looks forward to seeing the opportunities this multi-million dollar expansion will create for Georgians.
City of Hapeville Mayor Alan Hallman expressed excitement for PCNA launching the expansion of its North American headquarters within the Hapeville community.
The project symbolizes tremendous growth and opportunities for the City of Hapeville, according to Mayor Hallman.
Atlanta Mayor Keisha Lance Bottoms thanked Porsche for continuing to believe, choose, and invest in Atlanta.
The Atlanta Administration looks forward to working with Porsche’s leadership to strengthen the local economy through highly skilled and middle-wage opportunities.
The headquarters campus expansion near Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport further cements Atlanta’s reputation as a premiere international hub for commerce, according to Mayor Bottoms.
The Porsche Service Center South Atlanta will be a 20,815 square foot facility on 2.5 acres of land.
The new service center facility will include 13 lift bays to service customer vehicles, a Porsche Driver’s Selection boutique, as well as a reception area and waiting lounge for customers.
The Porsche Service Center South Atlanta is one of two standalone service centers for Porsche in the U.S.
The service center will offer greater service capacity and additional convenience for customers in Atlanta and surrounding cities.
The expected completion date for the service center is the third quarter of 2021.
Phase two of development is expected to begin in 2021 with construction of a parking structure.
Phase two will be followed by construction of the new Porsche Classic Factory Restoration facility and Porsche Experience Center Atlanta track extension.
This phase of the development is expected to be completed in mid-2023.
source_title: Porsche Expanding North American HQ In Georgia - Automotive - Business Facilities Magazine

facts: Porsche is set to launch its second all-electric vehicle, the Macan, during a star-studded event at Soho House New York on Monday, May 6th.
Actor Barry Keoghan will host the evening, which will also feature a live musical performance by British singer and songwriter RAYE.
The launch unveils the all-electric Macan in the striking color of Provence.

Ayesha Coker, Vice President of Marketing for Porsche Cars North America, Inc., emphasized the brand's enduring appeal in the fashion sector and its commitment to maintaining the Macan's status as a segment leader through its sleek and sporty design.
The all-electric Macan embodies a progressive and timeless design while delivering characteristic performance expected from the brand.
Porsche is setting new standards in e-performance with models like the Macan 4 and Macan Turbo.
The Macan 4, equipped with Launch Control, offers up to 300 kW (402 hp) of power.
The Macan Turbo boasts up to 470 kW (630 hp), enabling rapid acceleration and impressive top speeds.
Porsche Cars North America, Inc., headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, serves as the exclusive U.S. importer of various Porsche models.

The company supports a network of independently owned and operated Porsche centers across the U.S. to ensure customers receive an unparalleled experience reflecting the brand's history of automotive excellence and innovation.

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (importer and distributor of Porsche sports cars, Cayenne SUVs, and the Panamera four-door sports sedan) celebrated a landmark groundbreaking for its new headquarters, to be known as One Porsche Drive.
The new headquarters will be located on a 26.4-acre site adjacent to Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.
The site is the first complex to be built in the “Aerotroplis Atlanta” business district.

Employees from Porsche Cars North America and its affiliates (Porsche Financial Services, Porsche Business Services, and Porsche Consulting) attended the groundbreaking event alongside executives from Porsche Group companies in North America and Germany.
Most of Porsche’s North American operations will be consolidated under one roof at the new facility upon its completion in the second half of 2014.
Porsche’s presence has been embraced by Atlanta and the state of Georgia for the last 14 years, according to Matthias Mueller, President and CEO of Porsche AG.
The new facility will contribute to Georgia’s economic development and add glamour to the local community, according to Matthias Mueller.
The relationship between Americans and Porsche dates back to the early 1950s, serving as a powerful incentive for the company to safeguard it.
The investment in Atlanta testifies to Porsche’s belief in the future of the U.S. and confidence in this important market, according to Matthias Mueller.

Attendees celebrating the groundbreaking included Dr. Wolfgang Porsche (Chairman of the Supervisory Board of Porsche AG), Georgia Governor Nathan Deal, Atlanta Mayor Kasim Reed, Bernhard Maier (Member of the Executive Board Sales and Marketing of Porsche AG), Detlev von Platen (President and CEO of Porsche Cars North America), and Matthias Mueller.
One Porsche Drive will ultimately house 400 employees, including 100 jobs that will be new to Georgia.
In addition to corporate offices, the facility will comprise a Technical Service and Training Center, a Conference Center, and a Museum.
The headquarters campus will feature an industry-first Customer Experience Center with a 1.6-mile test track and handling road course to showcase Porsche vehicles.
The United States remains Porsche's single largest market, underscoring the company's commitment to U.S. customers and dealers, according to Detlev von Platen.
The ceremony symbolizes Porsche putting down permanent roots in Atlanta, which has been its home since 1998, according to Detlev von Platen.
Porsche is the first tenant of the Aerotropolis development site, according to Atlanta Mayor Kasim Reed.
Porsche's presence underscores Atlanta's continuing commitment to attract world-class companies and iconic brands, according to Mayor Kasim Reed.
The development sends a clear signal to other companies that Atlanta is open for business, according to Mayor Kasim Reed.

Neighboring facilities include the Delta Air Lines world headquarters, Wachovia Bank’s principal processing center, and Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport (the world's busiest airport, serving over 92 million travelers in 2011).
The new headquarters will be a terrific asset to Georgia’s economy and a unique travel destination for arriving travelers, according to Georgia Governor Nathan Deal.

Governor Nathan Deal expressed confidence that One Porsche Drive will become a renowned address standing for vision, innovation, and success, as well as one of the greatest driving experiences in the world.
source_title: Porsche Cars North America Celebrates A Landmark Groundbreaking | Automotive World

The 718, Macan, and Panamera posted significant year-to-date gains.

facts: RealTruck's debt trades at 60 cents on the dollar for its 2029 bonds, reflecting lingering concerns about its financial health.
The company's leverage ratio is projected to exceed 10 times EBITDA by year-end 2025, flagged as a red flag for credit stability by S&P Global Ratings.
S&P Global Ratings assigned RealTruck a "B-" issue-level rating with a negative outlook driven by tightening margins and negative cash flows.
U.S. tariffs have not directly impacted RealTruck due to USMCA exemptions, but indirect effects such as reduced consumer spending on discretionary automotive products pose a long-term threat.
RealTruck forecasts a 5% revenue decline for 2025, aligning with broader industry trends of depreciating truck values.
The company's annual revenue remains above $500 million.
RealTruck executed a debt-for-equity swap, converting its 0.00% 2027 Convertible Notes into 7.00% 2030 Senior Secured Convertible Notes to reduce principal debt.
The debt restructuring required issuing over 316 million new shares, diluting existing shareholders and sending the stock to record lows.
RealTruck has suspended operations at non-core facilities and implemented workforce reductions to preserve cash flow.
Asset sales have been constrained by weak market demand, with Q3 2025 data showing wholesale truck values have plummeted.
The company launched a 3D truck customization tool in May 2025 as part of its pivot to innovation.
RealTruck has emphasized its avoidance of off-balance-sheet liabilities and factoring arrangements to maintain lender confidence.

Broader market optimism, driven by strong Q3 2025 earnings from major banks like Bank of America, provides a tailwind for the financial sector but highlights the delicate balance RealTruck must strike with creditors.

The European Central Bank's May 2025 Financial Stability Review warns of heightened sensitivity to trade agreement news, meaning indirect tariff impacts could ripple through RealTruck's supply chains.
RealTruck acquired Vehicle Accessory Group in March 2025, its largest acquisition to date, expanding its manufacturing footprint and product portfolio.
The acquisition aligns with a broader industry trend where 12% of new Class 7–8 trucks sold in 2025 are electric, representing a fivefold increase since 2022.
RealTruck is positioning itself at the forefront of electrification to mitigate near-term risks and capture growth in a transitioning market.
source_title: RealTruck's Financial Stability and Lender Confidence: Navigating Post-Debt Risk Mitigation

The brand is launching a second generation update to the best-selling product as part of the RealTruck RetraxPRO Series.
Retrax was founded in 1997.
Brad Derosier serves as RealTruck Manufacturing Site Leader in North Dakota.
The new RealTruck RetraxPRO features improvements to strength, security, ease of use, and protection against the elements.
RealTruck RetraxPRO XR offers an integrated T-Slot rails option that connects with racks, cargo holders, tents, and cross bars.
RetraxPRO XR includes TraxLatch™, providing unlimited lockable stopping positions to cinch gear between the cover and tailgate.
Maura Rioux is RealTruck’s Sr. Director of Brand Marketing.
Retrax helped pioneer the retractable truck bed cover category.
The RealTruck lineup of premium retractable Retrax truck bed covers is designed and manufactured in the USA.
Retrax covers utilize low-profile designs with compact storage canisters to maximize cargo capacity.
The covers use durable materials for security and feature optional T-slot rails compatible with a broad range of cargo management accessories.
The one millionth tonneau cover was produced at the RealTruck facility in North Dakota.
The facility has been producing covers in Grand Forks, ND since 1997.

facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America is Georgia’s largest economic project-to-date.
HMGMA is on course to become the single most transformative influence on the Savannah region’s economy, workforce, public resources and way of life for the next half century.
The plant’s physical address is in what has traditionally been rural Bryan County.
The seismic impact of its $7.59 billion investment is rippling out across Chatham, Effingham and Bulloch counties and spreading across the state.
Per the Hyundai agreement, the company is committed to an average annual salary of $58,105.
In 2023, Hyundai Motor Company announced it is committed to raise salaries by 25% in a few years.

At 8,500 employees, that would be pushing about a half billion dollars' worth of payroll through the economy, according to Michael Toma, Fuller E. Callaway Professor of Economics at Georgia Southern University.
The deal that Hyundai signed to earn a 27-year property tax abatement involves capital commitments and the creation of around 8,500 direct jobs.

Indirect positions supported by LG’s joint venture EV battery facility and the 18 Hyundai suppliers that have committed to moving to the area are expected to reach nearly 38,000 throughout the state over the next 10 years.
HMGMA moved from announcement to actual production in less than two years.
HMGMA sits on nearly 3,000 acres that before development was mostly planted pines.
The plant is roughly the size of Tybee Island.
It is expected to pay leasehold and ad valorem property tax payments starting in 2026 once the initial construction is complete.
Bryan, Bulloch, Chatham, Effingham and Liberty counties are each within a 60-mile radius of the plant.
The north side of Bryan County will become the epicenter for the most transformative change.
Michael Toma said they are developing an electric vehicle industry from the ground up, including the full supply chain, from nothing.

Paige Clayton, assistant professor for the School of City & Regional Planning at the Georgia Institute of Technology, noted challenges with basic infrastructure capacity and housing constraints due to attracting people from other areas.
Clayton said the look and feel of the city will change to an extent but it won't become a very urban area in the near term.
The city of Pembroke is just eight miles from HMGMA and may see more commercial growth such as nail salons, doctor’s offices and places to eat.
Pembroke has approved rezoning for the addition of 3,000 homes.
Heartwood community on the south end of the county expects to see 10,000 homes at full build out.
Road improvements to support travel to and from and around the metaplant are already underway, including five roundabouts and a new interchange at I-16, totaling more than $180 million.
The frontage road that runs in between I-16 and HMGMA is complete.
Georgia Department of Transportation (GDOT) is ramping up road improvements for one of its biggest projects in the state.
Construction of five roundabouts is underway along a stretch of U.S. 280 between the Interstate Centre entrance to the north and the Hyundai plant entrance to the south.
A new interchange at the intersection of I-16 and Old Cuyler Road is scheduled to begin in quarter three of this year and will consist of three multi-lane roundabouts.
The road improvement projects amount to $187.3 million, according to GDOT.
Bryan County Schools is investing more than $200 million into new schools on the north and south end of the county.
The new Richmond Hill High School is under construction and slated to open this fall, costing $97 million.
An additional $35 million will be spent on athletic facilities at Richmond Hill High School.
Bryan County High School will cost $90 million.

In 2024, The Savannah Economic Development Authority (SEDA) created the Reginal Industry Support Enterprise (RISE) to address manufacturing hiring needs brought on by the industry's boom and its need for a robust manufacturing workforce.
Georgia's Quick Start program, a division of the Technical College System of Georgia paid for by Georgia taxpayers, currently partners with HMGMA to provide training for all Hyundai employees.
Training happens at two separate Quick Start facilities: Port City Logistics in Port Wentworth and Quick Start Advanced Manufacturing in Pooler.
The Quick Start Advanced Manufacturing facility is slated for a $10.6 million state-funded expansion.
Construction is also well underway at a HMGMA-dedicated Quick Start Hyundai Mobility Training Center, a $62.5 million state-funded facility.
Bryan County taxpayers could likely soon foot the bill for the construction of a new jail, which the county estimates could cost $75 to $100 million.

Sheriff Mark Crowe told the Savannah Morning News that the incoming growth is concerning and they have to keep up with that growth rate by adding more deputies every year, getting more vehicles and making sure deputies are trained accordingly.
Water supply and quality has remained a contentious issue during the metaplant's development with threatened lawsuits, permit challenges, and election upsets at the local level.
The metaplant will require up to 4 million gallons of water a day once it reaches full production.

An interlocal agreement between Bryan County and neighboring Bulloch County allowed for the drilling of four wells in Bulloch's farming community to pump up to 6.65 million gallons of pristine drinking and agricultural water daily from the Floridan Aquifer.
The Floridan Aquifer is an extensive reservoir covering 100,000 square miles beneath all of Florida and parts of Georgia, Alabama and South Carolina.
The remaining 2.65 million gallons is being pumped to supply the projected growth and development in North Bryan County.

The state created a pool funded in equal parts by Bulloch and Bryan counties and the Hyundai metaplant to address any potential significant impacts to private wells within a 5-mile radius of the new withdrawal sites.

Recently, the state approved $502 million for a comprehensive plan to address water infrastructure needs across the Coastal Empire, which involves creating an alternative surface water supply for Hyundai to replace the Bulloch County wells.
The plant is expected to hire 8,500 workers by 2030.

Savannah Morning News education and workforce development reporter Joseph Schwartzburt produced a four-part series, Behind the Wheel, that provides a detailed analysis of the jobs situation, from the promises made and the public and private dollars invested to make sure those promises are kept.
source_title: Communities prepare for change as Hyundai plant celebrates official opening

facts: The Department of Commerce is offering up to $225 million in CHIPS and Science Act funding to automotive supplier Bosch.
The funding includes an additional $350 million in loans from the CHIPS Program Office.
The funding supports Bosch’s $1.9 billion project to expand its Roseville, California, facility for silicon carbide semiconductor production for the automotive industry.
The project is expected to create up to 1,000 construction jobs.
The project is expected to create 700 manufacturing, engineering and research and development roles.
The Roseville wafer fab was acquired by Bosch in August 2023 from TSI Semiconductors Corp.
Bosch vowed to significantly grow its global portfolio of SiC semiconductors by the end of 2030.
The location will be Bosch’s first semiconductor facility in the country.
The facility extends the auto supplier’s international semiconductor manufacturing network.
Bosch launched a new spinoff company called Robert Bosch Semiconductor LLC for the transformation.
Commerce Secretary Gina Raimondo stated that SiC chips are important components for applications in the automotive, telecommunications, and defense industries because they utilize less energy.
Production at the 200-millimeter wafer facility is expected to start in 2026.
Production will focus on electric vehicle driving and charging efficiency.
The facility has the potential to represent 40% of U.S.-based SiC manufacturing capacity.
The Roseville facility will perform both front-end device manufacturing and backend testing, sorting and dicing processes.
Bosch plans to claim the advanced manufacturing investment credit to cover 25% of qualified capital expenditures.
The site received a $25 million tax incentive from the state of California grant.
source_title: Bosch nabs $225M in CHIPS funds | Manufacturing Dive

facts: Nader Hammoud serves as a Senior Application Engineer for Bosch's Two-Wheeler & Powersports team.
His role focuses on developing active safety systems and applying a 'calibrator mindset' to balance system limits for comfort and control.
Hammoud studied mechanical engineering at the American University of Beirut and completed graduate mechanical engineering studies at Georgia Tech.
Bosch associates on his team are based in Michigan and collaborate daily with teams in India, Japan, China, and other distant time zones.
The team modifies power steering systems originally developed for cars so they can be adapted for off-road vehicles such as Utility Task Vehicles (UTVs).
Team members undergo regular training at Rawhyde Adventures in California under the guidance of Jim Hyde, a well-known figure in two-wheeled off-road motorsports.
Bosch offers a two-year junior management program that Hammoud completed before joining the Two-Wheeler team.
The team tests safety systems on surfaces covered in basalt tiles that mimic oily or icy roads when sprayed with water to evaluate spin recovery and braking performance.
source_title: Finding the Limit | Bosch in the USA

facts: Bosch sees itself as a mobility software company and is changing its organizational structure to reflect customer requirements and open up further growth.
Dr. Markus Heyn is a member of the Bosch board of management and the future chairman of the five-strong Bosch Mobility sector board.
Some of the business sector's individual divisions will be redrawn effective January 1, 2024.
All divisions will be given horizontal, cross-divisional responsibilities.
Bosch aims for its mobility solutions to generate sales revenue of more than 80 billion euros worldwide by 2029.
The market for automotive software is driven by the trend toward software-defined vehicles and is likely to reach a volume of significantly more than 200 billion euros by 2030.
The projected 2030 automotive software market volume will be three times more than in 2020.
Bosch's current growth in the automotive software market is in double digits.
The entire automotive industry is putting a stronger focus on software development, with its share in development cost expected to be roughly 30 percent by 2030.
At Bosch, the share of software in development cost is already higher than the 30 percent industry projection.
More than 50 percent of all R&D associates working for Bosch Mobility are software engineers.
Software-defined vehicles offer speed of development and decoupling of software and hardware development as outstanding advantages.
Implementing new functions in existing systems will take days instead of years due to software-defined vehicle architecture.
Cars will feel like new for longer thanks to continuous software updates.
Drivers across the globe want to seamlessly integrate their vehicles into their digital world, similar to smartphones.
From 2025, Bosch expects that software-defined vehicles will be introduced on a broad scale.
Vehicle motion management serves as an example of how reorganization and intensified cross-divisional collaboration support new automotive engineering trends.
A new control concept stabilizes vehicles faster and more accurately by intervening in the braking system, electrical powertrain, and electric steering system.
The new control system helps shorten braking distance and requires considerably less corrective action from the driver.
The technical basis for the new control system is ESP10, the latest generation of the Bosch braking control system, and the Vehicle Dynamics Control 2.0 software function.
The new control system can be integrated in the central vehicle computer or available as a standalone software package instead of forming part of the ESP control unit.
Bosch anticipates a threefold increase in software applications and a tenfold increase in applications that access the cloud.
Software-defined vehicles require a new, centralized electrical and electronic (E/E) architecture.
More than 100 control units are installed in the latest premium-class vehicles.
Compact-class vehicles feature between 30 and 50 control units.
Bosch's focus is on making the complexity of electronic systems controllable and as reliable as possible.

The number of control units will be significantly reduced by using powerful computers for various vehicle domains such as cockpit and connectivity functions, driver assistance systems, automated driving, and powertrain.
Bosch is developing a uniform IT architecture for the entire vehicle, spanning from the cloud to the central vehicle computer to individual control units.
Bosch pursues a technology-neutral approach with fuel cells, batteries, and hydrogen engines while operating equally in software and hardware domains.
Bosch develops and manufactures key components of modern vehicles under one roof.
Bosch produces more than 250 million control units each year, which are configured with the company's proprietary software.
Bosch specializes in integrating software from diverse sources and resulting from collaboration between the automotive and IT industries.
The Bosch Mobility sector board defined the business sector's new configuration in consultation with associates and executives from individual divisions.
The reorganization is subject to observance of employee representatives' participatory rights.
The business sector will comprise seven divisions following the realignment.
The Electrified Motion division will cover everything relating to electric motors, from the Bosch e-axle to seat adjusters.
The Vehicle Motion division will deal with vehicle dynamics, from ABS and ESP to steering.
The Power Solutions division will handle combustion-engine technology, mobile and stationary fuel cells, electrolyzers, and hydrogen engines.
Cross-Domain Computing Solutions will develop solutions for areas ranging from automatic parking to automated driving.
Mobility Electronics will drive forward the development of control units and promote in-house semiconductor activities at Bosch.
Mobility Aftermarket will deal with the spare-parts market and the Bosch Car Service workshop franchise.
E-Bike Systems will supply systems solutions comprising drive units, rechargeable batteries, ABS, and connected displays for e-bikes.
The Bosch subsidiary ETAS will be given horizontal responsibility for hardware-agnostic software for operating systems and engineering tools.
source_title: Mobility in the software age: Bosch to realign its automotive-supply business for further growth - Bosch Media Service

facts: Federal authorities are questioning working conditions at a Georgia auto parts plant for the eighth time in four years.
The Occupational Safety and Health Administration launched an inquiry of Sewon American in LaGrange after complaints of excessive heat and the death of an employee.
Teresa Pickard died May 29 after saying she was having breathing troubles at work.
The state medical examiner has not released a cause of death for the employee.
The firm makes chassis and other parts for Kia and Hyundai.
Sewon American stated that Pickard's death was not work related.
Former employees told The Atlanta Journal-Constitution about sweltering conditions, inadequate air conditioning and poor access to water.
Sewon has faced fines in the past for ignoring safety problems that threaten workers.

facts: Leadership from Sewon America and Savannah Technical College signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) for a training partnership at the College’s Effingham Campus located at 2890 Hwy 21 South, Rincon, GA 31326.
STC President Dr. Kathy Love stated they look forward to a long-lasting partnership and will work closely with Sewon America to prepare people to qualify for jobs in Effingham County.
The training partnership MOU will include credit and non-credit training programs that could be offered at the College’s Effingham campus along with other campuses.
Sewon America Vice Chairman Eugene Kim expressed happiness about joining the future of the company's Rincon plant alongside Savannah Technical College through the MOU signing.
The partnership aims to prepare talented individuals who will possess a deep understanding of manufacturing and Sewon America.

In February 2023, Sewon America, a subsidiary of Korean-based Sewon Precision Industry Co., announced it will create 740 new jobs in Rincon and invest more than $300 million in a new Effingham County manufacturing facility.
The project is the largest known private investment in the City of Rincon to date.

The announcement fulfills a commitment to create jobs and investment in all four Savannah Harbor Interstate-16 Joint Development Authority (JDA) counties as a result of Hyundai’s Metaplant location in Bryan County, which is the largest economic development project in state history.
Suppliers for the Metaplant have now committed over $1.8 billion in investment, surpassing initial estimates by at least $800 million.

Effingham County Industrial Development Authority CEO and STC Board of Directors Vice Chairman Brandt Herndon expressed excitement over Sewon America and Savannah Technical College formalizing their relationship with the MOU signing.
Herndon stated certainty that Savannah Tech can provide a pipeline of quality, trained employees for Sewon’s new facility in Effingham County.
Sewon America operates one of the largest automobile body parts manufacturing plants in North America.
The company opened a manufacturing facility in LaGrange in 2008, which has since doubled in size.
Following the completion of the Rincon facility, Sewon is expected to support 1,600 jobs in Georgia.
On October 25, 2022, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground in neighboring Bryan County on its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility.
Georgia is positioned as a hub for the electric mobility industry by building on the assets that make the automotive industry successful.
Since 2020, more than 35 EV-related projects have announced more than $21 billion in investment and 26,700 jobs in Georgia.
