facts: The Polish Ministry of Economic Development and Technology extended a grant of up to USD $320 million (1.22 billion PLN) to Ascend Elements for a sustainable pCAM manufacturing facility in Poland.
The subsidy is one of the largest grants ever provided by the Republic of Poland.
The grant aligns with the European Union’s Temporary Crisis and Transition Framework (TCTF) to support Europe's shift toward a net-zero economy.
pCAM is a crucial material in the production of lithium-ion batteries used in electric vehicles and other advanced industrial applications.
Ascend Elements has pinpointed a location in Poland for its first advanced pCAM manufacturing plant in Europe.
CEO Linh Austin met with Poland’s Secretary of State Michał Jaros to formalize the grant offer.
The meeting marks a significant advancement in Ascend Elements' European expansion plans.
Pawel Pudlowski, Ph.D., Vice President of the Management Board at PAIH, stated the project is among the largest U.S. investments in Poland in recent years.

The project is expected to enhance Poland’s position within the global battery supply chain and promote regional development through technology advancements and collaborations with local educational institutions.
Ascend Elements intends to commercialize its Hydro-to-Cathode® technology at the Polish facility to produce sustainable NMC pCAM from recycled lithium-ion battery materials.
The Hydro-to-Cathode® process is more efficient and environmentally friendly than traditional methods, resulting in reduced carbon emissions and particulate matter.
A recent life cycle assessment highlighted that the Hydro-to-Cathode® process generates 49% lower CO2e emissions and 26% lower PM 2.5 emissions.
Ascend Elements aims to achieve further emission reductions by 2030 through additional decarbonization efforts.
Ascend Elements is expanding its presence in the United States alongside its Polish operations.
The company operates a major battery recycling facility in Covington, Georgia.
A new lithium recovery line at the Covington, Georgia facility is expected to produce up to 3,000 metric tons of Lithium Carbonate annually.
Ascend Elements is developing an advanced Hydro-to-Cathode® pCAM manufacturing facility in Hopkinsville, Kentucky.
The Hopkinsville, Kentucky facility is projected to become North America’s largest NMC pCAM producer by late 2026.
The Polish government grant underscores Ascend Elements’ commitment to building a sustainable and circular battery materials supply chain for European and global net-zero targets.
source_title: Poland Grants $320 Million to Support Ascend Elements’ Battery Plant - Battery-Tech Network

facts: The Biden administration is poised to loan nearly $671 million to Aspen Aerogels Inc.
The funding is intended to make a key component of electric car batteries in the election battleground state of Georgia.
The conditional financing was announced Oct. 16 by the U.S. Energy Department.
The funding will finance construction of a new manufacturing plant producing thermal barriers that help prevent battery fires.
The project is part of President Joe Biden’s push to build a domestic supply chain for electric vehicles and the advanced batteries that power them.
Jigar Shah, director of the Energy Department Loan Programs Office, stated: “We are making sure that the supply side is made here in this country.”
Jigar Shah added: “We have taken very seriously the onshoring of the entire supply chain.”
Lithium-ion battery cells can overheat and cause adjacent cells to do the same, leading to a fire.
Aspen Aerogels is based in Massachusetts.
Aspen has contracts to supply its thermal barriers to General Motors Co., Toyota Motor Corp., and other automakers.
The new plant will be located in Register, near Savannah, Georgia.
The facility is expected to supply enough thermal barriers for over 2 million electric vehicles annually.
Aspen has invested $300 million into the approximately $1 billion facility.
CEO Donald Young stated that Aspen plans to complete construction by late 2026 or early 2027.
The funding comes from the Energy Department’s Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing Loan Program.
CEO Donald Young noted that the funding was critical for the relatively small and fast-growing company.
Donald Young stated: “It’s a little hard to imagine how we’d do this without the support of the LPO program.”
Donald Young added: “Having access to this level of capital would be challenging for us.”
source_title: US to Loan EV Battery Parts Maker $671M for Georgia Plant - TT

facts: Aspen Aerogels is a mid-sized manufacturing company focused on aerogel-based insulation and thermal barrier products.
The company has between 201 and 500 employees.
Its insulation solutions include Cryogel and Pyrogel.
These insulation solutions are used by energy infrastructure operators and automakers to improve heat management and protect equipment, supporting efficiency and safety in demanding environments.
Aspen Aerogels is headquartered in Northborough, Massachusetts.
The company also manufactures at a facility in East Providence, Rhode Island.
Aspen serves the energy infrastructure sector and automotive manufacturers.
The company addresses corrosion under insulation and thermal management challenges in electrified systems.
In April 2026, an explosion occurred at the East Providence site.
The explosion led to a temporary closure of the East Providence site.
The East Providence site partially reopened following the explosion.

Right to Work states provide benefits for businesses as well as workers, causing companies to locate in Georgia.

Serena & Lily chose Georgia to grow its business and reach markets all over the world.
Development partners Chesterfield and Stonemont helped attract Serena & Lily to Effingham County.

Georgia’s spirit of collaboration and innovation has fostered impressive growth across the entire health and life sciences ecosystem.
Aspen Aerogels is investing $325 million toward a new location soon in Bulloch County, Georgia.
Aspen Aerogels’ investment will create over 250 new jobs.
Aspen Aerogels provides innovative, sustainable solutions for the electric vehicle and energy storage markets.
Aspen Aerogels employees will collaborate with Aspen’s network of world-class scientists, engineers, and additional skilled workforce to solve challenges of the changing mobility landscape.
The Development Authority of Bulloch County welcomed Aspen Aerogels to the Statesboro-Bulloch County community.

The region offers great site and logistics advantages.

facts: Aspen Aerogels, Inc. won a conditional commitment for a proposed DOE loan worth up to $670.6 million.
The loan is part of the Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing program.
The loan will finance the construction of Aspen’s second aerogel manufacturing facility in Register, Georgia.
Both the DOE and Aspen Aerogels must meet specific technical, legal, environmental, and financial conditions before finalizing the loan agreement and disbursing funds.
The Register Plant will manufacture PyroThin aerogel blankets, which are ultrathin thermal barriers designed to prevent thermal runaway in batteries.
The plant is projected to generate $1.2 billion to $1.6 billion in initial revenue.
The plant is projected to create up to 550 construction jobs.
The plant is projected to create 255 permanent positions.

Aspen President and CEO Donald R. Young stated that PyroThin can enable increased battery safety and performance, calling it a unique and differentiated product that solves an important and challenging problem.

Donald R. Young expressed excitement to further the DOE’s mission to onshore and re-shore domestic manufacturing technologies critical to meeting the federal goal of having half of all new vehicles sold in 2030 be zero-emissions vehicles.
Aspen announced preliminary results for the third quarter of 2024.
The company expects quarterly revenue of approximately $117 million.
This revenue expectation compares to a consensus estimate of $95.5 million.
The company expects a net loss of around $13 million.
The net loss includes a one-time charge of $27.5 million related to the extinguishment of its convertible note on August 19, 2024.
Aspen sees adjusted EBITDA of approximately $25 million for the quarter.
As of September 30, the company held about $113 million in cash and cash equivalents.
Aspen plans to report its third-quarter results on November 7, 2024.
ASPN shares rose 13.1% to $25.38 at the last check on Wednesday following the announcement.

facts: Don Young serves as President and CEO of Aspen Aerogels.

facts: U.S. EV demand reset lower as GM reduced EV production rates.

European EV pipeline projected $220 million linked to 2027 launches and over $450 million for 2028, across passenger and commercial vehicle platforms.
Seven European design wins secured for the Thermal Barrier segment, including Volvo Car, with additional awards expected in the year.
European EV programs incorporate battery cells from a diversified global supply base, including European, Korean, Japanese, and leading Chinese manufacturers.
Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) segment development is targeting both utility-scale external systems and modular rack-level indoor applications.

Domestic capacity in the U.S. provides policy advantages for domestic BESS projects.

A strategic review has been initiated with external advisers to assess growth strategy, capital allocation, and value creation options from a position of financial strength.
Annual fixed cash cost base was structurally reduced by approximately $75 million.
Adjusted EBITDA breakeven revenue target declines from approximately $330 million in fiscal 2024 to $175 million in fiscal 2027.
Cash and cash equivalents totaled $158.6 million at the end of fiscal year 2025.
Expected 2026 debt obligations include approximately $35 million in scheduled debt payments, including $24 million in term loan principal amortization.
Energy Industrial segment revenue is expected to grow by 20% in fiscal 2026, driven by subsea, LNG, and pent-up maintenance demand.
The company aims to scale the Energy Industrial business into a $200 million high-margin segment without incremental capital investment.
Market share in the subsea and LNG segment is extremely high; the lack of 2025 growth was due to a lack of available projects rather than lost bids.
A new North Sea pipe-in-pipe subsea project award won earlier in the year is expected to be delivered in Q3 2026.
LNG activity is positioned to roughly double versus 2025 in both project count and revenue contribution for 2026.
Pent-up demand for maintenance exists among refinery and petrochemical end users who minimized turnaround activities over the past year.
The company is adding customer-facing sales and technical service teams globally to support Energy Industrial growth.
GM production is expected to increase as downtime subsides, with EV volumes normalizing through 2026.
European OEM programs are expected to contribute approximately $10 million to $15 million of revenue in 2026.
Q1 2026 total revenue guidance is between $35 million and $40 million, with adjusted EBITDA between negative $13 million and negative $10 million.
Annualized production rate is expected to be approximately 72,000 units exiting Q4.
The company amended its MidCap credit agreement in December to enhance covenant flexibility and maintain a substantial liquidity cushion.
Net cash position is expected to expand from approximately $50 million today to over $70 million by the end of 2026.
Building materials and retrofit opportunities are being explored in Europe, targeting thermal efficiency regulations for existing buildings.
The company operates a capital-light, flexible manufacturing model that eliminates the need for new plant construction and scales using existing assets and swing capacity.

facts: Aspen Aerogels is targeting revenue of $1.6 billion and EBITDA of $400 million as it seeks to fully utilize its second aerogel manufacturing plant.

The auto industry is on track to invest a half a trillion dollars in the next five years to transition to electric vehicles.

Aspen's carbon aerogel initiative within Aspen Battery Materials is preparing to commercialize products.

Aspen's thermal barrier segment requires an annual revenue run rate of around $120 million to deliver 15% plus gross margins as production ramps up in Mexico.

Thermal barrier assembly hourly staff in Rhode Island peaked at 428 in June 2022 before decreasing to 280, while approximately 600 people are in Mexico ready for volumes.
Plant II will feature a linear process requiring little manual material handling and circular processes for key chemicals like CO2 and ethanol to reduce material costs and environmental impact.

Seven of the top eight EV OEMs are focused on high energy density, high nickel content chemistries and prismatic or pouch cell form factors, which are compatible with PyroThin cell-to-cell barriers.

Global transportation requirement updates (GT-R) focusing on thermal propagation warnings became mandatory in China, South Korea, and Japan last year, are being considered for implementation in the U.S. this year, and will come into effect in India next year.
Aspen's capital expenditures for 2022 are expected to range between $250 million and $300 million.

The company is proactively managing supply chain risks for raw materials such as xylenes, batting, and CO2, with nationwide CO2 shortages and tight labor markets posing near-term risks to throughput.

facts: Aspen Aerogels' proposed electric vehicle parts plant in Bulloch County will no longer come to fruition.
The plant was originally announced in 2022.
The project represented the single largest investment opportunity in the history of Bulloch County, with $352 million going towards it.
The proposed facility would have been 500,000 square feet.
The facility would have focused on producing aerogel thermal insulating material used to assemble batteries for electric vehicles.
An estimated 150 jobs would have been created from the project.
Aspen announced the decision to end construction on Friday, Feb. 14, 2025.
To meet long-term aerogel demand, Aspen will invest to maximize capacity at its East Providence manufacturing facility and utilize a flexible supply strategy.
Aspen has stopped construction of its planned second aerogel manufacturing facility in Statesboro, Georgia.
The company will assess options to derive value from the Statesboro assets, including relocating some equipment to upgrade and expand its existing plant.
This new path requires minimal capital.
Ricardo C. Rodriguez serves as Aspen Aerogels' Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer.
In early 2023, Aspen decided to right-time the construction of the Statesboro facility, pre-empting a reset in EV demand expectations.
Aspen subsequently ramped up its external manufacturing capacity for its Energy Industrial business.
The external manufacturing model has fully demonstrated its ability to efficiently increase aerogel supply.
Aspen is confident that a capital-light and modular capacity plan provides the most efficient path to creating value.

Aspen believes it is fully funded by a strong balance sheet, enabling it to evaluate opportunities for organic and inorganic growth, optimize its capital structure, and return capital to shareholders.

facts: Blue Bird Corporation (Nasdaq: BLBD) is the leader in electric and low-emission school buses.
Blue Bird donated an advanced Blue Bird Vision electric school bus to Peach County Schools (PCS) in Georgia.
Peach County is home to Blue Bird’s Fort Valley, Ga, manufacturing facility since the company’s founding nearly a century ago.
The local school district will utilize the advanced, zero-emission school bus for day trips, field trips, and on special routes.
Peach County Schools serves more than 4,000 students in seven schools.
The school district maintains an all-Blue Bird fleet of nearly 50 school buses.
These school buses travel nearly 4,000 miles a day to transport approximately 2,300 students to and from schools.

facts: Workers at Blue Bird Corporation's Fort Valley, Ga., facility voted on May 12, 2023, to join the United Steelworkers union (USW).
Approximately 1,400 workers at the facility are seeking a voice on workplace health and safety, work-life balance, and fair pay.
United Steelworkers International President Tom Conway welcomed the workers to the USW and congratulated them on the successful organizing effort.
Conway said, 'We're proud that Blue Bird workers chose to join our union, and we're ready to help them bargain a fair contract that accounts for their contributions to the company's success.'
USW District 9 Director Dan Flippo noted the vote represents one of the most significant recent organizing efforts by manufacturing workers in the South.

Flippo said, 'Workers at places like Blue Bird in many ways embody the future. They're the ones making the investments in our infrastructure a reality, the ones building the safer, cleaner communities for generations to come.'
Blue Bird has been approved to receive significant funding through the EPA's Clean School Bus program.
Flippo cautioned that federal investments like the EPA program require ensuring workers have a seat at the table.

Flippo added that corporations have historically viewed the South as a place to suppress wages and working conditions by preventing unionization, but USW members now have the opportunity for positive workplace changes.
Patrick Watkins, a Blue Bird worker on the volunteer organizing committee, said workers deserve fair pay, safe working conditions, and respect, and voted to take their future into their own hands.

The USW represents 850,000 workers across metals, mining, pulp and paper, rubber, chemicals, glass auto supply, energy-producing industries, healthcare, public sector, higher education, tech, and service occupations.
The successful vote at Blue Bird Corporation's Fort Valley facility is cited as an example of how unions empower employees on workplace health, safety, work-life balance, and fair pay.
source_title: Georgia Chron - Blue Bird Workers Vote to Join USW - 10226854

facts: Blue Bird Corporation reported quarterly earnings of 66 cents per share, exceeding analyst expectations of 48 cents.

facts: Bonnell Aluminum aims for complete supply chain visibility to drive strategic decisions across all departments with Oracle Fusion Analytics.
The company is a United States-based manufacturer of custom fabricated and finished aluminum extrusions in the Industrial Products sector.
Bonnell sought to connect complex data across HR, finance, and operations.
Previous disparate systems prevented management from having inventory visibility across its five manufacturing plants.
Plant managers previously had to combine onsite data using spreadsheets with a homegrown ERP reporting system.
This reliance on disparate systems led to inconsistent data and decisions across locations.
80% of Bonnell's focus is in custom manufacturing.
The company places high value on delivering products on time to customers.
Poor shipment visibility and delays caused by global material shortages made it difficult to keep up with growing customer expectations.
Michael Brown, Vice President of Information Technology at Bonnell Aluminum, stated: 'It was difficult to get a unified view of operations connecting complex data across multiple spreadsheets.'
Achieving visibility across finance, sales, and supply chain data was key to the company's mission of gaining full control over operations.
Getting this cross-departmental view was central to Bonnell choosing Oracle Fusion Cloud ERP and Fusion ERP Analytics as its preferred solution.
The company was impressed with Oracle’s continued commitment to innovation.
Bonnell decided to deploy Fusion Analytics across finance and HR departments in all locations simultaneously to improve decision-making.
Rolling out Oracle Fusion SCM Analytics is next on the list to complete the vision of full data integration across all plants.
With a single view of data across all locations, Bonnell Aluminum optimizes purchasing and makes timely inventory decisions based on what customers need.
Oracle Fusion Analytics provides all stakeholders, from plant management to key executives, with visibility into inventory, production floor, and back office operations with the click of a button.
Empowered with enhanced analysis of its Fusion Cloud data across ERP, HCM, and SCM, the company can identify problem areas in production that contribute to process inefficiencies and delayed orders.
Managers can better optimize labor across sites and direct spending based on real-time insights.

Beyond shop-floor analysis, Fusion Analytics helps Bonnell connect cross-departmental data, allowing finance and procurement teams to find savings with suppliers and negotiate better prices during sourcing.
Fusion Analytics will also help drive cultural change across the organization with a more data-driven approach.
Empowering every user with key insights is critical to improving processes and increasing collaboration across functions.
Bonnell Aluminum has over 65 years in the industry.
Its production facilities serve many of North America’s largest and most respected manufacturing companies through design solutions and unmatched extrusion capabilities and services.
source_title: Bonnell Aluminum unifies cross-departmental data with Oracle Analytics

facts: Global aluminum extrusion market valued at USD 45.9 million in 2025 and projected to reach USD 65.27 million by 2033 expanding at a CAGR of 4.5% between 2026 and 2033.
Market expansion is fueled by accelerating infrastructure modernization, electric mobility adoption, and the rising preference for lightweight, corrosion-resistant structural materials.
Top growth drivers include lightweight vehicle adoption at 28%, green building material usage at 34%, and renewable energy structure deployment at 22%.
North America market valued at USD 17 million driven by EV manufacturing.
Aluminum extruded profiles enable up to 40% weight reduction in vehicle structures compared to traditional steel components, directly improving fuel efficiency and EV range performance.
EV body structures can incorporate over 30% aluminum content, driving demand for high-strength extruded components.

Automotive OEMs are increasing the use of extruded aluminum crash structures, battery enclosures, and side-impact beams, with EV platforms now incorporating up to 35% aluminum content in structural assemblies.
Lightweight profile adoption has demonstrated vehicle mass reductions of nearly 20%, improving energy efficiency and extending driving range by measurable margins in new-generation electric vehicles.
Adoption rates in EV manufacturing facilities exceed 60% for aluminum-intensive structural components.
North America shows accelerating electrification-driven demand, where aluminum-intensive EV production increased by 22% in the past year.
Electric vehicle manufacturing facilities increased aluminum profile usage by over 25% in structural battery enclosures and crash systems.

Norsk Hydro invested USD 85 million to build a new aluminium casting line at its Henderson, Kentucky facility, adding 28,000 tonnes of annual recycled aluminium capacity targeted at supplying U.S. automotive manufacturers with sustainable extrusion inputs.

In June 2025, Constellium SE showcased low-carbon aluminium extrusion solutions featuring high-recycled-content battery enclosures and crash management systems made from over 90% post-consumer scrap as part of its £10 million CirConAl circular economy initiative.
Friction Stir Welding–compatible extrusion technology delivers 18% higher joint strength compared to conventional mechanical fastening standards.
Leading firms are achieving recycled aluminum usage levels above 50% in selected product lines to support sustainability-focused procurement policies.

Key suppliers and manufacturers in the supply chain include Constellium SE, Norsk Hydro, China Zhongwang Holdings, Hindalco Industries, Kaiser Aluminum, Gulf Extrusions, Jindal Aluminium, Bonnell Aluminum, and China Hongqiao Group.
Strategic initiatives include partnerships with EV manufacturers to develop lightweight crash management systems and facility automation upgrades delivering productivity gains of 10–15%.
Advanced billet preheating systems with induction heating reduce thermal loss by nearly 20%, improving energy efficiency and extrusion consistency for automotive component production.
Inline quenching technologies ensure controlled cooling rates, enhancing mechanical strength in 6xxx and 7xxx series alloys used in high-load applications like EV chassis systems.
source_title: Aluminum Extrusion Market Report: Dynamics, Growth Drivers & Future Outlook (2025–2033)

facts: Bonnell Aluminum is a subsidiary of Tredegar Corporation (NYSE: TG).
Bonnell Aluminum commends the Biden administration’s proclamation to strengthen the rules to qualify for trade benefits between the U.S. and Mexico.
Under the proclamation, aluminum must meet a "smelted and cast" requirement to qualify for relief from the Section 232 Tariff.
The proclamation prohibits preferential treatment for imports where the primary country of smelt, secondary country of smelt, or country of most recent cast was Belarus, China, Russia, or Iran.
This negotiated requirement recognizes the inherent unfair trade practice that occurs when U.S. trading partners process subsidized metal from countries like China and Russia.

facts: Boogook Georgia, LLC is a Korean automotive supplier known for its expertise in fluid-carrying systems for vehicles.
The company announced its entry into the United States market with the establishment of its first facility in Macon.
Boogook Georgia will undertake the redevelopment of an existing building located at 2600 Weaver Road within the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority (MBCIA) Industrial Park.
The company plans to invest $300,000 in leasehold improvements and a total capital investment of $6.8 million to support its operations in the region.
Joong Suk Oh serves as the Chief Executive Officer of Boogook Georgia, LLC.
CEO Joong Suk Oh highlighted the strategic decision to establish operations in Macon to better serve Korean automotive manufacturing partners in the local market.
The expansion positions the company to effectively cater to the Southeastern United States and contribute to enhanced regional operations.
Boogook Georgia aims to build a successful partnership with the MBCIA as it embarks on this new chapter.
The selection of Macon was motivated in part by its proximity to Hyundai Motor's Metaplant America in Savannah, a $5.5 billion facility.

Mayor Miller highlighted the area's convenient access to interstates and highways, positioning it as an ideal choice for industries seeking efficient product transportation.
Upon its opening in 2024, Boogook Georgia is projected to generate 80 full-time jobs.

The employment opportunities will provide professional roles spanning management, manufacturing, assembly, quality assurance, and delivery functions.

facts: Governor Brian Kemp and the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) announced sustained momentum for business recruitment and expansions in Fiscal Year 2024 on Thursday, August 29th, 2024.
Between July 1, 2023, and June 30, 2024, GDEcD’s Global Commerce team supported 429 facility expansions and new locations statewide.
FY2024 expansions and new locations resulted in more than $20.3 billion in investment and the commitment of 26,900 new private sector jobs.
83% of FY2024 expansions and new locations are directed to communities outside the 10-county Atlanta region, representing more than 18,300 new jobs and $12.5 billion in investment.
Metro Atlanta attracted nearly $7.8 billion in investments during FY2024 as a logistics and business hub.
Statewide, new locations accounted for $12.8 billion in investments, while expansions were responsible for 9,800 new jobs.
Governor Brian Kemp emphasized prioritizing growth in rural Georgia and investing in education, workforce development, and infrastructure.

Lt. Governor Burt Jones highlighted the necessity of workforce pipeline development through the Technical College System of Georgia, University System of Georgia, College and Career Academies, and private-public partnerships.
Speaker of the House Jon Burns noted historic investments in education, workforce development, and infrastructure alongside tax cuts and regulatory relief.
During FY2024, Governor Kemp led economic development missions to Korea and Switzerland focusing on strengthening relationships with Hyundai Motor Group and its supplier network.
In the prior year, Governor Kemp led similar economic development missions to Israel, France, and Switzerland.
Georgia attracted more than 8,100 new jobs and $5.9 billion in investments from international companies expanding or locating in the U.S. market during FY2024.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted the state's range of locations from major cities to small communities provides quality-of-life options for companies.
Georgia celebrated more than 50 years of continuous economic representation in Europe and Japan, which remain top sources of foreign direct investment.

Markets where Georgia maintains business and trade representation accounted for 29% of total new jobs and investments in FY2024.

facts: DTNA aims to increase its margins in the second half of the 2020s.
DTNA accentuates a position as the key engine of growth for parent company Daimler Truck.
The margin push will be driven by an ever greater focus on the heavy-duty vocational truck sector through Western Star, increased parts sales, reduced costs, and a decrease in capital expenditure.
Parent company Daimler Truck’s return on sales was 12.9% in 2024 and is expected to be 12% in 2025.
By 2030, DTNA expects its North American unit’s margin to reach up to 14%, which is 2 percentage points higher than the ceiling forecast at Capital Market Day in 2023.
DTNA’s margin floor for 2030 is rising to 10% from 9%.
DTNA margins came in at 9.2% in 2021 and 10.8% in 2022.
Daimler Truck’s ROS was 7.7% in 2022, 9.9% in 2023, and 8.9% in 2024.
The parent company is aiming for an overall ROS of 12% from its truck and bus units by 2030, including Mercedes-Benz Trucks and Asian truck units.
CEO John O’Leary stated DTNA has delivered consistently strong financial performance over the past years and is building on that momentum.
The strategic focus will be on increasing the rate of vocational truck market growth and optimizing the product lineup.
DTNA aims to increase its heavy-duty vocational truck sales by 60% by 2030.
DTNA is aiming for a heavy-duty vocational truck market share of more than 35% by 2030.
Western Star launched the X Series (47X, 49X and 57X) starting in 2020.
Western Star upped its market share over the first half of the 2020s, benefiting from competitors’ constrained capacity starting in 2023.
DTNA completely inverted its model at the Cleveland Truck Plant due to increased vocational orders and sales.
Vocational trucks are high consumers of parts, which drives aftermarket sales and boosts margins.
The shift is part of a companywide “Service First” program, one of five pillars of Daimler Truck’s “Stronger 2030” strategy unveiled at Capital Market Day 2025.
DTNA aims to capture a greater share of the second life cycle of parts and increase its third and fourth customer share beyond dealerships.
The Service First program will have three legs: stand-alone parts stores, direct fulfillment, and portfolio expansion.
DTNA cut costs by 185 million euros post-COVID pandemic.
In mid-2024, DTNA reduced its white-collar headcount by 200 employees.
DTNA plans to return operating efficiency to pre-COVID levels, reduce non-value-added complexity, increase automation, and improve efficiency through AI-identified strategies.
Companywide capital expenditure will peak in 2026.
Freightliner launched the fifth-generation Cascadia in October.
CEO Karin Radstrom said the parent company chose Cleveland partly because of pre-buy expectations, but market doubt and volatility increased.
CFO Eva Scherer noted the order environment is very challenging and targets set at the start of the year are becoming distant.
In May, Daimler Truck cut its 2025 North American truck sales guidance due to a weak U.S. freight market and trade policy uncertainty.
Marketwide heavy-duty Class 8 sales forecast for 2025 is 260,000 to 290,000 vehicles, down from previous expectations of 280,000-320,000 trucks.
DTNA is forecast to sell 155,000 to 175,000 trucks in 2025 across all classes, down from 180,000 to 200,000 before tariffs and reciprocal actions.
source_title: DTNA Aims to Increase Margins With Focus on Western Star - TT

facts: Daimler Truck North America (DTNA) commenced series production of the Freightliner eM2, a battery electric medium-duty truck designed for pick-up and delivery applications.
The eM2 is being manufactured at DTNA's truck manufacturing plant in Portland, Oregon.
First U.S. production units will be delivered to Ferguson, Hogan Truck Leasing, Penske Truck Leasing, PITT Ohio, Ryder System, TITAN Freight Systems, and Velocity Truck Rental & Leasing.
First Canadian units will be delivered to Brossard Leasing and Day & Ross.
The eM2 is built on the Freightliner M2 106 Plus platform.
The eM2 follows the introduction of the battery-electric Freightliner eCascadia in 2022.
DTNA claims a leadership position in the Class 8 market for the eCascadia based on Polk registration data from the beginning of the year until September 2023.
The eM2 features a proprietary, fully integrated, battery-electric Detroit ePowertrain.
The powertrain design places electric motors and a two-speed transmission directly on the drive axles to reduce energy costs per mile.
The class six single-motor model provides up to 190 continuous horsepower, a 194 kWh battery, and a typical range of 180 miles on a single charge.
The class seven dual-motor variant offers up to 255 continuous horsepower, supported by a 291 kWh battery, providing a typical range of 250 miles on a single charge.
The eM2 includes proprietary battery side impact protection and the Detroit Assurance suite of safety systems.

Standard safety features include Active Brake Assist 5 (ABA5), tailgate warning, Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) down to 0 mph, lane departure warning, intelligent high beams, automatic wipers and headlamps, and side guard assist.
The eM2 includes Detroit Connect, a connected vehicle solution that facilitates real-time battery health monitoring, charging status, and energy usage.
source_title: Daimler Truck N.A. begins production of the Freightliner eM2

facts: Daimler opted to expand its Portland footprint after considering two other sites in the greater Portland area.
Daimler Truck North America announced plans to construct a $40 million engineering facility and a $3 million training center at its Swan Island headquarters.
The expansion aims to bolster the company's electric truck manufacturing operations in Oregon.
A Daimler representative stated the decision to expand on Swan Island was made to aid in collaboration.
Construction on the engineering facility will begin in the third quarter of this year and is expected to open in 2025.
The Electric Vehicle Supply Equipment training center is expected to open in 2026, according to DTNA spokeswoman Whitney Anderson.
Daimler announced plans for a fully electric truck in 2019.
The company unveiled the eCascadia electric truck prototype in 2022, featuring a range of about 230 miles suitable for short- and medium-range shippers.
Sales of the truck had fallen short of expectations by the beginning of last year.
The company secured larger orders for the electric truck from customers including UPS and Sysco.
Company representatives told the Portland City Council last summer that the expansion project will bring 150 high-wage jobs to the area.
In August, the council granted Daimler an enterprise zone tax-break exemption and a $1.5 million grant for the training facility.
The engineering facility received a $700,000 state grant, one condition of which is maintaining the current employee headcount in Portland.
DTNA employs around 3,000 people in the Portland area.
The 110,000-square-foot engineering facility will house multiple research projects under one roof.
Research projects at the new facility will include the development of battery electric and hydrogen fueled vehicles.
In 1981, the German company purchased Freightliner, a Portland-based manufacturer of heavy duty trucks.
Freightliner spun off from Daimler Trucks AG, the world’s largest manufacturer of commercial vehicles, in 2021.
In 2016, Daimler opened a $150-million regional headquarters that received $20 million in tax incentives.
The regional headquarters has become a hub for electric vehicle research in the years since its opening.
The company operates its High Desert Proving Ground facility for new technologies in Madras.
The 249-acre proving ground features 3.5 miles of test track and 14 types of road conditions for testing various technologies including autonomous operation.
The test track is intended to help simulate a vehicle’s entire eight-year life cycle in only six months.
The test track is located in an enterprise zone, one of 74 in the state, that offers tax incentives for companies that bring jobs to an area.
Autonomous driving is thought to be a potential boon for companies facing low job retention among commercial drivers, especially long-haul commercial drivers.
Some tech companies have lately scaled back ambitions to develop driverless freight trucks.
The trucking industry in Oregon is affected by a labor shortage, though not as acutely as other industries, according to the latest Oregon Talent Assessment.
Job reskilling on technologies like route optimization and lane-assisted driving can aid in hiring, according to the assessment developed by the state in partnership with consultancy ECONorthwest.
Trucking employers report the top challenge with job reskilling is motivating employees to participate in training programs, followed by balancing training needs with current business operations.
source_title: Daimler Staying Put in Oregon With Planned $40M Swan Island Expansion – Oregon Business

facts: Workers at Daimler Truck North America facilities in six locations across North Carolina, Georgia and Tennessee voted by 96 percent on March 8 to authorize a strike when the current contract expires on April 26 at 12:01 a.m.
The company employs nearly 7,000 United Auto Workers members at facilities throughout the South.
Daimler Truck North America manufactures Freightliner trucks, heavy-duty Western Star trucks and Thomas Built Buses.
Daimler Truck is the largest manufacturer of commercial vehicles in the world, with over 100,000 employees across 35 countries.
The firm was spun off from German auto giant Daimler AG (subsequently renamed the Mercedes-Benz Group) in 2021, with Mercedes remaining the largest shareholder.
In 2023, Daimler Truck made a record €5.5 billion ($6 billion) in earnings before interest and taxes (EBIT), with the figure jumping 38 percent compared to a year earlier.

The company announced that it expected to exceed earnings forecasts for 2024 and raised its dividend by nearly 50 percent, from €1.30 to €1.90 a share, translating to approximately $1.5 billion for the company’s biggest investors.
Truck assemblers start at just $19.25 an hour at plants in North Carolina.
Workers cite insurance, benefits, cost of living, raises, and health and safety issues as motivations for the strike authorization vote.
Company executives at Ford, GM and Stellantis planned to make up for any pay increases by laying off tens of thousands of workers as part of the transition to electric vehicles.
In 2022, the UAW made an unsuccessful effort to convince Daimler workers to reopen their contract, implement changes demanded by the company, and extend the agreement by three years.
The UAW released a video highlighting low wages while pointing to plants Daimler has built in Mexico to foment anti-Mexican sentiment and American chauvinism.
Rank-and-file workers at Daimler are encouraged to appeal to fellow workers at Volvo and Mack Trucks to fight for their common interests.
source_title: Workers at Daimler Truck North America vote overwhelmingly to authorize strike  - World Socialist Web Site

facts: Daimler Trucks North America (DTNA) celebrated several electrification milestones during Earth Week in April 2021.
Vice President Kamala Harris visited the Thomas Built Buses (TBB) production plant in High Point, North Carolina.
VP Harris met with John O’Leary, president and CEO of DTNA, and TBB employees to review electric school bus benefits and commercial EV manufacturing technology.
John O’Leary stated that welcoming VP Harris was an honor and highlighted the recognition of their electric vehicles by the administration.
The visit coincided with the inauguration of Electric Island in Portland, Oregon.
Electric Island is the first public electric vehicle charging station specifically designed for commercial vehicles.
The station aligns with the blueprint of the West Coast Clean Transit Corridor Initiative (WCCTCI).
TBB’s Saf-T-Liner C2 Jouley operates tail-pipe emissions free and noise-pollution free with a 135-mile operating range.
Nearly 50 Jouley electric school buses have already been delivered to school districts across the U.S., from Alaska to Virginia.
The Freightliner Electric Innovation and Customer Experience Fleets currently include nearly 40 trucks in customer hands.
These fleet vehicles have accumulated more than 750,000 miles of real-world use.
A Freightliner eM2 and an eCascadia are touring the West Coast from Seattle to Los Angeles for customer demonstrations.
Freightliner recently opened ordering for the eCascadia and eM2 electric trucks.
Production for the eCascadia and eM2 is scheduled to begin in late 2022.
At series production start, the eCascadia will feature a 250-mile range and a maximum gross combined weight (GCW) of 82,000 lbs.
Daimler Trucks North America LLC is headquartered in Portland, Oregon.
DTNA manufactures, sells, and services commercial vehicles under the Freightliner, Western Star, Detroit, and Thomas Built Buses nameplates.
DTNA is a subsidiary of Daimler company.
source_title: Daimler Trucks North America Leads Electrification Charge During Earth Week - Thomas Built Buses

facts: Daimler Truck North America (DTNA) is expanding the use of electric trucks for logistics operations across multiple business units and regions, including North Carolina, South Carolina, Arizona, Ohio, Michigan, and Saltillo, Mexico.
DTNA started integrating Freightliner eCascadia battery electric semi-trucks into their logistics operation in the Pacific Northwest in December 2023.
The Freightliner eCascadia was launched in 2022.
DTNA began integrating operations at Daimler Truck Mexico’s truck manufacturing plant in Santiago in January 2024.
For short-haul operations, DTNA will begin using its Freightliner eM2 electric box trucks that were introduced to customers last year.
DTNA's strategy expands electrification within regional logistics networks serving its manufacturing plants and parts distribution centers (PDCs) within a 250-mile radius.
This logistics approach is strategically aligned with its supplier landscape to fully utilize truck capacity and optimize multi-stop schedules.
The strategy enables precise planning of transportation routes and charging points for DTNA's operations.
DTNA leverages existing charging infrastructure, such as “Electric Island”, the nation’s first-of-its-kind public charging station in Portland, Oregon, designed specifically for commercial vehicles.
DTNA plans to utilize future charging infrastructure, including Duke Energy’s Mount Holly Microgrid Center in the Carolinas and Michigan’s Mobility Charging Hub.
The company supports strategic partners in their electrification journey by offering Detroit eConsulting services, which include implementing depot charging at partner facilities.
Jeff Allen serves as senior vice president of operations and specialty vehicles at DTNA.

Jeff Allen stated: “Our commitment to sustainability extends across our entire value chain. As we witness the adoption of electric vehicles, such as our Freightliner eCascadia and Freightliner eM2 trucks, within our logistics network, we are not only driving sustainability but also laying the groundwork for adaptable and robust electric logistics solutions.”
Sustainability is an integral part of DTNA’s core business.
The company conducts life cycle carbon assessments for all of its activities to understand environmental impacts from production to disposal.
DTNA aims to achieve carbon neutrality for all new products and services from direct suppliers in Europe, the United States, and Japan by 2039.
As early as 2020, the Portland Truck Manufacturing Plant achieved CO₂-neutral production by reducing energy consumption and offsetting on-site emissions.
The Portland Truck Manufacturing Plant is where the Freightliner eCascadia and eM2 are built.
DTNA plans to achieve CO₂-neutral production at all of its remaining truck manufacturing plants by 2025.
DTNA is implementing sustainable supply chain measures and views suppliers as partners in its sustainability journey.
DTNA is proactively developing solutions to optimize the entire lifecycle of the batteries used in its electric trucks.
Battery lifecycle optimization strategies include remanufacturing, repurposing, and end-of-life recycling.
The integration of electric vehicles into DTNA’s logistics network exemplifies the company’s commitment to real-world sustainability initiatives.

By implementing such measures, DTNA solidifies its position as a leader in sustainable operations and demonstrates its belief that environmental responsibility is not only good for business, but essential for the future.
source_title: Daimler Truck North America Expands Electric Vehicle Use for Logistics Decarbonization

facts: C.R. England and Daimler Truck subsidiary Torc announced an autonomous trucking pilot in the United States.
Daimler Truck North America, NextEra Energy Resources, and BlackRock forged ahead with a public charging infrastructure joint venture named Greenlane.
Daimler Truck North America commemorated the production of its 750,000th vehicle at Mount Holly Truck Manufacturing.
Detroit celebrated the production of one million heavy-duty engines at its Redford, MI facility.
Thomas Built Buses celebrated the delivery of its 200th Proterra-powered electric school bus.
DTNA celebrated the production of its 800,000th vehicle at Cleveland Truck Manufacturing.
Sysco announced an intent to purchase up to 800 battery-electric Freightliner eCascadia from Daimler Truck North America to serve U.S. customers.
Daimler Truck’s independent subsidiary Torc Robotics collaborates with leading logistics companies on autonomous trucking.
Daimler Truck subsidiary Torc Robotics opened a Technology and Development Center in Stuttgart, Germany.
Detroit expanded its service training center to add EV courses.
Torc announced an advisory council to foster collaboration with existing logistics companies.
Highland Electric Fleets and Thomas Built Buses signed an agreement to make electric school buses an affordable option today.
Daimler Truck North America, NextEra Energy Resources, and BlackRock Renewable Power announced plans to accelerate public charging infrastructure for commercial vehicles across the U.S.
DTNA participated in Demand Detroit: ePowertrain.
DTNA highlighted one million real-world electric miles.
Daimler Trucks North America announced a partnership with the American Center for Mobility.
DTNA participated in Coolest Thing Made in NC finals and semi-finals.

DTNA operates an eMobility division.
DTNA includes SelecTrucks.

The facility is located at 1 Decoma Drive in Carrollton, Georgia, 30117.
Mecheal McPherson serves as a contact point for Accounts Payable and email inquiries.
Jennifer Morgan holds the role of Payroll Generalist at the company.

facts: Decostar Industries, Inc. is a manufacturer and supplier of automotive parts based in Carrollton, Ga.
The company will pay $38,500 and provide other relief to settle a religious discrimination lawsuit filed by the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC).

The EEOC charged that employee Dina Lucas Velasquez made numerous requests to be excused from Decostar's requirement that all employees work overtime hours on designated Saturdays because her religious beliefs prohibited her from working during the Sabbath.

Decostar initially approved Velasquez's request for accommodation until January 2014, when a new supervisor took over her department and repeatedly denied her ongoing request for a religious accommodation.
Decostar eventually discharged Velasquez on Oct. 27, 2014, after she refused to violate her religious beliefs.
The consent decree settling the lawsuit requires Decostar to adopt and implement a new policy for employees to request a religious accommodation for their bone fide religious beliefs.
The decree also requires that the company provide annual equal employment opportunity training to its managers.
The two-year decree further requires the company to post a notice to its employees about the lawsuit and to provide periodic reporting to EEOC about disability discrimination complaints.

Antonette Sewell, regional attorney for the agency's Atlanta District Office, stated that it is unconscionable and unlawful for employers to force members of their workforce to choose between their livelihood and their religion.
source_title: Decostar Industries to Pay $38,500 to Settle EEOC Religious Discrimination Suit | U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) - JDSupra

facts: Dekalb Tool & Die, Inc. was founded in 1962.
The company is based in Atlanta, Georgia.
It specializes in custom tool & die, metal stampings, and metal fabrications.
Product offerings include progressive dies, transfer dies, draw dies, and single-hit tooling to meet specific requirements of different industries.
The company provides CAD design services and delivers products via its own fleet of trucks.
It provides tooling services for the aerospace, defense, medical, and other industrial industries.
source_title: Dekalb Tool & Die, Inc. - Company Profile and Products | Metoree

facts: In December 1987, Nippondenso Co. Ltd. announced a $200 million plant would be built in Maryville with initial plans to hire 550 employees.
In July 1988, Mike Taira was named the first president of Nippondenso Tennessee.
In August 1989, the company shipped its first pass-through parts to Subaru from an Ellis Avenue office.
In March 1990, production began on instrument clusters at Plant 201.
In May 1990, production began on starters at Plant 101.
In June 1990, production began on alternators at Plant 101.
On October 16, 1990, Maryville operations officially grand opened, encompassing the Starter/Alternator and Instrument Clusters divisions.
In March 1991, production began on MAPS Sensors in the Electronic Products Division at Plant 202.
In May 1991, production began on A/C Amplifiers in the Electronic Products Division at Plant 202.
On May 9, 1991, the Electronic Products Division at Plant 202 grand opened.
In July 1991, Andy Kataoka became the second president of DMTN, serving until 1995.
In August 1991, production began on Engine ECUs in the Electronic Products Division at Plant 202.
In July 1992, Phase II expansion added 52,000 square feet to Plant 201 for instrument clusters.
In February 1993, Phase II expansion added 100,000 square feet to Plant 202 for electronic products.
In September 1994, the S/A Division at Plant 101 expanded by 116,000 square feet.
In November 1994, Plants 202 and 203 began a project to connect buildings, adding 126,000 square feet.
In July 1995, the company expanded into Athens with groundbreaking for the Ceramic Products Division.
Kurt Shimotsu became the third DMTN president in July 1995, serving until 1999.
In March 1996, Plant 201 reached a milestone of 5 million instrument clusters produced.
In May 1996, Plant 202 reached a milestone of 3 million Engine ECU and A/C products produced.
In June 1996, the company purchased Building 103 for future expansion, measuring 115,000 square feet.
In July 1996, Nippondenso officially changed its name to DENSO.
In August 1996, the Starter/Alternator Division expanded with a Cold Forging Project in Building 102.
In August 1996, Plant 101 reached a milestone of 10 million alternators produced.
In October 1996, Plant 101 reached a milestone of 10 million starters produced.
In January 1997, the Electronic Products Division at Plant 202 expanded to produce Micro ISO Relays.
In June 1997, the Electronic Products Division at Plant 202 expanded to produce Remote Keyless Entry systems, MAPS sensors, TAPS sensors, and Air-bag ECUs.
In September 1997, Plant 101 expanded alternator production with A-Pro technology.
In November 1997, the Athens Ceramic Products Division grand opened, producing oxygen sensors.
In December 1997, Maryville operations received ISO 14001 environmental certification.
In June 1998, Plant 201 reached a milestone of 10 million instrument clusters produced.
In June 1998, Plant 202 reached a milestone of 5 million Engine ECU and A/C ECUs produced.
In June 1998, production began on the Cold Forming Project in Building 102.
In October 1998, the Athens Fuel Injection Division grand opened.
In March 1999, Neal Ozeki became the fourth DMTN president, serving until 2004.
In August 2000, an Associate Center was dedicated as a 10th-anniversary gift.
In September 2000, a Logistics Center was established in Building 301.
In February 2001, Plant 202 reached a milestone of 30 million Micro-ISO Relays produced.
In April 2001, North American Integrated Logistics (NAIL) was established.
In April 2002, Plant 202 reached a milestone of 10 million Engine ECUs produced.
In April 2003, the Athens operations became independent as DENSO Manufacturing Athens Tennessee Inc.
In April 2004, Mack Hattori became the fifth DMTN president, serving until 2008.
In August 2005, a 15th-anniversary celebration included remodeling of company cafes.
In July 2006, North American Production Support (NAPS) started operations, including NAPC, NALC, and NATIE.
In October 2007, mass production began at the Body Division Plant 203 (Body 1 & 2).
In April 2007, Art Shimmura became the sixth DMTN president, serving until 2009.
In April 2008, the Body Division Plant 203 grand opened.
In March 2009, Plant 201 reached a milestone of 30 million instrument clusters produced.
In April 2009, Van Saka became the seventh DMTN president, serving until 2013.
In April 2009, Plant 101 reached a milestone of 30 million starters produced.
In August 2009, Plant 202 reached a milestone of 10 million Airbag ECUs produced.
In February 2010, Plant 202 reached a milestone of 15 million TAPS sensors produced.
In May 2010, the company announced plans for a future EcoPark wetland learning environment during its 20th anniversary.
In June 2010, Plant 202 reached a milestone of 20 million Engine ECUs produced.
In December 2010, Plant 101 reached a milestone of 50 million alternators produced.
In October 2011, the Machinery & Tools Division consolidated into the North American Production Promotion Center (NA-PPC) in Building 103.
In December 2011, EcoPark and Keep Blount Beautiful’s EcoCenter outdoor pavilion were dedicated as a partnership providing environmental lessons to elementary school students.
In July 2012, DNJP named DMTN’s Jack Helmboldt as the first American on its corporate board.
In April 2012, Plant 101 expanded production with the new Stop-start Starter product.
In April 2013, DMTN purchased Building 105, formerly the Key Safety Building.
In June 2013, Jack Helmboldt became the first American president and CEO of DMTN, serving from 2013 onward.
In March 2014, Plant 202 expanded production with the new EHV Inverter product and sent its first shipment.
In June 2014, DENSO Corp. named DMTN President & CEO Jack Helmboldt as a corporate executive director.
In September 2014, the company announced the DENSO Family Health Center as a 25th-anniversary gift.
In May 2015, the DENSO Family Health Center held its grand opening.
On August 25, 2015, DENSO Tennessee announced a $400 million investment at DMTN to create 500 new jobs in Blount County.
In July 2016, DENSO broke ground on an 186,528-square-foot warehouse as part of the $400 million expansion expected to create 500 new jobs.
In August 2017, DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee unveiled a new “Crafting the Core” brand.
On October 6, 2017, DENSO announced a $1 billion investment in a plant to build parts for hybrid and electric cars, creating 1,000 jobs.
In October 2017, the company cut the ribbon on the 186,528-square-foot consolidated warehouse.
source_title: The DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee Journey: 1987-2017 | News | thedailytimes.com

facts: DENSO celebrated the opening of its new thermal assembly plant and warehouse facility in Urbandale, Iowa this week.
The new facility is named DENSO Manufacturing Michigan Iowa Plant (DMIA).
The new facility represents a $500,000 investment.
The facility creates seven new jobs.
The company expects to expand to 20 employees within two years.
Production is expected to nearly double within two years.
The new Urbandale facility assembles cooling modules for the agricultural and construction industries.
The plant handles pass-through products, such as radiator assemblies and cooling modules for the construction, agriculture and electric power industries.
The plant is localizing products that were previously assembled in the United Kingdom for U.S. customers.
This latest announcement is part of DENSO’s recent expansion initiative to invest nearly $2 billion in North America.
More than $750 million dollars of the investment is allocated to the United States.
The initiative aims to strengthen and expand product development and manufacturing capabilities across the region.
DENSO executives and local city officials celebrated DMIA’s grand opening yesterday in a ribbon cutting ceremony.
Urbandale Mayor Bob Andeweg attended the celebration as a guest.
DENSO International America President and CEO Howard Sugi attended the celebration as a guest.
DENSO Manufacturing Michigan President John Kumagai attended the celebration as a guest.
DENSO Marston President David Hanson from the U.K. attended the celebration as a guest.
DMIA Plant Manager Paolo Vercelli attended the celebration as a guest.
Howard Sugi stated that while this is an extension of existing thermal operations in Michigan and the U.K., it is very much an Iowa facility.
DENSO has been in Iowa since 1974, when it opened its first facility in Cedar Falls to support heavy-duty, off-highway engine management and others.
Howard Sugi added that they are excited about opportunities for the new site and DENSO’s heavy-duty business in Iowa in the years ahead.

Paolo Vercelli stated that the opportunity to put down roots and grow could not be possible without the strong support of the Greater Des Moines Partnership, the city of Urbandale and the Urbandale Development association.
Vercelli expressed gratitude for the warm welcome and assistance received from the Urbandale Chamber of Commerce.
DENSO’s motto is “Protecting lives, preserving the planet and preparing a bright future for generations to come.”
Vercelli stated that DENSO looks forward to sharing this bright future in Urbandale.
source_title: DENSO Manufacturing Michigan Iowa Plant Celebrates Opening Of Urbandale Facility – UnderhoodService

facts: DENSO is ramping-up for the production of stop/start starters at its DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee (DMTN) plant in Maryville, TN.
The new starters are due to hit U.S. roads later this year.
The new starters will help improve fuel efficiency by cutting the engine when the vehicle is at a stop or in other idle traffic situations.
The new production line will result in approximately 75 positions over the next two years, DENSO says.
Consumers will see more vehicles equipped with stop/start technology as U.S. automakers look to meet upcoming fuel requirements.
While already seen in Japanese and European markets, stop/start technology on internal combustion engines is newer here in the U.S.
Stop/start systems stop the engine when the vehicle is idling at a stop light or in other traffic situations.
The technology helps reduce fuel consumption and CO2 emissions.
Depending on automaker specifications, the technology can help improve fuel efficiency by approximately 3 to 5 percent.
DENSO says it has future stop/start system technology that will bump fuel efficiency improvements up to more than 7 percent depending on the automaker’s overall systems approach.
source_title: DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee Ramps Up Production for Stop/Start Starters

facts: Global automotive supplier DENSO is investing nearly $1 billion in North America over the next four years.
The investment will result in more than 2,000 jobs across North America.
More than $750 million of the investment will be directed to the United States alone.
The U.S. investment will create more than 1,200 jobs.
DENSO aims to better support its North American customers and expand new business areas through this investment.
The investment will focus on localizing products, many of which will help automakers meet upcoming fuel requirements.
Terry Helgesen, senior vice president of Industry Relations at DENSO International America, Inc., stated that DENSO announced it would drastically localize products in the regions where it operates.
Helgesen noted that DENSO is not only making products in North America but also localizing critical tooling.
In the U.S., DENSO plans to invest in Michigan, Tennessee, Iowa, California, and North Carolina.

The U.S. investment includes an increase in research and development, expansion of existing production lines, creation of new production lines, and opening a new assembly and warehouse facility for Heavy Duty customers.
DENSO is investing in manufacturing more of its own equipment and dies in North America.
A building at DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee in Maryville, Tenn., has been dedicated for making proprietary machinery, serving as the regional headquarters for manufacturing machinery and dies.
DENSO plans to manufacture all of its critical manufacturing equipment in North America in the future.
The company has sourced several millions of dollars of business to American machine shops, which will positively impact the community and create additional jobs.
Currently, DENSO employs more than 14,000 people at 28 consolidated companies and affiliates in North America.
Of these North American entities, 26 are manufacturing facilities located in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico.
In the U.S. alone, DENSO currently employs more than 10,000 people across California, Michigan, North Carolina, South Carolina, Tennessee, Kentucky, Georgia, Iowa, Ohio, and Arkansas.
source_title: DENSO to Invest Nearly $1 Billion in North America Over Next Four Years | Automotive World

facts: The Gainesville-Hall County Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA) is among the top 50 fastest growing metros in the U.S.
Total population is over 220,000 with a daytime population in Gainesville exceeding 150,000 people.
Business and industry make long-term investments in Gainesville-Hall County because of its stability, skilled workforce, visionary leadership and quality of life.
The region is home to more than 330 manufacturing and processing concerns.
There are 66 locations of international companies representing 19 countries.
Since 2020, 77 new and expanded businesses have announced more than 4,700 jobs and $2.0 billion in new capital investment.

Allied industries such as automotive suppliers, plastics, consumer goods, food processing and life sciences locate in Gainesville-Hall County to develop, manufacture and distribute products around the world.
HALLmark 2025-2030 is a five-year economic development plan that makes recruiting technology businesses and industries a major focal point.
Workforce development is a high priority to support existing businesses as they expand next-generation technology applications.
Workforce development encourages growth in complementary businesses of life science companies, telecommunications, technology manufacturers, software developers, and tech support industries.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Dongwon Autopart Technology plans to invest $30 million in a new production facility in Emanuel County.

Dongwon Autopart Technology was established in 1971.
The company supplies components such as doorframes, seat frames and battery frames to automotive original equipment manufacturers.
The company supports more than 500 direct jobs in the Southeast, including at facilities in Hogansville, Ga., which will remain operational.
Dongwon Autopart Technology has been in the United States since 2003.
Construction on the new manufacturing facility will be located at the Highway 297 Industrial Park near Swainsboro.
The company will be hiring for positions such as management, system leads and production personnel, with hiring expected to begin in 2026.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia’s thriving automotive industry is creating new opportunities for Georgians statewide and expressed excitement for the project's impact on Emanuel County.

facts: Dongwon Autopart Technology is investing $30 million in Emanuel County.
The company is setting up a new facility in Emanuel County to serve Hyundai.
Its Meriwether County factory has supplied Kia Motors in West Point since that plant’s opening in 2009.
Dongwon Autopart makes seat, door and battery frames.
The company was the first tenant in the Meriwether Industrial Park, which has since attracted other Korean investors.
At one point, its operation employed more than 300 people.
Combined, Dongwon employs more than 500 across the Southeast U.S.
The company entered the U.S. market in 2003.
Heather Hollstein is the human resources manager at Dongwon Autopart Technology.
Heather Hollstein stated, “We are proud to be part of growing the state’s automotive industry – then and now.”
The company has set its sights on Swainsboro, just north of Interstate 16 and east of Dublin.
The company will hire 200 people at the Hwy 297 Industrial Park.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson praised Toombs County for preparing the Toombs Corporate Center as a top-tier GRAD (Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development) site in anticipation of wooing such projects.
Pat Wilson wrote on LinkedIn, “Projects like this do not happen by chance. Economic Development is intentional.”
Toombs attracted an $11.2 million investment plan from Shinsung Petrochemical, a Hyundai supplier also augmenting an existing footprint in the South.

Emanuel is the sixth largest county in Georgia by land area and has about 22,000 residents.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Dongwon Autopart Technology plans to invest $30 million in a new production facility in Emanuel County.
The automotive project is anticipated to create 200 new jobs over the next several years.
Dongwon Autopart Technology was established in 1971 and supplies components such as doorframes, seat frames, and battery frames to automotive original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).
The company currently supports more than 500 direct jobs in the Southeast, including at facilities in Hogansville, Georgia, which will remain operational.
Dongwon Autopart Technology has operated in the United States since 2003.
Construction on the new manufacturing facility will be located at the Hwy 297 Industrial Park near Swainsboro.
The company will hire for positions including management, system leads, and production personnel.
Hiring is expected to begin in 2026.

Representative Butch Parrish, Chairman of the House Rules Committee, highlighted state commitment to bringing high-quality jobs to rural communities and praised Emanuel County’s collaboration with state partners.
Emanuel County Development Authority Chairman Will Donaldson welcomed the company and noted the success of local economic development investments.

facts: Elan Technology’s manufacturing facility was constructed in 1997.
The facility has a 90,000 sq.ft. footprint located on 22 acres.
The facility is based in Midway, GA.
Since 1998, the company has been registered under the QS-9000 quality standard.
The company has transitioned its quality system to ISO 9001:2015.
Elan Technology is one of the largest technical glass manufacturers in the U.S.
The company produces glass preforms from hundreds of proprietary electronic glass compositions.
The glass preforms are commonly matched for glass-to-metal hermetic seals.
Glass materials are also offered as milled or spray dried powders.
Products are supplied to industries including the medical, defense, aerospace, lighting, automotive, and micro-electronics fields.
The company provides engineering support to assist customers with material selection and preform design.
Elan Technology has the capability to develop custom glass material compositions for specific customer applications.
As a leading ceramic manufacturer with nearly 80 years of experience, the company offers materials such as alumina ceramic, steatite, zirconia, and cordierite.
Ceramic materials are used for wear-resistant parts, insulators, and ceramic inserts for industrial applications.
The company provides full engineering support for material selection and component design for ceramics.
Elan Technology developed a special glass ceramic composite material adopted by customers as a replacement for traditional ceramics in certain applications.
The Elan 46 ceramic glass material eliminates the need for brazing and lowers manufacturing costs while providing proven performance.
The Elan 46 glass has a coefficient of thermal expansion from 110 to 220 (0°-300° × 10^-7 cm/cm/°C).
The Elan 46 glass can withstand metallizing or brazing up to 700°C.
The company provides contract spray drying services for applications such as catalysts, pigments, detergents, and ceramics.
The company processes millions of pounds of material annually through its spray drying services.
Experienced engineers work with customers to optimize the spray drying process for highest product quality.
Spray drying services support both small developmental projects and large volume production.
Storage, mixing, packaging, and atomization options are offered to customize processes to meet requirements.
The company offers full-service rotary calciner toll processing services utilizing highly-effective thermal processing treatments.
Toll processing services include calcination, thermal desorption, and drying for commonly calcined materials like catalysts, clay, dolomite, silica, and more.
Elan Technology maintains a complete precise machine shop to support tool and die fabrication for all of its presses.
The company's machining experts provide expert machining services to other industries.
Machining capabilities cover most materials, including all tool steels, aluminum, ceramic, carbide, and plastic.
Origin Glass is a division of Elan Technology that produces high-quality borosilicate glass for artistic applications.
Origin Glass offers a wide range of brilliant colors used by hobbyists and professional artists.
Boro Stix products are available from distributors located throughout North America and around the globe.
source_title: Elan Technology - Ceramic Manufacturers Glass Manufacturers

facts: Evco Plastics recently purchased a new 40,000-sq-ft facility in Calhoun, GA.
The new facility is located near the company’s CAL 1 plant and is designated as CAL 2.
CAL 2 adds 40,000 square feet to Evco’s footprint, bringing its total to more than one million square feet across 10 plants globally.
The new facility includes a 22,000-sq-ft white room.
The new facility includes a 2,100-sq-ft liquid silicone rubber (LSR) production area.
The facility is designed to meet demand from medical and commercial lighting customers.
Kate Bashir, Director of Strategy, stated the expansion was timed after assessing customer needs and industry developments.
CAL 2 will help meet customers’ high expectations and long-term production goals.
The new facility has room for 20 injection molding machines up to 500 tons.
There will be a separate room for new LSR machines.
The facility is equipped with two bridge cranes.
The facility is equipped with four material silos.
The facility includes a 725-sq-ft metrology lab.
CAL 2 will be a food-grade compliant facility.
The facility will feature highly automated lights-out production.
The inclusion of a white room allows for additional medical molding opportunities, representing a new capability for Evco.
The facility is scheduled to be fully operational in the fall of 2021.
Evco also is currently adding 13,000 square feet to its AMP facility in DeForest, WI to make room for more presses and expand on-site storage of raw materials and finished goods.
Evco was founded in 1964.
Evco has more than 1,700 employees.
Evco operates 235 injection molding machines ranging from 28 to 3,500 tons.
Gary Fortier, Director of US Operations, stated that reaching over one million square feet is a huge milestone for the company.
Gary Fortier stated the new space allows Evco to create more opportunities to meet ongoing customer and industry requirements.
source_title: Evco Plastics’ Footprint Surpasses One Million Square Foot Mark

facts: EVCO Plastics and Kistler have formed a partnership for zero-defect production in injection molding.
Manufacturers can exploit opportunities for optimization and added-value manufacturing by instrumenting a mold with cavity pressure sensors and process monitoring equipment.
EVCO Plastics is a globally recognized leader in injection molding and many additional technologies.
The family-run business was founded in Wisconsin in 1964.
EVCO Plastics has a workforce of over 1,800 employees spread across eleven facilities in the US, China, and Mexico.
Anna Bartz serves as the Director of Communications at EVCO Plastics.
Anna Bartz states that together with customers and partners, the company takes plastics processing to the next level.
Following some recent growth, EVCO Plastics now operates more than 260 molding machines.
The company offers state-of-the-art technology, close cooperation, tight tolerances, and high quality demand in injection molding.
EVCO Plastics provides technologies and services across the entire value chain, including automation, packaging, labelling, and more.
Precision Molding is one of EVCO's four main divisions.
The division's products and solutions cover diverse industries ranging from agriculture and powersports to packaging and consumer goods.
EVCO Plastics is involved in just about everything except automotive parts and implantable devices.
Medical technology accounts for roughly 20 percent of EVCO's annual sales.
Two EVCO sites are equipped with an ISO Class 8 Clean Room.
The clean room locations are the headquarters in Deforest, Wisconsin, and the Chinese production facility in Dongguan.

facts: Fictiv has created a strategic partnership with EVCO Plastics to deliver end-to-end injection molding supply chain solutions for U.S. OEMs.
The alliance combines Fictiv’s digitally connected global supply chain with EVCO’s extensive manufacturing capabilities.
EVCO Plastics operates over 1 million square feet of manufacturing space.
EVCO Plastics utilizes hundreds of injection molding machines, including presses as large as 4,400 tons.
The partnership leverages deep expertise in design for manufacturability (DFM), AI, human-powered materials data and insights, program management, and global logistics.
The collaboration aims to mitigate tariff concerns and deliver a highly resilient, fully managed supply chain to OEMs across multiple industries.
Mexico’s manufacturing ecosystem is described as emerging, formative, and fragmented, particularly for tooling and specialized secondary processes.
Fictiv streamlines these complexities to allow OEMs to improve material sourcing and lower BOM costs.
Fictiv GlobalFlex integrates tooling inserts produced in China with next-generation manufacturing facilities in the U.S. and Mexico.
This synergy enhances gross margins, accelerates production timelines, and elevates customer experiences.
The collaboration supports the entire product lifecycle, encompassing 3D printing, injection molding, assembly, testing, and packaging.
Advanced manufacturing capabilities include overmolding, gas-assist molding, in-mold decorating, and liquid silicone rubber (LSR) production.
The partnership holds ISO 9001:2015, ISO 13485, IATF 16949, and FDA compliance certifications to ensure quality and reliability.
Dave Evans, Co-Founder and CEO of Fictiv, stated the partnership simplifies global manufacturing supply chains by reducing the overall number of suppliers and management complexity.
Carlos Gonzales, head of EVCO Plastics de Mexico, noted that Fictiv handles complex workflow and supply chain challenges while EVCO focuses on manufacturing excellence.
The collaboration aims to provide scalable, tailored solutions with a shared commitment to innovation, quality, and sustainability in the manufacturing sector.
Fictiv operates four global manufacturing centers located in India, Mexico, China, and the U.S.
To date, Fictiv has delivered more than 35 million commercial and prototype parts for early-stage companies and large enterprises.
EVCO Plastics was founded in 1964.
EVCO Plastics employs over 2,100 people globally.
EVCO Plastics operates 270 injection molding machines ranging from 28 to 4,400 tons.
source_title: Fictiv & EVCO Partner to Tariff-Proof Injection Molding for U.S. OEMs

facts: Metals distributor Ryerson Holding Corp. (NYSE: RYI), Chicago, IL, has acquired Fanello Industries.
Fanello Industries is a metal service company based in Lavonia, GA.
Fanello supplies blanking, stamping, laser cutting, bending, and machining metal solutions to industries in the Southeast.
The company has 150 employees.
The company has annual revenue of $20 million.
