entity_name: Hyundai-LG Energy Solution Battery Cell Manufacturing Joint Venture
canonical_name: Hyundai-LG Energy Solution Georgia EV Battery Joint Venture
facts: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution (LGES) intend to establish a $7.084 billion (US$4.3b) battery cell manufacturing joint venture in Georgia, US.
The joint venture will be located where Hyundai’s “Metaplant America” is being built.
Battery production at the new plant is expected to start at the end of 2025.
The facility will have a 30GWh annual production capacity.
The capacity will be capable of supporting the production of 300,000 EVs.
This will be the second battery manufacturing joint venture for Hyundai and LGES.
Hyundai states the aim is to continue strengthening ties with their long-time partnership in electrification efforts.
Hyundai Motor Company president and chief executive Jaehoon Chang and LGES chief executive Youngsoo Kwon signed a memorandum of understanding in Seoul on May 26.
The MoU aims to produce EV batteries in the US and further accelerate Hyundai’s electrification efforts in North America.
Hyundai Motor Group president Jaehoon Chang stated that Hyundai Motor Group is focusing on its electrification efforts to secure a leadership position in the global auto industry.

Chang added that they will create a strong foundation to lead the global EV transition through establishing a new EV battery cell plant with LG Energy Solution, a leading global battery producer and long-time partner.
LGES chief executive Youngsoo Kwon stated that LGES will commit its best efforts to offering the ultimate sustainable energy solutions to customers.
Construction of the new battery plant is due to start in the second half of this year.
Hyundai Mobis will assemble battery packs using cells from the plant.
The assembled packs will be supplied to the group’s US manufacturing facilities for production of Hyundai, Kia and Genesis EV models.
This supply chain setup allows the group to respond quickly to soaring US EV demand.
With this joint venture, LGES will have seven battery plants operating or under construction in the US.
LGES is concentrating most of its resources in the US to expand production capacity.
Hyundai and LGES have long been partners in electrification, having previously worked on EV battery supplies for vehicles including the Elantra Hybrid, Kona Electric, and Ioniq 6.
The Hyundai Elantra Hybrid, an LPi hybrid vehicle introduced in 2009, was the group’s first electrified model.
In 2021, Hyundai and LGES started construction of an Indonesia battery cell joint venture set to begin production in the first half of 2024.
source_title: Hyundai and LG plan new EV battery plant – EVs & Beyond

facts: Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp will meet high-level executives from Hyundai Motor, LG Energy Solution (LGES), SK On and CJ Foodville during his three-day visit to Korea this week.
He will express gratitude for their investments in Georgia.
The visit is an apparent attempt to address negative public sentiment stemming from the mass detention of Korean workers in early September at a construction site in the state.
Kemp is scheduled to join interviews with Korean-language newspapers but does not plan to meet any government officials.
Kemp will arrive in Korea on Thursday morning and leave Saturday morning.
This is his third visit to Korea since his inauguration in 2019.
The trip was initially arranged to celebrate the 40th anniversary of Georgia's economic development office in Seoul.
A weeklong detention of more than 300 Korean workers sparked calls for Kemp to offer an apology during his visit.
The detentions followed a major immigration raid on Sept. 4 at the construction site of a plant jointly operated by Hyundai Motor and LGES.
Kemp will meet Hyundai Motor Group Vice Chair Chang Jae-hoon on Thursday afternoon to discuss the aftermath of the raid and plant construction progress.
He will have dinner with Chang Jae-hoon on Friday.
LGES CEO Kim Dong-myung and SK On CEO Lee Seok-hee will reportedly meet the governor on Friday.
Kemp stated: “Georgia has enjoyed a strong and friendly relationship with the Republic of Korea for 40 years now.”
Kemp added: “This visit reflects our commitment to deepening those ties even further, through the creation of opportunities that benefit both our people and our economies.”
Kemp's office said it has worked with the federal government on addressing visa issues and advocating for clarity in these areas.
Rep. Kim Joon-hyung of the minor opposition Rebuilding Korea Party said Kemp must offer a sincere apology, provide incentives to Korean investors and ensure safety for Korean workers.
Rep. Kim Joon-hyung is a former chancellor of the Korea National Diplomatic Academy.
Industry officials expect the governor will apologize or express regret over the immigration raid incident.
Skepticism remains that there is little a state government can do in response to federal government policies.
Georgia repeatedly emphasized the importance of Korean investment in the state before the visit.
Kemp remarked that the raid highlighted the need for U.S. visa system reform.
Rep. Lee Un-ju of the ruling Democratic Party of Korea led a delegation of lawmakers to Texas earlier this week.

Kemp made an unexpected appearance at the Texas venue and promised to submit a white paper to the White House to ensure Korean investors and engineers return home safely after finishing their work in Georgia.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development reaffirmed its long-standing relationship with Korea at the National Day of the Republic of Korea event on Oct. 8 at the Consulate General of the Republic of Korea in Atlanta.

The department posted on social media: “As Governor Brian Kemp prepares for his mission to Korea later this month, Georgia stands committed to strengthening cultural and business ties, especially during this festive time of Chuseok," Together, we look forward to a future of continued collaboration and success.”
Montana Gov. Greg Gianforte visited Korea earlier this week to meet with industry leaders and government officials, including Prime Minister Kim Min-seok and Trade Minister Yeo Han-koo.
Tennessee Gov. Bill Lee will also travel to Korea this week to meet representatives from Hyosung, LG Chem and SK On.
This occurs amid intensifying competition among U.S. states to retain Korean investors after the Georgia immigration raid.
source_title: Georgia governor to thank Hyundai, LG, SK, CJ during 3-day visit - The Korea Times

facts: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution officially kicked off construction of their joint electric vehicle battery plant in Indonesia.
A groundbreaking ceremony was held at the construction site for the battery plant in Karawang New Industry City near Jakarta and Hyundai's existing car factory.
Indonesian President Joko Widodo and several ministers and government officials attended the ceremony in person.
Hyundai Motor Group Chairman Chung Euisun and LGES CEO Kim Jong-hyun attended the ceremony online due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
Chairman Chung stated that Hyundai Motor Group is focusing on securing future competitiveness in the EV sector to become a global leader.
Chung noted that as part of this effort, they decided to construct a joint Indonesia battery cell plant with LG Energy Solution.
CEO Kim described the day as meaningful, stating that Indonesia's first next-generation battery cell plant will open the battery industry's future.
Kim added that the joint plant gets them one step closer to establishing the world's first EV-integrated supply chain system.
The two companies aim to complete construction of the plant in the first half of 2023.
Mass production is scheduled to start in the first half of 2024.
The plant will have an annual capacity of 10 gigawatts, enough to power approximately 150,000 EVs.
The plant could be expanded to increase production capacity by 30 gigawatts when needed to meet a rise in EV demand.
The plant is aimed at securing a stable supply of EV batteries for Hyundai Motor Co. and Kia Corp. starting in 2024.
The batteries produced at the plant will embed LGES's latest battery technologies that maximize the properties of nickel, cobalt and manganese.
These batteries will reduce chemical uncertainties by adding in high-performance aluminum.
Hyundai Motor Group aims to expand its EV lineup to more than 23 models by 2025.
Hyundai Motor Group plans to sell 1 million EVs by 2025.
The batteries will be installed as part of Hyundai and Kia's E-GMP platform.
In the first half of the year, the two companies agreed to construct a joint battery plant, with each taking half of the factory stakes.
In July, the two companies signed a cooperation deal with the Indonesian government to inject $1.1 billion for the plant.
The following month, the two companies established the joint venture.
The two companies believe the Indonesian battery plant will be able to meet the drastically rising global demand for EVs.

In 2019, the Indonesian government started to exempt luxury taxes for EVs and provide incentives pushing to foster the EV industry and supply chain, ranging from raw materials mining to manufacturing EVs and batteries.
source_title: Hyundai, LGES start construction of Indonesia joint battery plant - The Korea Times

facts: German company IMS Gear is set to construct its first plant on European soil outside of Germany in Osijek.
The future IMS Gear Croatia production facility will be constructed in the Nemetin Business Zone in Osijek.
This marks the first large-scale production site for the company on European soil outside its home country of Germany.

IMS Gear is a globally recognised industrial company that specialises in the development and production of extremely high-quality drive solutions for the automotive industry, as well as other branches of industry.
Osijek Mayor Ivan Radić visited the headquarters and production facilities of IMS Gear to assess the city's economic growth and investment potential.
The City of Osijek sold a 33,442 square metre plot of land to IMS Gear Croatia in 2023 for 1.1 million euros.
During a tour of the company's headquarters, Mayor Radić noted that IMS Gear employs just over three thousand people in Germany, Asia, and America.
According to Mayor Ivan Radić, every fourth car produced worldwide contains a part manufactured by IMS Gear.
Mayor Radić stated that since taking office in 2021, Osijek has seen an increase of 4,500 registered employees and expects this trend to continue with the new facility's arrival.
The project aims to create employment and encourage local inhabitants to remain living and working in Croatia, addressing the region's history of emigration and economic disparity.

facts: JAC Products Inc. was founded in 1967.
The company is a leading designer and manufacturer of automotive roof racks and cargo management systems.
JAC supplies its products to the world’s top car manufacturers.
Customers include Ford, Toyota, Honda, General Motors, Subaru, Renault, Hyundai, Chrysler, Kia Motors, Nissan, and Peugeot.
The company has offices around the world and manufacturing facilities in Michigan, Georgia, and Portugal.
JAC is a pacesetter with extruded aluminum designs.
Engineering and manufacturing capabilities include plastic injection molding and steel rollform for base components.
Jeff Cavalier serves as Engineering Manager for JAC Products.
Cavalier is part of the manufacturing staff at JAC’s Franklin, Georgia facility.
The Franklin, Georgia facility was originally set up as an aluminum extrusion plant in the mid-1960s.
JAC took over the Franklin facility 20 years ago to help supply aluminum-based products to the automotive industry.
Extrusion lines at the Franklin facility produce millions of pounds per year of 6XXX series aluminum for their products.
JAC has approximately 1500 employees in North America.
Competitors include the WKW Automotive Group, headquartered in Germany; DURA Automotive, with more than 21,000 employees in 15 countries; and the MINTH Group from China.
Cavalier states that compared to competitors, JAC is relatively small but turns products around faster for engineering and delivers better products due to its technologies.
Shaping and forming roof rack rails requires complex stack-up and tolerances defined by tenths and hundredths of a millimeter.
Manufacturing practices are challenged by multiple areas of responsibility, making the effect of deliverables very important.
Customer satisfaction is prioritized through quality parts delivered on time from new products to continuous improvement activities.
Cargo Management Systems for the trucking industry include side rails, header bars, bed dividers, load bars, lockable storage bins, and other storage options.
Cavalier considers JAC’s innovations with racking systems for work trucks to be top-notch and world-class.
The company is broadening its expertise on complex step rails.
JAC supports the aluminum substructure within the automotive industry as automakers switch from steel to aluminum to make cars lighter.
This shift requires JAC to make changes to the aluminum alloys provided to car manufacturers, who are also moving rapidly to robotic welding of parts rather than bolting them together.
JAC’s development group in Michigan stays closely involved with car manufacturers’ engineers.
Product Development constantly tries to understand market movement two, five, and ten years out to keep the company in conversations with OEMs.
Technology integration extends into cameras and LED lighting systems that can be incorporated into roof racks for 360-degree vehicle views and security systems.
Coatings and sealings on JAC’s products must withstand years of harsh car wash detergents and rough weather, requiring extensive testing of various finishes.
Cavalier notes that proximity to manufacturers in Michigan and the southeast, where more auto plants are being built, is helpful for meeting delivery needs.
The Franklin location helps minimize freight costs for customers.

Future plans include expanding territorial spread and product lines, growing diversity of products for OEMs, maintaining worldwide relationships, tapping into China, Central and South America markets, and diversifying beyond roof racks.
At a glance, JAC Products Inc. is headquartered in Saline, Michigan, and manufactures roof racks and cargo management systems.

facts: Global automotive parts supplier Kyungshin America Corporation (KAC) has selected Savannah Gateway Industrial Hub (SGIH) for its new operation.
SGIH is a 2,600-acre multimodal logistics park located near Georgia’s Port of Savannah.
KAC is a division of the South Korean Kyungshin Corporation.
KAC’s new operation will import and distribute automotive components to Hyundai Motor Group’s new $7.5B electric vehicle plant in Savannah.
KAC’s new operation will also distribute automotive components to the growing automotive manufacturing network throughout the southeastern United States.
Broe Real Estate Group Senior Vice President Sean Fitzsimmons stated that SGIH provides tremendous geographic and multi-modal benefits for park tenants.
KAC’s new facility is strategically positioned with the Port of Savannah and the new Hyundai Meta Plan plant, both within 15 miles.
This positioning creates exceptional supply chain efficiency and connectivity to global markets while leveraging Effingham County’s strong workforce and education system.
KAC produces wire harnesses for automotive companies and is a certified Tier One supplier to Hyundai.
KAC’s new facility will be 136,000 SF.
The facility is expected to open in February.
The move supports Kyungshin’s continued North American production expansion.
Effingham County Industrial Development Authority CEO Brandt Herndon welcomed KAC to Georgia and highlighted the dynamic partnerships that will serve Hyundai’s new Georgia production plant.
SGIH is a public-private partnership between the Effingham County Industrial Development Authority (ECIDA), Broe Real Estate Group, and OmniTRAX.
SGIH is located 12 miles from the Port of Savannah.
SGIH provides dual rail access to the Class I rail networks of CSX and Norfolk Southern.

Broe Real Estate Group acquires, develops and manages commercial real estate assets, including office and industrial properties, medical office buildings and multi-family communities across the country.
The Broe Group has a 50-year history of value-add real estate investing in Colorado and across the United States.

OmniTRAX is one of North America’s largest and fastest growing private railroad and transportation management companies, providing transportation and supply chain management services to railroads and ports.

The Broe Group was founded in 1972 as a private, multi-billion-dollar investment group with diversified holdings in real estate, rail, infrastructure, energy, agriculture, water, healthcare and technology throughout 37 North American states and provinces.

facts: French tire giant Michelin acquired Lehigh Technologies in late 2017.
Lehigh Technologies reincarnates end-of-life tires into a specialty material called micronized rubber powder (MRP).
MRP is already used by more than 60 companies, including seven of the world’s 10 biggest tire makers.
MRP serves as a feedstock for high-performance tires sold by Michelin.
MRP is sold to more than 42 plants in 15 countries.
Lehigh operates as an independent subsidiary of Michelin.
Lehigh's second manufacturing plant in Murillo del Fruto, Spain, was set to come online in July.
Lehigh's process uses liquid nitrogen to turn rubber scraps stripped of metals and fibers cold enough to be pulverized into a fine composite.
Lehigh sources material from tire recyclers or through closed-loop arrangements with tire manufacturers.
The MRP material helps save energy in the tire production process.
The new European facility has an initial annual capacity of 10,000 metric tons.
The European facility is located on the same site as a local tire recycler to ensure a steady stream of feedstock.
Lehigh’s first plant is located in Tucker, Georgia.
The Tucker, Georgia plant is the world’s MRP manufacturing site with an annual capacity of 140 million pounds.
Lehigh’s technologies are becoming increasingly integral to Michelin’s shift toward a closed-loop production cycle that uses fewer raw or virgin inputs.
Lehigh CEO Alan Barton stated the company has been looking for ways to have a significantly higher presence in recycling of materials than in the past, which fits their strategic direction.
As of April, MRP has been used in more than 500 million new tires and retreads.
Roughly 1 billion tires are headed for disposal annually worldwide, including more than 300 million in the United States.
Traditionally, the biggest non-fuel markets for tire scraps have included civil engineering projects like embankment fill or as an additive for asphalt.
Lehigh is actively developing new applications for MRP beyond markets traditionally associated with scrap rubber.
MRP is finding its way into plastics, consumer goods, coatings, sealants, construction materials, and car parts.
The durability and longevity of MRP make it appropriate for use in the foam that’s part of car headrests.
The carpet and flooring industry represents a potentially large market for MRP as part of underlays.
Mohawk actively embraces the use of recycled materials, repurposing 42 million pounds of old tires into welcome mats last year.
Testing and introducing new material takes significant time, often requiring a half-year in the laboratory to produce samples followed by several months for a production trial before test marketing.
source_title: How Michelin is finding a second life for tires | Trellis

Lehigh Technologies appointed Adel Halasa, a polymer scientist formerly with Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co., to its advisory board.
The appointments coincide with Lehigh’s growth and its continued drive to adopt micronized rubber powder (MRP) material in high-value applications.
Lehigh Technologies is expanding its presence in Asia and Europe.
CEO Alan Barton stated that MacMaster's experience in key market segments will help deliver the compelling value proposition of MRP to the tire industry.
MacMaster began his career with Yokohama in 1981 in sales and rose through multiple commercial roles to become vice president of the company’s North American Commercial Tire Division.
In 2001, MacMaster was named executive vice president at Yokohama.
As a new advisory board member, Halasa will provide guidance to Lehigh’s technical team.
Halasa served as a research and development fellow at Goodyear from 1983 to 2009.
Halasa has since served as an adjunct professor at the University of Akron.
Halasa has been listed on more than 300 patents.
Halasa was recognized in 1997 with the Charles Goodyear Medal for his work developing anionic solution polymerization of styrene-butadiene rubber (SBR).

Tom Rosenmayer, vice president of technology at Lehigh Technologies, stated the company's technology roadmap for the tire industry focuses on driving higher loading levels of MRP and developing MRPs with chemical reactivity.
Halasa’s depth of experience in polymers and structure-property relationships will help Lehigh develop new products faster, which is a key part of the company's growth strategy.
source_title: Lehigh Technologies Adds Industry Vets To Its Ranks - AftermarketNews

facts: Michelin announced that it has acquired Lehigh Technologies, a specialty materials company.
Lehigh Technologies uses patented cryogenic turbo mill technology to transform rubber from end-of-life tires and industrial goods into materials for new tires and other products.
The process reduces the amount of raw materials initially needed, such as elastomers and fillers from oil- and rubber-based sources.

Pete Selleck, chairman and president of Michelin North America, stated that using high-technology recycled materials helps achieve safer and more sustainable mobility while consuming less finite natural resources.

Christophe Rahier, senior vice president of strategic planning materials for the Michelin Group, noted the acquisition demonstrates Michelin’s strategic intent to extend its materials expertise beyond tires and foster advanced rubber recycled materials in tire and non-tire industries.
Lehigh Technologies is based in Tucker, Ga., near Atlanta.
The firm employs about 100 people.
Lehigh produces highly engineered, versatile raw materials called micronized rubber powder (MRP).
MRP is a low-cost, high-performance, sustainable material that substitutes for oil- and rubber-based materials used in manufacturing tires, plastics, asphalt, and construction materials.
Lehigh's customers include some of the largest tire companies in the world, as well as companies in construction materials, asphalt modification, and other markets.

Alan Barton, CEO of Lehigh Technologies, stated the acquisition provides an opportunity to continue growth with Michelin's backing to expand capabilities, execute a global growth strategy, and pursue new market opportunities.
Michelin completed the acquisition on Oct. 13.
source_title: Michelin acquires specialty materials company, Lehigh Technologies

facts: Lehigh Technologies, Inc. is located in Tucker, Ga.

The company produces a brand of vulcanized elastomer particles known as "PolyDyne" or "PD," which includes cured rubber particles, recycled rubber particles, ground tire rubber (GTR), and micronized rubber powder (MRP).
PD80 conforms to conventional 80 mesh standards, while PD40 conforms to conventional 40 mesh standards.
The company's micronized rubber powders can be produced via a cryogenic grinding system described by U.S. Pat. No. 7,445,170 and an impact mill described by U.S. Pat. No. 7,861,958.

Reclaimed elastomer materials like GTR and MRP are used as fillers in elastomer compositions (e.g., tire tread compounds for vehicle tires) and plastics compositions (e.g., as fillers for polyolefins).
Rubber powders, whether GTR or MRP, are typically significantly less expensive than virgin rubber or plastic, which tends to reduce the overall manufacturing cost of the composition.
Reincorporating reclaimed materials into elastomer and plastic compositions reduces landfill waste and results in a more environmentally-friendly product.
The use of recycled GTR or MRP provides a strategic supply chain hedge against petroleum-based supply chain price and supply volatility.
Functionalized reclaimed elastomer material can be obtained from Levgum, Ltd., which has a principal place of business in Kanot, Israel.
Devulcanization processes modify the chemistry of vulcanized particles by breaking chemical crosslinks formed during vulcanization through chemical, thermal, and/or mechanical treatment.
One functionalization method subjects particles to shear at temperatures less than 100° C. using a two-roll mill with a 0.1 mm nip, where rolls rotate at different speeds (e.g., 25 rpm and 12.5 rpm).
The reclaimed elastomer material used in experiments was made from vehicle tire buffings, butadiene rubber (BR), natural rubber (NR), or blends of NR and BR or SBR and NR.
Experimental SBR/BR elastomer blend formulations were tested with 10% functionalized material at 10, 20, 40, and 80 mesh sizes.
Samples comprising 10% 80 mesh functionalized MRP demonstrated an average tensile strength of 17.3 MPa and elongation of 613%.
Samples comprising 10% 40 mesh functionalized MRP demonstrated an average tensile strength of 16.3 MPa and elongation of 599%.

Testing showed that elastomer compositions comprising functionalized MRP of a particular mesh size exhibit tensile strength approximately 6% greater than compositions comprising functionalized powder one mesh size larger.
Samples comprising functionalized MRP of smaller mesh size demonstrate superior elongation characteristics compared to samples with larger mesh functionalized GTR.

The relative quality of tested samples was determined by taking the product of tensile strength and elongation for the respective sample and dividing that quantity by the product of the tensile strength and elongation for the control material.
Functionalized 80 mesh MRP approaches a relative quality of approximately 0.9, making it suitable as a substitute in high-performance tire tread applications.
An elastomer compound comprising these materials includes uncured elastomer, functionalized reclaimed elastomer material (between 3% and 30% by weight), a filler, and a crosslinking agent.
The article of manufacture comprising the cross-linked elastomer compound can be a tire.
source_title:
US9175155B2 - Methods of functionalizing reclaimed elastomer material and compositions comprising the same 
        - Google Patents

facts: Linde + Wiemann is expanding its manufacturing facility in Lavonia, Georgia.
Wet soil remediation and stabilization had to be completed in expansion areas before the project began.
Roads and parking were added earlier as part of site preparation.
Soil conditions were exacerbated by the time of year and weather.
Choate utilized soil cement to keep the project on schedule given the soil challenges.
The team leveraged existing experience and trade partner expertise to develop a plan for using soil cement.
The plant remained fully operational during the expansion and production realignment.
Choate created a complex series of digital equipment scanning and modeling of both existing and new production components to maintain operations.
This digital strategy allowed the team to reevaluate production flow prior to construction and plan for future manufacturing layout efficiencies without impacting operations.
A proprietary piece of stamping equipment, specially made in Europe, was the impetus for the expansion.
The machine’s shipping, arrival, and installation were crucial to the project’s successful completion.
Installation required several equipment pits to be dug, some as deep as 10 feet.
Design modifications made while the machine was crossing the Atlantic required additional pit construction.
The equipment arrived in Lavonia in seven shipping containers, with one weighing 38,000 pounds.
The entire facility space was dried-in prior to the stamping machine's arrival due to its centrality to the project.
An interior overhead crane delivery post dry-in required an unorthodox installation method.
Working with a crane supplier, small panels were removed in the roof to allow outside mobile cranes to access and lift the massive gantry system onto interior crane rails.
This safe crane installation method kept the project on schedule and allowed for future maintenance access through roof curbs.
Choate’s VDC team laser scanned, measured, and generated as-builts for existing elements to be moved to minimize downtime and ensure accuracy in equipment reinstallation.
The team guaranteed the accuracy, squareness, and straightness of crane rail beams within a 1/8-inch tolerance across the entire 320 feet of travel.
The team relied on laser scanning, measuring, and as-builting to ensure precision for the beams and rails.
Choate’s collaborative approach involves clients, suppliers, vendors, and partners.
Choate continually seeks to innovate processes with creative solutions and strategies that highlight thorough and quality work by its employee-owners.
source_title: Linde + Wiemann Manufacturing Expansion | Projects | Choate Construction

facts: Magna International is investing over $470 million to expand its operations across Ontario, Canada.

facts: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) reached multiple administrative settlement agreements with various companies across the Southeast region in 2024.

These agreements resolved allegations that the companies violated Tier II reporting requirements of the Emergency Planning and Community Right-to-Know Act (EPCRA) Section 312 and Toxic Release Inventory (TRI) reporting requirements of EPCRA Section 313.
Nidec Elesys Americas Corporation is located in Suwanee, Georgia.
Nidec Elesys manufactures automotive safety electronic controls including occupant detection, radar technology, and the safety system ECU.

On July 8, 2024, a CAFO was filed regarding Nidec Elesys Americas Corporation for alleged violations of Section 313 of EPCRA for failing to submit required Form R for lead compounds processed at the facility during calendar years 2021 and 2022.
The CAFO requires Nidec Elesys Americas Corporation to pay a penalty of $34,730.
Purafil Inc. is located in Doraville, Georgia.
Purafil manufactures air-filtration chemicals and equipment to remove gaseous contaminants and particulates from airports, schools, office buildings, museums, and government buildings.
On July 8, 2024, a CAFO was filed regarding Purafil Inc. for alleged violations of Sections 312 and 313 of EPCRA for failure to timely file Tier II and Form R reports.
The CAFO requires Purafil Inc. to pay a civil penalty of $82,700.
Hussmann Corporation is located in Suwanee, Georgia.
Hussmann provides merchandising cases, refrigeration systems, aftermarket services, and other items for food retailers.

On July 16, 2024, a CAFO was filed regarding Hussmann Corporation for alleged violations of Section 313 of EPCRA for failing to submit required Form R for chromium, copper, and manganese processed at the facility during calendar year 2021.
The CAFO requires Hussmann Corporation to pay a penalty of $74,123.
OFS Fitel, LLC is located in Norcross, Georgia.
OFS Fitel manufactures and provides optical fiber, optical fiber cable, connectivity, FTTX, and specialty photonics solutions.
On August 19, 2024, a CAFO was filed regarding OFS Fitel, LLC for alleged violations of Section 312 of EPCRA for failing to submit required Tier II forms.

The CAFO also resolves alleged violations of Section 313 of EPCRA for OFS Fitel for failing to submit required Form R for chlorine and hydrofluoric acid otherwise used at the facility during calendar year 2020.
The CAFO requires OFS Fitel, LLC to pay a penalty of $38,852.

Keriema Newman, director of the Enforcement and Compliance Assurance Division in EPA's Southeast region, stated: 'Citizens have a right to know about dangerous and toxic substances in their communities.'
Newman noted that homes, schools, businesses, fire stations, and churches are near these facilities, some of which are near population centers.

The EPA action underscores the importance of companies understanding the hazards of using hazardous substances like compounds containing lead and undertaking safe handling practices to protect public safety.

The purpose of submitting Tier II forms is to provide state, tribal, local officials, and the public with specific information about potential hazards, including locations and amounts of hazardous chemicals present at facilities during the previous calendar year.
Under federal TRI regulations, companies that use certain listed toxic chemicals must report their releases each year to the EPA using Form R (EPA Form 9350-1).
This information serves as the basis for the TRI, a collection of data readily reviewed by communities, government, and industry.
Public availability of this information incentivizes companies to reduce harmful chemical use and improve environmental performance.
TRI reporting informs surrounding communities about toxic chemicals that could potentially harm public health and the environment, offering transparency.
The Carlstar Group is located in Clinton, Tennessee.
On April 15, 2024, a CAFO was filed regarding The Carlstar Group for alleged violations of Section 313 of EPCRA for failure to report certain chemicals on Form R.
The CAFO requires The Carlstar Group to pay a civil penalty of $66,392.
Karnak-South, Inc. is located in Fort Lauderdale, Florida.

On June 11, 2024, a CAFO was filed regarding Karnak-South, Inc. for alleged violations of Section 313 of EPCRA for failing to submit required Form R for 1,2,4-Trimethylbenzene processed during calendar years 2020 and 2021.
The CAFO requires Karnak-South, Inc. to pay a penalty of $12,639.
Englander Enterprises (d.b.a. EEI Manufacturing Services) is located in Clearwater, Florida.

On July 8, 2024, a CAFO was filed regarding Englander Enterprises for alleged violations of Section 313 of EPCRA for failing to submit required Form R for lead processed during calendar years 2021 and 2022.
The CAFO requires Englander Enterprises to pay a penalty of $38,425.
Koch Foods of Gadsden is located in Gadsden, Alabama.
On August 21, 2024, a CAFO was filed regarding Koch Foods of Gadsden for alleged violations of Sections 312 EPCRA for failing to timely file its Tier II form.
The CAFO requires Koch Foods of Gadsden to pay a civil penalty of $1,500.
Loparex LLC is located in Eden, North Carolina.
On August 21, 2024, a CAFO was filed regarding Loparex LLC for alleged violations of Section 313 of EPCRA for failing to timely file its Form R.
The CAFO requires Loparex LLC to pay a civil penalty of $33,710.
Refresco Beverages US Inc. is located in Lakeland, Florida.

On August 26, 2024, a CAFO was filed regarding Refresco Beverages US Inc. for alleged violations of Section 313 of EPCRA for failing to submit required Form R for nitric acid used during calendar year 2020.
The CAFO requires Refresco Beverages US Inc. to pay a penalty of $25,110.
Bharat Forge PMT Technologie is located in Surgoinsville, Tennessee.
On August 26, 2024, a CAFO was filed regarding Bharat Forge PMT Technologie for alleged violations of Sections 312 and 313 of EPCRA for failing to timely file its Tier II and Form R.
The CAFO requires Bharat Forge PMT Technologie to pay a civil penalty of $39,445.

facts: Has world’s top market share of both brushless DC motors and motors for automotive power steering, according to the Company’s data.

In the fiscal year that ended in March 2025, automotive products accounted for 25.5% of sales.

facts: Nifco Inc. is a global leader in manufacturing plastic fasteners and precision plastic components, significantly impacting various industries, especially automotive.
The company adopted plastic as a lightweight, cost-effective alternative to metal fasteners during Japan's industrial growth.
Established on February 13, 1967, as Nippon Industrial Fastener Corporation in Japan.
Initial aim was to offer ingenious plastic solutions for labor and cost savings in industrial applications.
Operates in 17 countries, boasting over 35 production plants and 4 R&D centers.
Projected consolidated net sales of approximately ¥353 billion for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2025.
Co-founded on February 13, 1967, by Nichiei Bussan Co., Ltd. (60% stake) and Illinois Tool Works (ITW) (40% stake).
Initial capital for the company was 48 million yen.
Toshiaki Ogasawara's international education and connections facilitated the partnership with ITW.
Founders recognized a substantial opportunity in Japan's rapidly expanding automotive and home electronics sectors focused on enhancing manufacturing efficiency and reducing production costs.
Core problem addressed was the inherent inefficiencies and material limitations associated with traditional metal fasteners.
Initial business model revolved around the manufacturing and sale of industrial plastic fasteners, with the 'Plasti Rivet' being their first significant product.
Strategic shift to plastic alternatives promised considerable benefits including labor savings, reduced manufacturing expenses, and lighter end products.
Name NIFCO is an acronym derived from its original designation, 'Nippon Industrial Fastener Corporation'.
Rapidly developed product line beyond basic fasteners to include mini-dampers and push latches for the automotive sector.
Secured technical assistance contracts with major automotive manufacturers General Motors, Chrysler, and Ford Motor Company in 1972.
Achieved sales of 1 billion yen within its first five years and nearly 5 billion yen by its tenth year.
Consolidated sales surpassed 10 billion yen by 1980.
Listed on the Second Section of the Tokyo Stock Exchange in 1979 and moved to the First Section in 1984.
Global footprint expanded in 1983 with the co-founding of Nifco Taiwan Corporation.
Ventures into South Korea in 1985 and establishment of Nifco America Corporation in the United States in 1987 to serve North American automotive clients.
Entered European market in 1990 with acquisition of Elta Plastics Ltd. (now Nifco U.K. Ltd.) and established a subsidiary in Germany in 2006.
Diversified business in 1996 by acquiring Simmons Co., Ltd., a bedding and furniture manufacturer.
Received the 10th Plastics Award for product excellence in 1978.
Experienced first operating loss since founding in 2009 due to the Great Financial Crisis.
Transferred operations of Nifco Germany GmbH and Nifco KTW America Corporation in fiscal year ending March 31, 2024, to address hardship in German OEM business.
Maintained highest rating of 'AAA' in MSCI ESG Ratings as of May 2024.
Holds over 3,600 intellectual property rights.
First globally to develop the mini-damper for smooth motion in consumer electronics and automotive interiors.
Pioneered push latches and specialized fuel tank components meeting environmental standards.
Produces complex engine transmission parts.
Collaborations with Toyota, Honda, and Nissan ensure integration into global vehicle manufacturing lines.
Actively adapting to market demands by expanding product line to include components for electric vehicles (EVs).
R&D expenditures increased by approximately 25% from 2020 to 2023.
Prioritizing lightweight materials and sustainable manufacturing processes, promoting a circular economy.
Renewed corporate philosophy, logo, and slogan in 2018.
Completed Initial Public Offering on the Tokyo Stock Exchange in 2014.

Plans announced to open self-delivering solar power plants to advance carbon neutrality in 2025.
Forecasts consolidated net sales of ¥348,000 million and operating profit of ¥49,500 million for fiscal year ending March 31, 2026.
Plans aggressive business investments considering alliances, M&A, and Corporate Venture Capital.
Committed to integrating ESG management with business operations, addressing climate change by 2030.

canonical_name: The 2026 State of Automotive Investment Report by Global Location Strategies (GLS)
facts: The automotive industry contributes more than $1.2 trillion annually to the U.S. economy, representing up to 5% of GDP.
The number of announced automotive projects has declined since the pandemic, but capital expenditures remain consistent.
The size and complexity of each automotive investment have surged, concentrating billions of dollars in fewer, mission-critical commitments.
$1B-plus automotive projects now account for 43% of all automotive capital spending, up from just 18% a decade ago.
The average capital expenditure per project increased 24% (inflation-adjusted) between 2015 and 2024.
Investments are becoming fewer but significantly larger, increasing balance-sheet exposure per decision.
Employment per project is rising more slowly than capital, signaling capital deepening and advanced manufacturing rather than labor expansion.
Supplier investment has grown more selective, often waiting for clear OEM execution before committing capital.

Investment is increasingly concentrated in next-generation vehicle platforms, including battery-electric vehicles, hybrid systems, advanced battery manufacturing, and large-scale retooling of existing internal-combustion-engine plants.
Next-generation facilities require far more to be built out on day one compared to traditional auto plants.
These facilities must consider their ability to adjust strategies based on changing market demands and policy considerations.
Next-generation facilities require significantly more power and water, specialized space for high-tech operations such as battery production, and room for suppliers to operate nearby.
Bringing next-generation facilities online requires more coordination to staff, permit, and bring online, which increases the risk of delays.
These projects are multi-billion-dollar, ground-up commitments that must work at scale from the start.
Traditional cost advantages alone, such as incentives, no longer offset execution risk for automakers.
Automakers increasingly screen locations based on their ability to execute reliably rather than just incentive packages.
Large automotive facilities now compete directly with data centers, semiconductor fabrication plants, and other energy-intensive users for power and utilities.
Interconnection delays and grid constraints are increasingly stalling automotive projects nationwide.
Regions may show adequate labor totals on paper but lack the specialized commissioning and technical talent required during construction and ramp-up, the period when capital exposure is highest.
Multi-agency approvals, environmental reviews, and utility coordination frequently disrupt construction timelines, extending ramp-up and delaying returns.
The U.S. faces decentralized infrastructure, localized labor markets, and layered permitting that place more delivery risk on individual projects.
The European Union sees smaller, phased investments that limit exposure per project but face regulatory complexity and slower demand growth.
China utilizes coordinated infrastructure and clustered ecosystems to absorb execution risk at the system level.
The U.S. remains attractive for large, platform-defining investments only where delivery certainty can be demonstrated early.
Automotive leaders should now focus on excluding non-viable locations earlier in the site selection process.
Validating power and utility delivery before shortlisting sites is now a critical step.
Stress-testing workforce ramp assumptions is recommended for new automotive manufacturing playbooks.
Mapping permitting dependencies end-to-end is necessary to avoid delays.
Phasing mega-site infrastructure is advised to preserve flexibility.
Structuring incentives as risk-sharing tools rather than upfront discounts is the new standard.

Global Location Strategies President and CEO Didi Caldwell stated that automotive site selection has shifted from cost optimization and incentives to execution certainty, noting that locations must deliver power, workforce, and permitting on schedule for projects to move forward.
source_title: Billion-Dollar Automotive Mega-Projects Account for Nearly Half of Global Capital Expenditures | Manufacturing.net

facts: Nisshinbo Automotive Manufacturing, Inc. (NAMI) and Business Developement Center (MI) are part of the Nisshinbo Group, consisting of 59 subsidiaries and 40 affiliates worldwide.
The Nisshinbo Group was established in 1907 when it began producing yarns and threads.
In the 1940s, Nisshinbo was called upon to manufacture automotive friction materials and developed a process using woven fabric embedded with friction material.
Nisshinbo produces brakes and friction materials that serve diverse applications in the global transportation market.
The company maintains a library of more than one thousand different recipes, each specific to customer applications.
North American operations consist of dedicated facilities serving OEM transportation manufacturers in the NAFTA market.
The Business Development Center (BDC) is located in Sterling Heights, Michigan and provides team management, engineering, prototyping, and manufacturing capabilities.
NAMI is a state-of-the-art facility located in Covington, Georgia.
Together, these facilities produce high-quality friction materials and logistical support to serve transportation manufacturers.
The NAMI facility occupies 297,500 square feet on 72 acres.
The NAMI facility employs 307 people.
Production at the NAMI facility began in March 1999.
The Business Development Center (BDC) occupies 54,000 square feet on 4.2 acres.
The BDC employs 14 people.
Production at the BDC facility began in October 1997.

facts: Gov. Nathan Deal announced that Nisshinbo Automotive Manufacturing, Inc. (NAMI) will create 100 jobs and invest $72 million in an expansion of its Covington facility.
New jobs at the facility will include positions in production and administration.
The facility expansion is located in Newton County.
NAMI has maintained a presence in Newton County for more than two decades.

Along with its business development center in Sterling Heights, Michigan, NAMI’s Covington facility produces high-quality friction materials for automotive manufacturers and develops environmentally friendly, copper-free brake pads.
NAMI CEO Shigeru Matsuki announced the expansion together with the Governor’s Office and the Newton County Industrial Development Authority.
NAMI has been based in Covington for the last 20 years and hopes to grow there for the next 20 years and beyond.
Select Newton Vice President of Economic Development David Bernd noted that more than $70 million in Foreign Direct Investment is being created for 100 well-paying advanced manufacturing careers.
Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) Project Manager for International Investment Joseph Huntemann represented the Global Commerce Division in partnership with Select Newton.
Japanese-affiliated companies currently operate more than 500 facilities in Georgia that employ approximately 30,000 Georgians.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that NAMI’s success in Georgia is a combination of strong international relationships and a pro-business environment.
Gov. Nathan Deal emphasized that Georgia provides the business climate, skilled workforce and thriving automotive sector necessary for manufacturers like NAMI to enjoy long-term growth.

Gov. Nathan Deal noted that with this facility expansion in Newton County, NAMI will continue to benefit from close proximity to top vehicle manufacturers while also reaching customers across the nation and around the world with Georgia’s logistics infrastructure.

Gov. Nathan Deal highlighted that international economic missions are delivering tangible results, as NAMI’s commitment directly reflects the strength of Georgia’s manufacturing environment for global industry leaders.
Gov. Nathan Deal expressed appreciation for NAMI’s investment over the last 20 years and look forward to the future growth of this partnership.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that they had the opportunity to meet with NAMI leadership last year in Japan and are very excited to see the company expand in Newton County.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson expressed looking forward to NAMI’s continued success.
source_title: Nisshinbo Automotive Manufacturing, Inc. to create 100 jobs in Newton County - On Common Ground News - 24/7 local news

facts: OTR Wheel Engineering is headquartered at 6 Riverside Industrial Park, Rome, Georgia, 30161.
The company designs and manufactures its own wheels and tires, mounts them, and provides just-in-time delivery to OEMs, resellers, wholesale distributors, and aftermarket customers worldwide.
OTR supplies tire and wheel combinations for applications ranging from lawn and garden equipment to giant mining trucks.
The product line includes specialized off-the-road tires, wheels, mounted tire and wheel assemblies, and rubber tracks.
The HP-009 tire is a 6-ply option featuring an open tread pattern suitable for loose terrain and mud cleaning, with tread shoulders that protect rims and sidewalls from trail objects.
The WIZZARD tires are 6-ply options with an aggressive tread pattern designed for loose terrain, though they produce extra noise and wear on hardpack surfaces.
The Tomahawk tire features a very aggressive tread pattern intended for deep mud conditions, also resulting in increased noise on hard-packed surfaces.
All OTR tires are available with the exclusive TyrLyner system, a permanent, self-healing polyurethane rubber adhered to the inside of the tire casing.
The TyrLyner system seals cuts and punctures from objects up to 3/8-inch in diameter, maintains pressure, and provides sidewall and tread area protection by immediately fusing penetration holes shut.
OTR is a sponsor of the AMSOIL Grand National Cross Country Series (an AMA National Championship) and the Mid-East Racing Series.

OTR sponsors multiple PRO Class UTV racing teams and drivers, including Collin Truett, Mitch Truett, Alex Truett (Truett Racing), Jonathon Powers, Brent Vanderford, Donnie Harrison, Robert Boynton, and Jamey McCoy.
Racers using OTR tires have achieved 19 wins and 38 podium finishes, often switching between HP-009 and WIZZARD tires based on track conditions or running a mix for maximum traction.
source_title: OTR WHEEL ENGINEERING - ATVILLUSTRATED

facts: OTR is headquartered in Rome, Georgia, located 1-½ hours northwest of Atlanta.
OTR operates a global footprint comprising 32 facilities across the U.S., Canada, Mexico, England, Germany, Czech Republic, China, Thailand, and Sri Lanka.
OTR provides manufacturing, painting, and assembly; tire mounting, sequencing, and warehousing; subassembly; and foam- and tire-fill services.
OTR supports localization initiatives for OEMs by maintaining stocks for distribution partners to ensure coast-to-coast aftermarket coverage.
OTR operates 16 U.S. locations totaling nearly 2 million square feet that support well-known OEMs and distributors.
OTR's business model enables it to quickly open new facilities with dedicated teams and capacity.
In the U.S., OTR manufactures and paints wheels, rims, and components in four locations: Rome, Georgia; Omaha, Nebraska; Jacksonville, Florida; and Quincy, Illinois.
OTR provides foam- and tire-fill services in multiple U.S. facilities to service OEMs, distributors, and dealers nationwide.
Several U.S. locations provide subassembly services as a leveraged resource for OEMs, resulting in cost savings.

Other U.S. facility locations include Chambersburg, Pennsylvania; Timmonsville and Clinton, South Carolina; Jacksonville, Florida (2); Louisville, Mississippi; Mansfield, Texas; Hesston and McCook, Kansas; Phoenix, Arizona; Denver, Colorado; and Oconomowoc, Wisconsin.
OTR received Excel Industries Gold Supplier Awards in 2017, 2018, and 2019.
OTR was the Honda Challenging Spirit Award Winner for Support and Performance in 2019.
OTR won the Kubota Kaizen Community Award in 2013 and 2014.
OTR Canada is headquartered in Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario, with additional locations in Guelph and Sudbury, Ontario; Saskatoon, Saskatchewan; and Edmonton, Alberta.
All five Canadian facilities produce multi-piece rims and components for earthmoving, surface and underground mining, construction, and specialty vehicle applications.
The Sudbury facility recently installed a state-of-the-art bake oven system with temperature and humidity control and a new priming process to optimize adhesion and durability.
OTR supports a large aftermarket in Canada through partnerships with major distributors and dealers, including a wheel inspection and recertification program.
OTR Europe operates facilities in Derby and Suffolk, England; Sande, Germany; and the Czech Republic, providing tire mounting, sequencing, just-in-time supply, and warehousing.
The Suffolk facility manufactures and repairs wheels primarily for agricultural vehicles and provides foam-filling for OEM customers.
OTR's Czech Republic facility opened in 2013 to service Europe, Russia, and eastern European countries.
OTR's team speaks multiple languages and has experience setting up programs across Europe for OEM and aftermarket customers.

OTR's China facility is located in Wuxi, two hours northwest of Shanghai, and manufactures multi-piece steel rims, hubs, and components for surface and underground mining, material handling, heavy-duty, and specialty vehicles.
Wheels produced at the China facility range from 10″ to 63″, using HSLA grade steel with seamless forgings for critical sections.

The China facility utilizes sub-arc welding (SAW) and precision CNC machining to ensure tight tolerances, operates four paint lines with advanced pretreatment and primer systems, and conducts in-process ultrasonic and magnetic particle testing for weld integrity.
OTR's Thailand facility in Chon Buri manufactures steel wheels primarily for agricultural OEMs using automated construction for high-volume production with stringent quality and cycle time targets.
The Thailand facility provides value-added services including tire mounting and sequencing.

OTR's Sri Lanka facility in Ekala manufactures steel rims, wheels, accessories for off-the-road applications (22"-49"), and steel bands using cutting-edge technology and multiple paint lines with advanced 5-stage wash, wet, and powder coat systems compliant with OEM standards.
The Sri Lanka facility provides tire mounting and just-in-time supply services.
Rigorous testing at all production stages measures attributes such as tensile strength, dimensional accuracy, and compliance with ISO 9001:2015 standards and OEM specifications.
source_title: OTR Engineered Solutions - Locations

facts: PACCAR is a global technology leader in the design, manufacture and customer support of premium light-, medium- and heavy-duty trucks under the Kenworth, Peterbilt and DAF nameplates.
PACCAR also designs and manufactures advanced diesel engines, provides financial services, information technology, and distributes truck parts related to its principal business.
Kenworth Truck Company builds premium commercial vehicles for sale in the U.S., Canada, Mexico and Australia and for export throughout the world.
Peterbilt Motors designs, manufactures and distributes premium commercial vehicles in the US and Canada.
DAF Trucks manufactures trucks in the Netherlands, Belgium, Brasil and the United Kingdom for sale throughout Western and Eastern Europe, and export to Asia, Africa, North and South America.
PACCAR delivers its products and services to customers worldwide through an extensive dealer network of 2,200 locations.
PACCAR Global sells the company’s products in more than 100 countries and is expanding its dealer network in Asia and throughout the world.
Approximately half of PACCAR’s revenues and profits are generated outside the United States.
PACCAR Parts operates a network of parts distribution centers offering aftermarket support to Kenworth, Peterbilt and DAF dealers and customers around the world.
Aftermarket support includes customer call centers operating 24 hours a day throughout the year and technologically advanced systems to enhance inventory control and expedite order processing.

PACCAR Financial Services provides finance, lease and insurance services to dealers and customers in 24 countries including a portfolio of more than 180,000 trucks and trailers and total assets in excess of $13 billion.
The group includes PACCAR Leasing, a major full-service truck leasing company in North America, with a fleet of 38,000 vehicles.

Environmental responsibility is one of PACCAR’s core values, with the company regularly developing new programs to help protect and preserve the environment and establishing ambitious goals to further reduce emissions and enhance fuel efficiency in its truck models.
PACCAR is headquartered in Bellevue, Washington, USA.
The PACCAR Foundation grants millions of dollars each year for education, social services and the arts.
source_title: Get to Know PACCAR | PACCAR Inc

facts: PAI Industries is a Georgia-based manufacturer of parts for the heavy-duty truck industry.
The company expanded its in-house heat treatment capabilities with a NANO vacuum furnace system.
PAI partnered with vacuum furnace manufacturer ECM USA to transition its production line from outsourced heat treatment to in-house vacuum technology.
The new setup performs low pressure carburizing and through hardening around the clock.
The upgraded system will increase capacity and expedite production at PAI’s 112,000 square foot manufacturing facility.
The NANO furnace system is comprised of one module with three stacked heating cells.
The heating cells can be extended to six heating cells corresponding to two heating modules.
The system includes gas quenching cells for quenching parts.
The carburizing cells are stacked to minimize the installation footprint.
The furnace system integrates directly into the production line to reduce cycle time.
The integration simplifies the flow between machining and heat treatment.

facts: Rivian Automotive Inc., purveyor of the all-electric R1T and R1S, will receive $1.5 billion in incentives from state and local governments to build a new manufacturing facility in Georgia.
The state is offering a comprehensive incentive package that includes tax breaks to become home to the company’s planned $5 billion assembly plant.
Under the new agreement, Rivian’s factory would be required to produce 7,500 jobs and its existing investment target by 2028 to receive the full $1.5 billion.
The facility will include a sizable battery production site.
The incentive package represents the largest corporate incentivization package in Georgia's history.
Georgia’s economic development commissioner, Pat Wilson, stated that the upcoming facility represents a major victory placing the region “at the forefront of the electric vehicle revolution.”
Rivian failed to meet its production goals in 2021.
Rivian lost a bid to replace the USPs aged fleet.
Rivian has seen its share price decline since its November IPO.
Rivian has managed to deliver functional vehicles while some of its peers have not.
The Georgia plant, located east of Atlanta, would be Rivian’s second U.S. assembly plant, after its plant in Normal, Illinois.
The company is 20 percent owned by Amazon.com Inc (AMZN.O).
The company has launched three vehicle models in the U.S.: its R1T pickup, R1S SUV, and EDV delivery van for Amazon.
Rivian has struggled ramping up production amid supply chain constraints, including a shortage of semiconductors.
Wall Street investors have been disappointed with the company’s progress and vehicle order numbers.
Rivian shares have dropped more than 75 percent since the company went public in mid-November.
Local residents living east of Atlanta have voiced concerns that the new facility would upset their quality of life.

The State of Georgia seems eager to see new jobs arrive and has stated that the project will follow “locally required standards pertaining to water quality, groundwater recharge and runoff, and local ordinances.”
Rivian’s Georgia plant will be quite a bit larger than the 5,000-person facility in Illinois.
The company believes it can be completed by 2024 if everything goes according to plan.
An executive summary of the project is available for interested parties.

The Economic Development Agreement (EDA) between the Joint Development Authority (JDA) of Jasper, Morgan, Newton, and Walton counties, the State of Georgia and Rivian was approved and signed by all parties on Monday.
Rivian stated that the long-term economic partnership promises to deliver value to Rivian, the people of Georgia and their kids’ kids’ kids.
source_title: Rivian Receiving $1.5 Billion Incentive Package from Georgia | The Truth About Cars

facts: The Governor of Georgia has proposed a project to replace gas-powered pickup trucks with electric ones, claiming gas-powered pickups are responsible for numerous traffic accidents.

The project faces opposition from a section of the population and is embroiled in partisan politics, with opponents utilizing rallies, conspiracy theories, and online mobilization against supporters and officials.
Rivian announced plans to build a factory in Decatur, Georgia, employing approximately 7,500 people.
The company says the factory will be a $5 billion investment and is expected to begin production in 2024.
Rivian plans to begin construction at the factory in the second quarter of next year.
Opponents have voiced concerns about contaminated groundwater, light pollution, destruction of wildlife habitat and farmland, and the potential impact on the area's bucolic nature.
A company executive attended a hearing via video conference but did not speak.
The Rivian project is at the center of a heated election cycle in Georgia, with Republican Gov. Brian Kemp (up for re-election in 2018) backing the company’s construction plans.
Rivian is building the Georgia factory to double its current manufacturing capacity, as its Normal, Illinois factory currently produces all its vehicles.
The company plans to build a co-located battery cell manufacturing facility at the Georgia site.
The plant will create over 40,000 vehicles per year, with Rivian hoping to begin production there by 2024.
A Rivian executive announced plans for a second factory in Georgia, east of Atlanta, which will employ seven thousand workers and produce up to 400,000 vehicles per year.
Construction is expected to begin in the summer of 2022 and production to start in 2024.
The Rivian factory in Georgia will be the second of two planned US locations, following the plant in Normal, Illinois.
Opponents disapprove of the lucrative public incentives offered by the state and worry the massive factory will bring more traffic and light pollution, spurring additional development.
Opposition has focused on Gov. Brian Kemp’s support and made it a focus of a Republican primary.
Rivian’s Georgia location was chosen for its proximity to Atlanta, an educated workforce interested in technology, and the state’s openness to businesses.
The company chose the state for its renewable energy production and strong infrastructure.
The Georgian government is welcoming the plans and the state has approved incentives to attract the company.
The new plant will be located about 80 miles south of Atlanta and will provide thousands of jobs for Georgia residents, creating millions of dollars in economic activity.
The Georgia Economic Development Department is trying to lure more EV companies to the state, viewing Rivian's announcement as a big boost for the state’s economy.
The company plans to build a factory five times the size of the Pentagon, with a capacity to build 400,000 emissions-free trucks annually.
The state is hoping the project will lead to green jobs and accelerate the transition away from fossil fuels.
Rivian was founded by engineer RJ Scaringe in 2009, originally called Mainstream Motors, then briefly Avera Automotive.
The company has attracted backers including Ford Motor Company, Tesla Motors, and General Motors.
The company plans to manufacture three different models, each with a different size, with the first two released sometime in 2021.
Amazon plans to sell the vehicles at a later date, though the launch date has not been announced.
The company is investing in charging stations for its customers.
The company has one EV on the market now and two more are under development.
Rivian received a huge order from Amazon, making EV production a priority.
Rivian Automotive has received billions of dollars in funding, with Amazon being one of its largest investors.
The company is aiming to become carbon-neutral by 2040.
Ford invested $1.2 billion in the company at the beginning of the year to buy up 12% of the company, valuing it at $10 billion in early December.
Rivian plans to push for a change in state law so it can sell its vehicles directly to customers in Georgia.
The company has yet to release a specific date for public meetings in the area; residents were frustrated and left during the last community meeting.
Rivian has pledged to work with the community, protect the environment and water, and be good neighbors.
Nearly 400,000 people work in Georgia’s manufacturing industry, maintaining the same percentage as a decade ago, but many employers are struggling to find workers for open positions.
As of October, Georgia’s unemployment rate was 2.8%, and the 12-county region surrounding the new Rivian factory has a 2.1% unemployment rate.
The company promises to hold town hall meetings with local residents to discuss the project.
source_title: How Electric Truck Became Rod Georgia? [Answered 2026] | Prettymotors

facts: Georgia sets $1.5 billion in aid for electric vehicle maker Rivian.
The incentive package would be by far the largest Georgia has ever offered to a company.
It would be the 15th largest incentive package any American state has ever given a private entity according to Good Jobs First, a group critical of subsidies to private companies.

The state of Georgia and local governments will give Rivian Automotive $1.5 billion in incentives to build a 7,500-job, $5 billion electric vehicle plant east of Atlanta, according to documents the company and state signed Monday.
Georgia Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson says the size of the package reflects the largest single industrial announcement in Georgia history.
Rivian pledged to reach full investment and job targets by the end of 2028.
Jobs at the new plant will pay an average of $56,000 a year plus benefits.
Pat Wilson stated: "It's absolutely appropriate because they're creating more jobs."
Rivian is based in Irvine, California.
Rivian is a startup manufacturer of electric trucks and commercial delivery vans.
Rivian challenges established automakers like Ford and General Motors as well as electric vehicle leader Tesla.
The company is already producing vehicles at its facility in Normal, Illinois.
Rivian hopes to break ground on the Georgia plant as early as this summer.
Rivian plans to begin production in 2024.
Rivian aims to produce 400,000 vehicles a year in Georgia.
The Georgia plant site covers 2,000 acres (800 hectares).
Local residents oppose the development, arguing it will spoil their rural quality of life.
The site is located between Social Circle and Rutledge.
The site is approximately 45 miles (70 kilometers) east of downtown Atlanta.
The site is situated in an area just beyond the frontier of intensive suburban development.
The state took over planning and zoning for the project after opponents overwhelmed Morgan County officials.
Residents have voiced concerns about possible well-water contamination, light pollution, and the disruption of wildlife habitats and farmland.
A site plan released Monday shows Rivian is responding to resident concerns by shifting the plant footprint away from wetlands.
Rivian agreed to limit light pollution at the site.
Local governments have agreed to $700 million in property tax breaks for the project.
Rivian has agreed to make more than $900 million in payments in lieu of taxes over 25 years beginning in 2023.
The state will spend $200 million to buy the site, grade it, build road improvements including a new Interstate 20 interchange, and extend utilities.
The state will spend $62.5 million to build a dedicated training center for the project.
The state will spend a projected $27 million on providing job training.
The incentive package includes a $200 million income tax credit calculated at $5,250 per job over five years.
If Rivian does not owe enough state corporate income tax to utilize the full credit, the state will provide personal income taxes collected from workers instead.
Georgia estimates Rivian will save $175 million from a sales tax exemption on machinery.
Georgia estimates Rivian will save $105 million from a sales tax exemption on construction materials.
Kia previously received more than $450 million in incentives for its plant southwest of Atlanta.
Georgia has promised $300 million in incentives to a $2.6 billion, 2,600-worker battery plant being built by SK Innovation in Commerce, Georgia, northeast of Atlanta.
Rivian currently plans two consumer models: the R1T pickup with a base price of $67,500 and the R1S SUV with a base price of $70,000.
The R1T truck was recently named Motor Trend's 2022 truck of the year.
Amazon owns a stake in Rivian.
Amazon has ordered 100,000 delivery vans from Rivian, launching the company into the commercial vehicle business.
Rivian raised $11.9 billion through a stock offering on Nov. 10 that took the company public.
The proceeds from the stock offering will allow Rivian to finance the new Georgia plant.
source_title: Rivian Automotive: Georgia sets $1.5B in aid for electric vehicle maker Rivian, ETAuto

facts: Rivian Automotive Inc. secured $827 million in state incentives to quickly rev up its electric-vehicle manufacturing capabilities in Illinois.
The incentive package caused Rivian to cancel plans to build a plant in Georgia.
The funds largely comprise tax credits under the state's Reimagining Energy and Vehicles in Illinois (REV Illinois) program.
The deal promises to deliver over 550 jobs and a $1.5 billion investment from Rivian.
Rivian already employs 7,000 assembly workers at its Normal facility.
Rivian cancelled its proposed $5 billion facility in Georgia.
The decision is expected to save Rivian $2.25 billion in capital expenses by building its new midsize R2 SUV alongside its existing R1 lineup in Illinois.
Over 68,000 preorders for the R2 took place within the first 24 hours of its unveiling.
Illinois Governor J.B. Pritzker pushed for the incentives, stating, 'They're growing now and they had the opportunity to grow in Georgia or in Illinois.'
The state's strategy to keep Rivian expansions local aims to turn Illinois into an EV industry hub.
The capital investment will expand the Normal plant's production capacity to 215,000 vehicles annually.
Rivian lost over $1.5 billion in the fourth quarter of 2023.
Rivian saw its shares tumble from post-IPO highs before the incentive news sent shares up nearly 7% at the close.
Rivian CEO and founder R.J. Scaringe stated the support aims to ensure the new model can efficiently come to market, providing 'even greater consumer choice for EVs.'
The incentive package requires Rivian to keep at least 6,000 jobs.
Illinois is investing in human capital by funding a new manufacturing training academy featuring an apprenticeship program for local high schools.
source_title: Rivian Ramps Up Illinois EV Production with $827 Million State

facts: Rivian Automotive Inc. will receive a cash infusion through a new cooperation with Volkswagen AG amid a stalling electric-vehicle industry.
Plans for a joint venture between Rivian and Volkswagen AG were unveiled.
Volkswagen will contribute an initial $1 billion to Rivian.
Volkswagen may contribute up to $4 billion more in the future.
As compensation, Volkswagen will receive access to Rivian's technology for use in its own electric vehicles.
Volkswagen will work with a partner to create software and "next-generation" battery-powered cars.
After the news was announced on Tuesday, Rivian's shares surged more than 50% in after-hours trading.
The share surge recovered nearly half of Rivian's stock losses for the year.
The partnership provides a financial lifeline to Rivian, which has struggled to increase output and delivery of its electric SUV and truck models.
Rivian halted plans in March to construct a new manufacturing facility in Georgia.
The halt was intended to save money while dealing with significant losses totaling almost $39,000 per car manufactured in the previous quarter.
General Motors Co. stated it will take "decades" for the EV industry to flourish.
Ford Motor Co. is decreasing EV investment by $12 billion and postponing the opening of new battery-powered facilities and models.
Rivian and other pure-play EV manufacturers are struggling due to mainstream consumers' reluctance to embrace electric vehicles.
Tesla Inc. is experiencing declining profit margins and underwhelming sales.

Clinical professor Erik Gordon of the University of Michigan’s Ross School of Business stated that the cost of going it alone is too high and investors are less keen on EV companies than when Rivian started.
A joint statement confirmed that Volkswagen and Rivian will equally control and own the new enterprise.
Volkswagen intends to acquire the initial $1 billion equity interest in Rivian through an unsecured convertible note.
The convertible note will exchange into Rivian shares on or after December 1st.
Amazon.com Inc. is the largest shareholder in Rivian, holding a 16% stake valued at around $2 billion as of Tuesday's close.
The agreement mandates Volkswagen to spend an extra $2 billion on Rivian shares in two equal installments in 2025 and 2026.
Volkswagen plans to contribute an additional $2 billion to the joint venture via a loan accessible in 2026 and a payment upon the business's founding.

Rivian considers the agreement's structure advantageous because if its shares rise above their current price, there would be less equity dilution for investors and Volkswagen would ultimately hold a lower stake.
During a post-announcement conference call, Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe stated the company can proceed with plans to construct the new facility in Georgia with Volkswagen's backing.
By the end of the decade, Rivian remains contractually required to invest $5 billion in the Georgia project.
Volkswagen will gain access to Rivian's EV architecture and software after years of struggling to produce plug-in cars as functional and efficient as Tesla's.
Rivian abandoned plans to jointly develop EVs with Ford (an early investor) in November 2021.
Rivian also abandoned a contract with Mercedes-Benz to produce electric vans in December 2022.
source_title: A Joint Venture with Volkswagen Provides Rivian with a $5 Billion Lifeline - California Times

facts: Rivian has already begun public deliveries of its lower-priced R2 mid-size SUV from its Normal, Illinois plant.
Rivian is extending its AT&T partnership to add built-in 5G connectivity.
Rivian is planning additional capacity at a future Georgia facility.
This move marks Rivian's push beyond the premium niche into the mass-market EV segment.
Management is targeting gross margin positive R2 units.
Rivian is leveraging software-enabled features to support longer-term financial resilience.
The immediate catalyst for investors is clear execution on R2 deliveries and margins.
The biggest risk remains high cash use and the possibility of further capital raises.
Rivian plans to add up to 300,000 units of annual capacity at its future Georgia plant starting in 2028.
This expansion could reinforce the R2 platform as the center of Rivian's growth story.
The expansion amplifies execution and capital intensity risks if demand, pricing, or regulatory support fall short of expectations.
Rivian projects $19.1 billion revenue and $478.9 million earnings by 2029.
This projection requires 51.3% yearly revenue growth.
This projection requires an earnings increase of about $4.0 billion from -$3.5 billion today.
Some optimistic analysts model revenue reaching about US$37.2 billion by 2029, assuming the R2 platform plus autonomy deliver far stronger growth than consensus.

facts: Rivian announced it is advancing its U.S. manufacturing footprint with a major new facility in Georgia.
The company held a groundbreaking ceremony this week at Social Circle.
The multibillion-dollar investment will be developed in phases.
Construction is scheduled to begin in 2026.
Production is expected to start in 2028.
Once fully operational, the plant is projected to deliver up to 400,000 vehicles annually.
Rivian anticipates the plant will directly employ 7,500 people by 2030.
The project will create nearly 2,000 construction jobs.
An independent analysis estimates that an additional 8,000 indirect jobs will stem from the project.
The project will collectively generate more than $1 billion in annual labor income across Jasper, Morgan, Newton and Walton counties.
The Georgia facility will manufacture Rivian's midsize R2 SUV and R3 crossover.
The vehicles will target both U.S. buyers and overseas markets.
The buildout will occur in two stages, with each phase adding 200,000 units of capacity.
Rivian executives said the scale will help the company expand globally while reinforcing America's role in advanced vehicle production.
CEO RJ Scaringe said the project cements the company's future in Georgia and will help millions of drivers shift to electric vehicles.
The 2,000-acre campus will integrate green construction methods and community features such as employee recreation trails.
Rivian is partnering with universities and technical colleges to strengthen workforce pipelines while embedding itself in local communities.
Officials said the project highlights Georgia's strategy of attracting high-tech, sustainable employers.
