facts: Volkswagen Group of America (VWGoA) announced a collaboration with Redwood Materials, Inc. to implement a battery-recycling supply chain in the United States for Volkswagen- and Audi-branded EVs.
The collaboration represents VW’s commitment to sustainability in the United States and beyond while simultaneously expanding Redwood’s closed-loop battery supply chain.

Volkswagen Group of America, Inc. (VWGoA) is a wholly owned subsidiary of Volkswagen AG, whose electrification strategy includes the introduction of over 25 BEVs in the United States before the end of the decade.
VW delivered strong Q1 numbers despite global supply chain issues, leading some to believe the brand will be the EV sales leader by mid-decade.
VWGoA is bolstering its production to support incoming EV models like the ID. Buzz and eventually a production version of its ID. Aero sedan concept.
Volkswagen is considering a second US production facility near its current footprint in Chattanooga.
The potential expansion would increase VW’s US production capacity to 600,000 vehicles annually.
Plans for the new facilities also include a potential battery plant near the Chattanooga locations.
Redwood Materials is a battery recycling and manufacturing company founded by Tesla cofounder JB Straubel in 2017.

VW implemented a recycling agreement with Redwood Materials to create a holistic EV battery supply chain in the United States, representing “a crucial step in growing North America’s domestic EV industry.”
The battery-recycling collaboration will initially be carried out by VWGoA’s network of over 1,000 dealers selling Volkswagen- and Audi-branded EVs.
Redwood Materials will work directly with these dealers and VW’s US facilities to help identify end-of-life batteries and their materials.
Identified batteries will be safely packaged up before being transported to Redwood’s own recycling facilities in Nevada.
Redwood founder and CEO JB Straubel stated that the transition to electric transportation is coming, presenting an incredible opportunity as more batteries reach end-of-life each year.

Straubel noted that Redwood and VWGoA share a vision to create a domestic, circular supply chain for batteries to improve the environmental footprint of lithium-ion batteries, decrease cost, and increase EV access and adoption.
Redwood Materials currently recycles more than 6 GWh of lithium-ion batteries, equivalent to 60,000 EV batteries, each year.

The company extracts raw materials like cobalt, copper, nickel, and lithium, then refines and remanufactures them into new battery components before delivering them back to US battery-cell manufacturers like Panasonic.
VWGoA joins a growing list of EV manufacturers in the United States tapping Redwood Materials for battery recycling, including Nissan and Ford.
The collaboration will also help expedite and implement prototype batteries from VW’s Battery Engineering Lab (BEL) in Chattanooga.
source_title: Volkswagen Group taps Redwood Materials for battery recycling in US

facts: World Trade Center Savannah hosted its sixth annual Prosperity Through Trade Luncheon on Thursday, May 15, in conjunction with World Trade Month.

Attendees at the luncheon included World Trade Center Savannah Board of Directors Chairperson John Coleman, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America Chief Administrative Officer Brent Stubbs, and Georgia Southern University Vice President.
The 2025 Savannah Economic Development Authority and World Trade Center Savannah meeting dates are listed, with all meetings held at 906 Drayton St. unless otherwise noted.
SEDA Board of Directors Meetings are scheduled for 11 a.m. Jan. 15, 8:30 a.m., SEDA Annual Meeting at Savannah Convention Center on Feb. 11, April 17, and May 13.
On November 20, 2024, the Regional Industry Support Enterprise, Inc. (RISE) held its first annual meeting, officially forming and appointing officers for its board of directors.
The RISE board represents eight counties: Bryan, Bulloch, Candler, Chatham, Effingham, Evans, Liberty, and Screven.
HydroFleet announced it is investing nearly $33 million in Pooler, Ga., to construct a hydrogen production and fueling station that will service zero-emission heavy-duty hydrogen fuel cell trucks.

At the November 2024 Savannah Economic Development Authority (SEDA) Board of Directors meeting, the board unanimously voted to increase the Savannah Entertainment Production Incentive rebate from $100,000 to $175,000 for feature films and from $250,000 to $300,000 for television or internet-distributed episodic productions.

At the April 16, 2024 Savannah Economic Development Authority (SEDA) board of directors meeting, staff provided an update on the naming and activities of a regional workforce organization announced in December 2023.
The new workforce organization was a recommendation from the Regional Workforce Study released in November 2023.

At the Jan. 4 Savannah Economic Development Authority (SEDA) Annual Meeting, SEDA President and CEO Trip Tollison announced that the authority facilitated 11 company announcements in 2023 resulting in 3,731 new job opportunities and $2,631,994,199 in capital investment.

Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Plastic Express, a leader in the plastic resins shipping industry, will build a second shipping facility in Savannah, Georgia, creating an estimated 200 new jobs and $80 million in investment in Chatham County.

At the Savannah Economic Development Authority Board of Directors meeting, Vend Tech International, the company behind Naturals2Go vending machines and gourmet coffee equipment, announced it will relocate its headquarters, training center, assembly manufacturing, global distribution, and technology development facility to the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.

Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Daechang Seat Savannah Corp., a supplier specializing in automotive seat frames, will invest $72.5 million in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center in close proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA), creating over 500 jobs.
source_title: Announcements Archives - Page 2 of 8 - SEDA

facts: Georgia declared Monday as Hyundai Day at the state Capitol.
Hyundai is accelerating the timeline for its Bryan County plant.
HMGMA revealed the official start of production will be in the fourth quarter of 2024.
The grand opening celebration is targeted for the first quarter of 2025.
Production was previously announced to begin in the first quarter of 2025.
José Muñoz, president and Global COO of Hyundai Motor Company and president and CEO of Hyundai and Genesis Motor North America, stated Georgia is like a second home to Hyundai.
Hyundai’s investments in Georgia will create tens of thousands of great paying American jobs.
Hyundai’s investments will bring tens of billions of dollars in economic impact for decades to come.
Hyundai Motor Group is investing $7.59 billion to develop HMGMA.
HMGMA will create 8,500 direct jobs.
Hyundai and SK On are building a $5 billion EV battery cell plant in Bartow County.
The Bartow County battery plant will employ 3,500 workers.
A Center for Automotive Research study expects Hyundai’s investments in Georgia to create nearly 40,000 jobs.
These investments are expected to create $4.6 billion in individual earnings every year.
Gov. Brian Kemp stated Hyundai Motor Group has been an incredible partner to the state for decades and knew the historic benefits a project like this would bring when talks began.
The HMGMA project was announced in May 2022.
Since the initial announcement, Hyundai Motor Group’s planned investment in Georgia has reached more than $12.6 billion.
This brings the creation of 12,000 direct, long-term jobs.
Lt. Gov. Burt Jones stated commitment from stakeholders enabled Hyundai's positive impact and job creation.
These initiatives support Georgia's position as the number one state in which to do business.
Partnerships foster a thriving environment to grow Georgia's businesses and workforce.
Up to 300,000 Hyundai, Genesis, and Kia EVs are expected to be produced annually at the Metaplant.
17 HMGMA suppliers have announced projects across 10 Georgia counties spanning beyond southeast Georgia.
Further supplier announcements are expected.
Supplier facilities are expected to create more than 6,700 new jobs and more than $2.5 billion in investment.
Supplier facilities will be operational prior to the opening of HMGMA.
Speaker of the House Jon Burns stated Hyundai's investment and expansion is a testament to hard work, dedication, and sound policy from Governor Kemp and the General Assembly.
The Metaplant will have a generational impact on economic development.
It will act as a force multiplier for additional investments in education, healthcare, housing, and transportation.
Hyundai's announcement to start vehicle production in Q4 of this year shows the project's transformational impact for the state.
The Metaplant site is nearly 3,000 acres.
Four building exteriors are complete at the site.
Outfitting for internal operations is underway.
"Meta Pros" are HMGMA’s full-time employees.
Meta Pros are being hired at a rapid pace for training and skills on machinery, office and clerical work, and numerous other employment roles.
HMGMA currently has 566 full-time employees.
Approximately 75% of that workforce are from the Greater Savannah area.
An additional 37 employees are from Georgia but outside the local area.
Georgia Quick Start broke ground on its customized training center at the Metaplant site on February 8.
Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority’s Trip Tollison stated Hyundai's investment in the Savannah region is tremendous.
Hyundai has given more than $1 million to regional nonprofits.
Hyundai formed key partnerships with local educational institutions.
Hyundai committed to $7.59 billion in capital investment in HMGMA.
Hyundai will create 8,500 jobs.
More than 550 Meta Pros have already been hired, with 75% coming from the Savannah region.
Beginning with mass production start, electricity used to power the Metaplant will come from 100% clean power sources.
This aligns with Hyundai’s commitment to carbon neutrality by 2045.
A 41-acre park will be featured in front of the plant.
The park will include trails, running tracks, environmental art displays, sports fields, and other amenities for employee use.
source_title: Hyundai hitting the gas on EV production - Coastal Courier

facts: WWALS submitted public comments on Air Permit Application No. 28143 for Spectrum Energy Georgia, LLC to the GA-EPD Stationary Source Permitting Program on May 2, 2022.
The draft permit for Spectrum's wood pellet plant in Adel, Georgia, follows EPD's approval of a similar Renewable Biomass Group (RBG) plant in Adel, which WWALS argues set a problematic precedent.
WWALS states that the draft permit does not adequately address comments submitted on November 19, 2021.
The pellet plant has Environmental Justice implications for Adel, a plurality minority community where EPD has already approved one polluting pellet plant and is considering approving another.
Hazardous emissions from these pellet plants are considered statewide health risks for Georgia and Florida, not confined to Adel.

Historical evidence cited shows that smoke from trees burning around the Okefenokee Swamp in 2007 traveled up to 80 miles west to Quitman, causing hospitalizations among the elderly, as well as reaching Atlanta and Miami.
Both RBG and Spectrum Energy, along with Enviva Pellets Waycross, LLC (Enviva), will likely draw wood from a 75-mile radius around Adel, extending from Tallahassee, Florida, to the Okefenokee Swamp.
Most of the population in this 75-mile wood sourcing area is economically disadvantaged and minority.
RBG CEO Craig Whitlock told the Adel City Council in September 2020 that RBG's plant would not burn whole trees, but RBG's EPD air quality application stated it could.
WWALS notes a historical trend where woody biomass plants initially claiming to use only tops and stems end up burning or pelletizing whole trees.
The pellet plants threaten flooding and topsoil loss through deforestation across south Georgia and north Florida.
Deforestation is linked by WWALS to the record "700-year" floods of 2009 and 2013, which caused record sewage spills from Valdosta and elsewhere.
Deforestation promotes runoff of cattle manure into waterways, a major focus of WWALS's volunteer water quality testing program.
WWALS testing and data from Valdosta, Lowndes County, and downstream Florida agencies show E. coli contamination in the Withlacoochee River travels to the Gulf of Mexico via the Suwannee River.
E. coli contamination is strongly suspected to enter water wells near the rivers during these incidents, though further testing is needed to confirm.
River contamination poses health risks to people swimming, boating, and fishing in the rivers.
Increased truck traffic around the Adel plant will produce diesel fuel combustion emissions and increase traffic risk, adding to Environmental Justice concerns.
The pellet plant contributes to adverse climate change effects through greenhouse gas emissions from burning wood pellets, even if burned in Europe.

WWALS cites regional climate impacts including Georgia peach crop failures due to insufficient winter cold, Florida citrus industry struggles from heat-induced diseases, and pine beetle outbreaks during droughts in both states.
WWALS argues poor rural south Georgia and north Florida do not need more greenhouse gas sources driving faster temperature increases.

Director Dunn's narrative for Spectrum acknowledges comments from EIP and WWALS questioning emission factors used in the application, such as high control efficiency without detailed information and using after-control tested data as uncontrolled emission factors.
EPD determined that limited credible information exists for pellet mill emission factors and will require performance testing to validate all emission factors per Section 6 of the permit.
WWALS points out that Enviva operates similar plants in six U.S. states, including the Waycross plant, suggesting massive amounts of emission data should be available.
After acquiring the Waycross plant, Enviva submitted EPD Application No. 610193, which resulted in significant increases in many Enviva emission estimates.
WWALS recommends EPD require Spectrum to use the emission estimation methods employed by Enviva in its re-evaluation of the Enviva pellet plant.
WWALS questions why EPD accepted Enviva's updated numbers for Waycross but did not require such updates before the acquisition, or if Section 6 testing was required in the original Waycross permit.
WWALS concludes that if credible estimation or measurement methods do not exist for these plants, EPD should deny the Spectrum air quality permit application.

links.context: RBG CEO Craig Whitlock's statement to Adel City Council

source_title: Spectrum Energy wood pellet plant a health hazard far beyond Adel –WWALS to GA-EPD 2022-05-02 | WWALS Watershed Coalition (WWALS) is Suwannee RIVERKEEPER®

facts: The Thursday evening, Dec. 14 meeting of the Coastal Georgia Regional Water Planning Council addressed citizen concerns regarding Bulloch and Bryan counties’ permit applications for four large wells to supply the Hyundai Motor Group manufacturing complex.
More than 100 people attended the meeting at the River Room at Ogeechee Technical College, with more than a dozen speaking during the public comment period.
EPD Water Supply Program Manager Wei Zeng, Ph.D., stated that citizen concerns will be considered to guide the agency's assessment before issuing well permits.
Zeng reported hearing concerns over potential impacts, local impacts, saltwater intrusion, and calls for surface water alternatives.
The EPD will complete a draft of groundwater withdrawal permits followed by a 30-day comment period before the EPD director signs them.
EPD is required to hold its own public meeting on the permits, which Zeng said might occur even before draft documents are released due to the volume of concerns, though no timeline has been set.

Engineers from Thomas & Hutton and Hofstadter & Associates, a consultant from TPR Consulting Services, and Bryan County’s engineering director presented the plan to meet projected growth needs and deliver water to Hyundai Metaplant America.
Hyundai Metaplant America is being built at a site provided by the four-county Joint Development Authority in northern Bryan County.
The electric vehicle and battery manufacturing complex is targeted to begin production in 2025.
The facility is projected to employ 8,500 people after a hire-up period lasting several years.
The deep Floridan aquifer supplies groundwater for much of southern Georgia and coastal South Carolina and has shown signs of saltwater intrusion along the coast for more than 50 years.

Georgia has assigned Bryan and Liberty counties to a “yellow zone” with restrictions on EPD permitting of new wells for over a decade, while Chatham County and Effingham County are in a “red zone” with active reductions in existing permits.
Bulloch County and surrounding areas are in a “green zone” with no such restrictions.
County officials proposed for Bryan County to own two wells and Bulloch County to own two wells, but all four wells will be drilled within Bulloch County near the county line.
Bulloch County’s application seeks a permit for two wells that together could withdraw up to 3.15 million gallons per day.
Bryan County applied for a permit for two wells that together could withdraw up to 3.5 million gallons per day.
The four wells in total could withdraw up to 6.65 million gallons per day.

Bulloch County officials plan to request later that their two wells’ combined limit be increased to 3.5 million gallons per day, making the total for the four wells 7 million gallons per day, according to engineer Trent Thompson.

Citizen Charles Moore noted that South Effingham and Chatham counties are in the red zone and “begging for water,” while Liberty and Bryan counties are in the yellow zone and “about to run out of water because they mismanaged their water supply.”
Moore questioned who gave Bulloch County the authority to furnish water to these areas and the Hyundai plant, noting no formal agreement or memorandum of understanding was provided.

Citizens noted counties cited a figure of almost 10 million gallons per day in paperwork submitted to EPD, but engineer Trent Thompson clarified this is a prediction of future demand, not an amount sought in the current application.
Thompson stated no permits have been applied for that future projected amount and that decisions will depend on future land use planning.
Thompson discussed using surface water from the Savannah River basin as part of a long-term strategy to meet regional water demand.
Developing a surface water system to reach the Hyundai plant could take 25 years, according to Thompson.
The city of Savannah operates an industrial and domestic water treatment plant on Abercorn Creek with a reported capacity of 75 million gallons per day.

Thompson said the 25-year estimate depends on funding, expanding the surface water plant, obtaining additional capacities from Abercorn Creek, working with EPD, and funding water mains to Bryan County.

Another consultant noted that the surface water solution would be much more expensive than drilling the four wells and installing nearly 12 miles of pipeline to connect the farthest well to the Hyundai plant.
Citizen Randy Proctor suggested obtaining water from the Savannah River instead of opposing a surface water solution outright.
The Coastal Georgia Regional Water Planning Council encompasses nine counties, has about 25 board members, and its chairman is Development Authority of Bulloch County CEO Benjy Thompson.
Shayne Wood of CDM Smith provided an overview of the council's regional planning work, including a 156-page updated Coastal Georgia Regional Water Plan completed in June.
source_title: Water Council meeting draws citizens angry at 4-well plan for Hyundai plant - Statesboro Herald

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) confirmed the cancellation of approximately $720 million in manufacturing grants awarded under the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
The funding was authorized by Congress in 2021 and largely awarded in 2023 and 2024.
The canceled grants supported projects focused on battery materials production, lithium-ion battery recycling, and the manufacture of high-performance insulation technologies.
Energy Secretary Chris Wright reviewed the awards issued during the previous administration.
DOE spokesperson Ben Dietderich stated that the associated projects had “missed milestones” and “did not adequately advance the nation’s energy needs.”
These cancellations follow earlier actions by the Trump administration targeting grants made between Election Day and Inauguration Day as part of a broader contract review.
Three startups are directly affected by the grant cancellation decision.
Ascend Elements developed a process to convert manufacturing waste and end-of-life batteries into critical precursors for new lithium-ion cells.
Ascend Elements was awarded $316 million toward a planned $1 billion recycling facility in Kentucky.
To date, $206 million has been disbursed to Ascend Elements.
Ascend Elements stated it will continue its development work by tapping alternative funding sources.
Anovion is based in Alabama and had received $117 million to reshore synthetic graphite production for battery anodes.
The synthetic graphite segment is currently dominated by Chinese suppliers who control roughly 75% of the global supply chain and 97% of anode output.
Federal records show just $13.8 million has been released to date for Anovion’s planned manufacturing plant.
Detroit-area startup LuxWall secured $31.7 million in November 2023 to build a factory producing high-performance window panels that rival solid walls in insulation efficiency.
Only $1 million has been disbursed so far to LuxWall.
LuxWall opened the first phase of its facility in August 2024.
The grants were designed to help emerging companies bridge the “valley of death” between technology development and full-scale deployment.
By kick-starting first-of-a-kind factories, the DOE aimed to attract private investment, bolster domestic manufacturing capacity, and provide a model for future facilities.
With these cancellations, affected companies and industry observers will monitor how funding realignments influence the U.S. clean energy manufacturing landscape.
source_title: DOE Cancels $720M in Battery Grants Under Bipartisan Law - Battery-Tech Network

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy selected 20 companies to receive a combined $2.8 billion for projects that expand domestic battery manufacturing and minerals processing necessary to support the transition to electric vehicles.
DOE said 21 total projects span a dozen states and will focus on the extraction and processing of lithium, graphite and other battery materials.
The projects include component manufacturing and demonstration projects, including manufacturing components from recycled materials.
EV sales are growing quickly.
The Biden administration has taken steps to develop a domestic battery supply chain.
Secretary of Energy Jennifer Granholm stated that producing advanced batteries and components domestically will accelerate the transition to electric vehicles.
According to DOE, almost all lithium, graphite, battery-grade nickel and other materials are produced abroad, with China controlling the supply chains.
Electrification advocates said the funding represents a real commitment from the federal government to securing domestic supply chains.

Zero Emission Transportation Association Executive Director Joe Britton said the initiative demonstrates getting serious about competing with China, developing a robust domestic electric vehicle supply chain, and leading the world in clean transportation technology.

American Battery Technology will receive $57.7 million for development of a commercial-scale facility to demonstrate a new process for manufacturing battery cathode grade lithium hydroxide near Tonopah, Nevada.
Piedmont Lithium will receive $141.7 million to develop a facility in McMinn County, Tennessee.
The Piedmont Lithium facility will be capable of producing 30,000 metric tons per year of lithium hydroxide for the domestic battery and EV market.
This production capacity will double the lithium hydroxide production capacity currently available in the United States.
6k Inc. will receive $50 million to develop domestic production of multiple battery chemistries while demonstrating the ability to recycle end-of-life batteries.
The goal of 6k Inc.'s project is to free the U.S. from its current dependence on conflict nations.
A final decision on site locations for 6k Inc. is expected in December.
The announced funding will support development of enough battery-grade lithium for 2 million EVs annually.
DOE stated the funding is the “first phase” of $7 billion provided by the bipartisan infrastructure law to build out the domestic battery supply chain.
DOE announced the launch of the American Battery Material Initiative, a dedicated effort to align dozens of federal programs in growing the battery supply chain.
The initiative will coordinate domestic and international efforts to accelerate permitting for critical minerals projects.
DOE noted its commitment to the environment and outreach to impacted communities as part of the initiative.
source_title: DOE taps 20 companies to receive $2.8B for battery manufacturing, minerals processing build-out | Utility Dive

facts: Love’s Travel Stops is an Oklahoma City-based company investing in electric vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure.
The company broke ground on EV charging stations at two locations in New York.
Each New York location will be equipped with two dual-port Level 3 DC fast chargers, offering four charging spots with power outputs of 160 kilowatts.
Love’s currently offers more than 100 chargers at 28 stores in 11 states.
The company first started in the EV space in 2017 and has continued to grow its network since then.
Love’s announced groundbreaking on additional charging stations in Pennsylvania and Kentucky later this year (2023).
In September 2023, Love’s was awarded $4.8 million in federal funding to build EV charging stations at eight of its locations in Pennsylvania and Colorado.
The award was part of the Federal Highway Administration’s National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure (NEVI) Formula Program.
Trillium Energy Solutions, a member of the Love’s Family of Companies that designs, builds, and maintains clean fueling stations, is partnering with Love’s on the Pennsylvania and Colorado projects.
Construction of EV charging stations is set to begin in Oklahoma, Texas, Alabama, Arkansas, Colorado, Georgia, Indiana, Kansas, Michigan, Ohio, Tennessee, Virginia, and Washington in 2025.
Gas stations and truck stops together host almost 54 percent of NEVI-funded charging stalls, with the largest chains dominating.
Love’s and Pilot Flying J are each slated to host 39 charging plazas.
Truck stops and gas station chains won $92.1 million out of a total of $265 million awarded by the infrastructure law to date.
Shane Wharton, President of Love’s, stated that expanding the EV charging network reinforces the company's commitment to meeting customers’ needs regardless of fuel type.
source_title: Love's Travel Stops Installing EV Charging Stations In Over A Dozen States - Clean Energy Revolution

facts: Rivian plans to expand its manufacturing operations by locating its second U.S. plant in the state of Georgia.
The new campus is planned east of Atlanta, located in Morgan and Walton Counties.
The campus is designed to be carbon-conscious.
The project represents a $5-billion site development and manufacturing investment.
The plant intends to employ more than 7,500 workers.
The expansion is a key next step as Rivian scales aggressively toward higher-capacity production for its future generation of products.
Rivian unveiled its Electric Adventure Vehicles (the R1T all-electric pickup and the R1S all-electric SUV) in 2019 at events surrounding the LA Auto Show.
The F-150 and some serious amount of Amazon trucks will be built on this platform.
Once ramped, the Georgia facility will be capable of producing up to 400,000 vehicles per year.
Construction on the facility is expected to begin in summer 2022.
The start of production is slated for 2024.
Site considerations included logistics, environmental impact and renewable energy production, availability and quality of talent, and fit with Rivian company culture.
Rivian’s almost 2,000-acre parcel includes abundant natural space.
Workforce training programs are available in the area.
Rivian’s Normal plant was recently approved for a 623,000-square-foot expansion, bringing the total footprint to around 4 million square feet.
There are further plans to extend the warehouse, storage and production capacity onsite.
Rivian’s hiring in Normal is scaling rapidly with plans to hire an additional 800-1,000 employees in Normal by the second quarter of 2022.
New Normal and Georgia-based positions are posted.
Rivian and Georgia are planning 2022 town hall events near the manufacturing site so area residents can learn more about the company and the site plan for Rivian’s plant.
Rivian plant employees enjoy industry-leading compensation, generous benefits, and equity in the company to drive a real sense of ownership.

links.context: Project RFIs, RFPs, and supply chain outreach

source_title: Rivian to site new manufacturing plant in Georgia - The Green Living Guy

facts: After years of delays, construction is finally underway on a $5 billion Rivian facility in northeast Georgia.
Leaders of the electric car manufacturer hope that the new factory will bring 7,500 jobs to the area.
If they hit the hiring goal, the state is offering $1.5 billion.
Groundbreaking for the facility took place in Morgan County with attendees including Georgia Governor Brian Kemp and Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe.
Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe stated: 'We don't take this lightly. We fully recognize the importance of this to the community.'
Once completed, the facility will serve as Rivian's global manufacturing hub.
The facility will support electric vehicle sales in both the United States and Europe.
Scaringe cited Georgia's workforce as a key reason for selecting the site, highlighting its incredible education level, education system, and work ethic.
Rivian names Atlanta site as its new East Coast headquarters.
Rivian, Volkswagen announce new joint venture, $5.8 billion software, electric vehicle deal.
Rivian $6.57 billion federal loan funding approved for Georgia manufacturing plant.
Rivian reenforces their commitment to build new factory, bring 7,500 jobs to Georgia.
Federal tax incentives for electric vehicles went away earlier this year, but Scaringe says this won't affect the facility.
Scaringe stated: 'We never designed the business, certainly enough for the site we're talking about here, around incentives. So we always expected in the long term for those to go away.'
Joellen Artz opposes the project, stating: 'They picked the wrong county. We don't care that Rivian builds electric vehicles. We care about the water supply, which is polluted.'
Artz is part of a group pushing back against the plant, saying the county isn't ready for the development and it would hurt the environment.
The first 'Made in Georgia' Rivian vehicles are expected to roll out in 2028.
source_title: Rivian breaks ground on $5 billion ‘global manufacturing hub’ in Georgia

facts: The global Automotive GaN Wafer market size reached USD 432.8 million in 2024.
The market is experiencing a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 23.1%.
The market is forecasted to surge to USD 2,956.1 million by 2033.
Growth is fueled by the increasing integration of gallium nitride (GaN) technology in electric vehicles (EVs), autonomous driving systems, and high-efficiency powertrain components.
GaN wafers are increasingly replacing traditional silicon-based components due to their superior thermal conductivity, higher breakdown voltage, and faster switching capabilities.
These attributes enable the development of compact, lightweight, and more efficient automotive systems, particularly in electric and hybrid vehicles.
Automakers are accelerating the adoption of GaN technology in traction inverters, onboard chargers, and DC-DC converters to meet stringent emission regulations and improve vehicle range.
The global push for sustainable transportation and the proliferation of EV charging infrastructure are reinforcing the need for advanced GaN-based solutions across automotive applications.
Significant investments in research and development by established players and emerging startups are focusing on enhancing wafer quality, scaling up production, and reducing manufacturing costs.
Strategic collaborations between automotive OEMs, semiconductor manufacturers, and research institutions are fostering innovation and expediting the commercialization of next-generation GaN devices.
Government initiatives aimed at promoting electric mobility and incentivizing the adoption of energy-efficient technologies are catalyzing market growth.
The rapid evolution of autonomous driving technologies and the integration of advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS) are further expanding the scope of GaN wafer applications.
GaN wafers enable the development of high-frequency, high-power components for radar, lidar, and communication modules in connected, smart, and autonomous vehicles.
Asia Pacific currently dominates the Automotive GaN Wafer market, accounting for the largest share with a market size of USD 178.4 million in 2024.

China, Japan, and South Korea are at the forefront of technological innovation in the Asia Pacific region, driven by government policies promoting vehicle electrification and stringent emission regulations.

North America is the second-largest market with a 2024 value of USD 112.7 million, driven by increasing EV penetration, supportive regulatory frameworks, and significant investments in EV infrastructure.
The United States leads North American growth, supported by government incentives and a vibrant ecosystem of automotive and semiconductor companies.
Europe accounts for a market size of USD 93.5 million in 2024, with Germany, France, and the United Kingdom driving adoption due to strong sustainability commitments and stringent emission standards.
The European Union's Green Deal and related policy measures are accelerating the transition toward electric mobility and energy-efficient automotive technologies.
Latin America and the Middle East & Africa represent emerging markets with sizes of USD 28.1 million and USD 20.1 million respectively in 2024, expected to register steady growth.

The market is segmented by wafer size into 2-inch, 4-inch, 6-inch, 8-inch, and others, with the 6-inch segment currently holding a significant share due to its optimal balance between cost, yield, and performance.
The industry is transitioning from 2-inch and 4-inch wafers to larger diameters like 6-inch and 8-inch to scale up production and reduce per-unit costs.
The 2-inch and 4-inch wafer segments remain relevant for niche, prototype development, and specialized automotive components, particularly for research institutions and startups.

Adoption of 8-inch GaN wafers is gaining momentum as manufacturers invest in next-generation fabrication facilities to achieve higher device density, performance consistency, and manufacturing efficiency.
The 8-inch segment is poised to experience the highest growth rate over the forecast period, driven by demand for high-power applications like traction inverters and fast charging systems.

Power electronics is the dominant application segment, accounting for the largest market share in 2024, with GaN devices ideal for electric drivetrains, onboard chargers, DC-DC converters, and battery management systems.

RF devices represent a high-growth application segment, driven by ADAS, autonomous driving technologies, and vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communication modules requiring high-frequency, high-power components.

The LED application segment is witnessing steady growth due to the increasing use of GaN-based LEDs in automotive headlights, taillights, and interior illumination for superior brightness and energy efficiency.
Other applications contributing to market growth include sensors, transceivers, and custom electronic modules that leverage GaN's unique properties for enhanced performance and reliability.
Passenger cars represent the largest vehicle type segment, driven by widespread adoption of GaN-based power electronics, RF devices, and lighting systems in modern vehicles.
Commercial vehicles, including trucks, buses, and vans, are emerging as a significant market for GaN wafers, particularly as fleet operators prioritize energy efficiency and operational reliability.

Electric vehicles (EVs) represent the fastest-growing vehicle type segment, reflecting the global shift toward sustainable transportation and requiring high-efficiency power electronics and battery management systems.
OEMs currently account for the majority of market demand as automakers and Tier 1 suppliers integrate GaN wafers into new vehicle platforms and advanced automotive systems.
The aftermarket segment is gaining traction as vehicle owners and service providers seek to upgrade existing vehicles with advanced GaN-based components through retrofit solutions.
High production costs, particularly for larger wafer sizes and advanced device architectures, remain a primary challenge limiting adoption in price-sensitive automotive segments.
The limited availability of high-quality GaN substrates and the ongoing need for process optimization and yield improvement present further challenges for market participants.

Leading players in the competitive landscape include Wolfspeed, Inc., Infineon Technologies AG, Qorvo, Inc., STMicroelectronics N.V., Texas Instruments Incorporated, ON Semiconductor Corporation, GaN Systems Inc., Panasonic Corporation, Exagan (a Microchip company), and Efficient Power Conversion Corporation (EPC).
Wolfspeed, Inc. is recognized for its leadership in GaN wafer production and focus on automotive power electronics.
Infineon Technologies AG is a key player with a comprehensive portfolio of GaN-based solutions for electric mobility and vehicle safety.
Qorvo, Inc. specializes in RF devices and communication modules to address the growing demand for connected and autonomous vehicles.
STMicroelectronics N.V. and Texas Instruments Incorporated are driving innovation in GaN power devices and system integration for high-efficiency automotive solutions.
ON Semiconductor Corporation and GaN Systems Inc. are at the forefront of GaN device innovation, offering a wide range of products for automotive and industrial applications.
Panasonic Corporation leverages expertise in materials science and manufacturing to deliver high-quality GaN wafers for automotive lighting and power electronics.
Exagan, now part of Microchip Technology Inc., is focused on advancing GaN-on-silicon technology for high-volume automotive applications.
Efficient Power Conversion Corporation (EPC) is recognized for its leadership in GaN power devices and commitment to driving adoption across the automotive value chain.

Market participants are increasingly focusing on expanding production capacity, enhancing wafer quality, reducing manufacturing costs, and forging strategic partnerships with automotive OEMs and Tier 1 suppliers.
Mergers and acquisitions are enabling market participants to access complementary technologies, enhance product offerings, and achieve economies of scale.
The report covers a forecast period from 2025 to 2033, with historic data spanning 2018-2023 and a base year of 2024.
source_title: Automotive GaN Wafer Market Research Report 2033

facts: SK Battery America announced the lay-off of 958 workers at its plant in Commerce, Georgia on 6 March.
The layoffs reduce the company's workforce by 37% to roughly 1,600 employees.
The Commerce, Georgia plant opened in January 2022 at a cost of US$2.6bn.
The plant had supplied cells for the Ford F-150 Lightning before Ford cancelled the model in December 2025.
The layoffs follow the December dissolution of SK’s US$11.4bn battery joint venture with Ford.
The JV dissolution left SK with a KRW3.7tr (US$2.6bn) asset impairment and a Tennessee plant not expected to begin production until 2028.
Cognisant that regulations have sent EV demand backwards in the US, SK Battery America is expanding into the battery energy storage system (ESS) market.
There are no signs of a meaningful reversal in EV demand until at least 2028.
Congress eliminated the US$7,500 consumer EV tax credit in September 2025.
The Trump administration has moved to weaken fuel economy and emissions standards, removing regulatory incentives for automakers to commit to electrification timelines.
US EV penetration held flat at around 8% of new vehicle sales in 2025.
EV demand was stronger in the first nine months of 2025 but was effectively cancelled out by a crash from October onwards.
The layoffs hold political significance in Georgia, a state that has fluctuated between voting for republicans and democrats since 2018.
Other steps to roll back EV policy have specifically affected only those interested in buying one, or the automakers and suppliers involved in their production.

The SK layoffs are a direct consequence of federal policy and an inconvenient one for Georgia, which attracted more Inflation Reduction Act (IRA)-related green investment projects than any other state.
A Rivian plant in Georgia, granted IRA loans in the final days of the Biden administration, also appears unsteady.
Despite President Donald Trump’s vocal support for US manufacturing, the SK layoffs serve as a direct counterexample that could lead to blowback in the upcoming midterms.
A second Georgia plant, built jointly with Hyundai, is on track to begin production in the first half of 2026.
The Tennessee facility retains the option to supply both automotive and ESS cells.
The pivot toward energy storage is also being pursued by LG Energy Solution and Samsung SDI.
This pivot is emerging as the Korean battery industry’s primary hedge against the collapse of US EV demand.
Whether the ESS market is large enough to accommodate a pivot from all three players remains to be seen.
SK On faces steep challenges around profitability, posting an operating loss of KRW 441.4bn in the fourth quarter of 2025.
The Ford JV dissolution removed KRW 5.4tr in debt from the balance sheet and freed SK to pursue new customers without JV governance constraints.
The company secured at least one major ESS supply deal and a Nissan battery agreement in 2025.
The Georgia headcount reduction is the operational consequence of the pivot toward ESS being underway in earnest.
source_title: SK On trims Georgia workforce by 37% as US EV demand cools | Automotive World

facts: Hyundai Motor Group’s new MetaPlant America (HMGMA) is located in Ellaville, Georgia.
The plant began construction in October 2022.
HMGMA was completed after just two and a half years of construction.
The facility departs from the conventional car factory model by combining human labor with advanced automation.
Approximately 880 human workers are employed at the plant.
Over 950 robots are deployed across the facility.
More than 200 autonomous transport robots are used for parts transportation and vehicle handling.
48 parking robots are deployed at the site.
700 robots are involved in welding and painting tasks.
A line of over 10 waist-height robots can be seen moving parts across the factory floor upon entering.
Autonomous mobile robots (AMRs) communicate with a central control system to track production progress and optimize movements in real-time.
Unlike traditional plants where presswork and car body construction are automated, component sorting and transportation at HMGMA are handled by AMRs.
A company official stated: “This plant embodies Hyundai’s vision for the future and its manufacturing capabilities.”
Boston Dynamics' four-legged robot “Spot” inspects the vehicle’s exterior in the final assembly stage by capturing images and comparing them with reference points to identify defects.
Hyundai has introduced the world’s first system for automatically removing and installing vehicle doors using robots equipped with AI-powered camera systems.
Dozens of cameras have been integrated into the press process to detect imperfections in finished panels.
The painting process incorporates an innovative system that captures and analyzes around 50,000 images per vehicle to ensure quality.
These technological advancements are part of Hyundai’s ongoing efforts to experiment with future mobility solutions at HMGMA.
The plant is designed to support “low-volume, high-variety production,” reflecting a shift from mass production to autonomous driving, connected cars, and in-car technologies.
Some areas of the assembly plant have no conveyor belts, instead using autonomous transport robots to carry car bodies for a flexible production flow that adapts to different models and options.
A company official stated: “By overcoming the limitations of conveyor belts, we expect our production system to evolve, bypassing unnecessary steps and streamlining processes.”
The company plans to pilot the “All-New Atlas” humanoid robot, developed by its robotics affiliate Boston Dynamics, in the near future.
source_title: Georgia’s HMGMA: Hyundai’s vision for automated, flexible car production

facts: The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) is the state's sales and marketing arm and the lead agency for attracting new business investment, encouraging the expansion of existing industry and small businesses, locating new markets for Georgia products, and planning and mobilizing state resources for economic development.

GDEcD serves as the central hub for strategic planning, policy development, and administrative operations, coordinating statewide economic development initiatives, business recruitment, international trade, and arts promotion.

GDEcD is located in Atlanta's Technology Square, a vibrant innovation district adjacent to Georgia Institute of Technology, fostering collaboration with academia, research institutions, and technology industries.
GDEcD maintains a robust global presence through a network of strategically located international offices in key markets across Asia, Europe, and the Americas.

GDEcD's international offices actively promote Georgia as a premier business destination, assist Georgia-based companies with export development and market access, and work to attract foreign direct investment into the state.
GDEcD provides services including market intelligence, business matchmaking, site selection assistance, and support for international delegations.
GDEcD headquarters is located at 75 Fifth Street N.W., Suite 1200, Atlanta, Georgia, USA.

GDEcD operates a Europe Office at Opernplatz 14, 60313 Frankfurt am Main, Germany, serving as the primary point of contact for European businesses looking to expand or relocate to Georgia and for Georgia companies seeking to enter or expand in the European market, covering the entire European region.

GDEcD operates a China Office at Room 1805, Central Tower, 555 Huaihai Middle Road, Shanghai 200020, P.R. China, strengthening economic ties between Georgia and China and facilitating foreign direct investment and export opportunities.

GDEcD operates a Korea Office at #1010, Gwanghwamun Officia Building, 92 Saemunan-ro, Jongno-gu, Seoul 03186, Korea, building strategic economic partnerships with South Korea and leveraging strong existing business relationships in key industries.

GDEcD operates a Japan Office at Yamate Mansion, #101, 19-5 Uguisudani-cho, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo 150-0032, Japan, managing and expanding the economic relationship with Japan to attract significant foreign direct investment.

As of April 2025, GDEcD's senior leadership team has shown stability, with no significant new hires or departures at the Commissioner or Deputy Commissioner level reported between mid-2023 and mid-2024.

On June 20, 2024, Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Cirba Solutions will invest over $300 million to expand its lithium-ion battery recycling operations in Columbia County, creating more than 100 new jobs.
GDEcD played a key role in attracting the Cirba Solutions project, showcasing Georgia's commitment to the EV and sustainable technology sectors.
On May 22, 2024, the State of Georgia, alongside the Georgia Ports Authority and local officials, broke ground on an $18 million expansion of the Appalachian Regional Port (ARP) in Murray County.
Supported by GDEcD, the ARP expansion aims to enhance logistics and supply chain capabilities in Northwest Georgia, attract investment, create jobs, and improve connectivity to the Port of Savannah.
source_title: Where is Georgia Department of Economic Development Located? HQ, Global Offices & Company Insights

facts: Georgia was awarded a Gold Shovel by economic development business publication Area Development for achieving significant success in attracting new business investment and job creation in 2023.
The award announcement was made on Friday, June 28th, 2024, by the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).
Governor Brian P. Kemp credited economic development teams across all four corners of the state for creating thousands of jobs throughout Georgia.
Kemp stated that these efforts helped Georgia secure record-breaking investments and hundreds of thousands of jobs for its communities.
Kemp attributed Georgia's continued status as No. 1 for business to a pro-business approach, an unmatched workforce, world-renowned logistics, and letting the market work.

Area Development’s annual Gold and Silver Shovel Awards recognize state economic development agencies that demonstrate excellence in creating a business-friendly environment, fostering innovation, and implementing strategies that successfully attracted high-quality investment projects.
All states were invited to submit information to Area Development regarding major groundbreakings, investments, and business expansions occurring in 2023.
Area Development Editor Andy Greiner noted the awards celebrate outstanding achievements in fostering economic growth and creating jobs.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson highlighted that Georgia stands out for its diversity of industries leading to success, specifically naming advanced manufacturing, aerospace, e-mobility, life sciences, and fintech.
Wilson stated that these diverse industries were instrumental in achieving a Shovel Award again this year.
Wilson reflected on back-to-back record-breaking years for the state and expressed anticipation for future milestones with partners.

Wilson congratulated Governor Kemp, the Georgia legislature, the state economic development team, and all statewide partners for their dedication and support in creating long-term community opportunities.

links.context: Information about the 2024 Gold and Silver Shovel Awards and detailed profiles of winning states

source_title: Georgia Recognized for New Business Investment, Job Creation - Augusta CEO

facts: PT Aneka Tambang (IDX:ANTM) and Indonesia Battery Corporation (IBC) are developing a series of integrated electric vehicle projects spanning upstream and downstream, to strengthen their role in the national battery industry ecosystem.

In collaboration with global partner CBL – a joint venture between CATL, Brunp, and Lygend – Aneka Tambang (ANTAM) and IBC aim to reflect Indonesia’s strategic role in growing the EV ecosystem in the Association of Southeast Asia Nations.
For the upstream operations, Aneka Tambang and CBL have formed a joint venture for nickel mining operations to supply the battery industry.

Both companies will build rotary kiln-electric furnace (RKEF) and high-pressure acid leach (HPAL) processing plants and the Buli Industrial Area, where will be supported by clean energy, an integrated water system, and a cloud-based control centre.
The RKEF plant will adopt the world’s first automated ferronickel refining technology developed by Brunp.
The HPAL plant, with an annual capacity of 55,000 tons of nickel, will use advanced third-generation technology and a gravity-based, tiered layout to optimise material flow.
Meanwhile, for the midstream and downstream operations, IBC and CBL are constructing a battery cell plant as part of an integrated project.
The project includes developing facilities for producing battery active materials, precursor and cathode, as well as battery recycling.
The plant is designed to process 16,000 tons of nickel sulphate per year, producing 30,000 tons of precursors and 30,000 tons of cathode active material.
Aneka says semi-finished materials will be transported westward to Karawang from East Halmahera.

At this strategic industrial zone, IBC and CBL are building a battery cell factory with an initial capacity of 6.9 gigawatt per hour, which is forecast to scale up to 15 gigawatt per hour within five years.
The company notes this production line is anticipated to begin in 2026, producing cells for EVs and battery energy storage systems for domestic and export markets.
IBC and CBL will complete the battery cycle with a recycling plant capable of processing 20,000 tons of used batteries per year into reusable battery materials.
The construction of this integrated battery plant was officially initiated by the President of the Republic of Indonesia, Prabowo Subianto.
Subianto says the development of an integrated electric vehicle battery industry ecosystem is a tangible step toward realising Indonesia’s national vision.
“The dream of industrial downstreaming has existed since our first President Bung Karno,” he says.
Subianto adds that this is the largest project in Southeast Asia and a testament to Indonesia’s commitment to driving environmentally friendly energy.

The inauguration is considered a key step in Indonesia’s energy transition efforts and is expected to strengthen national industrial independence by using strategic minerals in the global EV supply chain.
Global EV sales grew 28% in 2024, bolstered by rising adoption rates in China .

New EV car sales share of all new car sales in China increased 10 percentage points in 2024 reaching 48%, according to the Department of Industry, Science and Resources (DISR) June 2025 Resources and Energy Quarterly.
By 2027, two out of every three cars sold in China is expected to be a battery electric or a plug-in hybrid vehicle.
Comparatively, the US and EU markets experienced much lower growth in 2024.
In the EU, EV car sales fell (from over 2.4 million in 2023) to less than 2.3 million in 2024.
EV sales are expected to remain flat in the EU to 2026, before increasing to 2.9 million in 2027.
DISR notes that increasing EV adoption in the EU beyond 2027 reflects current emissions performance standards that require fleet-average emissions to fall 45% by 2030.
In the US, EV sales grew (from just under 1.4 million in 2023) to above 1.5 million in 2024.
EV sales are expected to grow to 2.1 million in 2027, though adoption rates will remain around half that seen in the EU and much lower than in China.
source_title: ANTAM strengthens battery ecosystem role - Mining.com.au

canonical_name: Georgia Zero Emission Vehicle / Low Emission Vehicle Tax Credit
facts: Governor Nathan Deal signed the Transportation Bill which repeals the ZEV/LEV Tax Credits.
The $5000 state tax credit for leasing or purchasing an electric vehicle expires June 30, 2015.
Qualifying vehicles for the credit include the Nissan LEAF, Tesla Model S or Roadster, KIA Soul EV, VW eGolf, and Mitsubishi Mio.
To qualify, Georgia residents must take delivery of a qualifying new vehicle by midnight June 30, 2015, with a signed Motor Vehicle Purchase Agreement signifying delivery.
Tim Echols leases two Nissan LEAFs in Athens, charging them in his garage for about $20 per month on his electric bill.
The Nissan LEAF has an approximately 100-mile range, which can cause "range anxiety" when pushed to its limit.
Tesla owners experience less range anxiety but face higher car payments.
Tim Echols pays about $270 per month in lease payments for each of his two Nissan LEAFs over a 24-month term.
Nissan incorporates the $7500 federal tax credit into the transaction price, reducing the effective monthly cost to about $208 per car.
After accounting for the state credit over 24 months, Tim Echols pays approximately $62 out of pocket per month per car.
A garage charger installation costs about $700 including labor and eliminates the need for oil, water, transmission fluid, or gas.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development estimates that displacing one percent of petroleum-based miles in Georgia with electric vehicles keeps approximately $201 million annually in the state.
Each pure electric vehicle purchased keeps $2,242 annually in Georgia by fueling with electricity instead of petroleum products.
Many electric car owners shift charging to overnight hours due to an incentive from Georgia Power.
A study of 1,000 Georgia electric car owners found they reduced their annual electricity bill by $180 despite not buying gasoline for the entire year.
This "load shifting" reduces peak load electricity usage, ultimately saving money for all ratepayers.
Tim Echols serves on the Georgia Public Service Commission and regulates electricity, natural gas, and telecom for the state.
source_title: #electricity | Atlanta Electric Vehicle Development Coalition

facts: Governor Brian Kemp and First Lady Marty Kemp joined President and Global COO of Hyundai Motor Company and President and CEO of Hyundai and Genesis Motor North America José Muñoz to celebrate Hyundai Motor Group’s historic impact on Georgia.
An update was provided on the progress of Hyundai Motor Group's Metaplant America facility.
The Metaplant America facility will officially start vehicle production in the fourth quarter of this year (2024).

Governor Brian Kemp stated that Hyundai has been an incredible partner to Georgia for decades and is helping recruit businesses, give back to communities, and work with colleges and universities to connect Georgians with opportunities.
Hyundai and Genesis electric vehicles on display included the IONIQ 5, IONIQ 6, GV60, and Electrified GV70.
The hydrogen-powered XCIENT Fuel Cell Truck will be used for logistics needs between Hyundai’s manufacturing facilities and suppliers.
Governor Kemp officially declared February 26 as Hyundai Day throughout the state of Georgia.
The Georgia General Assembly passed a Resolution commending Hyundai Motor Group’s dedication to the state and recognizing February 26 as Hyundai Day at the State Capitol.
José Muñoz stated that Hyundai’s investments in Georgia will create tens of thousands of great-paying American jobs.
Hyundai’s investments in Georgia are expected to bring tens of billions of dollars in economic impact for decades to come.
The Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) project was originally announced in May 2022.
LG Energy Solution was named as the joint venture partner in the manufacture of battery components for the project.
An additional expansion of the LG Energy Solution battery facility was announced.
Hyundai and SK On are building an EV battery manufacturing facility in Bartow County to supply Hyundai Motor Group’s U.S. plants.
Hyundai Motor Group’s planned investment in Georgia has reached more than $12.6 billion.
The project is bringing the creation of 12,000 direct, long-term jobs.

Lieutenant Governor Burt Jones, House Speaker Jon Burns, legislative leaders, GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson, and officials from the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority attended the celebration.
Lt. Governor Burt Jones applauded Governor Kemp, state agencies, and the University System of Georgia, especially Georgia Tech, for their partnership with Hyundai.
These initiatives support Georgia's position as the number one state in which to do business.
Up to 300,000 Hyundai, Genesis, and Kia EVs are expected to be produced annually at the Metaplant.
Seventeen HMGMA suppliers have announced projects across 10 Georgia counties spanning beyond southeast Georgia.
Further supplier announcements are expected.
Supplier facilities are expected to create more than 6,700 new jobs and represent more than $2.5 billion in investment.
Supplier facilities will be operational prior to the opening of HMGMA.
Speaker of the House Jon Burns stated that Hyundai's Metaplant will have a generational impact as a force multiplier for investments in education, healthcare, housing, and transportation.
The nearly 3,000-acre Metaplant site already has four building exteriors complete.
Outfitting for internal operations is underway at the Metaplant site.
The Metaplant’s architecture incorporates technologically advanced elements, design features representing the Savannah area, Hyundai’s brand identity, nature, and clean energy.
HMGMA's full-time employees are referred to as "Meta Pros" and are being hired rapidly for training on machinery, office/clerical work, and other roles.
HMGMA currently has 566 full-time employees.
Approximately 75 percent of the current workforce is from the Greater Savannah area.
An additional 37 employees are from Georgia but outside the local area.
Georgia Quick Start broke ground on its customized training center at the Metaplant site on February 8, 2024.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Georgians will be trained in high-skill careers as Meta Pros at one of the most advanced vehicle assembly and battery plants in the world.
HMGMA partnered with local technical colleges to prepare students for entry-level employment at the Metaplant.

Electrical Vehicle Professional Technical Certificate of Credit (EVP TCC) training is available at Coastal Pines Technical College’s Baxley and Waycross campuses, Southeastern Technical College’s Vidalia campus, Savannah Technical College, and Ogeechee Technical College.

In September 2023, Georgia Institute of Technology (Georgia Tech) and Hyundai Motor Company signed a memorandum of understanding to collaborate on research for sustainable mobility, including developing a hydrogen economy, workforce development, and smart city initiatives.
U.S. News & World Report ranked Georgia Tech as the top public university and No. 3 nationally in energy and fuel research.
Georgia Tech President Ángel Cabrera stated that the partnership will advance science for key sectors and produce the next-generation workforce needed for these industries.
Since 1998, Hyundai has directed over $6 million towards local Georgia communities through philanthropic programs and partnerships.
Hyundai is promoting STEM programs in local K-12 schools and hosting educational programs on alternative energy sources.
Hyundai supports local organizations dedicated to affordable homeownership and focuses on environmental stewardship in the region.
Trip Tollison of the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority stated that Hyundai has given more than $1 million to regional nonprofits.
Hyundai has committed to $7.59 billion in capital investment in Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
The Metaplant project will create 8,500 jobs according to the Joint Development Authority.
Beginning with mass production, the electricity used to power the Metaplant will come from 100 percent clean power sources.
This aligns with Hyundai’s commitment to carbon neutrality by 2045.
A 41-acre park will be featured in front of the plant, including trails, running tracks, environmental art displays, sports fields, and amenities for employee use.
source_title: State Leaders Celebrate Hyundai Motor Group’s Historic Impact on Georgia - Savannah CEO

facts: On 5 December 2024, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) in cooperation with Glovis America announced it has deployed Hyundai XCIENT heavy-duty hydrogen fuel-cell electric trucks for clean logistics operations.
Initially, a total of 21 XCIENT trucks will be in operation at the facility.

Oscar Kwon, HMGMA CEO, stated that the company is committed to creating sustainable transportation solutions and that the Hyundai XCIENT hydrogen fuel-cell trucks support the Metaplant by releasing zero emissions and offering a cleaner alternative to traditional freight logistics methods.

The deployment represents an industry-leading initiative to reduce HMGMA's carbon footprint and promote sustainable practices in daily logistics operations using zero-emissions hydrogen fuel cell technology.
The Hyundai XCIENT Class 8 heavy-duty trucks will transport vehicle parts from HMGMA suppliers across the region to the Megasite on a daily basis.
During the initial rollout, the XCIENT trucks will transport parts between suppliers and the on-site Consolidation Center before expanding to a broader network of suppliers.
The 21 XCIENT vehicles will represent more than one-third of the Glovis America truck fleet at HMGMA.

Earlier this year at CES, Hyundai Motor Group announced HTWO, its new hydrogen value chain business brand encompassing the Group's businesses and affiliates for the entire hydrogen value chain from production and storage to transportation and utilization.

Jim Park, SVP of commercial vehicle and hydrogen business development at Hyundai Motor North America, stated that HMGMA's deployment of XCIENT for logistics is one of the main initiatives of HTWO and delivers on the strategy to showcase a closed-loop hydrogen logistics ecosystem and improve manufacturing environmental sustainability.
HTWO Logistics, a joint venture between Hyundai Motor Company and Glovis America, will install a mobile hydrogen refueling station at the Megasite for efficient refueling.
A hydrogen production and refueling station is being developed for the Megasite and will be announced at a later date.
HMGMA is Hyundai Motor Group's first dedicated Electric Vehicle mass-production plant located in Bryan County, GA.

The new Georgia facility will feature a highly connected, automated, and flexible manufacturing system that organically connects all elements of the EV ecosystem to realize customer value and become an intelligent manufacturing plant.
All production processes at the facility—including order collection, procurement, logistics, and production—will be optimized utilizing AI and data.
The innovative manufacturing system will create a human-centered work environment with robots assisting human workers.

Hyundai Motor Group is a global enterprise with a value chain based on mobility, steel, construction, logistics, finance, IT, and service, employing about 250,000 people worldwide across its Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis brands.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group Deploys XCIENT Hydrogen Fuel Cell Trucks for HMGMA Clean Logistics

facts: EVs continue to grow as a share of the market with new models arriving or on the way and deals available.
Recent uproar targeting Tesla over CEO Elon Musk’s political activities impacts the market.
The expected elimination of federal EV tax credits is anticipated.
Recent sales declines have occurred in certain EV segments.
The average daily commute is less than 30 miles, making maximum range potentially overkill for many buyers.
Many EVs promise battery ranges of 300 miles or more.
Stephanie Valdez-Streaty, director of industry insights for Cox Automotive, notes that more range comes with more price.
Cold weather conditions are harder on EV batteries and can affect range.
Most people charge their EVs at home, though multiunit building residents may find this challenging.

The U.S. Energy Department notes many EV owners meet daily driving range requirements by charging overnight with Level 1 equipment requiring no additional cost or installation if a dedicated branch circuit outlet is available near their parking location.
Level 2 (240 volt) chargers can be installed at home to speed up the charging process.
Level 2 charging provides 25 miles of range per hour according to the Energy Department.
Some utility companies offer special rebates for installing Level 2 chargers as well as programs that can save money on charging.
The Energy Department lists almost 73,000 publicly accessible EV charging stations with one or more ports on its website.
DC Fast Charging provides 100-200 plus miles of range per 30 minutes of charging according to the Energy Department.
Tesla’s Supercharger network promises more than 60,000 global superchargers for drivers of its vehicles with a recharge rate of up to 200 miles in 15 minutes.
Many charging stations are located along prime corridors such as the Ohio Turnpike.
Waffle House signed a deal with an arm of BP to bring fast charging to its restaurants.
Stephanie Valdez-Streaty suggests prime EV candidates have shorter commutes, live where gas is expensive, and have good home or work charging access.
People who regularly drive long distances, have consistently long commutes, live in rural areas with limited charging infrastructure, or need large trucks for hauling may not be ideal EV drivers.
In April, the average transaction price for a new EV was $59,255 and the average listing price for a used EV was $35,874 according to Cox Automotive.
AAA notes it costs less to charge an EV than a gas-powered vehicle.
Some maintenance needs differ for EVs, such as no longer requiring oil changes.
The expected end of federal tax credits for EV purchases would eliminate one type of incentive, but state and local jurisdictions could fill that gap.
A large number of used EVs are expected to come off lease, leading to lower-priced options.
Stephanie Valdez-Streaty remains bullish on EVs in the long run, stating the future is still electric.
source_title: Before you buy an EV, consider these 5 questions

canonical_name: Hyundai EV Lab at Effingham College & Career Academy
facts: Effingham County School District partnered with Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America to unveil the new Hyundai EV Lab at Effingham College and Career Academy on Friday, October 24.
The state-of-the-art lab provides students with hands-on learning opportunities in electric vehicle technology—an industry that continues to drive innovation and workforce growth in Georgia.

Todd Wall, ECSD Career, Technical, and Agricultural Education Coordinator, stated the EV Lab is an invaluable addition to the engineering program because it provides true hands-on experience where students assemble, take apart, troubleshoot, and problem-solve in real time.

HMGMA Chief Administrative Officer Brent Stubbs said Hyundai chose the region not just for its infrastructure or resources, but for its people and community, highlighting that the partnership with Effingham County Schools and ECCA reflects a spirit of working together to build the future and develop talent for mobility in Effingham County.
The Hyundai EV Lab was designed to bridge the gap between classroom learning and real-world application and allows students to build, operate, and troubleshoot fully functioning electric vehicles.

Aaron Parker, ECCA Engineering Teacher, noted that the lab offers a true, real-world, hands-on learning experience where students apply skills directly in the workforce, giving them a strong foundation for the future whether they continue at a technical college or university.

ECSD Superintendent Dr. Yancy Ford commended the collaboration, noting how the lab represents the district’s commitment to connecting education and industry and providing real-world skills that connect directly to the future of Georgia’s workforce.
Following the ceremony, students demonstrated their work by driving one of their completed vehicles out of the lab to the applause of guests, educators, and community partners.
Effingham College and Career Academy is located in Rincon and opened in the fall of 2010 as one of Georgia’s first college and career academies.
ECCA offers district high school students programming options including engineering and technology, STEM, and computer science.
In 2025, ECCA began offering a manufacturing pathway and welcomed its first 22 students with plans to expand the program in 2026 and beyond.
source_title: EV Lab Sponsored by HMGMA Opens at Effingham College & Career Academy - HMGMA
